EDCS-00381059.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Titus Flavius Titi filius Crispus veteranus legionis III Augustae ex tubicine princeps Gennarum Thamugadensium mortis suae futurae sollicitus dum advixit sibi et coniugi libertae Ulpiae Euphoretae et Flaviis Crispino et Aemerito sic filiis obsequentissimis ex HS II milibus nummum fecit hic est scul(ptus)\n
EDCS-00381059.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Titus Flavius Crispus, son of Titus, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, chief horn-blower of the people of Thamugadi, worried about his future death, made this [monument] for himself and his freedwoman wife, Ulpia Euphoreta, and his most obedient sons, Flavius Crispinus and Emeritus, during his lifetime, for 2000 sesterces. He is sculpted here.
EDCS-00381059.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion in Thamugadi for himself, his wife, and sons, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03301000.text_edition: \n usa Saturninus vivit sic annis LXXV signai monimenta filii sui heredes fecerunt\n
EDCS-03301000.translation: Gaius Saturninus lived 75 years. His sons, his heirs, erected this monument.
EDCS-03301000.summary: A funerary monument erected by sons for their deceased father, Gaius Saturninus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03301001.text_edition: \n Dis sacrum Manibus Mettun vixit annorum sic XLV Aviania Casta fecit Magnius Secundulus\n
EDCS-03301001.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Mettun lived 45 years. Aviania Casta and Magnius Secundulus erected this.
EDCS-03301001.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by Aviania Casta and Magnius Secundulus for Mettun, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03700824.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Invicto Pio Felici Augusto patri patriae tribunicia potestate IIII consuli II [proconsuli ------\n
EDCS-03700824.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus, Invincible, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, Father of the Fatherland, holding tribunician power for the 4th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul...
EDCS-03700824.summary: The beginning of a dedication to Emperor Gordian III, listing his titles and honors, likely from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-04000201.translation: Marcus Claudius Marcianus lived 51 years. He lies here.
EDCS-04000201.summary: A simple tombstone for Marcus Claudius Marcianus, who lived to be 51, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-04000310.text_edition: \n Rogatianus ab ortu vitae in functionis diem probatissimus Deo venerandi minister altaris vixit in ecclesia annis LXXVIII in pace accersitus IIII Kalendas Iunias\n
EDCS-04000310.translation: Rogatianus, most approved from the beginning of his life until the day of his service, a minister of the venerable God of the altar, lived in the church 78 years. Called to God in peace on the 4th day before the Kalends of June.
EDCS-04000310.summary: A funerary inscription for Rogatianus, a church minister who lived to 78 and died on May 29th, likely dating to Late Antiquity.

EDCS-04001494.text_edition: \n Ex auctoritate Imperatoris Nervae Traiani Caesaris Augusti Germanici Dacici consulis VI imperatoris XIIII Lucius Acilius Strabo Clodius Nummus legatus Augusti pro praetore inter Augustum et Ammaederenses et Musulamios\n
EDCS-04001494.translation: By the authority of the Emperor Nerva Traianus Caesar Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, consul for the 6th time, imperator for the 14th time, Lucius Acilius Strabo Clodius Nummus, imperial legate with praetorian authority, between the city and the Ammaederenses and Musulamii.
EDCS-04001494.summary: A legal or administrative decree issued by the governor Lucius Acilius Strabo Clodius Nummus under Emperor Trajan, likely dating to the early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-04901090.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Constantino Invicto Pio Felici Augusto et Licinio Liciniano nobilissimo Caesari\n
EDCS-04901090.translation: To our Lord, the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus, Invincible, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, and to Licinius Licinianus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-04901090.summary: A dedication to Emperors Constantine I and Licinius, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-04901091.text_edition: \n ddominis nnnostris Flavio Iulio Crispo et Liciniano Licinio iuniori et Flavio Claudio Constantino iuniori nobbilissimis Caessaribus II\n
EDCS-04901091.translation: To our Lords Flavius Iulius Crispus, Licinius Licinianus the Younger, and Flavius Claudius Constantinus the Younger, the three most noble Caesars.
EDCS-04901091.summary: A dedication to the Caesars Crispus, Licinius the Younger, and Constantine the Younger, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-04901092.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Aurelio Domino nostro Constantino iuniore Caesare\n
EDCS-04901092.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Aurelius, our Lord, Constantine the Younger Caesar.
EDCS-04901092.summary: A dedication to Caesar Constantine, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-05000824.text_edition: \n Piissimo ac clementissimo nobilissimo Caesari Marco Aurelio Numeriano Pio Felici Augusto ordo splendidissimus coloniae Lambaesitanae devotus numini maiestatique eius\n
EDCS-05000824.translation: To the most pious, most merciful, and most noble Caesar Marcus Aurelius Numerianus Pius Felix Augustus, the most distinguished council of the colony of Lambaesis, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-05000824.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the town council of Lambaesis to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Numerianus, likely dating to his reign (283-284 AD).

EDCS-05000825.text_edition: \n Pro salute Victoria Imperatorum Valeriani et Gallieni Augustorum et exercituum Etrucius Secundus et Caius Iulius Rogatianus hastati priores et Marcus Antonius Saturninus optio dedicaverunt\n
EDCS-05000825.translation: For the safety of the Emperors Valerian and Gallienus Augusti and their armies, Etrucius Secundus and Caius Iulius Rogatianus, senior centurions, and Marcus Antonius Saturninus, optio, dedicated this.
EDCS-05000825.summary: This inscription records a dedication for the safety of Emperors Valerian and Gallienus by military officers, dating to their joint rule (253-260 AD).

EDCS-06100629.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum totiusque domus divinae et Marci Aureli Comini Cassiani viri clarissimi legati Auggustorum pro praetore Caius Iulius Martialis et Titus Alfius Messor maggistri cum senioribus loci fecerunt dedicaverunt\n
EDCS-06100629.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the safety of our lords, the entire imperial family, and for Marcus Aurelius Cominus Cassianus, clarissimus legate with pro-praetorian authority, Caius Iulius Martialis and Titus Alfius Messor, magistrates, with the local elders, made and dedicated this.
EDCS-06100629.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by local magistrates and elders for the safety of the emperors and a governor, dating to the late Roman period.

EDCS-07400752.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Valerio Constantino nobilissimo Caesari principi iuventutis\n
EDCS-07400752.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, most noble Caesar, prince of the youth.
EDCS-07400752.summary: This inscription is a formal address to Caesar Constantine, likely dating to his early reign (c. 306-317 AD).

EDCS-07400759.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio patri patriae Marcus Acilius Mani filius Papiria Concessus aedilis praefectus iure dicundo ob honorem aedilitatis super legitimam quam rei publicae intulit adiectis at sic HS II nummum quanti pollicitus est HS II nummum ex HS IIII nummum posuit idemque dedicavit tempore aedilitatis suae decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-07400759.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland, Marcus Acilius Mani, son of Concessus, Papiria, aedile and prefect with judicial authority, for the honor of his aedileship, in addition to the legal amount which he presented to the public treasury, he contributed 2,000 sesterces, which he had promised, out of 4,000 sesterces, and he himself dedicated it during his term as aedile by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-07400759.summary: This inscription records a substantial donation by a local official, Marcus Acilius Mani, for public works and honors during his aedileship, dedicated to Emperor Antoninus Pius, dating to his reign (138-161 AD).

EDCS-07400769.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Aurelio Severo Antonino Augusto Pio Felice sic divi Septiimi sic Severi Pii Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi et Iuliae Domnae matri castrorum et senatus et patriae filio et Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti Parthico maximo Britannico maximo Germanico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVIIII imperatori III consuli IIII proconsuli res publica Vahartanensium miliarium constituit\n
EDCS-07400769.translation: The republic of the Vahartanenses established this milestone for the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, son of the deified Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, and for Julia Domna, mother of the camps, of the senate, and of the fatherland, son of Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, and great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 19th time, Imperator for the 3rd time, Consul for the 4th time, Proconsul.
EDCS-07400769.summary: This inscription records the erection of a milestone by the community of Vahartanes in honor of Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus), detailing his extensive imperial titles and lineage, dating to his reign (198-217 AD).

EDCS-08100168.text_edition: \n Tiragio Augusto sacrum Bilius Miro collegio dono dedit\n
EDCS-08100168.translation: Sacred to Tiragathus Augustus. Bilius Miro dedicated this as a gift with his guild.
EDCS-08100168.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deity Tiragathus by Bilius Miro and his guild, likely from Roman Britain.

EDCS-08100211.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Amandus, a slave of the Augusti, a guard, lived 21 years and 3 months. He lies here. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-08100211.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Amandus, a slave and guard, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100213.text_edition: \n Memoriae Iulius Felicianus vixit annos XXV Chaeremon Augusti nostri pater filio innocentissimo\n
EDCS-08100213.translation: For the memory of Julius Felicianus, who lived 25 years. Chaeremon, father of our Augustus, to his most innocent son.
EDCS-08100213.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julius Felicianus, who died at age 25, dedicated by his father Chaeremon, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100214.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Syrus Augusti nostri servus adiutor a commentaris vixit annos XXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-08100214.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Syrus, servant of our Augustus, an assistant in the record office, lived 35 years. He lies here.
EDCS-08100214.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Syrus, a servant and assistant in the record office, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08100216.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Satellius Rufinus Pancratius eques Romanus iuvenis morum incomparabilis vixit annis XVII mensibus VIII diebus X Caius Satellius Ianuarius pater infelicissimus desertus ab eo vice versa filio piissimo\n
EDCS-08100216.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Satellius Rufinus Pancratius, a Roman knight, a youth of incomparable character, lived 17 years, 8 months, and 10 days. Caius Satellius Ianuarius, his most unhappy father, abandoned by him, his most devoted son, in turn.
EDCS-08100216.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young Roman knight, Marcus Satellius Rufinus Pancratius, who died at the age of 17, dedicated by his grieving father.

EDCS-08100218.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Iulius Quirina Martialis Publiamus sic sacerdos Caelestis Sittianae loci primi vixit annos LXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-08100218.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Iulius Martialis Publiamus of the Quirina tribe, the foremost priest of the local Caelestis shrine, lived 75 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08100218.summary: This epitaph records the death and burial of Publius Iulius Martialis Publiamus, a prominent priest, who lived to be 75.

EDCS-08100222.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Iulius Felix, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, a cloth merchant, lived 97 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08100222.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Iulius Felix, a cloth merchant, who lived to be 97 years old.

EDCS-08100223.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ursus scaenicus stupidus IIII coloniarum vixit annos XXV\n
EDCS-08100223.translation: To the departed spirits. Ursus, an actor from the region of the four colonies, lived 25 years.
EDCS-08100223.summary: This epitaph records the short life of Ursus, an actor from the region of the four colonies, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-08100229.translation: Aemilius Felix, dearest son, lived 3 years.
EDCS-08100229.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilius Felix, a child who died at the age of 3.

EDCS-08100230.translation: Marcus Aemilius Fundanus, son of Quintus, [...
EDCS-08100230.summary: This inscription begins to record the details of Marcus Aemilius Fundanus, son of Quintus, but is incomplete.

EDCS-08100231.translation: Quintus Aemilius Quietus lived 35 years. Emilia Maxima lived 75 years. Her bones are here; may they rest well.
EDCS-08100231.summary: This funerary inscription records the lives of Quintus Aemilius Quietus (35) and Emilia Maxima (75), with a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-08100233.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Quintilla, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-08100235.translation: Gaius Aleius Certus lived 61 years. He made this for his father Libertius.
EDCS-08100235.summary: This inscription records the life of Gaius Aleius Certus, who lived 61 years and dedicated the monument to his father, Libertius.

EDCS-08100236.translation: To the departed spirits. For Alfia Fida, who lived [3] years M[...
EDCS-08100236.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Alfia Fida, whose age is partially preserved, and is otherwise incomplete.

EDCS-08100238.translation: Ampelis lived 37 years. She rests here. May her bones repose well.
EDCS-08100238.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Ampelis at age 37, with a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-08100239.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus [---], son of Marcus, Felix, lived 60 years.
EDCS-08100239.summary: A tombstone for Marcus [---] Felix, son of Marcus, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-08100241.translation: For the memory of Annius Milimo, who lived 60 years. His wife Magnia Rogatula placed this.
EDCS-08100241.summary: A tombstone erected by Magnia Rogatula for her husband Annius Milimo, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-08100242.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Annius Severus lived 95 years. He rests here.
EDCS-08100242.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Annius Severus, who lived 95 years and rests here.

EDCS-08100243.translation: Annia Clara lived 25 years. She rests here.
EDCS-08100243.summary: A tombstone recording that Annia Clara lived 25 years and rests here.

EDCS-08100244.translation: Annia Natalis lived 53 years. She rests here.
EDCS-08100244.summary: A tombstone recording that Annia Natalis lived 53 years and rests here.

EDCS-08100245.translation: Annia Primosa lived 35 years. She rests here.
EDCS-08100245.summary: A tombstone recording that Annia Primosa lived 35 years and rests here.

EDCS-08100246.summary: A tombstone fragment recording that Annia Rogata lived 35 years.

EDCS-08100247.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Antistius Rufus lived 17 years. He rests here.
EDCS-08100247.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Antistius Rufus, who lived 17 years and rests here.

EDCS-08100248.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antistia Procula lived 35 years. She rests here.
EDCS-08100248.summary: A tombstone for Antistia Procula, who lived 35 years and rests here.

EDCS-08100249.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antistia Stercorosa lived for 40 years.
EDCS-08100249.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antistia Stercorosa, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-08100250.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antistia Urbana lived for 45 years. She lies here.
EDCS-08100250.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antistia Urbana, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-08100251.translation: To the spirits of the departed. May your bones rest well for Antonia Censita, who lived for 80 years.
EDCS-08100251.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Censita, recording her lifespan and a wish for her rest.

EDCS-08100252.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Tuta, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-08100253.translation: Quintus Apronius Felix lived for 55 years. He lies here.
EDCS-08100253.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Apronius Felix, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-08100254.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Apronius Vitalis, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, made this for his wife while he was alive.
EDCS-08100254.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Apronius Vitalis, indicating he commissioned a monument for his wife during his lifetime.

EDCS-08100255.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Apronia Vitalis lived for 35 years. She lies here.
EDCS-08100255.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Apronia Vitalis, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-08100256.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely for Lucia Aquilia, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-08100257.translation: To the memory of Ar[---], daughter of Maxim[---], DEI[---] RENA[---], who lived for [---] years.
EDCS-08100257.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph commemorating an individual and recording her lifespan.

EDCS-08100258.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Arellius [---]RCIN[---].
EDCS-08100258.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Lucius Arellius, likely indicating his death.

EDCS-08100259.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Arruntius Manilianus, son of Lucius, of the Voting Tribe Quirina, lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-08100259.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Arruntius Manilianus, who lived 75 years, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100261.translation: Asinia Felicia, to a most rare wife, lived 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-08100261.summary: This is a tombstone for Asinia Felicia, who lived 40 years, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100262.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Atilius...
EDCS-08100262.summary: This is a fragment of a tombstone for Publius Atilius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100263.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Atilia Saturnina...
EDCS-08100263.summary: This is a fragment of a tombstone for Atilia Saturnina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100264.translation: Aufidia Dativa placed this for her husband Aelius Adventus, [her] son.
EDCS-08100264.summary: This is a dedication by Aufidia Dativa for her husband Aelius Adventus and her son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100265.translation: Avilia Musibia, daughter of Lucius, lived 13 years; she is buried here. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-08100265.summary: This is a tombstone for Avilia Musibia, who lived 13 years, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100266.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aulus Nerius lived 81 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-08100266.summary: This is a tombstone for Aulus Nerius, who lived 81 years, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100267.summary: This is a fragment of a tombstone recording the age of Aurelius Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100268.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Victor lived 62 years. Aurelia Cunic(a?) made this.
EDCS-08100268.summary: This tombstone records the death of Aurelius Victor, who lived 62 years, and was made by Aurelia Cunic(a?), dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100269.translation: In memory of Marcus Aurelius Victor, son of Marcus Aurelius, lived...
EDCS-08100269.summary: This is a fragment of a memorial inscription for Marcus Aurelius Victor, son of Marcus Aurelius, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100270.translation: To the shades of the departed. Baebia Iucunditas lived for 25 years.
EDCS-08100270.summary: This is a tombstone for Baebia Iucunditas, who died at the age of 25, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100271.translation: Blasia lies here; may her bones rest well. She lived for 9 years.
EDCS-08100271.summary: This is a tombstone for Blasia, who died at the age of 9, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100272.summary: This is a tombstone for Blatta, who died at the age of 5, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100273.translation: To the shades of the departed. Gaius Bombius Ciceronianus lived for 16 years; he lies here.
EDCS-08100273.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Bombius Ciceronianus, who died at the age of 16, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100274.translation: To the memory of Brinnia Ianuaris, a most rare wife. She lived for 50 years.
EDCS-08100274.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to Brinnia Ianuaris, a wife who lived to 50, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100275.translation: Lucius C. Emptor lived for 21 years; he rests here. C. [?] Ianuaria his mother placed him.
EDCS-08100275.summary: This tombstone records Lucius C. Emptor, who died at 21, and was placed by his mother C. Ianuaria, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100276.translation: To the shades of the departed. Caecilius Maro lived for 15 years.
EDCS-08100276.summary: This is a tombstone for Caecilius Maro, who died at the age of 15, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100277.translation: To the shades of the departed. Caecilius Optatus lived for 31 years; he lies here.
EDCS-08100277.summary: This is a tombstone for Caecilius Optatus, who died at the age of 31, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100278.translation: Sextus Caecilius Potitus, son of Gaius, of the tribe Quirina, lies here. He lived for 30 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100278.summary: This is a tombstone for Sextus Caecilius Potitus, who died at 30 and wished his bones to rest well, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100279.translation: Gaius Caecilius Urbanus lived for 38 years; he lies here.
EDCS-08100279.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Caecilius Urbanus, who died at the age of 38, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100280.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Caecilius Silvanus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 16 years.
EDCS-08100280.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Caecilius Silvanus, recording his name, father's name, tribal affiliation, and age at death, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08100281.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, sacred. Caecilia Casia lived 5 years.
EDCS-08100281.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Casia, recording her name and age at death, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08100282.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Caecilia Cicada lived 55 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08100282.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Cicada, recording her name, age at death, and a common funerary wish, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08100283.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Caecilia Euporia, his wife, lived 45 years. She lies here.
EDCS-08100283.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Euporia, recording her name, her relationship to the dedicator (implied husband), and her age at death, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08100284.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Celestina lived 5 years.
EDCS-08100284.summary: A funerary epitaph for Celestina, recording her name and age at death, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08100285.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Caelia Cilopatria lived 71 years. She lies here.
EDCS-08100285.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caelia Cilopatria, recording her name, age at death, and burial location, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08100286.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Caesennius Minnullus lived 50 years. His bones here.
EDCS-08100286.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caesennius Minnullus, recording his name, age at death, and disposition of remains, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08100287.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Caesia Novicia lived 60 years.
EDCS-08100287.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caesia Novicia, recording her name and age at death, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08100288.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, sacred. Camurius Ira[...]
EDCS-08100288.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph for Camurius Ira, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08100289.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Caninius Ianuarius lived 25 years. He lies here.
EDCS-08100289.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Caninius Ianuarius, recording his name, age at death, and burial location, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08100291.translation: Carisia Marcella, daughter of Titus, lived 90 years.
EDCS-08100291.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Carisia Marcella, recording her name, filiation, and age at death.

EDCS-08100292.translation: To the departed spirits. Carpus lived 73 years. His bones lie here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100292.summary: This funerary epitaph, dedicated to the 'Dis Manibus', records the name Carpus, his age at death, and a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-08100293.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cassius Restutus, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-08100294.translation: In memory of Cassia Castula, mother. She lived 101 years. Her sons erected this.
EDCS-08100294.summary: This funerary epitaph records the memory of Cassia Castula, her great age, and that it was erected by her sons.

EDCS-08100295.translation: To the departed spirits. Cassia Dubitata lived 35 years.
EDCS-08100295.summary: This funerary epitaph, dedicated to the 'Dis Manibus', records the name Cassia Dubitata and her age at death.

EDCS-08100296.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name Cassia Rufina, her filiation, age at death, and a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-08100305.translation: Cornelius Astarius lived 25 years. May you rest well here.

EDCS-08100306.translation: Cornelius Florentinus lived 75 years; he rests here.

EDCS-08100308.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelia Cereola, likely a tombstone dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100313.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Crepereius Iucundus, who lived 51 years.

EDCS-08100316.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Domitius Pocosus lived 20 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-08100316.summary: This is a tombstone for Domitius Pocosus, who lived 20 years and is buried here.

EDCS-08100317.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Domitius Sisario lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-08100317.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Domitius Sisario, who lived 50 years and is buried here.

EDCS-08100325.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Dutarius lived 40 years.
