EDCS-12900488.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name Aulus Varonius Himnus and his lifespan of 41 years.

EDCS-12900493.translation: To the departed spirits. [To the] departed spirits(?) Maritumus [3]lus lived 31 years.
EDCS-12900493.summary: A funerary epitaph, heavily restored, beginning with a dedication to the departed spirits and recording a lifespan of 31 years.

EDCS-12900505.translation: [3]ia Pusinni/[3] lived 35 years. She rests here.
EDCS-12900505.summary: A funerary epitaph, with some names lost, recording a lifespan of 35 years and stating the deceased rests here.

EDCS-12900506.translation: ISAT(?) BELLA lived 45 years. She rests here(?).
EDCS-12900506.summary: A funerary epitaph, with the name partly uncertain, recording a lifespan of 45 years and indicating the deceased rests here.

EDCS-13000815.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Ulpius Absens lived 100 years. He rests here. Marcus Ulpius Aprilis and Atimetus made this for their most devoted father.
EDCS-13000815.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Marcus Ulpius Absens, who lived 100 years, erected by his sons Marcus Ulpius Aprilis and Atimetus.

EDCS-13000816.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Ulpius Aprilis lived 26 years. He rests here. Ulpius Atime[tus] made this for his devoted brother.
EDCS-13000816.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Marcus Ulpius Aprilis, who lived 26 years, erected by his brother Ulpius Atime[tus].

EDCS-13000817.summary: A fragment of a funerary epitaph recording the name Ulpius Atime[tus] and the beginning of his lifespan.

EDCS-13000952.translation: To the gods of the departed. Lucius Cominius Rogatus and his sons made this tomb for their father, who lived [X] years.
EDCS-13000952.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by sons for their father, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13000953.translation: To the gods of the departed. Flavia Donata and her sons made this tomb for their mother, who lived 85 years.
EDCS-13000953.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by a mother and her sons for their mother, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13001384.translation: Sacred to Sol Invictus, for the perpetual health and safety of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 6th time, Consul for the 3rd time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul.
EDCS-13001384.summary: A religious dedication to Sol Invictus, honoring Emperor Aurelian, dating to his reign (270-275 CE).

EDCS-13001726.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus. In the consulship of Gentianus and Bassus, on the seventh day before the Ides of May. Gaius Iulius Frontoiannus and Titus Seius Pudens, magistrates of the Thibilis people.
EDCS-13001726.summary: A dedication to a local deity, Bacacus Augustus, with consular dating to 198 CE, made by local magistrates.

EDCS-13001734.text_edition: \n Bacaci Augusto sacrum duobus Philippis consulibus Titus Iulius Victor Sorotius et Quintus Messius Felix maggistri Thibilitanorum\n
EDCS-13001734.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus. In the consulship of the two Philippi, Titus Iulius Victor Sorotius and Quintus Messius Felix, magistrates of the Thibilis people.
EDCS-13001734.summary: A dedication to the local deity Bacacus Augustus, with consular dating to 244 CE, made by local magistrates.

EDCS-13001747.translation: To Quintus Lollius Urbicus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, consul, legate of the Emperor in Lower Germania, Fetial Legate of the Emperor in the Jewish expedition, by whom he was honored with a hasta pura and a golden crown, legate of the Tenth Legion Gemina, praetor candidate of the Emperor, tribune of the plebs candidate of the Emperor, proconsular legate of Asia, quaestor of the city, senatorial tribune of the Twenty-second Legion Primigenia, commissioner of roads, patron, by decree of the decurions at public expense.
EDCS-13001747.summary: A career inscription for Quintus Lollius Urbicus, detailing his military and administrative posts under Emperor Hadrian, likely mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-13001908.summary: A brief inscription possibly naming a settlement or a fort, likely Roman period.

EDCS-13001994.text_edition: \n Veneri Augustae ndus legatus Augusti pro praetore dedit\n
EDCS-13001994.translation: Veneri Augustae. The legate of the Emperor gave.
EDCS-13001994.summary: A dedication to the goddess Venus Augusta, made by a legatus Augusti pro praetore, date unspecified.

EDCS-13002010.translation: Synopsis. A silver Jupiter, in the Capitol, bearing on its head a silver crown of oak leaves with 30 leaves and 15 acorns; in its right hand, a silver orb bearing 20 [spears?] and a crown of 40 leaves; holding in its left hand a silver spear. Below the inscription of Longanus' name. Also in the nymphaeum: on the top corona, around the circumference, 40 letters illuminated in gold, distinguishing ivy shoots, 10 hanging cups illuminated in gold, 1 gilded cup, 6 bronze statues, 6 marble Cupids, 6 bronze Silani, 6 hand towels.
EDCS-13002010.summary: A detailed inventory or description of precious objects, including statues and adornments, possibly related to a temple or a wealthy individual's collection, from an unspecified period.

EDCS-13002025.translation: To the deified Pertinax, father of the Emperor. Lucius Scantius Iulianus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, who received the public equestrian rank, dedicated this statue, which he promised from the revenues of the amphitheater on the day of the games he presented out of his liberality to honor his tenure as aedile.
EDCS-13002025.summary: A dedication of a statue to the deified Emperor Pertinax by Lucius Scantius Iulianus, commemorating his liberality during his aedileship, likely early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13002032.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Karino Invicto Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo Germanico maximo tribuniciae potestatis bis consuli patri patriae consuli proconsuli Marcus Aurelius Decimus vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Numidiae ex principe peregrinorum devotus numini maiestatique eorum\n
EDCS-13002032.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, twice Germanicus Maximus, endowed with tribunician power twice, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul. Marcus Aurelius Decimus, a most perfect man, governor of the province of Numidia, formerly chief of peregrines, devoted to their divine majesty.
EDCS-13002032.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Carinus by Marcus Aurelius Decimus, the governor of Numidia, likely dating to Carinus's reign (282-285 AD).

EDCS-13002066.text_edition: \n Tito Caesernio Titi filio Palatina Statio Quintio Statiano Memmio Macrino consuli sodali Augustali legato pro praetore provinciae Africae legato legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis misso ad dilectum iuniorum a divo Hadriano in regionem Transpadanam tribuno plebis quaestori candidato divi Hadriani comiti eiusdem in Oriente XVvirum sic stlitibus iudicandis decreto decurionum patrono IIII coloniae pecunia publica\n
EDCS-13002066.translation: To Titus Caesernius, son of Titus, of the Palatine tribe, Statian, Quintian, Statianus Memmius Macrinus, consul, Augustal sodalis, legate with proconsular power of the province of Africa, legate of the Legio XIIII Gemina Martia Victrix sent for the conscription of juniors by the divine Hadrian into the Transpadane region, tribune of the plebs, quaestor-elect, escort of the same divine Hadrian in the East, a member of the Quindecemviri Sacris Faciundis, patron of the colony of [Carthage?] by decree of the decurions, at the expense of the state.
EDCS-13002066.summary: This is a lengthy titulary inscription honouring Titus Caesernius Macrinus, detailing his extensive career and benefactions, probably from the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002079.text_edition: \n Publio Iulio Iuniano Martialiano clarissimo viro consuli quaestori provinciae Asiae tribuno plebei sic praetori curatori civitatis Calenorum curatori viarum Clodiae Cassiae et Ciminiae praefecto aerari militaris proconsuli provinciae Macedoniae legato legionis III Augustae Severianae Alexandrianae praesidi et patrono res publica Cirtensium decreto ordinis dedit dedicavitque\n
EDCS-13002079.translation: To Publius Iulius Iunianus Martialis, a most distinguished man, consul, quaestor of the province of Asia, tribune of the plebs, praetor, curator of the city of the Caleni, curator of the roads Clodia, Cassia, and Ciminia, prefect of the military treasury, proconsul of the province of Macedonia, legate of Legio III Augusta Severiana Alexandriana, governor and patron. The state of Cirta, by decree of its council, gave and dedicated this.
EDCS-13002079.summary: This inscription records the honors bestowed by the city of Cirta upon Publius Iulius Iunianus Martialis for his service and patronage, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13002080.text_edition: \n Publio Iulio Publi filio Quirina Geminio Marciano consuli sodali Titio proconsuli provinciae Macedoniae legato Auggustorum pro praetore provinciae Arabiae legato Auggustorum super vexillationes in Cappadocia legato Augusti legionis X geminae legato pro praetore provinciae Africae praetori tribuno plebis quaestori tribuno laticlavio legionis X Fretensis et legionis IIII Scythicae IIIviro kapitali optimo constantissimo Durmius Felix primipilaris legionis III Cyreneicae sic strator in Arabia maioris temporis legationis eius honoris causa dono dedit\n
EDCS-13002080.translation: To Publius Iulius Geminius Marcianus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, consul, Titian sodalis, proconsul of the province of Macedonia, legate of the Augusti with proconsular power of the province of Arabia, legate of the Augusti with detachments in Cappadocia, legate of the Augustus of Legio X Gemina, legate with proconsular power of the province of Africa, praetor, tribune of the plebs, quaestor, military tribune of the first rank of Legio X Fretensis and Legio IIII Scythica. Durmius Felix, a primipilaris of Legio III Cyrenaica, formerly a strator during the prolonged legateship of that man in Arabia, as a gift in honor of his legateship, gave this.
EDCS-13002080.summary: This inscription honors Publius Iulius Geminius Marcianus with a detailed list of his offices and is a gift from Durmius Felix, a primipilaris, likely dating to the Severan period (late 2nd to early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-13002091.translation: To Publius Porcius Optatus Flamma, a most distinguished boy, son of Publius Porcius Optatus Flamma, a most distinguished man, former praetor among those promoted to senatorial rank, quaestor of the province of Baetica, priest of the Flaviales and Titiales. By the judgment of our most sacred and most strong Lord, Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Pius, having been honored by him, and by Antoninus Caesar, designated Emperor. Sent to Germany and to Pannonia respectively, due to their proper devotion. Gaius Volumnius Marcellus Caecilianus, honored with the equestrian order, erected this in memory of him.
EDCS-13002091.summary: This funerary monument for Publius Porcius Optatus Flamma, a young man, records his father's distinguished career and his own honors, and was erected by Gaius Volumnius Marcellus Caecilianus during the reign of Septimius Severus (c. 193-211 AD).

EDCS-13002104.translation: Sacred to the departed shades. Aprilis, a vernal helper of our Augustus, a clerk, lived 20 years and 32 days. Thesmus, his heir, erected this at his own expense.
EDCS-13002104.summary: This is a tombstone for Aprilis, a clerk (adiutor tabularii), who died at a young age, erected by his heir Thesmu, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13002105.translation: To the departed shades. For Publicus Namhamon, his dearest father, who lived 65 years. Urbanica Felicisima, Esmara Coilia, his granddaughters, made this for their dearest grandfather.
EDCS-13002105.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to Publicus Namhamon by his granddaughters, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13002106.translation: Sacred to the departed shades. Hilarus, adopted son of the imperial treasury, an advocate, lived 22 years.
EDCS-13002106.summary: This is a tombstone for Hilarus, an adopted son of the imperial treasury and an advocate, who died at age 22, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13002107.translation: Gaius Aufidius Maximus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, prefect of Cohors IIII Bracarum in Judea, military tribune of Legio XII Fulminata in Cappadocia, was the first to donate and dedicate the portico and guest rooms for the honor of the pontificate, contributing 10,000 sesterces to the public treasury, and he himself dedicated it.
EDCS-13002107.summary: This inscription records the donation and dedication of a portico and guest rooms by Gaius Aufidius Maximus to the public treasury, likely dating to the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002109.translation: Gaius Iulius Rufinus, son of Gaius, soldier of Cohors VII Praetoria, century of Naevius, lived 22 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002109.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Iulius Rufinus, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard, who died at age 22, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002110.translation: Quintus Rusticelius Arnensis, soldier of the century of Quintus Vitulus, is buried here.
EDCS-13002110.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Rusticelius, a soldier, dating to the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002118.translation: Baebia Casta, daughter of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, priestess of Juno, lived 54 years. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-13002118.summary: A funerary inscription for Baebia Casta, a priestess of Juno, who died at age 54, likely from the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002126.translation: To the memory of Lucius Fadius Felix, who served as aedile, quaestor, IIIvir, and fulfilled all offices, and was IIIvir for four colonies, father of the equestrian order of Romans, and of Rusticus Fronto Severus and Fabia Monnula. He lived 80 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002126.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Fadius Felix, a man who held numerous civic and religious offices, including aedile and quaestor, likely dating to the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002128.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Gargilius Felix, priest of Saturn, lived 95 years. He willingly fulfilled his vow. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002128.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Gargilius Felix, a priest of Saturn, who lived to 95, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002130.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Fundanius Modestus, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, was a IIIvir.
EDCS-13002130.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Fundanius Modestus, who held the office of IIIvir, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002133.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Postuma, priestess of Juno, lived 51 years.
EDCS-13002133.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Postuma, a priestess of Juno, who died at age 51, likely from the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002134.translation: Quintus Junius Firminus, son of Publius, of the Arnensis tribe, served as aedile, IIvir, quaestor, and perpetual flamen. He lived 67 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002134.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Junius Firminus, a civic and religious official who served as aedile, IIvir, and flamen, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002135.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Pater, priest of Belona, lived 60 years.
EDCS-13002135.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Pater, a priest of Belona, who lived to 60, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002138.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Pompeius Quintus, priest of Saturn, husband, lived 65 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002138.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Pompeius Quintus, a priest of Saturn and husband, who lived to 65, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002141.translation: Publius Sittius Dento, son of Publius, served as aedile, IIvir, quaestor, and quinquennial flamen. He lived 60 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002141.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Sittius Dento, a civic and religious official who served as aedile, IIvir, and flamen, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002173.translation: Publius Caecilius, a merchant, lived 55 years; he rests here.
EDCS-13002173.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Caecilius, a merchant, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-13002175.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Virius Ianuarius vixit LX\n
EDCS-13002175.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Lucius Virius Ianuarius lived 60 years.
EDCS-13002175.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits for Lucius Virius Ianuarius, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-13002194.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Calpur<ni=O>a Quaetea vixit annis LV \n
EDCS-13002194.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Calpurnia Quaetea lived 55 years.
EDCS-13002194.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits for Calpurnia Quaetea, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-13002195.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini Pii filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Armeniaco Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVIII consuli III decreto decurionum pecunia publica\n
EDCS-13002195.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Antoninus Pius, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grand-nephew of the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Armenicus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the 3rd time, by decree of the town councilors, at public expense.
EDCS-13002195.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, listing his imperial titles and noting the funding source for a project as public expense by decree of the town councilors.

EDCS-13002196.text_edition: \n us Sexti filius Quirina Cicero arcum quem ob honorem magisterii aediliciae iuris dictionis promiserat ex HS III milibus fecit idemque dedicavit\n
EDCS-13002196.translation: Cicero, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, built the arch, which he had promised for the honor of his magistracy with jurisdiction in legal matters, at a cost of 3,000 sesterces, and dedicated it himself.
EDCS-13002196.summary: This records the construction and dedication of an arch by Cicero, son of Sextus, for his magistracy, funded by himself at a cost of 3,000 sesterces.

EDCS-13002197.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Bombia Rogata Tuti filia vixit annos CI hic sita est\n
EDCS-13002197.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Bombia Rogata, daughter of Tuti, lived 101 years; she rests here.
EDCS-13002197.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits for Bombia Rogata, daughter of Tuti, who lived 101 years.

EDCS-13002198.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caecilia Extric<a=R>ta vixit annos LXI hic sita est \n
EDCS-13002198.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Caecilia Extricata lived 61 years; she rests here.
EDCS-13002198.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits for Caecilia Extricata, who lived 61 years.

EDCS-13002199.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Iulius Saturninus vixit annos LXI\n
EDCS-13002199.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Quintus Iulius Saturninus lived 61 years.
EDCS-13002199.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits for Quintus Iulius Saturninus, who lived 61 years.

EDCS-13002200.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Ianuaria vixit annos LI hic sita est\n
EDCS-13002200.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Iulia Ianuaria lived 51 years; she rests here.
EDCS-13002200.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits for Iulia Ianuaria, who lived 51 years.

EDCS-13002202.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Sittius Rusticus vixit annos XXXXVII hic situs est\n
EDCS-13002202.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Caius Sittius Rusticus lived 47 years; he rests here.
EDCS-13002202.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits for Caius Sittius Rusticus, who lived 47 years.

EDCS-13002203.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Vitruvius Victor vixit annos XLIX hic situs est\n
EDCS-13002203.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Vitruvius Victor lived for 49 years. He rests here.
EDCS-13002203.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name, age, and burial of Marcus Vitruvius Victor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002204.text_edition: \n Mius Cerealis vixit annos VII hic situs est\n
EDCS-13002204.translation: Mius Cerealis lived for 7 years. He rests here.
EDCS-13002204.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and age of a child named Mius Cerealis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002216.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 11th time, imperator for the 6th time, consul for the 5th time, father of the country, to the best and greatest prince.
EDCS-13002216.summary: A fragment of a dedicatory inscription honoring Emperor Trajan, listing his titles and honors, likely from the early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13002487.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ania Gaetula lived for 11 years. [Illegible name or epithet].
EDCS-13002487.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Ania Gaetula, with some illegible text, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002507.text_edition: \n Lari Libero Augusto sacrum Quintus Quadratius filius Quirina Quintulus aediliciae potestatis statuam cum aedicula et columnis quam in praefectura sua pro IIIviris agens sponte pollicitus est praeter legitima HS XX nummum decurionatus rei publicae inlata dedit dedicavitque locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-13002507.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater Augustus. Quintus Quadratius Quintulus, son of the Quirina tribe, holding the office of aedile, voluntarily promised and dedicated a statue with a small shrine and columns, which he had pledged in his prefecture while serving as a magistrate of the three men (IIIvir), beyond the legal requirements, for 20,000 sesterces to the treasury of the state, and dedicated it; the site was granted by a decree of the decurions.
EDCS-13002507.summary: A public dedication of a statue complex by Quintus Quadratius Quintulus to the god Liber Pater Augustus, funded privately but sanctioned by the local council, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002512.translation: Iulia Pamilla lived for 35 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13002512.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Pamilla, recording her name, age, and burial location, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002513.translation: Sittia Ianuaria lived for 30 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13002513.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sittia Ianuaria, recording her name, age, burial location, and a common wish for the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002952.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudia Concordia lived for 37 years. She is buried here. Amandus made (this) for his wife.
EDCS-13002952.summary: A funerary epitaph for Claudia Concordia, erected by her husband Amandus, recording her name, age, and burial, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003259.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto divi Antonini filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae apneboti sic imperatori II pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVIII consuli III cohors I Chalcidenorum equitata Caio Maesio Picatiano legato Augustorum pro praetore\n
EDCS-13003259.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of the deified Antoninus, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, thus emperor for the 2nd time, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the 3rd time, by the First Cohort of Chalcidians, cavalry, Caio Maesio Picatiano, legatus Augustorum pro praetore.
EDCS-13003259.summary: An inscription commemorating Emperor Marcus Aurelius, listing his extensive titles and honors, and dedicating it on behalf of the military unit and its governor, likely from the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-13003291.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucilia Terentia, an incomparable wife, and her son Lucilianus made (this) for Marcus Aurelius Fidelis, procurator, their most beloved father.
EDCS-13003291.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Fidelis, a procurator, dedicated by his wife and son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003304.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aulus Caelins Cuminus lived 40 years; he lies buried here.
EDCS-13003304.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Aulus Caelins Cuminus, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-13003305.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aebutius Publianus lived 10 years; he lies buried here.
EDCS-13003305.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Marcus Aebutius Publianus, who lived 10 years.

EDCS-13003307.translation: Aelia Bassa lived 40 years; she lies buried here.
EDCS-13003307.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Aelia Bassa, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-13003308.translation: Aemilius Iulius Tanusius lived 20 years; he lies buried here.
EDCS-13003308.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Aemilius Iulius Tanusius, who lived 20 years.

EDCS-13003309.translation: Lucius Aebutius Bassus, son of Publius, of the voting tribe Quirina, lived 21 years; he lies buried here.
EDCS-13003309.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Lucius Aebutius Bassus, who lived 21 years.

EDCS-13003310.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Aerenius Bassus lived 40 years; he lies buried here.
EDCS-13003310.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Lucius Aerenius Bassus, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-13003311.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Alfius Victorinus lived 11 years; he lies buried here.
EDCS-13003311.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Alfius Victorinus, who lived 11 years.

EDCS-13003312.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Arruntius Anaxar[...
EDCS-13003312.summary: A tombstone fragment, likely dedicated to Arruntius Anaxar, for the spirits of the departed.

EDCS-13003313.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Euplia, my dearest wife...
EDCS-13003313.summary: A tombstone fragment, dedicated to Aurelia Euplia, my dearest wife, for the spirits of the departed.

EDCS-13003316.translation: [?] Blandia lived ... years; her bones may rest well here.
EDCS-13003316.summary: A tombstone fragment for Blandia, possibly wishing her bones rest well, with an uncertain initial word.

EDCS-13003317.translation: Sacred to the shades of the departed. Bromius lived 10 years and is buried here. Bacchus erected this for his dearest son.
EDCS-13003317.summary: A funerary inscription for a boy named Bromius, who lived 10 years, erected by his father, Bacchus.

EDCS-13003318.translation: To the shades of the departed. Quintus C. Crispinus lived 60 years.
EDCS-13003318.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Quintus C. Crispinus, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-13003319.translation: To the shades of the departed. Caecilia Vitalis lived 21 years.
EDCS-13003319.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Caecilia Vitalis, who lived 21 years.

EDCS-13003320.translation: To the memory of Claudia Maxima, who lived 14 years, 2 months, and 4 days. Claudius Ceriolus made this for his most precious daughter.
EDCS-13003320.summary: A funerary inscription for Claudia Maxima, who died at age 14, erected by her father Claudius Ceriolus.

EDCS-13003321.translation: To the shades of the departed. Cominia Matrona lived 101 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13003321.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Cominia Matrona, who lived 101 years.

EDCS-13003322.translation: Publius Colosius Proculus lived 10 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003322.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Colosius Proculus, who died at age 10, with a wish for his peaceful repose.

EDCS-13003323.translation: Marcus Cominius Romanus lived 50 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13003323.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Marcus Cominius Romanus, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-13003324.translation: To the shades of the departed. Lucius Cominius Proculus lived 35 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13003324.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Lucius Cominius Proculus, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-13003326.translation: Cornelia Luculla lived 25 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13003326.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Cornelia Luculla, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-13003327.translation: To the shades of the departed. Cornelia Secunda lived 31 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13003327.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Cornelia Secunda, who lived 31 years.

EDCS-13003328.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Curia...
EDCS-13003328.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, for someone named Curia, with the rest of the inscription lost.

EDCS-13003329.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Dative lived for 10 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-13003329.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dative, who lived for 10 years, with a wish for her repose.

EDCS-13003330.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Domitia lived for 35 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13003330.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Domitia, who lived for 35 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13003331.translation: Gaius Fabius Aflanus lived for 21 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13003331.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Fabius Aflanus, who lived for 21 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13003332.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Faustilla lived for 39 years.
EDCS-13003332.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Faustilla, who lived for 39 years.

EDCS-13003334.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavia Ianuaris lived for 4 years.
EDCS-13003334.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Flavia Ianuaris, who lived for 4 years.

EDCS-13003335.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Furniae Frontillae, who lived for 4 years. Helvia Maximilla placed this for her daughter.
EDCS-13003335.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Helvia Maximilla for her daughter Furniae Frontillae, who lived for 4 years.

EDCS-13003336.translation: Sextus Gargilius Oriculus lived for 90 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13003336.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Gargilius Oriculus, who lived for 90 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13003338.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Orcivius Pusincinus lived for 71 years. He is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Orchivia Urbana lived for [6] years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13003338.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, likely for a husband and wife, Gaius Orcivius Pusincinus (71 years) and Orchivia Urbana (partially legible age).

EDCS-13003339.translation: Aulus Granius Lucullus lived for 85 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13003339.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Granius Lucullus, who lived for 85 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13003340.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Grania Meletine lived 30 years; she lies here.
EDCS-13003340.summary: A funerary epitaph for Grania Meletine, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003341.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Grania Saturnina lived 4 years; she lies here.
EDCS-13003341.summary: A funerary epitaph for Grania Saturnina, recording her short life of 4 years.

EDCS-13003342.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Chordionius Aelianus lived 50 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003342.summary: A funerary inscription for Chordionius Aelianus, including a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-13003343.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Chordionius Epaphroditus lived 85 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13003343.summary: A funerary epitaph for Chordionius Epaphroditus, noting his long life of 85 years.

EDCS-13003344.translation: To the memory of Hordionius Exuperantis, who lived 3 years. His father made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-13003344.summary: A funerary dedication from a father to his young son, Hordionius Exuperantis.

EDCS-13003345.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Hospes and Gaius Julius Gentius set this up for their most beloved brother, who lived 23 years.
EDCS-13003345.summary: A funerary monument erected by two brothers for their deceased sibling, Gaius Julius, who died at 23.

EDCS-13003346.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Julius Longinus lived 80 years.
EDCS-13003346.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Julius Longinus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-13003347.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Maximus lived 25 years; he lies here. May he rest.
EDCS-13003347.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Maximus, who died at 25.

EDCS-13003348.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Namphamo lived 60 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13003348.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Namphamo, recording his age of 60.

EDCS-13003349.translation: Decimus Julius Palma, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 10 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13003349.summary: A funerary epitaph for Decimus Julius Palma, a young boy who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-13003350.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Gentianus lived for 16 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003350.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Julius Gentianus, who died at age 16, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003351.translation: Gaius Julius Urbanus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 70 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003351.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Julius Urbanus, who died at age 70, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003352.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Julius Urbanus lived for 51 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003352.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Julius Urbanus, who died at age 51, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003353.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Iulius Ettus(?) lived for 21 years.
EDCS-13003353.summary: A tombstone fragment for Gaius Iulius Ettus (?), who died at age 21, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003354.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Matrona lived for 65 years.
EDCS-13003354.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Matrona, who died at age 65, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003355.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Mulsula lived for 40 years. She lies here.
EDCS-13003355.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Mulsula, who died at age 40, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003356.translation: Lucia Iullia Mustela, daughter of Lucius, lived for 13 years. Her bones may rest well here. [Uncertain reading of final character].
EDCS-13003356.summary: A tombstone for Lucia Iullia Mustela, who died at age 13, likely from the Imperial period, with a wish for her rest.

EDCS-13003357.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Octavia lived for 31 years. She lies here.
EDCS-13003357.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Octavia, who died at age 31, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003358.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Instanius Augentius lived for 20 years.
EDCS-13003358.summary: A tombstone fragment for Instanius Augentius, who died at age 20, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003360.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Licinius Celsus lived for 105 years.
EDCS-13003360.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Licinius Celsus, who died at age 105, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13003361.translation: Lucius Licinius Tertullus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 101 years; he rests here; may his bones lie peacefully.
EDCS-13003361.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Lucius Licinius Tertullus, who lived to be 101.

EDCS-13003362.translation: Manilia Amcismi, freedwoman of Lucius, rests here; she lived 60 years.
EDCS-13003362.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Manilia Amcismi, a freedwoman.

EDCS-13003364.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, partially preserved, for Quintus Vanlius Rusticus.

EDCS-13003365.translation: To the spirits of the underworld; Marcius...
EDCS-13003365.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, incompletely preserved, dedicated to the spirits of the underworld, likely for someone named Marcius.

EDCS-13003366.translation: Marcia, daughter of Optatus, lived 20 years; she rests here.
EDCS-13003366.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Marcia, daughter of Optatus.

EDCS-13003368.translation: To the spirits of the underworld; Quintus Minucius Felix lived 30 years.
EDCS-13003368.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Quintus Minucius Felix.

EDCS-13003369.translation: To the spirits of the underworld; Minucia Syntyche lived 40 years; she rests here.
EDCS-13003369.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Minucia Syntyche.

EDCS-13003371.translation: Mustiolus lived six years; he is here.
EDCS-13003371.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the short life and burial of a person named Mustiolus.

EDCS-13003372.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Nasidius Crispus.

EDCS-13003375.translation: To the spirits of the underworld; Lucius Oclatius Licinianus lived 6 years and...?
EDCS-13003375.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the very short life of Lucius Oclatius Licinianus, with an uncertain measurement of age.

EDCS-13003376.translation: Ofillia Matrona lived 60 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13003376.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ofillia Matrona, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003377.translation: To the departed spirits. Titus Ovinius Saturus lived 66 years. Minosa his wife lived 51 years. Their bones lie here; may they rest well.
EDCS-13003377.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Ovinius Saturus and his wife Minosa, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003378.translation: To the departed spirits. Papiria Acuta lived 60 years.
EDCS-13003378.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Papiria Acuta, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003379.translation: To the departed spirits. Percennius Quintilianus lived one month and is buried here.
EDCS-13003379.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Percennius Quintilianus, noting his very short lifespan, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003380.translation: To the departed spirits. PILLI(?) lived 15 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003380.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an individual identified as PILLI (name uncertain), dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003381.translation: To the departed spirits. Pompeius Ingenuus lived 80 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003381.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pompeius Ingenuus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003385.translation: To the departed spirits. Publicius Custos lived 30 years.
EDCS-13003385.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publicius Custos, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003387.translation: To the departed spirits. Drupilius Neptunalis lived 25 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13003387.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Drupilius Neptunalis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003391.translation: Scatia Fabula lived 40 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13003391.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Scatia Fabula, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003393.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. [I]sittius Crescentianus lived 66 years.
EDCS-13003393.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Isittius Crescentianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003394.translation: Publius Sittius Gaetulus lived [3] years; he rests here. To the departed spirits.
EDCS-13003394.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Sittius Gaetulus, recording his age at death and invoking the departed spirits, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13003396.translation: To the memory of Lucius Sittius Salvius, who lived 3 years and 3 months, a most loving son; he rests here.
EDCS-13003396.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Sittius Salvius, recording his age and expressing familial love, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13003399.translation: Sittia Candida lived 75 years; she rests here.
EDCS-13003399.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sittia Candida, recording her age at death, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13003400.translation: To the departed spirits. Sittia Marcel[l]a lived 61 years; may her bones rest well.
EDCS-13003400.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sittia Marcel[l]a, recording her age and invoking a blessing on her remains, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13003401.translation: To the memory of Sittia Novella, who lived 38 years.
EDCS-13003401.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sittia Novella, recording her age at death, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13003402.translation: Sittia Parthenope lived 30 years; she rests here.
EDCS-13003402.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sittia Parthenope, recording her age and place of rest, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13003403.translation: Spitia Tertia, daughter of Publius, lived 90 years; she rests here.
EDCS-13003403.summary: A funerary epitaph for Spitia Tertia, daughter of Publius, recording her age at death, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13003406.translation: To Tadiae Rogata, who lived 70 years; she rests here. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-13003406.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tadiae Rogata, recording her age and invoking a blessing on her remains, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13003407.translation: To the departed spirits. Valeria Cheromena lived 8 years and 6 days.
EDCS-13003407.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Cheromena, recording her very young age at death and invoking the departed spirits, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13003408.translation: To the departed spirits. Valeria Maximi[n]a lived 35 years.
EDCS-13003408.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Maximi[n]a, recording her age at death and invoking the departed spirits, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13003409.translation: To the divine spirits. Veneria (?) lived [years]
EDCS-13003409.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Veneria, likely indicating her age.

EDCS-13003410.translation: To the divine spirits. Gaius Vennius Marciamus lived 21 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13003410.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Gaius Vennius Marciamus at the age of 21.

EDCS-13003411.translation: To the divine spirits. Victor lived 10 years. Here you are well.
EDCS-13003411.summary: A funerary inscription for a child named Victor who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-13003414.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Hilara at the age of 27.

EDCS-13003415.translation: To Caecina Matrona (?). She lived 31 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13003415.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Caecina Matrona at the age of 31.

EDCS-13003437.text_edition: \n Divo Commodo divi Marci Antonini Germanici Sarmatici filio fratri Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici propagatori imperii pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate V imperatori consuli II \n
EDCS-13003437.translation: To the deified Commodus, son of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, Germanicus, Sarmaticus, brother of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus, the propagator of the empire, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 5th time, imperator for the 1st time, consul for the 2nd time.
EDCS-13003437.summary: A dedicatory inscription honoring the deified Emperor Commodus, listing his divine lineage and imperial titles up to Lucius Septimius Severus.

EDCS-13003438.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Octavia Fellicula sic vixit annos LXXXXV hic sita est\n
EDCS-13003438.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Octavia Fellicula lived 95 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13003438.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Octavia Fellicula at the age of 95.

EDCS-13003440.text_edition: \n Genio Celtianis Augusto sacrum Lucius Manilius Luci filius Quirina Potens quo numinis eius adiumentum senserit ex HS VIIIocto milibus sua pecunia fecit idemque dedicavit\n
EDCS-13003440.translation: Sacred to the Genius of Celtianis Augustus. Lucius Manilius, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, Potens, having felt the help of his divinity, made this from 80,000 sesterces with his own money and dedicated it himself.
EDCS-13003440.summary: A dedication to the Genius of Celtianis Augustus, funded by Lucius Manilius Potens who experienced divine aid.

EDCS-13003441.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini Pii filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti Lucio Aurelio Vero Augusto Armeniaco imperatori II tribunicia potestate III consuli II proconsuli decreto decurionum pecunia publica\n
EDCS-13003441.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Antoninus Pius, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus Armeniacus, imperator for the 2nd time, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, by decree of the decurions, with public funds.
EDCS-13003441.summary: A public dedication to Emperor Lucius Aurelius Verus, recording his titles and commissioned by local officials.

EDCS-13003444.text_edition: \n Iuliae Augustae matri castrorum coniugi Imperatoris Caesaris divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici fili divi Commodi fratris divi Antonini Pii nepotis divi Hadriani pronepotis divi Traiani Parthici abnepotis divi Nervae adnepotis Luci Septimi Severi Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate XIII imperatoris XI consulis III proconsulis propagatoris imperii fortissimi felicissimique principis Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi fili divi Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepotis divi Antonini Pii pronepotis divi Hadriani abnepotis divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepotis Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate VIIII consulis II proconsulis fortissimi felicissimique principis patris patriae nobilissimi et super omnes principes indulgentissimo sic res publica Castelli Celtianis decreto decurionum pecunia publica\n
EDCS-13003444.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the camps, wife of the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the deified Commodus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, abnepos of the deified Trajan Parthicus, and adnepos of the deified Nerva, Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 13th time, imperator for the 11th time, consul for the 3rd time, proconsul, propagator of the empire, the most courageous and fortunate prince, son of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, abnepos of the deified Hadrian, adnepos of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 9th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, the most courageous and fortunate prince, father of the country, most noble and most indulgent of all princes, the state of the Castellum Celtianis, by decree of the decurions, with public funds.
EDCS-13003444.summary: A complex official dedication honoring Empress Julia Domna, wife of Septimius Severus and mother of Caracalla, set up by the state of Castellum Celtianis.

EDCS-13003445.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Oppius Cai filius Quirina Rogatus veteranus legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis vixit annos LV hic situs est\n
EDCS-13003445.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Oppius Rogatus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, veteran of the I Adjutrix Pia Fidelis legion, lived 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003445.summary: A tombstone recording the death and military service of a veteran, Gaius Oppius Rogatus.

EDCS-13003446.text_edition: \n Quintus Bottius Titi filius Quirina Senecio magister pagi aediliciae iuris dictionis suo? nomine? etet Crescentis filiorum suorum statuis columnis \n
EDCS-13003446.translation: Quintus Bottius Senecio, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, master of the pagus, by his own authority and that of his sons Crescentis, [dedicated] statues and columns.
EDCS-13003446.summary: A dedication by Quintus Bottius Senecio, likely a local official, for statues and columns.

EDCS-13003447.text_edition: \n Bottio filio Quirina Senecioni filio ob munificentiam Luci Botti Senecionis patris eius Celtianenses aere conlato posuerunt decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-13003447.translation: The citizens of Celtianenses, by a decree of the decurions, with contributions of bronze, erected [this monument] to Bottius Senecio, son of Quirina and son of his father Lucius Bottius Senecio, because of his munificence.
EDCS-13003447.summary: A public monument erected by the citizens of Celtianenses to honor Bottius Senecio for his generosity.

EDCS-13003449.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Aemilius Aculus vixit annis XXXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-13003449.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aemilius Aculus lived 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003449.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Marcus Aemilius Aculus.

EDCS-13003450.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Aemilius Eroticus vixit annos LXI hic situs est\n
EDCS-13003450.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Aemilius Eroticus lived 61 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003450.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Quintus Aemilius Eroticus.

EDCS-13003451.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aemilius Faustus vixit annos XL hic situs\n
EDCS-13003451.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilius Faustus lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003451.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Aemilius Faustus.

EDCS-13003452.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Aemilius Luci filius Quirina Fortunatus vixit annos LXXXI hic situs est\n
EDCS-13003452.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Aemilius Fortunatus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 81 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003452.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Lucius Aemilius Fortunatus.

EDCS-13003453.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Aemilius Cai filius Quirina Martialis vixit annos XXXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-13003453.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Aemilius Martialis, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003453.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Gaius Aemilius Martialis.

EDCS-13003454.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Aemilius Musicus vixit annos LXXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-13003454.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Aemilius Musicus lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003454.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Quintus Aemilius Musicus.

EDCS-13003455.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aem<il=T>ia Marsa vixit annos LX \n
EDCS-13003455.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilia Marsa lived 60 years.
EDCS-13003455.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Aemilia Marsa.

EDCS-13003456.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Annaenius Felicio vixit annos LXXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-13003456.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Annaenius Felicio lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003456.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Caius Annaenius Felicio.

EDCS-13003457.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Annius [name illegible].
EDCS-13003457.summary: A funerary epitaph, partially preserved, dedicated to Publius Annius, with a missing name and lifespan.

EDCS-13003458.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Antistia filia Quirina Catulla vixit annos LXV hic sita est\n
EDCS-13003458.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Antistia Quirina Catulla, daughter of [father's name illegible], lived 65 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13003458.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Antistia Quirina Catulla.

EDCS-13003459.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Antonia Cai filia Ceriola vixit annos XXXX hic sita est\n
EDCS-13003459.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Antonia Ceriola, daughter of Caius, lived 40 years; she is buried here.

EDCS-13200164.translation: To the departed spirits. Calolia Primula lived for 60 years; she rests here.

EDCS-13200168.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Publius Caninius Modestus, son of Rublius, lived for 11 years, 4 months, and 13 days; he rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.

EDCS-13200170.translation: To the memory of Caninia Octavena, a most rare woman. A grieving father placed this because of misfortune. She lived for 27 years and 3 months; she rests here.
EDCS-13200170.summary: A funerary inscription placed by a grieving father for his daughter Caninia Octavena, who died at 27 years and 3 months.

EDCS-13200171.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Lucius Cantinius Saterus lived for 80 years.

EDCS-13200172.translation: Publius Carbonius Crescens lived for 70 years; he rests here.

EDCS-13200173.translation: To the departed spirits. Carisius Conductor lived for 12 years. May his bones rest well.

EDCS-13200174.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Cassius Martialis, recording his name and the duration of his life.

EDCS-13200175.translation: For the memory of the Scantiana family. Pater Saturninus made this for Saturi, Cassia Queta his wife, and all his most innocent family. Do not wonder at this road; it has such dust.
EDCS-13200175.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Pater Saturninus for his family, the Scantiani, potentially indicating a road or public space with a dusty surface.

EDCS-13200176.translation: To the departed spirits. Castricata Hospita lived for 62 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13200176.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Castricata Hospita, recording her age and a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-13200177.translation: Cata lived for 60 years. She lies here.
EDCS-13200177.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name and age of Cata, indicating her burial place.

EDCS-13200178.translation: [---]ius Catulus lies here. He lived for 85 years.
EDCS-13200178.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name and age of Catulus, indicating his burial place.

EDCS-13200179.translation: To the departed spirits. Tiberius Claudius Candidus lived for 51 years.
EDCS-13200179.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Candidus, recording his name and age.

EDCS-13200180.translation: Gaius Claudius Caudinus lived for 35 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13200180.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name and age of Gaius Claudius Caudinus, indicating his burial place.

EDCS-13200181.translation: Gaius Claudius Honoratus lived for more or less 30 years.
EDCS-13200181.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name and approximate age of Gaius Claudius Honoratus.

EDCS-13200182.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Claudius Martialis, dutiful, lived for 2 years.
EDCS-13200182.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Claudius Martialis, recording his name and very young age.

EDCS-13200183.translation: To the departed spirits. Claudius Mudius Virilio lived for 65 years.
EDCS-13200183.summary: This funerary epitaph records the names Claudius Mudius and Virilio, along with the age of 65, likely for Claudius Mudius.

EDCS-13200185.translation: Clodia Selene, daughter of Marcus, of the Aemilia tribe (?), lived 23 years; she lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13200185.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clodia Selene, recording her name, parentage, tribal affiliation, lifespan, and a prayer for her peaceful rest, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200187.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Clodius Hermetio lived 51 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13200187.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clodius Hermetio, recording his name, lifespan, and burial place, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200188.translation: Clodius Fidus (?) lived 25 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13200188.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clodius Fidus (?), recording his name, lifespan, and burial place, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200189.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Clodius Ingennus lived 3 years and 5 months; he lies here.
EDCS-13200189.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Clodius Ingennus, recording his name, lifespan in years and months, and burial place, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200190.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Clodius Paltanus lived 30 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13200190.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Clodius Paltanus, recording his name, lifespan, and burial place, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200191.translation: Gaius Clodius Primus lived 70 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13200191.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Clodius Primus, recording his name, lifespan, and burial place, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200192.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Clodius Satur, dearest son, who lived 8 years.
EDCS-13200192.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Clodius Satur, a young son, by his parents, noting his name, relationship, and lifespan, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200193.translation: Clodia Novella, daughter of Lucius, lived 25 years; she lies here.
EDCS-13200193.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clodia Novella, recording her name, parentage, lifespan, and burial place, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200194.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Clodia Satura lived 35 years; she lies here.
EDCS-13200194.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clodia Satura, recording her name, lifespan, and burial place, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200196.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [Woman's Name] Hospita lived 70 years.
EDCS-13200196.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Hospita, recording her lifespan and dedicating it to the departed spirits, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200197.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Coelius Betius the elder lived 20 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13200197.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Coelius Betius the elder at the age of 20.

EDCS-13200198.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Coelius Cas[---] lived 31 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13200198.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Coelius Cas[---] at the age of 31.

EDCS-13200199.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Coelius Aper lived 60 years.
EDCS-13200199.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Coelius Aper at the age of 60.

EDCS-13200200.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Coelius Colonus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 20 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13200200.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Coelius Colonus, son of Quintus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-13200201.translation: Sextus Coelius Secundus lived 9 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13200201.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Sextus Coelius Secundus at the age of 9.

EDCS-13200202.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cominia Getula lived 60 years. She is buried here. [---]
EDCS-13200202.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Cominia Getula at the age of 60.

EDCS-13200203.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Considius Marcianus Hermetis lived 9 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13200203.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Caius Considius Marcianus Hermetis at the age of 9.

EDCS-13200205.translation: Cornelius Asclepiades lived 70 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13200205.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Cornelius Asclepiades at the age of 70.

EDCS-13200206.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cornelius Concessus lived 91 years.
EDCS-13200206.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Cornelius Concessus at the age of 91.

EDCS-13200207.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Cornelius Diadumenus lived 90 years.

EDCS-13200208.translation: To the departed spirits. Cornelius Matelio lived 45 years.
EDCS-13200208.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelius Matelio, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-13200209.translation: Lucius Cornelius Nampulus lived [1]6[3] years; he rests here. Lucius Cornelius Saturninus lived [L][3] years; he rests here.
EDCS-13200209.summary: This is a double funerary epitaph recording the lives and burials of Lucius Cornelius Nampulus and Lucius Cornelius Saturninus.

EDCS-13200210.translation: To the departed spirits. Cornelius Optatus lived 41 years.
EDCS-13200210.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelius Optatus, who lived 41 years.

EDCS-13200211.translation: To the departed spirits. Cornelius Primitivus lived 33 years; he rests here.
EDCS-13200211.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelius Primitivus, who lived 33 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13200212.translation: Cornelius Rufus lived 95 years; he rests here.
EDCS-13200212.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelius Rufus, who lived 95 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13200213.translation: To the departed spirits. Sextus Cornelius Satur[...]. [Cornelia Maxim[...]
EDCS-13200213.summary: This is a damaged funerary inscription that likely records two individuals, Sextus Cornelius Satur[...], and Cornelia Maxim[...].

EDCS-13200214.translation: To the departed spirits. Cornelia Callityche lived 33 years; she rests here. Publius? P? C? her husband?
EDCS-13200214.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Callityche, who lived 33 years, with a possible mention of her husband.

EDCS-13200215.translation: To the departed spirits. Cornelia Marcia lived 40 years.
EDCS-13200215.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Marcia, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-13200219.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Cuculnius Fortunatus lived 80 years; he rests here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13200219.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Cuculnius Fortunatus, who lived 80 years and includes a wish for his repose.

EDCS-13200220.translation: To the gods, possibly named Nonia, daughter of Lucius, Herma lived for 7 years.
EDCS-13200220.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Herma, daughter of Lucius, who died at the age of 7, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200221.translation: To the gods Manes. Cupius Dexter lived for 85 years. To the gods Manes. Iulia Monula lived for 52 years.
EDCS-13200221.summary: This is a double funerary inscription commemorating Cupius Dexter (aged 85) and Iulia Monula (aged 52), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200222.translation: Sacred to the gods Manes. Curculio lived for 20 years.
EDCS-13200222.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Curculio, who died at the age of 20, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200223.translation: To the gods Manes. Romana lived for 61 years.
EDCS-13200223.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Romana, who died at the age of 61, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200224.translation: To the gods Manes. Decumia lived for 85 years. May all things favor [her].
EDCS-13200224.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Decumia, who died at the age of 85, likely from the Roman Imperial period, with a wish for her afterlife.

EDCS-13200225.translation: To the gods Manes. Lucretia Hospitilla lived for 46 years. May your bones rest well. To the gods Manes. Iulia Maura, a very rare young girl, lived for 31 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13200225.summary: This is a double funerary inscription for Lucretia Hospitilla (aged 46) and Iulia Maura (aged 31), with a wish for their rest, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200227.translation: Quintus Domitius Rogatus lived for 80 years. He rests here.

EDCS-13200228.translation: To the gods Manes. Quintus Domitius Saturninus, the sweetest and incomparable son, lived for 23 years. May your bones rest well here.
EDCS-13200228.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Domitius Saturninus, described as a sweet and incomparable son, who died at the age of 23, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200229.translation: To the memory of Domitia Marcellina, a very rare daughter, who lived for 1 year, 5 months, and 8 days. Domitius Aprilis, the father, placed this.
EDCS-13200229.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Domitius Aprilis for his daughter Domitia Marcellina, who died at the age of 1 year, 5 months, and 8 days, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200230.translation: To the gods Manes. Domitius Marcellinus, the father, made this for his daughter Domitia Mussa, who lived for one year and one month.
EDCS-13200230.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Domitius Marcellinus for his daughter Domitia Mussa, who died at the age of one year and one month, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200232.translation: Publius Dupidius Agathopus lived 101 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13200232.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age and burial of Publius Dupidius Agathopus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200233.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Dupidius Saxa lived 111 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13200233.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the 'Dis Manes' and recording the age and burial of Publius Dupidius Saxa, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200234.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of Dupidia Fausta, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200236.translation: Quintus Egrilius Clemens, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 35 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13200237.translation: Gaius Egrilius Qursor lived 88 years and 10 and a half months.

EDCS-13200238.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Egrilius Hilario lived 45 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13200238.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the 'Dis Manes' and recording the age and burial of Egrilius Hilario, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200239.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Epictesis lived 30 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13200239.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the 'Dis Manes' and recording the age and burial of Epictesis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200240.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Euphemus lived 90 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13200240.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the 'Dis Manes' and recording the age and burial of Euphemus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200242.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Fabius Cronta lived 15 years.
EDCS-13200242.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the 'Dis Manes' and recording the age of Quintus Fabius Cronta, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13200243.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Fabius Victor lived 19 years.
EDCS-13200243.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Lucius Fabius Victor, who died at age 19.

EDCS-13200244.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fabia Is/sula lived 25 years.
EDCS-13200244.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Fabia Is/sula, who died at age 25.

EDCS-13200245.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fabricia Aprilia lived 59 years; she rests here.
EDCS-13200245.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Fabricia Aprilia, who died at age 59.

EDCS-13200246.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Faosa(?) lived 35 years; she rests here.
EDCS-13200246.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Faosa(?), who died at age 35.

EDCS-13200247.translation: Lucius Farsuleius Faustus lived 14 years; he rests here.
EDCS-13200247.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Lucius Farsuleius Faustus, who died at age 14.

EDCS-13200248.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Felicia, who died at age 30.

EDCS-13200249.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Festulus lived 20 years; he rests here.
EDCS-13200249.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Festulus, who died at age 20.

EDCS-13200251.translation: Lucius Flavius <E=F>u<t=I>icus lived 80 years; he rests here.
EDCS-13200251.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Lucius Flavius <E=F>u<t=I>icus, who died at age 80.

EDCS-13200252.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Vitulus lived 25 years; he rests here.
EDCS-13200252.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Titus Flavius Vitulus, who died at age 25.

EDCS-13200253.translation: [To the spirits of the departed,] sacred. Flavia, daughter of Lucius, Rogata, lived 77 years; she rests here.
EDCS-13200253.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Rogata, daughter of Lucius, who lived 77 years.

EDCS-13200256.translation: To the departed spirits. Fonteia Vernalis lived 71 and a half years; she lies here.
EDCS-13200256.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Fonteia Vernalis, who lived 71.5 years.

EDCS-13200257.translation: Fortunata lived 40 years; she lies here.
EDCS-13200257.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Fortunata, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-13200258.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Fullonius Iucundianus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, a good man, lived 46 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13200258.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Fullonius Iucundianus, son of Quintus, who lived 46 years and is described as a good man.

EDCS-13200261.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Furfanius Forticulus, who lived 7 years.

EDCS-13200262.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Gabidius Gaiolus lived 60 years.
EDCS-13200262.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Marcus Gabidius Gaiolus, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-13200263.translation: Gabinia Luculla lived 101 years; her bones may rest well here.
EDCS-13200263.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gabinia Luculla, who lived 101 years, with a wish for her bones to rest well.

EDCS-13200264.translation: Caius Gargilius Caninianus, son of Caius, lived 15 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13200264.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Caius Gargilius Caninianus, son of Caius, who lived 15 years.

EDCS-13200265.translation: Lucius Gargilius Proculus lived 50 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13200265.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Lucius Gargilius Proculus, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-13200266.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Gaudianus Victor, son of Lucius, lived 67 years; may his bones rest well here.
EDCS-13200266.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Gaudianus Victor, son of Lucius, who lived 67 years, with a wish for his bones to rest well.

EDCS-13200267.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus G avius Publicianus lived 61 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13200267.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Marcus G avius Publicianus, who lived 61 years.

EDCS-13200268.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Gellius Potitus lived 20 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13200268.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Gellius Potitus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-13200269.translation: To the departed spirits. Gellia Daphne lived 101 years.
EDCS-13200269.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gellia Daphne, who died at the age of 101.

EDCS-13200272.translation: To the departed spirits. Geminius Ianuarius, son of Heliodorus, lived 30 years. To the departed spirits. Heliodorus lived 105 years; may your bones rest well here.
EDCS-13200272.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of two individuals, Geminius Ianuarius and Heliodorus, likely father and son, with ages of 30 and 105 respectively.

EDCS-13200273.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Geminius Secundus lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13200273.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Geminius Secundus, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-13200274.translation: To the departed spirits. Gemini VE[...]
EDCS-13200274.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph for someone named Gemini, with the rest of the text lost.

EDCS-13200275.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Geminia Candida lived 25 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13200275.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Geminia Candida, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-13200276.translation: Geminia Fortunata(?) lived 21 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13200276.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Geminia Fortunata, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-13200277.translation: To the departed spirits. Geminia Ingenua, a unique wife, most sweet preserver, mother of all, parent of all men, helping all, innocent, most chaste, outstanding, most rare, lived 81 years. She made no one sad; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13200277.summary: This is an elaborate funerary epitaph for Geminia Ingenua, who died at 81, celebrating her virtues and her impact on others.

EDCS-13200278.translation: Geminia Matrona, daughter of Aulus, lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13200278.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Geminia Matrona, daughter of Aulus, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-13200279.translation: Geminia Pota lived 40 years; may your bones rest well here.
EDCS-13200279.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Geminia Pota, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-13200280.translation: To the divine shades. Geminia Prima lived 105 years with her children. Smasala lived 20 years. Fidelis lived [3] years. Paulina lived 12 years. Martialis [lived] 8 years.
EDCS-13200280.summary: A funerary epitaph for Geminia Prima and other family members, recording their ages at death, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13200281.translation: To the divine shades. Geminia Procula Secundus lived 101 years. She left behind grandchildren: 3 from a son, 4 from a daughter. She is buried here.
EDCS-13200281.summary: A funerary epitaph for Geminia Procula Secundus, detailing her age and the number of surviving grandchildren, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13302429.text_edition: \n Urubius in pace vixit annis XXII qua die obit pridie Kalendas Ianuarias\n
EDCS-13302429.translation: Urubius lived in peace for 22 years. He died on the day before the Kalends of January.
EDCS-13302429.summary: An epitaph recording the lifespan and death date of Urubius, likely from the late Roman or early Christian period.

EDCS-13302431.text_edition: \n Iulia Fortunata famula Dei vixit in pace annis LXXXII die reditionis VIII Kalendas Februarias\n
EDCS-13302431.translation: Iulia Fortunata, servant of God, lived in peace for 82 years. She departed this life on the 8th day before the Kalends of February.
EDCS-13302431.summary: A Christian epitaph for Iulia Fortunata, recording her age and date of death, likely from the late Roman or early Christian period.

EDCS-13302570.translation: To the divine shades. Cannius Severus lived 90 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13302570.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cannius Severus, recording his age and burial place, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13302573.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Iulius Luci filius Quirina Iustus vixit annos LXXXI hic situs est\n
EDCS-13302573.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Marcus Iulius Iustus, son of Lucius, of the tribus Quirina, lived 81 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13302573.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Iulius Iustus, recording his age and burial place, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13600417.text_edition: \n Marco Valerio Maximiano Marci Valeri Maximiani quinquennalis sacerdotalis filio pontifici coloniae Poetovionensium equo publico praefecto cohortis I Thracum tribuno cohortis I Hamiorum civium Romanorum praeposito orae gentium Ponti Polemoniani donis donato bello Phartico sic allecto ab Imperatore Marco Antonino Augusto et misso in procinctu Germanicae expeditionis ad deducenda per Danuvium quae in annonam nnniae utriusque exercituum denavigarent praeposito vexillationum classium praetoriarum Misenatis item Ravennatis item classis Brittanicae item equitum Afrorum et Maurorum electorum ad curam explorationis Pannoniae praefecto alae I Aravacorum in procinctu Germanico ab Imperatore Antonino Augusto coram laudato et equo et phaleris et armis donato quod manu sua ducem Naristarum Valaonem interemisset et in eadem ala quartae militiae honorem adepto praefecto alae contariorum donis donato bello Germanico Sarmatico praeposito equitibus gentium nnum Naristarum Quadorum ad vindictam Orientalis motus pergentium honore centenariae dignitatis aucto salario adeptus procurationem Moesiae inferioris eodem in tempore praeposito vexillationibus et at sic detrahendam Briseorum latronum manum in confinio Macedoniae et Thraciae ab imperatore misso procuratori Moesiae superioris procuratori provinciae Daciae Porolissensis a sacratissimis impperatoribus in amplissimum ordinem inter praetorios allecto et mox legato legionis I adiutricis item legato legionis II adiutricis praeposito vexillationum Leugaricione hiemantium item legato legionis V Macedonicae item legato legionis I Italicae item legato legionis XIII geminae item legato Augusti pro praetore [legionis III Augustae] donis donato a nobilissimo [principe Marco Aurelio Commodo Augusto] expeditione secunda Germanica splendidissimus ordo Dianensium veteranorum aere conlato\n
EDCS-13600417.translation: To Marcus Valerius Maximianus, son of Marcus Valerius Maximianus, quinquennial priest and pontiff of the colony of Poetovio, who held the public horse, prefect of the I Cohort of Thracians, tribune of the I Cohort of Hamians of Roman citizens, overseer of the peoples of the Pontic region, honored with gifts in the Parthian war, thus selected by the Emperor Marcus
