EDCS-04000307.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Titedius Secundus, including a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-04000308.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Titurnius Ianuarius, dutiful, lived 41 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-04000308.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Titurnius Ianuarius, stating his lifespan and a wish for his rest.

EDCS-04000311.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ianuarius, son of Romanius, dutiful, lived 40 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-04000311.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ianuarius, son of Romanius, recording his lifespan and a wish for his rest.

EDCS-04001480.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Valeria Ofellia lived 100 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-04001480.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Ofellia, noting her exceptional lifespan and burial.

EDCS-04001482.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iunia Fortunata lived 39 years.
EDCS-04001482.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iunia Fortunata, stating her age at death.

EDCS-04001483.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. CZ O lived 80 years. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Donata lived 60 years.
EDCS-04001483.summary: This inscription records the deaths and lifespans of two individuals, CZ O and Donata.

EDCS-04001484.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aelius Eutices lived 76 years.
EDCS-04001484.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelius Eutices, stating his lifespan.

EDCS-04001485.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Megsen lived 32 years.
EDCS-04001485.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Megsen, stating his age at death.

EDCS-04001486.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Zabullus Cerialis lived 95 years.
EDCS-04001486.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Zabullus Cerialis, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-04001487.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 105 years.
EDCS-04001487.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus, specifying his tribe and lifespan.

EDCS-04001488.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ius Donatus lived 85 years.
EDCS-04001488.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ius Donatus, stating his lifespan.

EDCS-04001489.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Monument for Faust Maior, 125 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Monument for Lucius S. Felicio, 85 years.
EDCS-04001489.summary: A funerary monument inscription recording the lifespans of Faust Maior and Lucius S. Felicio.

EDCS-04001490.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Monument for Flavius Cerialis, 105 years.
EDCS-04001490.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavius Cerialis, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-04001495.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cotinus lived 80 years. His sons erected this in memory.
EDCS-04001495.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cotinus, erected by his sons and recording his lifespan.

EDCS-04001503.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Monument for Quartina, daughter of Lucius. She lived 80 years. Victorinus made this from his funds.
EDCS-04001503.summary: A funerary monument inscription for Quartina, made by Victorinus, specifying her lifespan.

EDCS-04001504.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Monument for Iulia Fortunata, 57 years.
EDCS-04001504.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Fortunata, stating her lifespan.

EDCS-04001505.summary: A dedication to the fortunate Felici, possibly a funerary or honorific inscription.

EDCS-04001506.translation: By the authority of the Emperor Nerva Trajan Caesar Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, Lucius Minicius Natalis, imperial legate with praetorian power, between the Musulamii and the Tisibenenses, 2 miles, 472 paces.
EDCS-04001506.summary: An inscription detailing a boundary demarcation or road construction authorized by Emperor Trajan and executed by legate Lucius Minicius Natalis, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-04001509.translation: To his mother and wife, Donatus made this.
EDCS-04001509.summary: A funerary inscription by Donatus dedicating a monument to his mother and wife.

EDCS-04001513.translation: By the authority of the Emperor Nerva Trajan Caesar Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, Lucius Minicius Natalis, imperial legate with praetorian power, between the Augusti and the Musulamii, 31 miles, 6800 paces.
EDCS-04001513.summary: This inscription records a boundary marker established by the legate Lucius Minicius Natalis under the authority of Emperor Trajan, dating to his reign (98-117 AD).

EDCS-04001514.translation: By the authority of the Emperor Nerva Trajan Caesar Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, consul for the 6th time, imperator for the 13th time, Lucius Acilius Strabo Clodius Nummus, imperial legate with praetorian power, between the Augusti and the Musulamii.
EDCS-04001514.summary: This inscription records the involvement of the legate Lucius Acilius Strabo Clodius Nummus under the authority of Emperor Trajan, likely related to a boundary or administrative marker, dating to Trajan's reign (98-117 AD).

EDCS-04001651.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription identifying Marcus Annius Hermes.

EDCS-04001655.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. LucustinDIus lived 55 years. His daughters made this.
EDCS-04001655.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of LucustinDIus, who lived 55 years, and was set up by his daughters.

EDCS-04001656.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Rogatina lived 30 years, more or less. Sacred to the departed spirits. Agrinius Faustianus lived 40 years, more or less. Agrinia Faustina, Agrinia Donatula, and Faustinianus made this for their most rare mother and father. She is buried here, he is buried here.
EDCS-04001656.summary: This funerary inscription records the lives of Iulia Rogatina (approx. 30 years) and Agrinius Faustianus (approx. 40 years), and was erected by their children, Agrinia Faustina, Agrinia Donatula, and Faustinianus.

EDCS-04001657.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia Rufina lived 61 years. Ulpius Maximus made the monument.
EDCS-04001657.summary: This funerary inscription records that Flavia Rufina lived 61 years and that Ulpius Maximus erected the monument for her.

EDCS-04001658.translation: It was worked on the Kalends of July. The day of the martyr January. Monnica made it from the workshop of Hadrian.
EDCS-04001658.summary: This inscription records the completion of a work, possibly a tomb or artifact, on July 1st by Monnica from Hadrian's workshop, potentially commemorating a martyr named January.

EDCS-04001660.translation: Daughter of Imilcho, lived A years.
EDCS-04001660.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the age of the daughter of Imilcho.

EDCS-04001661.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lived 20 years. Marcus Valerius marus made this at his own expense. He is buried here.
EDCS-04001661.summary: This funerary inscription records that Marcus Valerius marus lived 20 years and erected his own tomb.

EDCS-04001665.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Iulius Paulus lived 17 years. His grandmother made this for her most dutiful grandsons.
EDCS-04001665.summary: This funerary inscription records that Lucius Iulius Paulus lived 17 years and was honored by his grandmother.

EDCS-04001666.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Servilius Concessus lived for 15 years. His grandmother made this for her most dutiful grandchildren.
EDCS-04001666.summary: A tombstone commemorating Servilius Concessus, made by his grandmother for her grandchildren.

EDCS-04001667.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Emilius Datianus Lampius lived for 25 years and 7 months.
EDCS-04001667.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Emilius Datianus Lampius, likely a soldier given the abbreviation AVC.

EDCS-04001668.translation: Lucius Crepereius Crescens, dutiful, lived for forty-seven years.
EDCS-04001668.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Crepereius Crescens.

EDCS-04001669.translation: Crepereius Iunius, dutiful, lived for fifty and seven years.

EDCS-04001670.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulius Felix lived for 60 years. His sons made this for their dutiful father.
EDCS-04001670.summary: A tombstone for Iulius Felix, erected by his sons.

EDCS-04001671.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Publius Ridius Septiminus lived for 85 years. His heirs made this.
EDCS-04001671.summary: A tombstone for Publius Ridius Septiminus, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-04001673.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Orbius Orbianus lived for 55 years. Aelia Martilla placed this for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-04001673.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Orbius Orbianus, erected by his wife.

EDCS-04001674.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Macrinus Cassius lived for 80 years. He is buried here, he is buried here.
EDCS-04001674.summary: A tombstone for Macrinus Cassius, indicating his burial place.

EDCS-04001675.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Manilius Suriacus lived for 65 years. His sons Tertius and Gallus lovingly took care of this for their dutiful father. He is buried here.
EDCS-04001675.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Manilius Suriacus, erected by his sons.

EDCS-04001676.translation: Ulpia Getula lived for 65 years. Faustianus, her husband, placed this.
EDCS-04001676.summary: A tombstone for Ulpia Getula, erected by her husband.

EDCS-04001677.translation: Iulius Felix to Domitilia Ifigenia, his wife.
EDCS-04001677.summary: This is a funerary inscription from an unknown date, dedicated by a husband to his wife.

EDCS-04001678.translation: Valeria made this for her daughter, Anno, who lived for three years.
EDCS-04001678.summary: This is a funerary inscription from an unknown date, recording the death of a child and made by her mother.

EDCS-04001679.translation: Anchilnus with his family, the table.
EDCS-04001679.summary: This is a funerary inscription from an unknown date, possibly recording a funerary banquet or a monument with a table.

EDCS-04001680.translation: Siddin in peace lived for [X] years and [X] months.
EDCS-04001680.summary: This is a funerary inscription from an unknown date, recording the lifespan of an individual named Siddin.

EDCS-04001681.translation: To Felici, the holy one, a happy life in God.
EDCS-04001681.summary: This is a funerary inscription from an unknown date, honoring a Christian individual named Felici.

EDCS-04001689.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Euplia, who is also Eusna, lived for 21 years and 4 months.
EDCS-04001689.summary: This is a funerary inscription from an unknown date, recording the death of Euplia (also known as Eusna) at the age of 21 years and 4 months.

EDCS-04001690.summary: This is a funerary inscription from an unknown date, possibly a Christian epitaph mentioning the Cross and an individual named Serbus.

EDCS-04001693.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Augentita. Her husband Donatus made this monument at his own expense. Lived for 40 years.
EDCS-04001693.summary: This is a funerary inscription from an unknown date, dedicated by a husband to his wife Iulia Augentita, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-04001702.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Tullius Lucianus lived for 71 years. Iulia Optatula made this for her most beloved husband and Tullius Viator for his dearest father.
EDCS-04001702.summary: This is a funerary inscription from an unknown date, recording the death of Caius Tullius Lucianus at 71 years old, and was made by his wife and son.

EDCS-04001704.translation: Alypus, freedman of Decimus and son of Decimus, made this. Lived for 75 years.
EDCS-04001704.summary: This is a funerary inscription from an unknown date, recording the death of Alypus, a freedman and son of Decimus, who lived for 75 years.

EDCS-04001706.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Nonia Iulia lived 110 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-04001706.summary: A funerary epitaph for Nonia Iulia, who lived 110 years, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-04001707.translation: Aelius Liberalis lived 90 years. Veneria made this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-04001707.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aelius Liberalis, who lived 90 years, dedicated by his wife Veneria, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-04001712.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Cornelius Saturninus lived 50 years. Scoforoi, his wife, and her heirs placed this.
EDCS-04001712.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Cornelius Saturninus, who lived 50 years, dedicated by his wife and heirs, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-04001714.summary: A funerary epitaph for C. IA, who lived 120 years, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-04001717.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Donatulus lived approximately 45 years.
EDCS-04001717.summary: A funerary epitaph for Donatulus, who lived approximately 45 years, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-04001718.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Iulius Datulus lived 65 years. Iulius Paulinus, his son, made this for his well-deserving father. He is buried here.
EDCS-04001718.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Iulius Datulus, who lived 65 years, dedicated by his son Iulius Paulinus, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-04001719.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iunius Emeritus lived 41 years. Iunius Primus made this.
EDCS-04001719.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iunius Emeritus, who lived 41 years, dedicated by Iunius Primus, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-04001741.summary: A Christian funerary epitaph with a hopeful message, dated to the Late Antique period.

EDCS-04001744.translation: The Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of the divine Nerva, high priest, with tribunician power for the 13th time.
EDCS-04001744.summary: An inscription recording the titles and powers of Emperor Trajan, dated to 111 AD.

EDCS-04001775.translation: The Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus Germanicus, son of the divine Nerva, high priest, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, with tribunician power for the 3rd time.
EDCS-04001775.summary: An inscription recording the titles and powers of Emperor Trajan, dated to 101 AD.

EDCS-04001785.translation: To our Emperor, our lord, Constantine the Great, Victor, ever Augustus, born for the good of the state, (this is) XX.
EDCS-04001785.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Constantine the Great, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-04001786.translation: To our lord, Magnus Decentius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-04001786.summary: This is a dedication to Caesar Magnus Decentius, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-04001788.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maxentius, Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, XXI.
EDCS-04001788.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Maxentius, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-04901063.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sextus Atisius Pomponianus lived for [many] years.
EDCS-04901063.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Atisius Pomponianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04901065.translation: The estates of Sextus Julius P[---]
EDCS-04901065.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely related to property or ownership belonging to Sextus Julius P[---].

EDCS-04901066.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Julia Victoria, who was also called Monnica, 55 years old; her children dedicated this.
EDCS-04901066.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by children for their mother, Julia Victoria (Monnica), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04901068.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sextus Tutius Cornelianus lived 27 years. Caius Tutius [made this].
EDCS-04901068.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Tutius Cornelianus, set up by Caius Tutius.

EDCS-04901069.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tutius Saturninus lived 75 years. Varia Matrona made and dedicated this for her husband while she was alive.
EDCS-04901069.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Varia Matrona for her husband, Tutius Saturninus.

EDCS-04901071.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Idia Amata lived 30 years. Victorinus and Citinus made this.
EDCS-04901071.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Idia Amata, made by Victorinus and Citinus.

EDCS-04901073.translation: Liberalis lived 43 years. Iulia Victoria, a dutiful wife, made this for her husband.
EDCS-04901073.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Liberalis, made by his wife Iulia Victoria.

EDCS-04901074.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Valerius Maximus lived 70 years. Caecilia Donata [made this] for her husband from her own funds.
EDCS-04901074.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Maximus, funded by his wife Caecilia Donata.

EDCS-04901075.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Domitius Donatus lived 73 years.
EDCS-04901075.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Domitius Donatus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-04901078.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Geddes Ierhei, from the contingent of Palmyrenes, lived 58 years. His heirs had this made in accordance with his will.
EDCS-04901078.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Geddes Ierhei, a member of the Palmyrene contingent, whose heirs executed his testamentary wishes.

EDCS-04901080.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Pastor, decurion of the First Wing of Pannonians, lived [X] years.
EDCS-04901080.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Iulius Pastor, a decurion in the auxiliary cavalry, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-04901084.translation: To Flavius Aelius Minucius Victorinianus, son of the most excellent Flavius Aelius Victorinus, governor of the province of Numidia. Aelius Anniolus, attendant of the office, and Ulpius Rotulius set this up.
EDCS-04901084.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by officials for the son of a former governor of Numidia.

EDCS-04901087.translation: Dasius, son of Liccaio, of the Breuci tribe, an equestrian soldier holding the rank of sesquiplicarius.
EDCS-04901087.summary: This is an epitaph for Dasius, a Breucian cavalry soldier of equestrian rank.

EDCS-04901095.translation: The council and people of the colony of Cuiculitanus to Flavia Aeli Victorini, a most accomplished man, governor of the province of Numidia.
EDCS-04901095.summary: This is a public dedication by the city council and people of Cuiculitanus to their governor, Flavia Aeli Victorini.

EDCS-05000826.translation: Pinaria A bullina lived for [some number of] years.
EDCS-05000826.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pinaria A bullina, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-05000827.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sentia Gaetula lived for 14 years. Sentius Gaetulicus, her father, made and dedicated this.
EDCS-05000827.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Sentius Gaetulicus dedicating a monument to his daughter, Sentia Gaetula, who died at the age of 14.

EDCS-05000828.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sentius Getulicus lived for 50 years. Sentius Gusabius, his son, made and dedicated this.
EDCS-05000828.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Sentius Gusabius dedicating a monument to his father, Sentius Getulicus, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-05000829.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Naseus Iamakaris made this monument for his deserving brother, Iamakara, who served for 6 years and lived for 16 years.
EDCS-05000829.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Naseus Iamakaris dedicating a monument to his brother, Iamakara, who died at 16 after 6 years of military service.

EDCS-05000830.translation: Verosaca lived for 85 years, a well-deserving woman. Her sons, at their own expense, made and dedicated this to their mother.
EDCS-05000830.summary: This is a funerary inscription by the sons of Verosaca, dedicating a monument to her after her death at 85.

EDCS-05000831.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely related to family members, possibly a dedication or funerary notice.

EDCS-05000834.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, to Flavia Redducta, [my] wife.
EDCS-05000834.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Flavia Redducta, likely by her spouse.

EDCS-06100569.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Caelius Saturus lived for 51 years.
EDCS-06100569.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Caelius Saturus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-06100570.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Flavius Rufus, priest of Ceres, lived for 95 years. While alive, he never [broke or used?] a small valve.
EDCS-06100570.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Rufus, a priest of Ceres, who died at 95 and was known for his integrity.

EDCS-06100573.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Gargilius Pacatus lived for 30 years. He is buried here. May you rest well here.
EDCS-06100573.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Gargilius Pacatus, recording his age at death and burial.

EDCS-06100574.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Mustia lived for 65 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-06100574.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Mustia, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-06100575.translation: Iulia Rogata lived for 21 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-06100575.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Rogata, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-06100576.translation: Modia Severilla, daughter of Marcius, lived for 35 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-06100576.summary: A funerary epitaph for Modia Severilla, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-06100578.translation: Afinia Afrikana, daughter of Marcius, is buried here. She lived for 55 years.
EDCS-06100578.summary: A funerary epitaph for Afinia Afrikana, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-06100579.translation: Cornelia Extricata, daughter of Lucius, lived for 48 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-06100579.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelia Extricata, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-06100580.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Iulius Urbanus lived for 55 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-06100580.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Iulius Urbanus, recording his age at death and burial.

EDCS-06100581.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. I was Iulia Severa, born to Iulius Nepos, and I bore three children to Gaius Sittius. I lived for 35 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-06100581.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Severa, recording her age at death, her parentage, and her children.

EDCS-06100582.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sittia Honorata lived for 11 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-06100582.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sittia Honorata, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-06100583.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Extricata lived for 42 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-06100583.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Extricata, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-06100587.translation: Sacred to Baliddiri Augustus. Marcus Fabius Firmus and Lucius Maecius Extricatus fulfilled the vow they had promised and dedicated it.
EDCS-06100587.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment and dedication of a vow by Marcus Fabius Firmus and Lucius Maecius Extricatus to the divinity Baliddiri Augustus.

EDCS-06100589.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius D Venutus lived for 75 years.
EDCS-06100589.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the name and lifespan of Lucius D Venutus.

EDCS-06100590.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Flavius Cestor, Flavius Arinus.
EDCS-06100590.summary: This inscription, likely a tombstone, lists the names Flavius Cestor and Flavius Arinus, dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed.

EDCS-06100591.translation: Ianuaris lived for 30 years; he was buried here.
EDCS-06100591.summary: This tombstone records the name and lifespan of Ianuaris, stating he was buried in this location.

EDCS-06100593.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Caecilius Martialis, decurion of four colonies, lived for 90 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-06100593.summary: This inscription records the burial of Publius Caecilius Martialis, a decurion of four colonies, who lived for 90 years.

EDCS-06100594.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ae Dativa lived for 81 years. To the Spirits of the Departed. Sextus Clodius Saturinus lived for 95 years.
EDCS-06100594.summary: This inscription records the lifespans of two individuals, Ae Dativa (81 years) and Sextus Clodius Saturinus (95 years), dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed.

EDCS-06100596.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sallustius Senecio lived for 115 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-06100596.summary: This tombstone inscription records the name and exceptionally long lifespan of Sallustius Senecio, who lived for 115 years.

EDCS-06100597.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Saufeius Victor lived for 35 years.
EDCS-06100597.summary: This inscription records the name and lifespan of Lucius Saufeius Victor, dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed.

EDCS-06100598.translation: To Good Intelligence. Bocondes, Madris Ages, Pompeius I, Titus Flavius Felix the son, Caius Antonius Questor, Flavius Felicissimus, Rulius QVETS, Quintus Iulius Felix, Iulius, erected this.
EDCS-06100598.summary: This inscription records multiple individuals dedicating a monument to Bona Mens (Good Intelligence), likely a collegium or group.

EDCS-06100599.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Iulius Quintilianus of Aniensis, Monimus lived for 20 years.
EDCS-06100599.summary: This tombstone inscription records the names Quintus Iulius Quintilianus and Monimus, with the latter's lifespan of 20 years, dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed.

EDCS-06100600.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Grania Tertiola lived 59 years. May your bones rest well here where you are laid.
EDCS-06100600.summary: A funerary epitaph for Grania Tertiola, dating from antiquity.

EDCS-06100601.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Cornelia Sabina, [aged...]
EDCS-06100601.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelia Sabina, dating from antiquity.

EDCS-06100602.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Licinius Victor lived 60 years. Here he is laid, buried.
EDCS-06100602.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Licinius Victor, dating from antiquity.

EDCS-06100603.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Naevia Quintula, of most innocent life. Galerius Datosus set up this tomb for his most rare wife. She lived 35 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-06100603.summary: A funerary epitaph for Naevia Quintula, set up by her husband Galerius Datosus, dating from antiquity.

EDCS-06100604.translation: Gaius Prastina Messalinus dedicated this fishpond with his family to Aesculapius and Hygieia.
EDCS-06100604.summary: A dedication of a fishpond to the gods Aesculapius and Hygieia by Gaius Prastina Messalinus and his family, dating from antiquity.

EDCS-06100606.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Ulpius Longinus, tribune of the III Augusta legion, deserving of honor. Aurelia Flavia, his wife, with whom he lived for 13 years. He lived 50 years. She made this from 600 sesterces.
EDCS-06100606.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ulpius Longinus, a tribune of the III Augusta legion, set up by his wife Aurelia Flavia, dating from antiquity.

EDCS-06100607.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Quintus Lurius Rufinus, standard-bearer of the III Augusta legion, aged 25 years. Luria Aventina to her pious brother.
EDCS-06100607.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Lurius Rufinus, a standard-bearer of the III Augusta legion, set up by his sister Luria Aventina, dating from antiquity.

EDCS-06100608.translation: Gaius Iulius Silvanus, a veteran, made this for himself while alive and for his wife Postimia Donata.
EDCS-06100608.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Iulius Silvanus and his wife Postimia Donata, dating from antiquity.

EDCS-06100609.translation: Lucius Valerius D IIus Turo, Caius Iulius Valens F, Lucius Cetinius Cornicinus, Caius Attius Clemens from Sarmizegetusa M V V, Sextus Gavius Iulius Montanus M L, Laudicea, Publius Iulius Valens II, Caius Iulius Maximus from Antioch, Caius Octavius Amicus from Antioch, Caius Atellius Martianus from Apamea, retentus Aius Mastius from Chalcis, Caius Iulius Longinus A B, Caius Pompeius Cand from Cyrrhus, Caius Iulius Apollinaris from Cyrrhus, Amullius Celer from Claudiopolis, Lucius Iulius Nemaeus from Danaba, Marcus Passenius Or from Apamea, Lucius Clodius Rogatus from Volubilis, Lucius Antonius Valens from Antioch, Caius Caecilius Saturninus from Volubilis, Caius Domitius Valens from Tyre, Caius Cassius Terentianus from the camp, Caius Valerius Crispus from the camp, Lucius Gemellius Apollinaris from the camp, Terentius Saturninus from Antioch, Caius Iulius nacius from Chalcis, Marcus Antonius Valens from Antioch, Marcus Gavius Priscus from Hierapolis, Lucius Varius Nero from Hierapolis, Lucius Valerius Longinus from Doliche, Sextus Iulius Equitus from Cirta, Marcus Valerius nus from Volubilis, Caius Iulius Caius, Caius Eu nus, Publius Aurelius Quintus, Valerius Po, Marcus Iulius Latinus from Thamugadi, Cornelius Bassus from Co
EDCS-06100609.summary: A list of individuals, likely donors or subscribers, with their origins, associated with a project or foundation, dating from antiquity.

EDCS-06100610.translation: Gaius Verrius, Marcus Magius Maximus, Caius Cosconius Publicianus, Quintus Statius Avitus, Caius Maius Memor, Vibius Vitalis, Lucius Terentius Marcellus from Sufetula, Marcus Ulpius Modestus from the colony Carpitana, Cosconius C from Pompeiopolis, Lucius Lartius Albanus from the colony Munda, Quintus Marinius Postimus from Aeminium, Tiberius Claudius Severus from Claudiopolis, Marcus Sextilius Asiaticus from Marcianopolis, Lucius Iulius Secundus V, Secundus
EDCS-06100610.summary: A list of individuals, likely donors or subscribers, with their origins, associated with a project or foundation, dating from antiquity.

EDCS-06100611.translation: Titus Flavius Cornelius Caius Valerius Fortunatus Lucius Caelius Crescens Quintus Caecilius Fortunatus Caius Iulius Clemens Caius Iulius Rufus Sextus Pullaenius Lucius Manlius Quintus Iulius Caius Tamallius Caius Iulius Lucius Vitellius Titus Flavius Caius Caecilius Lucius Curius.
EDCS-06100611.summary: This inscription lists a series of Roman personal names, likely belonging to officials or members of a collegium.

EDCS-06100613.translation: Marcellus, quartermaster Vitalis from Hadrumetum, Saturninus, quartermaster, quartermaster, from Cirta, from Carthage, from Carthage, from Carthage, strator, from Ammaedara, from Ammaedara, R IEC.
EDCS-06100613.summary: This inscription appears to list individuals and their origins, possibly related to military or administrative roles, mentioning places like Hadrumetum, Cirta, and Carthage.

EDCS-06100614.translation: IVth Cohort, Tuburnica, squadron, from Cirta, from Carthage, from Cirta, cavalryman, from Cirta, from Theveste, quartermaster, quartermaster, from Cirta, scribe.
EDCS-06100614.summary: This inscription details military personnel and their origins, including a cavalryman and a scribe from various locations like Cirta and Carthage, likely from the IVth Cohort.

EDCS-06100616.translation: Second Cohort, quartermaster, from Vaga, from Theveste, from Cirta, optio.
EDCS-06100616.summary: This inscription records military personnel, specifically an optio from Cirta, with origins in Vaga and Theveste, possibly belonging to the Second Cohort.

EDCS-06100617.translation: Quintus Su Quintus Si Gavius Marcus Cr.
EDCS-06100617.summary: This inscription consists of fragmented personal names, making its purpose unclear.

EDCS-06100621.translation: Antonius Ampliatus Secundius Primus from Thuburbo Maius, Caius Caecilius Romanus from Tipasa, Hadrumetum, Marcus Iulius Caius Lusatius Tiro, Caius Attius Tabullianus, Quintus Glecius Fabricius, Titus Fulvius Paulinus, Caius Caecilius MIM, quartermaster.
EDCS-06100621.summary: This inscription lists numerous personal names, many with their places of origin such as Thuburbo Maius, Tipasa, and Hadrumetum, and includes the term 'castris' (quartermaster).

EDCS-06100622.translation: Sallustius, quartermaster, Victor, quartermaster, Donatus, quartermaster, Victor, from Cirta, Priscus, quartermaster, from Cirta, Mileu, Mileu, Cohors Q, from Cirta, scribe, Caius Antonius Ampliatus from Theveste, Marcus Omullius Lucanus from Bisica, Lucius Antonius Restutus from Carthage, Caius Arrius Rogatus, Caius Badius Saturninus from Theveste, Caius Iulius Maximus, quartermaster, Caius Iulius Namphamo from Carthage, Caius Secundius Primus from Thuburbo, Quintus Antonius Secundus, quartermaster, Caius Caecilus Romanus from Tipasa.
EDCS-06100622.summary: This inscription details numerous individuals, many associated with military quarters (castris) and originating from places like Cirta, Theveste, Carthage, and Thuburbo, likely related to military or administrative duties.

EDCS-06100623.translation: Ammaedara, edius Fortunatus, quartermaster, Marcus Munatius Romanus from Thagora, optio, Aelius Ianuarius Bulla, DO T, Cohors X, Lucius Maenius Felix from Carthage, Publius Caecilius Tannonius from Carthage, Caius Antonius Valens, quartermaster.
EDCS-06100623.summary: This inscription lists individuals, including an optio and quartermasters, with origins in Ammaedara and Thagora, and references to Carthage and Cohors X.

EDCS-06100624.translation: To Quinto Anicio Fausto, consular official, Tegonius Saturninus, perpetual quinquennial priest of the colony, patron.
EDCS-06100624.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Quinto Anicio Fausto, a consular official and perpetual priest of the colony of Thamugadi, honoring him as patron.

EDCS-06100625.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country, emperor for the second time, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 13th time, consul for the 4th time, dedicated the paved street, Marcus Valerius Etruscus, imperial legate pro praetore, patron of the colony, by decree of the decurions, with public funds.
EDCS-06100625.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a paved street in the colony of Thamugadi by the legate Marcus Valerius Etruscus, during the reign of Emperor Antoninus Pius, funded by the local council.

EDCS-06100626.translation: To Publius Julius Iunianus Tironillanus, most distinguished boy, son of Publius Julius Iunianus Martianalis, consul, patron of the colony and municipality, by decree of the town council of the colony of Thamugadi, with public funds.
EDCS-06100626.summary: This is a public dedication from the colony of Thamugadi to a young boy, son of a former consul and patron of the colony.

EDCS-06100627.translation: From Karthago. Practicing standard bearers, assistant to the office of the cornicularii. Marsus from Karthago. Fissius V. Aelia Thapsus. Helvius Felix I. Sextilius Donatus, candidate. Caius Dentilius Rogatus, duplicarius, O sesquiplicarius, beneficiary. Utica.lius Fortunatus, practicing standard bearer. edius Fortunatus, practicing standard bearer. Armatura. From Karthago. Felix iu. Ulla, keeper of arms. Cirta.lius Maximianus. Sabratha. Practicing standard bearers. nius t s. Silicius Flaccus, practicing standard bearer. From camps. Severianus VI. Felix Ulpia Culci. Cincari M. Assuribus Li. Arnensis. From Karthago. C. Ulpio Hadrumeto. Madaurensis I. Aelia Avita. Co, beneficiary of a broad stripe. Thubursicu Numidarum. Cuiculitanus, scribe. MI F us, candidate. Urubi. Aelia Tuburbo Maius. Lucius Aelius Felix. Cirta. Ianuarius, duplicarius. From Karthago. Felix, duplicarius. From Karthago. s Primus.
EDCS-06100627.summary: This inscription lists numerous individuals, many with military titles and origins, suggesting it may be related to a military unit or an administrative body in Roman Africa, possibly with a dating in the 3rd century CE based on the titles.

EDCS-06100628.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aurelius Iustus Marianus lived 40 years. Pontia Flavia made this for her most deserving husband.
EDCS-06100628.summary: A funerary dedication made by a wife for her deceased husband, who lived to be 40 years old.

EDCS-06100630.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Julius Auctor made this for his deserving wife, Sergia Amata, whom he married and who lived with him for 16 years, and who bore him 7 sons and one daughter, through the insistence of Caius Julius Martialis, her elder brother.
EDCS-06100630.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his wife, detailing their marriage length, number of children, and mentioning her brother as the instigator of the monument.

EDCS-06100631.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sempronia Atilia lived 55 years and 4 months. Titus Mussius Saturninus, Titus Mussius Sabinus, Quintus Sempronius Saturninus, Publius Arranius Martialis, Publius Arranius Donatus, Sempronia daughter of Publius, Atilia. The sons made this for their dearest mother.
EDCS-06100631.summary: A funerary monument erected by sons for their mother, Sempronia Atilia, providing her age at death and listing the names of the sons who commissioned it.

EDCS-06100632.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. His heirs made this. He lived 50 years.
EDCS-06100632.summary: A brief funerary dedication by the heirs of the deceased, stating his age at death.

EDCS-07400754.translation: Quintus Modius Septimus, beneficiary of Lambaesis. Retained: Quintus Mallius Faustus, practicing standard bearer. Thimida Regia. Apellenius Primus. Lucius Cornelius Festus. Simitthu. Lucius Julius Tertius. Arnensis. Carthage. Publius Caecilius Felix. Thuburbo Minus. Caius Julius Ianuarius. Publius Pompeius Urbanus, practicing standard bearer. Thamugadi. Caecilus Saturninus. Caius Arissius Rogatus. Ulpia. Leptiminus. Sextus Petronius Aelianus, practicing standard bearer. Sabratha. Terentius Marinus. Quintus Attius Quadratus. Thaenae. Caius Julius Saturninus. From camps. Publius Eppius Arabicus. Arnensis. Carthage. Publius Valerius Gallonius. Cincari. Marcus Rasinius Postumus. From camps. Anneus Felix. Caius Tadius Felix, practicing standard bearer. Iulia Uthina. Caius Julius Saturninus, practicing standard bearer. Sufetula. Publius Stlaccius Heros. Mascula. Iulius Baricio Celsius. Victor. Polio Cornificius Liberalis. Iulius Felicianus, duplicarius. Thiseditanus. Mevius Maximus. Aggar. Tadius Saturus. Abitina. Fabricius Feli, practicing standard bearer. Utica. Grasidius Martialis. Iulius Victor, candidate. From camps. Ves. Calpurnius Flaccus. Thuburbo Maius. Iulius Dexter, beneficiary of a tribune. Madauros. Gargilius Hilarus. Armatura. From camps. Moschus. Lucius Gavius VC. Publius Aelius.
EDCS-07400754.summary: This extensive inscription appears to be a list of individuals, many with military roles and geographical origins in Roman North Africa, possibly commemorating service or participation in a specific event or unit.

EDCS-07400755.translation: lius Aemilianus, from military service, perpetual priest, and Sextus Sinicius Rufus, perpetual priest.
EDCS-07400755.summary: This inscription records two individuals,lius Aemilianus and Sextus Sinicius Rufus, who held the prestigious office of perpetual priest (flamen perpetuus).

EDCS-07400757.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Publius Aelius Maximus willingly dedicated this altar for his deserving deed, which he had promised in honor of Publius Aelius Felix, perpetual priest. He donated it.
EDCS-07400757.summary: A dedication of an altar to Mercury Augustus by Publius Aelius Maximus, made in honor of Publius Aelius Felix, a perpetual priest.

EDCS-07400758.translation: To Cornelia Valentina Tucciana, flamen perpetual (priestess), wife of Marcus Plotus Faustus, imperial service man and perpetual priest, for her merits in the fatherland and for her munificence, by decree of the town council of the colony of Thamugadi.
EDCS-07400758.summary: A public dedication by the town council of Thamugadi to Cornelia Valentina Tucciana, wife of a perpetual priest, honoring her merits and generosity.

EDCS-07400760.translation: Grasidius Felix, the basket-maker, happily fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07400760.summary: This is a dedication by Grasidius Felix, a basket-maker, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-07400761.translation: Publius Flavius Pudens Pomponianus, a most distinguished man, dedicated at his own expense to the divine numen of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, conqueror of Parthia, conqueror of Britain, conqueror of Germany, chief priest, in his 18th year of tribunician power, Imperator for the 3rd time, Consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, and to Julia Augusta Pia Felix, mother of the emperor, mother of the camp, mother of the senate, and mother of the fatherland, and to the goddess of the fatherland, for public worship and the honor of the worthy city.
EDCS-07400761.summary: This is a dedication by Publius Flavius Pudens Pomponianus to Emperor Marcus Aurelius and Julia Domna, likely erected during Marcus Aurelius' reign (161-180 AD).

EDCS-07400763.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Manius Acilius Novellus [erected this] for his dearest daughter, Acilia Concessa.
EDCS-07400763.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Acilia Concessa, set up by her father, Manius Acilius Novellus.

EDCS-07400764.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Manius Acilius Novellus [erected this] for his dearest daughter, Acilia Fortunata.
EDCS-07400764.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Acilia Fortunata, set up by her father, Manius Acilius Novellus.

EDCS-07400766.translation: Gaius Julius Victorinus, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, from military service, perpetual priest of the municipality of Thubunenses, [erected this] during his lifetime for himself, and for his dearest late wife, Aelia Emerita, daughter of Titus, and for his Iulii.
EDCS-07400766.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Gaius Julius Victorinus, a local priest and former soldier, for himself, his deceased wife, and his descendants.

EDCS-07400767.translation: The Castrensis priest, perpetual priest, and quinquennial duumvir, made and dedicated [this] with statues and ornaments.
EDCS-07400767.summary: This is a dedication by a local magistrate named Castrensis, who held multiple religious and administrative offices in his municipality.

EDCS-07600462.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Mirantius lived for 30 years. His tent-mates dedicated this.
EDCS-07600462.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Iulius Mirantius, set up by his military tent-mates.

EDCS-07600468.translation: The Republic of the Dianenses [erected this] to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, conqueror of Parthia, conqueror of Sarmatia, conqueror of Germany, chief priest, in his 30th year of tribunician power, Imperator for the 3rd time, Consul for the 3rd time, proconsul.
EDCS-07600468.summary: This is a dedication by the community of Dianenses to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, likely during his reign (161-180 AD).

EDCS-07800139.translation: To the most noble Caesar, prince of the [youth].
EDCS-07800139.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to a Caesar, possibly a young imperial heir.

EDCS-07800142.translation: [...] on the day of Crescentius, [and] also for the decurions of the order.
EDCS-07800142.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a specific day and the local town council (decurions).

EDCS-07800151.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Custos.

EDCS-07800152.translation: Verecundus, veteran of the Third Legion, of the ITI OSO NI tribe.
EDCS-07800152.summary: This inscription identifies a veteran of the Third Legion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07800153.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Antonius Donatulus lived 6 years and sailed to Rome twice.
EDCS-07800153.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonius Donatulus, who died at age six after having traveled to Rome twice, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07800154.translation: Sittia Urbana, priestess of the Caucaria, lived 95 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-07800154.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Urbana, a priestess, who died at age 95, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07800155.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelius Faustus, who died at age 31.

EDCS-07800156.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades of Aelia Marina, who lived 15 years.
EDCS-07800156.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Marina, who died at age 15.

EDCS-07800157.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aemilius Fuscinus lived 35 years.
EDCS-07800157.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilius Fuscinus, who died at age 35.

EDCS-07800158.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilius Pompeius Crescens.

EDCS-07800159.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sextus Aemilius, son of [---], of the Quirina tribe, lived [---] years.
EDCS-07800159.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Aemilius, son of [---], of the Quirina tribe.

EDCS-07800160.translation: Lucius Annius Ganibio lived 10 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-07800160.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Annius Ganibio, who died at age 10.

EDCS-07800161.translation: Gaius Arruntius Verus, son of Gaius, of the Voltinian tribe, lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800161.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Arruntius Verus, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-07800162.translation: Gaius Arruntius Verus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinian tribe, lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800162.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Arruntius Verus, who lived for 70 years.

EDCS-07800164.translation: Titus Bruttius Ampliatus lived 90 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800164.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Bruttius Ampliatus, who lived for 90 years.

EDCS-07800165.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius C. Tisa lived 47 years.
EDCS-07800165.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to the departed spirits for Gaius C. Tisa, who lived 47 years.

EDCS-07800166.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Caecilius Felicianus lived 21 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800166.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to the departed spirits for Gaius Caecilius Felicianus, who lived 21 years.

EDCS-07800167.translation: Caecilia Urbana lived 91 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-07800167.summary: This is a tombstone for Caecilia Urbana, who lived for 91 years.

EDCS-07800168.translation: Q. Caecilia lived 20 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-07800168.summary: This is a tombstone for Q. Caecilia, who lived for 20 years.

EDCS-07800169.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caesonia Ausesis lived 5 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-07800169.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to the departed spirits for Caesonia Ausesis, who lived 5 years.

EDCS-07800170.translation: Callityche, slave of Gallus, lived 50 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-07800170.summary: This epitaph records the death of Callityche, a slave, who lived for 50 years and wishes her rest.

EDCS-07800171.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Calpurnia Grata lived 41 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-07800171.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to the departed spirits for Calpurnia Grata, who lived 41 years.

EDCS-07800173.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Cornelius Mus lived 16 years.
EDCS-07800173.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Publius Cornelius Mus.

EDCS-07800174.translation: Cornelia Tallusa lived 56 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-07800174.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial place of Cornelia Tallusa.

EDCS-07800175.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Crescens.

EDCS-07800176.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Made for a deserving spouse. Diamnasi Epagatio lived 58 years and 9 months; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800176.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by a spouse to Diamnasi Epagatio, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-07800177.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fabia Spia lived 50 years.
EDCS-07800177.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Fabia Spia.

EDCS-07800178.translation: Fabia Urbana lived 91 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-07800178.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial place of Fabia Urbana.

EDCS-07800179.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Facundus.

EDCS-07800180.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Fannius Salvianus lived 37 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800180.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial place of Lucius Fannius Salvianus.

EDCS-07800181.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Us Feli
EDCS-07800181.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription mentioning 'Us Feli'.

EDCS-07800182.translation: Felicia lived 45 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-07800182.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial place of Felicia.

EDCS-07800183.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavius Salsulus.
EDCS-07800183.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the spirits of the departed by Flavius Salsulus.

EDCS-07800184.translation: M. TV. I. IV. Helus lived 70 years. He is buried here. Fortunatus, his freedman, made this mausoleum for him as he deserved.
EDCS-07800184.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Helus, with his freedman Fortunatus dedicating a mausoleum to him.

EDCS-07800185.translation: Gargilia Nigrosa, daughter of Lucius, lived 37 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-07800185.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gargilia Nigrosa, recording her age and burial.

EDCS-07800187.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Geminia Felicia lived 23 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-07800187.summary: This funerary dedication records the life and burial of Geminia Felicia.

EDCS-07800188.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Ilara lived 31 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-07800188.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Ilara.

EDCS-07800189.translation: Lucius Iulius Rufinus lived 23 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-07800189.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and burial of Lucius Iulius Rufinus.

EDCS-07800190.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Iulius Severus lived 26 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-07800190.summary: This funerary dedication records the life and burial of Marcus Iulius Severus.

EDCS-07800191.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Victoria, recording her age.

EDCS-07800192.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Urbanilla lived 20 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-07800192.summary: This funerary dedication records the life and burial of Iulia Urbanilla.

EDCS-07800193.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Iunius Baricio lived 45 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-07800193.summary: This funerary dedication records the life and burial of Caius Iunius Baricio.

EDCS-07800194.translation: Lucius Licinius Successus lived for 37 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800194.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan and burial of Lucius Licinius Successus.

EDCS-07800195.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lived 60 years.
EDCS-07800195.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, noting a lifespan of 60 years.

EDCS-07800196.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Modius Chius lived for 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800196.summary: A tombstone dedicated to the Manes, recording the lifespan and burial of Lucius Modius Chius.

EDCS-07800197.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Octavius Felix lived for 75 years.
EDCS-07800197.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, noting the lifespan of Lucius Octavius Felix.

EDCS-07800198.translation: Lucius Papirius Paetus, freedman of Lucius, lived for 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800198.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan and burial of Lucius Papirius Paetus, a freedman.

EDCS-07800199.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Petronius Cerialis, son of Publius, of the Fabia tribe, lived for 16 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800199.summary: A tombstone dedicated to the Manes, recording the lifespan and burial of the young Publius Petronius Cerialis.

EDCS-07800200.summary: An incomplete inscription likely naming a person from Capua, Lucius Pompeianus, possibly a funerary or dedication text.

EDCS-07800201.translation: Popilius Barbatus lived for 13 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800201.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan and burial of the young Popilius Barbatus.

EDCS-07800202.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Popilius Callistus lived for 71 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800202.summary: A tombstone dedicated to the Manes, recording the lifespan and burial of Publius Popilius Callistus.

EDCS-07800203.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pudens lived for 2 years, 5 months, and 5 days.
EDCS-07800203.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, meticulously recording the lifespan of the very young Pudens in years, months, and days.

EDCS-07800204.summary: This inscription, possibly a dedication or funerary marker, names Publius Sael and an individual or deity named Om.

EDCS-07800205.translation: Caius Salustius lived 90 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800205.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Caius Salustius.

EDCS-07800206.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Quintus Satrius Fuscus, son of Quintus, lived 20 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800206.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name, parentage, age at death, and burial of Quintus Satrius Fuscus.

EDCS-07800207.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Satrius Nimphicus lived 105 years.
EDCS-07800207.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name and age at death of Lucius Satrius Nimphicus.

EDCS-07800208.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Secunda lived 27 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-07800208.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name, age at death, and burial of Secunda.

EDCS-07800209.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Seius Seianus lived 22 years.
EDCS-07800209.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name and age at death of Lucius Seius Seianus.

EDCS-07800210.translation: Marcus Sittius Crassus, son of Publius, of the Collina tribe, lived 65 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800210.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name, father's name, tribal affiliation, age at death, and burial of Marcus Sittius Crassus.

EDCS-07800211.translation: Titus Sittius Senex, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800211.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name, father's name, tribal affiliation, age at death, and burial of Titus Sittius Senex.

EDCS-07800212.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caius Sittius Urbanus, dutiful, lived 39 years; he rests here, happy and without pain.
EDCS-07800212.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name, epithets, age at death, and burial of Caius Sittius Urbanus.

EDCS-07800213.translation: Sittia Spica, daughter of Rufi Sitti, lived 70 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-07800213.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name, parentage, age at death, and burial of Sittia Spica.

EDCS-07800214.translation: Gaius Stertinius Gallus, son of Gaius, lived 95 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800214.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Gaius Stertinius Gallus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800215.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Talimus lived 35 years.
EDCS-07800215.summary: A funerary dedication to the Manes, recording the lifespan of Talimus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800216.translation: Lucius Valerius Secundus lived 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800216.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Lucius Valerius Secundus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800217.translation: Victoricus lived 18 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800217.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Victoricus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800225.translation: To PA AEO, from CAXAN, 11 years. SOTE Ioannes, from AN.
EDCS-07800225.summary: A very fragmentary inscription, possibly recording a dedication or a memorial with names and ages, origin unclear.

EDCS-08100126.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Bebia Ispes lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-08100126.summary: A funerary dedication to the Manes, recording the lifespan and burial of Bebia Ispes, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100127.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Servilius Argentillus lived 21 years, a most devoted brother.
EDCS-08100127.summary: A funerary dedication to the Manes, recording the lifespan and familial relationship of Servilius Argentillus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100128.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sittia Maxima lived 45 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-08100128.summary: A funerary dedication to the Manes, recording the lifespan and burial of Sittia Maxima, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100129.translation: Verna gladly fulfilled a vow to Victoria and Mercury.
EDCS-08100129.summary: A votive inscription recording Verna fulfilling a vow to the deities Victoria and Mercury, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100130.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Babulia Titia lived 63 years.
EDCS-08100130.summary: A funerary dedication to the Manes, recording the lifespan of Babulia Titia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100131.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Paternus lived for 23 years and 5 months. Ianuarius made this for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-08100131.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Paternus and dedicated by his father Ianuarius.

EDCS-08100133.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Fabatius Victor lived for 85 years.
EDCS-08100133.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Fabatius Victor.

EDCS-08100135.summary: A very brief, enigmatic inscription possibly referring to a person or an object named Laeta, or a metaphorical crown. Its context and purpose are unclear.

EDCS-08100136.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Cornelius Severus, pious, lived for [X] years, is buried here.
EDCS-08100136.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Marcus Cornelius Severus.

EDCS-08100137.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Hermais, slave of Quintus Servilius Macrinus, lived for 56 years. Felix, her son, placed here this pious mother. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08100137.summary: A funerary epitaph for Hermais, a slave of Quintus Servilius Macrinus, dedicated by her son Felix.

EDCS-08100138.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Victoria lived for 75 years. Volcius Victor made this for his dearest wife. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08100138.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Victoria, dedicated by her husband Volcius Victor.

EDCS-08100139.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Maria Boruc lived for 81 years. Dalianon and Caius made this for their mother. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08100139.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maria Boruc, dedicated by her children Dalianon and Caius.

EDCS-08100141.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iustae, slave of Caius Servilius Macrinus, lived for 26 years. Felix made this for his dutiful and well-deserving wife. She is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08100141.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iustae, a slave of Caius Servilius Macrinus, dedicated by her husband Felix.

EDCS-08100142.translation: By the authority of the Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, father of the fatherland, lands accepted from the Cirtensian public land have been separated from the public Cirtensian land.
EDCS-08100142.summary: An official decree under Emperor Hadrian, likely concerning land division or administration in the Cirtensian territory.

EDCS-08100144.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Urbana lived for 81 years.
EDCS-08100144.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Iulia Urbana.

EDCS-08100145.translation: To Marcus Domitius Martialis, from military service.
EDCS-08100145.summary: This is an inscription honoring Marcus Domitius Martialis, likely a dedication by him upon completion of military service.

EDCS-08100146.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Calidius Martialis lived 25 years.
EDCS-08100146.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Caius Calidius Martialis, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-08100147.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Seia Catervaria lived 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-08100147.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Seia Catervaria, who died at the age of 35 and is stated to be buried at this location.

EDCS-08100153.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Numida, daughter of Valeria Victoris, lived 105 years.
EDCS-08100153.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Numida, daughter of Valeria Victoris, who lived to be 105 years old.

EDCS-08100154.translation: Publius Arius Processus and his sons, members of the association, by order of their association, have donated a bench to the Genius of Amsiga.
EDCS-08100154.summary: This inscription records a donation of a bench by Publius Arius Processus and his sons to the Genius of Amsiga, made on behalf of their collegium (association).

EDCS-08100155.translation: Celsus, pontifex, prefect, from the Quirites' Virtus.
EDCS-08100155.summary: This is an epitaph or dedication honoring Celsus, who held significant religious and military offices.

EDCS-08100156.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Caius Aemilius Certus, son of [---].
EDCS-08100156.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mercury Augustus by Caius Aemilius Certus.

EDCS-08100157.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Marcus Vitruvius Gracilis fulfilled his vow, as seen, in accordance with his vision.
EDCS-08100157.summary: This inscription records Marcus Vitruvius Gracilis fulfilling a vow to Mercury Augustus.

EDCS-08100158.translation: Publius Caecilius Quadratus made and dedicated this with good will and his own money, together with his wife Iulia Fortuna, members of the association.
EDCS-08100158.summary: This inscription records the voluntary dedication of a monument by Publius Caecilius Quadratus and his wife Iulia Fortuna, funded by themselves.

EDCS-08100159.translation: Quintus Claudius Nampano, having been warned by a vision, gave and dedicated to his fellow members of the college of Mercury, with good will and from his own money.
EDCS-08100159.summary: This inscription records Quintus Claudius Nampano making a voluntary donation to his collegium of Mercury, prompted by a divine vision.

EDCS-08100160.translation: Quintus Julius Honoratianus donated a bench for his colleagues with his own belongings.
EDCS-08100160.summary: This inscription records a donation of a bench by Quintus Julius Honoratianus to his colleagues, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08100161.translation: Lucius Julius Victor, son of Lucius Julius Victor, promised a vow to the college of Mercury and faithfully carried it out and dedicated it with his own money.
EDCS-08100161.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Mercury by Lucius Julius Victor, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08100162.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Romulus placed the threshold, having been ordered to do so, and restored and dedicated the temple, which had fallen into ruin through age, on the Ides of October in the consulship of Macrinus and Celsus.
EDCS-08100162.summary: This inscription records the restoration and dedication of a temple to Mercury Augustus by Romulus in 209 CE.

EDCS-08100165.summary: This is a simple dedication to Gaius Munatius Saturninus, date uncertain.

EDCS-08100167.translation: To Victory Augustus, the people of Constantina [dedicate this] to Flavius Valerius Crispinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-08100167.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the people of Constantina to Victory Augustus and Flavius Valerius Crispinus, a Caesar, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-08100169.translation: V. I. F. Sextus Asicius Tabernarius dedicated a vow.
EDCS-08100169.summary: This inscription records a vow dedicated by Sextus Asicius Tabernarius, with an abbreviation V.I.F. likely meaning 'votum solvit libens merito', dating from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08100170.translation: To the Genius of Augustus, Lucius Julius AC.
EDCS-08100170.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Genius of Augustus by Lucius Julius AC, date uncertain.

EDCS-08100174.translation: To P. V. B. N., the founder and our lord Valens, triumphant, always Augustus, Publilius Ceionius Caecina Albinus, most eminent man, consular.
EDCS-08100174.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Valens by Publilius Ceionius Caecina Albinus, a consular official, likely from the late 4th century CE.

EDCS-08100177.translation: [---] PVM MO always T VM which catiores CANTIOV [---]
EDCS-08100177.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a dedication or funerary text, with incomplete names and titles, date uncertain.

EDCS-08100179.translation: With a conduit dedicated to the greater [benefit], may our most divine, perpetual, unconquered princes and Augusti be safe. Caecina Decius Albinus the younger, consular, sexagesimal official of the province of Numidia Constantina, under the care of Caecilius Cromatius Ecdicius Triumphalis, most perfect man, perpetual priest.
EDCS-08100179.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a conduit for the benefit of the emperors and the province of Numidia, by Caecina Decius Albinus the younger, a high-ranking official, likely from the late 4th century CE.

EDCS-08100180.translation: Flavius Avianus Caecilius, most distinguished man, consular governor of the province of Numidia, erected [this monument] so that it would endure in stature forever.
EDCS-08100180.summary: This is a dedication by Flavius Avianus Caecilius, a high-ranking official, for a monument intended to be permanent.

EDCS-08100184.translation: Publius Iulius Noricus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, honored with a public horse, dedicated [this] on the occasion of his aedileship.
EDCS-08100184.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Publius Iulius Noricus upon assuming the office of aedile.

EDCS-08100185.translation: For Publius Iulius Proximus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, aedile, IIIvir quinquennalis, prefect for dispensing justice of the colony of Veneria Rusicada, flamen of the deified Claudius, augur, master of augurs. Quintus Seius Novellus, son of Quintus Aulus, of the Quirina tribe, and Quintus Seius Novellus, his son.
EDCS-08100185.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Publius Iulius Proximus, listing his many public offices in the colony, and by his family members.

EDCS-08100186.translation: [He held the] public horse, was aedile, quaestor, IIIvir, prefect for dispensing justice of the colony of Minerva Chullu, and flamen.
EDCS-08100186.summary: This text lists various public offices held by an unnamed individual in the colony of Minerva Chullu.

EDCS-08100187.translation: On the occasion of the dedication, he presented theatrical games, and for the honor of Marcus Flamonius, perpetual genius of the people of our colony of Cirta, to whose shade he dedicated [this].
EDCS-08100187.summary: This inscription records the presentation of games and a dedication made in honor of Marcus Flamonius, the perpetual genius of Cirta.

EDCS-08100209.translation: Caius Naevius Saturninus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, from Cirta, a veteran of the legion, made [this] for himself while alive and for his descendants.
EDCS-08100209.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument set up by Caius Naevius Saturninus, a legionary veteran, for himself and his family.

EDCS-08100210.translation: A soldier of a praetorian cohort, he served for 18 years, lived for...
EDCS-08100210.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription for a soldier of a praetorian cohort, indicating his military service and lifespan.

EDCS-08100215.translation: To the honored father of the four colonies, master of Roman knights, most skilled. Marcellus and the Roman knights, to their dearest father, freedmen, freedwomen, children, and their descendants.
EDCS-08100215.summary: This is a dedication to a respected figure referred to as the 'father of four colonies' and 'master of Roman knights', likely by his family and fellow equestrians.

EDCS-08100217.translation: Prefect for dispensing justice of COG, lived 91 years. This is he.
EDCS-08100217.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph for an unnamed prefect for dispensing justice, stating his age at death.

EDCS-08100219.translation: To the departed spirits, in memory of Iulia Sidonia Felix, named only in name, whose threads the Fates cruelly broke before her time. Alas, the Fates' fires touched the wedding torches, all the Dryads groaned, and the girls grieved. Lucina also wept with dimmed light, because she was the only pledge of her parents. She was a priestess of the goddess Sistrata of Memphis. Here she rests, silent in eternal sleep. She lived 17 years, 4 months, 14 days. She is buried here.
EDCS-08100219.summary: This is a poetic funerary epitaph for Iulia Sidonia Felix, a priestess, who died young, lamenting her premature death and mentioning her roles and family connections.

EDCS-08100227.translation: Accia Luciosa lived 70 years. Quintus Cassius Campanianus made an eternal home for his dearest wife.
EDCS-08100227.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Accia Luciosa, set up by her husband Quintus Cassius Campanianus.

EDCS-08100228.translation: Aelius for Alexander, a man of high standing.
EDCS-08100228.summary: This is a very short inscription possibly commemorating Aelius and dedicated to Alexander.

EDCS-08100232.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Aemilia Fabaria.

EDCS-08100237.translation: To the departed spirits. Alfia Lucida, to her most loving husband, lived 50 years.
EDCS-08100237.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a husband, set up by his wife Alfia Lucida.

EDCS-08100240.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Annius Gemellus lived 30 years.
EDCS-08100240.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Publius Annius Gemellus.

EDCS-08100290.translation: Capito, son of Marcus, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 65 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08100290.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Capito, recording his lifespan and burial place.

EDCS-08100315.translation: Nice lived 85 years. She is buried here. Crescentianus lived 70 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08100315.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespans and burial places of Nice and Crescentianus.

EDCS-08100331.translation: lius, son of Marcianus, Sitianus lived 101 years, and Julius lived 5 years, and Rogata lived [---] years, and Fabricius lived [---] years, Victoriae lived 3 years, and Rogata lived 7 years. [---] A
EDCS-08100331.summary: This fragmented inscription records the lifespans of multiple individuals, likely family members.

EDCS-08100335.translation: CAVT lived 8 years and Ana lived 6 years. He lived 8 years. [---] RPVAV 8 years and Felicius lived 13 years for his most worthy brothers.
EDCS-08100335.summary: This fragmented inscription records the lifespans of multiple individuals, possibly children, and mentions brothers.

EDCS-08100336.translation: Ius, son of Quintus, Quirina Felicianus, a most rare father, lived 71 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100336.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Felicianus and includes a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-08100337.translation: Felix lived for 30 years and is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100337.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Felix, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100344.translation: Lucius Fonteius Sabinus lived for three years and five days; he is buried 23 [units of distance] from here.
EDCS-08100344.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Fonteius Sabinus, a child, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100349.translation: Gargilius, son of Publius, of the voting tribe Quirina.
EDCS-08100349.summary: This inscription is a tribal affiliation notice, likely for a citizen named Gargilius, dating to the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-08100367.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ianuaria, a child, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100390.translation: Iulia Eleuthera, most rare wife, lived for 10 years.
EDCS-08100390.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Eleuthera, a wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100395.translation: In memory of Iulia Helena Sirica, her most rare daughter, who lived for 2 years.
EDCS-08100395.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Helena Sirica, a child, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100401.translation: In memory of Iulia Romana, an innocent infant, 3 months old.
EDCS-08100401.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Romana, an infant, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100406.translation: Iunca lived for 60 years and is buried here.
EDCS-08100406.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iunca, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100419.translation: Torius Marcellus made [this] for his most beloved wife; she is buried here, she is buried here.
EDCS-08100419.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Torius Marcellus for his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100423.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maria Meciliani, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100425.translation: Ius Maximus lived for 61 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-08100425.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Ius Maximus.

EDCS-08100426.translation: Nia Maxima lived for 70 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-08100426.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Nia Maxima.

EDCS-08100427.translation: Ria Mellissa lived for 70 years; may your bones rest.
EDCS-08100427.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Ria Mellissa and includes a wish for her rest.

EDCS-08100430.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sextus Murdius lived for 2 years.
EDCS-08100430.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Murdius, recording his age at death.

EDCS-08100431.translation: Sextus Murtius Genialis, while alive and in his 63rd year, 9 months, and 4 days, inscribed this altar for himself and for the dearly loved children of his sons, who died long ago, and he will inscribe it as long as light and fortune allow.
EDCS-08100431.summary: This is a dedication of an altar by Sextus Murtius Genialis for himself and his deceased children, made when he was 63 years old.

EDCS-08100432.translation: To the departed spirits. The son of Quintus Murtius Urbicus, who lived for 33 years and [some months], most innocent.
EDCS-08100432.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the son of Quintus Murtius Urbicus, recording his age and innocence.

EDCS-08100444.translation: Valeria Peregrina placed this for her most exceptional husband.
EDCS-08100444.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Valeria Peregrina dedicated a monument to her husband.

EDCS-08100460.translation: S. Primitivus lived for 13 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-08100460.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of S. Primitivus.

EDCS-08100465.translation: Lucius Publicius Saturninus, father, lives; he lived for 100 years.
EDCS-08100465.summary: This funerary epitaph records the exceptionally long life of Lucius Publicius Saturninus.

EDCS-08100469.summary: This inscription is a simple funerary epitaph naming Aulus Pullaenius Faustus.

EDCS-08100476.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Rutilia Mustia lived 85 years.
EDCS-08100476.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Rutilia Mustia.

EDCS-08100505.translation: Leiaq Stella is buried here. She is buried here.
EDCS-08100505.summary: A funerary inscription marking the burial place of Leiaq Stella.

EDCS-08100508.translation: Terentia Rogata lived 60 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-08100508.summary: A funerary inscription for Terentia Rogata, including a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-08100519.translation: Vibia Rogata, daughter of Caius, lived 11 years.
EDCS-08100519.summary: A funerary inscription recording the short life of Vibia Rogata.

EDCS-08100521.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Victoria lived 36 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08100521.summary: A funerary inscription for Victoria, noting her age and burial.

EDCS-08100547.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Cusina Mustica, while alive, lived [---] years.
EDCS-08100547.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription for Cusina Mustica, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-08100568.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Kastula lived 70 years in peace. Navigius, her son, [---]
EDCS-08100568.summary: A funerary inscription for Kastula and her son, recording Kastula's age and peaceful life.

EDCS-08201705.translation: For the health and victory of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, the bravest, most fortunate, and of Julia Domna Augusta, mother of the emperor, of the camp, of the senate, and of the entire fatherland and divine household, Lucius M. Felix Quadratus, duovir, gave two statues and a quadriporticus to the people at his own expense for the republic.
EDCS-08201705.summary: A public dedication of statues and a portico by the duovir Lucius M. Felix Quadratus for the well-being of Emperor Caracalla and Julia Domna, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08600904.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of Barbarus, son of Saturninus.

EDCS-08601035.translation: Fidelis Paulina lived in peace more or less 18 years; she departed on the 5th day before the Ides of November in the 6th indiction.
EDCS-08601035.summary: A funerary inscription detailing the approximate lifespan and precise date of death of Fidelis Paulina.

EDCS-08601534.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Pompeius lived [for...]
EDCS-08601534.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life of Pompeius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900697.translation: To Quintus Tegonius Saturninus, a most rare father, from his son Latinus.
EDCS-08900697.summary: A funerary dedication from a son, Latinus, to his father, Quintus Tegonius Saturninus, likely Roman Imperial period.
