EDCS-08900698.translation: To Serenia Latiina, a most beloved mother, from her son Latinius.
EDCS-08900698.summary: A funerary dedication from a son, Latinius, to his mother, Serenia Latiina, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08901191.translation: A statuette of Cupid, the god, made of ivory, which was crafted at Athens by Phidias.
EDCS-08901191.summary: An inscription describing an ivory statuette of the god Cupid, crafted by Phidias at Athens, likely Hellenistic or Roman period.

EDCS-09001591.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus.
EDCS-09001591.summary: A dedication to Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus, likely from the Tetrarchic period (late 3rd/early 4th century CE).

EDCS-09001593.translation: To our Lords Iulius Constantius Augustus and Claudius Constantius.
EDCS-09001593.summary: A dedication to the Augusti Iulius Constantius and Claudius Constantius, likely from the Constantinian period (early 4th century CE).

EDCS-09001594.translation: To Flavius Claudius Constantinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-09001594.summary: A dedication to Flavius Claudius Constantinus, a Caesar, likely from the Constantinian period (early 4th century CE).

EDCS-09001595.translation: To Emperor Flavius Valerius Constantius, most indulgent prince.
EDCS-09001595.summary: A dedication to Emperor Flavius Valerius Constantius, likely from the Constantinian period (mid-4th century CE).

EDCS-09001596.translation: To our lord Emperor Gaius Valerius Diocletian, the Unconquered Augustus, for the 15th time.
EDCS-09001596.summary: A dedication to Emperor Diocletian, celebrating his 15th consulship, from the Tetrarchic period (late 3rd/early 4th century CE).

EDCS-09001610.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Probus, the Unconquered Augustus, with tribunician power, [for the...] 5 miles. To our Lord Jovian, the triumphant, ever-Augustus.
EDCS-09001610.summary: A complex inscription honoring Emperors Probus and Jovian, with a reference to a road of 5 miles, likely from the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-09001611.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus, the Invincible, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, for the eighth time.
EDCS-09001611.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Maximian, likely on a public monument, dated to one of his consulships.

EDCS-09001612.translation: To our lord Flavius Constantius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-09001612.summary: This is an inscription honoring Caesar Flavius Constantius, likely on a public building or monument.

EDCS-09001613.translation: To our lord Flavius Constantius, most blessed Caesar, the Pious.
EDCS-09001613.summary: This is an inscription honoring Caesar Flavius Constantius, likely on a public building or monument.

EDCS-09200583.translation: Saturninus Thusdritanus, an equestrian soldier of the emperor's personal guard, lived.
EDCS-09200583.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Saturninus Thusdritanus, an equestrian soldier, indicating his place of origin and status.

EDCS-09300562.translation: Caius Aelius Cres of Gortyn, the chief bucolic poet, made this for his wife Donata and their descendants.
EDCS-09300562.summary: This is a dedication by Caius Aelius Cres, a bucolic poet, for his family.

EDCS-09300565.translation: Publius Quintilius Vitalis for the honor of the priesthood.
EDCS-09300565.summary: This inscription records Publius Quintilius Vitalis assuming a priestly office.

EDCS-09300566.translation: Aelii Blandus Macrinus and Peregrinus made this for Titus Aelius Victorinus, a cornicularius (officer's attendant) of the prefect, with 23 years of service, an honest man, and their father.
EDCS-09300566.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by sons for their father, Titus Aelius Victorinus, detailing his military service and character.

EDCS-09300567.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius P. Ianuarius and his heirs made this for their deserving father. He lived 75 years, 6 months, [and some days].
EDCS-09300567.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius P. Ianuarius, erected by his heirs, providing his age at death.

EDCS-09300568.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Cameria Rogata made this for her deserving husband and the heirs of Terentius Capito, a veteran. He lived 85 years, 5 months, and 5 days.
EDCS-09300568.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a veteran named Terentius Capito, erected by his wife Cameria Rogata, providing his age at death.

EDCS-09300571.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Iunius Felix lived [some years]; the heirs of Caius Iulius Caecilianus Octavius made this, because he had bequeathed this in his will.
EDCS-09300571.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Iunius Felix, which was set up by the heirs of Caius Iulius Caecilianus Octavius as per a testamentary bequest.

EDCS-09300572.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits: a tomb for Gaius Octavius Saturninus, who lived 75 years.
EDCS-09300572.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to Gaius Octavius Saturninus, who lived for 75 years.

EDCS-09300574.translation: Marcus Aurelius Paetus, who lived 46 years and served 25 years as an equestrian *sesquiplicarius* in the III Augusta legion, made this for himself.
EDCS-09300574.summary: This inscription records a soldier, Marcus Aurelius Paetus, who served in the III Augusta legion and made a tomb for himself.

EDCS-09300575.translation: To Publius Iulius Quetus, his most excellent son. Publius Iulius Quetus, his father, lived 45 years.
EDCS-09300575.summary: This inscription is a dedication from a father, Publius Iulius Quetus, to his deceased son, also named Publius Iulius Quetus.

EDCS-09300577.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. A monument to Aelia Mon, a most rare mother. Germanus lived 100 years.
EDCS-09300577.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to Aelia Mon, a mother, by Germanus who lived to be 100 years old.

EDCS-09300578.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. In memory of Aemilia Nigra. Her husband and her sons made this. She lived 70 years.
EDCS-09300578.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by the husband and sons to Aemilia Nigra, who lived for 70 years.

EDCS-09300579.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. In memory of Aemilia Fortunis, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-09300579.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to Aemilia Fortunis, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-09300580.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. In memory of Publius Aemilius Campanus, a most rare brother. Publianus, a soldier, made this. He lived 40 years.
EDCS-09300580.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Publianus, a soldier, to his brother Publius Aemilius Campanus.

EDCS-09300581.translation: And Lucius M. Cittinus to his dearest father.
EDCS-09300581.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius M. Cittinus to his father.

EDCS-09300582.translation: Gaius Sallustius Felix, while alive, made this for himself and for Caecilia Donatula, his most rare wife, and dedicated it.
EDCS-09300582.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument commissioned by Gaius Sallustius Felix for himself and his wife Caecilia Donatula.

EDCS-09300584.translation: Securinus, with his sons, made this. May Marcianus fare well.
EDCS-09300584.summary: This inscription records Securinus and his sons as the makers of a monument, with a well-wishing for Marcianus.

EDCS-09300585.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Saturn, Julia Fortunata lived 30 years.
EDCS-09300585.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter and Saturn, likely on a tombstone, for Julia Fortunata who died at age 30.

EDCS-09300586.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Donata lived 110 years.
EDCS-09300586.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Spirits of the Departed for Iulia Donata, who lived to be 110 years old.

EDCS-09300587.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Gairnusus lived 25 years and 5 days and 3 hours. His father made this, dedicating it to his dearest son willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09300587.summary: A funerary inscription where a father dedicated a monument to his son, Iulius Gairnusus, who died at age 25.

EDCS-09300588.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Arminia Saturnina lived 73 years and 3 days and 2 hours. Her husband made this for his deserving, pious, most excellent wife, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09300588.summary: A funerary inscription by a husband for his wife, Arminia Saturnina, who died at age 73.

EDCS-09300589.translation: To the Campestrian Gods. Titus Aurelius Aurelianus, prefect of the cavalry of the 1st Wing of the Pannonians, made this.
EDCS-09300589.summary: A dedication to the Campestrian Gods by Titus Aurelius Aurelianus, a cavalry prefect, likely for a military benefit.

EDCS-09301502.summary: A dedication to the god Saturn, in his aspect as a bringer of fertility and abundance.

EDCS-09401640.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Afidia Sissoa lived 30 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-09401640.summary: A funerary inscription stating that Afidia Sissoa lived 30 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-09401641.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Iulius lived 5 years.
EDCS-09401641.summary: A funerary inscription for a child named Marcus Iulius who died at the age of 5.

EDCS-09401642.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Matronim lived 100 years. She is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Amon lived 90 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-09401642.summary: A double funerary inscription for Iulia Matronim, who lived 100 years, and Iulius Amon, who lived 90 years, both buried at this site.

EDCS-09401643.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Migin lived 70 years. Nina lived 75 years.
EDCS-09401643.summary: A double funerary inscription recording the lifespans of Migin (70 years) and Nina (75 years).

EDCS-09401647.translation: Pomponius Venustus, priest of Minerva Augusta and Saturn, set this up willingly with his mind.
EDCS-09401647.summary: This is a dedication to Minerva Augusta by a priest named Pomponius Venustus.

EDCS-09401651.translation: Lucius Iulius Saturninus, priest, paid his vow willingly with his mind.
EDCS-09401651.summary: This is a record of a vow being paid by a priest named Lucius Iulius Saturninus.

EDCS-09401652.translation: Gaius Iulius Ianuarius and his wife Aemilia Victorina, with their children, paid their vows willingly with their minds.
EDCS-09401652.summary: This inscription records a family, Gaius Iulius Ianuarius, Aemilia Victorina, and their children, fulfilling vows.

EDCS-09401653.summary: A simple dedication to the deity Silvanus Castrensis.

EDCS-09401656.translation: Marcus Romanius Marcellinus, son of Marcus, of the Camilia tribe, Decimius Rufinus, Prefect of the Third Augustan Legion, [dedicated this] after a vision.
EDCS-09401656.summary: This inscription records a military dedication by Marcus Romanius Marcellinus, Prefect of the Third Augustan Legion, likely in response to a vision.

EDCS-09700392.translation: From the workshop of Iunior, with good things for good people.
EDCS-09700392.summary: This inscription appears to be a shop or workshop sign, possibly advertising quality goods.

EDCS-09700394.translation: Quintus Antistius Agathopus paid his vow to Minerva Augusta willingly with his mind.
EDCS-09700394.summary: This is a votive inscription to Minerva Augusta by Quintus Antistius Agathopus.

EDCS-09700395.translation: To the August Deities, for the safety of the Emperors Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augusti, and Caesar Publius Septimius Geta, and Julia Domna, mother of the camps. Marcus Hostilius Callipus, son of Caius Hostilius Felix, and his wife Gargilia Donata, daughter of Lucius Gargilius Urbanus, [and] Marcus Hostilius V...
EDCS-09700395.summary: This extensive inscription records a dedication for the safety of the imperial family by Marcus Hostilius Callipus and his wife Gargilia Donata, likely during the reign of Septimius Severus and Caracalla.

EDCS-09700784.translation: To Gaius Julius Sallustius Saturninus Fortunatianus, most distinguished man, consular legate and governor with the power of a legate of the Emperor, the most distinguished order of the colony of Cuiculitanum, [as] patron.
EDCS-09700784.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Julius Sallustius Saturninus Fortunatianus as a patron of the colony of Cuiculitanum by the local elite.

EDCS-09701272.summary: This inscription likely indicates the origin or maker of an object, possibly pottery or a metalwork, produced by Felix's workshop.

EDCS-09701274.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, son of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the deified Commodus, nephew of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-nephew of the deified Hadrian, great-great-nephew of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-nephew of the deified Nerva, Arabicus Adiabenicus, propagator of the empire, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 5th time, imperator for the 8th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, father of the fatherland, father of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, propagator of the empire, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-09701274.summary: This is a lengthy imperial titulary inscription for Lucius Septimius Severus, listing his lineage and honors, dating to his 5th tribunician power and 2nd consulship.

EDCS-09701277.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, nephew of the deified Severus Pius, son of the deified Antoninus Magnus Pius, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul, father of the fatherland. The community of Silensium, devoted to his divine majesty and authority, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-09701277.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Severus Alexander by the community of Silensium, likely an honorific monument funded by public money, during his second tribunician power.

EDCS-09701278.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 4th time, imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul. The community of Silensium, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-09701278.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Gordian III by the community of Silensium, likely an honorific monument funded by public money, during his fourth tribunician power.

EDCS-09701281.translation: To Gaius Annianus Flavianus, decorated with public equestrian rank, procurator of the splendid province of Carthage, procurator of the Alpius Atrectianae, subprefect of the praetorian fleet of Misenum, prefect of the first cohort of Sabasteni, tribune of the 3rd Italian Legion, prefect of the 4th Raetian cohort. Gaius Vibius Marinus, centurion of the 3rd Augustan Legion, his best friend and fellow townsman.
EDCS-09701281.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Gaius Annianus Flavianus, a prominent official with various military and administrative roles, by his friend and fellow townsman, Gaius Vibius Marinus.

EDCS-09701286.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Pomponius Paulinus, son of Lucius, aged 70, made this memorial of his own death, from 500 sesterces.
EDCS-09701286.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the self-funded memorial of Lucius Pomponius Paulinus, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-09701287.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. The memory of Caius, son of Arnensis G Fuscus, a veteran of the 3rd Augustan Legion, who lived 90 years. His wife, the most worthy Ania, made and dedicated this to her husband.
EDCS-09701287.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius, a veteran of the 3rd Augustan Legion, set up by his wife Ania.

EDCS-09701288.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades and to perpetual security. For Lucius Junius Hilarus, most dutiful father, who lived [XX] years, and for Volusia Letina, most beloved mother, who lived [XX] years, and for Junius Hilarus, most rare brother. Junius Blandus and Emeritus, brothers and sons, made this.
EDCS-09701288.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by two brothers, Junius Blandus and Emeritus, for their parents and their brother.

EDCS-09701289.translation: For Fabia Graniola, most dutiful mother.
EDCS-09701289.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Fabia Graniola, identified as a most dutiful mother.

EDCS-09701290.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iunia Tertia lived 75 years. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Fabricius Satara lived 77 years. His heirs made this. Here he lies.
EDCS-09701290.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two individuals, Iunia Tertia and Fabricius Satara, and was erected by their heirs.

EDCS-09701816.translation: Aegidia Donatilla lived 48 years, died 9 days before the Kalends of January. I beg you, brothers and sisters, by your prayers and supplications for my spirit, to press on in the name of Christ.
EDCS-09701816.summary: This is a Christian epitaph recording the lifespan and a plea for prayers from the deceased Aegidia Donatilla.

EDCS-09701818.translation: Indagius, with his wife Faustina and sons, gladly paid a vow to Mercurius Silvanus.
EDCS-09701818.summary: This is a votive inscription where Indagius and his family paid a vow to Mercurius Silvanus.

EDCS-09701819.translation: Gaius Longeius Felix, having achieved his vow, gladly set up and dedicated this altar to the god Mercurius Silvanus and the Great Gods.
EDCS-09701819.summary: This is a dedication of an altar by Gaius Longeius Felix to Mercurius Silvanus and the Great Gods.

EDCS-09701820.translation: Dedicated to Mercurius Augustus. Gaius Julius Rogatus, son of Gaius, of the Fabia tribe, Banasa, a former speculator of the Hadrianic Curia of fortunate veterans of the III Augustan Legion, placed this, using 4,000 sesterces amplified with additional money beyond the legal sum, for the honor of the perpetual priesthood which they conferred upon him, and he himself dedicated it.
EDCS-09701820.summary: This is a dedication of a monument by a veteran, Gaius Julius Rogatus, to Mercurius Augustus in honor of his perpetual priesthood.

EDCS-09701821.translation: Dedicated to the August Genius of the colony. Gaius Publicius Veranus, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, and his brother Gaius Publicius Candidi, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, with Marcus Luccius Torquatus Bassianus, the August legate with pro-praetorian power, designated consul, and patron of the colony, dedicating it for the honor of the perpetual priesthood, made a temple from the ground up with a statue, exceeding the promised 10,000 sesterces by more than 20,000 sesterces, using 63,500 sesterces.
EDCS-09701821.summary: This is a building dedication by two brothers, Gaius Publicius Veranus and Gaius Publicius Candidi, and funded by the imperial legate Marcus Luccius Torquatus Bassianus, for the perpetual priesthood of the colony.

EDCS-09800376.translation: Written by the hand of XIPTDV ABTNEV, during the reign of our lord Flavius Justinian.
EDCS-09800376.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning the reign of Emperor Justinian, likely a legal or official document.

EDCS-09800383.translation: To our lords Magnio Maximus and Magnio Victor, forever Augusts.
EDCS-09800383.summary: This is a dedication to the emperors Magnio Maximus and Magnio Victor, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-09800390.translation: Julius Temarsa, veteran, chief priest, Marcus Aurelius.
EDCS-09800390.summary: This inscription names Julius Temarsa, a veteran and priest, along with Marcus Aurelius, possibly indicating a dedication or a commission.

EDCS-09800393.summary: This is a dedication to Cauni, who was a consul-designate, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-09800401.translation: It was built under our most pious lords Justinian and Theodora, forever Augusts, by the providence of Solomon, master of soldiers, ex-consul, prefect of Africa, and patrician.
EDCS-09800401.summary: This inscription records the construction of a building under the emperors Justinian and Theodora, commissioned by Solomon, a high-ranking official in the 6th century CE.

EDCS-09800406.translation: In the second year of Guntamund in Carthage, V S D SE, twenty-six measures only.
EDCS-09800406.summary: This inscription, likely from the Vandal period in North Africa, records a measurement of twenty-six units in the second regnal year of King Guntamund.

EDCS-09800407.translation: In the eighth year of King Guntamund, Massiese joined the dominical part of Adeudacus.
EDCS-09800407.summary: This inscription notes an event in the eighth regnal year of King Guntamund, involving individuals named Massiese and Adeudacus, likely from the Vandal period.

EDCS-09800411.translation: To Sol Invictus, the unconquered god, Marcus Julius Neon gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09800411.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Sol Invictus, dedicated by Marcus Julius Neon, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09800591.translation: To the divine spirits. Munatia Lul, most holy priestess and wife, lived for 70 years. Publius Sittius Ianuarius had this made for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-09800591.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Munatia Lul, recording her age and dedication by her husband, Publius Sittius Ianuarius, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09800592.translation: Caius Curvius Scaeva lived for 22 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09800592.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Curvius Scaeva, recording his age and burial place, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09800593.translation: To the divine spirits. Caius Julius Terentianus lived for 47 years; he is here.
EDCS-09800593.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Julius Terentianus, recording his age and burial place, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09800594.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Licinius Attianus lived 40 years.
EDCS-09800594.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Licinius Attianus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-09800595.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Licinius Messor lived 14 years.
EDCS-09800595.summary: A funerary epitaph for Licinius Messor, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-09800596.translation: Natalis Elcel lived 91 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09800596.summary: A funerary epitaph for Natalis Elcel, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-09800597.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Postumius Urbanus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-09800598.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Terentius Marcellinus lived 60 years. To the Spirits of the Departed. Urbana lived 80 years.
EDCS-09800598.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Terentius Marcellinus and Urbana, recording their lifespans.

EDCS-09800599.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Iulius Crescens lived 115 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-09800599.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Crescens, recording his lifespan and a wish for repose.

EDCS-09800600.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Seia Monina lived 105 years.
EDCS-09800600.summary: A funerary epitaph for Seia Monina, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-09800601.translation: Sacred to Fruit-Bearing Augustus, by the will of Claudius Maximus.
EDCS-09800601.summary: A dedication to Emperor Augustus, made according to the will of Claudius Maximus.

EDCS-09800602.translation: Emperor Nerva Traianus, in his ninth consulship, erected this at his own expense with public funds.
EDCS-09800602.summary: A monument erected during the consulship of Emperor Trajan, funded publicly and overseen by him.

EDCS-09800605.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius A tius SOLVOVSTA lived 27 years; he is buried here. NIS honor 19.
EDCS-09800605.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius A tius SOLVOVSTA, recording his lifespan, burial, and possibly a funerary honor.

EDCS-09800606.translation: Africanus lived 95 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-09800606.summary: A funerary epitaph for a man named Africanus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09800607.translation: To the divine spirits. Domitia Tertullina lived 81 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-09800607.summary: A funerary epitaph for Domitia Tertullina, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09800608.translation: Pusinia Extricata lived 31 years. To the divine spirits.
EDCS-09800608.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pusinia Extricata, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09800610.translation: Monument of Lucius Iulius Fronto. He lived 80 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-09800610.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Iulius Fronto, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09800614.translation: Orchivia Fa lived 5 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-09800614.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Orchivia Fa, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09800617.translation: To the divine spirits, in memory of Ateus.
EDCS-09800617.summary: A funerary dedication to the divine spirits, mentioning a person named Ateus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09800622.translation: Ora lived 82 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-09800622.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ora, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09800624.translation: Asella, most brilliant woman, in peace.
EDCS-09800624.summary: A funerary epitaph for a woman named Asella, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09800627.summary: A fragmentary inscription identifying Andreius as the son of Esphilus, likely a funerary or honorary monument from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09800628.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Augustus. Lucius S., son of S., quaestor, gave and dedicated the temple and the statue.
EDCS-09800628.summary: A dedication of a temple and statue to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Augustus by Lucius S., son of S., who held the office of quaestor, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09800629.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Julius Donatus lived 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09800629.summary: This tombstone records the death of Julius Donatus, who lived 45 years and is buried here.

EDCS-09800631.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Auficia Martilla lived 80 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09800631.summary: This tombstone records the death of Auficia Martilla, who lived 80 years and is buried here.

EDCS-09800632.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Decimus Egnatius Rogatus lived 65 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-09800632.summary: This tombstone records the death of Decimus Egnatius Rogatus, who lived 65 years, and includes a wish for his bones to rest well.

EDCS-09800633.translation: Ignatia Stica lived 30 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09800633.summary: This tombstone records the death of Ignatia Stica, who lived 30 years and is buried here.

EDCS-09800634.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fabia Cana lived 30 years.
EDCS-09800634.summary: This tombstone records the death of Fabia Cana, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-09800635.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Felix lived 30 years.
EDCS-09800635.summary: This tombstone records the death of Gaius Julius Felix, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-09800636.translation: Marcus Julius Felix lived 30 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-09800636.summary: This tombstone records the death of Marcus Julius Felix, who lived 30 years, and includes a wish for his bones to rest well.

EDCS-09800637.translation: Julius Gallus lived 105 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09800637.summary: This tombstone records the death of Julius Gallus, who lived 105 years and is buried here.

EDCS-09800638.translation: Gaius Julius Martis lived 70 years. May your bones rest.
EDCS-09800638.summary: This tombstone records the death of Gaius Julius Martis, who lived 70 years, and includes a wish for his bones to rest.

EDCS-09800639.translation: Iulia Fructosa lived 23 years; she is buried.
EDCS-09800639.summary: This tombstone records the death of Iulia Fructosa, who lived 23 years and is buried here.

EDCS-09800640.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Iulia Galla.

EDCS-09800641.translation: Iulia Gemella lived for 80 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09800641.summary: This tombstone records the lifespan and burial of Iulia Gemella.

EDCS-09800642.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Iulia Nina lived for 91 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09800642.summary: This tombstone, dedicated to the underworld spirits, records the lifespan and burial of Iulia Nina.

EDCS-09800643.translation: Iulia Urbana, daughter of Cai, lived for 19 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09800643.summary: This tombstone records the lifespan and burial of Iulia Urbana, daughter of Cai.

EDCS-09800644.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Iulia Urbicosa lived for 100 years.
EDCS-09800644.summary: This tombstone, dedicated to the underworld spirits, records the lifespan of Iulia Urbicosa.

EDCS-09800645.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Lucius lived for 23 years.
EDCS-09800645.summary: This tombstone, dedicated to the underworld spirits, records the lifespan of Lucius.

EDCS-09800646.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: May Matiana's bones rest well; she lived for 100 years.
EDCS-09800646.summary: This tombstone, dedicated to the underworld spirits, records the lifespan of Matiana and a wish for her peaceful repose.

EDCS-09800648.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Matrona lived for [...] years.
EDCS-09800648.summary: This tombstone, dedicated to the underworld spirits, records the lifespan of Matrona, with the exact age missing.

EDCS-09800649.translation: Rogatus lived for 105 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09800649.summary: This tombstone records the lifespan and burial of Rogatus.

EDCS-09800650.translation: Rogatus lived for 101 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-09800650.summary: This tombstone records the lifespan and burial of Rogatus, and includes a wish for his peaceful repose.

EDCS-09800653.translation: To the divine spirits. Tato and Perenima lived 21 years.
EDCS-09800653.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Tato and Perenima.

EDCS-09800657.translation: To the divine spirits. Quintus V Felix lived 55 years. He is here, may he rest well.
EDCS-09800657.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Quintus V Felix and a wish for his rest.

EDCS-09800659.translation: To the divine spirits. Quintus Q M lived 30 years.
EDCS-09800659.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Quintus Q M.

EDCS-09800666.summary: A fragmented inscription likely referring to deities or individuals.

EDCS-09800667.translation: To the divine spirits. Publius Sittius Messor, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, a veteran, lived 73 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-09800667.summary: A funerary inscription for the veteran Publius Sittius Messor, including a wish for his rest.

EDCS-09800668.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Hospita.

EDCS-09800669.translation: Pomi Quirina SV, pious, lived 50 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-09800669.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Pomi Quirina SV, indicating her burial.

EDCS-09800670.translation: To the divine spirits. Lucius Sitius, son of Publius. To the divine spirits.
EDCS-09800670.summary: A funerary inscription with a dedication to the divine spirits, likely for Lucius Sitius.

EDCS-09800671.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Momilius Catulonus.

EDCS-09800674.translation: Onoratus, in the name of his brother Iulius Urbanus Felix, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09800674.summary: A votive inscription where Onoratus fulfills a vow on behalf of his brother Iulius Urbanus Felix.

EDCS-09800675.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Iulius.

EDCS-09800678.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording a person's lifespan.

EDCS-09800679.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Arruntius Eusicnus.

EDCS-09800680.translation: Lucius Arruntius Lucinus lived 20 years, here he lies.
EDCS-09800680.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Lucius Arruntius Lucinus.

EDCS-09800681.translation: To the departed spirits, Lucius Arruntius Lucinus lived 30 years.
EDCS-09800681.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the lifespan of Lucius Arruntius Lucinus.

EDCS-09800682.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Arruntius Mustiolus.

EDCS-09800683.translation: To the departed spirits, Marcus Arruntius Mustiolus lived 10 years.
EDCS-09800683.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the lifespan of Marcus Arruntius Mustiolus.

EDCS-09800685.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Arruntius Severus.

EDCS-09800686.translation: To the departed spirits, Caius Iulius Arator lived 107 years, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-09800686.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the lifespan of Caius Iulius Arator and a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-09800687.translation: Lucius Iulius Urbanus, son of Lucius, lived 30 years, here he lies, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-09800687.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Lucius Iulius Urbanus, son of Lucius, and includes a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-09800688.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Mamonia, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800689.translation: Aeria Peregrina lived for 110 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09800689.summary: This epitaph records the exceptionally long life of Aeria Peregrina and her burial, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800690.translation: To the divine spirits of Sassoria Quintilla, who lived for 103 years.
EDCS-09800690.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to Sassoria Quintilla, commemorating her lifespan, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800691.translation: Sittia Iula, daughter of Publius, lived for 22 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09800691.summary: This epitaph records the short life and burial of Sittia Iula, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800697.translation: To the memory of Caius Julius Crescentis, who lived for 75 years.
EDCS-09800697.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Caius Julius Crescentis and his lifespan, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800700.summary: This tombstone records the lifespan of Musteolus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800703.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan of Rustatus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800704.translation: To the divine spirits of Nonius Pelimnus, who lived for 50 years.
EDCS-09800704.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to Nonius Pelimnus, noting his lifespan, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800705.translation: To the divine spirits of Quintus Papinius Acarius, 41 years.
EDCS-09800705.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Papinius Acarius, noting his age at death, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800715.translation: Scaeva, for the honor of his magistracy, with his sons Crescentianus and ...
EDCS-09800715.summary: This incomplete inscription likely records an honor related to a magistracy for Scaeva and his sons, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800716.summary: This is a dedication to Acius Niv T Saturninus.

EDCS-09801937.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan, burial place, and a wish for the deceased Lollius Palventius.

EDCS-09801938.translation: [---] lived 25 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-09801938.summary: A funerary inscription recording a lifespan and a wish for the deceased.

EDCS-09801939.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Cassia Rogata lived.
EDCS-09801939.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Cassia Rogata, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-09801944.translation: Gaius Julius Victor gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09801944.summary: This inscription records Gaius Julius Victor fulfilling a vow, possibly to a deity.

EDCS-09801945.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription with the name Quintus Petellius Rogatianus, potentially part of a funerary or dedication text.

EDCS-09801946.translation: Sacred to Augustus and the lord, the people of Commessana gladly fulfilled their vow: L. Zabbeus Antonius Martialis, Cornelius Extricatus, Marcus Julius Hilarus, Fallius Rusticus, Publicius Quetus, Antonius Crescento, Sentius Quirinus, Julius Namphamo, Sergius Baricio, Julius Victor, Marcus Caecilius Maturturis, Ponponius Mustivius, A. Africanus. Sacred to the lord Middon Augustus.
EDCS-09801946.summary: This long inscription records a group of individuals from Commessana fulfilling a vow to Augustus and a local lord, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09801947.translation: Sacred to Caelestis Augusta, the state of the Tidditani.
EDCS-09801947.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the community of Tidditani to the goddess Caelestis Augusta.

EDCS-09801948.translation: To Eventus Augustus, Felix, content and with a glad heart, dedicated the gift and also donated it.
EDCS-09801948.summary: This inscription records Felix dedicating a gift to Eventus Augustus, likely a deified emperor, with joy and satisfaction.

EDCS-09801949.translation: Dedicated to Imperial Fortune by Memmius Natalis.
EDCS-09801949.summary: This is a religious dedication to Imperial Fortune by Memmius Natalis.

EDCS-09801950.translation: Quintus Sittius Urbanus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, as aedile and quaestor, built this temple with its statue from the ground up with his own money, and dedicated it.
EDCS-09801950.summary: This is a building dedication by Quintus Sittius Urbanus, who served as aedile and quaestor, for a temple and statue.

EDCS-09801963.translation: For the health of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus, son of the divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the divine Commodus, grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva.
EDCS-09801963.summary: This inscription records a vow for the health of Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus, listing his extensive imperial genealogy.

EDCS-09801965.translation: By decree of the town council, the republic of the Tidditani [dedicated this] for Publius Septimius Geta, son of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia and Adiabene, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 5th time, imperator for the 9th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, father of the country, brother of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, grandson of the divine Marcus Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva.
EDCS-09801965.summary: This inscription, decreed by the town council of the Tidditani, dedicates something to Publius Septimius Geta, detailing his extensive imperial lineage and relationship to Emperor Caracalla and previous emperors.

EDCS-09801968.translation: By public money and by decree of the town council, [this was dedicated] to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, conqueror of Parthia, father of the country.
EDCS-09801968.summary: This inscription records a public dedication, funded by the state and approved by the town council, to Emperor Caracalla.

EDCS-09801970.translation: By the divine indulgence and foresight of our Lords, the Emperors Decius Gallus and Hostilianus Volusianus, Augustus, Marcus Cocceius Anicius Faustus Flavianus, consular governor and member of the XVviri sacris faciundis, curator and patron of the Cirtense colonies, provided water by clearing away the excess earth from the people and by leveling the adjacent mountain that had been exposed, for the well-being of the people.
EDCS-09801970.summary: This inscription details the provision of a water supply by Marcus Cocceius Anicius Faustus Flavianus, a distinguished official, during the reign of Emperors Decius Gallus and Hostilianus Volusianus.

EDCS-09801979.translation: By decree of the town council, [this monument was erected] with collected funds of 150,000 sesterces for Lucius Julius Civilis, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, who served as aedile, quaestor, prefect acting as a IIIvir, IIIvir, prefect of justice for the colony of Sarnia Mileu, twice quinquennial official, perpetual flamen, and prefect of youth of Cirta, to this deserving man.
EDCS-09801979.summary: This inscription records a public monument erected by the town council with collected funds for Lucius Julius Civilis, who held numerous civic and religious offices in Sarnia Mileu and Cirta.

EDCS-09801981.translation: Caius Memmius Rogatus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, as aedile, built this arch with its doors with his own money. Caius Memmius Rogatus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, as aedile, built this arch with its doors with his own money.
EDCS-09801981.summary: This inscription records the construction of an arch with doors by Caius Memmius Rogatus, who served as aedile, and it is a duplication of the same text.

EDCS-09801982.translation: By decree of the town council, his friends erected this for Quintus Voltius Natalis, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, who served as aedile and quaestor, to this deserving man, with collected funds.
EDCS-09801982.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by friends with collected funds for Quintus Voltius Natalis, who served as aedile and quaestor.

EDCS-09801984.translation: Lucius Pescennius Sedatus placed this for his deserving friend Quintus Voltius Maximus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, with his own money, by decree of the town council.
EDCS-09801984.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by Lucius Pescennius Sedatus with his own money for his deserving friend Quintus Voltius Maximus.

EDCS-09801985.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Cluventia Novela, priestess of Ceres.
EDCS-09801985.summary: A funerary dedication for Cluventia Novela, identified as a priestess of Ceres, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09801988.translation: For Luci Caecilius Urbanus, most distinguished boy, son of Sextus Pomponius Bassus, Quintus Cassius Pudentis, Quintus Valerius Bariccio, FOR Luci Amboni, Cons Publi, Restitutus Iulius Crescens, Crescens Stratonis, Phamogenes, and their sons who are listed above: Basilius, Antonius, Nampulus Dardanius, Ianuarius Quintus, Clodius Arulus, Antonius, Caecilius Nampulus.
EDCS-09801988.summary: A dedication for the education or upbringing of a distinguished boy, Lucius Caecilius Urbanus, mentioning his father and several patrons or guardians, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09801989.translation: Namphamo S Marcellus, freedman of Martialis C S, Bu s, freedman of Proclianus Valerius, Messor Martialis, Sallustius Rogatus, Vibius Secularis, Sittius Crescens the Younger, ullus Severus, Ianuarius Crepereius Saturninus.
EDCS-09801989.summary: A list of names, possibly of freedmen or members of a collegium, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09801990.translation: Ulpius Flavianus (VI), Claudius Felix (VI), Scantius M (VI), Eius Maximus (semis), An Cai (VII), Ananius Ar (semis), Sextius Fadianus (VI), Messius Tertianus (VI), Sextius Caecilianus (VI), Coponius Ilarus (VI), Aelius Flavianus (Luc), Herennius Flavius Flavianus (semis), Cef (VII), Iunius (VI), Vettius Rufus (VI), Flavius Donatus (semis), Herennius Potitus M (VI), Gargilius Ulpianus (VIII), Fulvius Iucundus (semis), Herennius Carm (VI), Clodius Net (VI), Ele Fan Bu (VI), Numesius Pocosus (semis), Herennius Antoninus (VI), Popilius Saturninus (VII), Pru fuo Refrius M (VI), Curius S Don (VI), Pulenius Getulicus (VI), Pulenius L (semis), Fulvius Saturninus (VI), Herennius Vinula (VI), Clodius Sabinus (VI), Herennius Sex (VI), Remmius Ianuarius (VI), Sextius Datus (V).
EDCS-09801990.summary: A list of names, possibly associated with a collegium or guild, with numerical notations that might indicate rank, years of service, or contributions, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09801991.translation: [Name illegible] made and dedicated this with his own money.
EDCS-09801991.summary: An inscription recording a dedication made and financed by an unnamed individual, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09801992.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, [name illegible] lived 65 years; she is buried here.

EDCS-09801993.translation: To Lucius, daughter of Saturnina, who lived 95 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09801993.summary: A funerary dedication for Saturnina, daughter of Lucius, stating her age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09801994.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Lucius Aelius Potitus, who lived 61 years.

EDCS-09802021.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Bonavia lived 21 years.
EDCS-09802021.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Bonavia, who lived 21 years.

EDCS-09802023.translation: To the spirits of the departed: C. Iulia lived 51 years.
EDCS-09802023.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the spirits of the departed for C. Iulia, who lived 51 years.

EDCS-09802026.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Caecilius Fortunatus lived 65 years.

EDCS-09802027.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Lucius Caecilius Fortunatus lived 30 years; he is buried here.

EDCS-09802383.translation: Otilius, son of Cinius, his wife Virginia lived for [---] years. May it go well for you, Quirinus.

EDCS-09802384.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Sittius Stephanus, I was one, but for two, I was considered as more, always I fulfilled my duty as long as I lived. My parents, remembering me thus, suddenly, when I was deprived of light, offered this with devotion, because I obeyed, pleased, and was silent towards my parents. He lived for 30 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-09802384.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Sittius Stephanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period, emphasizing his devotion to his parents and his life of obedience and silence.

EDCS-09802387.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Baebius Haelico lived for 55 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-09802388.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Veneria lived for 73 years. May her bones rest well.

EDCS-09802389.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valerius Hilarus lived for [5] years.

EDCS-10701952.translation: Mania Secunda, daughter of Lucius, is laid here. The beloved doll Mania lies here for [---] years. Her father, mourning her himself with sorrow, had brought her forth to the divine light, so that he might grasp hope with hope. Fleeing prematurely, she hid herself in the deep darkness. How much better it would have been for the unfortunate not to be born. She lived for 2 years and 13 days.
EDCS-10701952.summary: A poignant funerary epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period, for a young girl named Mania Secunda, lamenting her premature death and the grief of her father.

EDCS-10701963.translation: To Eucrati, Iunia Baccula, who kept faith, showed modesty, tolerated her husband, advised her sons well. For her deserving wife, Titus Flavius Faedrus [set this up].
EDCS-10701963.summary: A funerary epitaph from the Roman Imperial period dedicated by Titus Flavius Faedrus to his wife Iunia Baccula, praising her virtues.

EDCS-10701965.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Victorinus lived for [---] years.

EDCS-10701967.translation: Gaius Fabius Rogatianus and Gaius Crepereius Victor, priests of the god Saturn Augustus, willingly made their vow.
EDCS-10701967.summary: An inscription from the Roman Imperial period recording a votive offering made by two priests of the god Saturn Augustus.

EDCS-10701968.translation: He placed and dedicated [this] over another act of generosity, under the care of Publius Caecilius Caelianus, the priest of Saturn.
EDCS-10701968.summary: An inscription from the Roman Imperial period mentioning the dedication of a monument or offering by Publius Caecilius Caelianus, a priest of Saturn, in addition to other benefactions.

EDCS-11700029.translation: Domitius Secundus, father of the fatherland, restored the fallen [structure] of the state of Boseth, under Severinus Apronianus, the governor of the province of Numidia.
EDCS-11700029.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a public building in Boseth by a local official, dedicated during the governorship of Severinus Apronianus.

EDCS-11800729.translation: The splendid arch and the arch of Stattius.
EDCS-11800729.summary: This inscription appears to be a title or dedication for two arches, one described as 'splendid' and the other possibly named after Stattius.

EDCS-11800730.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely for a tombstone or a dedication by Marcus Annius Portus.

EDCS-11800732.translation: To the Divine Shades. Luaritana lived 101 years.
EDCS-11800732.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Luaritana, recording her age at death.

EDCS-11800733.translation: Lucia Cornelia Urbanila, daughter of Lucius, lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-11800733.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucia Cornelia Urbanila, recording her age and burial location.

EDCS-11800734.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Severus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-11800735.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Aemilius Novicitus.
EDCS-11800735.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Quintus Aemilius Novicitus.

EDCS-11800736.translation: To the Divine Shades. Manilia lived 101 years.
EDCS-11800736.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Manilia, recording her age at death.

EDCS-11800737.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Valerius Saturninus lived 55 years.
EDCS-11800737.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Valerius Saturninus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-11800738.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Caecilius Maximus lived 30 years.
EDCS-11800738.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Caecilius Maximus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-11800739.translation: Caecilia Nina lived for 55 years; she rests here.
EDCS-11800739.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caecilia Nina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800740.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Quintus Caecillius Baricio lived for 70 years; he rests here.
EDCS-11800740.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Caecillius Baricio, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800741.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Quintus Sittius Galicius lived for 30 years.
EDCS-11800741.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Sittius Galicius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800742.translation: By the authority of the Emperor Caesar Augustus Traianus.
EDCS-11800742.summary: This inscription records an act or decree made under the authority of Emperor Trajan, likely dating to his reign (98-117 AD).

EDCS-11800784.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Aelia Urbana lived 38 years, Aelia Secundula lived 16 years, Barea Marion lived 14 years, Barea Mustula lived 7 years, Temarsas Barei lived 2 years. Temarsas [the father?] made this for his deserving wife and children.
EDCS-11800784.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Temarsas for his wife and children, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100076.translation: To the memory of Quintus Iulius [and] anu; Quintus Iulius Iulitianus [lived/is buried here].
EDCS-12100076.summary: This inscription is a memorial to Quintus Iulius and Quintus Iulius Iulitianus, likely funerary, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100077.translation: Of Iulius Cannulus, lived 55 years, rests here for you.
EDCS-12100077.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Cannulus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100078.translation: Sacred to Augustus: Gaius Iulius Crescens willingly paid his vow and made an altar.
EDCS-12100078.summary: This is a religious dedication by Gaius Iulius Crescens to Augustus, likely dating to the early imperial period.

EDCS-12100084.translation: For the genius of the Bagatensian estate, for the health of Marcus Paccius Victor Rufinus of Maritima and Marcus Paccius Rufinus, the owners, Speratus the steward made this.
EDCS-12100084.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a steward named Speratus for the health of his patrons and the genius of their estate, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100086.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Cominia Blattica, daughter of Caius, lived for 9 years.
EDCS-12100086.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cominia Blattica, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100087.translation: To the Divine Manes. Pomponia Buturaria lived 51 years.
EDCS-12100087.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and age of the deceased, Pomponia Buturaria.

EDCS-12100088.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Icaman lived 115 years. Here.
EDCS-12100088.summary: A funerary epitaph stating the name and extreme age of the deceased, Icaman.

EDCS-12100090.translation: Maxima lived 69 years. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-12100090.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maxima, recording her age and a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-12100092.translation: Marce, may you live well, son of F. C., with your father.
EDCS-12100092.summary: An informal dedication or wish for well-being addressed to Marce, son of F. C.

EDCS-12100094.translation: The cupola of Donatus, son of Filius, made and dedicated for his father, who lived 85 years. Sacred to the Divine Manes.
EDCS-12100094.summary: A funerary monument, possibly a cupola or tomb, dedicated by Donatus to his father who lived 85 years.

EDCS-12100101.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Volumnius Felix lived 39 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100101.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Volumnius Felix, recording his age and a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-12100103.translation: Victory is given by a god.
EDCS-12100103.summary: A short religious inscription proclaiming that victory is divinely bestowed.

EDCS-12100104.translation: To the Divine Manes. Antonia Maxima lived [---] years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the Divine Manes. May you rest.
EDCS-12100104.summary: A funerary epitaph for Antonia Maxima, recording her name and a wish for her peaceful rest, with some missing information.

EDCS-12100107.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and age of the deceased, Quintillius.

EDCS-12100110.translation: Sacred to Mercurius Augustus. Lucius Caesennius Speratus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, made and dedicated the gift which he promised.
EDCS-12100110.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Mercurius Augustus by Lucius Caesennius Speratus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-12100111.translation: To Silvanus, from his own initiative and with a willing spirit, he made the space and dedicated it by decree of the decurions of Asina, as a municipal magistrate.
EDCS-12100111.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Silvanus of a public space by a local official in Asina.

EDCS-12100114.translation: During the consulships of Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the deified, for the eighth time, and Titus Statilius Taurus for the second time, Lucius Iulius Arrenus, as duumvir, assigned lands to the colonists by decree of the decurions, with the main north-south road on the left and the main east-west road on the right, beyond the main north-south road.
EDCS-12100114.summary: This inscription documents the assignment of land to colonists by a duumvir, Lucius Iulius Arrenus, during a specific consular period, likely in the late 1st century BCE or early 1st century CE.

EDCS-12100116.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription identifying Marcus Abius Lucianus.

EDCS-12100117.translation: To the departed spirits, Gaius Aelius.
EDCS-12100117.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, mentioning Gaius Aelius.

EDCS-12100118.translation: Aemilia Quinta, daughter of Lucius, lived for 75 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-12100118.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilia Quinta, stating her parentage, age at death, and burial location.

EDCS-12100119.translation: Aemilia Sequnda, daughter of Marcus, lived for 31 years; buried here.
EDCS-12100119.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilia Sequnda, stating her parentage, age at death, and burial location.

EDCS-12100120.translation: Albinia Maxima, daughter of Lucius, may you rest well; she is buried here, lived for 56 years.
EDCS-12100120.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Albinia Maxima, daughter of Lucius, stating her age at death, burial location, and a wish for her rest.

EDCS-12100121.translation: To the departed spirits, Antonius, son of Lucius.
EDCS-12100121.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, mentioning Antonius, son of Lucius.

EDCS-12100122.translation: To the departed spirits, Lucius Avilius Crescens lived for 105 years.
EDCS-12100122.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Avilius Crescens, stating his age at death and dedicating it to the departed spirits.

EDCS-12100124.translation: May your bones rest well, Quirina Babarus, lived for 61 years.
EDCS-12100124.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quirina Babarus, stating her age at death and offering a wish for her repose.

EDCS-12100125.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Bebia Nampula lived 69 years. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-12100125.summary: A funerary epitaph for Bebia Nampula, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100126.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Caecilius Mustacus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 35 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100126.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Caecilius Mustacus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100127.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. I. Caecilius Pannonius lived 51 years.
EDCS-12100127.summary: A funerary epitaph for I. Caecilius Pannonius, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100128.translation: Caecilius Rogatus lived 81 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12100128.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilius Rogatus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100130.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caecilia Mantina lived [unknown] years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100130.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Mantina, with an incomplete age, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100131.translation: Calpurnia Quartilla lived 25 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100131.summary: A funerary epitaph for Calpurnia Quartilla, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100132.summary: A funerary epitaph for Claudius Botor, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100133.translation: Publius Clodius Extricatus lived 61 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100133.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Clodius Extricatus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100134.translation: Marcus Clodius Proculus lived 40 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12100134.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Clodius Proculus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100135.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Cornelius Ianuarius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 80 years. He is buried here. To the Spirits of the Departed. Aemilia, daughter of Lucius, Amanda.
EDCS-12100135.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Cornelius Ianuarius and Aemilia Amanda, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100136.translation: Quintus Cornelius Mustus lived 51 years. May your bones rest here.
EDCS-12100136.summary: This epitaph records the life and death of Quintus Cornelius Mustus, who lived 51 years.

EDCS-12100139.translation: Hervinia Felicia Nautatis lived 6 years. May her bones rest well here.
EDCS-12100139.summary: This epitaph records the life and death of Hervinia Felicia Nautatis, who died at the age of 6.

EDCS-12100140.translation: Hervinia Lucina lived 55 years. May her bones rest well here.
EDCS-12100140.summary: This epitaph records the life and death of Hervinia Lucina, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-12100141.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. F. Fortis lived 100 years.
EDCS-12100141.summary: This epitaph records the death of F. Fortis, who lived 100 years, dedicated to the spirits of the underworld.

EDCS-12100142.translation: A. Felix lived 23 years. Buried here.
EDCS-12100142.summary: This epitaph records the burial of A. Felix, who lived 23 years.

EDCS-12100143.translation: Lived 65 years. Gabinia Olia, daughter of Tadias.
EDCS-12100143.summary: This epitaph records the life and death of Gabinia Olia, daughter of Tadias, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-12100144.translation: Ivius Geneius, son of Geneius, lived 60 years. Buried here.
EDCS-12100144.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Ivius Geneius, son of Geneius, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-12100145.translation: Caius Helvius Quetus lived 65 years. Buried here.
EDCS-12100145.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Caius Helvius Quetus, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-12100146.translation: In memory of Iulianus, who lived one year and five months. Herenius Maximus made (this) for his son.
EDCS-12100146.summary: This epitaph records the death of Iulianus, who lived one year and five months, and was made by his father, Herenius Maximus.

EDCS-12100147.translation: Herenius, son of Macrinus, lived 96 years.
EDCS-12100147.summary: This epitaph records the life and death of Herenius, son of Macrinus, who lived 96 years.

EDCS-12100149.translation: To the departed spirits. Omullius Felix made [this] for his dearest daughter Omullia Matror, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-12100149.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and death of Omullia Matror, dedicated by her father Omullius Felix.

EDCS-12100150.translation: Publius Hosidius Faustus, son of Lucius, lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12100150.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and burial of Publius Hosidius Faustus.

EDCS-12100151.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary epitaph or dedication, naming Tranquil Iulius Ex.

EDCS-12100153.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Honoratus Egregius, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, who lived 33 years, 3 months, and 22 days. Calpurnia Castula [placed this] for her husband; he is buried here, may his bones rest well.
EDCS-12100153.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and death of Caius Iulius Honoratus Egregius, erected by his wife Calpurnia Castula.

EDCS-12100154.translation: Lucius Iulius, son of Lucius, lived 100 years; he is buried here, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100154.summary: This funerary epitaph records the exceptionally long life and burial of Lucius Iulius.

EDCS-12100155.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Iulius Martialis, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 61 years; may his bones [rest well]. Iulia Optata lived 80 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100155.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lives and deaths of two individuals, Lucius Iulius Martialis and Iulia Optata.

EDCS-12100156.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Iulius Pistor lived.
EDCS-12100156.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely for Lucius Iulius Pistor, beginning with a dedication to the departed spirits.

EDCS-12100157.translation: Caius Iulius Postimus lived 9 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12100157.summary: This funerary epitaph records the short life and burial of Caius Iulius Postimus.

EDCS-12100159.translation: May your bones rest well. To the departed spirits. Iulia Gallica, daughter of Marcus, lived 50 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-12100159.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and burial of Iulia Gallica, with a standard invocation for the repose of the dead.

EDCS-12100160.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Luciosa lived 50 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-12100160.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and burial of Iulia Luciosa, with a dedication to the departed spirits.

EDCS-12100161.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia Regilla lived for 31 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-12100161.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and death of Iulia Regilla, who lived to be 31 years old.

EDCS-12100162.translation: Lived for 25 years. Her bones are laid here. May they rest well for you. To the Divine Shades, Iulia.
EDCS-12100162.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Iulia, who lived for 25 years and whose bones are interred here.

EDCS-12100163.translation: Iulia lived for 10 years. Her bones are laid here. May they rest well for you.
EDCS-12100163.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Iulia, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-12100164.translation: Marcus Manlius, son of Lochias, lived for 20 years.
EDCS-12100164.summary: This epitaph records the life of Marcus Manlius, son of Lochias, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-12100165.translation: To the Divine Shades. I Maximus lived for 120 years. His bones are laid here. Cornelia [lived] 80 [years].
EDCS-12100165.summary: This funerary inscription honors I Maximus, who lived to 120 years old, and also mentions Cornelia, who lived to 80.

EDCS-12100166.translation: Maximus lived for 55 years. His bones are laid here.
EDCS-12100166.summary: This epitaph records the life of Maximus, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-12100167.translation: To the Divine Shades. Of Caius Munatius Victor and Antonia Maxima, my wife, always well both in life and death.
EDCS-12100167.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Caius Munatius Victor and his wife Antonia Maxima, emphasizing their enduring relationship.

EDCS-12100168.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Munatius Victor of the Quirina tribe lived for 85 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-12100168.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Quintus Munatius Victor, a member of the Quirina tribe, who lived to be 85.

EDCS-12100170.translation: Gaius Occius Basillus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 21 years. His bones are laid here. May they rest well for you.
EDCS-12100170.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Gaius Occius Basillus, son of Marcus, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-12100171.translation: To the Divine Shades. Octavia, daughter of Caius, of the Fr[ --- ] tribe, lived for 60 years. Her bones are laid here.
EDCS-12100171.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Octavia, daughter of Caius, who lived to be 60 years old.

EDCS-12100174.translation: Optatila Celeris, daughter of Balienis, lived 35 years; she lies buried here.
EDCS-12100174.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Optatila Celeris, recording her name, parentage, and lifespan.

EDCS-12100175.translation: Lucius Peducaius Neptunalis lived 55 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-12100175.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Peducaius Neptunalis, recording his name and lifespan.

EDCS-12100176.translation: Pompeius Felix lived 55 years; he is laid here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100176.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Pompeius Felix, recording his name, lifespan, and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-12100177.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Ponponia.
EDCS-12100177.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes for Ponponia.

EDCS-12100178.summary: This is a tombstone inscription fragment for Pomponia, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-12100179.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Quintus Rogatus.
EDCS-12100179.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes for Quintus Rogatus.

EDCS-12100180.summary: This is a tombstone inscription identifying Sextus Salvidenius Silvanus, likely deceased.

EDCS-12100181.translation: Salustia Iuliana lived 61 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-12100181.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Salustia Iuliana, recording her name and lifespan.

EDCS-12100182.translation: Salustia Vitalis lived 21 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-12100182.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Salustia Vitalis, recording her name and lifespan.

EDCS-12100183.translation: Sasname, daughter of Arisim, lived 51 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-12100183.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Sasname, recording her name, parentage, and lifespan.

EDCS-12100184.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Scantilius Galus lived 103 years. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-12100184.summary: This funerary inscription honors Scantilius Galus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-12100185.translation: Caius Servilius Pudens lived 61 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100185.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Caius Servilius Pudens at his death and burial.

EDCS-12100186.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sittia Donata, freedwoman of Publius, lived 100 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100186.summary: This funerary inscription honors Sittia Donata, a freedwoman, recording her age and burial.

EDCS-12100187.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sitia Maxs lived 50 years.
EDCS-12100187.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Sitia Maxs at her death.

EDCS-12100188.translation: Soda, son of V- Cirta, lived 100 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12100188.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Soda at his death and burial.

EDCS-12100189.translation: Sister of Gallus Balonius, daughter, lived 25 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12100189.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of a woman identified by her relation to Gallus Balonius and her burial.

EDCS-12100190.translation: Suettia Quirina Procula, daughter of Lucius, lived 60 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100190.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Suettia Quirina Procula at her death.

EDCS-12100191.translation: Tabia Saberalis, daughter, lived 71 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12100191.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Tabia Saberalis at her death and burial.

EDCS-12100192.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Of Quintus Tanonius Nini, lived 80 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100192.summary: This funerary inscription honors Quintus Tanonius Nini, recording his age at death.

EDCS-12100193.translation: To the memory of Tannonia Faustina. Caius Iulius Honoratus Egregius lived 40 years for his incomparable sister.
EDCS-12100193.summary: This inscription is a memorial set up by Caius Iulius Honoratus Egregius for his sister Tannonia Faustina, recording her age at death.

EDCS-12100194.translation: Tannonia Tue lived for 7 years; may your bones rest well here.
EDCS-12100194.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a young girl named Tannonia Tue, who died at the age of 7.

EDCS-12100195.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Titenia Secunda lived for 85 years; she rests here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100195.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titenia Secunda, who lived to be 85 years old.

EDCS-12100196.translation: Quintus Tongius Pistor lived for 33 years and 5 days; he rests here.
EDCS-12100196.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quintus Tongius Pistor, who died at the age of 33.

EDCS-12100197.translation: Valeria Optata lived for 55 years; she rests here.
EDCS-12100197.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Valeria Optata, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-12100198.translation: Volumnius Sodalis lived for 15 years; may her bones rest well.
EDCS-12100198.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Volumnius Sodalis, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-12100199.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Volumnia Ingenua, who died at the age of 31.

EDCS-12100200.translation: Volumnia Quinta, daughter of Lucius, lived for 15 years; she rests here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100200.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Volumnia Quinta, daughter of Lucius, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-12100201.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Volumnia Tertulla, who lived for 65 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100201.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Volumnia Tertulla, who died at the age of 65.

EDCS-12100202.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Dia V Sana lived for 55 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100202.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dia V Sana, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-12100203.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Urbana lived for 15 years; she rests here.
EDCS-12100203.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Urbana, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-12100204.translation: He lived 6 years. Opsius placed this for the dearest Us. (This is a funerary dedication for a child.)
EDCS-12100204.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of a child named Us at the age of 6, dedicated by Opsius.

EDCS-12100205.translation: Artia Iavia lived 87 years; she lies here.
EDCS-12100205.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Artia Iavia, who lived to be 87 years old.

EDCS-12100208.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording a person's death at the age of 80.

EDCS-12100209.translation: HVSC lived 75 years; he lies here.
EDCS-12100209.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording that HVSC lived for 75 years and is buried here.

EDCS-12100211.translation: nus lived 61 years; he lies here.
EDCS-12100211.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for 'nus', recording his age at death as 61 and his burial location.

EDCS-12100213.translation: C A V H D I V E Q er lived 45 years; he lies here.
EDCS-12100213.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of C A V H D I V E Q er at the age of 45 and his burial.

EDCS-12100214.translation: Marcus Pellartius lived 41 years; may his bones rest well.
EDCS-12100214.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Pellartius, who lived 41 years, with a wish for his bones to rest well.

EDCS-12100215.translation: M A son lived 35 years; he lies here.
EDCS-12100215.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the son of M. A., who lived 35 years and is buried here.

EDCS-12100216.translation: is lived 35 years; he lies here.
EDCS-12100216.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording that 'is' lived for 35 years and is buried here.

EDCS-12100217.translation: s lived 35 years; he lies here.
EDCS-12100217.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording that 's' lived for 35 years and is buried here.

EDCS-12100220.translation: Triccius lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12100220.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age at death of Triccius.

EDCS-12100221.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age at death of an individual identified only by initials.

EDCS-12100223.translation: Cavn, son of Cavn, lived [X] years.
EDCS-12100223.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age at death of Cavn, with the age itself missing.

EDCS-12100225.translation: In the name of the Lord, these have been placed in this holy place, the goods of Fortunatianus the bishop, for the benefit of the resurrection, together with nine fathers, and while Heraclius and Oraclius governed the African province.
EDCS-12100225.summary: This is a funerary inscription concerning church property and its dedication for the resurrection, mentioning a bishop and provincial governors.

EDCS-12100227.translation: To the departed spirits. Sextus Fulvius Marianus lived 80 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100227.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age at death of Sextus Fulvius Marianus and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-12100228.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Caecilius Crispinus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12100228.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, tribe, age at death, and burial of Lucius Caecilius Crispinus.

EDCS-12100229.translation: Felix Nibilis, son of Felix, chief of the suburban Regians, lived 85 years.
EDCS-12100229.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, parentage, title, and age at death of Felix Nibilis.

EDCS-12100230.translation: By the authority of the Emperor Vespasian Caesar Augustus, public lands of the Cirta region have been assigned to the suburban Regians and Nicibes by Tullius Pomponianus Capito, the Emperor's legate.
EDCS-12100230.summary: This is an official inscription recording the assignment of public lands by imperial authority under Emperor Vespasian.

EDCS-12100231.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Considia Iulia lived healthily for 80 years.
EDCS-12100231.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and lifespan of Considia Iulia.

EDCS-12100232.translation: To our lord and father, we made this memorial, Canissus and Donatus.
EDCS-12100232.summary: This is a dedication inscription by Canissus and Donatus to a paternal figure.

EDCS-12100233.translation: To the memory of the most blessed martyrs, the birthday of Renatus, the Ides of January, anno ante adventum.
EDCS-12100233.summary: This inscription records a memorial dedicated to martyrs on a specific date.

EDCS-12100234.translation: Ovinia Fructuosa lived 30 years. Marcus Ovinius Longinus, her son, lived 6 years. Ovinius Modestus.
EDCS-12100234.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of Ovinia Fructuosa and her son Ovinius Modestus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100235.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius S. Ianuaris lived 70 years.
EDCS-12100235.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating the tomb to the Manes for Lucius S. Ianuaris, who lived 70 years.

EDCS-12100236.translation: To Mercury Augustus, sacred. Sextus Flavius Sabinianus, a Roman knight who held all offices, an augur, gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-12100236.summary: This inscription records a vow paid to Mercury Augustus by Sextus Flavius Sabinianus, a Roman knight and augur.

EDCS-12100237.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Sallustius Quetus lived 55 years. Maro, his son, made this for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-12100237.summary: This funerary inscription states that Maro dedicated this monument to his father, Quintus Sallustius Quetus, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-12100238.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Sallustius Victor lived 47 years.
EDCS-12100238.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Caius Sallustius Victor, who lived 47 years.

EDCS-12100240.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Hostilius Conconius from Dacia.
EDCS-12100240.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Marcus Hostilius Conconius, originating from Dacia.
