EDCS-12100241.translation: Aurellia Rogata lived 60 years. Sacred to the departed spirits.
EDCS-12100241.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Aurellia Rogata, who lived 60 years, and is dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-12100242.summary: This inscription records the age of Donatus, who lived 65 years, with a partially preserved dedication.

EDCS-12100243.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Patri, son of Pauli, son of Marini, son of Mamari. Victorina and Datuliani and Maura and Luciani.
EDCS-12100243.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to the Manes for Patri, with a list of other individuals, likely family members or associated persons.

EDCS-12100244.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aperonius Firindas made this funeral monument for his wife Cecilia Fortunata, who lived 57 years.
EDCS-12100244.summary: This funerary inscription records the monument made by Aperonius Firindas for his wife Cecilia Fortunata.

EDCS-12100245.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Donatus Victorinus and Honoratus Victor made this.
EDCS-12100245.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Donatus Victorinus and Honoratus Victor.

EDCS-12100246.translation: Sittius and Egnatius, his sons, made this.
EDCS-12100246.summary: This funerary inscription records the monument made by Sittius and Egnatius, his sons.

EDCS-12100248.translation: Iulia Monul, daughter of Iulius, of the Quirina tribe, Nigidia, lived 38 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-12100248.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Iulia Monul, who lived 38 years.

EDCS-12100249.translation: Marcus Lucilius Reburrus lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12100249.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Lucilius Reburrus, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-12100251.translation: Kalpurnia Ammia lived 60 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-12100251.summary: This funerary inscription records Kalpurnia Ammia, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-12100252.translation: [...] lived 65 years; may your bones rest well. Iulia Marcellina, daughter of Publius, lived 45 years; may your bones rest well. Caius Iulius Kalpurnianus lived 18 years; he is buried here; may your bones rest well. Publius Iulius Faustus Timon lived 20 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12100252.summary: This funerary inscription records the burials of Caius Iulius Kalpurnianus and Publius Iulius Faustus Timon, and the lifespans of Iulia Marcellina and another individual.

EDCS-12200310.translation: Lucius Iulius Crispinus lived 65 years; he is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-12200310.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Iulius Crispinus, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-12200311.translation: In memory of Marcus Iulius Iucundus, who lived 65 years.
EDCS-12200311.summary: This funerary inscription records the memory of Marcus Iulius Iucundus, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-12200312.translation: Publius Iulius Severus lived 60 years; he is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-12200312.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Publius Iulius Severus, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-12200313.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Ovinius Martialis lived 100 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12200313.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Publius Ovinius Martialis.

EDCS-12200318.translation: To the good, well; to the bad, badly. Dionisia, may you live! You who made such buildings, use them happily.
EDCS-12200318.summary: An inscription on a building by Dionisia, wishing well to good users and ill to bad users.

EDCS-12200319.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Primigenius lived 81 years.
EDCS-12200319.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Primigenius.

EDCS-12200320.translation: The names of the most blessed martyrs Crescentianus, Rogatus, Paulus, Nonas, November, Felix, Fortunatus.
EDCS-12200320.summary: A list of names of Christian martyrs, likely from a commemorative monument or calendar.

EDCS-12200321.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aufidius Optatus lived 95 years. Aufidius Arator and his mother made this for their father who deserved well. He is buried here.
EDCS-12200321.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Aufidius Optatus, erected by his son and wife, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-12200322.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Kyrilla, wife, lived 18 years. Philopaizon made this.
EDCS-12200322.summary: A funerary inscription for Kyrilla, erected by her husband Philopaizon, recording her age and marital status.

EDCS-12200323.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Namgur lived 75 years.
EDCS-12200323.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Iulius Namgur.

EDCS-12200324.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Burucia lived 20 years.
EDCS-12200324.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Iulia Burucia.

EDCS-12200325.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Minutus Castus lived 70 years.
EDCS-12200325.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Minutus Castus.

EDCS-12200326.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Manilia Vera lived 5 years and some months.
EDCS-12200326.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Manilia Vera, a young child.

EDCS-12200327.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Saturnus Augustus and the Genius of the Poctanensis estate of Posphorianus; Quintus Antistius Agathopus willingly paid his vow for the well-being of the Antistii.
EDCS-12200327.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Quintus Antistius Agathopus to Jupiter Saturnus Augustus and the Genius of a local estate, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12200328.translation: Sacred to the August Minerva; Quintus Antistius Agathopus willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-12200328.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Quintus Antistius Agathopus to Minerva Augusta, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12200354.translation: To the departed spirits; Acilius Servilianus lived 35 years.
EDCS-12200354.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death of Acilius Servilianus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12200355.translation: To the departed spirits; Caius Acilius Salaminus lived 81 years, he lies here.
EDCS-12200355.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death and burial place of Caius Acilius Salaminus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12200356.translation: Lucius Aemilius Victor lived 40 and a half years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-12200356.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death of Lucius Aemilius Victor, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12200357.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits; Aemilia Anucla lived 85 years.
EDCS-12200357.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death of Aemilia Anucla, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12200358.translation: To the departed spirits; Marcus Anicius Clemens lived 16 years.
EDCS-12200358.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death of Marcus Anicius Clemens, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12200359.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits; Anicia Honorata lived 75 years.
EDCS-12200359.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death of Anicia Honorata, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12200360.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits; Anicia Maxima lived 50 years.
EDCS-12200360.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death of Anicia Maxima, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12200361.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits; Titus Antonius Fortunatus lived.
EDCS-12200361.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Titus Antonius Fortunatus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12200362.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Antonius Receptus lived 59 years.
EDCS-12200362.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Antonius Receptus, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-12200363.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Tonia Malceta lived 70 years.
EDCS-12200363.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tonia Malceta, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-12200364.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Antonia Margarita lived 40 years.
EDCS-12200364.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Margarita, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-12200365.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Atilius Victor lived 45 years.
EDCS-12200365.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Atilius Victor, recording his name and age at death, possibly indicating a premature death given the phrasing 'sacred to'.

EDCS-12200366.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Quintus Au Matunus, while alive, lived 80 years.
EDCS-12200366.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Au Matunus, unusual for recording his age at death while still alive, suggesting he may have commissioned his own tombstone.

EDCS-12200367.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aufidia Maxima lived 65 years.
EDCS-12200367.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aufidia Maxima, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-12200368.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Aurelius Ianuarius lived [---] years.
EDCS-12200368.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelius Ianuarius, recording his name and indicating his age at death, though the exact duration is lost.

EDCS-12200369.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caelia Quinta, highlighting her piety and recording her exceptional lifespan of 100 years.

EDCS-12200370.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Callurnius Felix lived 25 years.
EDCS-12200370.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Callurnius Felix, recording his name and age at death, suggesting a young demise.

EDCS-12200371.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Calventius lived [---].
EDCS-12200371.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Calventius, recording his name and intended dedication, but missing the specific details of his life or death.

EDCS-12200372.translation: To the divine spirits, Kalvisius Favillarius lived 40 years.
EDCS-12200372.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased, Kalvisius Favillarius.

EDCS-12200374.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits, Cornelius Extricatus lived 51 years.
EDCS-12200374.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased, Cornelius Extricatus.

EDCS-12200375.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased, Dativus.

EDCS-12200376.translation: To the divine spirits, Extricata lived 35 years.
EDCS-12200376.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased, Extricata.

EDCS-12200377.translation: To the divine spirits, Fabia Honorata lived 60 years.
EDCS-12200377.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased, Fabia Honorata.

EDCS-12200378.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits, Caius Homulius Victor lived 40 years.
EDCS-12200378.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased, Caius Homulius Victor.

EDCS-12200379.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits, Caius Muinlis Ianuarius lived 55 years.
EDCS-12200379.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased, Caius Muinlis Ianuarius.

EDCS-12200380.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased, Ianuaria.

EDCS-12300143.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits, Marcus Iulius Urbanus lived 27 years.
EDCS-12300143.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased, Marcus Iulius Urbanus.

EDCS-12300144.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits, Caius Iulius [---]
EDCS-12300144.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph mentioning Caius Iulius.

EDCS-12300145.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Arator lived 57 years.
EDCS-12300145.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death of Iulius Arator.

EDCS-12300146.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Celsus lived 25 years.
EDCS-12300146.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death of Iulius Celsus.

EDCS-12300147.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Iulius Extricatus lived 75 years.
EDCS-12300147.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death of Lucius Iulius Extricatus.

EDCS-12300148.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Iulius Gemellus lived 25 years.
EDCS-12300148.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death of Quintus Iulius Gemellus.

EDCS-12300149.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Lucecrinus lived 60 years.
EDCS-12300149.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death of Caius Iulius Lucecrinus.

EDCS-12300150.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Martis lived 75 years.
EDCS-12300150.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death of Iulius Martis. The repetition of 'vixit' is likely a scribal error.

EDCS-12300151.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Iulius Maximinus lived 56 years.
EDCS-12300151.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death of Marcus Iulius Maximinus.

EDCS-12300152.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Proclianus lived 10 years.
EDCS-12300152.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death of Caius Iulius Proclianus.

EDCS-12300153.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Iulius Saturninus lived 65 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12300153.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death and burial location of Quintus Iulius Saturninus.

EDCS-12300154.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Saturus, aged 50.
EDCS-12300154.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age at death of Iulius Saturus.

EDCS-12300155.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucia Iulia Fortun lived 40 years.
EDCS-12300155.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucia Iulia Fortun, recording her death at age 40.

EDCS-12300156.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Honorata lived 31 years.
EDCS-12300156.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Honorata, recording her death at age 31.

EDCS-12300157.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Honorata lived 17 years. Anicia Victoria lived [---] years.
EDCS-12300157.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages at death for Iulia Honorata (17 years) and partially for Anicia Victoria.

EDCS-12300158.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Nina lived 15 [or 25?] years.
EDCS-12300158.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age at death for Iulia Nina, possibly 15 or 25 years.

EDCS-12300159.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Sambuca [lived] 65 years.
EDCS-12300159.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age at death for Iulia Sambuca, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-12300160.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Silvana lived 55 years.
EDCS-12300160.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Silvana, recording her death at age 55.

EDCS-12300161.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Urbana lived 60 years.
EDCS-12300161.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Urbana, recording her death at age 60.

EDCS-12300162.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Larinius Gemellus lived 95 years.
EDCS-12300162.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Larinius Gemellus, recording his death at age 95.

EDCS-12300163.translation: To the departed spirits. Maria Rustica lived 80 years.
EDCS-12300163.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maria Rustica, recording her death at age 80.

EDCS-12300164.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Pomponius Saturninus lived 80 years.
EDCS-12300164.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Pomponius Saturninus, recording his death at age 80.

EDCS-12300165.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Julia Urbana, daughter of S. Iulius, lived for [some number of] years.
EDCS-12300165.summary: This epitaph records the life span of Julia Urbana.

EDCS-12300166.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Sittius Baline lived for 70 years and is buried here.
EDCS-12300166.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Gaius Sittius Baline, who lived to 70 years old.

EDCS-12300167.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sittius Catullus lived for 95 years and is buried here.
EDCS-12300167.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Sittius Catullus, who lived to 95 years old.

EDCS-12300168.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sittius Martialis lived for 85 years.
EDCS-12300168.summary: This epitaph records the life span of Sittius Martialis, who lived to 85 years old.

EDCS-12300169.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Sittius Messor lived for 50 years and is buried here.
EDCS-12300169.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Sittius Messor, who lived to 50 years old.

EDCS-12300170.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Sittia Maxima lived for 50 years.
EDCS-12300170.summary: This epitaph records the life span of Sittia Maxima, who lived to 50 years old.

EDCS-12300171.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sittia Victoria lived for 85 years and is buried here.
EDCS-12300171.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Sittia Victoria, who lived to 85 years old.

EDCS-12300172.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vettia Tertullina lived for 75 years. May your bones be well [placed].
EDCS-12300172.summary: This epitaph records the life span of Vettia Tertullina, who lived to 75 years old, with a final wish for her repose.

EDCS-12300173.translation: Marcus Vinicius Niger lived for 105 years.
EDCS-12300173.summary: This epitaph records the life span of Marcus Vinicius Niger, who lived to 105 years old.

EDCS-12300174.summary: This epitaph records the life span of a person whose name is partially lost, who lived to 110 years old.

EDCS-12300175.translation: To Iustia, who lived 51 years. Drink and do not neglect.
EDCS-12300175.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Iustia, recording her age at death.

EDCS-12300180.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Antistius Asclepiades, to the most dutiful Dionidius Ciopius, lived 55 years, is buried here. Quintus Antistius Asclepiades lived 31 years, is buried here.
EDCS-12300180.summary: This is a double funerary epitaph from a family tomb, recording the ages at death for Dionidius Ciopius and Quintus Antistius Asclepiades.

EDCS-12300181.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Iulius Quadratus lived [X?] years.
EDCS-12300181.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Caius Iulius Quadratus.

EDCS-12300183.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Mesius Primus lived 100 years.
EDCS-12300183.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Mesius Primus.

EDCS-12300184.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Helvius Isperatus lived 45 years.
EDCS-12300184.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Helvius Isperatus.

EDCS-12300185.translation: To the Divine Shades. S. Nigrosi lived 101 years.
EDCS-12300185.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of S. Nigrosi.

EDCS-12300186.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius S. Baluzim lived 45 years.
EDCS-12300186.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Publius S. Baluzim.

EDCS-12300187.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sitia Macrina lived 61 years.
EDCS-12300187.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Sitia Macrina.

EDCS-12300188.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Viabula lived [age/years?]...
EDCS-12300188.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Viabula, with the text unfortunately incomplete.

EDCS-12300189.translation: To the divine spirits. In the consulship of Faustinus and Rufinus, on the day before the Kalends of April, Marcus Baebius Felix Quintillus and Marcus Cominius Maximus, the magistrates of the Thibilitani, [dedicated this] to Bacius Augustus.
EDCS-12300189.summary: This is a dedication to the imperial cult from the Thibilitani region, dated by the consuls Faustinus and Rufinus, and naming local magistrates.

EDCS-12300191.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus. On the Kalends of April, under the consulship of Duobus Aspris, Marcus Egrilius Saturninus and Quintus Manilius Calosus, masters of the Thibilitani.
EDCS-12300191.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus by the magistri of the Thibilitani during the consulship of Duobus Aspris.

EDCS-12300193.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus. On the day before the Kalends of April, under the consulship of Messalla and Aemilianus, Quintus Manilius Arator and Publius Memmius Castus, masters of the Thibilitani, Caius Alfenus Iustus the Younger.
EDCS-12300193.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus by the magistri of the Thibilitani, including Caius Alfenus Iustus Iunior, during the consulship of Messalla and Aemilianus.

EDCS-12300195.translation: In the presence of Praesens and Extricatus, consuls. Sacred to Bacacus Augustus. Lucius Iulius Felix and Quintus R Philippus, masters of the Thibilitani, fulfilled their vow willingly.
EDCS-12300195.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Bacacus Augustus by Lucius Iulius Felix and Quintus R Philippus, magistri of the Thibilitani, during the consulship of Praesens and Extricatus.

EDCS-12300196.translation: On the day before the Kalends of April, under the consulship of Maccarinus and Adventus, sacred to Bacacus Augustus. Marcus Manilius Augustalis Loquentius and Iulius Rusticinus, masters of the Thibilitani.
EDCS-12300196.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus by Marcus Manilius Augustalis Loquentius and Iulius Rusticinus, magistri of the Thibilitani, during the consulship of Maccarinus and Adventus.

EDCS-12300197.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus. On the day before the Kalends of April, under the consulship of Gratus and Seleucus, Caius Iulius Frontonianus and Titus Seius Pudens, twice masters of the Thibilitani.
EDCS-12300197.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus by Caius Iulius Frontonianus and Titus Seius Pudens, who served twice as magistri of the Thibilitani, during the consulship of Gratus and Seleucus.

EDCS-12300199.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus. On the Kalends of May, under the consulship of Maximus and Aelianus. L. Antistianus and Lucius Iulius Pa Statianus, VII.
EDCS-12300199.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus by L. Antistianus and Lucius Iulius Pa Statianus during the consulship of Maximus and Aelianus.

EDCS-12300201.translation: Under Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus and Marcellus II, consuls. Publius Labius, Lucius Naevius Vitalus, Caecilius Maximus, masters of the Thibilitani.
EDCS-12300201.summary: This inscription records the magistri of the Thibilitani, including Publius Labius, Lucius Naevius Vitalus, and Caecilius Maximus, during the consulship of Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus and Marcellus II.

EDCS-12300202.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus. On the Kalends of May, under the consulship of Albinus and Maximus. Sittius Novellus and Quintus Galerius Mustianus, masters of the Thibilitani.
EDCS-12300202.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus by Sittius Novellus and Quintus Galerius Mustianus, magistri of the Thibilitani, during the consulship of Albinus and Maximus.

EDCS-12300203.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus. Under the consulship of Alexander and Dionysius. Lucius Antonius Victor, perpetual flamen, and Caecilius, masters of the Thibilitani.
EDCS-12300203.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus by Lucius Antonius Victor, perpetual flamen, and Caecilius, magistri of the Thibilitani, during the consulship of Alexander and Dionysius.

EDCS-12300204.translation: Under Emperor our Lord Severus Alexander Augustus for the third time and Dionysius, consuls. Lucius Iulius M pes and Marcus Ulpius X, masters of the Thibilitani.
EDCS-12300204.summary: This inscription records the magistri of the Thibilitani, Lucius Iulius M pes and Marcus Ulpius X, during the third consulship of Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus and Dionysius.

EDCS-12300205.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus, on the day before the Kalends of Sextilis, when Pompeianus and Bo were consuls. [Signed by] Publius Flavius Libosus.
EDCS-12300205.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus, dated by the consulship of Pompeianus and Bo.

EDCS-12500151.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus, on the day before the Kalends of April, when Lupius and Maximus were consuls. [Signed by] Marcus Erucius Maximus Novelus Successus.
EDCS-12500151.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus, dated by the consulship of Lupius and Maximus.

EDCS-12500153.translation: Under our lord Emperor Maximius, sacred to Bacacus Augustus. [Signed by] Iunior Buturi and Iulius Ianuarius, magistrates of the Thibilitani.
EDCS-12500153.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus by the magistrates of the Thibilitani, under Emperor Maximius.

EDCS-12500155.translation: Under our lord Emperor Maximius for the third time and Africanus, on the Kalends of May. [Signed by] the perpetual priest and the magistrates of the Thibilitani.
EDCS-12500155.summary: This inscription records the tenure of a perpetual priest and magistrates of the Thibilitani, dated by the consulship of Emperor Maximius and Africanus.

EDCS-12500156.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus, on the day before the Kalends of April, when Perpetuus and Cornelius were consuls. [Signed by] Quintus Sittius Solutor and Felix, magistrates of the Thibilitani.
EDCS-12500156.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus by the magistrates of the Thibilitani, dated by the consulship of Perpetuus and Cornelius.

EDCS-12500157.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus, under our lord Emperor Gordian Augustus for the second time, when Pompeianus was consul, on the Kalends of May. [Signed by] Sextus A Feliciano, Quintus Iulius Victor.
EDCS-12500157.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus, dated by the consulship of Emperor Gordian and Pompeianus.

EDCS-12500158.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus, when Peregrinus and Aemilianus were consuls, on the day before the Kalends of May. [Signed by] Caius Iulius Rutilius Augustalis, magistrate of the Dotenses.
EDCS-12500158.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus by the magistrate of the Dotenses, dated by the consulship of Peregrinus and Aemilianus.

EDCS-12500159.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus, when Peregrinus and Aemilianus were consuls. [Signed by] Quintus Minucius Novianus and Iulianus Servilianus, magistrates of the Thibilitani.
EDCS-12500159.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus by the magistrates of the Thibilitani, dated by the consulship of Peregrinus and Aemilianus.

EDCS-12500160.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus, under our lord Emperor Philip and Titianus, on the Kalends of May. [Signed by] Lucius Gelius Rogatus, quinquennial magistrate of the Dotenses.
EDCS-12500160.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus by the quinquennial magistrate of the Dotenses, dated by the consulship of Emperors Philip and Titianus.

EDCS-12500161.translation: When Aemilianus and Aquilinus were consuls. [Signed by] Marcus Cominius Paternus.
EDCS-12500161.summary: This inscription records a private dedication or statement, dated by the consulship of Aemilianus and Aquilinus.

EDCS-12500162.translation: Sacred to Bacacus, during the consulship of Trajan and Gratus, on the second day before the Kalends of April; Pacuvius Amicus Lucius Optatus, freedman of Titus, the Augustan.
EDCS-12500162.summary: This dedication to Bacacus was made by Pacuvius Amicus Lucius Optatus during the consulship of Trajan and Gratus, likely in the early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12500163.translation: During the consulship of Gallus and Volusianus, on the day before the Kalends of April; Caius, son of Appius, Ianuarius, magistrate of the Thibilis.
EDCS-12500163.summary: This inscription records Caius Ianuarius, a magistrate of Thibilis, serving during the consulship of Gallus and Volusianus, in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12500165.translation: Sacred to Bacacus, during the second consulship of Valerian and the consulship of Galenus, on the sixth day before the Kalends of May; Vo Felix fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-12500165.summary: Vo Felix fulfilled a vow to Bacacus during the second consulship of Valerian and the consulship of Galenus, likely around 255 CE.

EDCS-12500166.translation: During the consulship of Valerian and Gallienus, the magistrates of Thibilis, Navrius and Exstricatus, fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-12500166.summary: The magistrates of Thibilis, Navrius and Exstricatus, fulfilled a vow during the joint consulship of Valerian and Gallienus, likely around 255 CE.

EDCS-12500167.translation: Sacred to Bacacus, during the consulship of Aemilianus and Castus, on the day before the Kalends of May; Quintus Licinius Neptunalis and Lucius Popinius Iustus.
EDCS-12500167.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus by Quintus Licinius Neptunalis and Lucius Popinius Iustus during the consulship of Aemilianus and Castus, likely in the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-12500168.translation: Sacred to Bacacus, during the consulship of Paternus and Marinianus, Caius Iulius Ianuarius.
EDCS-12500168.summary: Caius Iulius Ianuarius made a dedication to Bacacus during the consulship of Paternus and Marinianus, likely in the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12500170.translation: During the consulship of Aurelianus and Capitolinus, Iulius Urbanus with his colleague Iulius Iunior ... Restututus, the official...
EDCS-12500170.summary: This fragmented inscription records Iulius Urbanus and his colleague Iulius Iunior during the consulship of Aurelianus and Capitolinus, likely in the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-12500171.translation: To Probus Augustus, on the day before the Kalends of April; Marcus Ulpius Romanus Maximus, sacred to Bacacus, from the COA [consular administrative body?].
EDCS-12500171.summary: This dedication to Probus Augustus and Bacacus was made by Marcus Ulpius Romanus Maximus during the reign of Probus, likely in the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-12500172.translation: During the consulship of Carinus and Numerianus, Quintus Vibius and Lucius Hordeonius Claudius, magistrates of the Thibilis, [dedicated to?] Bacacus, the Augustan.
EDCS-12500172.summary: Quintus Vibius and Lucius Hordeonius Claudius, magistrates of Thibilis, made a dedication to Bacacus during the consulship of Carinus and Numerianus, likely in the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-12500173.translation: During the imperial consulship of Caesar Numerian and Severus, Aulus Sittius concerning fields...
EDCS-12500173.summary: This fragmented inscription mentions Aulus Sittius and relates to fields, during the imperial consulship of Numerian and Severus, likely in the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-12500174.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus. In the consulship of Saup (and) Postuma, on the Calends of May, the Ides (of April). Lucius Ortorius Rufus, magistrate of the Thibilitani.
EDCS-12500174.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus made by Lucius Ortorius Rufus, magistrate of the Thibilitani, on a specific date in the consular year.

EDCS-12500175.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus. N. (and) F. Magnentius.
EDCS-12500175.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus by N. and F. Magnentius.

EDCS-12500176.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus. Ianuarius, quinquennial magistrate, willingly fulfilled his vow on the Kalends of April.
EDCS-12500176.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Bacacus Augustus by Ianuarius, a quinquennial magistrate, on a specific date.

EDCS-12500177.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus. Sextus Lacius.
EDCS-12500177.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus by Sextus Lacius.

EDCS-12500178.translation: Sacred to Bacacus Augustus. Marcus Lucceius Victor and Quintus Va... Augustalis, consul.
EDCS-12500178.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bacacus Augustus by Marcus Lucceius Victor and Quintus Va... Augustalis, who is identified as a consul.

EDCS-12500185.translation: Q. N. Pulca and Lucius Pompusianus, magistrates of the Thibilitani.
EDCS-12500185.summary: This inscription identifies Q. N. Pulca and Lucius Pompusianus as magistrates of the Thibilitani.

EDCS-12500186.translation: Lucius Iulius Rufus, son of Marcius, magistrates of the Thibilitani.
EDCS-12500186.summary: This inscription identifies Lucius Iulius Rufus, son of Marcius, as a magistrate of the Thibilitani.

EDCS-12500188.summary: This inscription contains a partial name and title, likely recording a dedication or honorific.

EDCS-12500189.summary: This inscription records that Romulus Virinius was the builder of this monument.

EDCS-12500194.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius Masnit lived 115 years, buried here.
EDCS-12500194.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name, age, and burial place of Iulius Masnit.

EDCS-12500195.translation: Sextus Iulius Melior fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-12500195.summary: This inscription records Sextus Iulius Melior fulfilling a vow, likely on a votive monument.

EDCS-12500196.summary: This inscription records Sapon fulfilling a vow, likely on a votive monument.

EDCS-12500197.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Galara Quinta.

EDCS-12500198.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Cornelia Quarta placed this for her husband, Helvius Flavianus, who lived for 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12500198.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Cornelia Quarta for her deceased husband, Helvius Flavianus.

EDCS-12500199.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Novelia Varia placed this for her husband, Helvius Maximus, who lived for 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12500199.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Novelia Varia for her deceased husband, Helvius Maximus.

EDCS-12500200.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulius Sollutor, son of Decimus.
EDCS-12500200.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Sollutor, son of Decimus.

EDCS-12500201.translation: Ocania, daughter of Soricius, lived for [X] years.
EDCS-12500201.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Ocania, daughter of Soricius.

EDCS-12500202.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription possibly relating to a father named Penius.

EDCS-12500203.translation: Spes to his most chaste wife.
EDCS-12500203.summary: This inscription records an dedication by Speratus to his wife, Spes.

EDCS-12500204.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription possibly naming Spes, daughter of Salustius.

EDCS-12500205.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To my dear wife Iulia Minucia, beloved by me for many years, in such great grief, this is all I can give back: I made this funerary monument for you from solid marble, that you might chaste keep this name together with your body. She lived 31 years, here she rests. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ventidius Libullinus, during his lifetime, made this memorial for himself and for his exceedingly rare and incomparable wife, with whom he lived 9 years without any fault, and he himself lived as long as he could.
EDCS-12500205.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Minucia, set up by her grieving husband Ventidius Libullinus, who also includes himself and his life span.

EDCS-12500207.translation: Quirina tribe. Natalis, a most obedient mother and most dutiful daughter, lived 30 years, here she rests.
EDCS-12500207.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Natalis, recording her tribe, age at death, and filial devotion.

EDCS-12500209.translation: Spas, daughter of Maximi, lived 75 years, here she rests.
EDCS-12500209.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Spas, daughter of Maximi, stating her age at death.

EDCS-12500212.translation: Sacred to Bonus Eventus. From the will of Quintus Iulius Libo, a man of honorable memory, by his heirs Annios Titianus the Younger and Felix, for five thousand sesterces.
EDCS-12500212.summary: This records a dedication to the deity Bonus Eventus, funded by a legacy from Quintus Iulius Libo and executed by his heirs.

EDCS-12500214.summary: This is a short votive inscription recording Gaius Pomponius fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-12500215.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Redux Augusta, for the health of Antistius Adventus, Burrus, and their children. Agathopus, a freedman, dedicated it.
EDCS-12500215.summary: This is a dedication to Fortuna Redux Augusta, set up by the freedman Agathopus for the well-being of the Antistius family.

EDCS-12500216.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Redux Augusta, for the health of Antistius Adventus, Burrus, and their children. Agathopus, a freedman, dedicated it.
EDCS-12500216.summary: This is a dedication to Fortuna Redux Augusta, set up by the freedman Agathopus for the well-being of the Antistius family (likely a duplicate or variant of EDCS-12500215).

EDCS-12500217.translation: To the Genius of the household, for the health of Caius Iulius Faustianus. Lucius Iulius Urbanus made it with his own money out of piety.
EDCS-12500217.summary: This is a dedication to the Genius of the household, made by Lucius Iulius Urbanus for the health of Caius Iulius Faustianus.

EDCS-12500218.translation: To the victorious god, to Hercules the Invincible. Marcus Ius Marcellinus, a Roman knight and perpetual priest, [dedicated] this statue, which [he received] because of the honor of the duumvirate voluntarily conferred upon him by his order.
EDCS-12500218.summary: This inscription records Marcus Ius Marcellinus dedicating a statue to Hercules Invictus, an honor related to his duumvirate.

EDCS-12500219.translation: Sittia Iulla, daughter of Publius, made this temple to Juno from her own money.
EDCS-12500219.summary: This is a dedication of a temple to Juno, funded by Sittia Iulla.

EDCS-12500220.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Marcus Livius Felix, master of the village, priest of Augustus, gave this from his own money for the honors.
EDCS-12500220.summary: An inscription recording a dedication to Mercury Augustus by Marcus Livius Felix, a local official, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-12500222.translation: To Sol for piety, to Luna for piety.
EDCS-12500222.summary: A very brief inscription recording devotions to the deities Sol and Luna, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12500225.translation: To Venus Augusta, Mother of the Gods, the Great Idaean. Quintus Clodius Quintillus, son of Marcus, dedicated this in the name of his late wife Fuficia Vita.
EDCS-12500225.summary: An inscription dedicating a monument to Venus, Mother of Gods, and the Great Idaean, by Quintus Clodius Quintillus for his deceased wife, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12500226.translation: Sacred to Venus Erucina. Caius Julius Percennus, son of Caius, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-12500226.summary: An inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to Venus Erucina by Caius Julius Percennus, probably Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12500227.translation: Sacred to Victoria Augusta. The worshippers established the temple from its foundations. Caius Volussenius Rusticus dedicated it to the worshippers of Victoria Augusta.
EDCS-12500227.summary: An inscription detailing the construction and dedication of a temple to Victoria Augusta by its worshippers and Caius Volussenius Rusticus, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12500228.translation: Sacred to Victoria Augusta. Agathopus, a freedman, dedicated this for the welfare of Antistius Adventus and Burrus and their children.
EDCS-12500228.summary: An inscription recording a dedication to Victoria Augusta by a freedman, Agathopus, for the well-being of the Antistii family, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12500230.translation: Sacred to Victoria Augusta. Marcus Coranius Successus, master of the village, priest of the deified Augustus, gave this from his own funds for the office of master of the village.
EDCS-12500230.summary: An inscription recording a dedication to Victoria Augusta by Marcus Coranius Successus, a local official and priest of deified Augustus, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12500232.translation: Sacred to the deified Augustus. The people of Larinum to Emperor Vespasian Caesar Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, Consul V, Father of the Country; and to Titus Emperor Caesar, son of Augustus, with tribunician power, Consul III, designated IIII.
EDCS-12500232.summary: An inscription by the people of Larinum honoring Emperors Vespasian and his son Titus, dating to Vespasian's reign (AD 69-79).

EDCS-12500233.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, Trajan Hadrian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power V, Consul III. The people of Thibilis, at public expense.
EDCS-12500233.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Hadrian by the people of Thibilis at public expense, likely dating to Hadrian's reign (AD 117-138).

EDCS-12500234.translation: To Faustina Augusta, wife of Emperor Caesar Antoninus Augustus Armeniacus Parthicus Maximus, the Medic. The people of Thibilis, with public money by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12500234.summary: An inscription honoring Empress Faustina I by the people of Thibilis using public funds, likely during the reign of Antoninus Pius (AD 138-161).

EDCS-12500236.translation: To Acentria Vettia Saturnina, wife of Didius Iulianus.
EDCS-12500236.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Acentria Vettia Saturnina, wife of Didiua Iulianus.

EDCS-12500241.translation: To our most noble lord, Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius Augustus, by the public body of the most splendid municipality of Thibilis.
EDCS-12500241.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the municipality of Thibilis to Emperor Constantius II.

EDCS-12500242.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Galerius Valerius Maximianus, invincible, pious, fortunate, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 15th time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 6th time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, Valerius Antoninus, the most perfect man, governor of the province of Numidia Cirtensis, devoted to his divine majesty, erected this at public expense.
EDCS-12500242.summary: This is a public dedication erected by Valerius Antoninus, governor of Numidia Cirtensis, to Emperor Maximian, likely in the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-12700086.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Titus Flavius Rogatus, a vetted veteran, transferred from the III Augusta legion to the II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis in Pannonia Inferior, lived for 87 years. He made this in his own lifetime for 1,000 sesterces.
EDCS-12700086.summary: This is a self-funded funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Rogatus, a veteran of several legions, dating to the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-12700115.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Iulia Secunda, also called Catella, who lived 29 years and spent 13 years with her husband, Publius Claudius, a cornicularius of the Hemeseni cohort, his most exceptional wife.
EDCS-12700115.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Secunda, also known as Catella, set up by her husband, a soldier, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-12700119.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Maxima Montana, who lived 55 years. Mocimus Isamus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-12700119.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maxima Montana, erected by her husband Mocimus Isamus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-12700120.translation: To the god Malagbel. For the safety of our lords, the Emperors. Titus Claudius Iulius, centurion of the III Augusta legion, [erected this] for Aulus Julius Piso, legate of the emperors, proconsul.
EDCS-12700120.summary: This is a dedication to the god Malagbel by Titus Claudius Iulius, a centurion, for the well-being of the emperors and the governor Aulus Julius Piso, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-12700159.translation: Speculators: Acutius Ingenus, Publius Gargilius Felix, Th. Flavius Minervalis, Aelius Antonianus, beneficiarii consularis. Caelius Rogatianus, Cornelius Felix, Gabinius Rufus, Caecilius Felix, Marcus Mamius Felix, Iunius Donatus, Arellius Apollonius, Magnius Licinianus, Marcus Virrius Flavianus of Thamugade, Marcus Aurelius Hermias of Alexandria, Aurelius Quietus, Laronius Quintianus, Marcus Sallustius Flavianus, C. Tadius Victorinus, Calventius Acutianus, Calvisius Felix, Marius Cornelianus, Furfanius Secundus, Cornelius Aratoxen, Iulius Clarus, Iunius Gallus, Lucius Orbius Caelianus of Lambaesis, Sextius Lateranus, Postumius Paculus, Caecilius Rufus, Aelius Glyconianus, Egnatius Natalis, Recius Candidianus, Avilius Victor, Verecunda Hostilia Iuliana, candidati. Pontius Victor, Iulius Crescentianus, Arrius Saturninus, Licinius Fortunatianus, Cesellius Rogatianus, Sextius Vitalis, ex frumentariis. Aurelius Nilammon, Aufidius Successus, Avilius Silvanus, Verecunda, Lucius Hirrius Urbanus of Carthage, Iulius Valentin, quaestionarii. Iulius Donatus, Antonius Fortunatus, Marcus Aemilius Sedatus of Castra, Aemilius Crescens, Iulius Martialis, beneficiarii sexmenstres. Baebius Fortunatianus, Atilius Vegetus, Lucius Iulius Tertullus, Manlius Ingenuus, Pompeius Cottinus, haruspex. Antonius December.
EDCS-12700159.summary: This is a long list of military personnel, including speculators, beneficiarii, frumentarii, and quaestionarii, serving in various locations such as Thamugade, Alexandria, Lambaesis, and Carthage, likely dating to the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-12700161.translation: S. Apronius Vergilius Felix, from cohort I Aelia Prima, mensor frumentarius. Lucius Nonius Florus, candidatus, from the camp. Titus Flavius Aquilus, Aelia Zama. Lucius Flaminius Felix of Carthage. Caius Annius Crescens of Hadrumetum. Marcus Aurelius Germanus. Aurelia Thic. Titus Flavius Ianuarius Marsus of Thamugade. Lucius Sextilius Crescens, candidatus, from Hadrumetum. Lucius Agrius Ianuarius of Theveste. Caius Cassius Primus, beneficiarius of a tribune, from Carthage. Sempronius Ianuarius, candidatus. Lucius Egnatius Optatus, from the camp. Aemilius Silvanus, candidatus. Latinius Victor, candidatus, from Carthage. Iulius Arator, Aemilius Antheus, Furius Marcianus, candidatus speculator. [---]eminius Primus, [---]ius Saturninus, candidatus, from Zama. [---]ventius Felix, candidatus, from Hadrumetum. Iulius Diodorus of Lepcimagna. Iulius Felix of Carthage. S. Namphamus of Sicca. S. Quadratus of Carthage. Rusticus of Thysdrus. Maximus [---] antus [---] utius [---] Ad [---] Cirta. Crescens, trainee signiferorum, from Lepci. Sittianus of Cirta. S. Successus, from the camp. IVN Caius Iulius Publius, Narcius Caius Iulius V, Lucius Cornelius, Aelius Aurelius, Caius Iulius Ma, Marcus Motilius Claudius, Cornelius M Manilius V, Cerellius S, Antistius P, Quintus Aelius Fe, Aemilius Fe, Lucius Pinarius, Caius Aurelius, Lucius Trebius, Valerius Sittius, Lucius Aurelius Pr, Aemilius Ru, Caius Marius Sa, C. Ius Fel, Ius Cornelius EI.
EDCS-12700161.summary: This is a fragmented inscription listing numerous individuals, many identified by rank or origin, including soldiers, beneficiarii, and candidati from various North African cities, likely dating to the late 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-12700170.translation: Lucius Arruntius Maximus, perpetual flamen and king of the sacred rites, and the devotees of the god Sol: Quintus Porcius,arius Felix, Quintus Ge[---], Marcus Aurelius Fortunatus, immunis perpetuus, Primerius, Pigritis, Eusebius.
EDCS-12700170.summary: This inscription records Lucius Arruntius Maximus as the perpetual flamen and king of the sacred rites, along with other devotees of the sun god Sol, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12700172.translation: The colonists of the colony of Marchianae Traianae Thamugadensis, in recognition of the restoration of the city, presented a charter of patronage to Aelius Iulianus, perpetual presiding flamen, by order of the council and the people.
EDCS-12700172.summary: A colony honors its patron, Aelius Iulianus, with a charter of patronage for restoring the city.

EDCS-12700181.translation: In former times, when our lords Valentinian and Valens, perpetual Augusti, were in power, the desperate state of the baths was renewed, and their lost magnificence restored. The ugliness of ruin was overcome by the splendor of newness. Therefore, Publilius Ceionius Caecina Albinus, a most distinguished man, consular governor of the province of Numidia Constantina, restored, completed, and dedicated the summer baths to the splendor of both his homeland and the province with all effort, under the care of the perpetual good and upright flamines Aemilius Flavius, Fabius Praetextatus, Flavius Innocentius, Marius Secundinus, and Excusantius.
EDCS-12700181.summary: Publilius Ceionius Caecina Albinus, a consular governor, restored and dedicated the summer baths in Numidia Constantina during the reign of Valentinian and Valens.

EDCS-12700194.translation: Sacred to Caelestis Augusta, for the health of our lord Augustus. Publius Sittius Optatus, a Roman knight and perpetual flamen, together with the priests Octavius Emeritus and Caecilius Frumentius, and the basket-bearers Centrius Abundius, Grasidius Felix, Restutus Sirisinnus, Terentinus Fortunatus, and Extricatus, and the consecrated Communis, Saturninus, Donatus, Vincentius, Fructosus, Vitalis, and Felix, made (this) from their own means.
EDCS-12700194.summary: A group of priests and officials dedicated a sacred offering to Caelestis Augusta for the health of the Emperor, likely in Thamugadi.

EDCS-12700211.translation: For our Pious, Fortunate Emperors, Augusti, the colonists of Bassenses vowed a sacred altar to our Lords.
EDCS-12700211.summary: Colonists of Bassenses vowed a sacred altar to their Emperors.

EDCS-12800165.translation: Aurelius Pastor lived for ninety years. Aurelia lived for fifty years.
EDCS-12800165.summary: A tombstone recording the ages of Aurelius Pastor and Aurelia.

EDCS-12800334.translation: To Gaius Maesius Picatianus, Legate of the Emperor with Praetorian power, designated Consul.
EDCS-12800334.summary: An inscription honoring Gaius Maesius Picatianus, a high-ranking official.

EDCS-12800697.translation: Here is preserved the memory of the saints Faustinus and Saturninus, whose festival is celebrated on the day before the Nones of September. With God's help and the Lord Jesus Christ, Restutus the deacon, with his companions, fulfilled his vow to the Lord. Amen.
EDCS-12800697.summary: A deacon named Restutus dedicated a memorial for the saints Faustinus and Saturninus, with their feast day noted.

EDCS-12800698.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Geminius Felicianus, Aurelius Quintus, Iulius Ianuarius, Iulius Faustus, Safidius Extricatus, Iulius Primosus, Aemilius Secundianus, Aurelius Cerditus, Ianuarius Marcus Livius Julianus, Iulius Septiminus the priest, Iulius Nampamus, Iulius Primosus, Caecilius Donatianus, Fuficius Emeritus, Iulius Saturus, Iulius Saturninus, Caecilius Junius, Iulius Ingenuus, Caecilius Saturninus, Licinius Victor, Pompeius Muscius, Aurelius Amandus, Iulius Honoratus.
EDCS-12800698.summary: A list of individuals, including a priest, dedicating offerings to Silvanus Augustus.

EDCS-12800699.translation: soldier of the legion written above wrote from the cohort.
EDCS-12800699.summary: Fragmentary inscription mentioning a soldier of a specific legion and cohort.

EDCS-12900015.translation: Gunda lived for 67 years. With her son Sremirus, she lived for 13 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900015.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespans of Gunda and her son Sremirus.

EDCS-12900016.translation: For the memory of the Helvii. Gaius Helvius Gemellus and his wife Antonia Mustica made this for themselves and their family while alive.
EDCS-12900016.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Gaius Helvius Gemellus and Antonia Mustica for themselves and their family.

EDCS-12900022.translation: Volusita Honorata, freedwoman of Calvina, a devoted wife, lived for 46 years and is buried here.
EDCS-12900022.summary: This tombstone records the life and burial of Volusita Honorata, the freedwoman and wife of Calvina.

EDCS-12900023.translation: Gaius Horatius Chrysaor, freedman of Caius, lived for 32 years and is buried here. May your bones rest well; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Spendon, his freedman, set this up at his own expense.
EDCS-12900023.summary: This tomb records the life of Gaius Horatius Chrysaor, freedman of Caius, and was erected by his freedman Spendon.

EDCS-12900024.translation: Horatius Victor Quirina lived for 95 years and is buried here.
EDCS-12900024.summary: This tombstone records the life and burial of Horatius Victor Quirina.

EDCS-12900031.translation: For the memory of my most beloved son Ianuarius, who lived 10 months and [some days].
EDCS-12900031.summary: This inscription is a memorial for Ianuarius, the beloved son who died in infancy.

EDCS-12900032.translation: To the divine shades. Ianuaria lived for 39 years.
EDCS-12900032.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ianuaria, who died at the age of 39.

EDCS-12900082.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a name on a tomb, for Gaius Julius Signinarius.

EDCS-12900090.translation: N. Julius Vin Bassulus lived for 50 years and is buried here.
EDCS-12900090.summary: This tombstone records the life and burial of N. Julius Vin Bassulus.

EDCS-12900104.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a name on a tomb, for Iulia Extricata.

EDCS-12900130.translation: For the memory of Iulia Nampamilla, my dearest wife, I made this. She lived for 42 years and is buried here.
EDCS-12900130.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a husband to his wife, Iulia Nampamilla, who died at the age of 42.

EDCS-12900133.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Novicia, dearest daughter, lived 35 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. To Gaius Julius Acutus, most rare father, lived 50 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900133.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Iulia Novicia and her father, Gaius Julius Acutus, and expresses wishes for their repose.

EDCS-12900142.translation: Iulia Cerufina lived 37 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900142.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Iulia Cerufina and expresses wishes for her repose.

EDCS-12900143.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Saturnina lived for...
EDCS-12900143.summary: This incomplete funerary inscription records the name of Iulia Saturnina and the beginning of her age at death.

EDCS-12900160.translation: Decima Laberia Hilara lived 109 years and 2 months.
EDCS-12900160.summary: This funerary inscription records the exceptionally long lifespan of Decima Laberia Hilara.

EDCS-12900161.translation: Marcus Laetorius Hispanus made this offering.
EDCS-12900161.summary: This inscription records a dedication or offering made by Marcus Laetorius Hispanus.

EDCS-12900170.translation: Licinia, beloved daughter of Lucius, lived 100 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-12900170.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Licinia, noting her long life.

EDCS-12900179.translation: Son of Voltinius, Lucrecius lived 85 years.
EDCS-12900179.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age at death of Lucrecius.

EDCS-12900202.translation: To Publius Marcius Felix, brother, from Publius Iulius, of the Voltina tribe.
EDCS-12900202.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Publius Iulius to his brother, Publius Marcius Felix.

EDCS-12900209.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Marrutia Maxima lived 101 years.
EDCS-12900209.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marrutia Maxima, noting her advanced age.

EDCS-12900212.translation: Maximus lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900212.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Maximus, specifying his age at death.

EDCS-12900218.translation: Minutius Urbanus lived for 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900218.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Minutius Urbanus.

EDCS-12900227.translation: Aulus Mussius Primus, freedman of Aulus, lived for 20 years.
EDCS-12900227.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Aulus Mussius Primus, noting his status as a freedman.

EDCS-12900244.translation: O PITA lived for 21 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900244.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of O PITA.

EDCS-12900256.translation: To the departed spirits: Lucius Petronius Festus, who was also called Zabulius, made this for himself and his family.
EDCS-12900256.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Lucius Petronius Festus, also known as Zabulius, for himself and his family.

EDCS-12900259.translation: Proculus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900259.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Proculus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-12900263.summary: This is a funerary epitaph or a dedication, possibly incomplete, identifying Lucius Pompeius.

EDCS-12900267.translation: Aulus Pompeius Maritimianus, son of Aulus, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-12900267.summary: This is an inscription identifying Aulus Pompeius Maritimianus, son of Aulus, noting his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-12900268.translation: Lucius Naevius Libo, his paternal uncle, [erected this] to Aulus Pompeius Maritimianus, son of Aulus, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-12900268.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Naevius Libo to his nephew, Aulus Pompeius Maritimianus, identifying their familial relationship and tribal affiliations.

EDCS-12900286.translation: Sextus Propertius Modestus, son of Cai, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 60 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900286.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan, burial, and a wish for peaceful rest for Sextus Propertius Modestus.

EDCS-12900294.translation: Publicius Surus lived for 20 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-12900294.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Publicius Surus.

EDCS-12900301.translation: In the third consulship of Quadratus.
EDCS-12900301.summary: This inscription records a consular dating mention, likely from an official document or monument, referencing Quadratus' third consulship.

EDCS-12900302.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Securius Quintus.

EDCS-12900312.translation: To Sittia Rufina, the deserving. Iulia Potita, Minucia Saturi Cyrilla who is also called Punica, Antonini Porcia Procula, Horatia Procula, Anulla Saturi Pulla, Pauli Ingenua Veri Popilia Procilla, Basili Sittia Varilla, Potiti Flaccilla, Serani Honorata who is also called Marciana, Cimbri Vibia Laeta, Vibia Marcellina, Fausti Titia Decimi Marci Catuli Anucella, Orchivi Capitonis Pudentilla, daughter of Nices, Augurina who is also called Honorata, Rufina Seia, daughter of Lucius Clodius.
EDCS-12900312.summary: This is a complex funerary dedication listing numerous women (likely family members or friends) honoring Sittia Rufina, suggesting a close-knit social or familial network, possibly from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12900316.translation: Saeternatia Monna lived 60 years, she is buried here.
EDCS-12900316.summary: This is a funerary epitaph stating that Saeternatia Monna lived for 60 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-12900318.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Salvia Denta.

EDCS-12900338.translation: F. Seianus Candidus, son of F., lived 14 years, 5 months, and 7 days.
EDCS-12900338.summary: This is a funerary epitaph detailing the name, parentage, and precise age at death of a young boy named F. Seianus Candidus.

EDCS-12900358.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to record the names Gaius Sittius and Antistianus, possibly indicating a dedication or a statement of association.

EDCS-12900372.summary: This very brief inscription identifies Sextus Sittius as a husband, likely part of a larger funerary monument or a simple marker.

EDCS-12900376.translation: I ask you, traveler, who hurries, to slow your journey, so that you may learn in a few words that with all birthright, I, born not of foreign stock but of Scythian race, was born where Aetna girds the men with its mass. The place is known, most pleasant: Alba, in which the pine with fragrant foliage smells by the sea; Daphne the modest is green. A vitreous Naiad leaps in the place, and while she praises the one born of a similar gift, she swears that she is the D. there I am ash, here my bones will be honored. Thirty and twice five years I consumed, and alas, I had a unique daughter in my life; nature gives and takes back, not because she sins. So that you may not be reluctant to speak, may the bones of Publius Sittius Optatus lie gently.
EDCS-12900376.summary: This complex metrical epitaph laments the deceased Publius Sittius Optatus, born of Scythian stock but associated with Alba and Mount Etna, and expresses grief over his daughter, requesting gentle repose for his bones.

EDCS-12900425.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Titacius Felix lived 70 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-12900425.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the Divine Spirits, recording the name and age at death of Marcus Titacius Felix.

EDCS-12900441.translation: To Titus Longanius Venulus, a friend deserving of honor.
EDCS-12900441.summary: This is a dedication by an unnamed friend to Titus Longanius Venulus, likely on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-12900453.summary: This is a funerary inscription intended to commemorate Victorinus, with some of the text missing.

EDCS-12900456.translation: Lucius Vipsanius Silvanus Junior, son, erected this to the memory of his most innocent father, Lucius Vipsanius Silvanus.
EDCS-12900456.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Vipsanius Silvanus Junior for his father, Lucius Vipsanius Silvanus, indicating family and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-12900458.translation: Vitalis, aged 13, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900458.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Vitalis, who died at the age of 13, with a common wish for the deceased's repose.

EDCS-12900461.translation: To the memory of the Umbricius family. Lucius Umbricius Rufinus Senior, son of Lucius Umbricius Rufus, of the Quirina tribe, made this for himself and his family.
EDCS-12900461.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Umbricius Rufinus Senior creating a memorial for himself and his family, mentioning his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-12900467.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Volumnius Meridianus lived 91 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900467.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Publius Volumnius Meridianus, who lived 91 years, accompanied by a wish for his repose.

EDCS-12900469.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Banilla.

EDCS-12900476.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius C. Postumus lived 35 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-12900476.summary: This tombstone inscription commemorates Gaius C. Postumus, who died at 35, including a common wish for eternal rest.

EDCS-12900478.translation: To the departed spirits. CLESE ILER M. F. lived 90 years.
EDCS-12900478.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of CLESE ILER M. F., who lived 90 years, with a standard invocation to the departed spirits.

EDCS-12900480.translation: Gaius Camilius C. M. V. N. lived 2 years.
EDCS-12900480.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Camilius C. M. V. N., who died at the age of 2.

EDCS-12900483.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ilarcica lived 96 years.
EDCS-12900483.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Ilarcica.

EDCS-12900486.translation: T. Madus lived 40 years. May he rest well.
EDCS-12900486.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of T. Madus.

EDCS-12900491.translation: Cornelius Dyscolus erected this for his sweet son, Primianus.
EDCS-12900491.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by Cornelius Dyscolus for his son, Primianus.

EDCS-12900494.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Heraclides, to his most rare husband, Tadius, lived 33 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-12900494.summary: A funerary epitaph for Heraclides' husband, Tadius, who lived 33 years.

EDCS-12900498.summary: A funerary epitaph recording a very long lifespan, possibly for a woman.

EDCS-12900500.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Titius Didanus.

EDCS-12900501.summary: A funerary epitaph recording a long lifespan, possibly for a man named C.

EDCS-12900502.translation: Bonavia, daughter of Marcus, lived [---] years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900502.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Bonavia, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-12900504.translation: Aetia Cata lived [---] years. She is buried here.
EDCS-12900504.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Aetia Cata.

EDCS-12900527.translation: Mi enius Rufus erected this by divine will.
EDCS-12900527.summary: An inscription recording the erection of a monument by Mienius Rufus, attributed to divine will.

EDCS-12900537.translation: Iustus is a law unto himself.
EDCS-12900537.summary: This is a personal motto inscribed by Iustus, likely on a tomb or personal monument.

EDCS-12900539.translation: On the fourth day before the Nones of September, remember in the sight of the Lord the martyrdom of the saints Hortensius, Marianus, Jacobus, Datus, Iapus, Rusticus, Crispus, Tattus, Mettunus, Victor, Silbanus, Aegiptius, the holy ones, whose names are known to Him who made this, in the fifteenth indiction.
EDCS-12900539.summary: This inscription commemorates the martyrdom of several saints on a specific date, likely within a church or cemetery context.

EDCS-13000788.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Coronatus, an assistant in the record office, lived 50 years. His sons made this.
EDCS-13000788.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Coronatus, an assistant in a record office, erected by his sons.

EDCS-13000789.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilius Rogatus lived 110 years. His sons [made this].
EDCS-13000789.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Aemilius Rogatus, who lived to be 110 years old, erected by his sons.

EDCS-13000791.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gessia Fortunata lived 70 years. Iulius Rogatus made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-13000791.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Gessia Fortunata, erected by her husband Iulius Rogatus.

EDCS-13000792.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Granius Martialis lived 75 years. Secirunius, his heir and son, made this.
EDCS-13000792.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Caius Granius Martialis, erected by his son and heir Secirunius.

EDCS-13000794.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Nonius Fortunatus lived 25 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13000794.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Nonius Fortunatus, indicating he is buried at this location.

EDCS-13000795.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Petronius Victor lived 42 years. Valeria Victorina made this for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-13000795.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Petronius Victor, erected by his devoted wife Valeria Victorina.

EDCS-13000796.translation: Hail, Seusa! Sacred to the spirits of the departed. She lived 28 years. She gave and dedicated [this].
EDCS-13000796.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Seusa, who lived 28 years, and seems to have dedicated something herself or had it dedicated in her honor.

EDCS-13000809.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aelia Fortunata lived 100 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13000809.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Aelia Fortunata, who lived to be 100 years old, indicating she is buried at this location.

EDCS-13000810.translation: Castrensis lived 80 years. His son made this. He lies here.
EDCS-13000810.summary: A tombstone for Castrensis, who lived 80 years, erected by his son.

EDCS-13000811.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Iulius Felix lies here, having lived 35 years.
EDCS-13000811.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Iulius Felix, who lived 35 years, dedicated to the underworld spirits.

EDCS-13000812.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Pollius Saturninus lived 80 years. His entire family of sons made this for their dearest father. He lies here.
EDCS-13000812.summary: A tombstone for Pollius Saturninus, who lived 80 years, erected by his sons.

EDCS-13000814.summary: An inscription fragment mentioning Vipia, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-13000819.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the safety of Marcus Ulpius Quintianus, our lord. Proculus, his slave, made this.
EDCS-13000819.summary: An inscription recording a dedication by a slave named Proculus to Jupiter for the well-being of his master, Marcus Ulpius Quintianus.

EDCS-13000820.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Flavia Salvilia lived 90 years. Her sons made this for her in recognition of her merits.
EDCS-13000820.summary: A tombstone for Flavia Salvilia, who lived 90 years, erected by her sons.

EDCS-13000821.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Mevia Ianuaria lived 52 years.
EDCS-13000821.summary: A tombstone for Mevia Ianuaria, who lived 52 years.

EDCS-13000822.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Gessia Secunda lived 75 years. Her son Iulius Silvanus made this.
EDCS-13000822.summary: A tombstone for Gessia Secunda, who lived 75 years, erected by her son.

EDCS-13000823.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Iulius Salasus the Younger lived 45 years, 2 months, and 5 days. His wife Iuventia Calvina made this for him.
EDCS-13000823.summary: A tombstone for Caius Iulius Salasus the Younger, who lived 45 years, 2 months, and 5 days, erected by his wife.

EDCS-13000824.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Maxima lived 23 years, 2 months, and 16 days. Caius Iulius Salasus made this for his dearest daughter.
EDCS-13000824.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Maxima, who lived 23 years, 2 months, and 16 days, erected by her father.

EDCS-13000825.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Silvanus made this, while still alive, for Julia Maxima, his good wife. IUL MA 70.
EDCS-13000825.summary: A tombstone erected by Silvanus for his wife Julia Maxima.

EDCS-13000826.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. L. Capitiosus lived 89 years. His sons made this for their dearest father.
EDCS-13000826.summary: A tombstone erected by sons for their father, L. Capitiosus.

EDCS-13000827.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. L. Capitiosus lived 60 years.
EDCS-13000827.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of L. Capitiosus.

EDCS-13000828.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. P. Donata lived 70 years.
EDCS-13000828.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of P. Donata.

EDCS-13000829.summary: An inscription likely naming individuals, possibly on a tomb or dedication.

EDCS-13000830.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Secundus lived 70 years. His sons made this.
EDCS-13000830.summary: A tombstone erected by sons for Secundus.

EDCS-13000831.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Sextilius Marianus lived 67 years. Sextilia Maior made this for her pious husband. He is buried here.
EDCS-13000831.summary: A tombstone erected by Sextilia Maior for her husband Lucius Sextilius Marianus.

EDCS-13000832.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus lived 80 years for his pious father. EX. He is buried here.
EDCS-13000832.summary: A tombstone erected for a pious father named Quintus.

EDCS-13000846.translation: In the name of our Lord God and Savior Jesus Christ, in the time of the most blessed Faustinus, bishop of holy memory, he made this fortification of the Masticiana estate at his own expense.
EDCS-13000846.summary: A Christian inscription recording the construction of a fortification by Bishop Faustinus.

EDCS-13000847.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Thecusa lived 1 year. Iulius Victor, a beneficiarius of the legate of the XX Valeria Victrix legion, from the province of Britannia Superior, made this monument for his dearest sister.
EDCS-13000847.summary: A tombstone erected by Iulius Victor for his sister Iulia Thecusa, likely dating to the Roman presence in Britain.

EDCS-13000848.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Annius Servanus, son of Marcus, of the Papirian tribe, from Theveste, lived 18 years and 11 months. Marcus Annius Octavianus made this for his dearest son. He is buried here.
EDCS-13000848.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Annius Servanus, set up by his father, Marcus Annius Octavianus.

EDCS-13000849.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Catellia Donata lived 21 years. Catellius Faustus and Fau[---]...
EDCS-13000849.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Catellia Donata, likely set up by family members.

EDCS-13000850.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelia Tertulla, daughter of Caius, lived 38 years. Marcus Annius Octavianus made this for his most dutiful wife. She is buried here.
EDCS-13000850.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Tertulla, set up by her husband, Marcus Annius Octavianus.

EDCS-13000851.translation: Felicissima lived 8 years. Veneria set this up for her foster child.
EDCS-13000851.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Felicissima, set up by her foster mother, Veneria.

EDCS-13000852.translation: [Name illegible] lived 35 years, and Paetus P S, her husband, made this.
EDCS-13000852.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed woman, set up by her husband, Paetus P S.

EDCS-13000853.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Hortensius Castus lived 30 years. Hortensius Fortunatus, his father, made this for his son.
EDCS-13000853.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hortensius Castus, set up by his father, Hortensius Fortunatus.

EDCS-13000854.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Messius Silanus lived 76 years. His daughter made this for her dearest father. Good [people] to good [people]...
EDCS-13000854.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Messius Silanus, set up by his daughter.

EDCS-13000855.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Veneria lived 75 years. She made this for herself while alive, and for her dearest, most loving husband. Her sons Fortunatus and Matauta carved this monument for their dearest father and mother.
EDCS-13000855.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Veneria and her husband, set up by their sons Fortunatus and Matauta, with Veneria having contributed to it during her lifetime.

EDCS-13000856.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Her sons Felix and Iahinas made this for their dearest Katellia Donata. She lived 80 years.
EDCS-13000856.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Katellia Donata, set up by her sons Felix and Iahinas.

EDCS-13000857.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Suetonius Ianuarius, prefect of the First Cohort of Chalcidians, made this for his most sacred wife, Papiria Irena, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-13000857.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Papiria Irena, wife of Caius Suetonius Ianuarius, a military officer.

EDCS-13000858.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Felix lived 35 years. Marianus erected this altar for his son.
EDCS-13000858.summary: This is a funerary altar erected by Marianus for his son, Iulius Felix, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-13000859.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Gracilis, a veteran, lived 45 years. His wife, Horatia, made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-13000859.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Horatia for her deserving husband, Iulius Gracilis, a veteran who lived 45 years.

EDCS-13000860.translation: Iulia Victorina lived 17 years. Manilia Tertula made this for her most devoted daughter.
EDCS-13000860.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Manilia Tertula for her daughter, Iulia Victorina, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-13000861.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Dexter, a veteran, served in an auxiliary cavalry regiment as a curator of armaments, standard-bearer, served 26 years, discharged with honor. He served as duovir in his colony, Thelepte. He lived 85 years and was cremated here. Tutia Tertia, wife of Iulius Dexter, lived 70 years and was cremated here.
EDCS-13000861.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and military career of Caius Iulius Dexter, a veteran and former duovir of Thelepte, and his wife Tutia Tertia, who lived to 85 and 70 years respectively.

EDCS-13000863.translation: Hail Secunda, sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia Secunda was buried here and lived 40 years. Caius Aelius Annullinus made this for his most innocent wife.
EDCS-13000863.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Caius Aelius Annullinus for his wife, Flavia Secunda, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-13000864.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Salvidenia Secunda lived 60 years. Her sons, Iulii Saturninus and Felix, made this for their dearest mother. Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Cududus lived 71 years. His sons, Iulii Saturninus and Felix, made this for their dearest father.
EDCS-13000864.summary: This inscription records two funerary monuments for Salvidenia Secunda and Caius Iulius Cududus, both made by their sons Iulii Saturninus and Felix.

EDCS-13000871.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fortunatiana lived 7 years, 11 months, and 14 days and is buried here. Salvius made this for his dearest daughter.
EDCS-13000871.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Salvius for his daughter, Fortunatiana, who died at the age of 7 years, 11 months, and 14 days.

EDCS-13000872.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Lurius Phosphorus, dutiful, lived 98 years. Priest of Mercury.
EDCS-13000872.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Caius Lurius Phosphorus, a priest of Mercury, who lived 98 years.

EDCS-13000873.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaie Fufi Kapito lived 80 years. The heirs made this for their father.
EDCS-13000873.summary: This funerary monument was made by the heirs for their father, Gaie Fufi Kapito, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-13000874.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caie Fufi Maxime lived 45 years. Made for herself, a tomb.
EDCS-13000874.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caie Fufi Maxime, who lived 45 years and commissioned her own tomb.
