EDCS-13002144.summary: This is a family monument dedicated to Sittia Extricata, identified as a perpetual priestess, likely in the Roman period.

EDCS-13002147.translation: Quintus, son of Aelius, of the Quirina tribe, honored with public equestrian rank, as aedile, priest, chief priest twice, chief magistrate twice, and quinquennial prefect with judicial power in the colonies of Rusicada and Chullitanus, dedicated with his own money two statues of satyrs, which he had promised for the honor of his quinquennial magistracy, along with an added day of theatrical games with missiles, and he himself dedicated them.
EDCS-13002147.summary: This inscription records the dedication of two statues of satyrs and games by Quintus Aelius, a prominent magistrate and priest in the colonies of Rusicada and Chullitanus, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13002148.translation: Aedile, III-vir, prefect with judicial power in the colony of Veneria Rusicade, priest twice, perpetual flamen.
EDCS-13002148.summary: This is a cursus honorum listing the offices held by an individual in the colony of Rusicade, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13002150.translation: Aedile of four colonies, he dedicated statues with a tetrastyion and himself dedicated them.
EDCS-13002150.summary: This inscription records the dedication of statues and a tetrastyion by an individual who served as aedile for four colonies, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13002151.translation: III-vir, prefect with judicial power in three colonies.
EDCS-13002151.summary: This inscription lists the high-ranking judicial and administrative offices held by an individual in three colonies, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13002169.translation: Publius Iulius Noricus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, honored with public equestrian rank.
EDCS-13002169.summary: This is a record of an individual, Publius Iulius Noricus, being honored with equestrian rank, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13002176.translation: Gaius Munatius Extricatus lived 85 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002176.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Munatius Extricatus, recording his name, age, and burial place, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13002177.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Sittia Saturnina lived 95 years; may her bones rest well.
EDCS-13002177.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Sittia Saturnina, recording her age and a wish for her peaceful rest, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13002179.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Comica, daughter of Comicus, erected this; [she is] the heir.
EDCS-13002179.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, erected by Comica for herself or a relative, identifying her as the heir, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13002180.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Vibius Messor lived 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002180.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Vibius Messor, recording his age and burial place.

EDCS-13002182.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Repostus lived 25 years.
EDCS-13002182.summary: A funerary epitaph for Repostus, recording his age.

EDCS-13002183.translation: Victoria lived 30 years. Zonisius made this for his daughter.
EDCS-13002183.summary: A funerary epitaph for Victoria, made by her father Zonisius.

EDCS-13002184.translation: To Publius Iulius Geminus Marinianus, quaestor and broad-striped tribune in Cappadocia.
EDCS-13002184.summary: A dedication to Publius Iulius Geminus Marinianus, honoring his political and military career.

EDCS-13002185.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulius Quietus lived eight years.
EDCS-13002185.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Quietus, recording his age.

EDCS-13002186.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Sista Bulari lived 61 years.
EDCS-13002186.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sista Bulari, recording her age.

EDCS-13002187.translation: Postumia Geramanila lived 20 years. Her father made this for his most innocent daughter.
EDCS-13002187.summary: A funerary epitaph for Postumia Geramanila, made by her father.

EDCS-13002188.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Antonia Pantia, who lived 11 years.
EDCS-13002188.summary: A funerary epitaph for Antonia Pantia, recording her age.

EDCS-13002189.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Bibius Felix lived 55 years. Bibius Martialis made this for his father; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002189.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Bibius Felix, made by his son Bibius Martialis, recording his age and burial place.

EDCS-13002190.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Iovius lived 90 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002190.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iovius, recording his age and burial place.

EDCS-13002191.translation: M. Vius lived 78 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002191.summary: A funerary epitaph for M. Vius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002192.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Tito Aelius Primus Memore lived 58 years. His wife, who lived here, made this.
EDCS-13002192.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a wife to her deceased husband, Tito Aelius Primus Memore, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002193.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Vales Donta lived 77 years.
EDCS-13002193.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vales Donta, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002201.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Iulia P[ossin] lived 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002201.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia P[ossin], dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002205.translation: Sacred to the holy Attis. Caius Metteius Exuperans, a dendrophorus and decretarius, made this at his own expense and dedicated it with a willing heart.
EDCS-13002205.summary: A dedication to Attis by a dendrophorus and decretarius named Caius Metteius Exuperans, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002206.translation: Sacred to Augusta Bellona. Sextus Horatius Felix, priest and son of Sextus Horatius Quirinus Felix, along with his sons Proculus and Triumphalis, priests, renovated the temple with all its furnishings and paintings with his own money.
EDCS-13002206.summary: An inscription recording the renovation of a temple to Augusta Bellona by the priest Sextus Horatius Felix and his sons, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002207.translation: Sacred to Augusta Bellona. Publius Afinius Felix willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13002207.summary: A votive inscription to Augusta Bellona by Publius Afinius Felix, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002209.translation: Sacred to the Genius of the colony of Veneria Rusicada and to Augustus. Marcus Aemilius Ballator, in addition to 10,000 sesterces which he gave for the theatre's construction or upkeep at the people's request, erected two statues, one of the Genius of our fatherland and another of Annona Sacra, with his own money. On the occasion of their dedication, he provided a day of games with projectiles. The site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-13002209.summary: An inscription by Marcus Aemilius Ballator dedicating statues to the Genius of Veneria Rusicada and Annona Sacra, and funding games, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002211.translation: Sacred to Augustus Mercury. Seius Thesmus and Seia Syntyche willingly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-13002211.summary: A votive inscription to Augustus Mercury by Seius Thesmus and Seia Syntyche, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002212.translation: Sacred to Augusta Victoria. Lucius Cornelius Fronto Probianus, son of Lucius Cornelius Quirinus, decorated with the public horse, decurion of four colonies, perpetual priest of the deified Antoninus, dedicated a statue with a tetrastylo, which, in honor of his priesthood, he had promised in addition to 82,000 sesterces which he contributed to the state, and 20,000 sesterces from his decurionate, and other gifts he conferred upon the fatherland with his generosity from 30,000 sesterces; and he himself dedicated it. For its dedication, he also presented theatrical games with projectiles.
EDCS-13002212.summary: An inscription by Lucius Cornelius Fronto Probianus dedicating a statue to Augusta Victoria and funding games, likely dating to the reign of Antoninus Pius or later in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002214.translation: Gaius Valerius Antoninus, a most distinguished man and governor of the province of Numidia Cirtensis, restored this.
EDCS-13002214.summary: This inscription records the restoration of something by Gaius Valerius Antoninus, governor of Numidia Cirtensis.

EDCS-13002215.translation: Quintus [---]itus dedicated this on the third day before the Nones of January, with Sabinianus and Seleucus as consuls, and again on the third day before the Nones of March, with the same consuls.
EDCS-13002215.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Quintus [---]itus in January and March of an unspecified year, during the consulship of Sabinianus and Seleucus.

EDCS-13002218.translation: For the health of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix Sarmaticus Germanicus Britannicus, father of the fatherland, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 12th time, imperator for the 7th time, consul for the 5th time, Marcus Cosinius Celerinus, son of Marcus, of the tribe Quirina, promised and presented at his own expense a gladiatorial show and a hunt of various kinds of tusked and tame beasts, and also of beasts that fight on the ground, in the colony Veneria Rusicade.
EDCS-13002218.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Marcus Cosinius Celerinus, promising a gladiatorial show and animal hunt in honor of Emperor Commodus, likely from the late 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002222.translation: Gaius Granius Larensis, son of Caius, of the tribe Quirina, most devoted to his divinity, with his sons Granius Aculinus, Saturnus, and Festus, placed this at his own expense, a space granted by decree of the decurions, for the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 18th time, imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, son of the deified Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and of the deified Nerva, our invincible lord and Augustus.
EDCS-13002222.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Gaius Granius Larensis and his sons in honor of Emperor Caracalla, likely around 213 AD, with the site granted by local authorities.

EDCS-13002223.translation: To the divinity of Constantine, most holy and invincible.
EDCS-13002223.summary: This is a brief dedication to the divinity of Emperor Constantine, likely from the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-13002224.translation: In honor of the magnificent times of the greatest princes and lords of the world, Valentinian and Valens, ever Augusti, granaries were constructed with all haste for the security of the Roman people and provincials alike, and were dedicated by Publilius Ceionius Caecina Albinus, a most distinguished man, ex-consul, and sexfascalis of the province of Numidia Constantina.
EDCS-13002224.summary: This inscription records the dedication of granaries by Publilius Ceionius Caecina Albinus, an ex-consul and official, during the reign of Emperors Valentinian and Valens in the mid-4th century AD.

EDCS-13002225.translation: at the urging of Aelius Ampelius, a prominent man and perpetual flamen.
EDCS-13002225.summary: This inscription fragment mentions Aelius Ampelius, a prominent local priest, possibly related to the preceding or following inscription.

EDCS-13002226.translation: Lucius Cornelius Restitutus, prefect of the Flavian Pannonian fleet, dedicated this rare gift to his wife Pomponia Germanilla, a most distinguished woman, and to Claudius Claudianus, ex-consul of the two Pannoniae.
EDCS-13002226.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Cornelius Restitutus, a naval prefect, to his wife and a former governor.

EDCS-13002227.translation: Quintus Austurnius Lappianus, with his sons, decorated with the public horse, formerly aedile of four colonies, prefect of three colonies, twice ducenarius, brother of Tiberius Claudius Claudianus, ex-consul of the provinces and armies of Lower and Upper Pannonia, overseer of Dacian detachments, legate of legions XIII Gemina and V Macedonica Pia, candidate of the Augusti, and most devoted to them, a praetorian tutor, a priest of the seven overseers of the feast, a priest of Laurentes Lavinates, erected this at his own expense for his wife Gallitta, daughter of Publius, of the tribe Quirina, sister of Tiberius Claudius Claudianus, legate of the Augusti with pro-praetorian power of the provinces and armies of Lower and Upper Pannonia, overseer of Dacian detachments, legate of legions XIII Gemina and V Macedonica Pia, candidate of the Augusti, and most devoted to them, a praetorian tutor, a priest of the seven overseers of the feast, a priest of Laurentes Lavinates, a rare woman, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-13002227.summary: This lengthy inscription details the erection of a monument by Quintus Austurnius Lappianus for his wife Gallitta, listing his own extensive career and her family connections, including her brother Tiberius Claudius Claudianus, a prominent military and administrative figure, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13002229.translation: Tiberius Claudius Sabinus, former first spearman, made this while alive.
EDCS-13002229.summary: This inscription records a monument erected during the lifetime of Tiberius Claudius Sabinus, a former centurion.

EDCS-13002230.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Ollius Primigenius, a soldier of the Fourth Flavian Legion, with nineteen years of service, who lived for thirty-five years. Aelius Sabinus, his heir, and Italicus, his freedman, saw to its construction.
EDCS-13002230.summary: A funerary epitaph for a soldier of the Fourth Flavian Legion, commissioned by his heir and freedman.

EDCS-13002231.translation: Macrinus, a soldier of the Second Legion, lived for fifty-five years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002231.summary: A funerary epitaph for a soldier of the Second Legion, indicating his burial place and age.

EDCS-13002234.translation: Gaius Caecilius Gallus, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, holding the public horse, aedile, holding judicial authority as quaestor pro praetore, prefect for the II Viri, prefect of the Fabri, consular for the II time, and praetorian for the II time, holding quinquennial honors by decree of the decurions, from three out of five decuries, quinquennial prefect with judicial authority, priest of the Divine Julius for Rusicada, on his own behalf and that of Proxinia Procula, daughter of Marcus, his wife, and his children Galla and Gallus and Coruncania and Nigellina, constructed the tribunal and rostra with his own money. Gaius Caecilius Gallus, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, with his own money.
EDCS-13002234.summary: A lengthy dedication by Gaius Caecilius Gallus, a prominent local official and priest, detailing his various offices and his personal funding of public works including a tribunal and rostra, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13002235.translation: To the departed spirits of Caecilia Nigellina, daughter of Caecilius Gallus, priest of the province.
EDCS-13002235.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Nigellina, daughter of a provincial priest named Caecilius Gallus.

EDCS-13002236.translation: Marcus Fabius Fronto, augur, prefect with judicial authority, dedicated, along with theatrical performances, in addition to one thousand denarii, for the work of the theater, in the name of his son Senecio. Dedicated by the same consuls, the day before the Kalends of April.
EDCS-13002236.summary: A dedication by Marcus Fabius Fronto, an augur and official, of funds and theatrical games for the theater, in honor of his son Senecio, with a date provided by the consuls and calendar.

EDCS-13002237.translation: Marcus Fabius Fronto, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, augur, prefect with judicial authority, for the honor of his prefecture gave, in addition to the offering of denarii, in the name of his son Senecio, for the upkeep of the theater.
EDCS-13002237.summary: A dedication by Marcus Fabius Fronto, an augur and official, for the theater's upkeep, in honor of his son Senecio, likely associated with his prefecture.

EDCS-13002238.translation: Sextus Otacilius Restitutus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, brother of Marcus Otacilius Fructus, a pontiff, III vir, aedile, holding quaestorian power, augur, in addition to 20,000 sesterces legally due for the honor of the aedileship for the Republic, and 6,000 sesterces for the day of the games, and 34,000 sesterces legally due there for the honor of his augurship for the Republic, and for the 4,000 sesterces which he had promised for public entertainment, having made an additional contribution himself, he placed two *dextros* with his own money and dedicated them by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-13002238.summary: An inscription detailing Sextus Otacilius Restitutus' generous financial contributions to the Republic for his offices as III vir, aedile, and augur, including funds for games and public entertainment, dedicated by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-13002239.translation: Sextus Otacilius Restitutus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, brother of Marcus Otacilius Fructus, a pontiff, III vir, aedile, holding quaestorian power, augur, in addition to 20,000 sesterces legally due for the honor of the aedileship for the Republic, and 6,000 sesterces for the day of the games, and 34,000 sesterces legally due there for the honor of his augurship for the Republic, and for the 4,000 sesterces which he had promised for public entertainment, having made an additional contribution himself, he made two *dextros* with his own money and dedicated them by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-13002239.summary: An inscription detailing Sextus Otacilius Restitutus' generous financial contributions to the Republic for his offices as III vir, aedile, and augur, including funds for games and public entertainment, dedicated by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-13002240.summary: A fragment identifying an individual as a perpetual priest, son of Marcus.

EDCS-13002241.translation: To the perpetual [flamen] Sex[tius? ...] and Semiti...
EDCS-13002241.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning a perpetual priest and possibly other individuals, likely part of a dedication or funerary text.

EDCS-13002242.translation: Liberalis Junior and Liberia, his children, illuminated the vaults and joining structures with thirteen lamps, and decorated them with marble screens, adding two dolphins along the paths of the theatre. With fourteen marble slabs laid down along the upward slopes and the paths on the right and left, they made and dedicated them. The space was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-13002242.summary: This inscription records Liberalis Junior and Liberia's dedication of theatre improvements, funded by a decree of the local council, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-13002243.translation: Liberalis Junior and Liberia, his children, cleaned most of it and dedicated it after it had collapsed from age.
EDCS-13002243.summary: This inscription records Liberalis Junior and Liberia's restoration of a damaged structure, granted by decree of the decurions, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-13002246.translation: Envious points order the skilled one to play happily.
EDCS-13002246.summary: This is a hexameter verse, likely a school exercise or a witty epigram, with no specific historical context.

EDCS-13002247.translation: Pax sibi, alive, Antonius placed this altar as owner of this place, who has a wife and children. Having come to his maternal home to undertake business, he joined the sacred altar to the Gods of the Underworld. Antonius Pax lived 70 years. He is buried here. [---] Sambucus.
EDCS-13002247.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonius Pax, who lived 70 years and dedicated an altar, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002248.translation: Aelius Primus lived 57 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002248.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Aelius Primus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002249.translation: Sacred to the Gods of the Underworld. Lucio Aemilio, son of Lucius Restitutus, lived 28 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well. Sacred to the Gods of the Underworld. Lolliae, daughter of Lucius Pacata, lived 52 years. She is buried here. To his most devoted sister. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13002249.summary: This epitaph records the burials of Lucio Aemilio Restituto and Lolliae Pacata, with wishes for their repose, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002250.translation: Ingenua, the only daughter of Afraniorum Secundus and Olympias, chaste and modest, lived 21 years. Most devoted wife.
EDCS-13002250.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ingenua, daughter of Secundus and Olympias, and wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002251.translation: Sacred to the Gods of the Underworld. Albanius Honestus, also known as Secundianus, lived 35 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002251.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Albanius Honestus Secundianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002252.translation: Sacred to the Gods of the Underworld. Albanius Nepos lived 8 years and 7 months. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002252.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Albanius Nepos, a child, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002253.translation: Sacred to the Gods of the Underworld. Albania Faustina lived 30 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13002253.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Albania Faustina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002254.translation: Aulus Albius Rufus lived for 105 years.
EDCS-13002254.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Aulus Albius Rufus.

EDCS-13002255.translation: He lived for 58 years, 5 months, and 27 days with Albinus Felicio, his spouse; he lived for 61 years, 9 months, and 19 days, and with Sittius Martialis, he lived for 65 years and 13 days.
EDCS-13002255.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespans of a man and possibly his spouse and another individual, likely within a family context.

EDCS-13002256.translation: Alexander the younger to his dearest son, Alexander the father.
EDCS-13002256.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial from a father named Alexander to his son, also named Alexander.

EDCS-13002257.translation: Quintus Antonius Extricatus, son of Quintus of the Quirina tribe, lived for 50 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13002257.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial place of Quintus Antonius Extricatus.

EDCS-13002258.translation: Lucius Antonius Fuscus lived for 31 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13002258.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and burial place of Lucius Antonius Fuscus.

EDCS-13002259.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Lucius Antonius Martialis, son of Marcus of the Quirina tribe, lived for 45 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13002259.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the underworld spirits, recording the lifespan and burial place of Lucius Antonius Martialis.

EDCS-13002260.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Antonius Pontianus lived for 47 years.
EDCS-13002260.summary: This funerary inscription dedicated to the underworld spirits records the lifespan of Antonius Pontianus.

EDCS-13002261.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating Lucius Antonius Rogatus, possibly his name on a monument or tomb.

EDCS-13002262.translation: Marcus Antonius Quadratus lived for 28 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13002262.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and burial place of Marcus Antonius Quadratus.

EDCS-13002263.translation: Marcus Antonius Severus lived for 40 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13002263.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and burial place of Marcus Antonius Severus.

EDCS-13002264.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Antonia Issa lived 39 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13002264.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Issa, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002265.translation: Aponia, daughter of Lucius, Boutia, lived 30 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002265.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aponia Boutia, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002267.translation: Aurelia Fortunata lived 31 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002267.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Fortunata, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002268.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Bonosus, also called Philippus, lived 35 years. Valeria Aucta placed him, her most devoted son, here.
EDCS-13002268.summary: This funerary epitaph records Bonosus (Philippus) and was set up by his mother Valeria Aucta, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002269.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Buttius Viator lived 90 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-13002269.summary: This funerary epitaph for Lucius Buttius Viator dates from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002270.translation: C. Clara lived 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002270.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for C. Clara, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002271.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. C. Fortuna lived 90 years, three months, eight hours.
EDCS-13002271.summary: This funerary epitaph for C. Fortuna records her lifespan, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002272.translation: Marcus Caecilius Africanus, son of Marcus, of the tribe Quirina, lived 65 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002272.summary: This funerary epitaph for Marcus Caecilius Africanus dates from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002273.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Caecilius Maximus lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002273.summary: This funerary epitaph for Caius Caecilius Maximus dates from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002274.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Caelius Gallosus lived 75 years. Nigidia Veneria placed this for her devoted and deserving husband.
EDCS-13002274.summary: This funerary epitaph for Lucius Caelius Gallosus was set up by his wife Nigidia Veneria, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002275.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Quintus Caesius Commodus lived 22 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002275.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Caesius Commodus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-13002276.translation: Quintus Caesius Crencens, of the Quirina tribe, lived 84 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002276.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Caesius Crencens, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-13002277.translation: To the Divine Manes. Calvidia Issula lived 80 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002277.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Calvidia Issula, recording her age and burial.

EDCS-13002278.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Casia Quinta lived 70 years.
EDCS-13002278.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Casia Quinta, recording her age.

EDCS-13002279.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely a dedication or part of a larger monument for Tiberius Claudius Crescentianus.

EDCS-13002280.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Claudius Felix, recording his age.

EDCS-13002281.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Claudius Fortunatus lived 5 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002281.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudius Fortunatus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-13002282.translation: Tiberius Claudius Helenus lived 80 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13002282.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Helenus, recording his age, burial, and a wish for his afterlife.

EDCS-13002283.translation: Lucius Clodius Dionysius lived 31 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002283.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Clodius Dionysius, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-13002284.translation: Marcus Clodius Macer, 20 years old. His father made this for his son, who was jugulated.
EDCS-13002284.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Clodius Macer, recording his age and noting he was murdered, with his father commissioning the monument.

EDCS-13002285.translation: Clodia Serena, daughter of Lucius, lived happily until the day she died, born [she lived] 5 years and 35 days; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002285.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and burial of Clodia Serena, a young girl.

EDCS-13002286.translation: Lucius Cornelius Priscus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002286.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and burial of Lucius Cornelius Priscus.

EDCS-13002287.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Cornelius Restutus lived 4 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002287.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to the Manes, recording the short life and burial of Cornelius Restutus.

EDCS-13002288.translation: Cornelia Eutychis, freedwoman of Lucius, lived 80 years.
EDCS-13002288.summary: This tombstone records the life of Cornelia Eutychis, a freedwoman.

EDCS-13002289.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Cornelia Festa, daughter of M., lived 31 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002289.summary: This tombstone, dedicated to the Manes, records the life and burial of Cornelia Festa.

EDCS-13002290.translation: Crescens lived 38 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002290.summary: This tombstone records the life and burial of Crescens.

EDCS-13002291.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Crisius Faustus lived 40 years.
EDCS-13002291.summary: This inscription, dedicated to the Manes, records the life of Marcus Crisius Faustus.

EDCS-13002292.translation: Dido, daughter of Pactumeius, lived 18 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002292.summary: This tombstone records the life and burial of Dido, daughter of Pactumeius.

EDCS-13002293.translation: Lucius Domitius Urbanus lived 20 years. Domitia Paula lived 10 years; they are buried here.
EDCS-13002293.summary: This tombstone records the lives and burial of Lucius Domitius Urbanus and Domitia Paula.

EDCS-13002294.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Donata lived 5 years.
EDCS-13002294.summary: This inscription, dedicated to the Manes, records the short life of Donata.

EDCS-13002295.translation: Dupidia Anulla, daughter of Publius, [dedicates this] to Publius Honoratus, her daughter.
EDCS-13002295.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Dupidia Anulla to Publius Honoratus, likely a family memorial.

EDCS-13002296.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Etereius Nauarchus, freedman of Publius, lived 41 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002296.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Etereius Nauarchus, who died at the age of 41.

EDCS-13002297.translation: Hetereia Musa, freedwoman of Publius, lived 101 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002297.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Hetereia Musa, who died at the age of 101.

EDCS-13002299.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Fabius Anthus lived 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002299.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Fabius Anthus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-13002300.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fabius Marcianus lived 11 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002300.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fabius Marcianus, who died at the age of 11.

EDCS-13002301.translation: Fabia, daughter of Lucius, lived 90 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002301.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fabia, daughter of Lucius, who died at the age of 90.

EDCS-13002302.summary: This inscription records the age of Fabia Exoche at her death.

EDCS-13002304.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fabia Sympherusa lived 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002304.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fabia Sympherusa, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-13002305.translation: Flavonia Extricata, daughter of Lucius, lived 65 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002305.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavonia Extricata, who died at the age of 65.

EDCS-13002306.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fuficia Maia, the dutiful one, lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002306.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fuficia Maia, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-13002307.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld, in memory of Fulcinia Procula, mother, and Fabi Proculus, son.
EDCS-13002307.summary: This funerary inscription is a memorial to Fulcinia Procula and Fabi Proculus.

EDCS-13002308.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Fulvia Rufina, dutiful, lived 58 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13002308.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Fulvia Rufina.

EDCS-13002309.translation: Fustius Babius lived 15 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13002309.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Fustius Babius.

EDCS-13002310.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Cratus Nepos lived 5 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13002310.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Gaius Cratus Nepos.

EDCS-13002311.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Garrulius Felix, 65 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13002311.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Garrulius Felix.

EDCS-13002312.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Gavius Primigenius lived 60 years; he set this up in his own lifetime.
EDCS-13002312.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Gavius Primigenius, who set up his own tombstone during his lifetime.

EDCS-13002313.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gavia Trophime, daughter of Quintus, lived 20 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13002313.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Gavia Trophime.

EDCS-13002314.translation: Caius Gellius Ponticus, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, lived 55 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13002314.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Caius Gellius Ponticus.

EDCS-13002315.translation: Lucius Geminius Potitus lived 5 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13002315.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Lucius Geminius Potitus.

EDCS-13002317.translation: Gududio lived 25 years and 2 months, is buried here.
EDCS-13002317.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Gududio.

EDCS-13002318.translation: Horatia Achaica, daughter of Caius, lived 49 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002318.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Horatia Achaica.

EDCS-13002319.translation: Ianuaria Ratila lived 25 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13002319.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ianuaria Ratila, wishing her peaceful rest.

EDCS-13002320.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Ianuaria lived 5 years and 8 months; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002320.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young Ianuaria, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-13002321.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Ianuaria lived 55 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002321.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Ianuaria, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-13002322.translation: Instania Fortunata, her dearest daughter Cusina, lived [number] years.
EDCS-13002322.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph for Instania Fortunata and her daughter Cusina.

EDCS-13002324.translation: Aulus Iulius Felix, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 68 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002324.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Aulus Iulius Felix.

EDCS-13002325.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sextus Iulius Felix lived 1 year; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002325.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child named Sextus Iulius Felix, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-13002326.translation: Marcus Iulius Fronto, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 21 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002326.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Marcus Iulius Fronto.

EDCS-13002327.translation: Quintus Iulius Gallus, son of Aulus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002327.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Quintus Iulius Gallus.

EDCS-13002328.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Impuritanus lived 31 years.
EDCS-13002328.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes for Iulius Impuritanus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-13002329.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Marinus lived 105 years; his bones are laid here.
EDCS-13002329.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Marinus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-13002330.translation: Gaius Julius Meridianus lived 21 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-13002330.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Meridianus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-13002331.translation: Lucius Julius, grandson of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, Opnus, lived 20 years.
EDCS-13002331.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Julius, recording his age at death and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-13002332.translation: Iulius Rufinus lived 35 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-13002332.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Rufinus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-13002334.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Vindemi lived 9 years.
EDCS-13002334.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Vindemi, recording his age at death.

EDCS-13002335.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Buturaria lived 36 years.
EDCS-13002335.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Buturaria, recording her age at death.

EDCS-13002336.translation: To Iulia Cyrilla, daughter of Quintus, his most devoted wife. Marcus Iunius Optatus, her husband.
EDCS-13002336.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcus Iunius Optatus to his wife Iulia Cyrilla.

EDCS-13002337.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Extricata lived 51 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-13002337.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Extricata, recording her age at death.

EDCS-13002338.translation: Iulia Faustilla, daughter of Tiberius, lived 50 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-13002338.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Faustilla, recording her age at death.

EDCS-13002339.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Felicitas was buried at the age of 50.
EDCS-13002339.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Felicitas, recording her age at death.

EDCS-13002340.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Iulia Hellas.

EDCS-13002341.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Macia lived for 93 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002341.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan and burial place of Iulia Macia.

EDCS-13002342.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Severa, daughter of Caius, lived for 45 years. May your bones rest well, pious wife.
EDCS-13002342.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to Iulia Severa, recording her lifespan and expressing a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-13002343.translation: Iulia Urbana, daughter of Lucius, lived for 26 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002343.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan and burial place of Iulia Urbana.

EDCS-13002344.translation: Iulia Urbanilla, daughter of Aulus, lived for 60 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002344.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan and burial place of Iulia Urbanilla.

EDCS-13002345.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iunia Maubbal lived for 80 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002345.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan and burial place of Iunia Maubbal.

EDCS-13002346.translation: Lucia Posilna lived for 81 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002346.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan and burial place of Lucia Posilna.

EDCS-13002347.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. L. Sisus lived for 10 months; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002347.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan and burial place of L. Sisus, who died as an infant.

EDCS-13002348.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Licinius Macrinus lived for 25 years.
EDCS-13002348.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Lucius Licinius Macrinus.

EDCS-13002350.translation: Lucius Lorenius Eurydamas erected this in memory of his wife, Lorenia Privata, and his son, Lorenius Privatus.
EDCS-13002350.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Lorenius Eurydamas to commemorate his wife and son.

EDCS-13002351.translation: Lucilia Spes, daughter of Marcus, lived 21 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13002351.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Lucilia Spes.

EDCS-13002352.translation: Lucretia Restituta, daughter of Marcus, lived 25 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13002352.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Lucretia Restituta.

EDCS-13002353.translation: Caius Lurius Fidus, son of Quintus, lived 21 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002353.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Caius Lurius Fidus.

EDCS-13002354.translation: To the departed spirits. M. Marcianus lived 12 years.
EDCS-13002354.summary: A tombstone dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the life of M. Marcianus.

EDCS-13002355.translation: Lucius Manilius Urbanus lived 45 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002355.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Lucius Manilius Urbanus.

EDCS-13002356.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Marcius Crescens, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 20 years.
EDCS-13002356.summary: A tombstone dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the life of Publius Marcius Crescens.

EDCS-13002357.translation: Maridia Ingenua lived 50 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13002357.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Maridia Ingenua.

EDCS-13002358.summary: A tombstone recording the life of Meridianus.

EDCS-13002359.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Minucius Felix lived 25 years.
EDCS-13002359.summary: A tombstone dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the life of Quintus Minucius Felix.

EDCS-13002360.translation: Caius Modius Pirsinus, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, dutiful, lived 36 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-13002360.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Caius Modius Pirsinus, with a common epitaph wishing rest for the deceased.

EDCS-13002361.translation: Oppia Kara, daughter of Naevius Oppius, lived 75 years, is laid here.
EDCS-13002361.summary: This is a tombstone for Oppia Kara, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-13002362.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Ovinius Nicanro lived 55 years.
EDCS-13002362.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits and records the life span of Quintus Ovinius Nicanro as 55 years.

EDCS-13002363.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Pacius Valerianus lived 80 years.
EDCS-13002363.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits and records the life span of Lucius Pacius Valerianus as 80 years.

EDCS-13002364.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Pactumeius Diadumenus lived 82 years, is placed here.
EDCS-13002364.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits and records the life span of Publius Pactumeius Diadumenus as 82 years.

EDCS-13002365.translation: To the departed spirits. Pactimeia Dubitata, daughter of Publius Pactumeius Trophimus, lived 21 years, is laid here.
EDCS-13002365.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits and records the life span of Pactimeia Dubitata, daughter of Publius Pactumeius Trophimus, as 21 years.

EDCS-13002366.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Pacuvius Adventus lived 65 years.
EDCS-13002366.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits and records the life span of Quintus Pacuvius Adventus as 65 years.

EDCS-13002367.translation: To the departed spirits. Perelia Monnula lived 41 years, is laid here.
EDCS-13002367.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits and records the life span of Perelia Monnula as 41 years.

EDCS-13002368.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Petronius Vitalio lived 2 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13002368.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits and records the life span of Caius Petronius Vitalio as 2 years.

EDCS-13002369.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Pompeius Crescens lived 21 years, is placed here.
EDCS-13002369.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits and records the life span of Quintus Pompeius Crescens as 21 years.

EDCS-13002370.translation: Pompeia Chia lived 25 years, is laid here. May it be granted to my daughter to live chastely, so that she may learn to love her husband from our example.
EDCS-13002370.summary: This tombstone for Pompeia Chia, who lived 25 years, also contains a mother's wish for her daughter's future married life.

EDCS-13002371.translation: Pompeia Extricata, daughter of Quintus, lived 12 years; here she lies.
EDCS-13002371.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Pompeia Extricata, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-13002372.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Pomponia Monnosa lived 21 years; here she lies.
EDCS-13002372.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Pomponia Monnosa, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-13002373.translation: Porcia Agathemeris, daughter of Porcius, lived 75 years; here she lies.
EDCS-13002373.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Porcia Agathemeris, who died at the age of 75.

EDCS-13002374.translation: Porcia, daughter of Marcus (and) daughter of Quintus Maximus, lived 81 years; here she lies.
EDCS-13002374.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Porcia, daughter of Marcus and Quintus Maximus, who died at the age of 81.

EDCS-13002375.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quinta Veta lived 35 years.
EDCS-13002375.summary: An epitaph for Quinta Veta, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-13002376.translation: Iulia Regilla, daughter of Gaius, lived 75 years; here she lies.
EDCS-13002376.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Iulia Regilla, who died at the age of 75.

EDCS-13002377.translation: Remmia Chrysoporusa, from her own property.
EDCS-13002377.summary: An inscription likely indicating a dedication or burial made at the expense of Remmia Chrysoporusa.

EDCS-13002378.translation: Rubria Titulla, daughter of Titus, dutiful, lived 56 years; here she lies.
EDCS-13002378.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Rubria Titulla, who died at the age of 56.

EDCS-13002379.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Urbanus lived 85 years.
EDCS-13002379.summary: An epitaph for Urbanus, who died at the age of 85.

EDCS-13002380.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. S. Elpis lived 35 years.
EDCS-13002380.summary: An epitaph for S. Elpis, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-13002381.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Sadiarius lived 45 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002381.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Gaius Sadiarius.

EDCS-13002382.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Sallustius Fronto lived 62 years.
EDCS-13002382.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Gaius Sallustius Fronto.

EDCS-13002383.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sapiria lived 60 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002383.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Sapiria.

EDCS-13002385.translation: To the departed spirits. Satrius Clemens lived 85 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002385.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Satrius Clemens.

EDCS-13002386.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Satrius Muntanus lived 80 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002386.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Lucius Satrius Muntanus.

EDCS-13002388.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Scantius Felix lived 22 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002388.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Quintus Scantius Felix.

EDCS-13002389.translation: To the departed spirits. Seia Euticis lived [years]...
EDCS-13002389.summary: This incomplete funerary epitaph begins to record the lifespan of Seia Euticis.

EDCS-13002390.translation: To the departed spirits. Seia Mirsine lived 40 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002390.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Seia Mirsine.

EDCS-13002391.translation: To the departed spirits. Seia Taberna the pious lived 80 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002391.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Seia Taberna.

EDCS-13002392.translation: To the departed spirits. Sempronia, daughter of Amaryllis, lived 24 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002392.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Sempronia, daughter of Amaryllis.

EDCS-13002393.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits: Senior Saturninus made this for himself and his own.
EDCS-13002393.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Senior Saturninus for himself and his family.

EDCS-13002394.translation: Septimuleia Primigenia lived 55 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002394.summary: This is a tombstone for Septimuleia Primigenia, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-13002395.translation: Lucius Sergius Ispes, who lived 84 years.
EDCS-13002395.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and lifespan of Lucius Sergius Ispes.

EDCS-13002396.translation: Caius Sergius Rufus lived 81 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002396.summary: This tombstone records the name and lifespan of Caius Sergius Rufus.

EDCS-13002397.translation: Marcus Servilius Rufinus lived 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002397.summary: This tombstone records the name and lifespan of Marcus Servilius Rufinus.

EDCS-13002398.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits: He lived 21 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002398.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and burial of an unnamed individual.

EDCS-13002399.summary: This inscription records the name and lifespan of Silicia Hospitalis.

EDCS-13002401.translation: Publius Sittius Celsus lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002401.summary: This tombstone records the name and lifespan of Publius Sittius Celsus.

EDCS-13002402.translation: Caius Sittius Felix lived 81 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002402.summary: This tombstone records the name and lifespan of Caius Sittius Felix.

EDCS-13002403.translation: Lucius Sittius Orfitus lived 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002403.summary: This tombstone records the name and lifespan of Lucius Sittius Orfitus.

EDCS-13002404.translation: Publius Sittius Peregrinus lived for 18 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002404.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Sittius Peregrinus, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-13002405.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Sittia Ingenua lived for 35 years.
EDCS-13002405.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes, recording the life of Sittia Ingenua, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-13002406.translation: Sittia Rustica, daughter of Sextus, lived for 46 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002406.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sittia Rustica, daughter of Sextus, who died at the age of 46.

EDCS-13002407.translation: Sittia Silvana, daughter of Marcus, lived for 20 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002407.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sittia Silvana, daughter of Marcus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-13002408.translation: Publius Sulpicius Marinus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 25 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002408.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Sulpicius Marinus, son of Quintus, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-13002409.translation: Quintus Nonius Felix lived for 41 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002409.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Nonius Felix, who died at the age of 41.

EDCS-13002410.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Tilius Campanus lived for 23 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002410.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes, recording the life of Tilius Campanus, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-13002411.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. [He/She] of Quintus Tilioficiosus lived for 35 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002411.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes, likely for a relative of Quintus Tilioficiosus, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-13002412.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Totabia lived for 30 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002412.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes, recording the life of Totabia, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-13002413.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Tranius Cicliocci, son of [---], lived for 20 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13002413.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes, recording the life of Lucius Tranius Cicliocci, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-13002414.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Trebius Proculus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 11 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002414.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of a young boy, Marcus Trebius Proculus, who died at the age of 11.

EDCS-13002415.translation: Trebonius Crescens lived 13 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002415.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of a young boy, Trebonius Crescens, who died at the age of 13.

EDCS-13002416.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Tutilia Rogata lived 55 years.
EDCS-13002416.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Tutilia Rogata, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-13002417.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Valerius Festus lived 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002417.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Marcus Valerius Festus, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-13002418.translation: Valeria Urbana, daughter of Lucius, lived 85 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002418.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Valeria Urbana, who died at the age of 85.

EDCS-13002419.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius Ventilius Martialis lived 81 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002419.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Gaius Ventilius Martialis, who died at the age of 81.

EDCS-13002420.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Victor, who was also called Apiusius, lived 7 years, 7 months, and 5 days; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002420.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of a young boy named Victor (also known as Apiusius), who died at the age of 7 years, 7 months, and 5 days.

EDCS-13002421.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Vinicia Maxima, daughter of Caius, lived 43 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002421.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Vinicia Maxima, who died at the age of 43.

EDCS-13002423.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Himera lived 5 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002423.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of a young girl named Himera, who died at the age of 5.

EDCS-13002435.translation: Envious one, beware what is before your eyes.
EDCS-13002435.summary: This is a warning inscription, likely intended to deter potential grave robbers or those who might disturb the deceased.

EDCS-13002436.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vandia Procula, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002437.translation: Flavia Amanda, a citizen of Pannonia, rests here; she lived 8 years and 12 days.
EDCS-13002437.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavia Amanda, a young girl from Pannonia, recording her age at death.

EDCS-13002438.translation: May the spirit of Marinianus bring good fortune; may God ward off [evil].
EDCS-13002438.summary: This is a short inscription invoking good spirits and divine protection, possibly related to a burial or a protective charm.

EDCS-13002439.translation: Here rests in peace, of blessed memory, Iobina, a most distinguished woman, who lived more or less 75 years, interred on the eighth day before the Kalends of June in the consulship of the most distinguished man Eacus.
EDCS-13002439.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iobina, a noble woman, recording her age and burial date within a specific consulship, likely from the Late Roman period.

EDCS-13002440.translation: To Castor Augustus, sacred. Caecilia Domitilla fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-13002440.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Castor by Caecilia Domitilla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002442.translation: Honoratus, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, prefect for administering justice, for the triumvirate.
EDCS-13002442.summary: This inscription records the career or an appointment of Honoratus, a prefect and triumvir, possibly related to local administration in the Roman period.

EDCS-13002443.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gemina Pacata lived 41 years.
EDCS-13002443.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gemina Pacata, recording her age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002465.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Arruntia Saturnina lived 30 years. Aelius Lelita made this for his dearest wife here.
EDCS-13002465.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Arruntia Saturnina, erected by her husband Aelius Lelita, recording her age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002466.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Avidia Iulia lived 85 years. Secundus and Iulianus, sons, made this for their dearest mother and dedicated it.
EDCS-13002466.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Avidia Iulia, erected by her sons Secundus and Iulianus, recording her age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002467.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Catellus lived 55 years. Gaius Julius Emeritus made this pious monument at his own expense.
EDCS-13002467.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Catellus, erected by Gaius Julius Emeritus, recording the age of the deceased and the expense of the monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002468.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Octavius Martialis lived 85 years and is buried here. Octavia Saphoica made this for her devoted father.
EDCS-13002468.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Lucius Octavius Martialis and his burial, with the monument erected by his daughter Octavia Saphoica.

EDCS-13002469.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Passinicus the devoted lived 33 years.
EDCS-13002469.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Passinicus, likely a soldier given the context of other inscriptions.

EDCS-13002470.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Postumius Felix lived 91 years. His sons made this.
EDCS-13002470.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Postumius Felix, whose sons erected the monument after his death at the age of 91.

EDCS-13002471.translation: For IVL IS PEO. Tullius Basileus made the monument for his father.
EDCS-13002471.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Tullius Basileus erected a monument for his father.

EDCS-13002472.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Optatula lived 65 years. Caius Tullius Viator made this for his most beloved and well-deserving mother.
EDCS-13002472.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Optatula, with the monument erected by her son Caius Tullius Viator.

EDCS-13002473.translation: Iuliae Maiori, most beloved mother, lived 70 years. Iulius Valerius, a soldier of the II Cohort of Hamii, placed the monument for her, who well deserved it.
EDCS-13002473.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iuliae Maiori, erected by her son Iulius Valerius, a soldier of the II Cohort of Hamii.

EDCS-13002474.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription recording the exceptional lifespan of Germanus.

EDCS-13002478.translation: To his incomparable and most chaste wife, who had a rare affection, Marcus Pomentinus Maior, a centurion of the III Augustan Legion, made this monument for his wife, who well deserved it, of 18 years.
EDCS-13002478.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Marcus Pomentinus Maior, a centurion, for his wife, who died at the age of 18, praising her virtues and affection.

EDCS-13002479.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fabia Rogatina lived 55 years. Marcus Amphius made this for his most devoted mother. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-13002479.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fabia Rogatina, with the monument erected by her son Marcus Amphius, including a common funerary wish.

EDCS-13002480.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Saturninus lived 33 years. His devoted father, Iulius Flavianus, who lived a devoted life, made this.
EDCS-13002480.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Saturninus, erected by his father Iulius Flavianus, highlighting their devotion.

EDCS-13002481.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Licuria Quintula lived for 35 years. Felicius made (this).
EDCS-13002481.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Licuria Quintula by Felicius, likely her husband or relative.

EDCS-13002482.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Theodotus lived for 2 years and 29 days. Augendus made (this) for his son.
EDCS-13002482.summary: A funerary inscription recording the short life of Theodotus, dedicated by his father Augendus.

EDCS-13002483.translation: In memory of the blessed Montanus.
EDCS-13002483.summary: A memorial inscription dedicated to the memory of a blessed individual named Montanus.

EDCS-13002484.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilia Victoria lived for 31 years and 6 days. Her parents placed (this) for their most sweet and loving daughter, who is buried here.
EDCS-13002484.summary: A funerary inscription for Aemilia Victoria, dedicated by her parents, emphasizing her loving nature.

EDCS-13002488.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Camerius Lucrio lived for 62 years. His sons made (this).
EDCS-13002488.summary: A funerary inscription for Camerius Lucrio, dedicated by his sons.

EDCS-13002489.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Fortunata lived for 70 years. Her devoted son made (this).
EDCS-13002489.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Fortunata, dedicated by her devoted son.

EDCS-13002490.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gorgias lived for 21 years.
EDCS-13002490.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Gorgias at the age of 21.

EDCS-13002491.translation: To Ianuarius, who lived for 20 years. Made (this).
EDCS-13002491.summary: A funerary inscription for Ianuarius, who died at age 20.

EDCS-13002492.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gargilius Macer lived for 10 years.
EDCS-13002492.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Gargilius Macer at the age of 10.

EDCS-13002493.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Annius Primus lived for 70 years.
EDCS-13002493.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Caius Annius Primus at the age of 70.

EDCS-13002494.translation: Marcus Granius Secundus, son of Publius, gladly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Augustus at this altar.
EDCS-13002494.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Augustus by Marcus Granius Secundus.

EDCS-13002495.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Felix Ia, aged 120, is buried here, by his sons for his father.
EDCS-13002495.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Felix Ia, recording his age and burial by his sons.

EDCS-13002496.summary: This is a brief inscription indicating the origin or maker of a work, likely architectural or artistic.

EDCS-13002502.translation: The names of the perfect martyrs: Mettun, Secundus, Donatus, Miggin, Baric, Felix, Crescentianus, Iader, Minucius, Stiddinus, Miggin, Stiddinus.
EDCS-13002502.summary: This inscription lists the names of martyrs.

EDCS-13002503.translation: Lucius Rogatus lived in peace for 37 years and 3 months.
EDCS-13002503.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Rogatus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-13002505.translation: No one should begrudge my death with envy, and in your resurrection, I, Casthe, a nun, lived for 36 years.
EDCS-13002505.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Casthe, a nun, mentioning her age and a belief in resurrection.

EDCS-13002514.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Comocius the pious lived for 103 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002514.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Comocius, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-13002515.translation: To Trajan Hadrian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 7th time, Father of the Fatherland, Consul for the 3rd time, by decree of the decurions, with public funds.
EDCS-13002515.summary: This is an official dedication of a public work or monument to Emperor Hadrian, listing his titles and the funding source.

EDCS-13002519.translation: Philipus of Cyrene lived for 105 years.
EDCS-13002519.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Philipus of Cyrene, recording his age.

EDCS-13002520.translation: Iulia Cai Barahi lived for 70 years.
EDCS-13002520.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Cai Barahi, recording her age.

EDCS-13002521.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lollia Vitrix lived 25 years.
EDCS-13002521.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lollia Vitrix, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-13002522.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Virtia, aged 80.
EDCS-13002522.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Virtia, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-13002524.translation: Alikia lived 18 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002524.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Alikia, who died at the age of 18 and is buried here.

EDCS-13002525.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. B. C. Mona lived 33 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13002525.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for B. C. Mona, who died at the age of 33 and is buried here.

EDCS-13002631.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. FLCIIVNV REDI lived 63 years at Bonnae.
EDCS-13002631.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for FLCIIVNV REDI, who lived 63 years and died at Bonnae.

EDCS-13002633.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Autronius Rogatus Augentianus lived 19 years, 2 months, and 3 days.
EDCS-13002633.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Autronius Rogatus Augentianus, who died at the age of 19 years, 2 months, and 3 days.

EDCS-13002634.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Demetrius Victor, born here, lived 58 years, is buried here. His most dutiful son made this.
EDCS-13002634.summary: This funerary epitaph records Demetrius Victor, who lived 58 years and is buried here, and was made by his son.

EDCS-13002635.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Rogatus lived 35 years. His father made this.
EDCS-13002635.summary: This funerary epitaph records Rogatus, who lived 35 years and was commemorated by his father.

EDCS-13002636.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Silvanus, son of Gaetulicus, lived 104 years. Lucianus [made this] for his father.
EDCS-13002636.summary: This funerary epitaph records Silvanus, son of Gaetulicus, who lived 104 years and was commemorated by his son Lucianus.

EDCS-13002639.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Saturn Augustus. Publius Pomponius Maximus, the priest, made this vow with a willing spirit.
EDCS-13002639.summary: This is a votive inscription by Publius Pomponius Maximus, a priest, dedicating it to Jupiter and Saturn.

EDCS-13002640.translation: Gaius Pomponius Felix, priest, made this sacred offering to Lord Saturn Augustus and gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-13002640.summary: This is a dedication to Saturn Augustus by a priest named Gaius Pomponius Felix, who gladly paid his vow.

EDCS-13002641.translation: Lucius Avianius Felix, priest of Saturn, gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-13002641.summary: This is a vow paid by Lucius Avianius Felix, a priest of Saturn.

EDCS-13002671.translation: Felicissimus, with his family, made this sacred offering to Pluto Augustus and gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-13002671.summary: This inscription records a vow paid to Pluto Augustus by Felicissimus and his family.

EDCS-13002936.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aperius lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002936.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the death of Aperius, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-13002938.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Artorius Honoratus lived 80 years; he is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Casta lived 25 years.
EDCS-13002938.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the deaths of Marcus Artorius Honoratus and Caecilia Casta, with their ages at death.

EDCS-13002942.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the death of Augusta Ianuaria and her age at death.

EDCS-13002947.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Ianuarius lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002947.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the death of Gaius Ianuarius, who lived 70 years.

EDCS-13002948.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Berect lived 75 years. Lucius Cecilius Nampeus lived 82 years.
EDCS-13002948.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the deaths of Berect and Lucius Cecilius Nampeus, with their ages at death.

EDCS-13002949.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caelus Catus lived 35 years; he is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caelius Baric lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002949.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the deaths of Caelus Catus and Caelius Baric, with their ages at death and burial information.

EDCS-13002958.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavia Florida lived...
EDCS-13002958.summary: This is a tomb inscription that begins by recording the death of Flavia Florida but is incomplete.

EDCS-13002959.translation: Florus lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002959.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Florus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002971.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Emilia Matrona lived 105 years; she is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Crescens lived 95 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002971.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespans and burials of two individuals, Emilia Matrona and Iulius Crescens, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002977.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Solutor lived 20 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002977.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan and burial of Iulius Solutor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002993.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Nonius Vitalis lived 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002993.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan and burial of Lucius Nonius Vitalis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002994.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Petronius Iustus lived 36 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13002994.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan and burial of Petronius Iustus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003006.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Victoria lived 55 years.
EDCS-13003006.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Victoria, likely from the Roman Imperial period, though her burial is not explicitly stated.

EDCS-13003069.translation: To Iulia Domna Augusta, mother of the camps, wife of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 6th time, imperator for the 11th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, father of the country, restorer of public peace, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-13003069.summary: This is a dedication by the decurions (town councillors) of a public monument, likely a statue or honorific structure, to Iulia Domna, mother of Emperor Septimius Severus, dated to his reign in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13003071.translation: To Lartidia, daughter of Lucius, of Praeneste, wife of Titus Caesernius Concessus Severus, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-13003071.summary: This is a dedication by the decurions (town councillors) of a public monument, likely a statue or honorific structure, to Lartidia, wife of Titus Caesernius Concessus Severus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003072.translation: To Clodia Fabiana, to whom the order of the Pagus MON NEMIIIV PI V XVII decreed a statue, [in recognition of her service/value] for 17 years, aged 20.
EDCS-13003072.summary: This is a dedication by the local administration (ordo Pagi) of a village (Pagus) to Clodia Fabiana, likely honoring her for 17 years of service, with her age given as 20, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003074.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelia Philomene, dutiful, lived 24 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13003074.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan and burial of Aelia Philomene, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003075.translation: Aurelius Calazus made this for himself and his wife.
EDCS-13003075.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Aurelius Calazus for himself and his wife.

EDCS-13003076.translation: Birict, daughter of Baricis, devout, lived 51 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13003076.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Birict, daughter of Baricis, who lived 51 years.

EDCS-13003077.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Cirrenus Romulus, devout, lived 18 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003077.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Cirrenus Romulus, who lived 18 years.

EDCS-13003078.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelia Ianuaria, devout. Sacred to the departed spirits. Tiberius Claudius Silvanus, son of Datus.
EDCS-13003078.summary: This is a funerary inscription that names Cornelia Ianuaria and Tiberius Claudius Silvanus, son of Datus.

EDCS-13003079.translation: The parents [made this] for Cornelius Adventus Fatalis Pudentianus.
EDCS-13003079.summary: This funerary dedication was made by parents for Cornelius Adventus Fatalis Pudentianus.

EDCS-13003080.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription naming Cornelius Romanianus.

EDCS-13003081.translation: Gaius Decius Decianus, devout, lived 23 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003081.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Decius Decianus, who lived 23 years.

EDCS-13003082.translation: Publius Julius Florus, devout, lived 95 years.
EDCS-13003082.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Julius Florus, who lived 95 years.

EDCS-13003083.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Junius Villaticus, devout, lived 77 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003083.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Junius Villaticus, who lived 77 years.

EDCS-13003084.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Philologus, devout, lived 40 years.
EDCS-13003084.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Philologus, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-13003085.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Privatus, son of Pompeius, lived 14 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003085.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of a 14-year-old boy named Privatus.

EDCS-13003086.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Venerius Saturninus, dutiful, lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003086.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Caius Venerius Saturninus, who lived to 75.

EDCS-13003197.translation: To Rufus, son of Metatis, a honored Numidian, lived 50 years; his sons made this. He is buried in this place. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003197.summary: A funerary inscription commissioned by sons for their honored Numidian father, Rufus.

EDCS-13003198.translation: D(is) M(anibus). Guduis, daughter of Asurih, lived 60 years. D(iis) H(ic) M(anis).
EDCS-13003198.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of Guduis, daughter of Asurih, with a dedication to the departed spirits.

EDCS-13003199.translation: Flavia Fortunata, dutiful wife of Flavi Crispus, lived 50 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13003199.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Flavia Fortunata, wife of Flavi Crispus.

EDCS-13003201.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Minucius Saturninus and Marcus Minucius Catullinus made this for themselves and their descendants; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003201.summary: A funerary inscription establishing a family tomb for Caius Minucius Saturninus and Marcus Minucius Catullinus and their descendants.

EDCS-13003202.translation: Pompeius Sexti lived 72 years; his sons are his heirs.
EDCS-13003202.summary: A funerary inscription noting the age of Pompeius Sexti and identifying his sons as heirs.

EDCS-13003203.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Vettius Datus lived 55 years.
EDCS-13003203.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of Lucius Vettius Datus and dedicating the tomb to the departed spirits.

EDCS-13003204.translation: Quintus S. S., dutiful, lived [incomplete age].
EDCS-13003204.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription for Quintus S. S., noting he was dutiful.

EDCS-13003258.translation: Good Jupiter, I, Gallonius, prefect of a cohort, have placed this altar, which I vowed to you.
EDCS-13003258.summary: A votive inscription dedicating an altar to Jupiter by Gallonius, a cohort prefect.

EDCS-13003260.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Agria Prima lived 30 years. Iulius Antoninus made this for his wife.
EDCS-13003260.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his deceased wife, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13003262.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Flavius Felix, freedman, lived 50 years.
