EDCS-13003262.summary: A funerary inscription for a freedman named Flavius Felix, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13003263.translation: Sacred to the spirits. Aelia Donatilla, dutiful, lived 10 years. Her father Nisus made this for her, well-deserving.
EDCS-13003263.summary: A funerary dedication by a father for his young daughter, Aelia Donatilla, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13003264.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulius Rusticus lived 75 years. Iulius Ianus erected this tomb for his father, well-deserving.
EDCS-13003264.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and death of Iulius Rusticus, with his son Iulius Ianus erecting the tomb.

EDCS-13003265.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Octavia lived 95 years. Iulius Rogatus erected this small altar for his most worthy mother.
EDCS-13003265.summary: A funerary monument erected by Iulius Rogatus for his elderly mother, Iulia Octavia.

EDCS-13003266.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Sempronia Tertia lived [X] years. Horodionius Fortunatus made this for his most dutiful mother, well-deserving.
EDCS-13003266.summary: A funerary dedication by Horodionius Fortunatus for his mother, Sempronia Tertia.

EDCS-13003268.translation: In memory of the saints Primi and Quintasi.
EDCS-13003268.summary: A memorial inscription dedicated to two individuals identified as saints, likely of Christian origin.

EDCS-13003269.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marius Arrius lived 35 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13003269.summary: A simple funerary inscription for Marius Arrius, noting his age and burial.

EDCS-13003270.translation: lived 65 years. He is buried here. [Name] lived 20 years. lived 41 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13003270.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription recording the ages and burials of at least three individuals.

EDCS-13003271.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulius Severus lived 25 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13003271.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulius Severus, stating his age and burial place.

EDCS-13003272.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Seilius lies here, aged 15 years.
EDCS-13003272.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young individual named Seilius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003274.translation: To Liber and the Augusti, speedily. Maximus.
EDCS-13003274.summary: This is a dedication to the gods Liber and the Emperors, likely by an individual named Maximus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003275.translation: To Tellus Augusta. Urbanus, son of Publius Iulius Urbanus, of the voting tribe Quirina, honored with the public horse, quaestor, twice aedile of four colonies, prefect for the position of III viri, for the honor of his aedileship, gave 20,000 sesterces in addition to the public funds, with a tetrastyion, and he himself dedicated it; for the dedication, he presented theatrical games with shows, a place was given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-13003275.summary: This is a dedication of a public building and games by Urbanus, an official in four colonies, to the goddess Tellus Augusta, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13003276.translation: For Publius Septimius Geta, father of the Emperor Severus Augustus, most pious, grandfather of the Emperor Antoninus Augustus, most excellent prince, and of Publius Septimius Geta, most noble Caesar, 'most brave and most generous prince', the public affairs of the four colonies of the Cirta region, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-13003276.summary: This inscription honors Septimius Geta, father of Emperor Septimius Severus, and grandfather of Caracalla and Geta, funded by the four colonies of Cirta, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13003277.translation: To Paccia Marciana, formerly wife of the Emperor Severus Augustus, most pious, most excellent prince, the public affairs of the four colonies of the Cirta region, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-13003277.summary: This inscription honors Paccia Marciana, wife of Emperor Septimius Severus, funded by the four colonies of Cirta, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13003279.translation: Of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus.
EDCS-13003279.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription referring to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-13003285.translation: To Vilus Icetius. Augustorum IIN IFIIRO.
EDCS-13003285.summary: This is a dedication, possibly to the emperors and an individual named Vilus Icetius, with illegible text, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003286.translation: Marcus Motilius Valens, patron of the decurions, by decree, to the imperial legate pro praetore, consul-designate.
EDCS-13003286.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Marcus Motilius Valens, patron of the local council, to an imperial legate and consul-designate, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003289.translation: Marcus Coculnius Quintilianus to his wife Vitruvia Maxima, daughter of Lucius, his most rare wife, gave and dedicated.
EDCS-13003289.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a husband, Marcus Coculnius Quintilianus, to his wife Vitruvia Maxima, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003292.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Baebius Palmianus, priest of the second temple of Sittiana, who lived 74 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003292.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Baebius Palmianus, a priest of the temple of Sittiana, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003299.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Publius Publicius Fortis, freedman of the colony, lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003299.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Publicius Fortis, a freedman of the colony, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-13003306.translation: To the memory of Aelianus, his most sweet wife Leontis.
EDCS-13003306.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Leontis, wife of Aelianus.

EDCS-13003314.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius [---].

EDCS-13003325.translation: Cornelia Vitalis lived 8 years; Cornelia Candida lived 5 years.
EDCS-13003325.summary: This funerary epitaph records the short lives of two individuals named Cornelia.

EDCS-13003333.translation: Quintus Flavius Celsus, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003333.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Flavius Celsus, son of Lucius, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-13003337.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Gargilia Mamantina lived 35 years.
EDCS-13003337.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Gargilia Mamantina, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-13003359.translation: Laurentius lived 16 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003359.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Laurentius, who lived 16 years.

EDCS-13003363.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Manilia Pacata.
EDCS-13003363.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Manilia Pacata.

EDCS-13003367.translation: To the memory of Maxsima, most innocent girl. Restitutus (?).
EDCS-13003367.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Maxsima, a young girl, possibly by Restitutus.

EDCS-13003373.translation: To the memory of Navigius, who lived 14 years. As a sweet flower, a son who enjoyed life briefly, like a rose, like a narcissus. His parents, December (father) and Peculiaris (mother), made this for their devoted, worthy son. Weeping while looking at you, you offer solace. You will have remembrance beyond.
EDCS-13003373.summary: This funerary inscription is a memorial set up by parents, December and Peculiaris, for their son Navigius, who died at age 14, comparing his short life to flowers.

EDCS-13003374.translation: Nice lived 31 years for her deserving friend Chromatus.
EDCS-13003374.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Nice, likely erected by her friend Chromatus.

EDCS-13003382.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Piseilus Natalis lived 27 years.
EDCS-13003382.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Piseilus Natalis, a man who lived 27 years.

EDCS-13003383.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription that begins dedicating a monument to Procilia Pudens.

EDCS-13003384.translation: Publicius As a lived 55 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-13003384.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publicius As a, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-13003386.translation: Publius Raetius A placed this altar for his deserving wife.
EDCS-13003386.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Publius Raetius A dedicated an altar to his wife.

EDCS-13003390.translation: Saturninus, son of Caius, lived 35 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-13003390.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Saturninus, son of Caius, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-13003392.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for Publius Sillu, who lived 21 years.

EDCS-13003397.translation: The tomb of Marcus Situs Sittianus.
EDCS-13003397.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying the tomb belonging to Marcus Situs Sittianus.

EDCS-13003398.translation: Marcus Sittius Victorinus made [this] for his wife.
EDCS-13003398.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Marcus Sittius Victorinus made a monument for his wife.

EDCS-13003404.translation: Successa lived 25 years, she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003404.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Successa, who lived 25 years, with a final wish for her repose.

EDCS-13003405.translation: The bodies of Successaenus and Crementia, gifts of Successaeus.
EDCS-13003405.summary: This inscription records a donation of bodies by Successaeus, likely related to burial practices or funerary arrangements, with the dedicators being Successaenus and Crementia.

EDCS-13003412.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ovinia Veterilla lived 35 years.
EDCS-13003412.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ovinia Veterilla, who lived for 35 years.

EDCS-13003413.translation: Dexil lived 65 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003413.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Dexil, who lived for 65 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-13003428.summary: This inscription is fragmentary and its meaning is unclear, but it possibly contains a hopeful sentiment related to a 'body'.

EDCS-13003429.translation: Alas, meanwhile, while parents seek children, they will have free children; almost at the end of the year, I saw you, Separation from Rhodes.
EDCS-13003429.summary: This is a poetic or personal inscription reflecting on the desire for children and the bittersweet nature of life, possibly commemorating a departure or a specific event.

EDCS-13003430.translation: Donatus the cleric lived 9 years and rested in peace on the 7th day before the Ides of April.
EDCS-13003430.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Donatus, a cleric who died at the age of 9 on April 7th.

EDCS-13003431.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Balicus.
EDCS-13003431.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Balicus, dedicated to the spirits of the dead.

EDCS-13003432.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Nivalis lived 70 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003432.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Nivalis, who lived for 70 years, with a wish for his peaceful repose.

EDCS-13003433.translation: Iulia Matrona lived 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13003433.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Matrona, who lived for 35 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-13003434.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Euelpistus Augustalis of the colony, by testament, his sons, daughters, freedmen, and heirs, the temple.
EDCS-13003434.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Euelpistus Augustalis, as stipulated in his will, for his descendants and freedmen.

EDCS-13003435.translation: Bilas Cantas lies here. He lived 90 years.
EDCS-13003435.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Bilas Cantas, who is buried here.

EDCS-13003436.translation: Sacred to Augustus. Publius Antonius Nampulus made and dedicated this with his own money.
EDCS-13003436.summary: This dedicatory inscription records that Publius Antonius Nampulus funded and consecrated a monument to Augustus.

EDCS-13003439.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caius Iulius Matutinus lived 60 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003439.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Caius Iulius Matutinus, who is buried here.

EDCS-13003501.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caius Iulius Crescentianus lived 45 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003501.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Caius Iulius Crescentianus, who is buried here.

EDCS-13003526.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Natalis, daughter of Caius, lived 93 years. She lies here.
EDCS-13003526.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Iulia Natalis, who is buried here.

EDCS-13003538.translation: Quintus Nonius Conculus lived 40 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003538.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Quintus Nonius Conculus, who is buried here.

EDCS-13003558.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Urbana.

EDCS-13003563.summary: This dedicatory inscription records a sacred offering to the goddess Hygieia.

EDCS-13003564.translation: Decimus Aebutius Severus lived 60 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003564.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Decimus Aebutius Severus, who is buried here.

EDCS-13003565.translation: Lucius Aemilius Gemellus lived 85 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13003565.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Lucius Aemilius Gemellus, who is buried here.

EDCS-13003566.translation: Quintus Aemilius Ingenuus lived 74 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003566.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of Quintus Aemilius Ingenuus, who died at the age of 74.

EDCS-13003567.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Quaeta lived 21 years.
EDCS-13003567.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the life of Aemilia Quaeta, who died at 21.

EDCS-13003568.summary: A brief funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, for Agrius Maximus.

EDCS-13003569.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Anneus Rogatus lived 71 years.
EDCS-13003569.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the life of Lucius Anneus Rogatus, who died at 71.

EDCS-13003570.translation: Quintus Aurelius Nisus, freedman of Quintus, lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003570.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of Quintus Aurelius Nisus, freedman of Quintus, who died at 35.

EDCS-13003571.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Caelius Pacatus lived 85 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003571.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of Lucius Caelius Pacatus, who died at 85.

EDCS-13003572.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Clodius Maximianus lived 14 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003572.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of Clodius Maximianus, who died at 14.

EDCS-13003573.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of Dorus, who died at 25.

EDCS-13003574.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Hortensius, son of Hyginus, lived 28 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003574.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of Hortensius Hygini, who died at 28.

EDCS-13003575.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aulus Iulius Hermetio lived 25 years.
EDCS-13003575.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the life of Aulus Iulius Hermetio, who died at 25.

EDCS-13003576.translation: Gaius Julius Maximus, son of Gaius, lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003576.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and burial of Gaius Julius Maximus.

EDCS-13003577.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Sextus Julius Subitanus lived 23 years.
EDCS-13003577.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Sextus Julius Subitanus, dedicated to the underworld spirits.

EDCS-13003579.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Nigro lived.
EDCS-13003579.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Iulia Nigro, dedicated to the underworld spirits.

EDCS-13003580.translation: Toneius Iuvenalis lived 47 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003580.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and burial of Toneius Iuvenalis.

EDCS-13003581.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Quintus Lollius Victoricus lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003581.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and burial of Quintus Lollius Victoricus, dedicated to the underworld spirits.

EDCS-13003582.translation: Malia Optata, daughter of Publius, lived 45 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13003582.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and burial of Malia Optata.

EDCS-13003583.translation: Gaius Munatius Paetus, son of Gaius, who lived 45 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13003583.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and burial of Gaius Munatius Paetus.

EDCS-13003584.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Titus Nigidius Mustiolus lived 52 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003584.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and burial of Titus Nigidius Mustiolus, dedicated to the underworld spirits.

EDCS-13003585.translation: Publius Nigidius Vindex, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 81 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003585.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and burial of Publius Nigidius Vindex.

EDCS-13003586.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Paconia Monnula lived 30 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13003586.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and burial of Paconia Monnula, dedicated to the underworld spirits.

EDCS-13003587.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pactumeius Faustinus lived 27 years; he is buried here. To his most dutiful son.
EDCS-13003587.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pactumeius Faustinus, indicating his age and dedicating the tomb to his son.

EDCS-13003588.translation: Caius Pactumeius Philocalus lived 103 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003588.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name, age, and burial place of Caius Pactumeius Philocalus.

EDCS-13003589.translation: Pomponius Castresianus lived 31 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003589.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name, age, and burial place of Pomponius Castresianus.

EDCS-13003590.translation: Primitivus made this for his mother, who lived 55 years.
EDCS-13003590.summary: A funerary epitaph stating that Primitivus dedicated this monument to his mother, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-13003591.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Apronius Quetus.
EDCS-13003591.summary: A funerary dedication to the spirits of the departed by Apronius Quetus.

EDCS-13003592.translation: Lucius Sittius Felix lived 90 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003592.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name, age, and burial place of Lucius Sittius Felix.

EDCS-13003593.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Supraeus lived 95 years. O...
EDCS-13003593.summary: A funerary epitaph for Supraeus, recording his age and a possible abbreviation.

EDCS-13003594.translation: Neuma, freedwoman of Vibia Quinti, lived 39 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13003594.summary: A funerary epitaph for Neuma, identified as the freedwoman of Vibia Quinti, recording her age and burial.

EDCS-13003595.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vinicia Fortuna lived 20 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13003595.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vinicia Fortuna, recording her age and burial, dedicated to the spirits of the departed.

EDCS-13003596.translation: Let no one ever disturb this tomb. The infernal spirits...
EDCS-13003596.summary: A funerary inscription issuing a warning against disturbing the tomb, possibly invoking underworld deities.

EDCS-13003597.translation: Nius lived 78 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003597.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Nius.

EDCS-13003599.translation: Because the lofty roofs of the sacred building rise so high, a work which the diligent solicitude of the pious erected, the noble bishop and perpetual father Navigius, a minister of Christ's law, placed this church, to be venerated by its name, that all may admire this work of religion.
EDCS-13003599.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a church by Bishop Navigius, likely in the late Roman or early Christian period, praising his pious work.

EDCS-13003602.translation: To the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius, conqueror of Germany and Sarmatia, saviour of the human race, father.
EDCS-13003602.summary: This is a dedication to the deified Emperor Marcus Aurelius, honoring his military victories and his role as a protector and father figure.

EDCS-13003609.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Victor's daughter Extricata lived 61 years.
EDCS-13003609.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Extricata, daughter of Victor.

EDCS-13003610.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Caecilius Victorinus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, obeyed the command of his dearest father and willingly completed the mausoleum with a tribunal and altar; he lived 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003610.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Quintus Caecilius Victorinus, who completed a mausoleum as instructed by his father, and states his age at death.

EDCS-13003611.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Aventius, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 55 years.
EDCS-13003611.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Quintus Aventius, son of Publius.

EDCS-13003612.translation: Marcus Clodius Severus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 65 years; he is buried here. Marcus Clodius Clodianus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003612.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespans and burials of two individuals named Marcus Clodius, father and son or relatives, both of the Quirina tribe.

EDCS-13003613.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Cosconius Novatus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 65 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003613.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Cosconius Novatus and includes a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-13003614.translation: Quirina Firmus lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003614.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Firmus, likely a member of the Quirina tribe.

EDCS-13003615.translation: Titus Honoratus Rogatus lived 61 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003615.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Titus Honoratus Rogatus.

EDCS-13003616.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Quirina lived for 26 years.
EDCS-13003616.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Quirina, recording his age at death.

EDCS-13003617.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Nonius Turuscus, son of Marcus, of Quirina, lived for 37 years; may your bones rest well here.
EDCS-13003617.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Nonius Turuscus, recording his age and a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-13003618.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Arator, son of Salcidius, of Quirina, lived for [---] years.
EDCS-13003618.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Arator, recording his age, though the precise duration is lost.

EDCS-13003619.translation: Laecanius Victorinus, son of Cai, of Quirina, lived for 65 years; he rests here.
EDCS-13003619.summary: A funerary epitaph for Laecanius Victorinus, recording his age and burial place.

EDCS-13003620.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Volusiana Iulia lived for 20 years.
EDCS-13003620.summary: A funerary epitaph for Volusiana Iulia, recording her age at death.

EDCS-13003637.translation: Modia, daughter of Lucius, of Abuda, lies here; she lived for 81 years.
EDCS-13003637.summary: A funerary epitaph for Modia, daughter of Lucius, recording her place of burial and age.

EDCS-13003733.translation: Claudius Licinus lived for 95 years and 6 months. Claudia Fortunata, his most devoted wife, made this.
EDCS-13003733.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Claudia Fortunata for her husband Claudius Licinus, recording his age and her devotion.

EDCS-13003734.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fabia Silvana lived for 83 years and 11 days.
EDCS-13003734.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fabia Silvana, recording her age at death.

EDCS-13003735.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Marcus lived for 80 years and seven months and 8 days.
EDCS-13003735.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Marcus, recording his age at death in years, months, and days.

EDCS-13003742.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ulpia Ingenua lived for 15 years.
EDCS-13003742.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ulpia Ingenua, recording her age at death.

EDCS-13003758.translation: The memorial of Saint Maxima Donatilla and Secunda, erected by the lord bishop Faustinus of the city of Teveste, on the 5th day before the Ides of April, in the 13th indiction. The memorial of Saint Vincent the martyr, Archangel Michael and Gabriel, Saint Crispin the martyr.
EDCS-13003758.summary: This is a Christian epitaph from Teveste, possibly dating to the 6th century (due to the indiction), commemorating multiple saints including Maxima Donatilla, Secunda, Vincent, Michael, Gabriel, and Crispin, and erected by Bishop Faustinus.

EDCS-13003774.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fufidius Optatianus lived for 7 years. Iulius Tullianus and Victoria made [this] for their dearest-born slave. He is buried here.
EDCS-13003774.summary: This is a Roman funerary epitaph for a young slave named Fufidius Optatianus, erected by his owners, Iulius Tullianus and Victoria, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-13003775.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Statia Statiola lived for 72 years. Tulliana with her sons Iulis Natale Victore and Tulliano made [this] for their dearest mother, and for her grandchildren.
EDCS-13003775.summary: This is a Roman funerary epitaph for Statia Statiola, erected by her daughter Tulliana and grandsons, indicating a family memorial likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-13003780.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Felicianus lived for 16 years. His father made [this] memorial for his most devoted son.
EDCS-13003780.summary: This is a Roman funerary epitaph for Felicianus, who died at the age of 16, erected by his grieving father, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-13003784.translation: The memorial of Saint Germanilla Innocentis.
EDCS-13003784.summary: This is a very short inscription indicating the location of a memorial for Saint Germanilla Innocentis, possibly a dedication or marker.

EDCS-13003785.translation: Iahinus lived well in peace for 90 years. His sons made [this].
EDCS-13003785.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iahinus, who lived to be 90 years old, erected by his sons, likely from the late Roman or early Christian period.

EDCS-13003786.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Servilius, son of Publius, from Ioea, Saturninus, pious, lived for 76 years. His grandsons... born to the upper gods, protected.
EDCS-13003786.summary: This is a Roman funerary epitaph for Publius Servilius Ioea Saturninus, who lived to 76, erected by his grandsons, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-13003817.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Geminia Donata lived for 65 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. V A TV lived for 70 years.
EDCS-13003817.summary: This inscription appears to be a double epitaph, recording the deaths of Geminia Donata (aged 65) and another individual (aged 70) with abbreviated names, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13003818.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Dionisius lived for 80 years.
EDCS-13003818.summary: This is a Roman funerary epitaph for Iulius Dionisius, who lived to the age of 80, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-13003819.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sallustius Baric lived for 75 years. The heir made [this].
EDCS-13003819.summary: This is a Roman funerary epitaph for Sallustius Baric, who lived to 75, erected by his heir, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-13003834.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aemilius Cilo Musavis Musulamus lived 85 years.
EDCS-13003834.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilius Cilo Musavis Musulamus, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13003835.translation: Zelma, son of Faustus Nattabut, lived 43 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003835.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Zelma, son of Faustus Nattabut, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13003836.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Aurelius Urbanus lived 2 years; he is buried here. His son made this. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aurelia Victorina, dutiful, lived 10 years; she is buried here. And Flavia Urbana.
EDCS-13003836.summary: This is a complex funerary epitaph, likely for a father burying his young son, and perhaps later for his wife or another daughter, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13003837.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph for C. Victor, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13003845.translation: To Saturn, Lord, Augustus. Surdius Solutor restored the temple from his own funds, with good intentions.
EDCS-13003845.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to the god Saturn by Surdius Solutor, likely during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13003872.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Titus Flavius Maximianus, son of Titus Flavius Pudentis, of the Quirina tribe, dutiful, lived 20 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003872.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Maximianus, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13003873.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Pomponia Secura, dutiful, lived...
EDCS-13003873.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph for Pomponia Secura, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13100070.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Iulianus, Pious, Fortunate, victorious and triumphant, ever Augustus, restorer of the sacred rites, the most splendid council of the people of Thibilis erected and dedicated (this).
EDCS-13100070.summary: This is a dedication by the council of Thibilis to the emperor Flavius Claudius Iulianus, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-13100071.translation: To our lord Flavius Valentiniano Maximo, Pious, Fortunate, victorious and triumphant, ever Augustus, the most splendid council of the people of Thibilis (erected this) with public money by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-13100071.summary: This is a dedication by the council of Thibilis to the emperor Valentinian III, likely from the 5th century CE.

EDCS-13100072.translation: To our lord Flavius Gratianus, Pious, Fortunate, victorious and triumphant, ever Augustus, the most splendid council of the people of Thibilis (erected this) with public money by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-13100072.summary: This is a dedication by the council of Thibilis to the emperor Gratian, likely from the late 4th century CE.

EDCS-13100076.translation: To Quintus Antistius Adventus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, who served as consul, fetial priest, imperial legate with the rank of propraetor of Lower Germany, imperial legate for the Italian frontier and the Alps during the German campaign, curator of public works and places, imperial legate with the rank of propraetor of Arabia, imperial legate of the VI Ferrata and II Adiutrix legions (transferred to the latter during the Parthian campaign), who was awarded military honors: the mural crown, the golden crown, three spears, and two standards; praetor, legate with the rank of propraetor of Africa, tribune of the plebs, Seviri of the Roman Equites, quaestor with the rank of propraetor of Macedonia, military tribune of the I Minervia Pia Fidelis legion, and a commissioner of roads. Sextus Marcius Maximus erected and dedicated this at his own expense because of his remarkable benevolence towards him.
EDCS-13100076.summary: This is a funerary or honorary inscription dedicated by Sextus Marcius Maximus to Quintus Antistius Adventus, detailing his extensive career in Roman administration and military service, likely dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13100079.translation: Marcus Herennius Victor, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, a distinguished man, gave this gift with his own money.
EDCS-13100079.summary: This is a dedication of a gift by Marcus Herennius Victor, likely a private individual, possibly for religious or public purposes.

EDCS-13100080.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Sittius Rufinus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, honored with the public horse, a decurion of the Cirta, lived 27 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13100080.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Sittius Rufinus, a local official from Cirta, who died at the age of 27.

EDCS-13100082.translation: Quirina soldier, son of Quirina, of the Quirina tribe, soldier of the III Augusta legion, first cohort, first centurion's century, lived 22 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13100082.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier of the III Augusta legion who died at the age of 22.

EDCS-13100083.translation: To Lucius Brussius Hospes, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, perpetual priest of the Thibilitani, of rare piety towards his children. Brussi Maximus [erected this].
EDCS-13100083.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Brussi Maximus to Lucius Brussius Hospes, a perpetual priest of the Thibilitani, highlighting his piety.

EDCS-13100084.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Julius Phalacrio, priest. Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Licinia Monula, daughter of Lucius, priestess of Saturn.
EDCS-13100084.summary: This inscription records two individuals, Caius Julius Phalacrio and Licinia Monula, both identified as priests or priestesses, possibly on a shared funerary monument.

EDCS-13100085.translation: Iulia Albia, daughter of Gaius, priestess, lived 90 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13100085.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Albia, a priestess, who lived to the age of 90.

EDCS-13100086.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Senia Nampame, priestess [of ...].
EDCS-13100086.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Senia Nampame, identified as a priestess, with the deity possibly lost.

EDCS-13100087.translation: Publius Mucius Musicus, the symphonist, erected this monument for himself during his lifetime.
EDCS-13100087.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument erected by Publius Mucius Musicus, identified as a musician.

EDCS-13100088.translation: Marcus Marius Aemilianus, the aedile, supervised the construction and artisan work.
EDCS-13100088.summary: This is a record of public works, indicating that Marcus Marius Aemilianus, in his role as aedile, oversaw construction and craft activities.

EDCS-13100092.translation: The aedile restored the public works of the people of Thibilis.
EDCS-13100092.summary: This inscription records the restoration of public works by an aedile for the community of Thibilis.

EDCS-13100093.translation: Lucius Cupronius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, rebuilt a small shrine, ruined by age, with enlarged work at his own expense, by decree of the council.
EDCS-13100093.summary: This is a funerary or commemorative inscription recording Lucius Cupronius Rufus's personal expenditure to restore a shrine, likely in the late Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-13100097.translation: For Popilia Maxima, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-13100097.summary: This is a dedication, likely for a tomb or monument, to Popilia Maxima, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-13100101.translation: Felix, share the labor with me, Urania, and dare to reveal again the waters of the nymphs, which before, choked by thorns and buried by ruin, flowed in vain; behold, gradually you will discover where it flows with its ancient flow, now that it has been restored by someone's care, if you ask. Deposited on the fourth day before the Kalends of June, by Felix.
EDCS-13100101.summary: This dedicatory inscription describes the restoration of a spring or water source, likely commissioned by Felix and Urania and completed around the late 1st century BCE or 1st century CE.

EDCS-13100102.translation: Marcus Sittius Martialis, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, made this monument for himself and his family. Martialis lived 105 years. You who pass by and read this inscription, I ask you to read it all, so that you may learn to live well always. [---] my name [---] good [---] and I made no enemy of many people.
EDCS-13100102.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription for Marcus Sittius Martialis, who lived 105 years, and includes a plea for the reader to learn from his example of living well and making few enemies.

EDCS-13100104.translation: I chose to live for forty years and five, obeying my husband with fitting devotion, and made the poor rich by preserving my husband's well-earned property and by laboring and obeying; having completed my good life, I returned my body to the earth and my soul to the gods, my good character to posterity.
EDCS-13100104.summary: This funerary epitaph celebrates the virtuous life of a woman who lived for 45 years, emphasizing her devotion to her husband, her financial management, and her overall good character.

EDCS-13100106.translation: Pinna, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 56 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13100106.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pinna, son of Marcus, who lived 56 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-13100107.translation: Dida, son of Marcus, lived 81 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13100107.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Dida, son of Marcus, who lived 81 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-13100109.translation: Quintus Aedinius Matutinus lived 41 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13100109.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Aedinius Matutinus, who lived 41 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-13100110.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aedinia Sperata, daughter of Publius Aedinius, lived 31 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13100110.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the Divine Shades for Aedinia Sperata, daughter of Publius Aedinius, who lived 31 years.

EDCS-13100111.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Aelius Proclus lived 14 years, is buried here. To the departed spirits. Lucius Aelius Fortunatus lived 14 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13100111.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lives and burials of two individuals, Lucius Aelius Proclus and Lucius Aelius Fortunatus, likely brothers or relatives, who both died at the age of 14.

EDCS-13100114.translation: Marcus Aemilius Aemilianus, son of Marcus, lived 65 years, is buried here.

EDCS-13100115.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Lucius Aemilius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, Conductor. To the departed spirits, sacred. Iulia Libosa, daughter of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, Faosa, during her lifetime.
EDCS-13100115.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a family, mentioning Lucius Aemilius and Iulia Libosa, with an indication that Iulia Libosa set it up for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-13100227.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age at death of Sextus Aufidius Gallus.

EDCS-13100228.translation: Marcus Aufidius Ingenuus lived for 45 years; he is buried here.

EDCS-13100229.translation: Marcus Aufidius Rufinus, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 49 years; he is buried here.

EDCS-13100230.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aufidius, son of Sextus.

EDCS-13100238.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Avianius Felix, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 70 years; he rests here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Urbana Caecilia, daughter of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 65 years; she rests here.

EDCS-13100240.translation: Lucius Avianius Optatus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 67 years; he rests here.

EDCS-13100244.summary: A funerary inscription honoring Marcus Avianius Theuda, a freedman, and his wife Lucia Caecilia Restuta, also mentioning his patron.

EDCS-13100245.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Publius Avianius Felix, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe.

EDCS-13100246.translation: Aviania Satura, daughter of Marcus, lived 40 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13100246.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age and burial of Aviania Satura.

EDCS-13100247.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Gaius Avilius Victorinus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 28 years, is buried here. Gaius Avilius Quirina lived 47 years, is buried here.

EDCS-13100248.translation: Aurelius Arisus lived 3 years, is buried here.

EDCS-13100249.translation: Marcus Aurelius Bariccio, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 41 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13100249.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age and burial of Marcus Aurelius Bariccio.

EDCS-13100250.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Lucius Aurelius Felix, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 51 years, is buried here. [lacuna]

EDCS-13100251.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Caius Aurelius Martis, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 80 years. Dedicated to the departed spirits. Vettia Urbana, daughter of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 85 years.
EDCS-13100251.summary: A double funerary inscription recording the ages of Caius Aurelius Martis and Vettia Urbana, both of the Quirina tribe.

EDCS-13100252.translation: Marcus Aurelius Pudens, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 18 years; he is buried here.

EDCS-13100253.translation: Quintus Aurelius Quadratus lived for 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13100253.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Aurelius Quadratus, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-13100254.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Aurelius Severus lived for 70 years; he is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Aurelius Sallustianus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for...

EDCS-13100255.translation: Lucius Aurelius Silanus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe.

EDCS-13100256.translation: Quintus Aurelius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 20 years.

EDCS-13100258.translation: Aurelia Faustilla lived for 20 years; she is buried here.

EDCS-13100262.translation: To Aurelia Libulla, mother of Quintus, exceedingly rare.

EDCS-13100263.translation: Aurelia Libulla lived 65 years; she is buried here.

EDCS-13100305.translation: Lucius Caecilius Pudens, son of Lucius, lived 20 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-13100314.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilius Victor lived 75 years; he is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Munatia Urbana lived [X] years.

EDCS-13200063.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Honoratus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 62 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13200063.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Honoratus, stating his age, tribe, and burial place, with a wish for his repose.

EDCS-13200064.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Porcius Maximus lived 90 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13200064.summary: A funerary epitaph for Porcius Maximus, recording his age and burial place.

EDCS-13200071.translation: I, Lucius Praecilius Fortunatus, who am silent here, demonstrate my life in verses. I enjoyed bright light and the highest times. I Praecilius presented the trade of a silversmith from the CIRTENSIAN hearth. My fidelity was wondrous in me, and all truth was common to all. I, who did not pity myself, wherever I laughed, always enjoyed luxury with dear friends. After the death of my lady Valeria, the chaste, I found no one like her. I had a pleasing life with my holy wife. I celebrated my hundred birthdays honestly. Then the final day came, so that breath leaves the lifeless limbs. The epitaphs you read, I prepared in my life for my death, as Fortune willed. She never deserted me. Follow me like this. Come, I await you here.
EDCS-13200071.summary: This is a complex funerary epitaph written by Lucius Praecilius Fortunatus himself, detailing his life as a silversmith, his enjoyment of life, his virtuous character, and his preparation for death, urging others to join him.

EDCS-13200072.translation: I, Tanrocus, the sailor of the ship PRESILI Ipopotamia, made this.
EDCS-13200072.summary: This inscription records Tanrocus, a sailor of the ship PRESILI Ipopotamia, as the maker of the object, likely a dedication or marker.

EDCS-13200075.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To A. Servilia Zambaco, my dearest husband, lived 32 years; may you rest well here.
EDCS-13200075.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by A. Servilia Zambaco to her husband, recording his age and expressing a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-13200079.translation: To the memory of Aebutia Victoria, who lived six months.
EDCS-13200079.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating a child named Aebutia Victoria who died at six months of age.

EDCS-13200094.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription possibly naming Aemilia, daughter of Attius.

EDCS-13200100.translation: To the memory of Alector the Glutton.
EDCS-13200100.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a pet, likely a dog, named Alector the Glutton.

EDCS-13200134.translation: I was chaste and modest, Mnesithea, good to my husband, as I could be. Aurelia, my wife, who lived with me without quarrel and loved our children. Praise is also due to you, Rogatiana; I lived for you, always eager to live.
EDCS-13200134.summary: This is a lengthy epitaph from a wife to her deceased wife, praising her virtues and their happy marriage.

EDCS-13200149.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to be a dedication or memorial to Caecilius Severus by someone named Constans.

EDCS-13200184.translation: Tiberius Claudius Phoebus Granianus lived 53 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13200184.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Claudius Phoebus Granianus, who died at 53, with a wish for his afterlife.

EDCS-13300139.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Fortunatus lived for 55 years; may your bones rest well. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Co O (daughter?)
EDCS-13300139.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and death of Gaius Julius Fortunatus and mentions Iulia Co, possibly a relative.

EDCS-13300142.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Aemilius Sarnus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 75 years; he is buried here. To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Quinta, daughter of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 81 years; she is buried here. To the spirits of the departed. B. M. Quirina Rufina lived for 20 years.
EDCS-13300142.summary: This epitaph records the lives and burials of three individuals: Quintus Aemilius Sarnus, Iulia Quinta, and Quirina Rufina, with their ages and tribal affiliations.

EDCS-13300143.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Aemilia Mustia, stating her age.

EDCS-13300144.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Agrio Ianuarius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, known as Pendulio, lived for 80 years.
EDCS-13300144.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Lucius Agrio Ianuarius, son of Lucius, including his tribal affiliation and nickname.

EDCS-13301198.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Herennius Cirtensis, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 93 years.
EDCS-13301198.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Gaius Herennius Cirtensis, including his tribal affiliation and age.

EDCS-13301199.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Herennius Datius, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 33 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301199.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Marcus Herennius Datius, son of Marcus, stating his age and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-13301200.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Herennius Ianuarius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 26 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301200.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Lucius Herennius Ianuarius, son of Lucius, stating his age and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-13301201.translation: Gaius Herennius Martialius, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 31 years.
EDCS-13301201.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gaius Herennius Martialius.

EDCS-13301202.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Herennius Mutius, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 101 years; buried here.
EDCS-13301202.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Caius Herennius Mutius.

EDCS-13301203.translation: Gaius Herennius Primigenius lived 55 years; placed here.
EDCS-13301203.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gaius Herennius Primigenius.

EDCS-13301204.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Herennius Victor, son of Mustius, of the Quirina tribe, [sacred] to the departed spirits...
EDCS-13301204.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph, likely dedicated to Marcus Herennius Victor and possibly others.

EDCS-13301205.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Herennius, son of Lucius.
EDCS-13301205.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely a dedication by Caius Herennius, son of Lucius.

EDCS-13301206.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Herennius, son of Lucius.
EDCS-13301206.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely a dedication by Lucius Herennius, son of Lucius.

EDCS-13301207.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Herennius, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, [lived] X years.
EDCS-13301207.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Quintus Herennius, son of Marcus.

EDCS-13301208.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Herennia Burct was buried here; she lived 95 years.
EDCS-13301208.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial and lifespan of Herennia Burct.

EDCS-13301209.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Herennia Prima, freedwoman of Caius, lived 15 years.
EDCS-13301209.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Herennia Prima, freedwoman of Caius.

EDCS-13301210.translation: Herennia Secunda lived 25 years; placed here.
EDCS-13301210.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Herennia Secunda.

EDCS-13301211.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Victoria, daughter of Herennia Quinti.

EDCS-13301215.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Homullia Mustia lived 40 years.
EDCS-13301215.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Homullia Mustia, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-13301216.translation: Hordionia Salustia, daughter of Lucius, lived 75 years. She is buried here. To the spirits of the departed.
EDCS-13301216.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Hordionia Salustia, daughter of Lucius, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-13301217.translation: Quintus Hortesius Honoratus lived 56 years. Mas Tauriana.
EDCS-13301217.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life span of Quintus Hortesius Honoratus.

EDCS-13301218.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Victoria lived 60 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13301218.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Iulia Victoria, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-13301219.translation: Quintus Irius, son of Quintus, of the voting tribe Quirina, Felix.
EDCS-13301219.summary: This is a name inscription, possibly on a tomb, for Quintus Irius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe.

EDCS-13301220.translation: Iria Spica, daughter of Quintus, lived 19 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13301220.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Iria Spica, daughter of Quintus, who lived 19 years.

EDCS-13301222.translation: Gaius Julius Adauctus, son of Gaius, of the voting tribe Quirina, lived 40 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13301222.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Gaius Julius Adauctus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-13301223.translation: Gaius Julius Adiutor lived 75 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13301223.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Gaius Julius Adiutor, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-13301224.translation: Gaius Julius Adiutor, son of Lucius, of the voting tribe Quirina, lived 20 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13301224.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Gaius Julius Adiutor, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, who lived 20 years.

EDCS-13301225.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Iulius Adiutor lived for 100 years. Marcus Iulius Victor set this up for his father.
EDCS-13301225.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcus Iulius Victor for his father, Marcus Iulius Adiutor, who lived to be 100.

EDCS-13301226.translation: Quintus Iulius Adiutor, son of Quintus, lived for 51 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13301226.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death and burial of Quintus Iulius Adiutor at the age of 51.

EDCS-13301227.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Iulius Aelianus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 72 years. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilia Secunda lived for [---] years.
EDCS-13301227.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Iulius Aelianus, who lived to 72, and Aemilia Secunda.

EDCS-13301228.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Augustinus, son of Caius Iulius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 29 years.
EDCS-13301228.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Iulius Augustinus, who lived to 29.

EDCS-13301229.translation: Caius Iulius Celer, son of Caius, lived for 45 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13301229.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death and burial of Caius Iulius Celer at the age of 45.

EDCS-13301230.translation: Lucius Iulius Clarus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 65 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13301230.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death and burial of Lucius Iulius Clarus at the age of 65.

EDCS-13301231.translation: Iulius Crescens, son of Iulius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 17 years.
EDCS-13301231.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Iulius Crescens at the age of 17.

EDCS-13301232.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Crispinus, son of Iulius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 60 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13301232.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death and burial of Iulius Crispinus at the age of 60.

EDCS-13301233.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Iulius Dadarianus lived for 35 years. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flaminia Optatina lived for 50 years.
EDCS-13301233.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of Quintus Iulius Dadarianus (35) and Flaminia Optatina (50).

EDCS-13301234.translation: Caius Iulius Dexter is buried here.
EDCS-13301234.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Caius Iulius Dexter.

EDCS-13301235.translation: To the Divine Shades. Julius Faustianus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 51 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301235.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julius Faustianus, recording his name, parentage, tribal affiliation, age at death, and burial location.

EDCS-13301236.translation: Caius Iulius Faustus lived 19 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301236.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Faustus, recording his name, age at death, and burial location.

EDCS-13301237.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius Felix, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 90 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sittia Saturnina lived 70 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13301237.summary: This is a double funerary epitaph for Iulius Felix and Sittia Saturnina, recording their names, parentage, tribal affiliation, ages at death, burial location, and a common wish for rest.

EDCS-13301239.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Iulius Fortis, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 65 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301239.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Iulius Fortis, recording his name, parentage, tribal affiliation, age at death, and burial location.

EDCS-13301240.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulius Fortunatus. To the Divine Shades. Iulia Eutyche lived...
EDCS-13301240.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph, possibly for two individuals, Iulius Fortunatus and Iulia Eutyche, noting their devotion to the Divine Shades and the incomplete lifespan of Iulia Eutyche.

EDCS-13301241.translation: Caius Iulius Fronto, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived...
EDCS-13301241.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Fronto, recording his name, parentage, tribal affiliation, and an incomplete lifespan.

EDCS-13301242.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Iulius Hermetio, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, dedicated this in his own lifetime. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caecilia Rustica, daughter of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-13301242.summary: This inscription records two dedications to the Divine Shades: one by Caius Iulius Hermetio during his lifetime, and another for Caecilia Rustica.

EDCS-13301243.translation: Caius Iulius Homuncio, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 9 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301243.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Homuncio, recording his name, parentage, tribal affiliation, age at death, and burial location.

EDCS-13301244.translation: Lucius Iulius Homuccio, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301244.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Iulius Homuccio, recording his name, parentage, tribal affiliation, age at death, and burial location.

EDCS-13301245.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Honoratus, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-13301246.translation: Iulius Ianuarius lived 90 hours; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301246.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan in hours of Iulius Ianuarius.

EDCS-13301247.translation: Publius Iulius Laurus, son of Lucius, lived 11 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301247.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Publius Iulius Laurus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-13301248.translation: Caius Iulius Marcellus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301248.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Caius Iulius Marcellus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe.

EDCS-13301249.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulius Martialis lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301249.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to the Divine Spirits, recording the lifespan of Iulius Martialis.

EDCS-13301250.translation: Lucius Iulius Martialis lived 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301250.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Lucius Iulius Martialis.

EDCS-13301251.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulius Martis of the Quirina tribe lived 85 years. Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulius Urbanus, son of Iulius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 30 years.
EDCS-13301251.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to the Divine Spirits, recording the lifespans of two individuals, Iulius Martis and Iulius Urbanus.

EDCS-13301252.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Iulius Martis, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301252.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to the Divine Spirits, recording the lifespan of Lucius Iulius Martis, son of Lucius.

EDCS-13301253.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Iulius Maximus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301253.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to the Divine Spirits, recording the lifespan of Caius Iulius Maximus, son of Caius.

EDCS-13301254.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Iulius Maximus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 90 years.
EDCS-13301254.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to the Divine Spirits, recording the lifespan of Marcus Iulius Maximus, son of Marcus.

EDCS-13301255.translation: Iulius Maximus lived 90 years. Iulia Urbana lived 102 years.
EDCS-13301255.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespans of two individuals, Iulius Maximus and Iulia Urbana.

EDCS-13301256.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Julius Mundus, son of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 25 years. His sister set this up.
EDCS-13301256.summary: A funerary dedication for Gaius Julius Mundus, set up by his sister, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-13301257.translation: Gaius Julius Namphamo, son of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 45 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13301257.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Julius Namphamo, recording his age and burial place, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-13301258.translation: Quintus Julius Nampamo, son of Quintus, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 20 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13301258.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Julius Nampamo, recording his age and burial place, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-13301259.translation: Julius Nampamo, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 30 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13301259.summary: A tombstone for Julius Nampamo, recording his age and burial place, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-13301260.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Julius Nundinarius, son of Quintus, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 55 years. He is buried here. To the Divine Shades. Popilia Victoria, daughter of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe.
EDCS-13301260.summary: A fragmented inscription with two funerary dedications, one for Quintus Julius Nundinarius and another for Popilia Victoria, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-13301261.translation: Gaius Julius Optatus, son of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 50 years.
EDCS-13301261.summary: A tombstone fragment for Gaius Julius Optatus, recording his age, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-13301262.translation: Lucius Julius Optatus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 18 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13301262.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Julius Optatus, recording his age and burial place, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-13301263.translation: Marcus Julius Optatus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived [xx] years.
EDCS-13301263.summary: A tombstone fragment for Marcus Julius Optatus, recording his parentage and tribe, with an incomplete age, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-13301264.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Julius Optatus, son of Quintus, of the Voltinia tribe, set this up for himself while alive. Lived [xx] years. To the Divine Shades. Plotia Libosa, daughter of Plotus, lived [xx] years.
EDCS-13301264.summary: A fragmented inscription with two self-dedicated funerary monuments, one for Quintus Julius Optatus and another for Plotia Libosa, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-13301265.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Julius Polio, son of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 81 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13301265.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Julius Polio, recording his age and burial place, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-13301266.translation: Gaius Julius Posotimuos lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301266.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Posotimuos, who died at age 25.

EDCS-13301267.translation: Lucius Julius Proculus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301267.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Julius Proculus, who died at age 70.

EDCS-13301268.translation: Julius Proculus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 95 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301268.summary: A funerary inscription for Julius Proculus, who died at age 95.

EDCS-13301269.translation: Marcus Julius Quadratus lived 25 years; Quirina, daughter of Marcus, is buried here.
EDCS-13301269.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Julius Quadratus and Quirina, daughter of Marcus, who died at age 25.

EDCS-13301270.translation: Quintus Julius Quetus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 91 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301270.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Julius Quetus, who died at age 91.

EDCS-13301271.translation: Gaius Julius Quintillus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 90 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301271.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Quintillus, who died at age 90.

EDCS-13301272.translation: Lucius Julius Reginus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 52 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13301272.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Julius Reginus, who died at age 52.

EDCS-13302425.translation: Quintus Vultius Domitianus, Roman equestrian, completed the work himself and for Gaius Julius Saturninus, perpetual flamen and second magistrate of the Zaraitani.
EDCS-13302425.summary: An inscription recording the completion of a public work by Quintus Vultius Domitianus, benefiting himself and Gaius Julius Saturninus, a local magistrate.

EDCS-13302426.translation: Lucius Egnatius Saturninus made this for himself while alive and in memory of his most dutiful wife Aemilia Quintula, who lived 42 years.
EDCS-13302426.summary: A funerary monument erected by Lucius Egnatius Saturninus for himself and his deceased wife Aemilia Quintula, who died at age 42.

EDCS-13302427.translation: For the safety of Augustus, Marcus Petronius Priscus, son of Marcus, of the Fabia tribe, centurion of the III Augusta legion, set up this altar from a vision, willingly, on the 15th day before the Kalends of August, during the consulship of Largo and Messalinus.
EDCS-13302427.summary: A votive altar set up by Marcus Petronius Priscus, a centurion, for the safety of Augustus, dated to the consulship of Largo and Messalinus.

EDCS-13302428.translation: I am Saturnina, the most beloved wife of Caelus, who lived for twenty-five years; I am now placed in my husband's tomb, taken from his marriage bed.
EDCS-13302428.summary: This epitaph commemorates Saturnina, the wife of Caelus, and likely dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13302430.translation: On the day Gallicus died, the eleventh day before the Kalends of June, he entered into peace; he lived 25 years.
EDCS-13302430.summary: This epitaph records the death and age of Gallicus, who died on May 22nd and lived for 25 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13302434.translation: Caius Iulius Lucianus, a *beneficiarius*, gladly set this up to Victoriae Augustae, our lord Emperor Carus Augustus.
EDCS-13302434.summary: This dedication was willingly made by Caius Iulius Lucianus to the imperial cult of Emperor Carus Augustus, likely dating to his reign (282-283 AD).

EDCS-13302435.translation: To Bona Dea, Petronius Iustus, governor of the province, having recovered his health.
EDCS-13302435.summary: This inscription records the restoration of health of Petronius Iustus, a governor, to the goddess Bona Dea, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13302436.translation: Sacred to Pluto Augustus. Publius Aemilius Saturus, in his own name and in the name of his sons, set up two altars with his own money and dedicated them.
EDCS-13302436.summary: This dedication of two altars to Pluto Augustus was made by Publius Aemilius Saturus and his sons, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13302470.translation: Quintus Publicius Sarnus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13302470.summary: This is a votive inscription by Quintus Publicius Sarnus, who willingly fulfilled a vow, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13302471.translation: Caius Caecilius Baricio willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13302471.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caius Caecilius Baricio, who willingly fulfilled a vow, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13302548.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta, its worshippers.
EDCS-13302548.summary: This inscription indicates a dedication to Fortuna Augusta by her worshippers, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13302549.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Augustus. Caius Mummius Africanus made this place, which the muleteers frequent, at his own expense, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-13302549.summary: This inscription records Caius Mummius Africanus's construction of a place frequented by muleteers, dedicated to Jupiter Augustus and approved by the town council, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13302553.translation: To the divine spirits of the departed. Lucius Antonius Cirrenius lived 100 years; he is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13302553.summary: This epitaph commemorates Lucius Antonius Cirrenius, who lived to be 100 years old, and includes a wish for his peaceful repose, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13302554.translation: Marcus Arruntius Rogatus lived for 34 years.
EDCS-13302554.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of Marcus Arruntius Rogatus.

EDCS-13302555.translation: Aveia Paulina lived for 40 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13302555.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan and burial of Aveia Paulina, with a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-13302556.summary: An inscription that appears to be the name of an individual, possibly a tombstone or dedication fragment.

EDCS-13302557.translation: Dedicated to Mercury Augustus. Quintus Modius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, Honoratianus, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13302557.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Mercury Augustus by Quintus Modius Honoratianus.

EDCS-13302558.translation: To the divine spirit of Attis. Publicius Verecundus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13302558.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the divine spirit of Attis by Publicius Verecundus.

EDCS-13302559.translation: Dedicated to Silvanus Augustus. Titus Nigidius Rusticus, a cistifer (follower of Cybele), willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13302559.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Augustus by Titus Nigidius Rusticus.

EDCS-13302562.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Caeius Sem [lived] 55 years.
EDCS-13302562.summary: A tombstone inscription for Lucius Caeius Sem, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-13302563.translation: Caius Caecilius Faustillus lived for 18 years.
EDCS-13302563.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of Caius Caecilius Faustillus.

EDCS-13302564.translation: Quintus Caecilius Fortis lived for 45 years; he is buried here. Ovinia Polla lived for 55 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13302564.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespans and burials of two individuals, Quintus Caecilius Fortis and Ovinia Polla.

EDCS-13302565.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Betelia, wife of Materonius, lived for 60 years. May your bones rest well. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld, for the memory of the Caecilii. Marcus Lucius Caecilius Quirina Fortunatus made this while alive for himself and [others].
EDCS-13302565.summary: A tombstone inscription for Betelia and for members of the Caecilii family, indicating it was erected by Marcus Lucius Caecilius Fortunatus during his lifetime.

EDCS-13302566.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Caius Clodius Flaccus, who lived 70 years. May your bones rest well here. For Flaviae Maurae, his wife, who lived 60 years. May your bones rest well here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Clodius Faustus, in his lifetime, erected this tomb and returned to the world of the living to enjoy good things for many years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Alfia Lucilla, who lived 20 years according to her character. May your bones rest well here.
EDCS-13302566.summary: This is a collection of funerary inscriptions for Caius Clodius Flaccus, Flaviae Maurae, Caius Clodius Faustus, and Alfia Lucilla, likely from the same tomb complex, commemorating their lives and deaths.

EDCS-13302568.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Antonia Monnica lived 61 years. May your bones rest well here. Volumnius Felix erected a statue and two altars inscribed with a single name for his dearest wife, deserving of reward.
EDCS-13302568.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Antonia Monnica after 61 years and a dedication by her husband, Volumnius Felix.

EDCS-13302571.translation: Titus Cornelius Severianus and Quintilius Paternus Quintilianus, sons, provided this from their own money.
EDCS-13302571.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Titus Cornelius Severianus and Quintilius Paternus Quintilianus, possibly for a family monument or public work.

EDCS-13303473.translation: Quintus Iulius Rogatus, son of Quintus, lived 100 years. He lies here.
EDCS-13303473.summary: This is a simple tombstone recording the name, parentage, age at death, and burial of Quintus Iulius Rogatus.

EDCS-13303474.translation: Caius Iulius Rufus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 3 years. He lies here.
