EDCS-13500409.translation: Lucius Licinius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, from Natalissius, lived 17 years, is placed here.
EDCS-13500409.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Licinius, a young man, recording his name, filiation, tribal affiliation, and age at death.

EDCS-13500410.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Licinius Optatinus, a child, recording his name and age at death, though the burial location is not explicitly stated.

EDCS-13500411.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aulus Licinius Optatus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-13500411.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Aulus Licinius Optatus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, likely an epitaph for a deceased individual.

EDCS-13500412.translation: Licinius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 42 years. Iulia Sapida, daughter of Iulius Sarpilus, [and] Quintus Licinius Possessor placed this for [their] parents.
EDCS-13500412.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the names and ages of Licinius, Iulia Sapida, and Quintus Licinius Possessor, who set up the monument for their parents.

EDCS-13500413.translation: Marcus Licinius Pudens lived 40 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13500413.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Licinius Pudens, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-13500414.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Publius Licinius Rogatus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 91 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13500414.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Licinius Rogatus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-13500415.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Brussia Urbana, daughter of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years. Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Licinius Solutor, son of Urbanius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years.
EDCS-13500415.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the ages of Brussia Urbana and Marcus Licinius Solutor, suggesting they may have been a couple or related.

EDCS-13500416.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Ti[tia?] Honorata. Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Licinius Sosianus.
EDCS-13500416.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph mentioning Tia Honorata and Licinius Sosianus.

EDCS-13500417.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Licinius Urbanus, recording his age.

EDCS-13500418.translation: Marcus Licinius Urbanus, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 60 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13500418.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Licinius Urbanus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-13500419.translation: Publius Licinius Urbanus, son of Lucius, lived 25 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13500419.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Licinius Urbanus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-13500420.translation: Quintus Licinius Urbanus lived 65 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13500420.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Licinius Urbanus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-13500421.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Livia M[---]. Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Licinius.
EDCS-13500421.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph mentioning Livia and Marcus Licinius.

EDCS-13500423.translation: Licinia Galla, daughter of Lucius, lived 18 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13500423.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licinia Galla, indicating her age at death.

EDCS-13500424.translation: Licinia Honorata, daughter of Caius, lived 51 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13500424.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licinia Honorata, indicating her age at death.

EDCS-13500425.translation: Licinia Quirina Honorata, daughter of Licinius, lived [---] years.
EDCS-13500425.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licinia Quirina Honorata, indicating her age at death, with the age partially lost.

EDCS-13500426.translation: Licinia Sabina, daughter of Marcus, lived 16 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13500426.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licinia Sabina, indicating her age at death.

EDCS-13500427.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Licinia Urbana Quirina, daughter of Rogatus, lived 71 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13500427.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licinia Urbana Quirina, indicating her age at death.

EDCS-13500428.translation: Marcus Livius Felix, son of Marcus, from Quirina, lived 14 years.
EDCS-13500428.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Livius Felix, indicating his age at death.

EDCS-13500429.translation: Marcus Livius Felix from Quirina lived [---] years and is buried here.
EDCS-13500429.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Livius Felix, indicating his age at death, with the age partially lost.

EDCS-13500430.translation: Veneria made this for Marcus Livius Maximus from Quirina, aged 71.
EDCS-13500430.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Veneria for Marcus Livius Maximus, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-13500431.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Livius Secundus, son of Livi Peregrinus, from Quirina, lived 95 years and is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Monula, daughter of Caecilius Felix, from Quirina, lived 32 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13500431.summary: This is a double funerary inscription for Marcus Livius Secundus and Caecilia Monula, indicating their ages at death.

EDCS-13500432.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Livius Secundus, son of Marcus, from Quirina, lived 35 years and is buried here.
EDCS-13500432.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Livius Secundus, indicating his age at death.

EDCS-13500433.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Gullia Mustia lived for... Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus Lollius Victor.
EDCS-13500433.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits, mentioning two individuals, Gullia Mustia and Quintus Lollius Victor.

EDCS-13500435.translation: Caius Lucceius Honoratus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13500435.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Lucceius Honoratus, stating his age at death and that he is buried here.

EDCS-13500436.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucceius, son of Quintus. Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Valeria, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-13500436.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits, commemorating two individuals, Lucceius and Valeria, likely related.

EDCS-13500437.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucceia Tertullina, daughter of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 65 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13500437.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucceia Tertullina, stating her age at death and that she is buried here.

EDCS-13500438.translation: Lucilia Columna lived for 30 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13500438.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucilia Columna, stating her age at death and that she is buried here.

EDCS-13500439.translation: Aulus Lucius gato lived for 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13500439.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Lucius gato, stating his age at death and that he is buried here.

EDCS-13500440.translation: Quintus Lutatius Alfianus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-13500440.summary: This inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Quintus Lutatius Alfianus.

EDCS-13500441.translation: Lucius M. Verna lived for 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13500441.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius M. Verna, stating his age at death and that he is buried here.

EDCS-13500443.translation: M. Marcella lived for 110 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13500443.summary: This is a funerary inscription for M. Marcella, stating her exceptional age at death and that she is buried here.

EDCS-13500444.translation: Macer, son of Publius, lived for 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13500444.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Macer, son of Publius, stating his age at death and that he is buried here.

EDCS-13500445.translation: CI Macer lived 10 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500445.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for CI Macer, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-13500446.translation: Sextus Maecilius Seneca lived 70 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500446.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Maecilius Seneca, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-13500447.translation: Maecius Punicus, son of Lucius, lived 90 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500447.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maecius Punicus, son of Lucius, who died at the age of 90.

EDCS-13500448.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Magnius Secundus, dutiful, lived 45 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500448.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Magnius Secundus, who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-13500449.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Sextus Mallius Ianuarius lived 80 years, is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Fuficia, daughter of Fuficius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 80 years.
EDCS-13500449.summary: This inscription records two individuals: Sextus Mallius Ianuarius, who died at 80, and Fuficia, daughter of Fuficius, who also died at 80.

EDCS-13500450.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Messia Matrona lived... Quintus Malius Lentus lived 55 years.
EDCS-13500450.summary: This inscription seems to record the lifespan of Messia Matrona and Quintus Malius Lentus, with the latter dying at 55.

EDCS-13500451.translation: Mania Romana, daughter of Quintus, lived 20 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500451.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Mania Romana, daughter of Quintus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-13500452.translation: Manilius Faustus lived 91 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500452.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Manilius Faustus, who died at the age of 91.

EDCS-13500453.translation: Quintus Manilius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, Mascel lived 13 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500453.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Manilius Mascel, son of Quintus, who died at the age of 13.

EDCS-13500454.translation: Sextus Manilius Natalis, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 90 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500454.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Manilius Natalis, son of Caius, who died at the age of 90.

EDCS-13500455.translation: Here lies Manilius Novellus; he lived 22 years. Manilia Victoria; she lived 7 years. Here she lies.
EDCS-13500455.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Manilius Novellus and Manilia Victoria, likely siblings or family members, with their ages at death.

EDCS-13500456.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Quintus Manilius Pannonius lived 81 years. Here he lies.
EDCS-13500456.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Quintus Manilius Pannonius, noting his age at death.

EDCS-13500457.translation: Manilia Quinta, daughter of Lucius, of the voting tribe Quirina, lived 31 years.
EDCS-13500457.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan of Manilia Quinta, identifying her father and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-13500458.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius Manlius, son of Lucius.
EDCS-13500458.summary: This funerary inscription identifies Lucius Manlius, son of Lucius, likely as the deceased.

EDCS-13500461.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcius Ingenuus lived 31 years. Here he lies.
EDCS-13500461.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Marcius Ingenuus, noting his age at death.

EDCS-13500462.summary: This inscription simply records the name of Quintus Marcius Quintillus, possibly a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-13500463.translation: Titus Marcius, son of Publius, of the voting tribe Quirina, Roman citizen.
EDCS-13500463.summary: This inscription identifies Titus Marcius, son of Publius, noting his tribal affiliation and citizenship.

EDCS-13500464.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Titus Marcius, son of Titus, of the voting tribe Quirina, lived 5 years. Here he lies.
EDCS-13500464.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial and age at death (5 years) of Titus Marcius, son of Titus, identifying his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-13500465.translation: Marcia Borocia lived 35 years. Here she lies.
EDCS-13500465.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Marcia Borocia and her age at death.

EDCS-13500466.translation: Marcia Euphrosine lived 40 years. Here she lies.
EDCS-13500466.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Marcia Euphrosine and her age at death.

EDCS-13500467.translation: Marcia Galla, daughter of Marcus, lived 30 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13500467.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Marcia Galla, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13500468.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Marcia Procula Quirina, daughter of Quintus, lived 20 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13500468.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording the life and burial of Marcia Procula Quirina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13500469.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Marius Catervarius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13500469.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording the life and burial of Lucius Marius Catervarius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13500470.translation: Caius Marius Dexster, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 23 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13500470.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Caius Marius Dexster, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13500471.translation: Caius Marius Honoratus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-13500471.summary: An inscription recording the name and tribal affiliation of Caius Marius Honoratus, likely a funerary marker but lacking a verb or explicit mention of death.

EDCS-13500472.translation: Marcus Marius Martialis, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 36 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13500472.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Marcus Marius Martialis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13500473.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Publius Marius Rogatus lived 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13500473.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording the life and burial of Publius Marius Rogatus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13500474.translation: Marcus Marius Scaeva, son of Caius, lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13500474.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Marcus Marius Scaeva, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13500475.translation: Maria Galla, daughter of Lucius, lived 95 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13500475.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Maria Galla, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13500476.translation: Maria Respecta, daughter of Publius, lived 75 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13500476.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Maria Respecta, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13500477.translation: Maria, daughter of Marcus, Secundilla, lived 60 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500477.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Maria Secundilla, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-13500478.translation: Maria Silvana, daughter of Lucius, lived 75 years, is buried here. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-13500478.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Maria Silvana, recording her name, age, and a common funerary wish.

EDCS-13500479.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maria Quirina Secura, daughter of Dilectus, lived 100 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500479.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording the name and long lifespan of Maria Quirina Secura.

EDCS-13500480.translation: L. Aelius Martialis lived 70 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500480.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for L. Aelius Martialis, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-13500481.translation: Martialis lived 2 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500481.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a child named Martialis, recording his short lifespan.

EDCS-13500482.translation: Mattius Ador lived 70 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500482.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Mattius Ador, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-13500483.translation: Marcellus, son of Marcus Quirinus, lived 45 years; Quirina Maxima, daughter of Quirinus, lived 106 years.
EDCS-13500483.summary: This inscription records the lifespans of two individuals, presumably a father and daughter, named Marcellus and Maxima.

EDCS-13500484.translation: L. Iulius Maximus, son of Caius Quirinus, lived 25 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500484.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for L. Iulius Maximus, recording his name, parentage, and age at death.

EDCS-13500485.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Maxima, my wife, lived 57 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500485.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated to the Manes, for Maxima, recording her age and relationship to the dedicator.

EDCS-13500486.translation: Maxima, daughter of Castus, lived 30 years.
EDCS-13500486.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Maxima, daughter of Castus, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-13500487.translation: Maxima lived 43 years. May the gods of the underworld [allow her to rest] in peace.
EDCS-13500487.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Maxima who died at the age of 43.

EDCS-13500488.translation: Maxima, 13 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-13500488.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Maxima, aged 13.

EDCS-13500490.translation: From the tribe Quirina, Maximosus lived 60 years, is buried here. [---]
EDCS-13500490.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maximosus, who lived 60 years, and includes his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-13500491.translation: Marcus Memmius Martialis, son of Publius, of the tribe Quirina, lived 55 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500491.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Memmius Martialis, son of Publius, recording his age at death.

EDCS-13500492.translation: Quintus Memmius Pudens, son of Quintus, of the tribe Quirina.
EDCS-13500492.summary: This inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Quintus Memmius Pudens.

EDCS-13500493.summary: This is a short inscription identifying a person named Memmius Victor.

EDCS-13500495.translation: Memmia Optata, daughter of Quintus, lived 80 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13500495.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Memmia Optata, daughter of Quintus, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-13500496.translation: Lucius Messius, son of Lucius, from Baricio.
EDCS-13500496.summary: This inscription records the name and parentage of Lucius Messius, with a place name associated.

EDCS-13500497.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Messius Donatus, of the tribe Quirina, lived 70 years, is buried here. His heirs set [this up]. Messi R. and Ma. made [this].
EDCS-13500497.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Quintus Messius Donatus at age 70, with his heirs setting up the monument and possibly indicating the makers.

EDCS-13500498.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Messius Rogatus, son of Quintus, of the tribe Quirina, lived 75 years, is buried here. Tuscilia Libosa, daughter of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, lived [---].
EDCS-13500498.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Quintus Messius Rogatus (age 75) and Tuscilia Libosa, both of the tribe Quirina.

EDCS-13500499.translation: Quintus Messius Rogatus, son of Quintus, lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13500499.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Messius Rogatus, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-13500501.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cornelia Vitalis, daughter of Cornelius, lived for... Lucius Metilius Barbarus, son of Marcus.
EDCS-13500501.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Manes and records the life of Cornelia Vitalis and Lucius Metilius Barbarus, though the ages are missing.

EDCS-13500502.translation: Quintus Mevius Pacatus lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13500502.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Mevius Pacatus, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-13500503.translation: Quintus Minucius Chrysippus lived 90 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13500503.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Minucius Chrysippus, who died at the age of 90.

EDCS-13500504.translation: Iulia Boroc, daughter of..., lived... years; she is buried here. Lucius Modius Felix, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13500504.summary: This inscription records the burial of Iulia Boroc and Lucius Modius Felix, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-13500505.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Maximilla, daughter of Quirina, lived 70 years; she is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Modius Ianuarius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 51 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13500505.summary: This inscription records the burials of Iulia Maximilla, who died at 70, and Caius Modius Ianuarius, who died at 51.

EDCS-13500506.translation: Modia Flora lived 80 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13500506.summary: This is a tombstone for Modia Flora, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-13500507.translation: Via Monnula, daughter of Quintus, who...
EDCS-13500507.summary: This fragment likely records a person named Via Monnula, daughter of Quintus, but is too incomplete to provide further details.

EDCS-13500508.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Mucius Candidus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-13500508.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes for Publius Mucius Candidus.

EDCS-13500509.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Mucius Genialis, son of Mucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 19 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13500509.summary: This is a tombstone for Publius Mucius Genialis, who died at the age of 19.

EDCS-13500510.translation: Mucia Athanais, while alive, set up this monument for herself. She lived for [many] years.
EDCS-13500510.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Mucia Athanais for herself, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-13500511.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Munatius Saturninus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 65 years.
EDCS-13500511.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Munatius Saturninus, recording his name, parentage, tribal affiliation, and age at death.

EDCS-13500513.translation: Musicus lived for 35 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13500513.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Musicus, recording his name and age at death, indicating burial at the site.

EDCS-13500514.translation: Mustia lived for 41 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13500514.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mustia, recording her name and age at death, indicating burial at the site.

EDCS-13500515.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Mustula.
EDCS-13500515.summary: This is a funerary inscription, possibly a tombstone, dedicated to the departed spirit of Mustula.

EDCS-13500516.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to record a filiation and a number, likely part of a funerary or dedicatory context.

EDCS-13500517.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Myra lived for 25 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13500517.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Myra, recording her name, age at death, and indicating burial at the site.

EDCS-13600001.translation: Gaius Iulius Fuscus lived for 47 years. He is buried here. Magnia, daughter of Magnus, lived for 27 years. She is buried here. Publius Sittius Fuscus lived for 75 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13600001.summary: This is a multi-person funerary inscription recording the names, ages at death, and burial locations of three individuals: Gaius Iulius Fuscus, Magnia, and Publius Sittius Fuscus.

EDCS-13600002.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Iulius Honoratus lived for 35 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-13600002.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Iulius Honoratus, recording his name, age at death, and a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-13600003.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Iulius Naso lived for 80 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13600003.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Naso, recording his name, age at death, and a wish for his peaceful repose.

EDCS-13600004.translation: For the memory of Lucius' sons, the Iulii. Natalicus lived for 30 years. Iulia Helena made this for her dearest brother.
EDCS-13600004.summary: A tombstone erected by Iulia Helena for her brother Natalicus, who lived 30 years, dating likely to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13600005.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Julius Optatus lived for 8 years.
EDCS-13600005.summary: A funerary dedication for Gaius Julius Optatus, who died at the age of 8, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13600006.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Julius Urbanus lived for 26 years. May your bones rest well here. Quintus Julius Julianus lived for 15 years.
EDCS-13600006.summary: A tombstone commemorating two individuals, Quintus Julius Urbanus and Quintus Julius Julianus, who lived 26 and 15 years respectively, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13600007.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Extricata lived for 42 years. She rests here. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-13600007.summary: A funerary dedication for Iulia Extricata, who died at the age of 42, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13600008.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Primosa lived for 38 years. May her bones rest well. She rests here. G. Quartilla lived for 30 years. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-13600008.summary: A tombstone commemorating two women, Iulia Primosa and G. Quartilla, who died at ages 38 and 30 respectively, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13600009.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Rosula lived for 66 years.
EDCS-13600009.summary: A funerary dedication for Iulia Rosula, who died at the age of 66, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13600010.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Saturnina lived for 60 years.
EDCS-13600010.summary: A funerary dedication for Iulia Saturnina, who died at the age of 60, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13600011.translation: Iulia Urania lived for 30 years. She rests here.
EDCS-13600011.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Urania, who died at the age of 30, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13600012.translation: Iulia Urbana lived for 60 years. She rests here.
EDCS-13600012.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Urbana, who died at the age of 60, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13600013.translation: Iulia Urbana lived for 20 years. She rests here.
EDCS-13600013.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Urbana, who died at the age of 20, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13600014.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased Libosus.

EDCS-13600015.translation: Lucius Licinius Capo lived 85 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13600015.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age and burial place of Lucius Licinius Capo.

EDCS-13600016.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of Marcus Licinius Racatus.

EDCS-13600017.translation: In memory of Licinius Rogatianus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, who lived 70 years; Licinius Honoratus lived 20 years.
EDCS-13600017.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates two individuals, Licinius Rogatianus and Licinius Honoratus, with their ages.

EDCS-13600018.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Lollius Felix lived 95 years; he is buried here. Publius Lollius Felix lived 55 years; he is buried here. Alfia Laeta lived 45 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13600018.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to the Divine Shades and records the ages and burial places of Caius Lollius Felix, Publius Lollius Felix, and Alfia Laeta.

EDCS-13600020.translation: Luria Sorica lived 65 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13600020.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of Luria Sorica and includes a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-13600021.translation: Lucius Manilius Mustiolus lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13600021.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age and burial place of Lucius Manilius Mustiolus.

EDCS-13600022.translation: [...]nia lived 51 years; she is buried here. Quintus Marcius Gallio lived 20 years; he is buried here. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-13600022.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages and burial places of two individuals, one whose name is incomplete, and Quintus Marcius Gallio.

EDCS-13600023.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Marcius Liberalis, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 70 years; may your bones rest well. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caecilia Prima lived 38 years; she is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13600023.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to the Divine Shades and records the ages and burial places of Quintus Marcius Liberalis and Caecilia Prima.

EDCS-13600024.translation: Quintus Marcius Rufinus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 70 years; he is buried here; may his bones rest well. Helvia Pacata lived 101 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13600024.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages and burial places of Quintus Marcius Rufinus and Helvia Pacata.

EDCS-13600025.translation: Marcia Parthenope lived 90 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13600025.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the lifespan and burial place of Marcia Parthenope.

EDCS-13600026.translation: Titus Munatius Pedianus lived 80 years; he is buried here. Caius E. Ti lived 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13600026.summary: This tombstone inscription records the lifespans of Titus Munatius Pedianus and Caius E. Ti.

EDCS-13600027.translation: Octavia Felicia, daughter of Quintus, lived 25 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13600027.summary: This tombstone inscription records the lifespan of Octavia Felicia and includes a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-13600028.translation: Octavia Rogata lived 57 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13600028.summary: This tombstone inscription records the lifespan of Octavia Rogata and includes a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-13600029.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of Marcus Postimius Saturninus.

EDCS-13600030.translation: Postimia Fortunata lived 18 years; may your bones rest well; she is buried here.
EDCS-13600030.summary: This tombstone inscription records the lifespan of Postimia Fortunata and states her burial place.

EDCS-13600031.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. In memory of Primitivus Iunior, who lived 13 years, 7 months, and 20 days. Primitivus Nepos lived 2 years and 1 month.
EDCS-13600031.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording the ages of Primitivus Iunior and Primitivus Nepos.

EDCS-13600032.translation: Proculus lived 90 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well. Nia Quintilla lived 60 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13600032.summary: This tombstone inscription records the lifespans of Proculus and Nia Quintilla and states their burial places.

EDCS-13600033.translation: Quintus Pescennius Publicio lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13600033.summary: This tombstone inscription records the lifespan of Quintus Pescennius Publicio and states his burial place.

EDCS-13600034.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Petilius Barbarus lived 35 years.
EDCS-13600034.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording the lifespan of Petilius Barbarus.

EDCS-13600035.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Petilius Gaetulus lived 85 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13600035.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Petilius Gaetulus, who lived 85 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13600036.translation: Petronia Mustia lived 45 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13600036.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Petronia Mustia, who lived 45 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13600162.translation: To Mars Augustus, Crescens: I gave what I promised.
EDCS-13600162.summary: This is a votive inscription to Mars Augustus by Crescens, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13600163.translation: To Minerva the Holy, Titus Aelius Silvanus, perpetual priest of the colony of Thamugadi, fulfilling his vow with Silvanus and his three sons.
EDCS-13600163.summary: This is a votive inscription to Minerva by Titus Aelius Silvanus, a prominent citizen of Thamugadi, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13600164.translation: To Pluto Augustus, for the safety of the Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar Augusti, Gaius Valerius Secundus restored and dedicated a temple ruined from its foundations.
EDCS-13600164.summary: This is a dedication inscription by Gaius Valerius Secundus for the restoration of a temple to Pluto Augustus, mentioning the emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13600166.translation: Hostilius Felix, by favor of the colony.
EDCS-13600166.summary: This is an inscription indicating a grant or favor bestowed upon Hostilius Felix by the colony, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13600167.translation: Arrius Fortunatus, by favor of the colony.
EDCS-13600167.summary: This is an inscription indicating a grant or favor bestowed upon Arrius Fortunatus by the colony, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13600168.translation: To the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Nerva, master of the Sarmatic and Germanicus Maximus, the record office of the 3rd Augustan legion, while designated consul.
EDCS-13600168.summary: This is a dedication inscription for the record office of the 3rd Augustan legion, honoring the deified emperors Hadrian and Nerva and mentioning military titles, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13600169.translation: To Minerva Augustus, from Heliodorus, freedman of the emperor, a clerk, with Corinthus, our emperor's steward, and his assistants.
EDCS-13600169.summary: This is a dedication to Minerva Augustus by Heliodorus, an imperial freedman and clerk, along with his colleagues, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13600170.translation: Sacred to Minerva Augustus, from Diogenes and the assistants of the clerks of the military treasury.
EDCS-13600170.summary: This is a dedication to Minerva Augustus by Diogenes and other clerks of the military treasury, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13600171.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Dolichenus, Sol Invictus, Felix, guardian of the arms of the Third Legion Augusta.
EDCS-13600171.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus and Sol Invictus by a guardian of the arms of the Third Legion Augusta, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-13600176.translation: Macer, freedman of Augustus, restored the wall of the temple of the goddess Caelestis with his own money from the ground up.
EDCS-13600176.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple wall by Macer, a freedman of Augustus, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-13600192.translation: The cavalry of the province of Africa dedicated this altar to military discipline, to the Augusti, by Quintus Anicius Faustus, consular.
EDCS-13600192.summary: This is a dedication by the mounted troops of Africa to military discipline, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13600193.translation: For the health of the Invicti Emperors Severus and Antoninus, Most Holy Augusti, and of Julia Augusta Pia, their Most Holy Mother, Claudius Gallus, legate of the Augusti with proconsular authority, consul-designate, decorated with military gifts by the Invicti Emperors on their second, most fortunate expedition in Parthia, prefect of detachments of the Fourth German Legion on the aforementioned expedition, legate of the Twenty-second Primigenia Legion, curator of the city of Thessaloniki, with his most noble wife Flavia Silva Prisca and his most noble son Flavius Catulus Munatianus and his most noble daughter Claudia Galitta, arranged for the completion of the temple begun by Tertullus, from the generosity of the goddess Caelestis.
EDCS-13600193.summary: This extensive inscription records the completion of a temple dedicated to the goddess Caelestis by Claudius Gallus, a high-ranking official, on behalf of the emperors Severus and Antoninus and empress Julia Domna, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13600222.translation: By the authority of Emperor Vespasian Caesar Augustus, the public lands of the Cirta region were assigned to the Nicibes and the Suburburans Regians, through Tullius Pomponianus Capito, legate of Augustus.
EDCS-13600222.summary: This inscription records the assignment of public lands by Emperor Vespasian, administered by his legate Tullius Pomponianus Capito, likely dating to the Flavian period (69-96 AD).

EDCS-13600230.translation: Gaius Habellius Donatus, also called Purpurius, a dancer, lived as long as he could; however, he was buried.
EDCS-13600230.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Gaius Habellius Donatus, also known as Purpurius, a dancer, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13600231.translation: Gaius Julius Silvanus erected this tomb, for his own memory and that of his descendants, for Cicero of Thabudeis, a man of honored memory, to ensure future goodwill towards him.
EDCS-13600231.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Gaius Julius Silvanus for himself and his descendants, in memory of Cicero of Thabudeis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13600271.translation: Caelestis, the priest, willingly fulfilled his vow with Iulia Victorina.
EDCS-13600271.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a vow fulfilled by Caelestis, a priest, with Iulia Victorina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13600415.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vincentius is here, the pride of pantomimists, living forever in the mouths of the people, beloved not only for his skill in stagecraft as all are, but because he was upright, good, innocent in all his ways, and continent. When he danced, he always held the theatre until the evening hours. Here he is buried. Now he has walls for his ramparts. He lived for twenty-three years, flourishing but chaste in life. Vincentius was more eloquent in his gestures.
EDCS-13600415.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Vincentius, a celebrated pantomime dancer, praising his artistic skill and virtuous character, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13600416.translation: Tiberius Claudius Proculus Cornelius, prefect of the Second Cohort of Bracari, tribune of the milliary Aelia Dacorum cohort, prefect of the Sulpician Ala, procurator of the province of Syria for auditing accounts, procurator of the mines of Pannonia and Dalmatia, procurator of the Vergilian treasury in Spain, also at the levy of recruits for two legions, the Italian and the Legionaries, throughout Italy with Julius Verus, procurator of the Thevestine region, procurator of the four public services of Africa. Found by Augusti libertus, a record-keeper of the Third Legion Augusta.
EDCS-13600416.summary: This inscription details the extensive career of Tiberius Claudius Proculus Cornelius, listing his various military and administrative posts, and notes his discovery by a freedman record-keeper of the Third Legion Augusta, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13700001.translation: Publius Naevius Inpetratus, son of Rogatus, lived for 56 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13700001.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Publius Naevius Inpetratus.

EDCS-13700002.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Naevia Cututa lived for [X] years.
EDCS-13700002.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the name and incomplete lifespan of Naevia Cututa.

EDCS-13700003.translation: Nice lived for 12 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13700003.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Nice.

EDCS-13700004.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Nilus lived for 17 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13700004.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the lifespan and burial place of Nilus.

EDCS-13700005.translation: Numeria Venusta, daughter of Numerius, lived for 75 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13700005.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Numeria Venusta.

EDCS-13700006.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Numisia Lillosa, daughter of Numisius, lived for 87 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13700006.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the lifespan and burial place of Numisia Lillosa.

EDCS-13700007.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Nunius Felix, who lived for [X] years; Vettia Urbana, who lived for 51 years; Nunia Felicia, daughter of Nuni, who lived for 30 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13700007.summary: A complex funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the lifespans of Marcus Nunius Felix, Vettia Urbana, and Nunia Felicia.

EDCS-13700008.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Nunnius Lap[idus?]; Sacred to the departed spirits. Axia, daughter of Marcus, [widow?] of Iutus.
EDCS-13700008.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription, possibly recording two individuals, Marcus Nunnius Lapidus and Axia.

EDCS-13700009.translation: Marcus Nunius Maximus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 30 years.
EDCS-13700009.summary: An inscription recording the name, tribal affiliation, and lifespan of Marcus Nunius Maximus.

EDCS-13700010.translation: Lucius Nunius Pudens, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 105 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13700010.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name, tribal affiliation, lifespan, and burial place of Lucius Nunius Pudens.

EDCS-13700011.translation: Lucius Nunius Sandula, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 21 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700011.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Nunius Sandula, who died at age 21.

EDCS-13700013.translation: Quintus Nunnius Victor, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 100 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700013.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quintus Nunnius Victor, who died at age 100.

EDCS-13700014.translation: Nunia Iulia, daughter of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700014.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Nunia Iulia, who died at age 25.

EDCS-13700015.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Nunia Nina, daughter of Marcelus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 70 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700015.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated to the spirits of the underworld for Nunia Nina, who died at age 70.

EDCS-13700016.translation: Quarta Nunia, daughter of Lucius, dutiful, lived 11 years. Here lies...
EDCS-13700016.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quarta Nunia, who died at age 11.

EDCS-13700017.translation: Octavia Mustia, daughter of Lucius, lived 85 years.
EDCS-13700017.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Octavia Mustia, who died at age 85.

EDCS-13700018.translation: Octavia Vitalis lived 45 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700018.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Octavia Vitalis, who died at age 45.

EDCS-13700019.translation: Marcus Ofellius Ianuarius lived 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700019.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Ofellius Ianuarius, who died at age 45.

EDCS-13700020.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Ofellia Victoria, daughter of Marcus, lived 55 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700020.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated to the spirits of the underworld for Ofellia Victoria, who died at age 55.

EDCS-13700021.translation: Quintus Oppius Saturninus lived 20 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700021.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quintus Oppius Saturninus, who died at age 20.

EDCS-13700022.translation: Oppia Maxima lived 31 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700022.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Oppia Maxima, who died at age 31.

EDCS-13700024.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Oppia, daughter of Oppius.
EDCS-13700024.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes for Oppia, daughter of Oppius.

EDCS-13700025.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld: Orbia Metillia, daughter of Gaius, lived 75 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700025.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Orbia Metillia, daughter of Gaius, who died at age 75.

EDCS-13700026.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Caius, son of Orcius.
EDCS-13700026.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes for Caius, son of Orcius.

EDCS-13700027.translation: Otacilia Victoria, daughter of Caius, lived 50 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700027.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Otacilia Victoria, daughter of Caius, who died at age 50.

EDCS-13700028.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld: Fuficia. Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld: Caius, son of P.
EDCS-13700028.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes, fragmented and mentioning Fuficia and Caius, son of P.

EDCS-13700029.translation: Caius P. Respectus lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700029.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius P. Respectus, who died at age 25.

EDCS-13700030.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld: Publius, son of Spurius.
EDCS-13700030.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes for Publius, son of Spurius.

EDCS-13700031.translation: Maxima lived 20 years; she is buried here. Livius Pacatus, son of Quinti, of the tribus Quirina, lived 90 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700031.summary: This epitaph records two individuals: Maxima, who died at age 20, and Livius Pacatus, son of Quinti, who died at age 90.

EDCS-13700032.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld: Pacconius, son of Quinti, of the tribus Quirina, placed this for his living husband. Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld: Sittia Rufina, daughter of Marci, lived.
EDCS-13700032.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Sittia Rufina for her husband and mentions her own life span, though it is cut off.

EDCS-13700033.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Pacconia Urbana lived 101 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700033.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death and age of Pacconia Urbana.

EDCS-13700034.translation: Quintus Crescens, son of Quintus, lived...
EDCS-13700034.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age of Quintus Crescens, with his age incomplete.

EDCS-13700035.translation: Pentatius, son of Pentatius, from the voting tribe Quirina, lived 100 years.
EDCS-13700035.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age of Pentatius.

EDCS-13700036.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name of Pentatia Rogata.

EDCS-13700037.translation: Percennia Galla, daughter of Publius, lived 95 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700037.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death and age of Percennia Galla.

EDCS-13700038.translation: Lucius Perelius Crescens lived 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700038.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death and age of Lucius Perelius Crescens.

EDCS-13700039.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Persius Severus, son of Lucius, from the voting tribe Quirina, lived 81 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Valeria Castula, daughter of Quintus, lived 55 years.
EDCS-13700039.summary: This is a double funerary epitaph recording the deaths and ages of Lucius Persius Severus and Valeria Castula.

EDCS-13700040.translation: Victor, freedman of Titus Pescennius, lived 40 years.
EDCS-13700040.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death and age of Victor, a freedman.

EDCS-13700041.translation: Aulus Petronius Macer, son of Caius, from the voting tribe Quirina, lived 95 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700041.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death and age of Aulus Petronius Macer.

EDCS-13700042.translation: Publia Petronia Urbana, daughter of Saturninus.
EDCS-13700042.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name of Publia Petronia Urbana.

EDCS-13700043.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Pinneus, son of Gaius Pinneus, of the tribe Quirina. Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Aurelia, daughter of Gaius Aurelius Gargilius.
EDCS-13700043.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two individuals, Gaius Pinneus and Iulia Aurelia, likely a married couple or family members.

EDCS-13700044.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Plania Caecilia lived.
EDCS-13700044.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Plania Caecilia, recording her life.

EDCS-13700045.translation: Quintus Plotius Crescens lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700045.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Plotius Crescens, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-13700046.translation: Gnaeus Pompeius Catulus lived 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700046.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gnaeus Pompeius Catulus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-13700047.translation: Lucius Pompeius Epaphra, freedman of Lucius Pompeius, lived; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700047.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Pompeius Epaphra, a freedman, recording his burial.

EDCS-13700048.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Nunia Gallina, daughter of Gallonius, of the tribe Quirina, lived 41 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Pompeius Extricatus lived 51 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700048.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two individuals, Nunia Gallina and Marcus Pompeius Extricatus, likely a couple, recording their ages and burial.

EDCS-13700049.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Pompeius Faustus, son of Gaius, lived 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700049.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Pompeius Faustus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-13700050.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Pompeius Felix lived 9 years.
EDCS-13700050.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Pompeius Felix, recording his young age.

EDCS-13700051.translation: Marcus Pompeius Honoratus, son of Marcus, of the tribe Quirina, lived 65 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700051.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Pompeius Honoratus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-13700052.translation: Publius Pompeius Maximus, son of Publius, of the tribe Quirina, lived 30 years; he is buried here. Arnesia Maxima lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700052.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two individuals, Publius Pompeius Maximus and Arnesia Maxima, likely a couple, recording their ages and burial.

EDCS-13700053.translation: Gaius Pompeius Palmosus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700053.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Pompeius Palmosus, recording his name, father's name, tribe, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-13700054.translation: Gnaeus Pompeius Simnius, son of Gnaeus, of the Simnia tribe, lived 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700054.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gnaeus Pompeius Simnius, recording his name, father's name, tribe, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-13700055.translation: Pompeia Galla, daughter of Pompeius, lived 45 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700055.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pompeia Galla, recording her name, father's name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-13700056.translation: Pompeia Tertia, daughter of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 91 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700056.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pompeia Tertia, recording her name, father's name, tribe, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-13700057.translation: Pompeia Vitalis, daughter of Lucius, lived 81 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700057.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pompeia Vitalis, recording her name, father's name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-13700058.translation: Pomponius Arator, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived [---] years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700058.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pomponius Arator, recording his name, father's name, tribe, and burial, with an incomplete age.

EDCS-13700059.translation: Pomponius Celsus of the Quirina tribe lived 70 years.
EDCS-13700059.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pomponius Celsus, recording his name, tribe, and age, with an incomplete burial statement.

EDCS-13700060.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Pomponius Felix, son of Quintus, lived 70 years; he is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Pomponius Passus lived 15 years. Iulia Pomponia, sister, lived 12 years; they are buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Vibia M[---]nu, daughter of Vibius, lived [---] years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700060.summary: This is a complex funerary epitaph honoring multiple individuals from the Pomponius and Vibia families, recording their names, ages, relationships, and burials.

EDCS-13700061.translation: Pomponius Arator, son of Geminius, lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700061.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pomponius Arator, recording his name, father's name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-13700062.translation: Marcus Pomponius Natalis lived 15 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700062.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Pomponius Natalis, recording his name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-13700063.translation: Gaius Pomponius Victor, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived [X] years. Quintus Pomponius Solutor, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 20 years.
EDCS-13700063.summary: This epitaph records the lives of two individuals, Gaius Pomponius Victor and Quintus Pomponius Solutor.

EDCS-13700064.translation: Marcus Pomponius Urbanus, grandson of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years.
EDCS-13700064.summary: This epitaph records the life of Marcus Pomponius Urbanus, grandson of Gaius.
