EDCS-13700196.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Titus Tullius Donatus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, of rare affection, and [---]
EDCS-13700196.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Shades for Titus Tullius Donatus, noted for his rare affection, with the rest of the inscription missing.

EDCS-13700197.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iullius Iulianus lived 30 years.
EDCS-13700197.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Shades for Iullius Iulianus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-13700198.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Tullia Matrona at the age of 55.

EDCS-13700199.translation: Iullia Rogata lived 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700199.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death and burial of Iullia Rogata at the age of 35.

EDCS-13700200.translation: Sextus Turanius Hepesus is buried here; he lived 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700200.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Turanius Hepesus, who lived 60 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13700201.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. V. Borocia lived 50 years. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Numerius Ibilius AI lived [---] years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700201.summary: This inscription contains two separate funerary dedications, one for V. Borocia and another for Numerius Ibilius AI, with the latter's age incomplete.

EDCS-13700202.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Valerius Aemilius, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, set this up for himself while alive; he lived [---] years; he is buried here. Pompeia Libosa, daughter of Marcus, his dearest wife, made this for her husband; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700202.summary: This is a complex funerary monument with a self-dedication by Marcus Valerius Aemilius and a dedication by his wife, Pompeia Libosa, to him, both dated to when they were alive or for the deceased.

EDCS-13700203.translation: Lucius Valerius Carito, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 31 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700203.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Carito, who died at age 31.

EDCS-13700204.translation: Publius Valerius Donatus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 111 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700204.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Valerius Donatus, who died at the exceptional age of 111.

EDCS-13700205.translation: Lucius Valerius Felix lived 14 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700205.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Felix, who died at age 14.

EDCS-13700206.translation: Marcus Valerius Felix, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-13700206.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely an epitaph, for Marcus Valerius Felix.

EDCS-13700207.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Caius Publicius Crescius lived 15 years.
EDCS-13700207.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Publicius Crescius, who died at age 15.

EDCS-13700208.translation: Publius Pullius Comatus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13700208.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Pullius Comatus, who died at age 70.

EDCS-13700209.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Senecio lived 50 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13700209.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Senecio, who died at age 50, with a wish for the repose of his bones.

EDCS-13700210.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Recta Faustilla lived 60 years.
EDCS-13700210.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Recta Faustilla, who died at age 60.

EDCS-13700211.translation: Sa Galla lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13700211.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sa Galla, who died at age 25.

EDCS-13700212.translation: Marcus Sallustius Saturninus lived 25 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13700212.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Sallustius Saturninus, who died at age 25, with a wish for the repose of his bones.

EDCS-13700213.translation: Gaius Sallustius Urbanus lived 18 years, he is buried here. Marcus Sallustius Valens lived 16 years, he is buried here. May your bones rest well. Lucilia Maximina lived 50 years, she is buried here. Cecilia Urbanilla, daughter of Marcius, lived 81 years, she is buried here. Marcus Sallustius Quadratus, son, lived 18 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-13700213.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the ages and burial places of several individuals from the Sallustii family.

EDCS-13700214.translation: Sallustia Matrona lived 30 years. Sallustia Lullosa lived 11 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13700214.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of two individuals, Sallustia Matrona and Sallustia Lullosa.

EDCS-13700215.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sallustia Optatina lived 18 years, she is buried here. Gaius Sallustius Anteros lived 70 years. To the spirits of the departed. Caelia Optata lived 80 years, she is buried here. Gaius Sallustius Adarbal lived 60 years, he is buried here. Lucius Sallustius Faustinus lived 50 years, he is buried here. Gaius Sallustius Crispus lived 60 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-13700215.summary: This is a funerary inscription for several members of the Sallustii family, listing their ages at death.

EDCS-13700217.summary: A brief funerary inscription recording the age of Saturnina.

EDCS-13700218.translation: Saturninus lived 50 years. Aemilia Iulia lived... years
EDCS-13700218.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription recording the age of Saturninus and partially that of Aemilia Iulia.

EDCS-13700219.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Novellus made this for his sister Sentia Tertia, who lived 18 years.
EDCS-13700219.summary: A funerary inscription where Novellus commemorates his sister Sentia Tertia, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-13700220.translation: Silicia Matutina lived 50 years, she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13700220.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of Silicia Matutina and a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-13700221.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcellus, son of Marcus Sitius Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 90 years. May you rest well here, and those of the Sittii. Urbani lived 14 years. [---] lived... years
EDCS-13700221.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcellus, son of Marcus Sitius Lucius, with additional fragmented mentions of other Sittii family members.

EDCS-13700333.translation: Sacred to the Unconquered God Mithras. Marcus Valerius Maximianus, legate of the emperor with praetorian rank.
EDCS-13700333.summary: A dedication to the god Mithras by Marcus Valerius Maximianus, an imperial legate, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13700334.translation: For the Unconquered God Mithras, for the health and safety of the most distinguished man Celsianus, actor and notary, and of Gaius Fabius Fabianus Vetilius Lucilianus, legate of the emperor with praetorian rank. Celsianus willingly made this vow.
EDCS-13700334.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras by Celsianus, an actor and notary, for the well-being of himself and the imperial legate Gaius Fabius Fabianus Vetilius Lucilianus.

EDCS-13700335.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, Valerius Florus, a most distinguished man, governor of the province of Numidia Militiana, set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-13700335.summary: A dedication to the god Sol Invictus Mithras by the governor of Numidia Militiana, Valerius Florus.

EDCS-13700360.translation: The emperors Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Lucius Aurelius Commodus Augusti, conquerors of Germany and Sarmatia, the bravest, restored a part of the amphitheater from the ground up and the entire podium, which had been corrupted by age, through the Third Legion Augusta.
EDCS-13700360.summary: An imperial rescript by Marcus Aurelius and Commodus, indicating the restoration of an amphitheater by the Third Legion Augusta.

EDCS-13700362.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, father of the country, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the second time, emperor for the third time, consul for the second time, repaired and adorned the work of the amphitheater through Gaius Julius Lepidus Tertullus, his clarissimos legate with the power of a pro-praetor, by the Third Legion Augusta.
EDCS-13700362.summary: An imperial inscription by Septimius Severus detailing the repair and adornment of an amphitheater by the Third Legion Augusta under the legate Gaius Julius Lepidus Tertullus.

EDCS-13700366.translation: Sacred to the August Mother of the Gods. Grattius Mustiolus, aedile for the second time, prefect, willingly and deservedly paid his vow for the five-year period.
EDCS-13700366.summary: A religious dedication to the Mother of the Gods by Grattius Mustiolus, an official in his second term as aedile and prefect, fulfilling a quinquennial vow.

EDCS-13700380.translation: Sacred to Fortuna and Augusta Ceres. Caius Caecilius Rusticus, priest, paid his vow.
EDCS-13700380.summary: A religious dedication to Fortuna and Ceres by the priest Caius Caecilius Rusticus.

EDCS-13700411.translation: While Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the divine, was consul for the eighth time, and Titus Statilius Taurus for the second time, Lucius Iulius Arrenus, duumvir, assigned the lands to the colonists, with the decumanus on the left and the decumanus on the right beyond.
EDCS-13700411.summary: A land assignment inscription from the time of Augustus and Titus Statilius Taurus, mentioning the duumvir Lucius Iulius Arrenus.

EDCS-13800001.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. In memory of Caius Tammudius Urbanus, son of Caius, of the voting tribe Quirina, built this for himself while alive. He lived 75 years.
EDCS-13800001.summary: A funerary dedication to the Dis Manes by Caius Tammudius Urbanus, who erected his own tombstone at the age of 75.

EDCS-13800002.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Valerius Clarus, son of Quintus, of the voting tribe Quirina, lived 52 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13800002.summary: A funerary dedication to the Dis Manes for Quintus Valerius Clarus, who lived 52 years.

EDCS-13800003.translation: Vettius Maximus lived 70 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13800003.summary: A tombstone inscription for Vettius Maximus, recording his age and a wish for his bones to rest well.

EDCS-13800004.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Vibius Rufus lived 80 years, more or less. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13800004.summary: A funerary dedication to the Dis Manes for Marcus Vibius Rufus, who lived approximately 80 years.

EDCS-13800005.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Ulpius Ianuarius made this himself during his lifetime for his dearest wife, Iulia Primosa.
EDCS-13800005.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Lucius Ulpius Ianuarius for his wife, Iulia Primosa.

EDCS-13800006.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of Itala.

EDCS-13800007.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. RIOAISIDA lived for 40 years.
EDCS-13800007.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of RIOAISIDA.

EDCS-13800125.translation: For the emperor Marcus Aemilius Aemilianus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, one mile.
EDCS-13800125.summary: An inscription marking a distance of one mile for Emperor Aemilianus, likely from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-13800126.translation: For our emperors and lords, Publius Licinius Valerianus and Publius Licinius Gallienus Invicti Pii Felices Augusti, ten [miles/units].
EDCS-13800126.summary: An inscription honoring Emperors Valerian and Gallienus, likely from their joint reign (253-260 CE).

EDCS-13800134.translation: For Gaius Julius Rufinus Laberius Fabianus Pomponius Triarius Erucius Clarus Sosius Priscus Salinus Collianus, son of Erucius Clarus, ordinary consul, and grandson of Erucius Clarus, ordinary consul, patron, the council and people of the Dianensians, because of the merits of his father, by whom he protected, defended, and supported them individually and collectively in all matters with outstanding loyalty...
EDCS-13800134.summary: A dedication by the town of Dianensium to Gaius Julius Rufinus Laberius Fabianus Pomponius Triarius Erucius Clarus Sosius Priscus Salinus Collianus, honoring his father's merits.

EDCS-13800138.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Lucius Domitius Marcellus, son of Lucius, of the Papirian tribe, a veteran, formerly a duplicarius of the 1st Pannonian cavalry ala, had this made at his own expense for 2000 sesterces and dedicated it himself.
EDCS-13800138.summary: A dedication to Mercury Augustus by a military veteran, Lucius Domitius Marcellus, for the construction and dedication of a shrine at his own expense.

EDCS-13800139.translation: Quintus Iulius Frontinus, prefect of the 8th cohort of volunteers, gave this as a gift to his native goddess.
EDCS-13800139.summary: A dedication to a 'native goddess' by Quintus Iulius Frontinus, a military prefect.

EDCS-13800140.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Venus Augusta. Iulius Felix, son of Marcus, an[n]...
EDCS-13800140.summary: A partial inscription dedicated to Fortuna and Venus Augusta by Iulius Felix.

EDCS-13800141.summary: An inscription dedicating something to the deified Empress Faustina Augusta.

EDCS-13800143.translation: Flavius Herodes, the consular governor, established a bronze measure for wheat and a bronze measure for wine.
EDCS-13800143.summary: This is a public inscription from an unknown location, dated to the Roman imperial period, recording the establishment of measures for grain and wine by a governor.

EDCS-13900001.translation: Quintus Caecilius Senex lived 72 years; he is buried here. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-13900001.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from an unknown location, likely Roman imperial period, recording the lifespan and burial of Quintus Caecilius Senex.

EDCS-13900002.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Cocilius Iulius lived 80 years.
EDCS-13900002.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from an unknown location, likely Roman imperial period, recording the lifespan of Marcus Cocilius Iulius.

EDCS-13900003.translation: Lucius Iulius Pacatus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived [many?] years; he is buried here. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-13900003.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from an unknown location, likely Roman imperial period, recording the lifespan and burial of Lucius Iulius Pacatus.

EDCS-13900004.translation: Modia Quieta lived 75 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900004.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from an unknown location, likely Roman imperial period, recording the lifespan and burial of Modia Quieta.

EDCS-13900005.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. In memory of his wife M. V. Quartilla, who lived and lived well, his sons made this.
EDCS-13900005.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from an unknown location, likely Roman imperial period, dedicated to a wife by her sons, commemorating her life.

EDCS-13900006.translation: To the Divine Manes. Uggea lived 103 years.
EDCS-13900006.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from an unknown location, likely Roman imperial period, recording the remarkable lifespan of Uggea.

EDCS-13900007.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from an unknown location, likely Roman imperial period, recording the lifespan of Sulpicius Po, though incomplete.

EDCS-13900008.translation: A most rare wife lived 10 years; may her bones rest well.
EDCS-13900008.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from an unknown location, likely Roman imperial period, commemorating a wife who died very young.

EDCS-13900011.translation: By the authority of Publius Cassius Secundus, legate of Augustus, concerning the public lands of the Siguitani.
EDCS-13900011.summary: This is a legal or administrative inscription from an unknown location, Roman imperial period, issued by a legatus Augusti concerning land rights of the Siguitani people.

EDCS-13900012.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Iulius Felix, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, father of Roman knights, lived 65 years, is buried here. Iulia Rufina, daughter of Caius, wife of Iulius Felix, lived 30 years.
EDCS-13900012.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Iulius Felix and his wife Iulia Rufina, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13900013.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. I. Marcella lived 80 years, is here, may she rest well.
EDCS-13900013.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for I. Marcella, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13900014.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Iulius Caecilianus lived 60 years, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13900014.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Iulius Caecilianus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13900015.translation: To the memory of Iulius Felix and Rufina his wife and their children Optatus, Honoratus, Rufinus, grandsons of Caius Iulius Nampulus and Allia Optata his wife, and of [his] parents Marcus Iulius Felix.
EDCS-13900015.summary: This is a memorial inscription for members of the Iulii family, including parents and grandchildren, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13900016.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Iulius Musolicus lived 68 years, may your bones rest well. His sons made [this]. Sacred to the departed spirits. Publia Maxima lived 60 years, may you rest well here.
EDCS-13900016.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Iulius Musolicus and Publia Maxima, set up by their sons, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13900017.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Iulius Rufinus lived 65 years, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13900017.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Iulius Rufinus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13900018.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pulaenia Augusta lived 65 years, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13900018.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pulaenia Augusta, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13900019.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Sallustius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, Felix. Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Sallustius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, Septiminus, lived 21 years, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13900019.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Sallustius and Lucius Sallustius, possibly brothers, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13900020.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Sallustius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, Urbanus, lived 101 years, may your bones rest well. Domitia Urbanilla lived 85 years, may she rest well here.
EDCS-13900020.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Sallustius Urbanus and Domitia Urbanilla, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13900021.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Sittius Mascel, son of Marcus Sittius Mascel, who lived 75 years, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-13900021.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Sittius Mascel, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13900040.translation: Sacred to Liber and Libera. Gaius Aemilius Felicianus, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, quaestor, aedile, pontifex, made this at his own expense.
EDCS-13900040.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deities Liber and Libera by a local magistrate named Gaius Aemilius Felicianus.

EDCS-13900150.translation: To the memory of Flavius Elpidius, who was also called Europides, who lived ten years.
EDCS-13900150.summary: This inscription is a tombstone recording the memory of a child named Flavius Elpidius who died at the age of ten.

EDCS-13900151.translation: To the memory of Pompeia Primitiva, daughter of Tsias.
EDCS-13900151.summary: This inscription is a tombstone recording the memory of a woman named Pompeia Primitiva.

EDCS-13900155.translation: Sacred to Caelestis Augusta. Tiberius Claudius Mascellus made this with his own money.
EDCS-13900155.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Caelestis Augusta by an individual named Tiberius Claudius Mascellus.

EDCS-13900156.translation: Sacred to the deified Emperor. Acutus making this for the safety of the Emperor.
EDCS-13900156.summary: This inscription records a dedication to a deified emperor, made by Acutus for the emperor's safety.

EDCS-13900157.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the deified Commodus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus, propagator of the empire, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 5th time, acclaimed emperor for the 8th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, father of the fatherland, father of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, propagator of the empire, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-13900157.summary: This is a lengthy titulature of Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus, listing his imperial titles and ancestry, likely from an official monument.

EDCS-13900158.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the camps, wife of the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the deified Commodus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Parthicus Arabicus Parthicus Adiabenicus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 7th time, acclaimed emperor for the 11th time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, the most brave and holy prince, mother of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, the most indulgent prince. By decree of the decurions of the community of Silensium.
EDCS-13900158.summary: This inscription honors Julia Domna, wife of Septimius Severus and mother of Caracalla, commemorating her titles and the decree of the community of Silensium.

EDCS-13900159.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, the most brave and fortunate prince, son of the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the deified Commodus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Parthicus Arabicus Parthicus Adiabenicus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 7th time, acclaimed emperor for the 11th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, propagator of the empire, father of the fatherland, the most brave and fortunate prince, the most indulgent and most noble prince. By the community of Silensium.
EDCS-13900159.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Caracalla, listing his imperial titles and his relationship to Septimius Severus, with a decree from the community of Silensium.

EDCS-13900160.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, grandson of the deified Severus Pius, son of the deified Antoninus Magnus Pius, Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul, father of the fatherland. By the community of Silensium, devoted to his divine majesty, by public funds.
EDCS-13900160.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Severus Alexander by the community of Silensium, funded by public money.

EDCS-13900161.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antoninus Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 4th time, acclaimed emperor for the 3rd time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul. By the community of Silensium, by decree of the decurions, with public funds.
EDCS-13900161.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Gordian III by the community of Silensium, highlighting his imperial titles and funded by public money.

EDCS-13900162.translation: To Cornelia Salonina Augusta, mother of the camps, his wife.
EDCS-13900162.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Cornelia Salonina, likely erected by her husband, a soldier, referring to her titles as Augusta and mother of the camps, implying a date in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900164.translation: Valerius Firmus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, set this up in his lifetime. His wife, Pompeia Libosa, daughter of Marcus, dearly beloved, lies here; the husband made this.
EDCS-13900164.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Valerius Firmus for himself and his wife, Pompeia Libosa, during his lifetime, indicating a Roman Republican or Imperial date.

EDCS-13900165.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valeria Fortunata lived 80 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Valerius Fortunatus.
EDCS-13900165.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life spans of Valeria Fortunata and Valerius Fortunatus, dedicated to the departed spirits, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900166.translation: Valerius Fronto lived 30 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13900166.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Valerius Fronto at the age of 30, indicating a Roman Imperial date.

EDCS-13900167.summary: This is a very brief funerary epitaph, likely an inscription on a tombstone or marker, for Valerius Fronto, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900168.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the long life of Publius Valerius Fuficius, indicating a Roman Imperial date.

EDCS-13900169.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cosia Prima lived 25 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Valerius Ianuarius lived 65 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13900169.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the life spans of Cosia Prima and Valerius Ianuarius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900170.translation: Titus Valerius Ingenuus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, son of Hordionius, lived 75 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13900170.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Valerius Ingenuus, son of Titus and belonging to the Quirina tribe, indicating a Roman Imperial date.

EDCS-13900171.translation: Caius Valerius Martialis lived 25 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13900171.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Caius Valerius Martialis at the age of 25, indicating a Roman Imperial date.

EDCS-13900172.translation: Sextus Valerius Martialis, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 67 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13900172.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Valerius Martialis, son of Sextus and belonging to the Quirina tribe, indicating a Roman Imperial date.

EDCS-13900173.translation: Sextus Valerius Martial, son of Sextus, of the tribe Quirina, lived 65 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13900173.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextus Valerius Martial, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900174.translation: Marcus Valerius Maxumus lived 70 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13900174.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Maxumus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900175.translation: Iulia Pacata lived 40 years; she lies here. Quintus Valerius Maximus, son of Quintus, of the tribe Quirina, lived 50 years; he lies here. Quintus Valerius Onus, son of Quintus, lived 6 years.
EDCS-13900175.summary: A funerary epitaph for members of the Valerius family, including Iulia Pacata, Quintus Valerius Maximus, and Quintus Valerius Onus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900176.translation: Marcus Valerius Mustius, son of Marcus, of the tribe Quirina, lived 110 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13900176.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Mustius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900177.translation: To the departed spirits. Valerius Quetus, son of Marcus, of the tribe Quirina, dedicated this to himself while alive; he lived 85 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13900177.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Valerius Quetus to himself during his lifetime, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900178.translation: Publius Valerius Rogatus, son of Publius, of the tribe Quirina, lived 60 years and 3 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13900178.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Valerius Rogatus, who lived for 63 years, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900179.translation: Publius Valerius Sever lived 100 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13900179.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Valerius Sever, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900180.translation: Lucius Valerius Sextianus, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, lived 27 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13900180.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Sextianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900181.translation: Marcus Valerius Solutor, son of Sextus, of the tribe Quirina, lived [---] years; he lies here.
EDCS-13900181.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Solutor, with the duration of his life missing, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900182.translation: Publius Valerius Statius, son of Publius, of the tribe Quirina, lived 61 years; he lies here.
EDCS-13900182.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Valerius Statius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900183.translation: Marcus Valerius Tertius, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900183.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Tertius, recording his name, tribal affiliation, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-13900184.summary: A very brief funerary epitaph for Valerius Tertius, likely indicating his age at death.

EDCS-13900185.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Valerius Victor, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900185.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Valerius Victor, mentioning his name, tribal affiliation, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-13900186.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Valerius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-13900186.summary: A funerary epitaph fragment for Caius Valerius, indicating his name, parentage, and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-13900187.translation: Valeria, daughter of Victor, is buried here. Her parent is buried here.
EDCS-13900187.summary: A funerary epitaph fragment mentioning Valeria and her parent, both of whom are buried at this location.

EDCS-13900188.translation: Valeria Celsa, daughter of Celsinus, lived 85 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900188.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Celsa, detailing her name, parentage, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-13900189.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Dativa, daughter of Caius, lived 30 years; she is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Annia Urbana lived 60 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900189.summary: A double funerary epitaph recording the lives and burials of Valeria Dativa and Annia Urbana.

EDCS-13900190.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Mellina, dearest daughter, her mother Galatea erected this. She lived 21 years. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Galatea, freedwoman, lived 65 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900190.summary: A double funerary epitaph, one for Mellina erected by her mother, and another for Valeria Galatea, a freedwoman, detailing their ages and burials.

EDCS-13900191.translation: Valeria Iulia, daughter of Caius, lived 50 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900191.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Iulia, recording her name, parentage, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-13900192.translation: Valeria Matrona, daughter of Publius, lived 85 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900192.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Matrona, providing her name, parentage, age at death, and burial location.

EDCS-13900193.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of Valeria Maxima, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-13900194.translation: Valeria Maxima, daughter of Veg, lived 11 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-13900194.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan and burial of Valeria Maxima, daughter of Veg.

EDCS-13900195.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Maximilla lived 41 years.
EDCS-13900195.summary: This inscription, dedicated to the Manes, records the lifespan of Valeria Maximilla, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-13900196.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Meletine lived 120 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-13900196.summary: This tombstone, dedicated to the Manes, records the exceptional lifespan and burial of Valeria Meletine.

EDCS-13900197.translation: Valeria Monnula, daughter of Titus, lived 35 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-13900197.summary: This tombstone records the lifespan and burial of Valeria Monnula, daughter of Titus.

EDCS-13900198.translation: Valeria Namphame lived 13 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900198.summary: This inscription records the burial of Valeria Namphame, who lived 13 years.

EDCS-13900199.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of Valeria Rogata, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-13900200.translation: Valeria Rogata lived 10 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-13900200.summary: This tombstone records the lifespan and burial of Valeria Rogata, who died at age 10.

EDCS-13900201.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Rogata.
EDCS-13900201.summary: This inscription, dedicated to the Manes, likely commemorates Valeria Rogata on a tombstone.

EDCS-13900203.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Quirina Rogata, daughter of Marcus, lived 101 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-13900203.summary: This tombstone, dedicated to the Manes, records the long lifespan and burial of Valeria Quirina Rogata, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-13900204.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valeria Rustica, daughter of Publius, lived 16 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900204.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Rustica, who died at 16 years of age.

EDCS-13900205.translation: Valeria Satura, daughter of Publius, lived 17 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900205.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Satura, who died at 17 years of age.

EDCS-13900206.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valeria Quirina Secunda, daughter of Quintus, lived 55 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900206.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Quirina Secunda, who died at 55 years of age.

EDCS-13900207.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valeria Victoria lived 15 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900207.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Victoria, who died at 15 years of age.

EDCS-13900208.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valeria Vitalis, a devoted mother, lived 51 years; sons and heirs made this.
EDCS-13900208.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Vitalis, a mother who died at 51, with the monument erected by her children.

EDCS-13900209.translation: Valeria Vitalis, daughter of Titus, lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900209.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Vitalis, daughter of Titus, who died at 25 years of age.

EDCS-13900211.translation: Veneria lived 18 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900211.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Veneria, who died at 18 years of age.

EDCS-13900212.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Ventidius Vitalio, son of Quintus, lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900212.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Ventidius Vitalio, who died at 35 years of age.

EDCS-13900213.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ventidia Matrona lived 90 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900213.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ventidia Matrona, who died at 90 years of age.

EDCS-13900214.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Venulus lived 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900214.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Venulus, who died at 55 years of age.

EDCS-13900215.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vesidia Ammona, daughter of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 15 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13900215.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vesidia Ammona, recording her age at death.

EDCS-13900216.translation: Vesidia Satura, daughter of Caius, lived for 7 years and some days. She is buried here.
EDCS-13900216.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vesidia Satura, recording her age at death.

EDCS-13900217.translation: Quintus Vetilius Victor lived for 8 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13900217.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Vetilius Victor, recording his age at death.

EDCS-13900218.translation: Vettenia Optata, daughter of Lucius, lived for 40 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-13900218.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vettenia Optata, recording her age at death.

EDCS-13900219.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Vettius Catervarius lived for 90 years. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Carvilia Spica lived for 75 years. She is here.
EDCS-13900219.summary: This combined funerary epitaph records the ages of death for Quintus Vettius Catervarius and Carvilia Spica.

EDCS-13900220.translation: Marcus Vettius Felix, sacred to the spirits of the departed. Perfecta Quintula lived for 212 years.
EDCS-13900220.summary: This inscription, possibly a combined epitaph, mentions Marcus Vettius Felix and records the lifespan of Perfecta Quintula.

EDCS-13900221.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Vettius Murentius lived for 24 years. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Vettius Felix lived for 35 years.
EDCS-13900221.summary: This combined funerary epitaph records the ages of death for Caius Vettius Murentius and Caius Vettius Felix.

EDCS-13900222.translation: Quintus Vittius Primus lived for 51 years. Geminia Iulia lived for [some years].
EDCS-13900222.summary: This combined funerary epitaph records the age of Quintus Vittius Primus and begins to record the age of Geminia Iulia.

EDCS-13900224.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vethia Vitalis lived for 35 years. Daughter of Faelicus.
EDCS-13900224.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of Vethia Vitalis and identifies her father.

EDCS-13900225.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Vibius Felix, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 50 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13900225.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Vibius Felix, recording his age at death and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-13900226.translation: Quintus Vibius Martialis, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900226.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quintus Vibius Martialis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900227.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vibia [---] Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Vibius Res, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-13900227.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the 'Dis Manes' (departed spirits), likely from a tomb, and records the names of Vibia and Lucius Vibius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900228.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Vibia Vitalis, dearest daughter, Aurelius Olympus lived 1 year and 10 months; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900228.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Aurelius Olympus for his daughter Vibia Vitalis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900229.translation: Vibia Urbanilla, daughter of Germanus, lived 55 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900229.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Vibia Urbanilla, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900230.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vibia, daughter of Caius, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-13900230.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the 'Dis Manes' (departed spirits), likely from a tomb, and records the name Vibia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900231.translation: The son Victor lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900231.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a man identified only as Victor, son of [---], dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900232.translation: Victor, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900232.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Victor, son of Lucius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900233.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Victoria lived [---] years.
EDCS-13900233.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the 'Dis Manes' (departed spirits), likely from a tomb, and records the name Victoria, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900234.summary: This inscription appears to be a tombstone recording the age of Saturus and possibly Victoria, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900235.translation: Quintus Vinius Caecilius, son of Quetus, who lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900235.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quintus Vinius Caecilius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900236.translation: Lucius Vinius Optatus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 73 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900236.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Vinius Optatus, who lived 73 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13900237.translation: Marcus Vinucius Rufus lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900237.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Vinucius Rufus, who lived 75 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13900238.translation: Caius Vipsanius Reginus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 16 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900238.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Vipsanius Reginus, who lived 16 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13900239.translation: Quintus Vipsanius Urbanus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 100 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900239.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Vipsanius Urbanus, who lived 100 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13900240.translation: Quintus Vipsanius Urbanus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 7 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900240.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Vipsanius Urbanus, who lived 7 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13900241.translation: Vipsania Maxima, daughter of Marcus, lived 13 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900241.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vipsania Maxima, who lived 13 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13900242.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Viria Borocia, daughter of Virius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 60 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900242.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits for Viria Borocia, who lived 60 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13900243.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vit Bo lived [---] years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900243.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits for Vit Bo, whose age is illegible, and who is buried here.

EDCS-13900244.translation: To his daughter Vitalis, [---]nia, lived 21 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900244.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vitalis, daughter of [---]nia, who lived 21 years and is buried here.

EDCS-13900245.translation: Marcus Vitruvius Luciscus, son of Quintus, lived 80 years.
EDCS-13900245.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Vitruvius Luciscus, son of Quintus, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-13900246.translation: Vitruvia Arbuscula lived 20 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-13900246.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vitruvia Arbuscula, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900247.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Vitruvia Lucilla, daughter of Lucius, lived 17 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-13900247.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vitruvia Lucilla, daughter of Lucius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900248.translation: Ulpia Rogata lived 35 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-13900248.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ulpia Rogata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900249.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Umbrius, son of Umbrius, of the Quirina tribe, from Udsinna, lived 50 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-13900249.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Umbrius, an Umbrian from Udsinna, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900250.translation: Volussenius, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, an ...
EDCS-13900250.summary: An incomplete inscription possibly recording the name and tribal affiliation of a Roman citizen.

EDCS-13900251.translation: Quirina Urbanus lived 75 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-13900251.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quirina Urbanus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900252.translation: [...]linus lived [...] years; his father set this up for his dearest son.
EDCS-13900252.summary: A funerary epitaph with a patronymic and a dedication by a father to his son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900255.translation: [...]ius lived 110 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-13900255.summary: A funerary epitaph for an individual named [---]ius who lived to an exceptional age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900257.translation: [---]oposus lived 87 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-13900257.summary: A funerary epitaph for an individual named [---]oposus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900258.translation: [---]anus lived [...] years; [---]ica lived 85 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-13900258.summary: A funerary epitaph recording two individuals, one male and one female, with one exceptionally old, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900259.translation: She lived 75 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900259.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of a woman.

EDCS-13900260.translation: Iel, son of Birus, lived 80 years. [---] lived 15 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900260.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespans of two individuals, likely father and son.

EDCS-13900263.translation: Ndata lived 70 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-13900263.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of a woman.

EDCS-13900265.translation: Sia lived 25 years; she is buried here. Sias lived 65 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900265.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespans of two individuals, likely related.

EDCS-13900266.translation: [---]e placed [this monument]. He lived 65 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900266.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of a man who placed the monument.

EDCS-13900267.translation: [---]nus lived 61 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900267.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of a man.

EDCS-13900268.translation: He lived 61 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900268.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of a man.

EDCS-13900272.translation: Vip lived 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900272.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of a man.

EDCS-13900273.translation: [---]tor lived 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900273.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of a man.

EDCS-13900275.translation: [---]nalis lived 51 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13900275.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of a man.

EDCS-13900276.translation: Gaius lived 50 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-13900276.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of Gaius.

EDCS-13900277.translation: Laevinus lived 45 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-13900277.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of Laevinus.

EDCS-13900278.translation: Lucius, son of Lucius, lived 40 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-13900278.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of Lucius, son of Lucius.

EDCS-13900279.translation: He lived 40 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-13900279.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of an unnamed individual.

EDCS-13900280.translation: He lived 35 years, he placed this monument to himself while alive, he lived 50 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-13900280.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of an unnamed individual who erected their own monument.

EDCS-13900281.translation: He served in the military, lived 35 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-13900281.summary: This is a tombstone recording the military service and age at death of an unnamed individual.

EDCS-13900282.translation: He lived 35 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-13900282.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of an unnamed individual.

EDCS-13900284.translation: Avitus lived 27 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-13900284.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of Avitus.

EDCS-13900285.translation: Appana lived 27 years, sacred to the departed spirits, she is buried here.
EDCS-13900285.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of Appana, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-13900286.translation: Restitutus, son of Ani, of the voting tribe Quirina, lived 25 years.
EDCS-13900286.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of Restitutus, son of Ani.

EDCS-13900287.translation: Nus lived 20 years, is placed here.
EDCS-13900287.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Nus.

EDCS-13900288.translation: Son of Quintus, Quirina lived 20 years, is placed here.
EDCS-13900288.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Quirina, daughter of Quintus.

EDCS-13900289.translation: Nim lived 19 years, is placed here.
EDCS-13900289.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Nim.

EDCS-13900290.translation: Priai lived 19 years, is placed here.
EDCS-13900290.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Priai.

EDCS-13900291.translation: R lived 15 years, is placed here.
EDCS-13900291.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of R.

EDCS-13900294.translation: Felix lived 9 years, is placed here.
EDCS-13900294.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Felix.

EDCS-13900295.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Quirina nia.

EDCS-13900301.translation: Iul lived years, is placed here.
EDCS-13900301.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Iul, with an incomplete lifespan.

EDCS-13900303.translation: Faustinus was laid to rest in peace, lived 95 years, was laid to rest on the 12th day before the Kalends of May in the fifth indiction.
EDCS-13900303.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial date of Faustinus, likely from the 4th century AD due to the mention of indiction.

EDCS-13900481.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Clodius Pupienus Maximus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul for the second time, proconsul, and to the Emperor Caesar Decimus Caelus Balbinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul for the second time, proconsul, and to Marcus Antonius Gordianus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth, grandson of the deified Gordians.
EDCS-13900481.summary: This is a dedication honoring the emperors Pupienus and Balbinus, and the caesar Gordianus III, likely dating to 238 AD.

EDCS-13900483.translation: Aurelius Ianuarius lived 24 years in honor of the martyrs; he departed faithful on the 15th day before the Kalends of June. His brother Petrus made this table.
EDCS-13900483.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Ianuarius, detailing his age at death and mentioning his brother Petrus as the dedicator.

EDCS-13900596.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Julius Hispano, a veteran former decurion of the Pannonian cavalry, lived 90 years. Because of his universally recognized generosity, his daughter-in-law, Ponponia Gemellina, made this.
EDCS-13900596.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Hispano, a veteran cavalry officer, noting his age and the generosity for which he was remembered by his daughter-in-law, Ponponia Gemellina.

EDCS-13900599.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Maldatacbalis lived 75 years. These merits belong to his sons; this is the eternal home.
EDCS-13900599.summary: A funerary inscription for Maldatacbalis, who lived 75 years, with a note about his merits and his sons.

EDCS-13900600.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gerrasus, a veteran former prefect of a cohort, lived 80 years. His sons restored these merits.
EDCS-13900600.summary: A funerary inscription for Gerrasus, a veteran former cohort prefect, who lived 80 years, with his sons noted as having restored his merits.

EDCS-14000006.translation: Titus CCNVRI T TMVNI, a Lusitanian veteran, lived [XX] years.
EDCS-14000006.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription for Titus CCNVRI T TMVNI, a Lusitanian veteran, indicating his age at death.

EDCS-14000007.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aelius Baric...
EDCS-14000007.summary: A very fragmentary funerary inscription for Aelius Baric, likely honoring the deceased.

EDCS-14000008.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Aelius Rogatus, stating his age at death.

EDCS-14000009.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aemilius Impetratus lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-14000009.summary: A funerary inscription for Aemilius Impetratus, who lived 50 years and is stated to be buried here.

EDCS-14000010.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aemilius Iucundus lived 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-14000010.summary: A funerary inscription for Aemilius Iucundus, who lived 55 years and is stated to be buried here.

EDCS-14000011.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Aemilius Optatus lived 10 years; he is buried here. May you rest well here.
EDCS-14000011.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Aemilius Optatus, who lived 10 years, stating he is buried here and offering a wish for his rest.

EDCS-14000012.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aemilia Honorata lived 90 years.
EDCS-14000012.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Honorata, recording her age at death.

EDCS-14000013.translation: Mesor Antonius lived 87 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-14000013.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Mesor Antonius, recording his age at death and burial location.

EDCS-14000014.translation: Lucius Antonius Rogatus lived 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-14000014.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Antonius Rogatus, recording his age at death and burial location.

EDCS-14000015.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Antonius Satur, recording his age at death.

EDCS-14000016.translation: Aris, son of Aubsar, lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-14000016.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aris, son of Aubsar, recording his age at death and burial location.

EDCS-14000017.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Arnesius Rufus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-14000018.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Arnesia Flora lived 85 years.

EDCS-14100016.translation: Rutilia Rogata, daughter of [father's name], lived 28 years, is buried here.

EDCS-14100017.translation: Gaius Septimius Iuvenalis, the dutiful, lived 30 years, is buried here.

EDCS-14100018.translation: [---] Tertia lived 43 years, more or less.

EDCS-14100024.translation: Donatus made this cistern for Serotinus, the manager of the estate of the municipium of Puteoli, as a good creation, with bitter roots but sweet fruit.
EDCS-14100024.summary: This inscription records the construction of a cistern by Donatus for Serotinus, the estate manager at Puteoli.

EDCS-14100027.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aemilia Rogata lived 55 years.
EDCS-14100027.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilia Rogata, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-14100028.translation: He built a temple from its foundations, complete with a pronaos, columns, and marble seats, to Caelestis Aeternae Augustae, and he dedicated it; he also built the street lying below this temple, 350 feet long, with all its buildings, columns, porticoes, and four arches; he also established the market; the street is named after her.
EDCS-14100028.summary: This inscription records the extensive construction and dedication of a temple, a street, and a market by an unnamed official to the goddess Caelestis.

EDCS-14100030.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Fortunas lived 90 years.
EDCS-14100030.summary: This is a tombstone for Fortunas, who died at the age of 90.

EDCS-14100031.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintastus lived 65 years.
EDCS-14100031.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintastus, who died at the age of 65.

EDCS-14100032.translation: To the sacred spirits of the departed. Iulia Burucia lived 20 years.
EDCS-14100032.summary: A funerary dedication for Iulia Burucia, who died at age 20.

EDCS-14100033.translation: To the sacred spirits of the departed. Sittia Urbana lived...
EDCS-14100033.summary: A funerary dedication for Sittia Urbana, with her age at death not fully recorded.

EDCS-14100035.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavis the infant lived 9 years; Pequarius lived 3 years.
EDCS-14100035.summary: A funerary dedication for two children, Flavis and Pequarius, likely siblings, with their ages at death.

EDCS-14100036.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavius Donatus lived 90 years. Fortunatus erected this for his father at his own expense.
EDCS-14100036.summary: A funerary dedication by Fortunatus for his father, Flavius Donatus, who lived to be 90.

EDCS-14100037.translation: To the memory of Potronia Satura, a grateful wife to her beloved master, beautiful, good, pious, beyond repair, a maiden modest and chaste. She lived 28 years. FI EVLVS A made this.
EDCS-14100037.summary: A funerary dedication by FI EVLVS A for his wife Potronia Satura, highlighting her virtues and age at death.

EDCS-14100038.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius T S lived 81 years.
EDCS-14100038.summary: A funerary dedication for Caius T S, who lived to be 81.

EDCS-14100039.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Annia Venusta lived...
EDCS-14100039.summary: A funerary dedication for Annia Venusta, with her age at death not fully recorded.

EDCS-14100040.translation: Aurelius gladly paid his vow with a willing spirit.
EDCS-14100040.summary: A votive dedication by Aurelius, indicating the fulfillment of a promise.

EDCS-14100041.translation: Marcus Iulius Sulla, son, gladly paid his vow as was deserved.
EDCS-14100041.summary: A votive dedication by Marcus Iulius Sulla, son, indicating the fulfillment of a deserved promise.

EDCS-14100042.summary: A dedication or memorial inscription to Sabinia Tranquillina Augusta.

EDCS-14100043.translation: By the authority of Emperor Vespasian Caesar Augustus, the public lands of Cirta were assigned to the Nicibes and the Suburbicary Regians by Tullius Pomponianus Capito, legate of Augustus.
EDCS-14100043.summary: This is a rescript from Emperor Vespasian, issued through his legate, concerning the assignment of public lands in Cirta to local communities, likely dating to his reign (69-79 CE).

EDCS-14100044.translation: Antistia Luculla, daughter of Publius, lived for 17 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-14100044.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a young woman named Antistia Luculla, likely dating to the Imperial period, based on the formulaic expression and the wish for the deceased's bones to rest well.

EDCS-14100045.translation: He lived for 12 years; he is buried here. Quintus Caecilius Rusticus lived for 17 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-14100045.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the deaths of two individuals, one of whom is identified as Quintus Caecilius Rusticus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14100046.translation: Caecilia Cyrilla, daughter of Quinti, lived for 18 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-14100046.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a young woman named Caecilia Cyrilla, likely dating to the Imperial period, indicated by the formulaic phrasing and the wish for the deceased's bones to rest well.

EDCS-14100047.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caecilia Septimina lived for 20 years; may you rest well here.
EDCS-14100047.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caecilia Septimina, who died at the age of 20, likely dating to the Imperial period due to the invocation of the Di Manes and the formula for the deceased.

EDCS-14100048.translation: Marcus Cornelius Donatus lived for 12 years; father [erected this] for his dearest son.
EDCS-14100048.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by a father for his son, Marcus Cornelius Donatus, who died at the age of 12, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14100049.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Didustia Lucia, daughter of Caius, a most rare woman, lived for 40 years; she is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Lurius Vibianus lived for 25 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-14100049.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for two individuals, Didustia Lucia and Gaius Lurius Vibianus, with the latter explicitly stating a wish for the deceased's bones to rest well, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14100051.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Furfania Rogata lived for 40 years; she is well placed here.
EDCS-14100051.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Furfania Rogata, who died at the age of 40, likely dating to the Imperial period, given the invocation of the Di Manes.

EDCS-14100053.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Genila lived for about 43 years. Ianuarius made [this] for his most beloved and deserving wife.
EDCS-14100053.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Ianuarius for his wife Genila, who lived to be about 43, likely dating to the Imperial period, indicated by the dedication to the Di Manes and the marital context.

EDCS-14200046.summary: A funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits by Caius Perellius Sterceius.

EDCS-14200047.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits; Titus Petellius Gemellus lived for 30 years. HVRS...
EDCS-14200047.summary: A funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits for Titus Petellius Gemellus.

EDCS-14200048.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits; Suria Donata lived for 42 years.
EDCS-14200048.summary: A funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits for Suria Donata.

EDCS-14200053.translation: Caius Clodius Rogatus lived for 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-14200053.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan and burial place of Caius Clodius Rogatus.

EDCS-14200054.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits; Iulia, daughter of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, Extricata lived for 60 years; Caius, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, Martialis lived for 10 years.
EDCS-14200054.summary: A funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits for two individuals, Iulia Extricata and Caius Martialis, likely from the same family.

EDCS-14200055.translation: Iulia Casta, daughter of Lucius Iulius, so...
EDCS-14200055.summary: An incomplete inscription likely commemorating Iulia Casta, daughter of Lucius Iulius.

EDCS-14200056.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits; Iulia, daughter of Publius, Honorata lived for 6 years and 10 months; she is placed here.
EDCS-14200056.summary: A funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits for Iulia Honorata, a child who died at the age of 6 years and 10 months.

EDCS-14200057.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. In memory of Marcus Marius Fronto, who lived 15 years. Marcus Marius F, his brother, [erected this] in accordance with his will.
EDCS-14200057.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Marius Fronto, erected by his brother.

EDCS-14200058.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Tadius Ignatus, who lived 85 years, is buried here.
EDCS-14200058.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tadius Ignatus, recording his death and burial.

EDCS-14200059.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Tadius Maximinus.
EDCS-14200059.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tadius Maximinus.

EDCS-14200060.translation: Here lies. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Tadia Honorata, who lived 12 years, is buried here.
EDCS-14200060.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tadia Honorata, recording her death and burial.

EDCS-14200061.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Titus Vocusvonis, who lived 25 years, is buried here.
EDCS-14200061.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Vocusvonis, recording his death and burial.

EDCS-14200065.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Titus Flavius Donatus, who lived 15 years, is buried here.
EDCS-14200065.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Donatus, recording his death and burial.

EDCS-14200066.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Geminius Publi Florus, who lived 47 years.
EDCS-14200066.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Geminius Publi Florus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-14200068.translation: Publius Caelius Mustiolus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived...
EDCS-14200068.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Caelius Mustiolus, recording his name and tribal affiliation, with an incomplete lifespan.

EDCS-14200069.translation: Quintus Longanus Verus, who lived 25 years, is buried here. May your bones rest well. Quintus Longanus Moderatus, who lived 20 years, is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-14200069.summary: This is a paired funerary epitaph for Quintus Longanus Verus and Quintus Longanus Moderatus, recording their ages and burials.

EDCS-14200070.translation: To Sosiae Fructuosa, dearest daughter of Sotira, who lived 5 years, 9 months, and 7 days. Sosius Stephanus, her father, [erected this].
EDCS-14200070.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sosiae Fructuosa, erected by her father Sosius Stephanus, detailing her age at death.

EDCS-14200071.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Valeria Saturnina, dutiful, lived 78 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-14200071.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Saturnina, recording her name, piety, age, and burial location.

EDCS-14200072.translation: I, Victoria, made this in memory of my mother-in-law, Cesatoia, who lived 61 years.
EDCS-14200072.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Victoria, commemorating her mother-in-law Cesatoia and stating her age at death.

EDCS-14200073.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Palladius made this for Donata, in Christ.
EDCS-14200073.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Palladius to Donata, indicating a Christian context.

EDCS-14200074.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Calpurnius Libosus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 45 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-14200074.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Calpurnius Libosus, providing his name, father's name, tribal affiliation, age, burial location, and a wish for his afterlife.

EDCS-14200085.translation: Felix fulfilled his vow to Mercury Silvanus.
EDCS-14200085.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Felix's fulfillment of a vow to the deity Mercury Silvanus.

EDCS-14200090.translation: Lucius Lucanius Heraclida, according to his vow.
EDCS-14200090.summary: This is a votive inscription indicating that Lucius Lucanius Heraclida fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-14200092.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Caesar, son of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius.
EDCS-14200092.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Aurelius as Caesar, naming his adoptive father and the emperor.

EDCS-14300003.translation: In the name of the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit, the remains of 108 holy martyrs, Marcus, Optatus, and others, were deposited on the day before the Nones of May, in the third indiction, by the most blessed Bonifatius, bishop, with his own hands, under the reign of our lord Maurice Tiberius and Constantina Augustus, during the time of the glorious Gennadius, master of soldiers of Africa, and of the most fortunate presbyter and martyr Cresconius, for the return of the day before [date unclear].
EDCS-14300003.summary: This is a lengthy inscription detailing the deposition of relics of 108 martyrs, including Marcus, Optatus, and Cresconius, under the bishop Bonifatius during the reigns of Maurice Tiberius and Constantina, with specific dating information.

EDCS-14300004.translation: Holy Three: Hope, Faith, Charity; Saint Januarius, Saint Maxima, Saint Secunda, Saint Donatilla, Saint Geminius.
EDCS-14300004.summary: This inscription lists several Christian saints, including the theological virtues of Hope, Faith, and Charity, likely for veneration.

EDCS-14300005.summary: This is a simple inscription naming Saint Donatus, likely for veneration.

EDCS-14300006.translation: To Vibia, daughter of Justus, from Celsus, a freedwoman, to the retired bishop, in the 13th indiction.
EDCS-14300006.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a retired bishop by Vibia, daughter of Justus, likely a Christian epitaph from the 6th century.

EDCS-14300007.summary: This is a possessive inscription or dedication to Saint Regulianus.

EDCS-14300008.summary: This is a possessive inscription or dedication to Saints Celsus and Baricus.

EDCS-14300009.summary: This is a possessive inscription or dedication to Saints Tiani and Donatianus.

EDCS-14300011.translation: Felix and Maximus made this incomparable gift.
EDCS-14300011.summary: This is a dedication, likely votive or commemorative, by Felix and Maximus.

EDCS-14300012.translation: Table of Hamartolinus, Table of Serbunis.
EDCS-14300012.summary: This inscription records two tables belonging to Hamartolinus and Serbunis, possibly funerary or religious markers.

EDCS-14300014.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Aulus Saturus.

EDCS-14300015.translation: Satura lived for 55 years, she is buried here.
EDCS-14300015.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Satura, indicating her burial place.

EDCS-14300018.translation: Nardus lived for 40 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-14300018.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Nardus, indicating his burial place.

EDCS-14300020.translation: Lived for 25 years, the husband made this for his dearest wife, she is buried here.
EDCS-14300020.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, set up by a husband for his wife, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-14300021.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Ama, aged 25.
EDCS-14300021.summary: A tombstone inscription dedicating a burial to the Divine Spirits for Ama, who died at 25.

EDCS-14300024.translation: Pius lived 20 years. May your bones rest well. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-14300024.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the age of Pius (20 years) and offering wishes for his afterlife.

EDCS-14300026.translation: The testimony of Aulus Ephius, his wife, follows in the Sacred Register.
EDCS-14300026.summary: An inscription referencing a testimony left by a wife concerning her husband, Aulus Ephius, to be recorded in a sacred register.

EDCS-14300035.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Saufeius. Sacred to the Divine Spirits.
EDCS-14300035.summary: A tombstone inscription dedicating the burial to the Divine Spirits and mentioning Saufeius.

EDCS-14300038.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Buturaia lived 90 years. Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Baricio lived 85 years.
EDCS-14300038.summary: A tombstone inscription dedicating the burial to the Divine Spirits and recording the ages of Iulia Buturaia (90) and Caius Baricio (85).

EDCS-14300040.translation: To the memory of Publius Theophilus, who lived...
EDCS-14300040.summary: A tombstone inscription dedicated to the memory of Publius Theophilus, whose age is incomplete.

EDCS-14300042.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caius Aelius Aelianus lived 80 years.
EDCS-14300042.summary: A tombstone inscription dedicating the burial to the Divine Spirits and recording the age of Caius Aelius Aelianus (80).

EDCS-14300043.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Laelia Ianuaria lived 66 years. To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Iulius Camarus lived 65 years.
EDCS-14300043.summary: A tombstone inscription dedicating the burial to the Divine Spirits and recording the ages of Laelia Ianuaria (66) and Lucius Iulius Camarus (65).

EDCS-14300044.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caius M. Felix lived 11 years. Caius M. Vindex lived 17 years. May [his] bones rest.
EDCS-14300044.summary: A tombstone inscription dedicating the burial to the Divine Spirits for Caius M. Felix (died at 11) and Caius M. Vindex (died at 17), with a wish for rest.

EDCS-14300045.translation: By the authority of the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, the lands accepted from the Cirtense people have been separated from public ownership.
EDCS-14300045.summary: An official inscription, likely from the reign of Hadrian, concerning the demarcation of land belonging to the Cirtense people.

EDCS-14300048.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Rogata lived 25 years.
EDCS-14300048.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Rogata, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-14300049.translation: For the memory of Victorinus, aged 75, and Iulia Iucunda, aged 70, his children Mesucius, Honoratus, and others made [this].
EDCS-14300049.summary: A funerary monument erected by sons Mesucius and Honoratus for their parents Victorinus and Iulia Iucunda.

EDCS-14300051.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aulus Sat[---], who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-14300052.translation: For Aelius Titus Decentus, the 8th day before the Ides of October.
EDCS-14300052.summary: An inscription commemorating Aelius Titus Decentus, with a date likely marking his death or a memorial day.

EDCS-14300053.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Maximina lived 45 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-14300053.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Maximina, who died at the age of 45 and is buried at this location.

EDCS-14300055.translation: In memory of Victories, in peace.
EDCS-14300055.summary: A brief memorial inscription, possibly funerary, for someone named Victories.

EDCS-15000093.translation: By the authority of the Emperors Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Publius Septimius Geta, Augusti, lands, pastures, and springs were assigned, supervised by Epagathus and Manilius Caecilianus, cornicularius to the legate of consular rank, by order of Anicius Faustus, through Marcus Gennius Felix, evocatus of the III Augusta legion.
EDCS-15000093.summary: An official decree from the reigns of Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, concerning the assignment of lands and resources, overseen by imperial officials and military personnel.

EDCS-15000097.translation: To the Divine Pertinax, father, the I Pannoniorum cavalry alae.
EDCS-15000097.summary: A dedication by the I Pannoniorum cavalry alae to the deified Emperor Pertinax.

EDCS-15000098.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antoninus Gordianus, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, the Gordian cavalry alae is devoted to his divine spirit.
EDCS-15000098.summary: A dedication by the Gordian cavalry alae to Emperor Gordian III, expressing devotion to his divinity.

EDCS-15100041.translation: Quintus Memmius Rogatus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, as aedile, made this arch with its doors at his own expense.
EDCS-15100041.summary: An inscription recording that Quintus Memmius Rogatus, an aedile, financed and constructed a public arch with doors.

EDCS-15100055.translation: Felix placed this table for himself and for his mother Rogata, in honor of the most blessed martyrs, from the dormitory of the faithful Felicis and Maximi, where also equals are assured concerning chastity.
EDCS-15100055.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument set up by Felix for himself and his mother Rogata, dedicated to martyrs.

EDCS-15100063.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gargilia Extricata lived 10 years. Gargilius Coddeus made this tomb for his deserving daughter; may God judge whoever removes it.
EDCS-15100063.summary: This is a tombstone for Gargilia Extricata, made by her father Gargilius Coddeus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100064.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Julia Fortunata made this mausoleum for herself while alive and for her son Vibius Rogatianus. I forbid anyone dead from entering my property. Rogatianus lived 39 years. Julia Fortunata lived 79 years.
EDCS-15100064.summary: This inscription records a mausoleum built by Julia Fortunata for herself and her son Vibius Rogatianus, specifying funerary restrictions, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100118.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Aurelius Felix, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, made this for himself while alive and for his wife Petronia Satullica. He lived [X] years.
EDCS-15100118.summary: This tombstone commemorates Lucius Aurelius Felix, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, and his wife Petronia Satullica, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15200103.translation: Quintus Marius Saturus, a soldier of the legion, made the headstone and base at his own expense.
EDCS-15200103.summary: This inscription records a soldier, Quintus Marius Saturus, who paid for his own tomb marker, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15200111.translation: To Jupiter the Protector of the Most Valiant Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus and Julia Soemia Bassiana Augusta, mother of our Augustus, Marcus Aurelius Gordianus, centurion, prefect of a detachment of the III Augusta legion, devoted to their divine power.
EDCS-15200111.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Marcus Aurelius Gordianus, a centurion, honoring Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and his mother Julia Soemia Bassiana, likely from the reign of Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-15300125.translation: For the health of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus and his children, the colonists of the Massipian estate restored the buildings collapsed by age with their own funds; they also made two arches at their own expense, under the order of Provincialis, freedman of the emperor and procurator, and dedicated by the same.
EDCS-15300125.summary: This inscription records the restoration of buildings and the construction of arches by the colonists of the saltus Massipiani for the health of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-15300127.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gellius Saturninus lived 55 years.
EDCS-15300127.summary: This is a tombstone for Gellius Saturninus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.
