EDCS-20800697.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marcus Calpurnius Calpurnianus, brother of a centurion of the III Augusta Legion.

EDCS-20800698.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Carinius Fortunatus, a veteran, who lived 81 years. Calpurnius Tevestinus, a speculator, placed this for his father.
EDCS-20800698.summary: A tombstone for the veteran Lucius Carinius Fortunatus, who lived 81 years, erected by his son, a speculator.

EDCS-20800699.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Publius Calventius Germanus, son of Quintus, a doctor, who lived 70 years. Publius Calventius Italicus, a signifer of the III Augusta Legion, made this for his most devoted father.
EDCS-20800699.summary: This epitaph honors Publius Calventius Germanus, a doctor, erected by his son, a signifer of the III Augusta Legion.

EDCS-20800702.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Canidius Fortunatus, a beneficiarius of the III Augusta Legion, who lived 57 years. Geminia Manica, his wife, [erected this] for her deserving husband.
EDCS-20800702.summary: A funerary monument for Canidius Fortunatus, a beneficiarius of the III Augusta Legion, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-20800703.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Licinia Valentina lived 17 years. Caius Censorius Primus, a tesserarius of the legion, made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-20800703.summary: A funerary epitaph for Licinia Valentina, set up by her husband Caius Censorius Primus, a legionary tesserarius.

EDCS-20800704.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cervius Respectus, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, lived 40 years. Sempronius Saturninus, his heir, made this for his deserving uncle. Sacred to the departed spirits. Cervius Bassus, a beneficiarius of a tribune, lived 60 years. Saturninus made this for his father.
EDCS-20800704.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cervius Respectus and Cervius Bassus, set up by their respective heirs, Sempronius Saturninus.

EDCS-20800706.translation: Hail and farewell to the departed spirits, Claudius. May you fare well. Tiberius Claudius Firmus, a centurion of the III Augusta Legion, lived 60 years. Claudia Rufa, with her freedmen and heirs, made this for her deserving patron, for whom a mausoleum was built in Rome on his estates for 50,000 sesterces.
EDCS-20800706.summary: A funerary epitaph and will for Tiberius Claudius Firmus, a centurion, set up by his wife Claudia Rufa, including details about a mausoleum.

EDCS-20800707.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Tiberius Claudius Eutyches, freedman of Tiberius Claudius Justus, a centurion of the III Augusta Legion. He lived 29 years, 11 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-20800707.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Eutyches, a freedman of a centurion in the III Augusta Legion.

EDCS-20800708.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Tiberius Claudius Maximus, a centurion of the III Augusta Legion, who lived 50 years. His wife Claudia Maxima and daughter Claudia Maximilla made this for their deserving husband and father.
EDCS-20800708.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Maximus, a centurion in the III Augusta Legion, set up by his wife and daughter.

EDCS-20800709.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Clodia Lacaena lived 54 years. Lucius Clodius Fortunatus, an armorer of the III Augusta Legion, Lucius Clodius Saturninus, a centurion of the I Flavia Equitata Cohort, Lucius Clodius Crescens, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, and Clodia Vitalis, his dearest daughter, made this for their beloved mother. Caius Clodius Vitalis made this for his wife.
EDCS-20800709.summary: A funerary epitaph for Clodia Lacaena and Caius Clodius Vitalis, set up by their children and family, including several military members of the III Augusta Legion.

EDCS-20800710.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Clodius Honoratus, an optio of the III Augusta Legion, lived 45 years. Cornelia Rufina made this for her husband for 1,000 sesterces.
EDCS-20800710.summary: A funerary epitaph for Clodius Honoratus, an optio in the III Augusta Legion, set up by his wife Cornelia Rufina.

EDCS-20800711.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valeria Quinta lived 65 years. Comidius Vitalis, a beneficiarius of a tribune, her grandson, saw to its construction.
EDCS-20800711.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Quinta, arranged by her grandson Comidius Vitalis, a beneficiarius of a tribune.

EDCS-20800712.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Comidius Quietus, also known as Gutulus, lived 8 years. Comidius Vitalis, a beneficiarius of a tribune, his brother, saw to its construction.
EDCS-20800712.summary: A funerary epitaph for the young Comidius Quietus, set up by his brother Comidius Vitalis, a beneficiarius of a tribune.

EDCS-20800713.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Lucius Cornelius Cato, a centurion of the III Augusta Legion, also known as Caligatus, with 14 years of service. Marcus Cornelius Cato and Aurassius, his sons.
EDCS-20800713.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Cornelius Cato, a centurion of the III Augusta Legion, set up by his sons Marcus Cornelius Cato and Aurassius.

EDCS-20800714.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Athenia Rufina, who lived 17 years and 53 days. Lucius Cornelius Felix, a *beneficiarius*, to his wife, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-20800714.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Athenia Rufina, set up by her husband Lucius Cornelius Felix, a *beneficiarius*.

EDCS-20800715.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Marcus Cornelius Festus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, an architect, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-20800715.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Cornelius Festus, a soldier and architect of the Third Augustan Legion.

EDCS-20800716.translation: Benigna Maximilla, daughter of Caius, to her most exceptional husband, Caius Cornelius Florentinus, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion; and Cornelius Florentinus, son and heir, to his incomparable father; and Cornelius Calliphorus Primianus, freedmen, to their dearest patron, had this made at a cost of 26,000 sesterces.
EDCS-20800716.summary: This funerary monument was erected by family members and freedmen for Caius Cornelius Florentinus, a centurion of the Third Augustan Legion.

EDCS-20800718.translation: To Caius Cornelius Florentinus, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion, twice decorated, Piissimus OIC A, camp clerk, *evocatus* E E Raet, lived...
EDCS-20800718.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Cornelius Florentinus, a centurion and camp official of the Third Augustan Legion, with some of his military honors recorded.

EDCS-20800719.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Popilia Galla, dutiful, who lived 70 years. Quintus Cornelius Florus, *tesserarius* of the Third Augustan Legion, to his dearest mother.
EDCS-20800719.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Popilia Galla, set up by her son Quintus Cornelius Florus, a *tesserarius* of the Third Augustan Legion.

EDCS-20800720.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Quintus Cornelius Numerianus, a *beneficiarius* of a legate, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-20800720.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Cornelius Numerianus, a *beneficiarius* of a legate.

EDCS-20800721.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Caius Cornelius Pietas, of the armoury of the Third Augustan Legion, who lived 41 years. His dutiful brother made it.
EDCS-20800721.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Cornelius Pietas, associated with the armoury of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his brother.

EDCS-20800722.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Caius Cornificius Fortunatus, *mensor* of the Third Augustan Legion, for himself during his lifetime, together with his betrothed.
EDCS-20800722.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Cornificius Fortunatus, a *mensor* of the Third Augustan Legion, set up by himself for himself and his betrothed during their lifetimes.

EDCS-20800723.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Iulia Fortunata, who lived 28 years and 10 months, with her betrothed, who lived 12 years. Cornificius Fortunatus, *mensor* of the Third Augustan Legion, made this for his dearest betrothed.
EDCS-20800723.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Fortunata and her betrothed, Cornificius Fortunatus, a *mensor* of the Third Augustan Legion, who set it up for her.

EDCS-20800724.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Genialis, father. Quintus Dexius Licinianus, *optio* of the Third Augustan Legion, to his most dutiful father.
EDCS-20800724.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Genialis, set up by his son Quintus Dexius Licinianus, an *optio* of the Third Augustan Legion.

EDCS-20800725.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Licinia, mother, Quintus Dexius Licinianus, optio of the Third Augustan Legion, for his most dutiful mother.
EDCS-20800725.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Quintus Dexius Licinianus to his mother, Licinia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20800726.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Firmina, sister, Quintus Dexius Licinianus, optio of the Third Augustan Legion, for his most dutiful parent.
EDCS-20800726.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Quintus Dexius Licinianus to his sister, Firmina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20800727.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed and to the deserving Iulia Delicata, who lived 44 years. Didius Fructus, beneficiary of a laticlavian, made this.
EDCS-20800727.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Delicata, set up by Didius Fructus, a soldier of unspecified rank, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20800728.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed and to my son and lord, Aulus Didius Verulus, who lived 9 years, 7 months, and 12 days. Aulus Didius Fructus, beneficiary of a laticlavian, made this for the deserving.
EDCS-20800728.summary: A funerary monument for a young boy, Aulus Didius Verulus, dedicated by his father and lord, Aulus Didius Fructus, a soldier, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20800729.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed and to Gaius Domitius Babillianus, aged 11 years. Gaius Domitius Alexander, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion, [made this].
EDCS-20800729.summary: A funerary inscription for a young boy, Gaius Domitius Babillianus, dedicated by his father, a centurion in the Third Augustan Legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20800730.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Domitius Felix, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion, who lived 58 years, 8 months, and 13 days. Octavius Pylades, freedman, [made this].
EDCS-20800730.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Domitius Felix, a centurion of the Third Augustan Legion, set up by his freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20800731.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Domitius Pudens, son of Gaius Domitius, of the Maecia tribe, Pelagonia, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 78 years. [His] sons [set up this monument] for [their] deserving father, who is buried here.
EDCS-20800731.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Domitius Pudens, a centurion in the Third Augustan Legion, dedicated by his sons, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20800732.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Egnatius Donatus, canditatus of the Third Augustan Legion, made this for his deserving husband, who lived 45 years.
EDCS-20800732.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Egnatius Donatus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, set up by his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20800733.translation: To Ulpia Prisca, an honorable woman, [his] dearest wife, Marcus Flavius Caecilianus Telesphorianus, a frumentarius of the Fifth Macedonian Legion. [This monument].
EDCS-20800733.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Marcus Flavius Caecilianus Telesphorianus, a soldier supplying grain for the Fifth Macedonian Legion, to his wife Ulpia Prisca, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20800734.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavius Ceprucius, a veteran and former aquilifer, who lived 50 years. Valeria Rufina, his wife and heir, [made this] for her husband.
EDCS-20800734.summary: A funerary inscription for Flavius Ceprucius, a veteran aquilifer of the Third Augustan Legion, set up by his wife and heir, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20800736.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Domitianus, aide to Julia Quaeta, his mother, made this. He lived 65 years. He lies here.
EDCS-20800736.summary: This is a tombstone set up by Titus Flavius Domitianus for his mother, Julia Quaeta, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800738.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Italus, physician of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 10 years.
EDCS-20800738.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Flavius Italus, a physician in the Third Augustan Legion, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800739.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavius Maximus, of the armaments of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 35 years. Maria Donata, his wife, and his sons made this for their deserving father.
EDCS-20800739.summary: This is a tombstone erected by the wife and sons for Flavius Maximus, associated with the armaments of the Third Augustan Legion, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800740.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aemilius Felix wrote this for Titus Flavius Onesiphorus, physician of the Third Augustan Legion.
EDCS-20800740.summary: This tombstone records the death of Titus Flavius Onesiphorus, a physician in the Third Augustan Legion, with Aemilius Felix as the scribe, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800741.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Lanoscu, centurion of the legion.
EDCS-20800741.summary: This is a fragment of a tombstone for Titus Flavius Lanoscu, a centurion of a legion, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800742.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Restutus, aide to a tribune, lived 45 years. Julia Hospita [made this].
EDCS-20800742.summary: This tombstone records the death of Titus Flavius Restutus, an aide to a tribune, and was likely set up by Julia Hospita, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800745.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Gargilius Felix, optio of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 35 years. His sister made this.
EDCS-20800745.summary: This tombstone records the death of Lucius Gargilius Felix, an optio of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his sister, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800746.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gavia Monica lived 40 years. Valerius Rufinus, a tesserarius, made this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-20800746.summary: This tombstone was erected by Valerius Rufinus, a tesserarius, for his wife Gavia Monica, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800747.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Granius Sexstianus, signifer of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 30 years and [some months]. Granius RCFDSDAVS.
EDCS-20800747.summary: This tombstone records the death of Marcus Granius Sexstianus, a signifer of the Third Augustan Legion, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800748.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Herennius Victorinus, a trainee equestrian of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 27 years. He lies here.
EDCS-20800748.summary: This tombstone records the death of Herennius Victorinus, a trainee equestrian of the Third Augustan Legion, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800749.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Restuta Donata lived 80 years. Homullius Ianuarius, an immunis of the Legio III Augusta, made this for his dearest mother, who is buried here.
EDCS-20800749.summary: A funerary epitaph for Restuta Donata, dedicated by her son Homullius Ianuarius, an immunis of Legio III Augusta.

EDCS-20800750.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Hosidius Geta lived 78 years. Hosidius Felicissimus, a scribe of the Legio III Augusta, made this for his most dutiful father.
EDCS-20800750.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Hosidius Geta, dedicated by his son Hosidius Felicissimus, a scribe of Legio III Augusta.

EDCS-20800751.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Iulius Alexander, a veteran of the Legio III Augusta and formerly an optio, lived 45 years, and said: 'You were not, now you are, soon you will cease to be.' He is buried here. Quintus Ventidius Africus, a veteran and formerly an optio, made this for his most worthy father-in-law.
EDCS-20800751.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Iulius Alexander, a veteran and former optio of Legio III Augusta, dedicated by his father-in-law Quintus Ventidius Africus.

EDCS-20800752.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Gaius Julius Atticus, son of Gaius, of the Collina tribe, an optio of the Legio III Augusta, aged 45. Gaius Gemellus Apollinaris, a standard-bearer, and Lucius Terentius Saturninus, an optio, and Felix, his heirs, made this for a cost of 2,000 sesterces.
EDCS-20800752.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Atticus, an optio of Legio III Augusta, funded by his heirs Gaius Gemellus Apollinaris and Lucius Terentius Saturninus.

EDCS-20800754.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tito Iulio Mauro, also called Ruzeratus, lived 18 years. He is buried here. Iulius Bassus, a centurion of the Legio XXII Primigenia, made this for his most excellent freedman.
EDCS-20800754.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tito Iulio Mauro, dedicated by his patron Iulius Bassus, a centurion of Legio XXII Primigenia.

EDCS-20800755.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Pistrix lived 100 years. She is buried here. Iulius Bassus, a centurion of the Legio XXII Primigenia, made this for his excellent nurse.
EDCS-20800755.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Pistrix, dedicated by her patron Iulius Bassus, a centurion of Legio XXII Primigenia.

EDCS-20800756.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Iulius Quadratus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, from Cirta, lived 50 years. He is buried here. Iulius Bassus, a centurion of the Legio III Augusta, and Anucella, and Modestus, a soldier of the Cohors I Urbana, and Saturninus, a scout of the same legion, made this for their excellent brother.
EDCS-20800756.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Iulius Quadratus, dedicated by his brothers Iulius Bassus (centurion, Legio III Augusta), Modestus (soldier, Cohors I Urbana), and Saturninus (speculator, Legio III Augusta).

EDCS-20800758.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Iulius Clarus, a veteran formerly a beneficiarius laticlavii, lived 55 years. Iulia Emerita, Gaius Iulius Clarus, and Gaius Iulius Etruscus, his heirs, made this.
EDCS-20800758.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Iulius Clarus, a veteran, dedicated by his heirs Iulia Emerita, Gaius Iulius Clarus, and Gaius Iulius Etruscus.

EDCS-20800759.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Iulius Processus, a soldier of the Legio III Augusta, lived 36 years. Iulius Donatus, his brother and heir, made this.
EDCS-20800759.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Iulius Processus, a soldier of Legio III Augusta, dedicated by his brother and heir Iulius Donatus.

EDCS-20800761.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Donatus, a duplicarius of the Legio III Augusta, lived 50 years. Iulia Castula made this for her husband.
EDCS-20800761.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Donatus, a duplicarius of Legio III Augusta, dedicated by his wife Iulia Castula.

EDCS-20800762.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius Marcius Saecularis, centurion of the III Augusta legion, lived 40 years. Iulia Ingenua, his wife and most dutiful heir, made this for her incomparable husband, buried here, for 1000,000 sesterces.
EDCS-20800762.summary: A funerary monument for Julius Marcius Saecularis, funded by his wife and heir, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20800763.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Julius Emeritus, trumpeter of the III Augusta legion, lived 35 years and 8 months. His mother and his wife Rufina made this.
EDCS-20800763.summary: A funerary monument for Caius Julius Emeritus, a trumpeter in the III Augusta legion, erected by his mother and wife, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20800764.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Julius Emeritus, cornicen (horn blower) of the III Augusta legion, made this for his father, Caius Iulius Proculus, son of Caius, of the Collina tribe, from Antioch.
EDCS-20800764.summary: A funerary monument for Caius Iulius Proculus, erected by his son Caius Julius Emeritus, a horn blower in the III Augusta legion, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20900296.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lived 35 years. Iulia Secundula, his mother, and his siblings Iulius Saturninus and Gratus, made this for Caius Iulius Felix, a beneficiarius (staff officer).
EDCS-20900296.summary: A funerary monument for Caius Iulius Felix, a staff officer, erected by his mother and siblings, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20900297.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Caius Iulius Fortunatus, who served in the armorum (possibly a type of auxiliary unit or equipment bearer), lived 37 years. Sextus Pompeius Severianus, a veteran, made this for his dearest son-in-law with his own money. His bones rest here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-20900297.summary: A funerary monument for Caius Iulius Fortunatus, erected by his father-in-law, a veteran of the Roman army, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20900298.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Iulia Respecta, aged 33. Iulius Iustus, centurion of the III Augusta legion, for his most rare wife.
EDCS-20900298.summary: A funerary monument for Iulia Respecta, erected by her husband, a centurion in the III Augusta legion, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20900299.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Livianus, son of Caius, from Arethusa, a veteran aquilifer (eagle-bearer) of the III Augusta legion, transferred from the III Gallica legion, made and dedicated this in his lifetime in memory of his eternal, impending death, for himself and his family.
EDCS-20900299.summary: A funerary monument for Iulius Livianus, a veteran eagle-bearer, made by himself for his family, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20900300.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia Flaccilla lived 38 years. Iulius Longinianus, centurion of the [III Augusta] legion, for his deserving mother.
EDCS-20900300.summary: A funerary monument for Flavia Flaccilla, erected by her son, a centurion in the III Augusta legion, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20900301.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Bonitta lived 71 years. Iulius Longinianus, centurion of the [III Augusta] legion, for his mother-in-law and Processa, his daughter, for his deserving mother.
EDCS-20900301.summary: A funerary monument for Bonitta and Processa, erected by Iulius Longinianus, a centurion in the III Augusta legion, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20900302.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Caius Iulius Maritimus, son of Caius, of the Claudia tribe, formerly centurion of the VI Victrix legion, centurion of the XX Valeria Victrix legion, centurion of the II Augusta legion, centurion of the III Augusta legion, lived 45 years, 5 months, and 13 days. Salviena Metiliana, his wife, made this for her most devoted husband, who deserved well of her, at her own expense. It was managed by her freedman, Salvienus Trophimus. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-20900302.summary: A funerary monument for Caius Iulius Maritimus, a distinguished centurion who served in multiple legions, erected by his wife, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20900303.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Victorina, to her innocent daughter. Caius Iulius Martialis, arms custodian of the III Augusta legion.
EDCS-20900303.summary: A funerary dedication by Caius Iulius Martialis for his daughter Iulia Victorina, who was the arms custodian of the III Augusta legion.

EDCS-20900304.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Proculus lived 25 years. Caius Iulius Martialis, arms custodian of the III Augusta legion, to his dearest brother.
EDCS-20900304.summary: A funerary dedication by Caius Iulius Martialis for his brother Caius Iulius Proculus, who lived 25 years and was arms custodian of the III Augusta legion.

EDCS-20900305.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Victorina lived 50 years. Iulius Martialis, arms custodian of the III Augusta legion, to his most innocent mother.
EDCS-20900305.summary: A funerary dedication by Iulius Martialis, arms custodian of the III Augusta legion, for his mother Iulia Victorina, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-20900307.translation: Lucius Iulius Primus, standard-bearer of the III Augusta legion, and Lucius Furnasidius Asclepiades, arms custodian of the same legion, and his heirs made this.
EDCS-20900307.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Iulius Primus, standard-bearer of the III Augusta legion, and Lucius Furnasidius Asclepiades, arms custodian of the same legion, and his heirs.

EDCS-20900308.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Urbana lived 23 years. Fulvius Quietus, arms custodian, together with his daughter Iulia Vitalis and Iulius Quaetianus, made this for his deserving mother.
EDCS-20900308.summary: A funerary dedication by Fulvius Quietus, arms custodian, for his deserving mother, also mentioning his daughter Iulia Vitalis and Iulius Quaetianus.

EDCS-20900309.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Bruttiae Secundae, to her mother, lived 60 years. Caius Iulius Quintianus, son, centurion of the legion.
EDCS-20900309.summary: A funerary dedication by Caius Iulius Quintianus, a centurion, for his mother Bruttiae Secundae, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-20900310.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Iuliae Quantae, sister, lived 25 years. Caius Iulius Quintianus, brother, centurion of the legion.
EDCS-20900310.summary: A funerary dedication by Caius Iulius Quintianus, a centurion, for his sister Iuliae Quantae, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-20900311.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Bruttiae Rogatae, aunt, lived [XX] years. Caius Iulius Quintianus, son of his sister, centurion of the legion.
EDCS-20900311.summary: A funerary dedication by Caius Iulius Quintianus, nephew and centurion, for his aunt Bruttiae Rogatae.

EDCS-20900312.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Iuliae Almyrdi, nurse, lived 63 years. Caius Iulius Quintianus, foster-child, centurion of the legion.
EDCS-20900312.summary: A funerary dedication by Caius Iulius Quintianus, foster-child and centurion, for his nurse Iuliae Almyrdi, who lived 63 years.

EDCS-20900313.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Felix lived 6 years and 10 months. Caius Iulius Quintus made this for his most innocent son.
EDCS-20900313.summary: A funerary dedication by Caius Iulius Quintus for his son Caius Iulius Felix, who lived 6 years and 10 months.

EDCS-20900314.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Herennia Fortunata lived 33 years. Iulius Quintus of the arms corps of the III Augusta Legion made this for his most innocent wife.
EDCS-20900314.summary: A funerary epitaph for Herennia Fortunata, set up by her husband Iulius Quintus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-20900315.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Cornelius Felix, dutiful, lived 58 years and is buried here. Quintus Iulius Rogatianus, standard-bearer, and Cornelia Marciana made this for their most dutiful father. He is buried here.
EDCS-20900315.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Cornelius Felix, set up by his son Quintus Iulius Rogatianus and daughter-in-law Cornelia Marciana, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-20900317.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Caius Iulius Saturninus, centurion of the III Augusta Legion, lived 60 years. Iulia Glyconis and her heirs made this for their most dutiful father, through Iulius Chrysophes, freedman and procurator.
EDCS-20900317.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Saturninus, set up by his daughter and heirs, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-20900318.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Caius Iulius Saturninus, formerly a decurion of an ala. He lived 50 years. Iulius Longinianus, centurion of the legion, [made this] for his father.
EDCS-20900318.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Saturninus, set up by his son Iulius Longinianus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-20900320.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Caius Iulius Viator, arms custodian of the III Augusta Legion. [Made by] Iulius Messor, his brother.
EDCS-20900320.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Viator, set up by his brother Iulius Messor, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-20900321.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Caius Iulius Victor, trumpeter of the III Augusta Legion, lived 33 years. Aufidia Donata, the heir and mother, made this.
EDCS-20900321.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Victor, set up by his mother Aufidia Donata, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-20900322.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Caius Iunius Tertius of the arms corps of the III Augusta Legion lived 50 years.
EDCS-20900322.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iunius Tertius, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-20900323.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iunius Venustus, aquilifer, lived 30 years. His mother made this for her dearest son.
EDCS-20900323.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iunius Venustus, set up by his mother, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-20900324.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Kalemerus Maurus, scribe of the III Augusta Legion, lived 35 years. His mother Abulia Luc made this.
EDCS-20900324.summary: A funerary epitaph for Kalemerus Maurus, set up by his mother Abulia Luc, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-20900325.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Laelius Faustus, cornicularius of a broad-striped senator, served 23 years, lived 41 years. Antonia Silvana made this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-20900325.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Laelius Faustus, set up by his wife Antonia Silvana, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-20900327.translation: Dedicated to the underworld deities. Lucius Licinius Gratianus, standard-bearer of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 30 years. His father, Vibius Licinius Gratianus, made this.
EDCS-20900327.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Licinius Gratianus, a standard-bearer in the Third Augustan Legion, dedicated by his father, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20900328.translation: To Lucius Licinius, former aquilifer. Caius Iulius Za and Marcus Gargilius [made this].
EDCS-20900328.summary: An inscription likely honoring Lucius Licinius, a former aquilifer (eagle-bearer), with Caius Iulius Za and Marcus Gargilius as dedicants, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20900329.translation: Dedicated to the underworld deities. Lollius Victor, a librator of the Third Augustan Legion, with 11 years of service, aged 33, his mother made this for her dearest son.
EDCS-20900329.summary: A funerary dedication for Lollius Victor, a librator (surveyor or possibly a rank) in the Third Augustan Legion, made by his mother and dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20900330.translation: Dedicated to the underworld deities. To Lucius Longeius Felix, imaginifer of the Third Augustan Legion, who lived 35 years. Marcus Modius Felix, a mensor (measurer), his heir, made this.
EDCS-20900330.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Longeius Felix, an imaginifer (standard-bearer of the emperor's image) in the Third Augustan Legion, made by his heir, Marcus Modius Felix, and dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20900331.translation: Dedicated to the underworld deities. Publius Longinus Martialis, trumpeter of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 50 years.
EDCS-20900331.summary: A funerary dedication for Publius Longinus Martialis, a trumpeter in the Third Augustan Legion, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20900332.translation: Dedicated to the underworld deities. To Valeria Concessa, also called Sassa, who lived 55 years. Manilius Fortunatus, a beneficiarius praefecti (officer's adjutant), and Mallius Alexander [made this].
EDCS-20900332.summary: A funerary inscription for Valeria Concessa, made by Manilius Fortunatus, an officer's adjutant, and Mallius Alexander, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20900333.translation: Dedicated to the underworld deities. To Lucius Mantius Hispanus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, centurion of the Seventh Gemina Felix Legion, centurion of the Second Augustan Legion, IX hastatus prior, who lived 30 years. His son, Lucius Mantius Caecilianus, made this for his dearest father.
EDCS-20900333.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Mantius Hispanus, a centurion who served in both the Seventh Gemina Felix and Second Augustan Legions, made by his son, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20900334.translation: Dedicated to the underworld deities. To Iunia Caeciliana, wife of Mantius Hispanus, centurion of the Third Augustan Legion, who lived 20 years. Her son, Lucius Mantius Caescilianus, made this for his dearest mother.
EDCS-20900334.summary: A funerary inscription for Iunia Caeciliana, wife of a centurion in the Third Augustan Legion, made by her son, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20900335.translation: Dedicated to the underworld deities. To Caius Manilius Saturus, immunis (exempt from duties) of the Third Augustan Legion, who lived 30 years. Aelia Maxima, his wife, and Manilia Saturnina, his daughter, made this for their dearest.
EDCS-20900335.summary: A funerary monument for Caius Manilius Saturus, an immunis in the Third Augustan Legion, made by his wife and daughter, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20900336.translation: Dedicated to the underworld deities. To Cocceia Marcia, his dearest wife, and to Mattia Cocceiana, his sweetest daughter. Quintus Mattius Quartus Ficinus, principalis of the Third Augustan Legion, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-20900336.summary: A funerary dedication arranged by Quintus Mattius Quartus Ficinus, a principalis in the Third Augustan Legion, for his wife Cocceia Marcia and daughter Mattia Cocceiana, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20900337.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Peregrina lived for 71 years. Maturius Peregrinianus, centurion.
EDCS-20900337.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated to Peregrina, who lived to 71, erected by Maturius Peregrinianus, a centurion, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20900338.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Metilius Secundus, of the armoury of Legio III Augusta, lived for 40 years. Gaius Clodius Secundus, a soldier in the centuria of Hilario of Legio III Augusta, had this made by his deserving brother.
EDCS-20900338.summary: This tombstone records the death of Gaius Metilius Secundus, a soldier in Legio III Augusta, who lived to 40, and was commemorated by his brother Gaius Clodius Secundus.

EDCS-20900339.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Minucius Donatus, centurion of Legio III Augusta, lived for 25 years.
EDCS-20900339.summary: This tombstone records the death of Lucius Minucius Donatus, a centurion of Legio III Augusta, who lived to 25.

EDCS-20900340.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Modius Donatus lived for 22 years. Modius Crescentianus, a beneficiary of Legio III Augusta, made this for his dearest.
EDCS-20900340.summary: This tombstone records the death of Modius Donatus, who lived to 22, and was commemorated by Modius Crescentianus, a beneficiary of Legio III Augusta.

EDCS-20900341.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Modius Felix, a measurer of Legio III Augusta, lived for 60 years. Arranius Saturninus made this.
EDCS-20900341.summary: This tombstone records the death of Marcus Modius Felix, a measurer of Legio III Augusta, who lived to 60, and was commemorated by Arranius Saturninus.

EDCS-20900342.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Munatius Repentinus, from Carthage, lived for 87 years. Lucius Munatius Repentinus, optatio of the praetorians.
EDCS-20900342.summary: This tombstone records the death of Lucius Munatius Repentinus, from Carthage, who lived to 87, and was commemorated by Lucius Munatius Repentinus, an optatio.

EDCS-20900343.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Mundius Honoratus, a veteran, formerly an optio of Legio III Augusta, lived for 65 years. His son and wife.
EDCS-20900343.summary: This tombstone records the death of Gaius Mundius Honoratus, a veteran and former optio of Legio III Augusta, who lived to 65, and was commemorated by his son and wife.

EDCS-20900344.translation: Gaius Mustius, son of Gaius, Aurelia Fortunatus Lambaes, veteran, formerly a standard-bearer.
EDCS-20900344.summary: This tombstone records Gaius Mustius, son of Gaius, and Aurelia Fortunatus Lambaes, a veteran standard-bearer.

EDCS-20900345.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Iulius Pollia Castus, a cornicularius of Legio III Augusta, lived for 32 years. Iulius Proculus, his brother, made this. He is buried here.
EDCS-20900345.summary: This tombstone records the death of Marcus Iulius Pollia Castus, a cornicularius of Legio III Augusta, who lived to 32, and was commemorated by his brother Iulius Proculus.

EDCS-20900346.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. M. Claudianus, medic of Legio III Augusta, lived for 20 years and 10 months. M. Clemens made this for his dearest brother.
EDCS-20900346.summary: This tombstone records the death of M. Claudianus, a medic of Legio III Augusta, who lived to 20 years and 10 months, and was commemorated by his brother M. Clemens.

EDCS-20900347.translation: Aulus Nonius Longinus, a centurion of the Third Augustan Legion, made this for his daughter who was snatched from him by a bitter death.
EDCS-20900347.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a centurion of the Third Augustan Legion for his deceased daughter.

EDCS-20900349.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Postumius Namphamo, heir, made this for his parent, Quintus Numisius Iuvenalis, a scribe of the Third Augustan Legion, who lived for [number] years.
EDCS-20900349.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by an heir for his parent, a scribe in the Third Augustan Legion.

EDCS-20900350.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aulus Octavius Aufidianus, armor bearer of the Third Augustan Legion, lived for 30 years. Ticia Galla for her husband, and Iulius Felix and Ulpius Rus, soldiers of the Third Augustan Legion, from the cohort [made this].
EDCS-20900350.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of an armor bearer of the Third Augustan Legion, with provisions made by his wife and fellow soldiers.

EDCS-20900351.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Octavius Caecilianus, a soldier discharged from the Third Augustan Legion, lived for 27 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-20900351.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a discharged soldier of the Third Augustan Legion who lived for 27 years.

EDCS-20900352.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Antonia Felicitas made this monument for her husband, Caius Offelius Honoratus, a strator of the legion.
EDCS-20900352.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by a wife for her husband, a strator in a legion.

EDCS-20900353.translation: For the memory of Memmia Faustina. Marcus Oppius Antiochianus, a centurion of the Third Augustan Legion, made this.
EDCS-20900353.summary: This memorial inscription was erected by a centurion of the Third Augustan Legion for Memmia Faustina.

EDCS-20900354.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Senia Iulia Monnica lived for 24 years; she is buried here. Papinius Salvius, standard-bearer of the Third Augustan Legion, for his dearest wife.
EDCS-20900354.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Senia Iulia Monnica, the wife of a standard-bearer in the Third Augustan Legion.

EDCS-20900355.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Aemilius Felix, standard-bearer of the same legion, his heir, saw to it that this was made for his best friend, Pomponius Honoratus, a tesserarius of the Third Augustan Legion, who lived for 42 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-20900355.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Pomponius Honoratus, a tesserarius of the Third Augustan Legion, for whom his heir had a monument erected.

EDCS-20900356.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Pomponius Maximus, son, from a cornicularius, made this for his father, Lucius Pomponius Felix, who began his military service 20 years ago, served for 25 years, and lived for 35 years after his discharge, thus living for 80 years.
EDCS-20900356.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Lucius Pomponius Felix, a former soldier, made by his son who also served in the military.

EDCS-20900357.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Pomponius Maximus, a veteran from a cornicularius, lived for 91 years. Iulius Longinianus, a centurion of the legion, for his deserving stepfather.
EDCS-20900357.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Caius Pomponius Maximus, a veteran, and was made by his stepson, a centurion.

EDCS-20900358.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Victoria, most devoted mother, lived 25 years and is buried here. Lucius Popilius Felix, a regular soldier of the legion, made this.
EDCS-20900358.summary: This is a tombstone for Victoria, set up by her son Lucius Popilius Felix, a soldier in a legion.

EDCS-20900359.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Lucius Privatvs Victorinus, centurion of the III Augusta legion, from Traianopolis.
EDCS-20900359.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Lucius Privatvs Victorinus, a centurion in the III Augusta legion, likely from Traianopolis.

EDCS-20900360.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Ptolemeus Victor, a *cornicularius*, made this monument in his lifetime.
EDCS-20900360.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Marcus Ptolemeus Victor, a *cornicularius*, during his own lifetime.

EDCS-20900361.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publicia Monna lived 37 years. Her brother, Publicius Rufus, a *beneficiarius*, made this.
EDCS-20900361.summary: This is a tombstone for Publicia Monna, erected by her brother Publicius Rufus, a soldier.

EDCS-20900362.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Ziora made this for her incomparable wife Rufi Rufini, centurion of the III Augusta legion, and for her most beloved father, Rufinius Vitalis. He lived 55 years. Reader, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-20900362.summary: This tombstone honors Rufi Rufini, a centurion, and his father Rufinius Vitalis, set up by Iulia Ziora.

EDCS-20900363.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Decimus Rutilius Donatus, decurion of the colony of Hadrumetum, lived 75 years and six months. Lucius Rutilius Paulinus, centurion of the III Augusta legion, the first spear, made this for his most beloved father.
EDCS-20900363.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Decimus Rutilius Donatus, a decurion, by his son Lucius Rutilius Paulinus, a centurion.

EDCS-20900364.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Caius Rutilius Severus, trumpeter of the III Augusta legion.
EDCS-20900364.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Rutilius Severus, a trumpeter in the III Augusta legion.

EDCS-20900365.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sabinia Spes lived 20 years. Quintus Sabinius Ingenuus, a standard-bearer of the III Augusta legion, made this for his freedwoman who had deserved well of him.
EDCS-20900365.summary: This tombstone for Sabinia Spes was erected by Quintus Sabinius Ingenuus, a standard-bearer in the III Augusta legion, for his freedwoman.

EDCS-20900366.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sallustius Ianuarius, standard-bearer of the III Augusta legion, with 27 years of service, lived 50 years. His heirs made this.
EDCS-20900366.summary: This funerary monument for Sallustius Ianuarius, a standard-bearer in the III Augusta legion, was erected by his heirs.

EDCS-20900367.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Mevia Aemiliana, also known as Menitana, lived 24 years and 5 months. Sempronius Donatus, a *beneficiarius* of a tribune, made this for his incomparable wife.
EDCS-20900367.summary: This tombstone for Mevia Aemiliana was erected by her husband Sempronius Donatus, a soldier.

EDCS-20900368.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Servilius Rusticus, scribe of the Third August Legion, Pious, Victorious, lived for 43 years. Servilius Fortunatus to his most devoted brother.
EDCS-20900368.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Servilius Rusticus, a scribe in the legion, erected by his brother, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20900369.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Sextius Donatus, standard-bearer of the Third August Legion, lived for 60 years, is buried here.
EDCS-20900369.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Sextius Donatus, a standard-bearer in the legion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20900371.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Valeria Fortunata, who lived 80 years, most dutiful mother. Lucius Silius Rufinus, a standard-bearer of the Third August Legion, and Silius Quietus, a soldier of the same legion, made this.
EDCS-20900371.summary: A tombstone for Valeria Fortunata, erected by her son who was a standard-bearer in the legion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20900372.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sirifius Candidus, an official of the most distinguished man, lived for 35 years. Manilia Secunda made this for her most devoted husband and son.
EDCS-20900372.summary: A tombstone for Sirifius Candidus, an official, erected by his wife and for their son, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20900373.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Spani Victor, formerly an assistant.
EDCS-20900373.summary: A tombstone for Spani Victor, who held a military assistant role, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20900374.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tannonius Donatus, of the armory of the Third August Legion, lived for 35 years. Iulius Primus and Tannonia Dativa, his sister, made this; he is buried here.
EDCS-20900374.summary: A tombstone for Tannonius Donatus, associated with the legion's armory, erected by his sister and Iulius Primus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20900376.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Cnaeus Tannontius Maior, assistant, who lived for 45 years. His heir made this for 1000 sesterces.
EDCS-20900376.summary: A tombstone for Cnaeus Tannontius Maior, a military assistant, erected by his heir and funded by 1000 sesterces, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20900377.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Gaius Terentius Cerealis, veteran and formerly keeper of the armory of the legion, who endured the days of battle 8 times 10. Gaius Terentius Faustus and Gaius Terentius Saturninus, his sons, made this for their pious, good, deserving father, with the supervision of Gaius Terentius Successus, his guardian.
EDCS-20900377.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Terentius Cerealis, a veteran who served as armory keeper, erected by his sons and supervised by his guardian, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20900378.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Terentius Secundus Opitergo, tesserarius of the Third August Legion, lived for 42 years. Lucius Furnasidius AS, his heir and of the armory, made this.
EDCS-20900378.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Terentius Secundus Opitergo, a tesserarius (signaller) in the legion, erected by his heir who was also involved with the armory, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20900379.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Titinius Pius, keeper of the armory of the Third August Legion, who lived for 45 years. Aurelia Ianuaria, his wife, made this.
EDCS-20900379.summary: A tombstone for Titinius Pius, an armory keeper in the legion, erected by his wife, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20900380.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Tonneius Martialis, a veteran, lived 93 years. His son Tonneius Martialis, a clerk of the Third Augustan Legion, and his grandson Martialis made this.
EDCS-20900380.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Tonneius Martialis, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his son and grandson.

EDCS-20900381.translation: Lucius Tonneius Martialis, a wax tablet maker for the legion, made this for himself and his family while still alive.
EDCS-20900381.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Tonneius Martialis, an official responsible for wax tablets in the legion, for himself and his family.

EDCS-20900382.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Tullius Celsus, exempt from duty in the Third Augustan Legion, lived 40 years. Nonius [erected this].
EDCS-20900382.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Tullius Celsus, an exempt soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, likely erected by Nonius.

EDCS-20900383.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Tullius Felix lived 25 years, 2 months, and 17 days. Lucius Boncius Secundus, his uncle, erected this in memory of him, a trainee aquilifer of the Third Augustan Legion.
EDCS-20900383.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Tullius Felix, a trainee aquilifer of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his uncle.

EDCS-20900384.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tutius Maximus, a speculator of the Third Augustan Legion, erected this for his most dutiful father, Marcus Tutius Maximus, a veteran, who lived 75 years.
EDCS-20900384.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Tutius Maximus, a veteran and speculator of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his son.

EDCS-20900385.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Valerius Emeritus, a veteran and formerly a beneficiarius of the legion, lived 46 years. Quintus Valerius Numerianus erected this for his most dutiful paternal uncle.
EDCS-20900385.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Valerius Emeritus, a veteran and former beneficiarius, erected by his paternal uncle Quintus Valerius Numerianus.

EDCS-20900386.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Valerius Aelia Felix, son of Publius, an aquilifer, lived 50 years.
EDCS-20900386.summary: A tombstone for Publius Valerius Aelia Felix, an aquilifer, likely erected by family.

EDCS-20900387.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Bernaclus lived 2 years, 2 months, and 5 days. Valerius Marcianus, a signifer of the Third Augustan Legion, erected this for the deserving child.
EDCS-20900387.summary: A tombstone for a young child named Bernaclus, erected by Valerius Marcianus, a signifer of the Third Augustan Legion.

EDCS-20900388.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ulpia lived 61 years. Valerius Modestus, a beneficiarius of a tribune, erected this.
EDCS-20900388.summary: A tombstone for Ulpia, erected by Valerius Modestus, a soldier in a tribune's staff.

EDCS-20900389.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sextus Anicius Crescens, an optio of the same legion, erected this for his deserving friend, Marcus Valerius Pollia Secundus, son of Marcus, a standard-bearer of the Third Augustan Legion, who lived 31 years.
EDCS-20900389.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Valerius Pollia Secundus, a signifer of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his friend and fellow officer, Sextus Anicius Crescens.

EDCS-20900390.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Valerius Plancianus, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, and Valerius Plancus, keeper of the arms, made this for their deserving brother at their own expense.
EDCS-20900390.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to a brother by two soldiers of the III Augusta Legion, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20900391.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Valerianus, a centurion of the III Augusta and XXII Primigenia Legions, arranged for the construction of this tomb for his friend Lucius Valerius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Collina tribe, from Antioch, a centurion of the III Augusta and XXII Primigenia Legions.
EDCS-20900391.summary: A funerary inscription for a centurion, arranged by a friend who was also a centurion, mentioning his origins in Antioch and service in two legions, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21000544.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Oretus Optatus lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-21000544.summary: A simple funerary inscription recording the name, age, and burial place of Oretus Optatus.

EDCS-21000648.translation: Hail, Catte! For the eternal memory of Publius Aelius Cattus and Aquilia Rogata, his wife. Cattus, 104 years old, vigorous, while inspecting the stone quarries and builders, within the space of a year, made this for his most beloved wife and for Memmia Severa Valentina, his daughter, his daughter-in-law. And he dedicated it together with his sons Pollianus and Cassianus, and his grandsons, successfully.
EDCS-21000648.summary: A funerary inscription for a very old man, Cattus, who dedicated a monument to his wife and daughter, and mentions his sons and grandsons, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21000649.translation: Made by the workshop of Iulius Felix of Uneni, persevering in good faith according to the agreement, together with Iahine.
EDCS-21000649.summary: An inscription recording a commission or agreement made by a craftsman, Iulius Felix of Uneni, and another individual, Iahine.

EDCS-21000650.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Berula, obedient, without fault, a wife to her husband and tender to her children, torn away [from them], I entrust you, our pledges, to our hand; Renatus, so that the names of your mother may be spoken by the children of my eyes [i.e., children born after her death]. I preferred you dear, anxious now without you, enclosed in the tomb, I rest. A pious woman. She lived 25 years, 8 months, 17 days; she is buried here. Renatus, a tax collector of IIII public works in Africa, serving in Thamugadi, made this for his most chaste wife Berula.
EDCS-21000650.summary: A funerary inscription for Berula, who died young, set up by her husband Renatus, a tax official in Thamugadi, and includes a poetic epitaph lamenting her death and praising her character.

EDCS-21000651.translation: Turia Faustiana, blessed, lived 75 years; she rested on the Kalends of April.
EDCS-21000651.summary: A funerary inscription stating the name, age, and date of death of Turia Faustiana.

EDCS-21000652.summary: A simple funerary inscription recording the name and age of Iulia Anulia.

EDCS-21000653.translation: Iulia Vadana, daughter of Lucius, lived 70 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-21000653.summary: A funerary inscription stating the name, patronymic, age, and burial place of Iulia Vadana.

EDCS-21000654.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Sittius Felix, son of Publius Sittius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-21000654.summary: A funerary inscription recording the name, father's name, tribal affiliation, age, and burial place of Quintus Sittius Felix.

EDCS-21000655.translation: To the Divine Manes. Dassia Iuliosa lived 51 years.
EDCS-21000655.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Dassia Iuliosa, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-21000656.translation: Iulia Bidbal lived 80 years; she is placed here.
EDCS-21000656.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Bidbal, recording her lifespan and burial place.

EDCS-21000657.translation: Marcus Cassius Crescens, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 51 years; he is placed here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-21000657.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Cassius Crescens, recording his lifespan, tribal affiliation, burial place, and a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-21000658.translation: Iulius Optatus, son of Baneonis, lived 72 years.
EDCS-21000658.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Optatus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-21000659.translation: Quintus Iulius Severus lived 42 years; he is placed here.
EDCS-21000659.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Iulius Severus, recording his lifespan and burial place.

EDCS-21000660.translation: Marcus Iulius Tiro lived 19 years; he is placed here.
EDCS-21000660.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Iulius Tiro, recording his lifespan and burial place.

EDCS-21000661.translation: Iulia Viciba, daughter of Marcus, lived 30 years; she is placed here.
EDCS-21000661.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Viciba, recording her lifespan and burial place.

EDCS-21000662.translation: Caius Iulius Quadratus, son of Titus, lived 65 years; he is placed here.
EDCS-21000662.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Quadratus, recording his lifespan and burial place.

EDCS-21000663.translation: Lollia Modesta, daughter of Lucius, lived 41 years; she is placed here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-21000663.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lollia Modesta, recording her lifespan, burial place, and a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-21000664.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Iulius Maximus lived 50 years.
EDCS-21000664.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Iulius Maximus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-21000665.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Quintus Sertius Baricio, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21000666.translation: Sacred to the Comites of the god Saturnus Augustus. Publius Iulius Fortunatus and Iulia Luciosa, his wife, having been warned by a vision of the head, gave and dedicated this. Sacred to the Comites of the god Saturnus Augustus. Publius Iulius Fortunatus and Iulia Luciosa, his wife, having been warned by a vision of the head, gave and dedicated this.
EDCS-21000666.summary: This is a religious dedication to the Comites of Saturnus Augustus, made by a couple, Publius Iulius Fortunatus and Iulia Luciosa, after receiving a divine admonition, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100002.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sextus Vettius Geminus, a veteran and former standard-bearer, lived 60 years. Licinia MV and Vettius.
EDCS-21100002.summary: This is a tombstone for Sextus Vettius Geminus, a veteran and former standard-bearer, who lived to be 60, with a mention of Licinia MV and Vettius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100004.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Vitellius Atillianus, son of Titus, a centurion of the III Augusta legion, centurion of the IIII Flavia, VII Claudia, XII Fulminata, XV Apollinaris, VIII Augusta, II Augusta, VII Claudia, II Adiutrix, VI Victrix legions, served 48 years and lived 71 years. Vitellius Achilleus, his guardian and freedman, erected this for 10,000 sesterces.
EDCS-21100004.summary: This tombstone commemorates Titus Vitellius Atillianus, a centurion who served in multiple legions, and details his military career and lifespan, erected by his guardian and freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100006.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gabibia Hilara lived 45 years. Marcus Ulpius Saturninus, standard-bearer of the III Augusta legion.
EDCS-21100006.summary: This tombstone records the death of Gabibia Hilara at age 45 and mentions Marcus Ulpius Saturninus, a standard-bearer in the III Augusta legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100008.translation: Centurion of the I Adiutrix legion, centurion of the XX Valeria Victrix legion, centurion of the XI Claudia legion, centurion of the I Italica legion, centurion of the III Bracaraugustanorum cohort. Marcus Tuccius Proculus lived 62 years and made this monument for 8,200 sesterces, erected by his most dutiful sons.
EDCS-21100008.summary: This tombstone honors Marcus Tuccius Proculus, a centurion who served in several legions and a cohort, detailing his lifespan and the cost of his monument, erected by his sons, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100009.translation: Marcus Mauc, of the Voltinia tribe, 45 years old, and [serving in] the legion's IV cohort, I [legion?]. By his will, a monument was to be made for 100 denarii, and his freedman Chresimus Irenaeus arranged it in his homeland for his well-deserving patron.
EDCS-21100009.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument commissioned by Marcus Mauc, specifying its cost and that it was arranged by his freedman, Chresimus Irenaeus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100010.translation: Pollentia, centurion of the III Augusta legion, centurion of the XIIII Gemina legion.
EDCS-21100010.summary: This inscription lists two centurion posts held by someone named Pollentia, serving in the III Augusta and XIIII Gemina legions, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100011.translation: Evocatus of Augustus ordered this to be made by his will, with the freedman Cio Philodespotos overseeing it.
EDCS-21100011.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument commissioned by an evocatus Augusti (a veteran recalled to service) and arranged by his freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100012.translation: [...] from the evocati of Augustus, from the optio [...] the totality [...]
EDCS-21100012.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that mentions 'evocati Augusti' and an 'optio', suggesting a military context, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100014.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Edinius Felix, a veteran, lived 55 years. Cornificia Victoria made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-21100014.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Cornificia Victoria to her late husband, Edinius Felix, a veteran, and dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100015.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Aedinius Fortunatus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 30 years. Quintus Aedinius Felix made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-21100015.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Quintus Aedinius Fortunatus, a soldier in the III Augusta legion, and was erected by his father.

EDCS-21100016.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Emeritus, a veteran, lived 55 years. Aelius Emeritus made this for his most rare father, along with his brothers.
EDCS-21100016.summary: This funerary inscription honors Publius Aelius Emeritus, a veteran, and was commissioned by his son, Aelius Emeritus, and his brothers.

EDCS-21100017.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Hermias, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 26 years. Terentia, his mother, made this.
EDCS-21100017.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Publius Aelius Hermias, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, and was erected by his mother, Terentia.

EDCS-21100018.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Aelius Leonidius, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 35 years. Lucius Antonius Felix, a veteran, made this for his deserving son-in-law. He lies here and was his guardian.
EDCS-21100018.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Lucius Aelius Leonidius, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, who died at 35, and was erected by his father-in-law, Lucius Antonius Felix.

EDCS-21100019.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Maior, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, lived 68 years. Aelia Primitiva, freedwoman, and Terentius Summachus, his heirs, made this according to his will, for 1200 sesterces.
EDCS-21100019.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of veteran Publius Aelius Maior, who lived to 68, and was erected by his heirs as stipulated in his will.

EDCS-21100020.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Minucianus, son of Publius, from Sardis, a soldier, lived 35 years. Publius Aelius Minucianus, his brother and heir, saw to its construction.
EDCS-21100020.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Publius Aelius Minucianus, a soldier, who lived 35 years, and was erected by his brother and heir.

EDCS-21100021.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aelius Montanus, a soldier of the III legion, lived 33 years. Valeria Matronica, his wife, made this.
EDCS-21100021.summary: This funerary epitaph was created by Valeria Matronica for her deceased husband, Marcus Aelius Montanus, a soldier of the III legion.

EDCS-21100022.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Terentia Victorina lived 25 years. Marcus Aelius Proculaenus, a soldier of the legion, made this for his most rare wife.
EDCS-21100022.summary: This funerary inscription honors Terentia Victorina, who died at 25, and was erected by her husband, Marcus Aelius Proculaenus, a soldier.

EDCS-21100023.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Aelius Severus, son of Titus, from Hadrumetum, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lies here.
EDCS-21100023.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Titus Aelius Severus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion from Hadrumetum, is buried here.

EDCS-21100024.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelia Napoca lived 55 years for Publius Aelius Victor, a veteran. Publius Aelius Eutices, Aelia Successa, and Aelia Secunda had this made for their deserving patron with filial piety.
EDCS-21100024.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Aelius Victor, a veteran, set up by his family, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-21100025.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Aemilius Felix, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 19 years. Quintus Aemilius Dativus, his father, made this for his deserving son. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilia Ianuaris lived 5 years and 12 days. Quintus Aemilius Dativus, her father, made this.
EDCS-21100025.summary: This inscription records two funerary monuments for a soldier named Quintus Aemilius Felix and a child named Aemilia Ianuaris, both erected by Quintus Aemilius Dativus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100026.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Aemilius Paulus, a veteran, lived 70 years. His sons made this for their well-deserving father. He is buried here.
EDCS-21100026.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Aemilius Paulus, a veteran, erected by his sons, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100027.translation: Gaius Aemilius Victor, a veteran, [erected this] during his own lifetime.
EDCS-21100027.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Gaius Aemilius Victor, a veteran, for himself during his lifetime, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100028.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Aemilius Victor, a veteran, during his own lifetime dedicated this for himself and for his wife, Petronia Venusta, for 4,000 sesterces.
EDCS-21100028.summary: This is a self-dedicated funerary monument for the veteran Gaius Aemilius Victor and his wife Petronia Venusta, funded with 4,000 sesterces, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100029.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Agrilius Priscus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, of Italian origin. He served 21 years and lived 41 years. A fellow townsman made this for his deserving friend.
EDCS-21100029.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Agrilius Priscus, an Italian soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by a fellow townsman, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100030.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Publius Alfius Proculus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion. Bombia Verecunda, his wife, made this for her deserving husband. He lived 45 years.
EDCS-21100030.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Alfius Proculus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, erected by his wife Bombia Verecunda, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100031.translation: From the Collina tribe, Cibessos. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Aelius Alexander, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, in the century of Aemilius Silvanus, pious, lived 42 years. Publius Aelius Occavianus, a surveyor of the same legion, made this.
EDCS-21100031.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Aelius Alexander, a soldier from Cibessos serving in the III Augusta legion, erected by Publius Aelius Occavianus, a legionary surveyor, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-21100032.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Annius Reginus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 35 years. Gaius Iulius Rullius, a regionarius, made this gift for his son.
EDCS-21100032.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annius Reginus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by Gaius Iulius Rullius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100033.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Annius Saturninus, a veteran, lived 60 years. His wife, Saturnina, made this.
EDCS-21100033.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annius Saturninus, a veteran, erected by his wife Saturnina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100034.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Antonius Felix, veteran, lived 70 years. He made this for himself while alive. He is buried here.
EDCS-21100034.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Antonius Felix, a veteran who made his own tomb.

EDCS-21100035.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Antonius Felix, veteran, made this for his deserving father, Lucio Antonio Ibero, who lived 95 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-21100035.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Lucius Antonius Felix for his father, Lucio Antonio Ibero.

EDCS-21100036.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Antonius Felix, soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 27 years and 6 months. Octavia Prima [made this] for her dearest brother.
EDCS-21100036.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Felix, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his sister Octavia Prima.

EDCS-21100037.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Antonius Fortunatus, veteran, lived 70 years. Marcus Antonius Fortunatus [made this].
EDCS-21100037.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Fortunatus, a veteran, likely made by himself or a relative of the same name.

EDCS-21100038.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Antonius Proculus, veteran, and the protection of friends, lived 82 years. His sons made this for their most worthy father.
EDCS-21100038.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Proculus, a veteran, erected by his sons.

EDCS-21100039.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Decimia Honorata, of Lambaesis by birth, lived 61 years. Her husband, Marcus Antonius Proculus, a veteran, made this for his sweetest wife, along with his sons.
EDCS-21100039.summary: A funerary epitaph for Decimia Honorata, erected by her husband Marcus Antonius Proculus, a veteran, and their sons.

EDCS-21100040.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Quintus Apollonius Demetrianus, soldier of the III Augusta legion. His heirs made this.
EDCS-21100040.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Apollonius Demetrianus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-21100041.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Aponius Rogatus, soldier, lived 25 years. Aponia Monna made this with her own money.
EDCS-21100041.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Aponius Rogatus, a soldier, erected by Aponia Monna.

EDCS-21100042.translation: Quintus Aranius Rufinus, soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 50 years. Aranius Secundus made this for his deserving brother.
EDCS-21100042.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Aranius Rufinus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his brother Aranius Secundus.

EDCS-21100043.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aristius Severus, veteran, lived 60 years. His wife made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-21100043.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aristius Severus, a veteran, erected by his wife.

EDCS-21100045.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Attanius, a veteran of the third legion, made this for his most devoted wife, Caecilia Sa, who lived 30 years. From 24 denarii.
EDCS-21100045.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a veteran for his wife, dated by her lifespan and mention of a sum of money.

EDCS-21100046.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Attius Festus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, a soldier in the Third Augustan Legion, lived 20 years.
EDCS-21100046.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, noting his tribal affiliation and lifespan.

EDCS-21100047.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Attius Germanus, a soldier in the Third Augustan Legion, lived 43 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-21100047.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, stating his lifespan and that he is interred here.

EDCS-21100048.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aufidius Collatus, a soldier in the Third Augustan Legion, lived 30 years. His brother, Lucius Aufidius Utilis, [erected this].
EDCS-21100048.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, placed by his brother.

EDCS-21100049.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aufidius Rogatus, a soldier in the Third Augustan Legion, lived 60 years.
EDCS-21100049.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, stating his lifespan.

EDCS-21100050.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aufidius Verus, an equestrian in the Third Augustan Legion, lived 30 years. His mother made this for her deserving son.
EDCS-21100050.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a mother for her son, an equestrian in the Third Augustan Legion.

EDCS-21100051.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Avidia Rufina lived 1 year, 1 day, and 25 months (i.e., 2 years and 1 month). Caius Avidius Rufinus, a veteran, [erected this] for his most sweet daughter, from his wife Flavia Secunda.
EDCS-21100051.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a veteran father and his wife for their infant daughter.

EDCS-21100053.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Aurelius Celsus, an equestrian from the singulares of the province of Upper Moesia, who was killed. Aurelia Estea, Prisca, Longina, and Claudia Faustina erected this for their deserving brother. He lived 36 years.
EDCS-21100053.summary: This is a funerary dedication by sisters for their brother, an equestrian killed in service in Upper Moesia.

EDCS-21100054.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Gallinia lived 65 years. Marcus Aurelius Hospitalis, a veteran, made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-21100054.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a veteran husband for his wife.

EDCS-21100055.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aurelius Hospitalis, a veteran from the Third Augustan Legion, made this for himself while alive, at his own expense, for his deserving self. He is buried here.
EDCS-21100055.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-21100056.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius Ianuarius, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, lived 40 years. Gabinia Fortunata dedicated this to her husband.
EDCS-21100056.summary: A funerary dedication for Aurelius Ianuarius, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, set up by his wife Gabinia Fortunata.

EDCS-21100057.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Turrania Rogata made this tomb for Gaius Aurelius Ianuarius, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-21100057.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Aurelius Ianuarius, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, made by Turrania Rogata.

EDCS-21100058.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius Marcianus, a veteran of the III Augusta Legion, lived 72 years and served for [some duration]. According to the terms of the will, his son and heirs had this made for their most dear father, at a cost of 10,600 sesterces. Caius Tius Victor, their uncle and guardian, saw to its construction.
EDCS-21100058.summary: A funerary monument for Aurelius Marcianus, a veteran of the III Augusta Legion, funded by his son and heirs according to his will and overseen by his uncle and guardian.

EDCS-21100059.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Aurelius Mercator, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, lived 35 years. Lucius Aemilius Saturninus, his heir, made this for the deserving man.
EDCS-21100059.summary: A funerary dedication for Quintus Aurelius Mercator, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, made by his heir Lucius Aemilius Saturninus.

EDCS-21100060.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius Saturninus, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, lived 26 years. His wife made this.
EDCS-21100060.summary: A funerary monument for Aurelius Saturninus, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, made by his wife.

EDCS-21100061.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aurelius Secundinus, a veteran, lived 65 years. Brutia Rogata, his mother, made this.
EDCS-21100061.summary: A funerary dedication for Marcus Aurelius Secundinus, a veteran, made by his mother Brutia Rogata.

EDCS-21100062.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Aurelius Victor, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, lived 50 years. Victorina made this.
EDCS-21100062.summary: A funerary monument for Quintus Aurelius Victor, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, made by Victorina.

EDCS-21100063.translation: F. Aurelius, discharged from the legion with honor after [some number] years.
EDCS-21100063.summary: A record of honorable discharge for F. Aurelius from a legion.

EDCS-21100064.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Blosius Maximus, a veteran, lived 52 years. His sons made this for the deserving man.
EDCS-21100064.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Blosius Maximus, a veteran, made by his sons.

EDCS-21100065.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Caecilius Aurelianus, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, from Hadrumetum, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion with 22 years of service, lived 40 years. His heir made this.
EDCS-21100065.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Caecilius Aurelianus, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion from Hadrumetum, made by his heir.

EDCS-21100066.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Caecilius Felix, a veteran, lived 65 years. Aemilia Ianuaria, his wife, made this.
EDCS-21100066.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Caecilius Felix, a veteran, set up by his wife Aemilia Ianuaria.
