EDCS-21100067.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Caecilius Maximus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 50 years. Caninia Silvana made this for his deserving service.
EDCS-21100067.summary: This funerary epitaph records Gaius Caecilius Maximus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, and was set up by Caninia Silvana.

EDCS-21100068.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aviania Castula made this for herself while alive. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caecilius Restutus, a soldier of the IIII Augusta legion, lived 25 years. Aviania Castula made this for her dearest betrothed.
EDCS-21100068.summary: This funerary inscription honors both Aviania Castula, who made it for herself, and her betrothed, Caecilius Restutus, a soldier of the IIII Augusta legion, by Aviania Castula.

EDCS-21100070.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Lucius Caecilius Secundus, son of Lucius, of the Horatia tribe, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, who lived 24 years, his brother Caius Caecilius Concessus, a soldier of the same legion, made this.
EDCS-21100070.summary: This funerary epitaph records Lucius Caecilius Secundus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, and was set up by his brother Caius Caecilius Concessus.

EDCS-21100071.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Caecilius Successus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, made this for his most devoted mother, Nonia Tertia.
EDCS-21100071.summary: This funerary epitaph records Gaius Caecilius Successus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, and was set up by his mother Nonia Tertia.

EDCS-21100072.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Coelius Crescens made this for his deserving son, Quintus Coelius Vitalis, a soldier.
EDCS-21100072.summary: This funerary epitaph records Quintus Coelius Vitalis, a soldier, and was set up by his father Quintus Coelius Crescens.

EDCS-21100073.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caelio Macedon, a veteran, lived 60 years. Cassia Concessa made this.
EDCS-21100073.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caelio Macedon, a veteran, erected by Cassia Concessa.

EDCS-21100074.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Calpurnius Felix, a soldier of the III Augusta legion.
EDCS-21100074.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Calpurnius Felix, a soldier of the III Augusta legion.

EDCS-21100075.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publius Calvidius Fuscus, a veteran, lived 71 years. His sons made this for their deserving father.
EDCS-21100075.summary: This funerary epitaph records Publius Calvidius Fuscus, a veteran, and was erected by his sons.

EDCS-21100076.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Calvisius Victor, a veteran, lived 61 years.
EDCS-21100076.summary: This funerary epitaph records Gaius Calvisius Victor, a veteran.

EDCS-21100077.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Calvisia Procula lived for 66 years. Caius Carminius Messor, a veteran, made this for his most exceptional wife.
EDCS-21100077.summary: A funerary monument recording the lifespan of Calvisia Procula and made by her husband, Caius Carminius Messor, a veteran.

EDCS-21100078.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Carminius Valens, a soldier of the VII Gemina Felix legion, in the centuria of Laecanus, lived for 30 years, served for 9 years, is buried here, according to the provisions of his will made for his heir.
EDCS-21100078.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Carminius Valens, a soldier of the VII Gemina Felix legion, detailing his age, service length, and burial according to his will.

EDCS-21100079.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Cassius Donatus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived for 40 years and 21 months. His brother Cassius made this for his well-deserving brother.
EDCS-21100079.summary: A funerary monument for Marcus Cassius Donatus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his brother.

EDCS-21100080.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aufidia Secunda lived for 60 years. Lucius Cassius Rufus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-21100080.summary: A funerary inscription for Aufidia Secunda, who lived to 60, dedicated by her husband, Lucius Cassius Rufus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion.

EDCS-21100081.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vibia Himerilla made this for her most devoted husband, Caius Cassius Secundus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, who lived for 50 years.
EDCS-21100081.summary: A funerary monument for Caius Cassius Secundus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his devoted wife, Vibia Himerilla.

EDCS-21100082.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Cerennius Severus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, lived for 65 years. When he had arranged for 2000 sesterces to be spent on his funeral and monument, his son Publius Cerennius Primitivos and his freedwoman Cerennia Hilara, adding 500 sesterces from their own funds, erected this for the well-deserving. He is buried here.
EDCS-21100082.summary: A funerary monument for Publius Cerennius Severus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, detailing his burial provisions and the additional contributions by his son and freedwoman.

EDCS-21100083.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Cerficius Crescens, an equestrian soldier of the III Augusta legion. Cerficius Honoratus made this for his brother.
EDCS-21100083.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Cerficius Crescens, an equestrian soldier of the III Augusta legion, dedicated by his brother.

EDCS-21100084.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Harnius Maccus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived for 40 years. Mababme made this tomb.
EDCS-21100084.summary: A funerary monument for Caius Harnius Maccus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by Mababme.

EDCS-21100085.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Claudius Gallicus, an equestrian of the III Augusta legion, served for 24 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-21100085.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Claudius Gallicus, an equestrian of the III Augusta legion, indicating his service length and burial place.

EDCS-21100086.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudius Gallicus, an equestrian of the legion, made this for his most devoted sister, Claudia Cardela.
EDCS-21100086.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Claudius Gallicus, an equestrian soldier, to his sister Claudia Cardela.

EDCS-21100087.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Clodius Gedus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, lived 21 years. Mania Monica for her deserving husband.
EDCS-21100087.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Clodius Gedus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, who died at the age of 21, set up by his wife Mania Monica.

EDCS-21100088.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Clodius Victor, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, lived 47 years. Clodius Tertullus made this for his dearest brother.
EDCS-21100088.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Clodius Victor, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, at the age of 47, erected by his brother Clodius Tertullus.

EDCS-21100089.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Comidius Iustus, a veteran, lived 53 years. Mator for her deserving husband.
EDCS-21100089.summary: This tombstone inscription commemorates Comidius Iustus, a veteran who lived to 53, set up by his wife Mator.

EDCS-21100090.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Cominius Maximus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, lived 40 years. His wife made this with a willing heart.
EDCS-21100090.summary: This tombstone records the death of Cominius Maximus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, at the age of 40, erected by his wife.

EDCS-21100091.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Caius Cominius Sodalis, an equestrian soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, lived 46 years.
EDCS-21100091.summary: This tombstone inscription is for Caius Cominius Sodalis, an equestrian soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, who lived to 46.

EDCS-21100093.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Caius Cornelius Liberalis, a veteran, lived 70 years. His son Liberalis made this for his father.
EDCS-21100093.summary: This tombstone inscription commemorates Caius Cornelius Liberalis, a veteran who lived to 70, erected by his son, also named Liberalis.

EDCS-21100094.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Publius Cornelius Victorinus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, lived 26 years. His heirs, Cornelia Victoria and Florentius, buried him here.
EDCS-21100094.summary: This tombstone records the death of Publius Cornelius Victorinus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, at the age of 26, by his heirs Cornelia Victoria and Florentius.

EDCS-21100095.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Cornelius Sulla, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, lived 50 years. His brother Lucius Cornelius Ant buried him here.
EDCS-21100095.summary: This tombstone inscription is for Lucius Cornelius Sulla, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, who died at 50, erected by his brother Lucius Cornelius Ant.

EDCS-21100096.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Cornificius Felix, a veteran, lived 50 years. He made this for himself.
EDCS-21100096.summary: This tombstone inscription records that the veteran Cornificius Felix, who lived to 50, made the monument for himself.

EDCS-21100097.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Crepereius Fortunatus, an equestrian soldier, lived here. Iulia Crispina for her excellent husband.
EDCS-21100097.summary: This tombstone inscription is for Quintus Crepereius Fortunatus, an equestrian soldier, erected by his wife Iulia Crispina.

EDCS-21100098.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Domitius Antoninus, soldier of the III Augusta legion. [He made this] willingly and gladly. [Erected by] Maximo.
EDCS-21100098.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Domitius Antoninus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, likely erected by Maximo. The date is inferable to the 2nd or 3rd century CE, a period when this legion was active in North Africa.

EDCS-21100099.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Domitius Felix, soldier of the legion, lived 45 years. Aemilius Marsus made [this] for his friend.
EDCS-21100099.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Domitius Felix, a soldier who lived to 45, erected by his friend Aemilius Marsus. The military context suggests a date within the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100100.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Julia Tumele, mother, lived 80 years. Domitius Iulianus, soldier of the III Augusta legion, and Aemilia Spenika, her daughter-in-law, and Rogatianus, her sons, made [this] for their dearest mother.
EDCS-21100100.summary: A tombstone for Julia Tumele, who lived to 80, erected by her son Domitius Iulianus (a soldier of the III Augusta legion), her daughter-in-law, and another son. The inscription suggests a family commemorated their mother, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100101.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Lucius Domitius Optatus, soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 35 years. Claudia [made this].
EDCS-21100101.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Domitius Optatus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion who lived to 35, likely erected by Claudia. The military affiliation points to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100102.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Lucius Domitius Rufus, soldier of the III Augusta legion, Lucanian century, lived 35 years. Valerius Faustus made [this] for [him] who deserved well of him. D(is) M(anibus) E(ius).
EDCS-21100102.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Domitius Rufus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by Valerius Faustus. The military detail and dedication indicate a Roman Imperial period date.

EDCS-21100103.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Decimus Domitius Sardonikus, [lived] 70 years. Pollia Alexandria made [this]. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Quintus Domitius Sardonikus, soldier of the VII cohort of Lusitanians, lived 18 years. Domitiae [made this] for their most beloved brother.
EDCS-21100103.summary: This complex inscription records two deaths: Decimus Domitius Sardonikus (70 years old), for whom Pollia Alexandria made a dedication, and his brother Quintus Domitius Sardonikus (18 years old), a soldier, for whom 'Domitiae' made a dedication. The mention of a cohort suggests a Roman military context and likely an Imperial date.

EDCS-21100104.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Domitius Valens served in the III Augusta legion, lived 70 years.
EDCS-21100104.summary: A tombstone for Domitius Valens, a veteran of the III Augusta legion who lived to 70. The inscription dates to the Roman Imperial period due to the military context.

EDCS-21100105.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Annius Felix, soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 50 years. Annius Donatus made [this] for his deserving brother. He lies here.
EDCS-21100105.summary: A tombstone for Annius Felix, a soldier of the III Augusta legion who lived to 50, erected by his brother Annius Donatus. This inscription dates to the Roman Imperial period, given the military affiliation.

EDCS-21100106.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Gaius Ficilius Silvanus, veteran, lived 66 years. Iulia Dextriana [made this] for her husband.
EDCS-21100106.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Ficilius Silvanus, a veteran who lived to 66, erected by his wife Iulia Dextriana. The term 'veteranus' suggests a Roman Imperial period date.

EDCS-21100107.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Titus Flavius Crescentius the Younger, lived 12 years and 6 months. Titus Flavius Crescens, veteran, placed [this] for his deserving son.
EDCS-21100107.summary: A tombstone for Titus Flavius Crescentius the Younger, who died at 12 years and 6 months, erected by his father Titus Flavius Crescens, a veteran. This memorial dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100108.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Titus Flavius Felix, son of Titus, of the Papiria tribe, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, served for 7 years, lived for 28 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-21100108.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Felix, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, who served for 7 years and lived for 28 years.

EDCS-21100109.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Titus Flavius Felix, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, lived for 40 years.
EDCS-21100109.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Felix, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-21100110.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Valeria Manilia, dear and most sweet wife, a wife deserving of incomparable affection, who lived for 38 years. Her husband, Flavius Impetratus, a veteran, made this for 1000 sesterces; and if you who pass by read this, I ask you to say: 'Sweet Manilia, may the earth be light upon you.'
EDCS-21100110.summary: A funerary monument erected by Flavius Impetratus, a veteran, for his wife Valeria Manilia, specifying a sum of 1000 sesterces and including a wish for a light earth upon her.

EDCS-21100111.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Antonia Iucunda lived for 38 years. Flavius Ingenuus, a veteran, made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-21100111.summary: A funerary inscription by Flavius Ingenuus, a veteran, dedicated to his wife Antonia Iucunda, who lived for 38 years.

EDCS-21100112.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Titus Flavius Saturninus, a loyal cavalryman of the legion, lived for 38 years.
EDCS-21100112.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Saturninus, a cavalryman of a legion, who lived for 38 years.

EDCS-21100113.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus Flavius Seleucus, a veteran, lived for 50 years. Aurelia Monnica made this for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-21100113.summary: A funerary monument erected by Aurelia Monnica for her husband Quintus Flavius Seleucus, a veteran who lived for 50 years.

EDCS-21100115.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Furius Felix, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, of the centuria of Flavius Tartianus, lived for 37 years.
EDCS-21100115.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Furius Felix, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, serving in the centuria of Flavius Tartianus, who lived for 37 years.

EDCS-21100116.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Gargilius Rogatianus, a soldier of the Third Legion, lived for 45 years.
EDCS-21100116.summary: A funerary inscription for Gargilius Rogatianus, a soldier of the Third Legion, who lived for 45 years.

EDCS-21100117.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sextus Gavius Felix, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta.
EDCS-21100117.summary: A funerary inscription for Sextus Gavius Felix, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta.

EDCS-21100118.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Made by Gavia Verina for her wife of the Third Legion Augusta.
EDCS-21100118.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Gavia Verina for her wife, who was associated with the Third Legion Augusta.

EDCS-21100119.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Geminius Saturninus, a veteran, lived 80 years. Geminii Faustus Felix and Saturninus made this for their father.
EDCS-21100119.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Geminius Saturninus, a veteran, erected by his sons.

EDCS-21100120.translation: For the memory of Publius Granius Auctus, a veteran of the Caesennian legion. He lived 80 years. Publius Granius Primus, son of Publius, a soldier, made this at his own expense.
EDCS-21100120.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Granius Auctus, a veteran, paid for by himself and erected by his son.

EDCS-21100121.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Helvius Claudianus, a veteran, lived 60 years. Ulpia Iulia, his wife, made this.
EDCS-21100121.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Helvius Claudianus, a veteran, erected by his wife.

EDCS-21100122.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Helvius Rufinus served in the army for 24 years.
EDCS-21100122.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Helvius Rufinus, a soldier, recording his years of service.

EDCS-21100123.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Helvius Saturus, a veteran, lived 75 years. Helvia Satura, his dutiful daughter, made this.
EDCS-21100123.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Helvius Saturus, a veteran, erected by his daughter.

EDCS-21100124.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Hervinius Urbanus, a veteran, is buried here. He lived 53 years. Mulvia Prima made this for her well-deserving husband. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-21100124.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Hervinius Urbanus, a veteran, erected by his wife.

EDCS-21100125.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Hortesius Sabinus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 35 years. His heir made this.
EDCS-21100125.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Hortesius Sabinus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his heir.

EDCS-21100126.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Hostilius Felix, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 27 years. Arruntius Felix, his heir, made this.
EDCS-21100126.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Hostilius Felix, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his heir.

EDCS-21100127.translation: A soldier of the III Augusta legion, sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Iulius Alexander lived 43 years. His wife, Paternia Epipania, made this.
EDCS-21100127.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Alexander, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his wife.

EDCS-21100128.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Gaius Iulius Apollinaris, a veteran of the III Augusta legion. Iulia Ingenua, his most devoted daughter, made this for her father. He lived 60 years.
EDCS-21100128.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Apollinaris, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, erected by his daughter.

EDCS-21100129.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Aquilo, veteran, lived 60 years, is buried here. Gaius Julius Rufinus, his son, made this for his most deserving father.
EDCS-21100129.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Aquilo, a veteran, erected by his son.

EDCS-21100130.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Arator lived 75 years. His sons made this for their deserving father.
EDCS-21100130.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Arator, erected by his sons.

EDCS-21100131.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Arcelaus, soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived [---] years.
EDCS-21100131.summary: A funerary epitaph for Arcelaus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-21100132.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Gaius Julius Aufidianus, veteran, lived 52 years. Clodia Victoria, also called Pupa, his wife and heir, made this for him deserving.
EDCS-21100132.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Aufidianus, a veteran, erected by his wife and heir.

EDCS-21100133.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Caecilius, soldier of the III Augusta legion, with 27 years of service. Iulia Sabina made this for her husband.
EDCS-21100133.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Caecilius, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his wife.

EDCS-21100134.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius Demetrianus, soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 25 years. His brothers made this for him deserving.
EDCS-21100134.summary: A funerary epitaph for Julius Demetrianus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his brothers.

EDCS-21100136.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Gaius Julius Domitius, veteran, lived 75 years. Gaius Julius Domitius, soldier of the III Augusta legion, his son, and Helvia Data, his wife, made this for him deserving.
EDCS-21100136.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Domitius, a veteran, erected by his son and wife.

EDCS-21100137.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Donatus, soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 46 years, served 23 years. Numisia Ianuaria, his appointed heir, made this for him deserving.
EDCS-21100137.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Donatus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his heir.

EDCS-21100138.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Donatus lived 50 years. Pedania Vitalica, his mother, made this.
EDCS-21100138.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Donatus, erected by his mother.

EDCS-21100139.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius Donatus served 26 years and some months. Hedera made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-21100139.summary: A funerary epitaph for Julius Donatus, recording his military service and erected by his wife.

EDCS-21100140.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Emeritus, a cavalryman of the Third Augustan Legion, lived for 32 years. Gaius Caelus made this for his deserving brother.
EDCS-21100140.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Emeritus, a cavalryman of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his brother.

EDCS-21100141.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Julius Faustinus, a soldier of the legion, lived for [unknown years]. Iulia Donata made this for her deserving wife.
EDCS-21100141.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Julius Faustinus, a soldier, erected by his wife Iulia Donata.

EDCS-21100142.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Felix, a veteran, lived for 69 years. Gaius Julius Felix made this for his dearest father.
EDCS-21100142.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Felix, a veteran, erected by his son of the same name.

EDCS-21100143.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Annius Florus lived for 50 years. Iulius Felix, a cavalryman of the Third Augustan Legion, made this for his deserving mother-in-law.
EDCS-21100143.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Annius Florus, erected by his mother-in-law Iulius Felix, a cavalryman of the Third Augustan Legion.

EDCS-21100144.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Felix, a veteran, lived for 90 years. Vibia Celsula made this for her husband.
EDCS-21100144.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Felix, a veteran, erected by his wife Vibia Celsula.

EDCS-21100145.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Felix lived for 3 years. Gaius Julius Felix, a soldier of the legion, made this for his son from his peculium.
EDCS-21100145.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Felix, a child soldier, erected by his father of the same name.

EDCS-21100146.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Felix, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, lived for 34 years. His brother Gaius Julius Vitalis placed this here.
EDCS-21100146.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Felix, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his brother Gaius Julius Vitalis.

EDCS-21100147.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Fortis, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, lived for 28 years and served for 5 years. His heir Honorata placed this.
EDCS-21100147.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Fortis, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his heir Honorata.

EDCS-21100148.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Fortunatus, a veteran, lived for 46 years.
EDCS-21100148.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Fortunatus, a veteran.

EDCS-21100149.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Fortunatus, a dutiful soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, lived for 30 years. His tent-mate Iulius Nepos made this for his deserving comrade.
EDCS-21100149.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Fortunatus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his contubernalis (tent-mate) Iulius Nepos.

EDCS-21100150.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Iulius Fortunatus, son of Quintus, of the Pollia tribe, a soldier in the camp of the Third Legion Augusta, lived 28 years. Iulius Rufus, Antistianus, Rufinus, and Laudicia made this for their most devoted brother.
EDCS-21100150.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Iulius Fortunatus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, set up by his siblings, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-21100151.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Iulius Gargilianus, a veteran, lived 58 years. His heirs made this.
EDCS-21100151.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Iulius Gargilianus, a veteran, whose heirs erected the monument, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-21100152.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Iulius Ianuarius, a veteran of the Third Legion Augusta, lived 60 years. Vibia Victoria made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-21100152.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Iulius Ianuarius, a veteran of the Third Legion Augusta, erected by his wife, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-21100154.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Martialis, a soldier, lived 25 years. Iulius Donatus, a veteran, [made this] for his dearest son.
EDCS-21100154.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Martialis, a soldier, erected by his father, a veteran, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-21100155.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Maximus, an equestrian of the Third Legion Augusta, lived 33 years.
EDCS-21100155.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Maximus, an equestrian of the Third Legion Augusta, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-21100157.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Primitius, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, with 22 years of service, lived 42 years. Iulia Tice made this for her deserving wife.
EDCS-21100157.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Primitius, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, erected by his wife, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-21100158.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Iulius Collina Proculus Adan, a veteran of the Third Legion Augusta, lived 62 years. Iulius Proculus, his son, made this; he is buried here.
EDCS-21100158.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Iulius Collina Proculus Adan, a veteran of the Third Legion Augusta, erected by his son, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-21100159.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Rufina Urbica, farewell. [She] lived 50 years. [He lived] 30 years with me. Iulius Sabinus, a veteran, made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-21100159.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Rufina Urbica, erected by her husband Iulius Sabinus, a veteran, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-21100160.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Saturninus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, lived 45 years. His mother, Iulia Rogata, made this with her own money.
EDCS-21100160.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Saturninus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, funded by his mother, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-21100161.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Saturninus, a veteran, lived 80 years. His deserving wife made this for him.
EDCS-21100161.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Saturninus, a veteran, erected by his wife, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-21100162.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Saturninianus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived for 40 years. Gaius Postumius Martialis, an equestrian and his heir, made this.
EDCS-21100162.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Julius Saturninianus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his heir, Gaius Postumius Martialis.

EDCS-21100164.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Severus, an equestrian of the III Augusta legion, lived for 40 years. Flavia Fortunata, with her children and heirs, made this for her most deserving husband.
EDCS-21100164.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Julius Severus, an equestrian of the III Augusta legion, dedicated by his wife Flavia Fortunata and their children.

EDCS-21100166.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Syriacus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion. Iulia Frontilla, his most devoted wife, made this.
EDCS-21100166.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Julius Syriacus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, erected by his wife Iulia Frontilla.

EDCS-21100167.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Vestalis, a veteran, lived for 53 years.
EDCS-21100167.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Julius Vestalis, a veteran, likely erected by family.

EDCS-21100168.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maria Victoriola lived for 50 years. Gaius Julius Victor, a veteran, made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-21100168.summary: A funerary monument for Maria Victoriola, erected by her husband Gaius Julius Victor, a veteran.

EDCS-21100169.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Fortunatus lived for 5 years and 2 months. Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Victor, a veteran, made this for himself during his lifetime.
EDCS-21100169.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Julius Fortunatus, who died at a young age, and also a self-funded monument for the veteran Gaius Julius Victor.

EDCS-21100170.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Victor, a soldier of the III Augusta legion.
EDCS-21100170.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Victor, a soldier of the III Augusta legion.

EDCS-21100171.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Victor, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived for 45 years. Made for his dearest son.
EDCS-21100171.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Julius Victor, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his son.

EDCS-21100172.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Quintus Laelius Saturus, a veteran, who lived for 62 years. Petellia Satura made and dedicated this for her worthy husband, with their children.
EDCS-21100172.summary: A funerary monument for the veteran Quintus Laelius Saturus, erected by his wife Petellia Satura and their children.

EDCS-21100173.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Marcus Laetorius Syriacus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, of the century of Antonius Felix, who lived for 45 years and served for 21 years. Gaius Laetorius Rufus made this for his deserving brother.
EDCS-21100173.summary: A funerary monument for Marcus Laetorius Syriacus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his brother Gaius Laetorius Rufus.

EDCS-21100174.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Licinius Aquila Bera, son of Marcus Licinius, of the Collina tribe, a veteran of the Third Legion Augusta, lived 85 years. His son, Marcus Licinius Candidus, made this for his deserving father.
EDCS-21100174.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Licinius Aquila Bera, a veteran of the Third Legion Augusta, erected by his son.

EDCS-21100175.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Licinius Marsus, a veteran, lived 80 years. His heir, Caius Licinius Marsus, made this for his deserving father.
EDCS-21100175.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Licinius Marsus, a veteran, erected by his heir who shares the same name.

EDCS-21100176.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Licinius Secundus, son of Titus Licinius, of the Galeria tribe, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta with 27 years of service, lived 47 years. He lies here.
EDCS-21100176.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Licinius Secundus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, indicating his service length and age at death.

EDCS-21100177.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Licinius Victor, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, lived 43 years. Optata made this for her most dutiful brother. He lies here.
EDCS-21100177.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Licinius Victor, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, erected by his sister Optata.

EDCS-21100178.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Livius Firmus, a veteran, lived 62 years. Caelia Saturnina made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-21100178.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Livius Firmus, a veteran, erected by his wife Caelia Saturnina.

EDCS-21100179.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Marcus L. Capito, an equestrian soldier, lived 35 years. Lo Crescens made this for his brother.
EDCS-21100179.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus L. Capito, an equestrian soldier, erected by his brother Lo Crescens.

EDCS-21100181.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Lucretius Viator, a veteran, lived 65 years. He made this for himself during his lifetime.
EDCS-21100181.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Lucretius Viator, a veteran, who commissioned his own tomb during his lifetime.

EDCS-21100182.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Publius Maevius Macrinus, a veteran, lived 60 years. Maevii Herennianus and Saturninus made this for their deserving father.
EDCS-21100182.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Maevius Macrinus, a veteran, erected by his sons Herennianus and Saturninus.

EDCS-21100183.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Magnius Fortunatianus, of the Quirina tribe, from Caralis, lived 22 years, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta. The heir made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-21100183.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Magnius Fortunatianus, a young soldier of the Third Legion Augusta from Caralis, whose heir erected the monument.

EDCS-21100185.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Mallius Fortunatus, a veteran, lived 70 years. Valeria Concessa made this for her husband, together with [their] sons.
EDCS-21100185.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Mallius Fortunatus, a veteran, erected by his wife Valeria Concessa and their sons.

EDCS-21100186.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Manilius Extricatus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 10 years.
EDCS-21100186.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gaius Manilius Extricatus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-21100187.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Manilius Fuscus, a veteran, is buried here. Gaius Julius Barbarus made this for an excellent citizen, well-deserving.
EDCS-21100187.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the veteran Manilius Fuscus, with the tombstone erected by Gaius Julius Barbarus.

EDCS-21100188.translation: Manilius, an old veteran woman, made this for herself during her lifetime and for her deserving spouse. Lived X years.
EDCS-21100188.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Manilius, a veteran woman, for herself and her husband, indicating she lived for 10 years.

EDCS-21100190.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Marius Felix, a veteran, lived 56 years. Marcia Maria and his heirs made this for their most rare husband and dearest father.
EDCS-21100190.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the veteran Marcus Marius Felix, who lived 56 years, with the monument erected by his wife and heirs.

EDCS-21100192.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Memmius Secundus, a veteran, made this for himself during his lifetime, who lived 70 years.
EDCS-21100192.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the veteran Memmius Secundus, who made it for himself and lived for 70 years.

EDCS-21100193.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Memmius Secundus, a veteran, for his dearest son, Memmius Felix.
EDCS-21100193.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Memmius Secundus, a veteran, to his son, Memmius Felix.

EDCS-21100194.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Arruntia Ingenua lived 44 years. Her husband, Minucius Victor, a veteran, made this.
EDCS-21100194.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Arruntia Ingenua, who lived 44 years, erected by her husband, the veteran Minucius Victor.

EDCS-21100195.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Modius Victor, an equestrian, lived 46 years. His heirs made this.
EDCS-21100195.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Modius Victor, an equestrian, who lived 46 years, with the monument erected by his heirs.

EDCS-21100196.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Mucatralis Tanurius made this for his father and patron, Mucatralis Auluzeni, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, who lived 30 years, by nationality a Thracian, born in the city of Augusta Traianensium.
EDCS-21100196.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Mucatralis Tanurius to his father and patron, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion of Thracian origin, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-21100197.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Mucius Moderatus, an equestrian of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 40 years. Gaius Mucius Moderatus made this for his most dutiful father.
EDCS-21100197.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Mucius Moderatus, an equestrian of the Third Augustan Legion who lived 40 years, with the monument erected by his son of the same name.

EDCS-21100198.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Munatius Ianuarius, a soldier of the legion, lived 30 years. Pomponius Carito, his heir, made this.
EDCS-21100198.summary: A tombstone for Munatius Ianuarius, a legionary, erected by his heir.

EDCS-21100199.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Mundicius Fortunatus, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, lived 40 years. Iulius Crescens made this for his deserving comrade.
EDCS-21100199.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Mundicius Fortunatus, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, erected by his comrade Iulius Crescens.

EDCS-21100200.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Mundicius Rogatus, after 25 years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-21100200.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Mundicius Rogatus, recording his 25 years of military service.

EDCS-21100201.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Mundius Primus, father of Mundia Honorata, a veteran, lived 80 years. His heirs made this for their most dutiful grandfather.
EDCS-21100201.summary: A tombstone for Caius Mundius Primus, a veteran, erected by his heirs for him and his daughter Mundia Honorata.

EDCS-21100202.translation: For Horatia Saturnina, daughter of Quintus Horatius Fuscus, a veteran. Caius Mustius Fortunatus, a veteran, [made this] for his most innocent wife.
EDCS-21100202.summary: A tombstone for Horatia Saturnina, daughter of a veteran, erected by her veteran husband Caius Mustius Fortunatus.

EDCS-21100203.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Basus, a soldier of the legion, lived 20 years. Iulia Rogata made this.
EDCS-21100203.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Basus, a legionary, erected by Iulia Rogata.

EDCS-21100204.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Nonius Mus lived [unknown years]. He served 17 years and is buried here.
EDCS-21100204.summary: A tombstone for Publius Nonius Mus, recording his 17 years of military service.

EDCS-21100205.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Syria, [a soldier] of the III Augusta Legion, [aged] 23 years and 8 months, is buried here. Caius Livius Alexander, her brother, made this for her, his deserving sister.
EDCS-21100205.summary: A tombstone for Syria, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, erected by her brother Caius Livius Alexander.

EDCS-21100206.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Numisius Donatus, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion, is buried here.
EDCS-21100206.summary: A tombstone for Caius Numisius Donatus, a soldier of the III Augusta Legion.

EDCS-21100208.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Octavius Bassianus, a veteran, lived 75 years. Iulia Successa made this for her best husband. He is buried here.
EDCS-21100208.summary: A tombstone for the veteran Octavius Bassianus, erected by his wife Iulia Successa.

EDCS-21100209.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Octavius Marcellus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, is buried here.
EDCS-21100209.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Octavius Marcellus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta.

EDCS-21100210.translation: To the departed spirits. His most loving wife made this for her excellent husband, Quintus Octavius Urbanus, a veteran, who lived 65 years.
EDCS-21100210.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a wife to her husband, Quintus Octavius Urbanus, a veteran.

EDCS-21100211.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ostorius Naso, a soldier of the legion, made this.
EDCS-21100211.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ostorius Naso, a soldier of a legion.

EDCS-21100212.translation: To the departed spirits. Sextus Factumeius Severus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, who lived 25 years; his dearest brother made this.
EDCS-21100212.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Factumeius Severus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, made by his brother.

EDCS-21100213.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fructus and Ingenua made this for their excellent patron, Quintus Passius Secundus, a veteran, who lived 81 years.
EDCS-21100213.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Fructus and Ingenua to their patron, Quintus Passius Secundus, a veteran.

EDCS-21100214.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pescenius Victor, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, who lived 45 years; Iulia Labenia made this for her husband.
EDCS-21100214.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pescenius Victor, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, made by his wife Iulia Labenia.

EDCS-21100215.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Petidius Naso, his brother, made this for Petidius Donatus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, with 15 years of service, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-21100215.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Petidius Donatus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, made by his brother Petidius Naso.

EDCS-21100216.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Petronius Cerialis, his most devoted brother, made this for Gaius Petronius Cerialis, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-21100216.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Petronius Cerialis, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, made by his brother Petronius Cerialis.

EDCS-21100217.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pettius Victor, a soldier of the legion, her husband, made this for his deserving wife, Pinaria Processina, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-21100217.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pinaria Processina, made by her husband Pettius Victor, a soldier of a legion.

EDCS-21100218.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Pomponius Maximus, her son, made this for his mother, Helvidiae Procula, who lived 80 years.
EDCS-21100218.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Helvidiae Procula, made by her son Lucius Pomponius Maximus.

EDCS-21100219.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Pomponius Catullinus, soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 40 years.
EDCS-21100219.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Pomponius Catullinus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-21100220.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publius Pomponius Donatus, soldier, lived 40 years. His heirs made this.
EDCS-21100220.summary: This is a tombstone for Publius Pomponius Donatus, a soldier, for whom his heirs made this monument after his death at age 40.

EDCS-21100221.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publius Pomponius Ianuarius, soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 45 years. Pomponius Nivalis and Euticia, his freedmen and heirs, made this for their deserving patron.
EDCS-21100221.summary: This tombstone was erected by the freedmen and heirs of Publius Pomponius Ianuarius, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, who died at age 45.

EDCS-21100223.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. P. S., a veteran from the Third Augustan Legion, from Antioch, lived about XX years. His wife Seppia made this.
EDCS-21100223.summary: This tombstone records the death of P. S., a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion from Antioch, and was erected by his wife Seppia.

EDCS-21100224.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Titus Riburrinus Galeria Fuscus Luco, soldier of the Seventh Gemina Felix Legion, Passinian cohort, 8 years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-21100224.summary: This tombstone marks the burial place of Titus Riburrinus Galeria Fuscus Luco, a soldier with 8 years of service in the Seventh Gemina Felix Legion, Passinian cohort.

EDCS-21100225.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Rufius Gallicus C., soldier of the [---] Legion.
EDCS-21100225.summary: This is an incomplete tombstone inscription for Lucius Rufius Gallicus C., a soldier of an unidentified legion.

EDCS-21100226.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. His fellow veteran made this for Gaius Rutilius Bellicus, a veteran.
EDCS-21100226.summary: This tombstone was erected by a fellow veteran for Gaius Rutilius Bellicus.

EDCS-21100227.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. R. Saturninus, veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 50 years, lies buried here.
EDCS-21100227.summary: This tombstone records the death of R. Saturninus, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-21100229.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Sallustius Amenus, a veteran, lived 57 years. The sons of Sallustii made this.
EDCS-21100229.summary: This tombstone was erected by the sons of Sallustii for Lucius Sallustius Amenus, a veteran who died at age 57.

EDCS-21100231.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Sallustius Modius, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 72 years. Quintus Sallustius Nonianus, his grandson, made this with his co-heirs for his grandfather. He is buried here.
EDCS-21100231.summary: This tombstone was erected by Quintus Sallustius Nonianus and his co-heirs for his grandfather, Quintus Sallustius Modius, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion who died at age 72.

EDCS-21100232.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Peregrina made this for her well-deserving husband, Quintus Sallustius Venustus, a veteran, who lived 71 years.
EDCS-21100232.summary: This funerary monument records the life and death of a veteran named Quintus Sallustius Venustus, commemorated by his wife.

EDCS-21100233.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Seius Licinianus, a soldier, lived 60 years.
EDCS-21100233.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of a soldier named Seius Licinianus.

EDCS-21100234.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Marcus Sentius Rogatus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 50 years.
EDCS-21100234.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Marcus Sentius Rogatus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion.

EDCS-21100235.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Maximus, a slave of our Caesar, and his heir, made this for Gaius Septimius Victor, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-21100235.summary: This funerary monument records the life of Gaius Septimius Victor, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, and mentions Maximus, a slave of Caesar, as his heir.

EDCS-21100236.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Marcus Sextilius IAHN, a veteran, lived 56 years. Iulia Victoria made this for her most beloved husband.
EDCS-21100236.summary: This funerary monument records the life of the veteran Marcus Sextilius IAHN, commemorated by his wife Iulia Victoria.

EDCS-21100237.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Sittius Ianuarius, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 32 years. Sittius Castus, his brother, made this.
EDCS-21100237.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Sittius Ianuarius, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, commemorated by his brother.

EDCS-21100238.translation: To Tenacis Aic, soldier of the III Augusta legion.
EDCS-21100238.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Tenacis Aic, a soldier of the III Augusta legion.

EDCS-21100239.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Titus Speccius Faustus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 40 years. Ulpia Aeternitas, his wife, [made this].
EDCS-21100239.summary: This funerary monument records the life of Titus Speccius Faustus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, commemorated by his wife.

EDCS-21100241.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Titus Statius Fortis, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 25 years. Ianuarius Saturninus, his heir and fellow soldier, made this.
EDCS-21100241.summary: This funerary monument records the life of Titus Statius Fortis, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, commemorated by his heir and fellow soldier.

EDCS-21100242.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Gaius Iulius Pontius, his brother, made this for Gaius Steius Cai filius Pollia Sabinianus Castris, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-21100242.summary: This funerary monument records the life of Gaius Steius Cai filius Pollia Sabinianus Castris, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, commemorated by his brother.

EDCS-21100243.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Tadius Datus, a veteran, lived 68 years. Iulius Baricio made this for the deserving dead.
EDCS-21100243.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Tadius Datus, a veteran, dedicated by Iulius Baricio.

EDCS-21100246.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Caius Valerius Castus, son of Caius, of the Papirian tribe, a soldier in the III Augusta legion, lived 40 years. Caius Iulius Saturninus made this for his deserving brother. He is buried here.
EDCS-21100246.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Valerius Castus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, made by his brother, Caius Iulius Saturninus.

EDCS-21100247.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Aelia Victoria made this for Valerius Crispus, her veteran husband, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-21100247.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius Crispus, a veteran, dedicated by his wife Aelia Victoria.

EDCS-21100248.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Valerius Donatus, a veteran, lived 75 years. His son made this for his father.
EDCS-21100248.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius Donatus, a veteran, made by his son.

EDCS-21100249.translation: Iulius Calicles, his heir, made this for Valerius Faustus, a soldier in the III Augusta legion, for 800 sesterces.
EDCS-21100249.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius Faustus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, funded by his heir, Iulius Calicles.

EDCS-21100250.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Valerius Felix, a soldier in the III Augusta legion, lived 50 years. His sons made this.
EDCS-21100250.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius Felix, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, made by his sons.

EDCS-21100251.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Caius Valerius Felix, a cavalryman in the III Augusta legion, lived 2 years. Antonia Matronica made this for her most beloved husband.
EDCS-21100251.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Valerius Felix, a cavalryman in the III Augusta legion, made by his wife Antonia Matronica.

EDCS-21100253.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. For Valerio Felici, soldier of the III Augusta legion, and Valerio Victori, his son.
EDCS-21100253.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Valerius Felici, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, and his son Valerius Victori.

EDCS-21100254.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Caius Valerius Firmus lived 40 years, a soldier in the III Augusta legion. Pompeia Fortunata made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-21100254.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Valerius Firmus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, made by his wife Pompeia Fortunata.

EDCS-21100255.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Valerius Fortunatus, a legionary cavalryman, lived 27 years. His sister Larcia Tertulla set this up. He is buried here.
EDCS-21100255.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Fortunatus, a legionary cavalryman, set up by his sister Larcia Tertulla.

EDCS-21100256.translation: Dedicated to the underworld gods. Valerius Rufus, an equestrian of the III Augusta legion, lived 35 years. Valeria Tertulla, his sister, had this made for her brother Valerius Successus and her relative Successus, her husband, who deserved well.
EDCS-21100256.summary: A funerary monument commemorating Valerius Rufus, erected by his sister and dedicated to him and her husband.

EDCS-21100257.translation: Dedicated to the underworld gods. Lucius Valerius Fortunatus, a soldier, lived 25 years.
EDCS-21100257.summary: A funerary monument commemorating Lucius Valerius Fortunatus, a soldier, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100258.translation: Valerius Maximus, a veteran, lived 65 years. Iulia Germanilla made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-21100258.summary: A funerary monument for the veteran Valerius Maximus, erected by his wife Iulia Germanilla.

EDCS-21100259.translation: Dedicated to the underworld gods. For Gaius Valerius Maximus, son of Gaius, a veteran, who lived 75 years. Quintus Vettius Laetus and Lucius Vettidius Tertullus, his heirs, made this.
EDCS-21100259.summary: A funerary inscription for the veteran Gaius Valerius Maximus, commissioned by his heirs Quintus Vettius Laetus and Lucius Vettidius Tertullus.

EDCS-21100260.translation: Dedicated to the underworld gods. For Gaius Valerius Optatus of Milevum, son of Gaius, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, who lived 26 years. Valerius Felix made this for his brother.
EDCS-21100260.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Valerius Optatus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his brother Valerius Felix.

EDCS-21100261.translation: Dedicated to the underworld gods. Publius Valerius Optatus, a veteran, lived 81 years and 5 months.
EDCS-21100261.summary: A funerary monument for the veteran Publius Valerius Optatus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100262.translation: Dedicated to the underworld gods. Lucius Valerius Galeria Rufinus, a soldier of the VII Gemina Felix legion, in the century of Lucius Egnatius, served for 9 years and lived 28 years. Here he is buried. May the earth be light for you. His heir arranged this.
EDCS-21100262.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Galeria Rufinus, a soldier of the VII Gemina Felix legion, who died at 28, with his heir arranging the burial.

EDCS-21100263.translation: Dedicated to the underworld gods. For Kaninia Victoria, who lived 80 years. Lucius Valerius Saturninus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, for his most beloved son, deserving.
EDCS-21100263.summary: A funerary monument for Kaninia Victoria, erected by her husband Lucius Valerius Saturninus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, for their son.

EDCS-21100264.translation: Dedicated to the underworld gods. Gaius Valerius Secundus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 40 years. Iulius Rusticus, his heir, made this.
EDCS-21100264.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Valerius Secundus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his heir Iulius Rusticus.

EDCS-21100265.translation: Dedicated to the underworld gods. Valerius Secundus, a soldier of 20 years' service, lived 45 years. Iulia made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-21100265.summary: A funerary monument for Valerius Secundus, a soldier with 20 years of service, erected by his wife Iulia.

EDCS-21100266.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Vettius Satianus, son of Marcus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, [erected by] his brothers.
EDCS-21100266.summary: This is a funerary dedication by the brothers of Marcus Vettius Satianus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion.

EDCS-21100267.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Vettius Saturninus, a veteran, lived 60 years. Domitia Potita [erected this] for her husband, and Vettia [did the same].
EDCS-21100267.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of veteran Lucius Vettius Saturninus, with dedications from his wife Domitia Potita and another person, Vettia.

EDCS-21100268.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Vetulenius Bulla Festus, of the Quirina tribe, an equestrian of the Third Augustan Legion, is buried here; he lived 32 years. His heir made this; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-21100268.summary: This funerary epitaph for Lucius Vetulenius Bulla Festus, an equestrian of the Third Augustan Legion who died at 32, was erected by his heir.

EDCS-21100270.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Vibius Felix, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 50 years. Roscia Saturnina made [this] for her husband. He is buried here.
EDCS-21100270.summary: A funerary inscription for veteran Marcus Vibius Felix, who lived to 50, erected by his wife Roscia Saturnina.

EDCS-21100271.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Vinntus Potitus, a veteran, made [this] in his lifetime for himself and for his most faithful wife. He lived [unknown] years.
EDCS-21100271.summary: A self-funded funerary monument by veteran Quintus Vinntus Potitus for himself and his wife.

EDCS-21100272.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Ulpius Nero, born in Berytus, a veteran.
EDCS-21100272.summary: A simple funerary dedication to veteran Lucius Ulpius Nero, originally from Berytus.

EDCS-21100273.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tiberia Catula lived 85 years. Caius Umbonius Saturninus erected [this] for his dearest mother.
EDCS-21100273.summary: This funerary dedication was made by Caius Umbonius Saturninus for his mother, Tiberia Catula, who lived to 85.

EDCS-21100274.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Voconius Securus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 25 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-21100274.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Voconius Securus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-21100275.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Volusius Felix, son of Caius, a veteran, lived 80 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-21100275.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Caius Volusius Felix, a veteran who lived to 80.

EDCS-21100277.translation: [---]estamae, from Ostia, 60 years of age, Horatianus, of the Third [Augustan Legion].
EDCS-21100277.summary: This fragment likely refers to a person named Horatianus from Ostia, possibly a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, who died at 60.

EDCS-21100279.translation: To the memory of his incomparable brother, Licis, his most beloved father, for their piety, the legionary of the III Augusta made [this].
EDCS-21100279.summary: This inscription is a dedication honoring the deceased father and brother of the dedicator, who was a soldier in Legio III Augusta.

EDCS-21100280.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. A veteran made [this] for Licinia Iusbus, his dearest.
EDCS-21100280.summary: This funerary inscription records a veteran's dedication to his deceased wife, Licinia Iusbus.

EDCS-21100281.translation: A veteran made [this] for himself and his family.
EDCS-21100281.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a veteran for himself and his relatives.

EDCS-21100282.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Aurelia Karka, his foster-daughter, lived for 32 years. Adventus Augusti verna, a steward of the III Augusta legion, [dedicated this].
EDCS-21100282.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Aurelia Karka at age 32 and is dedicated by Adventus Augusti verna, a steward of Legio III Augusta.

EDCS-21100283.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. For Cassius, a steward of the III Augusta Pius Vindex legion, who lived 110 years, 7 months, and 21 days. Ursinus, a cashier of the same legion, made [this] for him as one who deserved well.
EDCS-21100283.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Cassius, a steward in Legio III Augusta, who lived to an extraordinary age, and was dedicated by Ursinus, a legionary cashier.

EDCS-21100285.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Hygino, his most faithful slave, lived for 23 years. Adventus Augusti verna, formerly a steward of the III Augusta legion, [dedicated this].
EDCS-21100285.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Hygino, a slave of Adventus Augusti verna, who was formerly a steward in Legio III Augusta.

EDCS-21100286.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Fortunata lived for 27 years. Marullus Iulianus, from the office of accounts, her heir, [dedicated this] to his most beloved wife.
EDCS-21100286.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Fortunata at age 27 and is dedicated by her heir and husband, Marullus Iulianus.

EDCS-21100288.translation: Certus, quaestor, aedile, [received/made] the measurements with the scale and the sundial...
EDCS-21100288.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records the duties or achievements of an official named Certus, possibly related to public works or administration.

EDCS-21100289.translation: ...on account of the honor of the aedileship / by Arteor and Acilius(?), the aediles.
EDCS-21100289.summary: This fragmentary inscription refers to an aedileship and names two individuals, possibly as aediles themselves or as contributors.

EDCS-21100290.translation: To Publius Aelius Maximus, his father, and Lorenia Processa, his mother, and Acutia Flaccinilla, his wife, Publius Aelius Processus, a Roman knight and perpetual priest, [dedicated this] for himself and his parents.
EDCS-21100290.summary: This inscription is a dedication made by Publius Aelius Processus, a Roman knight and priest, to his parents and wife, for himself and his family.

EDCS-21100292.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilius Demetrianus lived 50 years. Fortunata made this monument for her husband, who rests here, through the curiales.
EDCS-21100292.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Aemilius Demetrianus, commissioned by his wife Fortunata and the curiales, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100293.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Avillius Celsus, priest of Saturn, lived 80 years. Avillia Saturnina, his daughter, made this for her deserving father.
EDCS-21100293.summary: This funerary epitaph records Avillius Celsus, a priest of Saturn, and was set up by his daughter Avillia Saturnina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100294.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Caecilia Octavia, who lived 40 years, and her children Veteriosa, who lived 10 years, and Gogius, who lived 8 years. Faustinus, perpetual priest and aedile, made this for his most deserving departed.
EDCS-21100294.summary: This funerary inscription honors Caecilia Octavia and two children, Veteriosa and Gogius, and was dedicated by Faustinus, a local magistrate and priest, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100295.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Mallius Donatus, who held the office of duumvir, who lived 75 years, for his wife Tannonia Valeria.
EDCS-21100295.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Quintus Mallius Donatus, a former duumvir, and was made by his wife Tannonia Valeria, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100296.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Memius Comes lived 80 years. The curiales made this for him; his wife Iovia made this for him.
EDCS-21100296.summary: This funerary dedication for Memius Comes was made by both the curiales and his wife Iovia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100297.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Munatius Aetus lived 55 years. His devoted sister Clementilla, priestess of Ceres, made this.
EDCS-21100297.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Lucius Munatius Aetus, erected by his sister Clementilla, a priestess of Ceres, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100298.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Numerius Victor, son of Honoratus, chief priest of the god.
EDCS-21100298.summary: This funerary epitaph identifies Caius Numerius Victor as a chief priest of a deity, son of Honoratus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100299.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Petronius Victor, a money changer, lived 35 years. His father and brother, Petroni Namphamones.
EDCS-21100299.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Petronius Victor, a money changer, and was erected by his father and brother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100300.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Pomponius Nepotianus, a decurion, lived 43 years. Cornelia Taecusa made this for her dearest husband, who rests here.
EDCS-21100300.summary: This funerary epitaph for Quintus Pomponius Nepotianus, a decurion, was erected by his wife Cornelia Taecusa, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100301.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sallustia Victoria, high priestess, lived 95 years and 7 months. Iulius Faustinus and Iulia Donata made this for their well-deserving mother.
EDCS-21100301.summary: This funerary inscription honors Sallustia Victoria, a high priestess, and was erected by her children Iulius Faustinus and Iulia Donata, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100302.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Sittia Garama lived 36 years. Publius Scantius Proculus, priest, father and grandson, made this for his sweetest daughter.
EDCS-21100302.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sittia Garama, dedicated by her father and grandson.

EDCS-21100307.translation: To his friends, from the generous Fabio Felice.
EDCS-21100307.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Fabio Felice to his friends.

EDCS-21100309.translation: Sincerus, the divine Pauellus, his own beloved Admirandus.
EDCS-21100309.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sincerus Pauellus, described as beloved by Admirandus.

EDCS-21100314.translation: Such was Aconia; that my fate might be short, we were all equal.
EDCS-21100314.summary: This is a philosophical epitaph reflecting on mortality, possibly for Aconia.

EDCS-21100315.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Acuvius Proculus lived 17 years.
EDCS-21100315.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Acuvius Proculus.

EDCS-21100316.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aedia Fortunata lived 11 years. Her mother made this for her daughter.
EDCS-21100316.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aedia Fortunata, dedicated by her mother.

EDCS-21100317.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Aelius Agnito, the most dutiful and deserving teacher, who lived 50 years, 2 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-21100317.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelius Agnito, a teacher, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-21100318.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publius Aelius Augendus, freedman of Publius Aelius, is buried here; he lived one year and six days. Publius Aelius Armenius, his natural father, [made this] with singular affection.
EDCS-21100318.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the infant Publius Aelius Augendus, dedicated by his father.

EDCS-21100319.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publius Aelius Castresis lived 70 years.
EDCS-21100319.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Aelius Castresis.

EDCS-21100320.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Aelius Datus lived 4 years. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Aelius Datus lived 2 years and 7 months.
EDCS-21100320.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording two children named Lucius Aelius Datus who died at different ages.

EDCS-21100321.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Aelius Dionisianus lived for 65 years.
EDCS-21100321.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Publius Aelius Dionisianus, who lived to be 65 years old.

EDCS-21100322.translation: To the Divine Spirits. I made this for Publius Aelius Euticetus during my lifetime.
EDCS-21100322.summary: This is a funerary inscription where the dedicator made their tomb while still alive for Publius Aelius Euticetus.

EDCS-21100323.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aelius Flavianus lived for 30 years. Papiria Victo [made this].
EDCS-21100323.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Aelius Flavianus, who lived 30 years, and was made by Papiria Victo.

EDCS-21100324.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Brothers made this for Aelius Fortunatus, their beloved brother, who lived for 20 years.
EDCS-21100324.summary: This funerary inscription records that brothers commissioned this tomb for Aelius Fortunatus, who lived 20 years.

EDCS-21100325.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Aelius Fortunatus lived for 17 years.
EDCS-21100325.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Publius Aelius Fortunatus, who lived 17 years.

EDCS-21100326.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Aelius Herodianus lived for 3 years. His father, Publius Aelius Mucatra, made this.
EDCS-21100326.summary: This funerary inscription records that Publius Aelius Mucatra made this tomb for his son, Publius Aelius Herodianus, who died at the age of 3.

EDCS-21100328.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caecilia Lonicia made this for her husband, Marcus Aelius Ianuarius, who lived for 60 years.
EDCS-21100328.summary: This funerary inscription records that Caecilia Lonicia commissioned this tomb for her husband, Marcus Aelius Ianuarius, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-21100329.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Aelius Lucius' son, who lived for 60 years at Hadrumetum, ordered the monument to be made for himself in his will from 500 sesterces, which his pious and dearest wife Octavia Saesula undertook to have made by adding an additional 500 sesterces.
EDCS-21100329.summary: This funerary inscription records that Lucius Aelius Lucius' son, who lived 60 years and was from Hadrumetum, left instructions in his will for this monument to be built, which his wife Octavia Saesula funded and supervised.

EDCS-21100331.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aelius Maximino lived for 1 year. Iulia [made this].
EDCS-21100331.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Aelius Maximino, who lived 1 year, and was likely made by Iulia.

EDCS-21100332.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caius Aelius Maximus lived for 47 years. Aelia Tertiana made this for her brother.
EDCS-21100332.summary: This funerary inscription records that Aelia Tertiana made this tomb for her brother, Caius Aelius Maximus, who lived 47 years.

EDCS-21100333.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Aelius Renatus lived 1 year, 6 months, and 4 days. Heracles made this for his son.
EDCS-21100333.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the short life of Publius Aelius Renatus, dedicated by Heracles.

EDCS-21100334.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Aelius Septimianus lived 40 years. Claudia Fortunata made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-21100334.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Aelius Septimianus, dedicated by his wife Claudia Fortunata.

EDCS-21100335.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Aelius [---] made this for his son [---].
EDCS-21100335.summary: A fragmented funerary epitaph recording a dedication to a son named Aelius, with an incomplete lifespan.

EDCS-21100336.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Publius Aelius lived [---] years. Valerianus lived [---] years.
EDCS-21100336.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph that seems to record the lifespans of two individuals, Publius Aelius and Valerianus.

EDCS-21100337.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Antonius Iulianus made this for his most devoted wife, Aelia Ammia, who lived 20 years, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-21100337.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Aelia Ammia, dedicated by her husband Caius Antonius Iulianus.

EDCS-21100340.translation: To the memory of Aelia Eunoea, his most rare wife. Publius Aelius Barytimus made this.
EDCS-21100340.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Aelia Eunoea by her husband Publius Aelius Barytimus.

EDCS-21100342.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaia Aelia Macrina lived 3 years. Lucius Aelius Macer, her father, made this for his most devoted daughter.
EDCS-21100342.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of the young Gaia Aelia Macrina, dedicated by her father Lucius Aelius Macer.

EDCS-21100343.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelius Nemesio made this for Aelia Marcella, an infant of 20 days.
EDCS-21100343.summary: A funerary epitaph for a very young child, Aelia Marcella, dedicated by Aelius Nemesio.

EDCS-21100344.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Aelia Maxima Lambaesitana lived 32 years. Lucius Iulius Potitus is buried here with his most rare wife and their children.
EDCS-21100344.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Aelia Maxima Lambaesitana, with a dedication from her husband Lucius Iulius Potitus.

EDCS-21100345.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Aelia Prima lived 60 years. Aelius Victor, her eldest son, willingly made this for his most sweet mother, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-21100345.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Aelia Prima, dedicated by her son Aelius Victor.

EDCS-21100346.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aelia Romana lived 26 years. Her husband made this.
EDCS-21100346.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Aelia Romana, set up by her husband.

EDCS-21100347.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Co(rnelius?) Saturus made this for his well-deserving mother, Aelia Victorina, who lived 73 years.
EDCS-21100347.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Aelia Victorina, set up by her son.

EDCS-21100348.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sextilia Tertulla, his wife, made this for Lucius Aemilius Balbus Avitus Cassianus, together with Aemilia Marciana, his sister.
EDCS-21100348.summary: A tombstone inscription for Lucius Aemilius Balbus Avitus Cassianus, set up by his wife and sister.

EDCS-21100349.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Aemilius Buturus lived 75 years. Aemilia Candida made this for her dearest father.
EDCS-21100349.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Publius Aemilius Buturus, set up by his daughter.

EDCS-21100350.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aemilius Eupropes lived 35 years. Aurelia Theodora made this for her most dutiful husband.
EDCS-21100350.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Marcus Aemilius Eupropes, set up by his wife.

EDCS-21100351.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aemilius Felix, son of Marcus Aemilius of the Arnensis tribe, lived 71 years in Carthage. His wife and heir, Mescenia Ingenua, made this for him, who deserved it.
EDCS-21100351.summary: A tombstone inscription for Marcus Aemilius Felix, set up by his wife and heir.

EDCS-21100352.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cornelia Donata made this for her husband, Marcus Aemilius Primus.
EDCS-21100352.summary: A tombstone inscription for Marcus Aemilius Primus, set up by his wife.

EDCS-21100354.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aemilius Rogatianus lived 17 years.
EDCS-21100354.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Marcus Aemilius Rogatianus.

EDCS-21100355.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilius Rogatianus made this for his dearest son, Aemilius Valens, who lived 1 year and 6 months.
EDCS-21100355.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Aemilius Valens, set up by his father.

EDCS-21100356.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Flavia made this for her husband, Aemilius Victor, who lived 54 years, who deserved it. Buried here.
EDCS-21100356.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Aemilius Victor, set up by his wife.

EDCS-21100357.translation: "To the departed spirits. Livius Saturninus dedicated this to his mother Aemilia Candida, who lived 60 years."
EDCS-21100357.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Livius Saturninus to his mother Aemilia Candida.

EDCS-21100358.translation: "To the departed spirits of Aemilia Festa, who was also called Salvia, a most beloved mother."
