EDCS-21100358.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Aemilia Festa (also known as Salvia), described as a most beloved mother.

EDCS-21100360.translation: "To the departed spirits. Aemilia Fuscula lived 15 years. Her father Aemilius made this for his daughter."
EDCS-21100360.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Fuscula, who died at age 15, dedicated by her father Aemilius.

EDCS-21100361.translation: "To the departed spirits. Aemilia Honorata dedicated this to her most beloved husband Iulius Viator, who lived 50 years. To the departed spirits. Aemilia Honorata dedicated this to her father-in-law, who lived 80 years."
EDCS-21100361.summary: This inscription records the dedication by Aemilia Honorata to her husband Iulius Viator (died aged 50) and her father-in-law (died aged 80).

EDCS-21100362.translation: "To the departed spirits. Aemilia Hospita lived 20 years."
EDCS-21100362.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Hospita, who died at age 20.

EDCS-21100363.translation: "To the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Aemilianus, a veteran, dedicated this to his most rare soul, Aemilia.); who lived 60 years."
EDCS-21100363.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by the veteran Caius Iulius Aemilianus to his wife Aemilia Ingenua, who died at age 60.

EDCS-21100364.translation: "To the departed spirits. Flavius Celsus made this for his wife Aemilia Respecta, his most rare mistress, who lived 50 years."
EDCS-21100364.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Flavius Celsus to his wife Aemilia Respecta.

EDCS-21100365.translation: "To the departed spirits. Quintus Congius Augustalis made this for his dutiful wife Aemilia Rufina, who lived 20 years. She lies here."
EDCS-21100365.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Quintus Congius Augustalis to his wife Aemilia Rufina.

EDCS-21100366.translation: "To the departed spirits. Aufidius Zabulius made this for his most beloved wife Aemilia Saturnina, who lived thirty-five years. He lies here."
EDCS-21100366.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Aufidius Zabulius to his wife Aemilia Saturnina.

EDCS-21100367.translation: "To the departed spirits. Cornelia Donata dedicated this to her daughter Aemilia Saturnina, who lived 24 years."
EDCS-21100367.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Cornelia Donata to her daughter Aemilia Saturnina.

EDCS-21100368.translation: To the departed spirits. Aemilia Tertulla lived for 60 years. Her sons made this for a deserving mother.
EDCS-21100368.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Tertulla, erected by her sons.

EDCS-21100369.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caio Afranius Sura. His father, Afranius Victor, made this.
EDCS-21100369.summary: This funerary epitaph records the dedication by Afranius Victor for his son, Caio Afranius Sura.

EDCS-21100370.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Afrania Annibona, who lived 50 years. Geminius Faustus made this for his mother. Sacred to the departed spirits. For Baebia Caelasia, who lived 20 years. Geminius Faustus made this for his wife.
EDCS-21100370.summary: This inscription is a double funerary epitaph for Afrania Annibona and her daughter Baebia Caelasia, erected by Geminius Faustus for both his mother and wife.

EDCS-21100371.translation: To the departed spirits. His son, Papiria Albinus.
EDCS-21100371.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely recording a son named Albinus, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-21100372.translation: To Alexandro [son of] Alexandro, from [his] parents, [he] made [this] and [---].
EDCS-21100372.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, possibly for a son named Alexandro, erected by his parents.

EDCS-21100373.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Alius Lartidius and Alia Lartidia, the dutiful, deserving one. Aelia Iuliosa made this for her deserving mother, Alia Lartidia.
EDCS-21100373.summary: This funerary epitaph records the dedication by Aelia Iuliosa for her mother, Alia Lartidia, and potentially another relative, Alius Lartidius.

EDCS-21100374.translation: To the departed spirits. For Amator, who lived 50 years. Octa Valeria made this for her deserving father.
EDCS-21100374.summary: This funerary epitaph records the dedication by Octa Valeria for her father, Amator.

EDCS-21100375.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Amphius Ammonianus of Carthage, dutiful, lived 15 years and 130 days. His father, Amphius Victor, made this for his son.
EDCS-21100375.summary: This funerary epitaph records the dedication by Amphius Victor for his son, Titus Amphius Ammonianus, who died at a young age.

EDCS-21100376.translation: To the departed spirits. For Amullia, daughter of Caius, the African. She lived 27 years. And for Acutia Emerita. She lived 3 years. Caius Amullius Africus, her father, made this for his daughter and his granddaughter, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-21100376.summary: This funerary epitaph records the dedication by Caius Amullius Africus for his daughter Amullia and his granddaughter Acutia Emerita.

EDCS-21100377.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Angelicus, who lived 6 years. His father made this for his dutiful son.
EDCS-21100377.summary: This funerary epitaph records the dedication by a father for his young son, Angelicus.

EDCS-21100379.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Anneius Felix lived 44 years and 10 months. Marcella dedicated this.
EDCS-21100379.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Anneius Felix, placed by Marcella.

EDCS-21100380.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Annius Fortunatus made this for himself during his lifetime.
EDCS-21100380.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Caius Annius Fortunatus, made by himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-21100381.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Annius Valens lived 80 years. Lucius Antonius Viator dedicated this to his dearest father. Iulia Successa lived 60 years. Lucius Antonius Donatus dedicated this to his most dutiful mother.
EDCS-21100381.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Antonius Viator for his father, Lucius Annius Valens, and by Lucius Antonius Donatus for his mother, Iulia Successa.

EDCS-21100382.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Annius Ursulus, son of Caius, from Sabatina, from Mantua.
EDCS-21100382.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Caius Annius Ursulus, noting his origin and parentage.

EDCS-21100383.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Annia Faustina lived 28 years. Her husband dedicated this to his dearest wife.
EDCS-21100383.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Annia Faustina, placed by her husband.

EDCS-21100384.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caia Annia Maximina lived 33 years.
EDCS-21100384.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Caia Annia Maximina.

EDCS-21100385.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Annia Paulina lived 75 years. Caius Iulius Theodotus dedicated this to his deserving mother.
EDCS-21100385.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Annia Paulina, placed by her son, Caius Iulius Theodotus.

EDCS-21100386.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Annia Quartilla, dutiful, lived 45 years. Iulius Maximus dedicated this to his most cherished wife.
EDCS-21100386.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Annia Quartilla, placed by her husband, Iulius Maximus.

EDCS-21100387.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Annia Venusta. Calvisius, son of [---], dedicated this.
EDCS-21100387.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Annia Venusta, placed by Calvisius.

EDCS-21100388.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Anteia Saturnina lived 40 years. Caius Coponius Proculus dedicated this to his wife with his own money.
EDCS-21100388.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Anteia Saturnina, placed by her husband, Caius Coponius Proculus.

EDCS-21100389.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Antistius Victor made this for his father, Lucius Antistius Victor.
EDCS-21100389.summary: A funerary dedication from a son to his father, dated by the reuse of the father's name.

EDCS-21100390.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. [They] made this for their dearest wife Antistia Saturnina while alive. She lived for 60 years.
EDCS-21100390.summary: A funerary dedication made by a living couple for their wife, Antistia Saturnina, who lived for 60 years.

EDCS-21100391.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Antonius lived 80 years. Marcus Antonius made this for his deserving father.
EDCS-21100391.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcus Antonius for his father, who lived 80 years and was well-deserving.

EDCS-21100392.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Antonius Bassus lived 65 years. Caius Iulius Theodotus made this for his deserving mother-in-law.
EDCS-21100392.summary: A funerary dedication by Caius Iulius Theodotus for his mother-in-law, Antonius Bassus, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-21100393.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Antonius Felix lived [unknown years]. Lucius Fabricius Donatus made this monument for his deserving father.
EDCS-21100393.summary: A funerary dedication for a father, made by his son Lucius Fabricius Donatus.

EDCS-21100394.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Antonius Marinus [and] Crescentia [did this] for the deserving.
EDCS-21100394.summary: A funerary dedication by Antonius Marinus and Crescentia for a deserving individual.

EDCS-21100395.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Antonius Proculus lived 42 years. His sons made this for their father.
EDCS-21100395.summary: A funerary dedication made by the sons for their father, Marcus Antonius Proculus, who lived 42 years.

EDCS-21100396.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Antonius Saturninus lived 16 years. His brothers made this for the deserving.
EDCS-21100396.summary: A funerary dedication by brothers for Lucius Antonius Saturninus, who lived 16 years.

EDCS-21100397.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Antonius Sterculus lived 2 years and 9 months.
EDCS-21100397.summary: A funerary dedication for Antonius Sterculus, who died at the age of 2 years and 9 months.

EDCS-21100398.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Antonius Valens lived 24 years. His son Marcus Antonius made this for [him as] a dutiful [son].
EDCS-21100398.summary: A funerary dedication from a son, Marcus Antonius, for his father, Marcus Antonius Valens, who lived 24 years.

EDCS-21100399.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antonia Castula lived 13 years.
EDCS-21100399.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Antonia Castula, who died at 13 years of age.

EDCS-21100400.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Antonia Cyrilla, most virtuous, from Lambaesis, lived 16 years as a wife and 33 years in total. Antonius Proculus made this.
EDCS-21100400.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Antonia Cyrilla, wife of Antonius Proculus, who died at 33 years of age.

EDCS-21100401.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Antonia Dafna lived 19 years. Antonius Kaprarius, father, made this for his dutiful daughter.
EDCS-21100401.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Antonia Dafna, who died at 19 years of age and was commemorated by her father.

EDCS-21100402.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Antonia Ianuaria lived 35 years. Cornelius Candidus made this for his most rare wife.
EDCS-21100402.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Antonia Ianuaria, who died at 35 years of age and was commemorated by her husband.

EDCS-21100403.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Antonia Marina lived 23 years. Mother for her daughter.
EDCS-21100403.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Antonia Marina, who died at 23 years of age and was commemorated by her mother.

EDCS-21100404.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antonia Mellita lived 60 years. Her sons made this for her.
EDCS-21100404.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Antonia Mellita, who died at 60 years of age and was commemorated by her sons.

EDCS-21100405.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antonia Piperus lived 70 years. She left grandchildren and daughters-in-law. She acted well. Made this.
EDCS-21100405.summary: This funerary inscription records that Antonia Piperus lived to 70 years of age, left descendants, and was remembered for her good conduct.

EDCS-21100406.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Antonia Processa, sister.
EDCS-21100406.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Antonia Processa, likely by a sibling.

EDCS-21100407.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Antonia Sabina, from Lambaesis, lived 60 years. Lucius Antonius Oceanus made this for his deserving mother.
EDCS-21100407.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Antonia Sabina, who died at 60 years of age and was commemorated by her son.

EDCS-21100408.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Antonia Saturnina lived 35 years. Caius Iulius Theodotus, the husband, made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-21100408.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Antonia Saturnina, who died at 35 years of age and was commemorated by her husband.

EDCS-21100409.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Antonia Veneria lived 50 years. Tonneius Martialis [made this] for his wife.
EDCS-21100409.summary: A funerary dedication by Tonneius Martialis for his wife Antonia Veneria, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-21100410.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Antonia Venusta, freedwoman of Antonius Lambaesitanus, lived 60 years. Antonius Primus, her husband, also a freedman of the same patron, made this.
EDCS-21100410.summary: A funerary dedication by Antonius Primus for his wife Antonia Venusta, a freedwoman, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-21100411.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Antonia Victoria lived 80 years. Naevius Capito and his sons made this for her, as she deserved.
EDCS-21100411.summary: A funerary dedication by Naevius Capito and his sons for Antonia Victoria, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-21100412.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Antonia Victorica made this for her sister Antonia Pollina, as she deserved. [She] lived 25 years.
EDCS-21100412.summary: A funerary dedication by Antonia Victorica for her sister Antonia Pollina, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-21100413.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Aponia Adiutrix, a most devoted mother.
EDCS-21100413.summary: A funerary dedication for Aponia Adiutrix, described as a most devoted mother.

EDCS-21100414.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aponia Faosa lived 60 years. Her husband made this for himself and for his most rare wife.
EDCS-21100414.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for himself and his wife Aponia Faosa, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-21100415.translation: For Aponia Faustinidia, who lived 67 years. Aponius Felix, her son, kindly made this for his mother.
EDCS-21100415.summary: A funerary dedication by Aponius Felix for his mother Aponia Faustinidia, who lived 67 years.

EDCS-21100416.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Apronius Urbanus lived 95 years. Numisia Ianuaria made this for her husband.
EDCS-21100416.summary: A funerary dedication by Numisia Ianuaria for her husband Lucius Apronius Urbanus, who lived 95 years.

EDCS-21100417.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Aquilius Rogatinus lived 25 years. His brother made this.
EDCS-21100417.summary: A funerary dedication by a brother for Caius Aquilius Rogatinus, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-21100418.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aquilia Faustina lived 56 years.
EDCS-21100418.summary: A funerary dedication for Aquilia Faustina, who lived 56 years.

EDCS-21100419.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Arelia Marcella, dutiful, lived 22 years. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-21100419.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Arelia Marcella, who lived for 22 years, and includes a wish for her remains to rest in peace.

EDCS-21100420.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Arelia Nina lived 21 years. Clodius made this.
EDCS-21100420.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Arelia Nina, who lived for 21 years, and states that Clodius erected the monument.

EDCS-21100421.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Arminia Saturnina lived 80 years, a dutiful mother.
EDCS-21100421.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Arminia Saturnina, who lived for 80 years and was a dutiful mother.

EDCS-21100422.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Arrania Ospita lived 30 years. Arrtupor made this.
EDCS-21100422.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Arrania Ospita, who lived for 30 years, and states that Arrtupor erected the monument.

EDCS-21100423.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Arrius Celerinus lived 50 years and 9 days. Arria Luppula made this for her well-deserving freedman and foster-father.
EDCS-21100423.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Arrius Celerinus, who lived for 50 years and 9 days, and was honored by Arria Luppula, his freedwoman and foster-father.

EDCS-21100424.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Arrutius Cascastus lived 18 years and 3 months. His happiest mother and dearest brother made this.
EDCS-21100424.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Arrutius Cascastus, who lived for 18 years and 3 months, and was honored by his mother and brother.

EDCS-21100425.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Arruntius Mercurius lived 80 years. His sons Arrunti Longus Saturninus, Felix Valens, and Marcella made this.
EDCS-21100425.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Arruntius Mercurius, who lived for 80 years, and was honored by his sons.

EDCS-21100426.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Arruntius Suriacus lived 26 years. Lucius Arruntius Saturninus made this for his dutiful brother.
EDCS-21100426.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Caius Arruntius Suriacus, who lived for 26 years, and was honored by his brother Lucius Arruntius Saturninus.

EDCS-21300108.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Arruntia Donata, dearest aunt, lived 30 years. Iulius Baricio made this.
EDCS-21300108.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Arruntia Donata, who lived for 30 years, and states that Iulius Baricio erected the monument.

EDCS-21300109.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. For Arruntia Victoria, mother. Publicius Victorinus made this.
EDCS-21300109.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Arruntia Victoria, mother, and states that Publicius Victorinus erected the monument.

EDCS-21300110.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Arruntia Victoria lived 18 years. Lucius Arruntius Saturninus made this for his devoted sister.
EDCS-21300110.summary: A tombstone erected by Lucius Arruntius Saturninus for his sister Arruntia Victoria, who died at age 18.

EDCS-21300112.summary: A funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits for Assuri.

EDCS-21300113.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Allius Saturus lived 70 years. Iulia Restuta made this for her husband.
EDCS-21300113.summary: A tombstone set up by Iulia Restuta for her husband Allius Saturus, who died at age 70.

EDCS-21300114.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Atillia Primulla, who lived 50 years. Octavius Urbanus made this for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-21300114.summary: A tombstone erected by Octavius Urbanus for his mother Atillia Primulla, who died at age 50.

EDCS-21300115.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caius Atinius Sequndus lived 37 years. Caius Atinius Speratus made this for his brother.
EDCS-21300115.summary: A tombstone erected by Caius Atinius Speratus for his brother Caius Atinius Sequndus, who died at age 37.

EDCS-21300116.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Atinius Festus Utica lived 2 years and 11 months.
EDCS-21300116.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Caius Atinius Festus Utica at the age of 2 years and 11 months.

EDCS-21300117.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Attia Fortunata lived 45 years. Gillia Monula her mother made the monument.
EDCS-21300117.summary: A tombstone erected by Gillia Monula for her daughter Attia Fortunata, who died at age 45.

EDCS-21300118.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Atia Iuliana, who lived 1 year, 4 months, and 3 days. Atia her mother made this for her devoted daughter.
EDCS-21300118.summary: A tombstone erected by Atia for her daughter Atia Iuliana, who died at age 1 year, 4 months, and 3 days.

EDCS-21300119.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Audenia Facunda the devoted lived 38 years.
EDCS-21300119.summary: A tombstone for Audenia Facunda, a devoted woman who lived 38 years.

EDCS-21300120.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aufidia Galla lived 40 years. Iulius Victor her husband made this.
EDCS-21300120.summary: A tombstone erected by Iulius Victor for his wife Aufidia Galla, who died at age 40.

EDCS-21300121.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aufidia Ianuaria lived 31 years.
EDCS-21300121.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aufidia Ianuaria, who lived 31 years.

EDCS-21300122.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavius Datus made this for his most rare wife, Aufidia Quarta, who lived 41 years, 3 months, and 27 days.
EDCS-21300122.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Aufidia Quarta and was erected by her husband, Flavius Datus.

EDCS-21300123.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Liberalis made this for his deserving wife, Aviania Saturnina, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-21300123.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Aviania Saturnina and was erected by her husband, Liberalis.

EDCS-21300124.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Avitius Tziro lived 30 years. Avitius Z erected this for his deserving freedman.
EDCS-21300124.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Avitius Tziro and was erected by his patron, Avitius Z.

EDCS-21300125.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. [This is for] Aurelius Alfius, her deserving husband.
EDCS-21300125.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Aurelius Alfius, the deserving husband of the deceased.

EDCS-21300126.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius [made this] for his most beloved parent, Aurelius Eutychianus.
EDCS-21300126.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Aurelius Eutychianus, the beloved parent of Aurelius.

EDCS-21300127.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Aurelius Maximus, son of Lucius Aurelius, lived 46 years. Aurelius Fortunatus arranged for this to be made for his most dutiful brother.
EDCS-21300127.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Lucius Aurelius Maximus and was erected by his brother, Aurelius Fortunatus.

EDCS-21300128.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Aurelius Optatus, son of Lucius Aurelius, lived 50 years. His most dutiful brother, Aurelius Fortunatus, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-21300128.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Lucius Aurelius Optatus and was erected by his brother, Aurelius Fortunatus.

EDCS-21300129.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Aurelius Saturninus lived 32 years. Made through his brothers.
EDCS-21300129.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Lucius Aurelius Saturninus and indicates it was erected by his brothers.

EDCS-21300130.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Aurelius Saturninus lived 22 years. His most dutiful brother, Aurelius Fortunatus, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-21300130.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Caius Aurelius Saturninus and was erected by his brother, Aurelius Fortunatus.

EDCS-21300131.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aurelius Valerianus, who lived 45 years, was laid here by Iulia Gallitina, his wife.
EDCS-21300131.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Valerianus, set up by his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300132.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Victor was made by his father, Victor.
EDCS-21300132.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Victor, set up by his father, Victor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300133.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Maxima, a maiden who lived 13 years and two months, was memorialized by her parents.
EDCS-21300133.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia Maxima, a young maiden, set up by her parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300134.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Maxima, daughter of Lucius, who lived 2 years, was seen to by her most dutiful father, Aurelius Fortunatus.
EDCS-21300134.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia Maxima, a young child, commissioned by her father, Aurelius Fortunatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300135.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Octavia, who lived 80 years and (was in) Lambese for 1 year, was made by Aurelius S for his wife.
EDCS-21300135.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia Octavia, mentioning her age and a period in Lambese, set up by her husband Aurelius S, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300136.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Salvia, who lived 30 years, was memorialized by her daughter, Iulia Salvia, for her deserving mother.
EDCS-21300136.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia Salvia, set up by her daughter Iulia Salvia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300137.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Aurelia Vercellae, sweetest wife, who lived more or less 17 years. I, Anthemus, her husband, was not there, I am not, I do not miss (you).
EDCS-21300137.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia Vercellae, expressing grief and absence by her husband Anthemus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300138.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Verina, who lived 21 years, was placed here by Aurelius Alexander for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-21300138.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia Verina, set up by her husband Aurelius Alexander, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300139.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Baburio Sapancio, brother. Baburius Campanus made this for his dutiful parents.
EDCS-21300139.summary: A funerary epitaph mentioning Baburio Sapancio and Baburius Campanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300140.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Baburio Sedatus, brother.
EDCS-21300140.summary: A funerary epitaph for Baburio Sedatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300141.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Baburia Fortunata, my sister, and her daughter Rogatula.
EDCS-21300141.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Baburia Fortunata and her daughter by an unknown relative.

EDCS-21300142.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Baburia Quartula, my sister, and her son Lucius Valerius.
EDCS-21300142.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Baburia Quartula and her son by an unknown relative.

EDCS-21300143.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Basilia, my wife, who lived 25 years. Claudius Theonicus, her husband, made this for her who deserved well of him. May the earth lie lightly upon you, whoever you are who reads this.
EDCS-21300143.summary: A tombstone set up by Claudius Theonicus for his wife Basilia, who died at age 25.

EDCS-21300145.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Bibia Gentosa, who lived 88 years.
EDCS-21300145.summary: A funerary inscription for Bibia Gentosa, who lived 88 years.

EDCS-21300146.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Quintus Bieratus, son of Marcus, aged 20. Cordo Sabatina Mantua [dedicated this?].
EDCS-21300146.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Bieratus, son of Marcus, who died at age 20.

EDCS-21300147.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Blesia Saturnina of Hadrumetum, who lived 78 years. Her sons Lucius Terentius Saturninus and Lucius Terentius Fortunatus made this.
EDCS-21300147.summary: A tombstone erected by Lucius Terentius Saturninus and Lucius Terentius Fortunatus for their mother Blesia Saturnina, who died at age 78.

EDCS-21300148.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Quintus Botio Urbanus.
EDCS-21300148.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Botio Urbanus.

EDCS-21300150.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Qapia Cosmia, who lived 110 years. Quintus Caecilius Felix placed this for his deserving grandmother.
EDCS-21300150.summary: A tombstone erected by Quintus Caecilius Felix for his grandmother Qapia Cosmia, who died at the remarkable age of 110.

EDCS-21300151.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Lucius Caecilius Ing, who lived 80 years. Verus made this for his deserving father.
EDCS-21300151.summary: A tombstone erected by Verus for his father Lucius Caecilius Ing, who died at age 80.

EDCS-21300152.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Caecilia Castula, my deserving daughter, who lived 11 years.
EDCS-21300152.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by Caecilius Nigrinus for his daughter Caecilia Castula, who died at age 11.

EDCS-21300153.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Caecilius Sextianus lived 31 years. Lucius Caecilius Victor made this for his deserving brother.
EDCS-21300153.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Lucius Caecilius Sextianus, dedicated by his brother Lucius Caecilius Victor.

EDCS-21300154.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caecilius Superatus Secundus Torquatus made this for his deserving father, Lucius Caecilius Venator, who lived 80 years.
EDCS-21300154.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Lucius Caecilius Venator, dedicated by his sons Caecilius Superatus Secundus Torquatus.

EDCS-21300155.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Caecilius Victor lived 58 years. Cornelius [made this?].
EDCS-21300155.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Caius Caecilius Victor, with a partial dedication by Cornelius.

EDCS-21300156.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Donatula made this for herself while alive. She lived 80 years.
EDCS-21300156.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Caecilia Donatula, who set up her own monument during her lifetime.

EDCS-21300157.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Emerita lived 50 years.
EDCS-21300157.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Caecilia Emerita, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-21300158.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Valerius Antoninus made this for his most rare fiancée, Caecilia Getula, who lived 3 years, 7 months, and 17 days.
EDCS-21300158.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Caecilia Getula, dedicated by her fiancé Caius Valerius Antoninus.

EDCS-21300159.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Ingenua lived 70 years. Antonius Fortunatus made this for his mother.
EDCS-21300159.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Caecilia Ingenua, dedicated by her son Antonius Fortunatus.

EDCS-21300160.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Marcella lived 24 years. Marcella made this for her deserving daughter.
EDCS-21300160.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Caecilia Marcella, dedicated by her mother Marcella.

EDCS-21300161.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Saturnina lived 37 years. Sallustius Cel made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-21300161.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Caecilia Saturnina, dedicated by her husband Sallustius Cel.

EDCS-21300162.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Terentius Bassus made this for his deserving wife, Caecilia Secunda, who lived 32 years. She lies here.
EDCS-21300162.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Caecilia Secunda, dedicated by her husband Publius Terentius Bassus.

EDCS-21300163.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilia Severa lived 5 years. Verus made this for his sweet daughter.
EDCS-21300163.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Caecilia Severa, made by her father Verus.

EDCS-21300164.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilia Tertula lived 13 years. Caecilius Felix made this for his deserving daughter.
EDCS-21300164.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a girl named Caecilia Tertula, made by her father Caecilius Felix.

EDCS-21300165.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilia Tertulla lived 50 years. Her sons and sons-in-law made this for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-21300165.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caecilia Tertulla, erected by her sons and sons-in-law.

EDCS-21300166.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilia Veneriosa lived 35 years. Caecilius Legatheius made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-21300166.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caecilia Veneriosa, made by her husband Caecilius Legatheius.

EDCS-21300167.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Caelus Annianus lived 84 years. His sons Caelus Annianus Felix and Donatus and Zibbiena made this.
EDCS-21300167.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Caelus Annianus, erected by his sons.

EDCS-21300169.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Caelus Caelitas lived 13 years.
EDCS-21300169.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Caelus Caelitas, likely erected by his family.

EDCS-21300170.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Decimus Caelius Crescens made this for his deserving wife Julia.
EDCS-21300170.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julia, made by her husband Decimus Caelius Crescens.

EDCS-21300171.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caelia Fortunata, most devoted, lived 35 years, 3 months, and 19 days.
EDCS-21300171.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caelia Fortunata, recording her lifespan with precision.

EDCS-21300172.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Iulius Dexter made this for his loving wife Caelia Generosa.
EDCS-21300172.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caelia Generosa, made by her husband Publius Iulius Dexter.

EDCS-21300173.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Caelia Urbica, mother.
EDCS-21300173.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caelia Urbica, identifying her as a mother.

EDCS-21300174.translation: To the departed spirits. Caesellia Victoria lived 70 years. Cornelius Victor and Marcia, her children, made this for their most devoted mother.
EDCS-21300174.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caesellia Victoria, set up by her children, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300175.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caesennius Primulus lived 6 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Caesennia Satura lived 18 years.
EDCS-21300175.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the short lives of Caesennius Primulus and Caesennia Satura, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300176.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Caesius Felix lived 33 years. Caesius Felix made this for his deserving son; he is buried here.
EDCS-21300176.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Caesius Felix, set up by his father of the same name, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300177.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caetia Donata lived 60 years. Aemilia Saturnina and Aufidius Zabulius made this for their dearest mother.
EDCS-21300177.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caetia Donata, set up by her children, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300178.translation: To the departed spirits. Caetia Tertia lived 25 years. Caetia Donata made this for her sister.
EDCS-21300178.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caetia Tertia, set up by her sister Caetia Donata, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300179.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Calpurnius Flamininus made this for himself while alive. I pleaded cases, fortunate until the third day before the Kalends of [date]. Marriage and children stole all my glory. He lived 75 years.
EDCS-21300179.summary: A self-penned funerary epitaph for Lucius Calpurnius Flamininus, reflecting on his life and achievements, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300180.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caninia Maximina lived 33 years.
EDCS-21300180.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caninia Maximina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300181.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caninia Tertulla lived 37 years. Marcus Liburnius Felix made this for his wife, who had treated him well.
EDCS-21300181.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caninia Tertulla, set up by her husband Marcus Liburnius Felix, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300182.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caninia Victorina lived 7 years. Caninius Victor made this for his daughter, grieving.
EDCS-21300182.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caninia Victorina, set up by her grieving father Caninius Victor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300183.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Capatius Valerianus lived 65 years. Venustus, his son, made this for his dutiful father.
EDCS-21300183.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Capatius Valerianus, set up by his son Venustus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21300184.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Carvillius Fortunatus.
EDCS-21300184.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Carvillius Fortunatus, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-21300185.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Cascellius Optatus, son of Caius, of the Arnensian tribe.
EDCS-21300185.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Cascellius Optatus, identifying his father and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-21300186.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Cassius Marcellinus lived 80 years. His daughter made this.
EDCS-21300186.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of Cassius Marcellinus and that his daughter erected the monument.

EDCS-21300187.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Cassia Faustina, a child, who lived 4 years. Cassia Rufina made this for her devoted daughter.
EDCS-21300187.summary: This funerary epitaph honors the young Cassia Faustina, erected by her mother Cassia Rufina.

EDCS-21300188.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Cassia Valentina lived [duration]. Victor made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-21300188.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cassia Valentina, erected by her husband Victor, mentioning his wife's name.

EDCS-21300189.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Castricia Paulina, who lived 70 years. Her two 'curiae' (colleagues/members of a group) Iulia and Traiana made this for their deserving wife/colleague.
EDCS-21300189.summary: This inscription honors Castricia Paulina, who lived to 70, and was erected by two individuals named Iulia and Traiana, possibly indicating a close relationship or shared membership.

EDCS-21300190.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Catullina lived 3 years, 1 month, 10 days. Iulia Tert, her mother.
EDCS-21300190.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child named Catullina, recording her brief lifespan and erected by her mother, Iulia Tert.

EDCS-21300191.translation: To Caunius Candidus, from the province of Asia Minor, who lived 1595 years (numerically represented as MDVCIM, likely an error or symbolic representation). [He was] a man of good standing (v.c.).
EDCS-21300191.summary: This inscription commemorates Caunius Candidus, possibly a prominent figure from Asia Minor, with a highly unusual recorded lifespan.

EDCS-21300192.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Cecina, his daughter, who lived 13 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Reducta, who lived [duration]. Her father placed this for his daughters.
EDCS-21300192.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument from a father who placed it for two daughters, Cecina and Reducta, one of whom lived to 13 years old.

EDCS-21300193.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Celodia Pupa, who lived 51 years. Her daughter, his heir, made this.
EDCS-21300193.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Celodia Pupa lived 51 years and that her daughter, who was also her heir, erected the monument.

EDCS-21300194.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cirra, a servant from Lepcis, lived 50 years. Bellicus, her husband, made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-21300194.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cirra, set up by her husband Bellicus.

EDCS-21300195.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudius Antonius Primus lived 73 years. Privatus made this for his father.
EDCS-21300195.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudius Antonius Primus, set up by his son Privatus.

EDCS-21300196.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Claudius Baculus, who lived 5 years and 40 days. Teltonius Erosiona made this for his dearest foster-son.
EDCS-21300196.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudius Baculus, a young child, set up by his foster-father.

EDCS-21300197.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Claudius Crescentianus lived 34 years.
EDCS-21300197.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Claudius Crescentianus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-21300198.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Tiberius Claudius Hilarus, also known as Saponius, lived 7 years. Claudius Flavianus made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-21300198.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Hilarus (Saponius), a young child, set up by his father Claudius Flavianus.

EDCS-21300199.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antonia Piperus made this for her devoted son Claudius Pietas, who lived 10 years. To the spirits of the departed. Antonia Piperus made this for Claudia Libosa, who lived 17 years.
EDCS-21300199.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by Antonia Piperus for two children, her son Claudius Pietas and daughter Claudia Libosa.

EDCS-21300200.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Claudius Rogatus lived 90 years. Claudius Victor made this for his deserving father.
EDCS-21300200.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Claudius Rogatus, set up by his son Claudius Victor.

EDCS-21300201.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Claudius Quintus Victor lived 30 years. His son, Cecilius Secundus, made this for his dearest, deserving son.
EDCS-21300201.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudius Quintus Victor, set up by his son Cecilius Secundus.

EDCS-21300202.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Claudia Cara, most loving, who lived 80 years. Sextius Felix placed this.
EDCS-21300202.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Cara, placed by Sextius Felix.

EDCS-21300203.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Claudia Faustina, dutiful, lived 25 years, is buried here. Quintus Claudius Honoratus, son and heir, made this for his most dutiful mother.
EDCS-21300203.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Faustina, set up by her son and heir Quintus Claudius Honoratus.

EDCS-21300204.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gnaeus Badisius Leo made this for his wife Claudia Successa, most humane, most holy, most faithful, most obedient, who lived 37 years, deserving well of him.
EDCS-21300204.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Gnaeus Badisius Leo for his wife Claudia Successa.

EDCS-21300205.translation: To the departed spirits. Claudia Victoria lived 30 years. Caius Petronius Datus, her husband, made this for her, deserving well.
EDCS-21300205.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Caius Petronius Datus for his wife Claudia Victoria.

EDCS-21300206.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. He lived 5 years. His grandfather made this.
EDCS-21300206.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a child who lived 5 years, made by his grandfather.

EDCS-21300207.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Clodia Emerita lived 20 years. She made this.
EDCS-21300207.summary: This funerary inscription records that Clodia Emerita lived 20 years and made the monument herself.

EDCS-21300209.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Clodia Optata lived 45 years. Lurius Felix made this for his most sweet mother.
EDCS-21300209.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Lurius Felix for his mother Clodia Optata.

EDCS-21300210.translation: Clodia Placida lived 43 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-21300210.summary: This funerary inscription records that Clodia Placida lived 43 years and is buried here.

EDCS-21300211.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Clodia Pupa the dutiful lived 23 years.
EDCS-21300211.summary: This funerary inscription records that Clodia Pupa lived 23 years.

EDCS-21300212.translation: To her grandmother, Ces Munatianus [made this] for Clodia Galitta, his best wife.
EDCS-21300212.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Ces Munatianus for his wife Clodia Galitta, referring to her as his grandmother in error or affection.

EDCS-21300213.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cluvius Renatus lived 16 years. Felix made this for his brother.
EDCS-21300213.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Felix for his brother Cluvius Renatus, who died at 16.

EDCS-21300214.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cluvia Fortunula lived 60 years. Valerius Clodianus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-21300214.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Valerius Clodianus for his wife Cluvia Fortunula.

EDCS-21300215.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Cnegilius Heliodorus Fidelis lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-21300215.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Cnegilius Heliodorus Fidelis, recording his name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-21300216.translation: To Comicia Emerita and also to... Comicius Emeritus [in honor of his] parents.
EDCS-21300216.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a dedication by Comicius Emeritus in honor of his parents, including Comicia Emerita.

EDCS-21300217.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudia Rogatina made this for the guild of Fabricius, [as a monument to her] devoted husband; he lived 55 years.
EDCS-21300217.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Claudia Rogatina for her husband, who was devoted and a member of the guild of Fabricius.

EDCS-21300218.translation: To the departed spirits and to Concordia. She lived 28 years.
EDCS-21300218.summary: This is a tombstone for an individual named Concordia, recording her age at death.

EDCS-21300219.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Coponius Proculus made this for himself during his lifetime.
EDCS-21300219.summary: This is a tombstone that Lucius Coponius Proculus prepared for himself while still alive.

EDCS-21300220.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Cornelius Liberalis lived 38 years. Ulpia Emerita his mother and his sons Cornelius Liberalis and Mauritius made this; he is buried here.
EDCS-21300220.summary: This tombstone honors Caius Cornelius Liberalis, recording his age at death and erected by his mother and sons.

EDCS-21300221.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelius Felix made this for his dearest son, Publius Cornelius Saturninus the Younger; he lived 4 years and 3 months.
EDCS-21300221.summary: This tombstone honors Publius Cornelius Saturninus the Younger, recording his age at death and erected by his father, Cornelius Felix.

EDCS-21300222.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Cornelius Secundus, a most devoted father, lived 80 years. Lucius Cornelius Crescentianus made this for [him as] one well-deserving.
EDCS-21300222.summary: This tombstone honors Lucius Cornelius Secundus, recording his age at death and erected by Lucius Cornelius Crescentianus.

EDCS-21300223.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Cornelius Victor lived 80 years. Caius Caecilius Nigrinus made this for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-21300223.summary: This tombstone honors Marcus Cornelius Victor, recording his age at death and erected by his son, Caius Caecilius Nigrinus.

EDCS-21300224.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelia Dativa lived 40 years. Quintus Laelius Maximus, her husband, made this.
EDCS-21300224.summary: This tombstone honors Cornelia Dativa, recording her age at death and erected by her husband, Quintus Laelius Maximus.

EDCS-21300225.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Cornelia Felicia lived 70 years.
EDCS-21300225.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Cornelia Felicia, who lived to be 70 years old.

EDCS-21300226.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Cornelia Rogatina lived 31 years. Her devoted sister made this.
EDCS-21300226.summary: This funerary inscription records that Cornelia Rogatina lived 31 years and was honored by her devoted sister.

EDCS-21300227.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Publius Cossius Gallus lived 92 years. Iulia Rogata made this for her devoted husband.
EDCS-21300227.summary: This funerary monument honors Publius Cossius Gallus, who lived 92 years, and was erected by his devoted wife, Iulia Rogata.

EDCS-21300228.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Crepereius Maximus lived 6 years, Crepereia Proculosa lived 4 years.
EDCS-21300228.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication for Quintus Crepereius Maximus (aged 6) and Crepereia Proculosa (aged 4).

EDCS-21300229.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Crepereia Crispina lived 15 years. Her father, Flavius Felix, made this.
EDCS-21300229.summary: This funerary monument records that Crepereia Crispina lived 15 years and was honored by her father, Flavius Felix.

EDCS-21300230.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Crepereia Crispina lived 9 years, 8 months, and 9 days. Crepereia Crispina made this for her most beloved daughter.
EDCS-21300230.summary: This funerary inscription honors Crepereia Crispina, who died at the age of 9 years, 8 months, and 9 days, and was dedicated by her mother of the same name.

EDCS-21300231.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Crepereia Donata, most loving wife. She lived 60 years.
EDCS-21300231.summary: This funerary dedication is for Crepereia Donata, a devoted wife who lived 60 years.

EDCS-21300232.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Crepereia Lucisca lived 18 years.
EDCS-21300232.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Crepereia Lucisca, who lived 18 years.

EDCS-21300233.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Crepereia Quieta lived 30 years.
EDCS-21300233.summary: This funerary monument records that Crepereia Quieta lived 30 years.

EDCS-21300234.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Cretia Ianuaria lived 30 years and 3 days.
EDCS-21300234.summary: This funerary dedication records that Cretia Ianuaria lived 30 years and 3 days.

EDCS-21600158.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Chrysis, who deserved well. Publius Glycerus, her husband.
EDCS-21600158.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Publius Glycerus to his wife Chrysis.

EDCS-21600159.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Cupius Cecilianus lived 40 years. He is well.
EDCS-21600159.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Cupius Cecilianus, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-21600160.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Marcus Cuspius Felix, pious. He lived 80 years. Cuspia Faustina, daughter of Marcus, made [this] for her most excellent father.
EDCS-21600160.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Cuspia Faustina for her father, Marcus Cuspius Felix, who lived to be 80.

EDCS-21600161.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Publius Cuspius lived 7 years. His mother arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-21600161.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Publius Cuspius at the age of 7, with his mother arranging the monument.

EDCS-21600162.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. C. Dama lived 80 years. Novellus set [this] up for his father.
EDCS-21600162.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates C. Dama, who lived to 80, and was set up by his son Novellus.

EDCS-21600163.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Cattia Marciosa lived 7 years. Her brothers with [their] mother.
EDCS-21600163.summary: This epitaph marks the death of Cattia Marciosa at age 7, with her brothers and mother as dedicants.

EDCS-21600164.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Calvidius Felix lived.
EDCS-21600164.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Lucius Calvidius Felix.

EDCS-21600165.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Dativa, sister, who deserved well. Her brother set [this] up. She lived 15 years and 8 months.
EDCS-21600165.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a brother for his sister Dativa, who died at age 15 years and 8 months.

EDCS-21600166.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Marcus Demetrius Felix, most rare father, who deserved well. His son Demetrius.
EDCS-21600166.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Marcus Demetrius Felix, described as a rare and deserving father, by his son Demetrius.

EDCS-21600167.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Demetria Polla, daughter of Lucius Demetrius, pious. She lived 12 years. Lucius Demetrius Martialis set [this] up for his well-deserving sister, from 500 denarii.
EDCS-21600167.summary: This funerary monument, funded with 500 denarii, was erected by Lucius Demetrius Martialis for his sister Demetria Polla, who died at age 12.

EDCS-21600168.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Dentonius Adiutor lived 70 years. Tuccia Victoria made this for her most rare wife. To the Spirits of the Departed. Tuccia Reposta lived 30 years. Tuccia Victoria made this for her most rare daughter.
EDCS-21600168.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Tuccia Reposta and her mother, with dedications made by Tuccia Victoria.

EDCS-21600169.summary: This inscription is a simple name inscription, likely for a tomb, identifying Diodorus Gemellus.

EDCS-21600170.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Domitius Ammonianus lived 30 years. Uturpia Gemina made this for her husband.
EDCS-21600170.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Domitius Ammonianus and was erected by his wife, Uturpia Gemina.

EDCS-21600172.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Domitius Iulianus lived 40 years. Aemilia Spenika placed this for her most beloved husband.
EDCS-21600172.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Domitius Iulianus and was erected by his wife, Aemilia Spenika.

EDCS-21600173.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Domitius Mansuetus lived 70 years. His son Domitius Nestor made this for his most dutiful father. He lies here.
EDCS-21600173.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Domitius Mansuetus and was erected by his son, Domitius Nestor.

EDCS-21600174.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Domitius Maximus.
EDCS-21600174.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription that names Domitius Maximus.

EDCS-21600175.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Domitius Castor for his deserving colleague Domitius Sacer.
EDCS-21600175.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Domitius Castor honoring his colleague Domitius Sacer.

EDCS-21600176.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Domitius Saturninus lived 23 years. He lies here.
EDCS-21600176.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Domitius Saturninus.

EDCS-21600178.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Domitius Valens placed this for Sullenia Ianuaria, his most rare wife. She lived 70 years.
EDCS-21600178.summary: This funerary inscription records Gaius Domitius Valens dedicating a monument to his wife, Sullenia Ianuaria.

EDCS-21600179.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Domitia Saturnina lived 40 years. Genialis made this for his wife. Papiria Saturnina lived 7 years. He made this for his dearest daughter.
EDCS-21600179.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Papiria Saturnina and her mother Domitia Saturnina, with dedications made by Genialis.

EDCS-21600180.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Domitia Silvana lived 51 years. Julius Vitalis made this for his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-21600180.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Domitia Silvana and dedicated by her son, Julius Vitalis.

EDCS-21600181.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Domitia Successa lived 70 years. Claudius Victor made this for his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-21600181.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Domitia Successa and dedicated by her son, Claudius Victor.

EDCS-21600182.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Domitia Valentina lived 35 years. Caecilius Dexter made this for his wife.
EDCS-21600182.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Domitia Valentina and dedicated by her husband, Caecilius Dexter.

EDCS-21600183.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Domitia Victorina lived 65 years. Julia Pannonia and Victoria made this for their daughter.
EDCS-21600183.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Domitia Victorina and dedicated by her daughters, Julia Pannonia and Victoria.

EDCS-21600184.translation: Her mother made this for Duronia Fortunata, who lived 38 years.
EDCS-21600184.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Duronia Fortunata and dedicated by her mother.

EDCS-21600185.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Egnatia Secundula lived [---] years.
EDCS-21600185.summary: A funerary epitaph for Egnatia Secundula, with her lifespan partially illegible.

EDCS-21600186.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Egrilius Fuscus lived 75 years. Messia Monna made this for her husband.
EDCS-21600186.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gaius Egrilius Fuscus and dedicated by his wife, Messia Monna.

EDCS-21600187.translation: To the spirits of the departed. His mother made this for Aelius Maximus, who lived 7 years.
EDCS-21600187.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Aelius Maximus and dedicated by his mother.

EDCS-21600188.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aegrilia Recepta made this for her well-deserving son, Aegrilus Receptus, who lived 3 years.
EDCS-21600188.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Aegrilus Receptus and dedicated by his mother, Aegrilia Recepta.

EDCS-21600189.translation: To the spirits of the departed. His son made this for Aegrilius Urbanus, who lived 100 years.
EDCS-21600189.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the exceptional lifespan of Aegrilius Urbanus and dedicated by his son.

EDCS-21600190.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Erasinus lived 18 years.
EDCS-21600190.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Erasinus at the age of 18.

EDCS-21600191.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Hermes lived 25 years. Hiacintus placed this in memory of his dearest brother.
EDCS-21600191.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Hiacintus for his brother Hermes, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-21600192.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Eutices lived 40 years. Flavia Tharsa for her husband.
EDCS-21600192.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Flavia Tharsa for her husband Eutices, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-21600193.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Candida made this for her well-deserving freedman Eutychianus.
EDCS-21600193.summary: This is a tombstone inscription by Iulia Candida for her freedman Eutychianus.

EDCS-21600194.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Fabio Com lived 23 years. His father made this for his son.
EDCS-21600194.summary: This is a tombstone inscription by a father for his son Lucius Fabio Com, who died at 23.

EDCS-21600195.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Fabius Maximus, a veteran, lived 55 years. Cecilia Donatula made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-21600195.summary: This is a tombstone inscription by Cecilia Donatula for her husband, the veteran Lucius Fabius Maximus, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-21600196.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Fabius Museus lived 50 years. Munia Rogatula made this for her deserving wife.
EDCS-21600196.summary: This is a tombstone inscription by Munia Rogatula for her husband Lucius Fabius Museus, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-21600197.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Fabius Rogatus lived 85 years. Titinia Emerita for her dearest husband.
EDCS-21600197.summary: This is a tombstone inscription by Titinia Emerita for her husband Gaius Fabius Rogatus, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-21600198.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fabius Saturninus lived 38 years. His heir made this.
EDCS-21600198.summary: This is a tombstone inscription by the heir of Fabius Saturninus, who lived 38 years.

EDCS-21600199.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fabia Matrona lived 45 years. Fabius Severus made this for his most dutiful mother; she is buried here.
EDCS-21600199.summary: This is a tombstone inscription by Fabius Severus for his mother Fabia Matrona, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-21600200.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Fabia Victoria lived 2 years; her father and mother [dedicated this].
EDCS-21600200.summary: A funerary dedication to Fabia Victoria, set up by her parents, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21600201.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Fabricius Donatus lived 15 years; his father Fabricius S. made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-21600201.summary: A funerary dedication to Lucius Fabricius Donatus, made by his father, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21600202.translation: Fabricius Felicius, his son and heir, made this.
EDCS-21600202.summary: A funerary dedication to Fabricius Felicius, made by his son and heir, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21600203.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Fabricia Emerita, most worthy patroness, Fabricius Ianuarius and Rufina [dedicated this].
EDCS-21600203.summary: A funerary dedication to Fabricia Emerita, a patroness, made by Fabricius Ianuarius and Rufina, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21600205.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Felicia at the age of 16, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21600206.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Felicus lived 10 months; his father Quincius made this for him.
EDCS-21600206.summary: A funerary dedication to Felicus, who lived 10 months, made by his father, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21600207.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Felicus Victor Meridianus; his most devoted mother Marcia Victoria [dedicated this].
EDCS-21600207.summary: A funerary dedication to Felicus Victor Meridianus, made by his mother, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21600208.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavia Flora lived 20 years and Lonilius Victorinus, her son, lived 3 years; her devoted husband set this up.
EDCS-21600208.summary: A funerary dedication to Flavia Flora and her young son Lonilius Victorinus, set up by her husband, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21600209.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavius Amandus lived 9 months; he is buried here.
EDCS-21600209.summary: A funerary inscription for Flavius Amandus, who lived 9 months, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21600210.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment, possibly recording the names of two individuals, Flavius Amandus and T. Honoratus, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21600211.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Apollinaris lived 75 years. Titus Aelius Musicus, freedman of Augustus, made this for his dearest, well-deserving friend at his own expense.
EDCS-21600211.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating Titus Flavius Apollinaris, erected by his friend Titus Aelius Musicus, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-21600212.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Flavius Castus made this tomb for his deserving husband.
EDCS-21600212.summary: A funerary epitaph with a tomb inscription, commissioned by a wife for her husband, Flavius Castus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-21600214.translation: Titus Flavius Datus for himself while alive.
EDCS-21600214.summary: A funerary epitaph, likely a self-dedicated tombstone, by Titus Flavius Datus, probably from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-21600215.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Flavius Felix lived 2 years, 8 months, and 3 days. Flavius Felix made this for his son.
EDCS-21600215.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child named Flavius Felix, who died at a very young age, set up by his father, also named Flavius Felix.

EDCS-21600216.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Flavius Maximus lived 18 years. Flavius Felix made this for his brother.
EDCS-21600216.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavius Maximus, who died at 18, erected by his brother Flavius Felix.

EDCS-21600217.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Restutus lived 10 years. Iulia Hospita made this for her most dutiful son. Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Flavianus lived 5 years. Iulia Hospita made this for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-21600217.summary: A funerary epitaph for two sons, Titus Flavius Restutus and Titus Flavius Flavianus, erected by their mother Iulia Hospita.

EDCS-21600218.translation: Titus Flavius Saturninus for his son Fortunatus, who lived 21 years, 1 month, and 11 days.
EDCS-21600218.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fortunatus, son of Titus Flavius Saturninus, who died at age 21.

EDCS-21600219.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Saturus lived 20 years. Flavius Datus for his brother.
EDCS-21600219.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Saturus, who died at age 20, erected by his brother Flavius Datus.

EDCS-21600220.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Flavia Anucia made this for her well-deserving mother Flavia Tertia, who lived 65 years.
EDCS-21600220.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Tertia, who lived 65 years, commissioned by her daughter Flavia Anucia.

EDCS-21600221.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed for the innocent infant Flavia Aurelia, who lived 11 months.
EDCS-21600221.summary: A funerary epitaph for an infant named Flavia Aurelia, who died at 11 months of age.

EDCS-21600222.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Fabia Clementia lived 25 years. Cornelia Rogata, her excellent mother, placed this.
EDCS-21600222.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fabia Clementia, set up by her mother, Cornelia Rogata.

EDCS-21600223.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Flavia Dativa lived 20 years. Iulius Aemeritus, for his most beloved wife.
EDCS-21600223.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Dativa, erected by her husband, Iulius Aemeritus.

EDCS-21600224.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Flavia Donata lived 35 years. Vibius Saturninus, her husband.
EDCS-21600224.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Donata, placed by her husband, Vibius Saturninus.

EDCS-21600225.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Flavia Fortunata lived 60 years. Cornelius Felix and Octavia Rogata, her son-in-law and daughter, made this.
EDCS-21600225.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Fortunata, erected by her son-in-law Cornelius Felix and daughter Octavia Rogata.

EDCS-21600226.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Flavia Fortunata, daughter of Lucius, who lived 35 years and 2 months, his most rare wife. Marcus Licinius Saturninus.
EDCS-21600226.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Fortunata, set up by her husband Marcus Licinius Saturninus.

EDCS-21600227.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Flavia Germana, daughter of Marcus, who lived 36 years. Publius Octavius Florus, his heir, made this for her as she deserved from his own resources.
EDCS-21600227.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Germana, erected by her heir Publius Octavius Florus.

EDCS-21600228.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Flavia Ingenua lived 16 years. Titus Flavius Successus made this for his most devoted sister.
EDCS-21600228.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Ingenua, erected by her brother Titus Flavius Successus.

EDCS-21600229.translation: For the memory of Flavia Iuliose, his most devoted wife. Marcus Servilius Rogatus made this.
EDCS-21600229.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Iuliose, dedicated by her husband Marcus Servilius Rogatus.

EDCS-21600230.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Flavia Marcella lived 28 years. Flavius Datus, for his sister.
EDCS-21600230.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Marcella, erected by her brother Flavius Datus.

EDCS-21600231.translation: Flavia Marcia lived 22 years. Flavius Datus, for his sister.
EDCS-21600231.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Marcia, erected by her brother Flavius Datus.

EDCS-21600232.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavia Maxima lived 45 years. Caelius Successus made this for his deserving wife, sweet to him.
EDCS-21600232.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Flavia Maxima by her husband, Caelius Successus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21600233.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavia Optata lies here. She lived 10 years, 11 months, and 10 days. Titus Flavius Pomponianus, her father, made this in recognition of her outstanding character, her singular modesty, and her beauty.
EDCS-21600233.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Optata, set up by her father, Titus Flavius Pomponianus, highlighting her virtues and beauty.

EDCS-21600234.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavia Restuta lived 1 year and 10 months. Pompeius Restutus, father, and Aticinia, mother, made this for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-21600234.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Restuta, erected by her parents, Pompeius Restutus and Aticinia.

EDCS-21600235.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Her husband made this for Flavia Secundula. She lived 20 years.
EDCS-21600235.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Secundula, who lived 20 years, dedicated by her husband.

EDCS-21600236.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavia Sterceia lived 87 years. She lies here.
EDCS-21600236.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Sterceia, recording her death at the age of 87.

EDCS-21600237.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavia Tertia lived 27 years. Quintus Arruntius Datus, her husband, made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-21600237.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Tertia, who lived 27 years, dedicated by her husband, Quintus Arruntius Datus.

EDCS-21600238.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavia Tertia lived 20 years. Flavius Paulinus made this for his deserving daughter.
EDCS-21600238.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Tertia, who lived 20 years, dedicated by her father, Flavius Paulinus.

EDCS-21600239.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavia Tranquilla lived 55 years. Lucius Domitius Secundus made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-21600239.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Tranquilla, who lived 55 years, dedicated by her husband, Lucius Domitius Secundus.

EDCS-21600240.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavia Victoria lived 35 years. Her heirs made this.
EDCS-21600240.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Victoria, who lived 35 years, erected by her heirs.

EDCS-21600241.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Flavia Victoria. She lived 18 years. Her husband made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-21600241.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Victoria, who lived 18 years, dedicated by her husband.

EDCS-21600242.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia Urbana lived 40 years. Her son Tiberius Flavius M, a veteran, lived 46 years and a month. Claudia made this for her wife.
EDCS-21600242.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Flavia Urbana and Tiberius Flavius M by their son and wife, respectively.

EDCS-21600243.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia Zaea lived 80 years. Her son Manilius made this for his mother.
EDCS-21600243.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Flavia Zaea by her son Manilius.

EDCS-21600244.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Florius Processus lived 20 years. Florius Crescens made this for his dearest son. He is buried here.
EDCS-21600244.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Florius Processus by his father Florius Crescens.

EDCS-21600245.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Folius Rogatus lived 5 years for his dearest son.
EDCS-21600245.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Quintus Folius Rogatus, son of an unnamed father.

EDCS-21600246.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fortunata lived 90 years. Her brothers made this for their sister.
EDCS-21600246.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Fortunata by her brothers.

EDCS-21600247.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fortunata Natalica lived 10 years. Caius Iulius [made this for her].
EDCS-21600247.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Fortunata Natalica by Caius Iulius.

EDCS-21600248.translation: [...lived] years. Iulius Su Libo and Fortunatus made this monument for their parent for 1200 sesterces, according to his instructions. He is buried here.
EDCS-21600248.summary: A funerary monument endowed with 1200 sesterces for a parent, erected by their sons Iulius Su Libo and Fortunatus.

EDCS-21600249.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Frugius Fusculus lived 40 years. His wife Martilla made this.
EDCS-21600249.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Frugius Fusculus by his wife Martilla.

EDCS-21600250.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fuficia Rustica lived 35 years. Lucius Antonius Quirinalis made this for his most rare wife.
EDCS-21600250.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Fuficia Rustica by her husband Lucius Antonius Quirinalis.

EDCS-21600251.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Fullonius Lucustarius lived 35 years. Valeria Saturnina made this for her deserving son-in-law.
EDCS-21600251.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Caius Fullonius Lucustarius by his mother-in-law Valeria Saturnina.

EDCS-21600252.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Fullonius Paulus, father, lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-21600252.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death and burial of Gaius Fullonius Paulus, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-21600253.translation: Marcus Fulonius Proculus made [this] for the most dutiful.
EDCS-21600253.summary: A dedication by Marcus Fulonius Proculus, likely for a family member, possibly a wife or mother.

EDCS-21600254.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Fulsinia Saturnina lived 18 years; Mlasina lived 6 years.
EDCS-21600254.summary: A funerary inscription recording the deaths of Fulsinia Saturnina (aged 18) and Mlasina (aged 6).

EDCS-21600255.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Fulvia Procula lived 80 years; Iulia Procula set this up for her mother.
EDCS-21600255.summary: A funerary inscription by Iulia Procula for her mother, Fulvia Procula, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-21600256.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Fulvia Procula lived 27 years; Fulvia Casta made [this] for her dearest sister.
EDCS-21600256.summary: A funerary inscription by Fulvia Casta for her sister, Fulvia Procula, who died at the age of 27.

EDCS-21600257.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Fundania Redducta lived 59 years and 8 months and 13 days; Caecilius Emeritus made [this] for his dearest mother.
EDCS-21600257.summary: A funerary inscription by Caecilius Emeritus for his mother, Fundania Redducta, who lived 59 years, 8 months, and 13 days.

EDCS-21600258.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Gaia Iulia, daughter of Gaius Iulius Celer, who lived 16 years and 2 days; Gaius Iulius Flaccus for his most dutiful wife and one who deserved well of him, and Gaius Iulius Celer, father, for his most dutiful daughter, made [this].
EDCS-21600258.summary: A joint funerary inscription by a husband and father for Gaia Iulia, who died at 16 years and 2 days.

EDCS-21600259.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Galeria Pieris lived 40 years; Gaius Galerius made [this] for his sister.
EDCS-21600259.summary: A funerary inscription by Gaius Galerius for his sister, Galeria Pieris, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-21600260.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Galeria Vera. Certus, her brother, made [this].
EDCS-21600260.summary: A funerary inscription by Certus for his sister, Galeria Vera.

EDCS-21600261.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gallicia Victoria lived 32 years; Firmius Campanus made [this] for his dearest wife.
EDCS-21600261.summary: A funerary inscription by Firmius Campanus for his wife, Gallicia Victoria, who died at the age of 32.

EDCS-21600262.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Titus Gargilius Ianuarius of the Palatine tribe, his heir, made this for his well-deserving friend at a cost of one million sesterces.
EDCS-21600262.summary: A funerary monument set up by an heir for a well-deserving friend, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-21600264.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Gargilius Felix made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-21600264.summary: A self-funded funerary monument for Quintus Gargilius Felix, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-21600265.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gargilius Hilarus lived 50 years; his brother Gargilius Estricatus made this.
EDCS-21600265.summary: A funerary monument for Gargilius Hilarus, erected by his brother, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-21600266.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gargilius Primus; Fortunata, his devoted wife, made this. He lived 60 years.
EDCS-21600266.summary: A funerary monument for Gargilius Primus, erected by his wife, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-21600267.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Gargilius Victor lived 25 years; his father Gargilius Felix made this.
EDCS-21600267.summary: A funerary monument for Quintus Gargilius Victor, erected by his father, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-21600268.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Gellius Victor lived [Gelli?] years.
EDCS-21600268.summary: A funerary monument for Marcus Gellius Victor, indicating a lifespan possibly related to the Gelli family, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.
