EDCS-21600269.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gellia Saturnina lived 26 years. Gellius [erected this?].
EDCS-21600269.summary: A funerary monument for Gellia Saturnina, likely erected by a relative named Gellius, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-21600270.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Ulpius Salvius made this for his most rare wife, Geminia Priscula, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-21600270.summary: A funerary monument for Geminia Priscula, erected by her husband, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-21600271.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Silius Alexander made this for his mother, Geminia Saturnina, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-21600271.summary: A funerary monument for Geminia Saturnina, erected by her son, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-21600272.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Geminia Saturnina lived 70 years. Pomponia Saturnina made this.
EDCS-21600272.summary: A funerary monument for Geminia Saturnina, erected by Pomponia Saturnina, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-21600273.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Geminia Victorina lived 27 years. Sabinius Emeritus made this for his sweetest wife.
EDCS-21600273.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Geminia Victorina, dedicated by her husband Sabinius Emeritus.

EDCS-21600274.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Glitia Berectina lived 80 years.
EDCS-21600274.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Glitia Berectina.

EDCS-21600275.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Golius Privatus lived 25 years. Golius Optatus, his brother, made this.
EDCS-21600275.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Golius Privatus, made by his brother Golius Optatus.

EDCS-21600276.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Golulius Golulianus lived 35 years.
EDCS-21600276.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Golulius Golulianus.

EDCS-21600277.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Grania Victoria lived 30 years.
EDCS-21600277.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Grania Victoria.

EDCS-21600278.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Helvio Valentino lived 23 years. Helvius Valentinus, his father, made this.
EDCS-21600278.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Helvio Valentino, made by his father Helvius Valentinus.

EDCS-21600279.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Helvia Modia lived 45 years. Aelius Emeritus Berenicianus made this for his most cherished mother.
EDCS-21600279.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Helvia Modia, dedicated by her son Aelius Emeritus Berenicianus.

EDCS-21600280.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Herenia Accepta lived 70 years. Aurelia Terentia made this for her mother.
EDCS-21600280.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Herenia Accepta, made by her mother Aurelia Terentia.

EDCS-21600281.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Herenia Maiorica lived 25 years. Messius, her uncle, made this.
EDCS-21600281.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Herenia Maiorica, made by her uncle Messius.

EDCS-21600282.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Herennia Statia lived 15 years, 6 months, and 22 days. Victor, her father, and Marisa, her mother, placed this for their most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-21600282.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Herennia Statia, detailing her age and placement by her parents.

EDCS-21600283.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Herennia Tertia lived 12 years. Peregrinia Fortunata, her mother.
EDCS-21600283.summary: A funerary dedication to Herennia Tertia, who died at age 12, by her mother.

EDCS-21600284.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Antonius Celtiber made this for his dearest wife, Hertilia Redducta.
EDCS-21600284.summary: A funerary dedication to Hertilia Redducta by her husband, Gaius Antonius Celtiber.

EDCS-21600285.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. The dearest mother made this for her son, Helvius Felix, Hertilia Redducta.
EDCS-21600285.summary: A funerary dedication to Helvius Felix by his mother, Hertilia Redducta.

EDCS-21600286.translation: To the spirits of the departed, to the deserving Hilarus. He lived 40 years. Ict made this.
EDCS-21600286.summary: A funerary dedication to Hilarus, who lived 40 years, by Ict.

EDCS-21600287.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Homidius Agricola lived 32 years. His wife made this.
EDCS-21600287.summary: A funerary dedication to Quintus Homidius Agricola, who lived 32 years, by his wife.

EDCS-21600289.translation: Horatia, daughter of Quintus, had this made for the genius of her most gentle and loving husband and for her Iuno, Lucius Spellatius Saturninus.
EDCS-21600289.summary: A dedication related to the genius and Iuno of Lucius Spellatius Saturninus, commissioned by his wife Horatia.

EDCS-21600290.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Hortensius Donatus lived 25 years. His heir Arruntius Felix made this.
EDCS-21600290.summary: A funerary dedication to Marcus Hortensius Donatus, who lived 25 years, by his heir Arruntius Felix.

EDCS-21600291.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Maximus made this for his most dutiful brother, Hostilius Fortunatus.
EDCS-21600291.summary: A funerary dedication to Hostilius Fortunatus by his brother, Maximus.

EDCS-21600292.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Ianuaria lived 14 years. Gaius Julius Victorinus [made this] for his most rare wife.
EDCS-21600292.summary: A funerary dedication to Ianuaria, who died at age 14, by her husband Gaius Julius Victorinus.

EDCS-21600293.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To G[---] Ianuariatas, his sweetest son. He lived 10 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-21600293.summary: A funerary dedication to G[---] Ianuariatas, who died at age 10, by his parent.

EDCS-21600294.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Indagius lived for 13 years. His mother made this.
EDCS-21600294.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Indagius at age 13, made by his mother.

EDCS-21600295.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Iulius lived for 42 years.
EDCS-21600295.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Caius Iulius at age 42.

EDCS-21600296.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Iulius lived for 50 years. Iulia Aemisina made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-21600296.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Caius Iulius at age 50, made by his wife Iulia Aemisina.

EDCS-21600297.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Iulius Asellius lived for 27 years. Asellia Ianuaria his mother, and his brothers Iulius Pomponianus and Fortunatus made this.
EDCS-21600297.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Asellius, who died at age 27, made by his mother and brothers.

EDCS-21600298.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Iulius Expeditus lived for 65 years. Asellia Ianuaria his wife, and his sons and heirs Iulius Pomponianus and Fortunatus made this for their most exceptional father.
EDCS-21600298.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Expeditus, who died at age 65, made by his wife and sons.

EDCS-21600299.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Iulius Ianuarius lived for 21 years. Caius Iulius Expeditus made this for his sweetest son.
EDCS-21600299.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Ianuarius, who died at age 21, made by his father Caius Iulius Expeditus.

EDCS-21600300.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Iulius Atticus lived for 25 years. Caius Iulius Saturninus made this for his deserving brother.
EDCS-21600300.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Atticus, who died at age 25, made by his brother Caius Iulius Saturninus.

EDCS-21600302.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Iulius Caprarius lived for 61 years. Timinius Rogatus made this for his deserving son-in-law.
EDCS-21600302.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Caprarius, who died at age 61, made by his son-in-law Timinius Rogatus.

EDCS-21600303.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Iulius Castresis. He lived 19 years and 5 months.
EDCS-21600303.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Castresis, who died at age 19 and 5 months.

EDCS-21600304.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publius Iulius Castus lived for 45 years. Aemilia Felicissima made this for her good husband. May your bones rest well; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-21600304.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Iulius Castus, who died at age 45, made by his wife Aemilia Felicissima.

EDCS-21600305.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Celer lived 37 years. His mother made this for her sweetest son.
EDCS-21600305.summary: A funerary epitaph by a mother for her son, Gaius Julius Celer, who died at age 37.

EDCS-21600306.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Domitus lived 75 years. Iulius Petronianus and Agrippa made this for their deserving father.
EDCS-21600306.summary: A funerary epitaph by two sons, Iulius Petronianus and Agrippa, for their father, Gaius Julius Domitus, who died at age 75.

EDCS-21600307.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Donatus lived 40 years. Gelia Victoria made this for her deserving wife.
EDCS-21600307.summary: A funerary epitaph by Gelia Victoria for her deserving wife, Gaius Julius Donatus, who died at age 40.

EDCS-21600308.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Donatus lived 45 years.
EDCS-21600308.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gaius Julius Donatus, who died at age 45.

EDCS-21600309.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Faustinus lived 9 years. His father, Gaius Julius Felix, made this for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-21600309.summary: A funerary epitaph by a father, Gaius Julius Felix, for his son, Gaius Julius Faustinus, who died at age 9.

EDCS-21600310.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Felix made this for his dearest wife, Valeria Tertula, who lived 40 years, in memory of his devotion.
EDCS-21600310.summary: A funerary epitaph by Iulius Felix for his wife, Valeria Tertula, who died at age 40, commemorating his devotion.

EDCS-21600312.translation: Lived 20 years. Her husband, Gaius Julius Felix, made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-21600312.summary: A funerary epitaph by a husband, Gaius Julius Felix, for his wife, who died at age 20.

EDCS-21600313.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Fortunatus lived 47 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-21600313.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Fortunatus, who died at age 47, marking his burial place.

EDCS-21600314.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Hyginus lived 25 years. Victoria made this.
EDCS-21600314.summary: A funerary epitaph by Victoria for Gaius Julius Hyginus, who died at age 25.

EDCS-21600315.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. His freedman, Bonus, made this for his most devoted patron, Iulio Ianuario, a deserving cavalryman.
EDCS-21600315.summary: A funerary epitaph by a freedman, Bonus, for his patron, Iulio Ianuario, who was a deserving cavalryman.

EDCS-21600316.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Julius Ianuarius lived 76 years and [dedicated this to] Claudia Victoria, his wife.
EDCS-21600316.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Ianuarius and his wife Claudia Victoria, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21600317.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Julius Laetus lived 85 years. His heirs made [this].
EDCS-21600317.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Gaius Julius Laetus lived 85 years and that his heirs erected the monument.

EDCS-21600318.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulius Macer lived 5 years. His father S. made [this].
EDCS-21600318.summary: This funerary epitaph states that Iulius Macer lived 5 years and was commemorated by his father.

EDCS-21600319.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Julius Marcellus lived 57 years. Helvidia Saturnina made [this] for her most beloved husband.
EDCS-21600319.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life span of Gaius Julius Marcellus and was dedicated by his wife, Helvidia Saturnina.

EDCS-21600320.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulius Martialis lived 25 years. Salvia, his most beloved wife, made [this] for him, deserving.
EDCS-21600320.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Iulius Martialis, who lived 25 years, and was dedicated by his wife Salvia.

EDCS-21600321.translation: To the Divine Shades. Whoever you are passing by, who reads the inscription written, I beg you, touched by piety, to say: 'May the earth be light for you, Ianuaria.' She lived 65 years. Iulius Messor, and Iulius Rufus, and Ianuarius made [this] for their most pious mother.
EDCS-21600321.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ianuaria, who lived 65 years, with a plea for passers-by to wish her peace, and was dedicated by her husband Iulius Messor and sons Rufus and Ianuarius.

EDCS-21600323.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caius Iuli Olympi lived 50 years. Herennia Accepta made [this] for her well-deserving wife.
EDCS-21600323.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life span of Caius Iuli Olympi and was dedicated by his wife, Herennia Accepta.

EDCS-21600324.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulio Pio lived three years. Faustus, his father, [made this] for his deserving son.
EDCS-21600324.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Iulio Pio lived for three years and was commemorated by his father, Faustus.

EDCS-21600325.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caius Iulius Porcianus lived 21 years. Iulius Ianuarius made [this] for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-21600325.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Caius Iulius Porcianus lived 21 years and was dedicated by his father, Iulius Ianuarius.

EDCS-21600326.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulius Proculus [lived] 45 years.
EDCS-21600326.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph stating that Iulius Proculus lived 45 years.

EDCS-21600327.translation: Marcus Iulius Collina Proculus made (this) for the most dutiful.
EDCS-21600327.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Marcus Iulius Collina Proculus, likely for a female relative or deity.

EDCS-21600328.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Saturnina arranged for this to be made for her dearest husband Iulius Regulus, who lived 70 years.
EDCS-21600328.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulius Regulus, who lived 70 years, and was arranged by his wife Iulia Saturnina.

EDCS-21600329.translation: Iulius Rogatus made (this) for his most dutiful father, who lived 42 years.
EDCS-21600329.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of a father, who lived 42 years, and was made by his son Iulius Rogatus.

EDCS-21600330.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulius Rogatus made (this) for his father C., who lived 75 years.
EDCS-21600330.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulius Rogatus's father, named C., who lived 75 years, and was made by his son.

EDCS-21600331.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Rogatus lived 80 years; his sons, his heirs, made this at their own expense.
EDCS-21600331.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Caius Iulius Rogatus, who lived 80 years, and was made by his sons.

EDCS-21600332.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For his dearest son Iulius Donatus, who lived 27 years, Iulius Rogatus.
EDCS-21600332.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulius Donatus, who lived 27 years, and was made by his father Iulius Rogatus.

EDCS-21600333.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Marcellina made (this) for her most excellent husband Caius Iulius Romanus, who lived 39 years.
EDCS-21600333.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Caius Iulius Romanus, who lived 39 years, and was made by his wife Iulia Marcellina.

EDCS-21600334.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Maiorica made (this) for her deserving brother Caius Iulius Salvianus, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-21600334.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Caius Iulius Salvianus, who lived 40 years, and was made by his sister Iulia Maiorica.

EDCS-21600335.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Saturninus lived 40 years; Sextia Fuscinila, his wife.
EDCS-21600335.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulius Saturninus, who lived 40 years, and was made by his wife Sextia Fuscinila.

EDCS-21600336.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Rogata, heir, (made this) for her husband Caius Iulius Saturninus, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-21600336.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Caius Iulius Saturninus, who lived 40 years, and was made by his wife and heir Iulia Rogata.

EDCS-21600337.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius Saturninus lived 21 years. Julius Regulus and Iulia Saturnina made [this] for their son. Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Claros lived 27 years. Iulius Regulus and Iulia Saturnina made [this] for their deserving son.
EDCS-21600337.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of two sons, Iulius Saturninus and Iulius Claros, with parents Julius Regulus and Iulia Saturnina as dedicants.

EDCS-21600338.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Iulius Saturus lived 2 years. His mother, Cecilia Victoria, [made this].
EDCS-21600338.summary: This funerary epitaph records the early death of Gaius Iulius Saturus, dedicated by his mother Cecilia Victoria.

EDCS-21600339.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Iulius Severus lived 80 years. His most devoted daughter, Iulia Antonina, made [this].
EDCS-21600339.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Gaius Iulius Severus, dedicated by his devoted daughter Iulia Antonina.

EDCS-21600340.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Iulius Silvanus lived 17 years. His deserving paternal uncle's sons, Iulius Marinus and Magnus, made [this] for their deserving cousin.
EDCS-21600340.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Gaius Iulius Silvanus, dedicated by his cousins Iulius Marinus and Magnus.

EDCS-21600341.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Iulius Silvanus, son of Quintus, lived 65 years, and Iulia Domitia, daughter, lived 9 years. Quintus Iulius Cens made [this] for his dearest father and daughter.
EDCS-21600341.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Quintus Iulius Silvanus and his daughter Iulia Domitia, dedicated by Quintus Iulius Cens.

EDCS-21600342.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Tiopila lived 15 years.
EDCS-21600342.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Caius Iulius Tiopila at the age of 15.

EDCS-21600343.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Tonneius Victor lived piously for 14 years. His father, Tonneius, made [this] for his son.
EDCS-21600343.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Lucius Tonneius Victor at the age of 14, dedicated by his father.

EDCS-21600344.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius Victor made [this] for his most rare wife, Iulia Octavia, who lived 50 years.
EDCS-21600344.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulia Octavia, dedicated by her husband Julius Victor.

EDCS-21600345.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Victor lived 18 years. His mother, Caecilia Victoria, made [this] for her son.
EDCS-21600345.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Caius Iulius Victor at the age of 18, dedicated by his mother Caecilia Victoria.

EDCS-21600346.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Quintus Iulius Victoris, who lived 100 years. Iulius Pudens Victor Caesianus saw to its making.
EDCS-21600346.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Quintus Iulius Victoris at the age of 100, with Iulius Pudens Victor Caesianus overseeing its construction.

EDCS-21600347.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Gaius Julius Vitulus lived 25 years and [some days].
EDCS-21600347.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Gaius Julius Vitulus, likely a tombstone indicating his lifespan.

EDCS-21600349.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Urbana once lived 6 years.
EDCS-21600349.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Urbana, indicating she died at the age of six.

EDCS-21600350.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia lived 13 years. Victor made this from his own goods.
EDCS-21600350.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia, who died at thirteen, funded by Victor from his own resources.

EDCS-21600351.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Agrippina lived 45 years. Her sons and heirs [made this] for their most dutiful mother.
EDCS-21600351.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Agrippina, dedicated by her sons and heirs.

EDCS-22000441.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Iulia Ammia, who lived 50 years. Lucius Antonius Albanus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-22000441.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Antonius Albanus to his wife, Iulia Ammia.

EDCS-22000442.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Iulia Clivia, who lived 53 years. Petronia [made this] for her sister, and may your bones rest well. Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Claudia Maxima, who lived 102 years. Victoria [made this] for her mother, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22000442.summary: This inscription combines two funerary dedications: one from Petronia to her sister Iulia Clivia, and another from Victoria to her mother Claudia Maxima.

EDCS-22000443.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Iulia Crispinia, daughter of Caius, his most chaste wife.
EDCS-22000443.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Crispina, wife of Caius.

EDCS-22000444.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Iulia Donata, wife. Sittius Fatalis erected this.
EDCS-22000444.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by Sittius Fatalis to his wife, Iulia Donata.

EDCS-22000445.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Donata lived 35 years. Honorata made this for her fellow member; she lies here.
EDCS-22000445.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Donata, made by Honorata, possibly a fellow member of a society or club.

EDCS-22000447.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Donatula lived 32 years. Her parents and husband made this.
EDCS-22000447.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Donatula, made by her parents and husband.

EDCS-22000448.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Emerita lived for 17 years.
EDCS-22000448.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life span of Iulia Emerita.

EDCS-22000449.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Extricata lived for 11 years and 11 days.
EDCS-22000449.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life span of Iulia Extricata.

EDCS-22000450.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Felicia lived for 42 years. Lolius Ianuarius made this memorial for his wife.
EDCS-22000450.summary: A funerary monument erected by Lolius Ianuarius for his wife Iulia Felicia.

EDCS-22000451.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Felicita lived for 40 years. He made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-22000451.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Iulia Felicita by her husband.

EDCS-22000452.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iuliae Flaviae lived for 35 years. Iulius Primus, her husband, for his wife.
EDCS-22000452.summary: A funerary inscription by Iulius Primus for his wife, Iuliae Flaviae.

EDCS-22000453.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Staberius Felix made this for his most loving wife, Iuliae Fortunatae, who is also called Pusinna.
EDCS-22000453.summary: A funerary monument erected by Caius Staberius Felix for his wife, Iuliae Fortunatae (also known as Pusinna).

EDCS-22000454.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Her tutor arranged for this to be made for Iuliae Frontillae, mother of Iuli Frontinis.
EDCS-22000454.summary: A funerary monument for Iuliae Frontillae, mother of Iuli Frontinis, arranged by her tutor.

EDCS-22000455.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Fron
EDCS-22000455.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription for Iulia Fron.

EDCS-22000456.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Galla lived for 38 years. Quintus Clodius Felix made this for his most faithful wife.
EDCS-22000456.summary: A funerary monument erected by Quintus Clodius Felix for his wife, Iulia Galla.

EDCS-22000457.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Gala lived for 37 years. Marius Clemens made this for his most rare wife.
EDCS-22000457.summary: A funerary monument erected by Marius Clemens for his wife, Iulia Gala.

EDCS-22000458.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Julia Generosa, his wife, who lived 22 years. Lucius Ofelius Valens made this for her, as she deserved well.
EDCS-22000458.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Lucius Ofelius Valens for his wife, Julia Generosa.

EDCS-22000459.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Julia Germana lived 60 years. Caius Annius Fortunatus placed this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-22000459.summary: This funerary dedication was made by Caius Annius Fortunatus for his wife, Julia Germana.

EDCS-22000460.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Julia Honorata, dearest wife. Flavius Casto made this monument for his wife, who lived 46 years.
EDCS-22000460.summary: Flavius Casto erected this monument for his wife, Julia Honorata.

EDCS-22000461.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Julia Ospita, who lived 80 years. Caius Caecilius Rogatianus, his grandson, for his grandmother.
EDCS-22000461.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Caius Caecilius Rogatianus for his grandmother, Julia Ospita.

EDCS-22000462.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Julia Ingenua, who lived 55 years. Her heir made and dedicated this.
EDCS-22000462.summary: The heir of Julia Ingenua erected and dedicated this funerary monument for her.

EDCS-22000463.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Julia Istatera, his wife, who lived 32 years.
EDCS-22000463.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Julia Istatera, wife of an unnamed dedicant.

EDCS-22000464.translation: To the Divine Shades. Julia Iucunda made this for her son Lucius, who deserved well, who lived 15 years.
EDCS-22000464.summary: Julia Iucunda erected this funerary monument for her son, Lucius.

EDCS-22000465.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Julia Iuliana Rufina, who lived...
EDCS-22000465.summary: This funerary inscription is incomplete and records the name Julia Iuliana Rufina and her age at death.

EDCS-22000466.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Julia Libosa lived 50 years. Her daughters made this for their deserving mother.
EDCS-22000466.summary: The daughters of Julia Libosa erected this funerary monument for their mother.

EDCS-22000467.translation: To the Divine Shades. Julia Licinia lived 55 years. She made this for herself while alive and for her husband.
EDCS-22000467.summary: Julia Licinia erected this funerary monument for herself and her husband during her lifetime.

EDCS-22000468.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Marcella lived 60 years. Mother and son made this.
EDCS-22000468.summary: A funerary dedication by a mother and son for Iulia Marcella, who lived to be 60 years old.

EDCS-22000469.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Marcella lived 16 years. Flavius Datus made this for his sister's daughter.
EDCS-22000469.summary: A funerary dedication by Flavius Datus for his niece, Iulia Marcella, who lived to be 16 years old.

EDCS-22000470.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Marcia lived 25 years. Pomponius Marsus made this for his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-22000470.summary: A funerary dedication by Pomponius Marsus for his wife, Iulia Marcia, who lived to be 25 years old.

EDCS-22000471.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Matrona lived 40 years. Quintus Annius Victor, her heir, made this.
EDCS-22000471.summary: A funerary dedication by Quintus Annius Victor for his heir, Iulia Matrona, who lived to be 40 years old.

EDCS-22000472.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Matrona lived 41 years. Caecilius Verus made this for his sweetest wife.
EDCS-22000472.summary: A funerary dedication by Caecilius Verus for his wife, Iulia Matrona, who lived to be 41 years old.

EDCS-22000473.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Mat lived 40 years. Lollius Max made this for his wife.
EDCS-22000473.summary: A funerary dedication by Lollius Max for his wife, Iulia Matrona, who lived to be 40 years old.

EDCS-22000474.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Maxima lived 55 years. Mother of her sons, most sweet, made this.
EDCS-22000474.summary: A funerary dedication by the sons for their mother, Iulia Maxima, who lived to be 55 years old.

EDCS-22000475.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Maxima lived 35 years. Egrilius Donatus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-22000475.summary: A funerary dedication by Egrilius Donatus for his wife, Iulia Maxima, who lived to be 35 years old.

EDCS-22000476.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Maxima lived approximately 22 years. Dutiful brothers made this.
EDCS-22000476.summary: A funerary dedication by two brothers for their sister, Iulia Maxima, who lived to be approximately 22 years old.

EDCS-22000477.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Maxima lived 40 years. Flavius Felix, her husband, made this.
EDCS-22000477.summary: A funerary dedication by Flavius Felix for his wife, Iulia Maxima, who lived to be 40 years old.

EDCS-22000478.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Maxima lived 9 years. Her most unhappy father made this.
EDCS-22000478.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Maxima, commemorating her life of 9 years and dedicated by her father.

EDCS-22000479.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iuliae Monnicae lived 40 years. Quintus M S made this for his pious wife.
EDCS-22000479.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iuliae Monnicae, who lived 40 years, dedicated by her husband Quintus M S.

EDCS-22000480.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Munatia lived 26 years, 8 months, and 20 days. Lurius Felix made and dedicated this for his most rare wife.
EDCS-22000480.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Munatia, who lived 26 years, 8 months, and 20 days, dedicated by her husband Lurius Felix.

EDCS-22000481.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Mustia, beautiful and chaste, lived 55 years.
EDCS-22000481.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Mustia, commemorating her life of 55 years and noting her beauty and chastity.

EDCS-22000483.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Peregrina lived 70 years. Oratius Antonius made this for his mother.
EDCS-22000483.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Peregrina, who lived 70 years, dedicated by her son Oratius Antonius.

EDCS-22000485.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iuliae Primulicae lived 13 years. Iulius Ianuarius made this.
EDCS-22000485.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iuliae Primulicae, who lived 13 years, dedicated by Iulius Ianuarius.

EDCS-22000486.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iuliae Prisculae lived 3 years. Her parents, Iuli Formica and Prisca, saw to it that this was made for their most pious daughter.
EDCS-22000486.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iuliae Prisculae, who lived 3 years, commissioned by her parents Iuli Formica and Prisca.

EDCS-22000487.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Quintilla lived 30 years and [some number of] days.
EDCS-22000487.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Quintilla, commemorating her life of 30 years and some days.

EDCS-22000488.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iuliae Quintulae lived 30 years. Her parent, Iulius Nestor, made this.
EDCS-22000488.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iuliae Quintulae, who lived 30 years, dedicated by her parent Iulius Nestor.

EDCS-22000489.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iuliae Restutae, his excellent wife, who lived [some number of] years. Caius Iulius or made this.
EDCS-22000489.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iuliae Restutae, dedicated by her husband Caius Iulius.

EDCS-22000490.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Rogata lived for 45 years. Saturninus made this for his dearest mother.
EDCS-22000490.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Iulia Rogata and was dedicated by her son Saturninus.

EDCS-22000491.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Rogata lived for 70 years. Her dutiful sons made this for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-22000491.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Iulia Rogata and was dedicated by her sons.

EDCS-22000492.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iuliae Romana lived for 30 years. Terentus made this for his wife.
EDCS-22000492.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Iuliae Romana and was dedicated by her husband Terentus.

EDCS-22000493.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Rustica lived for 32 years. Geminius Venustus made this for his deserving relative.
EDCS-22000493.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Iulia Rustica and was dedicated by her relative Geminius Venustus.

EDCS-22000494.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Sabina lived for 30 years. Antonius Victor made this for his most precious wife.
EDCS-22000494.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Iulia Sabina and was dedicated by her husband Antonius Victor.

EDCS-22000495.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Sallustia lived for 70 years. Titus Iulius Munatianus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-22000495.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Iulia Sallustia and was dedicated by her husband Titus Iulius Munatianus.

EDCS-22000497.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Salviana lived for 40 years. Iulia Minorica made this for her deserving sister.
EDCS-22000497.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Iulia Salviana and was dedicated by her sister Iulia Minorica.

EDCS-22000498.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Saturnina lived for 30 years.
EDCS-22000498.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Iulia Saturnina.

EDCS-22000499.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Saturnina lived for 35 years for her dearest wife.
EDCS-22000499.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Iulia Saturnina and indicates it was made for her dearest wife.

EDCS-22000500.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Saturnina lived for 35 years. Rogatus made this for his deserving daughter-in-law. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Timinius Publianus lived for 32 years. Rogatus his very dear son made this for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-22000500.summary: This inscription contains two funerary epitaphs: one for Iulia Saturnina, dedicated by her daughter-in-law Rogatus, and another for Timinius Publianus, dedicated by his son Rogatus.

EDCS-22000501.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Sclepia lived 30 years. Sittius Honoratus made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-22000501.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Iulia Sclepia by her husband, Sittius Honoratus.

EDCS-22000502.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Spes lived 60 years. Calventius made this.
EDCS-22000502.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Spes, made by Calventius.

EDCS-22000503.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Spes, daughter, lived one day for one year. Her father made this for his most beloved daughter. Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelia Iulia lived 23 years. Sextus Iulius Socinus made this for his most rare wife and daughter.
EDCS-22000503.summary: A complex funerary epitaph for Iulia Spes and her mother Aelia Iulia, set up by Sextus Iulius Socinus.

EDCS-22000504.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Spes lived 35 years. Her father made this.
EDCS-22000504.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Spes, made by her father.

EDCS-22000505.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Successa lived 40 years. Titus Flavius Datus made this for his mother.
EDCS-22000505.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Successa, made by her son Titus Flavius Datus.

EDCS-22000506.translation: To the departed spirits. For Iulia Tiopila, who lived 27 years.
EDCS-22000506.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Tiopila, recording her age at death.

EDCS-22000507.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Valentina lived 30 years. Aviania Venusta placed this for her mother.
EDCS-22000507.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Valentina, placed by her daughter Aviania Venusta.

EDCS-22000508.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Valeria lived [unknown] years. Quintianus and Caius, sons, made this for her while she was alive.
EDCS-22000508.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Valeria, set up by her sons Quintianus and Caius during her lifetime.

EDCS-22000509.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Valeria lived 40 years. Caius Iulius Maius made this for his wife.
EDCS-22000509.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Valeria, dedicated by her husband Caius Iulius Maius.

EDCS-22000510.translation: To the departed spirits. For Iulia Venustula, who lived 16 years, 6 months, and 25 days. Iulius Ianuarius made this for his most rare foster-child.
EDCS-22000510.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Venustula, made by her foster-father Iulius Ianuarius.

EDCS-22000511.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Victoria made this for her son and husband while still alive, for herself and her son. Her son was making it for his mother, Cot Emeria.
EDCS-22000511.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Iulia Victoria for her son and husband, with her son completing it.

EDCS-22000512.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Victoria lived for 33 years. Her husband, with their most innocent sons, made this.
EDCS-22000512.summary: This is a funerary monument made by the husband and sons of Iulia Victoria, who lived for 33 years.

EDCS-22000513.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Victoria lived for 50 years. Caius Iulius Mar made this for his mother.
EDCS-22000513.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Caius Iulius Mar for his mother, Iulia Victoria, who lived for 50 years.

EDCS-22000514.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Victoria lived for 65 years.
EDCS-22000514.summary: This is a funerary monument commemorating Iulia Victoria, who lived for 65 years.

EDCS-22000515.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Victoria lived for 75 years. Iulia Victoria the daughter made this.
EDCS-22000515.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Iulia Victoria the daughter for her mother, Iulia Victoria, who lived for 75 years.

EDCS-22000516.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Victorina lived for 25 years. Pomponius Secundus, her husband, mother, and sons, made this.
EDCS-22000516.summary: This is a funerary monument made by the husband, mother, and sons of Iulia Victorina, who lived for 25 years.

EDCS-22000517.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Iulia Victorina, from Lambaese, who lived 25 years, 8 months, and 17 days. Marcus Sextilius Laetus, also known as Cardelus, made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-22000517.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Marcus Sextilius Laetus for his wife, Iulia Victorina, who lived 25 years, 8 months, and 17 days.

EDCS-22000518.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Vitali lived for 38 years. Aegrilius Receptus made this for her, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-22000518.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Aegrilius Receptus for his wife, Iulia Vitali, who lived for 38 years.

EDCS-22000519.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Iulia Vitali, daughter of Lucius. She lived for [unknown years]. Marcus Licinius Saturninus made this for his most rare wife.
EDCS-22000519.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Marcus Licinius Saturninus for his wife, Iulia Vitali, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-22000520.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iunius Ianuarius lived for about 8 years.
EDCS-22000520.summary: This is a funerary monument commemorating Iunius Ianuarius, who lived for about 8 years.

EDCS-22000521.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Iunius Marinus lived 19 years. Sertius made this for his brother.
EDCS-22000521.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Iunius Marinus, made by his brother Sertius.

EDCS-22000522.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iunius Martialis lived 85 years. Aurelius Commodus, Antonius Felix, and Afranius Victor, his heirs, made this.
EDCS-22000522.summary: This funerary epitaph records that the heirs Aurelius Commodus, Antonius Felix, and Afranius Victor commissioned this monument for Iunius Martialis, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-22000523.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pius made this for his faithful son, Lucius Iunius Privatus, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-22000523.summary: This funerary epitaph states that Pius commissioned this monument for his son Lucius Iunius Privatus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-22000524.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Paconius Maximus made this for his deserving wife, Iunia Fabia, who lived 45 years.
EDCS-22000524.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Paconius Maximus commissioned this monument for his wife Iunia Fabia, who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-22000525.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iuventia Ianuaria, his sister, made this for Iuventia Victoria, who lived 45 years.
EDCS-22000525.summary: This funerary epitaph states that Iuventia Ianuaria commissioned this monument for her sister Iuventia Victoria, who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-22000526.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Labienus Saturninus made this for his son, Publius Labienius Fortunatus of Tuburbo, who lived 14 years, 9 months, and 15 days, who benefited him greatly.
EDCS-22000526.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Quintus Labienius Saturninus commissioned this monument for his son Publius Labienius Fortunatus, who died at the age of 14 years, 9 months, and 15 days.

EDCS-22000527.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Her husband made this for Laelia Pusinna, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-22000527.summary: This funerary epitaph states that the husband of Laelia Pusinna commissioned this monument for her, after she died at the age of 25.

EDCS-22000528.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Volusius Saturninus [made this] for his most dutiful daughter, Lelia Felicitas, who lived 15 years.
EDCS-22000528.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Gaius Volusius Saturninus commissioned this monument for his daughter Lelia Felicitas, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-22000529.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelius made this for his well-deserving sister, Lelia Fortunata, who lived 21 years.
EDCS-22000529.summary: This funerary epitaph states that Aurelius commissioned this monument for his sister Lelia Fortunata, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-22000530.translation: Marcus Liburnius Felix, his father, made this for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-22000530.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Marcus Liburnius Felix commissioned this monument for his son.

EDCS-22000531.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Liburnius Saturninus lived 96 years.
EDCS-22000531.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Lucius Liburnius Saturninus, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-22000532.translation: To the departed spirits. Liburnia Baesia, dutiful, lived [number] years.
EDCS-22000532.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Liburnia Baesia, whose age is partially lost.

EDCS-22000533.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Licinius Bassus lived 3 years and 2 months.
EDCS-22000533.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short lifespan of Lucius Licinius Bassus, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-22000534.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Licinius Meritus lived 20 years. C. Salsu made (this).
EDCS-22000534.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Licinius Meritus, with the maker identified as C. Salsu.

EDCS-22000535.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Of Marcus Licinius Saturninus, son of Marcus Licinius, lived 15 years and 2 months. For a most dutiful son, Marcus Licinius Saturninus (made this).
EDCS-22000535.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Licinius Saturninus, set up by his father of the same name, recording his age at death.

EDCS-22000536.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Licinia Emilia, mother, lived 96 years. Licinius Rogatus, son, made (this).
EDCS-22000536.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Licinia Emilia, made by her son Licinius Rogatus.

EDCS-22000537.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Licinia Castula lived 5 years, 20 days.
EDCS-22000537.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the very short lifespan of Licinia Castula, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-22000538.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Licinia Novela lived 30 years.
EDCS-22000538.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Licinia Novela, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-22000539.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Licinia Prima lived 25 years.
EDCS-22000539.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Licinia Prima, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-22000540.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Livileius Ultor lived 6 years. His parents made (this) for him.
EDCS-22000540.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short lifespan of Livileius Ultor, made by his parents.

EDCS-22000541.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Livius Saturninus lived 85 years. Livius Saturninus, his son, and Candida, his wife, set this up.
EDCS-22000541.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Livius Saturninus, erected by his son and wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000542.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Lollius Felix lived 60 years. Antonia Donata had this made for her most rare husband.
EDCS-22000542.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Lollius Felix, commissioned by his wife Antonia Donata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000543.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Lollius Victor lived 18 years. Lollius Victor placed this for his deserving son. He lies here.
EDCS-22000543.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lollius Victor, erected by his father of the same name, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000544.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Lollia Maxsima lived 40 years. Her husband Arruntius Donatus made this.
EDCS-22000544.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lollia Maxsima, erected by her husband Arruntius Donatus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000545.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Lucia Quintula lived 27 years.
EDCS-22000545.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucia Quintula, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000546.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Lucretius Lucanus lived 80 years. Lucretius Lucanus made this for his father.
EDCS-22000546.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucretius Lucanus, erected by his son of the same name, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000547.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Lucretius Victor lived 33 years. Lucretius Lucanus made this for his father.
EDCS-22000547.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucretius Victor, erected by his father Lucretius Lucanus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000548.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Lucretius Victor lived 52 years.
EDCS-22000548.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Lucretius Victor, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000550.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Lusius Primus lived 50 years. Iulia Prima had this made from her own funds for her deserving husband.
EDCS-22000550.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lusius Primus, erected by his wife Iulia Prima, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000551.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Lucia Antestia Saturnina lived 30 years. Her father set this up for his dearest daughter.
EDCS-22000551.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucia Antestia Saturnina, erected by her father, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000552.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Lucius Magnuis Iucundus, his dearest father, from his son Magnii.
EDCS-22000552.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Magnuis Iucundus by his son.

EDCS-22000553.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Magnius Pupinus lived for 11 years.
EDCS-22000553.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short life of Lucius Magnius Pupinus.

EDCS-22000554.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Magnius Samannicus lived for 15 years.
EDCS-22000554.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short life of Lucius Magnius Samannicus.

EDCS-22000556.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Magnia Felicia lived for 16 years. Sinsicus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-22000556.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Magnia Felicia, made by her husband Sinsicus.

EDCS-22000557.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Magnia Festa lived for 55 years. Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aelius Aterius lived for 80 years. Felix Aterianus and Marcus Quintus, his sons, made this.
EDCS-22000557.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring two individuals, Magnia Festa and Aelius Aterius, with their sons acting as dedicants.

EDCS-22000558.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Magnia Saturnina lived for 45 years. Her son made this for his deserving mother.
EDCS-22000558.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Magnia Saturnina, dedicated by her son.

EDCS-22000559.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Malia Fortunata lived for 25 years, and her son Felicianus. She is placed here.
EDCS-22000559.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Malia Fortunata and her son Felicianus.

EDCS-22000560.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus Mamius Honorinus lived for 16 years and 2 months.
EDCS-22000560.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Quintus Mamius Honorinus.

EDCS-22000561.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Manilius Felix lived for 60 years. Manilia made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-22000561.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Manilius Felix, made by his wife Manilia.

EDCS-22000562.translation: Manius Manilleianus, aged 2 years. Proculeianus his father placed this.
EDCS-22000562.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Manius Manilleianus, dedicated by his father Proculeianus.

EDCS-22000563.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Tito Manilio Secundo, son of Titus, of the Papiria tribe, from Theveste, who lived 70 years. Rutilia Victoria made this for her most devoted husband, who deserved well of her; he is buried here.
EDCS-22000563.summary: A funerary monument for Tito Manilio Secundo, erected by his wife Rutilia Victoria, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000564.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Manilius Soricioni, son of Fuscus. Julius Barbarus made this on account of his father's merits.
EDCS-22000564.summary: A funerary dedication to Manilius Soricioni, made by Julius Barbarus in recognition of his father's services.

EDCS-22000565.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Manilia Candida, his most rare wife, who lived 30 years. Manilius Prunicus made this.
EDCS-22000565.summary: A funerary dedication for Manilia Candida, erected by her husband Manilius Prunicus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000566.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Manilia Donata, who lived 25 years. Her mother, Iulia Euclia, made this for her daughter.
EDCS-22000566.summary: A funerary monument for Manilia Donata, erected by her mother Iulia Euclia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000567.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Manilia Tertia, who lived 75 years. Manilius made this for his dearest mother.
EDCS-22000567.summary: A funerary dedication for Manilia Tertia, erected by her son Manilius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000568.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Lucius Mantius Victorinus, who lived 13 years. Valeria made this for her son.
EDCS-22000568.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Mantius Victorinus, erected by his mother Valeria, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000570.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Fullo lived 70 years. His sons made this for him.
EDCS-22000570.summary: A funerary dedication for Marcus Fullo, erected by his sons, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000571.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Marcia Marcella, who lived 25 years. Aelius Marcianus made this for his pious mother.
EDCS-22000571.summary: A funerary monument for Marcia Marcella, erected by her son Aelius Marcianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000572.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcia Severa lived 30 years; she is buried here. Clodius Clodianus made this for his most rare wife.
EDCS-22000572.summary: A funerary monument for Marcia Severa, erected by her husband Clodius Clodianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000573.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Marcia Siripa, who lived about 11 years. Her excellent parents made this for her on account of her merits.
EDCS-22000573.summary: A funerary dedication for Marcia Siripa, erected by her parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000574.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcia Victoria lived 45 years. Marius Victor set this up for his dearest wife.
EDCS-22000574.summary: A funerary dedication by Marius Victor to his wife Marcia Victoria, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-22000575.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marco Mario, freedman of his patron, lived 70 years. They made this.
EDCS-22000575.summary: A funerary inscription for Marco Mario, a freedman, erected by his patrons, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-22000576.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Marius Communis lived 65 years.
EDCS-22000576.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Marius Communis, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-22000577.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quinto Mario Felici lived 60 years.
EDCS-22000577.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quinto Mario Felici, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-22000578.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marius Felix lived 14 years.
EDCS-22000578.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marius Felix, a child, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-22000579.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Marius Ianuarius lived 50 years. Marius set this up for his deserving brother.
EDCS-22000579.summary: A funerary dedication by Marius for his brother Caius Marius Ianuarius, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-22000580.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Marius Natalis lived 72 years. Iulia Donata made this for her husband and for our son Iovianus, who lived 12 years.
EDCS-22000580.summary: A funerary dedication by Iulia Donata for her husband Caius Marius Natalis and son Iovianus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-22000581.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Marius Saturninus lived 61 years. Tannonia Valeria for her dearest wife.
EDCS-22000581.summary: A funerary dedication by Tannonia Valeria for her wife Marcus Marius Saturninus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-22000582.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Rasinius Postimus made this for his excellent wife, Maria Caecina.
EDCS-22000582.summary: A funerary dedication by Rasinius Postimus for his wife Maria Caecina, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-22000583.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Maria Luciosa lived 64 years. Her son Valerianus made this.
EDCS-22000583.summary: A funerary dedication by Valerianus for his mother Maria Luciosa, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-22000584.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Maria Matronica lived for 16 years.
EDCS-22000584.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life of Maria Matronica, who died at age 16.

EDCS-22000585.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Maria Prima, lived 35 years. Ag made [this] for his wife.
EDCS-22000585.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Maria Prima, who died at age 35, and was set up by her husband Ag.

EDCS-22000586.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Maria Secundula, a most beloved wife, lived 40 years. Flavius Maximus made [this] for his wife, and Flavius Faustus for his mother.
EDCS-22000586.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Maria Secundula, who died at age 40, and was set up by her husband Flavius Maximus and son Flavius Faustus.

EDCS-22000587.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Maximus lived for 15 years. Namphamus made [this].
EDCS-22000587.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Maximus, who died at age 15, and was set up by Namphamus.

EDCS-22000588.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Maxima, a most beloved wife. Primulus made [this]. Lived 20 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-22000588.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Maxima, who died at age 20, and was set up by her husband Primulus.

EDCS-22000589.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Memmius Ianuarius lived for 90 years. Caius Memmius Fortunatus made [this] for his father.
EDCS-22000589.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Caius Memmius Ianuarius, who died at age 90, and was set up by his son Caius Memmius Fortunatus.

EDCS-22000590.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Memmia Iuliosa lived for 6 years and 2 months. Caius Memmius Fortunatus made [this] for his natural daughter.
EDCS-22000590.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Memmia Iuliosa, who died at age 6 years and 2 months, and was set up by her father Caius Memmius Fortunatus.

EDCS-22000591.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Memmia Saturnina lived for 10 years. Memmius Fortunatus made [this] for his natural daughter.
EDCS-22000591.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Memmia Saturnina, who died at age 10, and was set up by her father Memmius Fortunatus.

EDCS-22000592.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Metius Valens made [this] for himself while alive. Lived for 75 years.
EDCS-22000592.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording that Metius Valens set up his own tombstone while alive and lived for 75 years.

EDCS-22000593.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Lucius Mindius Felix, lived 51 years. His heirs made [this].
EDCS-22000593.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Lucius Mindius Felix, who died at age 51, and was set up by his heirs.

EDCS-22000594.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Minucius Iucundus lived 90 years.
EDCS-22000594.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Marcus Minucius Iucundus, dedicated to the underworld spirits.

EDCS-22000595.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Minucius Moderatus lived 51 years.
EDCS-22000595.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Minucius Moderatus, dedicated to the spirits of the underworld.

EDCS-22000596.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Minucius Rufinus, to his dearest mother Minucia Rogatina, may your bones rest well, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22000596.summary: A funerary inscription from Lucius Minucius Rufinus to his mother Minucia Rogatina, expressing wishes for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-22000597.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Fabius Rogatus, to his dearest wife Minucia Secunda, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-22000597.summary: A funerary inscription from Caius Fabius Rogatus to his wife Minucia Secunda, stating her name and lifespan.

EDCS-22000599.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Marcus Modius Antonianus, an innocent boy, who lived 13 years.
EDCS-22000599.summary: A funerary inscription for a young boy named Marcus Modius Antonianus, dedicated to the spirits of the underworld.

EDCS-22000600.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Modius December, a veteran, lived 80 years. Aelia Rogatina made this for her dearest husband. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aelia Rogatina lived 80 years; she made this in her lifetime.
EDCS-22000600.summary: A funerary monument set up by Aelia Rogatina for her husband Modius December, a veteran, who lived 80 years, with a reciprocal dedication by Aelia Rogatina for herself.

EDCS-22000601.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Modia Celsina made this for her well-deserving wife, Lucius Minucius Martialis, who lived approximately 50 years.
EDCS-22000601.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Modia Celsina for her husband Lucius Minucius Martialis, stating his approximate lifespan.

EDCS-22000602.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Modia Conservata, daughter of Caius, pious, lived 27 years. She lies here.
EDCS-22000602.summary: A funerary inscription for Modia Conservata, stating her parentage, piety, lifespan, and burial location.

EDCS-22000603.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Mucius Messor made this for his deserving sister, Muccia Fusca, most unfortunate; she lived 35 years.
EDCS-22000603.summary: A funerary inscription from Mucius Messor to his sister Muccia Fusca, lamenting her early death at 35 years old.

EDCS-22000604.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Mucius Moderatus Iunior lies here. Iulius Barbarus made this for a worthy citizen.
EDCS-22000604.summary: A funerary monument for Mucius Moderatus Iunior, erected by Iulius Barbarus for a deserving citizen.

EDCS-22000605.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Mucia Faustina lived 20 years. Marcus Fabius Commodus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-22000605.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a husband to his wife, Mucia Faustina, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-22000606.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Munatius Martialis, son of Caius, of the Arnensis tribe, lived 46 years in Carthage. Lucius Domitius Secundus made this for his deserving son-in-law with his own money.
EDCS-22000606.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Munatius Martialis, who died at age 46, dedicated by his father-in-law, Lucius Domitius Secundus.

EDCS-22000607.translation: Musia Crescentila, mother, made this sacred to the spirits of the departed while alive. Tetia Honorata lived 26 years.
EDCS-22000607.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tetia Honorata, who died at age 26, dedicated by her mother, Musia Crescentila.

EDCS-22000608.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Martialis made this for his most beloved daughter, Marcia Marciosa, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-22000608.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a father, Martialis, to his daughter, Marcia Marciosa, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-22000609.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Naevia Silicia lived 27 years, 10 months, and 23 days; she is buried here.
EDCS-22000609.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Naevia Silicia, recording her lifespan of 27 years, 10 months, and 23 days.

EDCS-22000610.translation: Chresima, the husband, [made this] for his most rare wife, Nereis.
EDCS-22000610.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a husband, Chresima, to his wife, Nereis.

EDCS-22000611.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Eutyches, the husband, made this for his most beloved wife, Nigra.
EDCS-22000611.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a husband, Eutyches, to his wife, Nigra.

EDCS-22000612.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Nonius Felix, the son, lived one year, 10 months, and 17 days. Nonius Primus, the father, made this for his son.
EDCS-22000612.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Nonius Felix, who died at the age of one year, 10 months, and 17 days, dedicated by his father, Nonius Primus.

EDCS-22000613.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aponius Saturninus, the son, saw to the construction for his mother, Nonia Secunda, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-22000613.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nonia Secunda, commissioned by her son, Aponius Saturninus.

EDCS-22000614.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Novantia Procula brought this plot here for herself and her three sons, having lived [many] years.
EDCS-22000614.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Novantia Procula and her three sons, indicating she arranged for the burial plot during her lifetime.

EDCS-22000615.translation: To the Divine Manes. Novantia Victorina lived for about 35 years.
EDCS-22000615.summary: A funerary dedication to Novantia Victorina, who died around the age of 35.

EDCS-22000616.translation: To the Divine Manes. Numenius made this tomb for Zoe, out of love; she lived 40 years.
EDCS-22000616.summary: A funerary dedication by Numenius for Zoe, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-22000617.translation: To the Divine Manes. Octavius Clarus lived for 26 years. Felicianus, Potosa, and Felicia made this for their most loving uncle.
EDCS-22000617.summary: A funerary dedication made by Felicianus, Potosa, and Felicia for their uncle Octavius Clarus, who died at 26.

EDCS-22000618.translation: To the Divine Manes. Octavius Dovolinus, dutiful, lived for 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-22000618.summary: A funerary dedication for Octavius Dovolinus, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-22000619.translation: To the Divine Manes. Octavia Adventina lived for 60 years. Octavius Urbanus made this for his sweetest daughter.
EDCS-22000619.summary: A funerary dedication by Octavius Urbanus for his daughter Octavia Adventina, who died at 60.

EDCS-22000620.translation: To the Divine Manes. Octavia Clara lived for 25 years. Caius Pollius Felix made this for his most loving wife.
EDCS-22000620.summary: A funerary dedication by Caius Pollius Felix for his wife Octavia Clara, who died at 25.

EDCS-22000621.translation: To the Divine Manes. Octavia Catula lived for 42 years and 1 month. Marius Rogatianus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-22000621.summary: A funerary dedication by Marius Rogatianus for his wife Octavia Catula, who died at 42 years and 1 month.

EDCS-22000622.translation: To the Divine Manes. Octavia Ianuaria lived for 24 years. Manilius Felix made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-22000622.summary: A funerary dedication by Manilius Felix for his wife Octavia Ianuaria, who died at 24.

EDCS-22000623.translation: To the Divine Manes. Octavia Rogata lived for 60 years. Octavius Urbanus made this for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-22000623.summary: A funerary dedication by Octavius Urbanus for Octavia Rogata, who died at 60.

EDCS-22000624.translation: To the Divine Manes. Octavia Rufina lived for 95 years, 7 months, and 15 days. Caecilius Emeritus made this for his sweetest grandmother.
EDCS-22000624.summary: A funerary dedication by Caecilius Emeritus for his grandmother Octavia Rufina, who died at 95 years, 7 months, and 15 days.

EDCS-22000625.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Octavia Saturnina, daughter of Octavius Maximus, lived for 4 years.
EDCS-22000625.summary: A funerary epitaph for Octavia Saturnina, recording her name, parentage, and age at death.

EDCS-22000626.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Octavia Victoria lived for 22 years. Annaeus, her husband, made this.
EDCS-22000626.summary: A funerary epitaph for Octavia Victoria, mentioning her husband and age at death.

EDCS-22000627.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Octavia Victoria lived for 70 years. Valerius Saturus, Martia, and Felix made this for their dearest mother.
EDCS-22000627.summary: A funerary epitaph for Octavia Victoria, erected by her children and recording her age at death.

EDCS-22000628.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Ombilius Maximus lived for 60 years. Iulia Butura made this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-22000628.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Ombilius Maximus, erected by his wife and recording his age at death.

EDCS-22000629.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. He made this for his dearest wife, Lucius Omulius Victor, who lived for 25 years.
EDCS-22000629.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Omulius Victor, erected by his wife and recording his age at death.

EDCS-22000630.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. His wife, Omulia Victoria, made this, who lived for 43 years.
EDCS-22000630.summary: A funerary epitaph for Omulia Victoria, erected by her husband and recording her age at death.

EDCS-22000631.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Publius Vassius, dearest son, who lived for 6 years. Caius Ottacilius Faustus, father, made this.
EDCS-22000631.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Vassius, erected by his father and recording his age at death.

EDCS-22000632.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Pacilius Musianus lived for 70 years. Quintus Pacilius Felix arranged for this to be made for his incomparable brother.
EDCS-22000632.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Pacilius Musianus, arranged by his brother and recording his age at death.

EDCS-22000633.translation: S. Paconius lived for 30 years; he is buried here. Caius Aelius Saturninus and Aelius Possessor, the heirs, made this.
EDCS-22000633.summary: A funerary epitaph for S. Paconius, stating his age at death and that the monument was erected by his heirs.

EDCS-22000634.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Papinia Maxima, who lived for 27 years.
EDCS-22000634.summary: A funerary epitaph for Papinia Maxima, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-22000635.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Herais, mother, made this for her most beloved and deeply missed son, Parammon.
EDCS-22000635.summary: A funerary dedication by a mother for her son, Parammon.

EDCS-22000636.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Passidenius Saturninus lived 31 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-22000636.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Passidenius Saturninus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-22000637.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Peculiaris lived 8 years, a most worthy grandson.
EDCS-22000637.summary: A funerary dedication for a grandson named Peculiaris, noting his age.

EDCS-22000638.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Perellius Rogatianus lived 85 years. Perellius Rogatianus, grandson and heir, made this.
EDCS-22000638.summary: A funerary dedication for Perellius Rogatianus, made by his grandson and heir of the same name.

EDCS-22000639.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Pescenius Ianuarius lived 40 years. Pescenia Victoria, his heir, placed this for her father.
EDCS-22000639.summary: A funerary dedication for Pescenius Ianuarius, made by his heir, Pescenia Victoria.

EDCS-22000640.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Caelia Rogatia, wife of Marcus Peticius Lontinus. She lived 60 years. Her sons Marcus Peticius Peticianus and Marcus Peticius Aristo made this for their most dutiful mother.
EDCS-22000640.summary: A funerary dedication by two sons for their mother, Caelia Rogatia.

EDCS-22000641.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus Petronius Britto lived 40 years. Petronius Gallicus, by his instruction, took care to have this made.
EDCS-22000641.summary: A funerary dedication for Quintus Petronius Britto, arranged by Petronius Gallicus according to Britto's instructions.

EDCS-22000642.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Titus Petronius Halus lived 60 years; he is buried here. Valeria Zosime made this for her incomparable husband, on account of his kindness towards her.
EDCS-22000642.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife, Valeria Zosime, for her husband, Titus Petronius Halus, praising his kindness.

EDCS-22000643.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus Petronius Victor lived 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-22000643.summary: A funerary dedication for Quintus Petronius Victor, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-22000644.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Petronia Monna the dutiful lived 50 years. Iulius Petronianus and Agrippa made this for their deserving mother.
EDCS-22000644.summary: A funerary dedication by two sons, Iulius Petronianus and Agrippa, for their mother, Petronia Monna.

EDCS-22000645.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Petronia Primula lived 61 years. Her husband Passius Secundus made this, as heir.
EDCS-22000645.summary: This is a tombstone for Petronia Primula, erected by her husband Passius Secundus, dating to a period when the use of 'Dis Manibus' was common for funerary inscriptions.

EDCS-22000646.summary: This is a tombstone for Petronia Venusta, likely a simple epitaph indicating the name of the deceased.

EDCS-22000647.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Pherecydes, my pupil.
EDCS-22000647.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Pherecydes, a pupil, consecrated to the spirits of the departed.

EDCS-22000648.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [He/She] lived 12 years.
EDCS-22000648.summary: This is a tombstone for an individual who lived 12 years, dedicated to the spirits of the departed.

EDCS-22000649.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Placida Sanctina, formerly.
EDCS-22000649.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Placida Sanctina, dedicated to the spirits of the departed.

EDCS-22000650.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Plotia Flaviosa lived 25 years. Durius Plotinus made this for his most beloved wife.
EDCS-22000650.summary: This is a tombstone for Plotia Flaviosa, erected by her husband Durius Plotinus, dating to the period of common funerary inscriptions.

EDCS-22000651.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Pompeius Caprarius lived 5 years.
EDCS-22000651.summary: This is a tombstone for the young Publius Pompeius Caprarius, who lived 5 years, dedicated to the spirits of the departed.

EDCS-22200691.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Pompeius Felix, who lived 60 years. Pompeia Charmosyne made this for her father who deserved well of her.
EDCS-22200691.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Pompeius Felix, erected by his daughter Pompeia Charmosyne, dating to the period of common funerary inscriptions.

EDCS-22200692.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Pompeius Proculus lived 80 years. His sons dutifully erected this.
EDCS-22200692.summary: This is a tombstone for Publius Pompeius Proculus, erected by his sons, dating to the period of common funerary inscriptions.

EDCS-22200693.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gnaeus Pompeius Saturninus, dutiful, lived 27 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-22200693.summary: This is a tombstone for Gnaeus Pompeius Saturninus, noting his piety and place of burial, dating to the period of common funerary inscriptions.

EDCS-22200694.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pompeia Procula lived 92 years. Annius Fortunatus made this for his mother.
EDCS-22200694.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pompeia Procula, set up by her son, Annius Fortunatus.

EDCS-22200695.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pompeia Securia lived 45 years. Marina made this for her deserving mother.
EDCS-22200695.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Pompeia Securia, commemorated by her mother, Marina.

EDCS-22200696.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Pompeia Urbana, mother. Lucius Antistius Victor, son, made this.
EDCS-22200696.summary: A funerary inscription for Pompeia Urbana, dedicated by her son, Lucius Antistius Victor.

EDCS-22200697.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pomponius Donatus lived 1 year.
EDCS-22200697.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Pomponius Donatus who died at the age of one.

EDCS-22200698.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Pomponius Felix lived 40 years. Publius Pomponius Donatus made this for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-22200698.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Publius Pomponius Felix, who lived 40 years, and was commemorated by his son, Publius Pomponius Donatus.

EDCS-22200699.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Pomponius Felix lived 5 years. Publius Pomponius Donatus made this for his well-deserving brother.
EDCS-22200699.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Publius Pomponius Felix, who died at age five, and was dedicated by his brother, Publius Pomponius Donatus.

EDCS-22200700.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Pomponius Fortunatus lived 20 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Pomponius Castus lived 10 years. Pomponius and Iulia Victorina made this for their sons.
EDCS-22200700.summary: This funerary monument records the deaths of two brothers, Caius Pomponius Fortunatus (aged 20) and Caius Pomponius Castus (aged 10), commemorated by their parents.

EDCS-22200701.translation: Gaius Pomponius Victor, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, from Theveste, set this up for himself and his wife during his lifetime.
EDCS-22200701.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Gaius Pomponius Victor for himself and his wife while he was alive.

EDCS-22200702.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pomponia Dativa lived 24 years. Her brother Pomponius Datus made this; she is buried here.
EDCS-22200702.summary: This funerary epitaph for Pomponia Dativa, who died at age 24, was made by her brother, Pomponius Datus.

EDCS-22200703.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Pomponia Donata, wife of the highest quality. Marcus Licinius Saturninus made this.
EDCS-22200703.summary: This funerary inscription is a memorial for Pomponia Donata, set up by her husband, Marcus Licinius Saturninus.
