EDCS-22200704.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pomponia Fortunata lived for 20 years. Sittius Honorarius made this for his wife.
EDCS-22200704.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Sittius Honorarius to his wife, Pomponia Fortunata, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-22200705.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Pomponia Fortunata, mother of two sons. Titus Flavius Vitalis and Aurelia [made this] in her memory.
EDCS-22200705.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pomponia Fortunata, identified as a mother, with contributions from Titus Flavius Vitalis and Aurelia.

EDCS-22200706.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pomponia Gallica lived for two years and three months. Her mother, Caecilia Gallica, made this for her innocent daughter.
EDCS-22200706.summary: A funerary epitaph by Caecilia Gallica for her daughter, Pomponia Gallica, who died at the age of two years and three months.

EDCS-22200707.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pomponia Optatula lived for 3 years. Her mother, Pomponia Saturnina, made this.
EDCS-22200707.summary: A funerary epitaph by Pomponia Saturnina for her daughter, Pomponia Optatula, who died at the age of 3.

EDCS-22200708.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pomponia Rufina lived for 13 years and 8 months. For a deserving daughter.
EDCS-22200708.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pomponia Rufina, who died at the age of 13 years and 8 months, dedicated by her parents as a deserving daughter.

EDCS-22200709.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pomponia Tertiola lived for [unknown age]. Geminia Saturnina made this for her daughter.
EDCS-22200709.summary: A funerary epitaph by Geminia Saturnina for her daughter, Pomponia Tertiola.

EDCS-22200710.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavia Ingenua made this for her well-deserving husband, Marcus Pontius Esperio.
EDCS-22200710.summary: A funerary epitaph by Flavia Ingenua for her husband, Marcus Pontius Esperio.

EDCS-22200711.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pontia Iulia lived for 32 years. Sulpicius Felix made this for his wife.
EDCS-22200711.summary: A funerary epitaph by Sulpicius Felix for his wife, Pontia Iulia, who died at the age of 32.

EDCS-22200712.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pontia Prisca lived for 65 years. Sulpicius Felix [made this] for his mother-in-law.
EDCS-22200712.summary: A funerary epitaph by Sulpicius Felix for his mother-in-law, Pontia Prisca, who died at the age of 65.

EDCS-22200713.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Pontia Quintia, daughter of Caius, who lived for 40 years. Caius Amulius Africus made this for his most dutiful wife, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-22200713.summary: A funerary epitaph by Caius Amulius Africus for his wife, Pontia Quintia, daughter of Caius, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-22200714.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Porcius, son of Aegrilia, lived 10 years. Aegrilia made this for her deserving son, in recognition of her dutiful affection.
EDCS-22200714.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Porcius, erected by his mother Aegrilia, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-22200715.translation: To the spirits of the departed, a sacred offering. Porcius Felix made this for his dearest brother, Sextus Porcius Golulianus.
EDCS-22200715.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextus Porcius Golulianus, erected by his brother Porcius Felix, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-22200716.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Porcius Verus lived 45 years. Aegrilia placed this for her deserving wife.
EDCS-22200716.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Porcius Verus, erected by his wife Aegrilia, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-22200717.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Porcia Vitalis lived 42 years. Umbrius Laetus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-22200717.summary: A funerary epitaph for Porcia Vitalis, erected by her husband Umbrius Laetus, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-22200718.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Run made this for Porocelius, his fellow slave. He lived 24 years.
EDCS-22200718.summary: A funerary epitaph for Run, a fellow slave of Porocelius, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-22200719.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Postumia Iunilla, daughter of Quintus, lived 11 months and 23 days.
EDCS-22200719.summary: A funerary epitaph for the infant Postumia Iunilla, daughter of Quintus, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-22200720.translation: To the spirits of the departed, a sacred offering. Postimia Rogatula lived 26 years.
EDCS-22200720.summary: A funerary epitaph for Postimia Rogatula, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-22200721.translation: To the spirits of the departed, a sacred offering. Postimia Victoria lived 25 years.
EDCS-22200721.summary: A funerary epitaph for Postimia Victoria, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-22200722.translation: To the spirits of the departed, a sacred offering. Primitiva lived 12 years. Philetus, her father, for his pious daughter.
EDCS-22200722.summary: A funerary epitaph for Primitiva, erected by her father Philetus, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-22200723.translation: To the spirits of the departed, a sacred offering. Primus lived 15 years. To the spirits of the departed, a sacred offering. Donata lived 13 years.
EDCS-22200723.summary: A double funerary epitaph for Primus and Donata, likely siblings, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-22200724.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Processus lived 30 years.
EDCS-22200724.summary: A funerary dedication recording the lifespan of Processus.

EDCS-22200725.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld, for Ptolomea, daughter of Maximina, who deserved well.
EDCS-22200725.summary: A funerary dedication for Ptolomea, daughter of Maximina.

EDCS-22200726.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Lucius Publicius Victorinus, the best patron; from his freedmen and heirs, Publicii Euhodus and Fortunatus.
EDCS-22200726.summary: A funerary dedication by freedmen and heirs to their patron, Lucius Publicius Victorinus.

EDCS-22200727.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Pullaena Quieta, who lived 70 years; from her sons, who deserved well.
EDCS-22200727.summary: A funerary dedication for Pullaena Quieta by her sons.

EDCS-22200728.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Pupia Saturnina, who lived 85 years. Fabricius Felix, her son and heir, made this.
EDCS-22200728.summary: A funerary dedication for Pupia Saturnina by her son and heir, Fabricius Felix.

EDCS-22200729.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Purtisia Firmina, who lived 55 days. Titus Purtisius Firmus [made this].
EDCS-22200729.summary: A funerary dedication for Purtisia Firmina by Titus Purtisius Firmus.

EDCS-22200730.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Pusinna, also called Felicitas, who lived 13 years.
EDCS-22200730.summary: A funerary dedication for Pusinna (Felicitas), who died at 13 years old.

EDCS-22200731.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sacred. For their dearest son, Publius Tertullus and his mother Estricata, PI AIBIANO, made this.
EDCS-22200731.summary: A funerary dedication by Publius Tertullus and his mother Estricata for their son and grandson, PI AIBIANO.

EDCS-22200733.translation: For the well-deserving. Quartinus, the father, made this for his son, who lived 1 year and 3 days.
EDCS-22200733.summary: A funerary dedication by a father, Quartinus, for his son who died in infancy.

EDCS-22200734.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Q. Avitus lived 80 years.
EDCS-22200734.summary: A funerary dedication recording the lifespan of Q. Avitus.

EDCS-22200735.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Gargilius made this for Quintus Cuminius.
EDCS-22200735.summary: A funerary dedication made by Lucius Gargilius for Quintus Cuminius.

EDCS-22200736.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Rantifana, or Licinia Aphrodite, made this for Marcus Rantifanus Glyco, a most rare youth, who lived 27 years.
EDCS-22200736.summary: A funerary dedication made by Rantifana/Licinia Aphrodite for her exceptionally talented young husband, Marcus Rantifanus Glyco, who died at age 27.

EDCS-22200737.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Rasinus lived 18 years.
EDCS-22200737.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life of Lucius Rasinus, who died at age 18.

EDCS-22200738.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Rasinius Castus lived 83 years and 5 months.
EDCS-22200738.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life of Lucius Rasinius Castus, who died at age 83 years and 5 months.

EDCS-22200739.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Refrius Faustinus lived 17 years and 5 months. His brother Refrius Maximianus and his nurse Hermes, most unfortunate, made this.
EDCS-22200739.summary: A funerary dedication made by a brother and nurse for Refrius Faustinus, who died at age 17 years and 5 months.

EDCS-22200740.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Refrius Maximianus, her son, and Hermes, his freedman, made this for Refria Vibiana, his mother, who lived 48 years.
EDCS-22200740.summary: A funerary dedication made by a son and freedman for Refria Vibiana, who died at age 48.

EDCS-22200741.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Robustania Saturnina of Tugga, who lived 88 years. Her son-in-law, Marcus Antonius Proculus, made this for his dearest wife from his own means.
EDCS-22200741.summary: A funerary dedication made by a son-in-law for his wife, Robustania Saturnina, who died at age 88.

EDCS-22200742.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Her son erected this for Rubria Fortunata, his worthy mother.
EDCS-22200742.summary: A funerary dedication made by a son for his mother, Rubria Fortunata.

EDCS-22200743.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For my wife Severia, who lived 40 years. Rusticanus made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-22200743.summary: A funerary dedication made by Rusticanus for his wife Severia, who died at age 40, and also for himself.

EDCS-22200744.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valeria Successa made this for her most rare daughter Rutilia Celestina, who lived 26 years.
EDCS-22200744.summary: A funerary dedication made by Valeria Successa for her daughter Rutilia Celestina, who died at age 26.

EDCS-22400694.translation: To Quintus Agrius Rusticianus, an excellent man, procurator of our Augustus in the province of Karthago, procurator of the private treasury throughout Italy, procurator of the whole domain of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, the bravest, [---]nicus.
EDCS-22400694.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Agrius Rusticianus, a high-ranking imperial official, likely a funerary monument or a dedication to him.

EDCS-22700001.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Sabinius Ingenuus lived 75 years. Bucia Saturnina for her most rare wife.
EDCS-22700001.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Quintus Sabinius Ingenuus, dedicated by his wife Bucia Saturnina.

EDCS-22700002.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Father and mother dedicated this to their sons, Quinto Safidio Iuliano and Safidiae Victoriae.
EDCS-22700002.summary: A funerary dedication by parents for their deceased children, Quinto Safidio Iuliano and Safidia Victoria.

EDCS-22700003.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Sallustius Speratus lived 70 years.
EDCS-22700003.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Marcus Sallustius Speratus.

EDCS-22700004.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sallustia Monnica lived 40 years, for her dearest friend, who deserved well.
EDCS-22700004.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sallustia Monnica, dedicated by a friend.

EDCS-22700005.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sallustia Quarta lived 60 years. Lollius Victor made this for his mother. She is buried here.
EDCS-22700005.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sallustia Quarta, dedicated by her son Lollius Victor.

EDCS-22700006.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sallustia Saturnina lived 45 years. Caius Annius Fortunatus and his sons made this for his wife and their mother, who deserved well.
EDCS-22700006.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sallustia Saturnina, dedicated by her husband Caius Annius Fortunatus and their sons.

EDCS-22700007.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sallustia Secundula lived 40 years. Lollius Victor made this for his wife. She is buried here.
EDCS-22700007.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sallustia Secundula, dedicated by her husband Lollius Victor.

EDCS-22700008.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Salvius Potitus lived 17 years.
EDCS-22700008.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Salvius Potitus.

EDCS-22700009.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Father and mother made this for their sweetest daughter. She lived 8 years and 11 days.
EDCS-22700009.summary: A funerary dedication by parents for their young daughter, recording her age at death.

EDCS-22700010.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Salvia Maxima lived 15 years.
EDCS-22700010.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Salvia Maxima.

EDCS-22700011.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Satrius Rogatus lived 51 years. Iulia Silvana made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-22700011.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Iulia Silvana for her deceased husband, Quintus Satrius Rogatus.

EDCS-22700012.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Her husband made this for the deserving Saturnina Felix.
EDCS-22700012.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by a husband for his deceased wife, Saturnina Felix.

EDCS-22700014.translation: Saturninus and Proculina for their son.
EDCS-22700014.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Saturninus and Proculina for their son.

EDCS-22700016.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Saturnina made this for her deserving son. He lived 7 years.
EDCS-22700016.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Saturnina for her son, recording his age at death.

EDCS-22700017.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Scantius Probatus lived 60 years. Clodianus Quintastus Iulianus Commodus made this for the most honorable man.
EDCS-22700017.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Scantius Probatus, who lived 60 years, erected by Clodianus Quintastus Iulianus Commodus.

EDCS-22700018.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Scantia Extricata lived 45 years. Victoria Commodus and Evatia made this for their mother.
EDCS-22700018.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Scantia Extricata, who lived 45 years, erected by her children Victoria Commodus and Evatia.

EDCS-22700019.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Secunda Gemina lived 35 years. Lucius Pontius Donatus made this.
EDCS-22700019.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Secunda Gemina, who lived 35 years, erected by Lucius Pontius Donatus.

EDCS-22700020.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Here rests Secura, a most devoted woman, whose affection was incomparable. She lived 23 years. Eros made this for his most rare wife, a devoted woman.
EDCS-22700020.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Secura, who lived 23 years, erected by her husband Eros, praising her devotion and affection.

EDCS-22700021.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sediasorus lived 24 years. His wife Sedia Fortunatus made this for me.
EDCS-22700021.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sediasorus, who lived 24 years, erected by his wife Sedia Fortunatus.

EDCS-22700022.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sedia Tertulla lived 65 years. Calidius Victor made this for his pious mother.
EDCS-22700022.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Sedia Tertulla by her son Calidius Victor, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700023.translation: To the Divine Shades. Seia Octavia lived 35 years. Aelius Herodianus made this for his wife who deserved well of him.
EDCS-22700023.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Seia Octavia by her husband Aelius Herodianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700024.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sempronius Impetratus lived 75 years. His granddaughter Impetrata made this.
EDCS-22700024.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Sempronius Impetratus by his granddaughter Impetrata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700025.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Sempronia Processa, his most pious daughter, who lived 30 years. Sempronius Impetratus her father and Caecilia Amoena her mother made this.
EDCS-22700025.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Sempronia Processa by her parents Sempronius Impetratus and Caecilia Amoena, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700026.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Manilius Senecio rests here. He lived 43 years. His most pious wife made this.
EDCS-22700026.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Quintus Manilius Senecio by his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700027.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sentius Surus lived 90 years.
EDCS-22700027.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Sentius Surus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700028.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Septimia Aequitas, an incomparable wife. Marcus Aurelius Ammianus and his daughter Aurelia Ammiana arranged for this to be made for their most sweet mother.
EDCS-22700028.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Septimia Aequitas by her husband Marcus Aurelius Ammianus and daughter Aurelia Ammiana, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700029.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Sergia Marcia Bassila, dearest mother, who lived 40 years. Caius Sergius Alfius Flavianus made this, as bequeathed in his will, from 2000 sesterces.
EDCS-22700029.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Sergia Marcia Bassila by her son Caius Sergius Alfius Flavianus, funded by an inheritance and dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700030.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sergi Victoricus lived 22 years and 10 months. His mother made this.
EDCS-22700030.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Sergi Victoricus by his mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700031.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sertoria Fortunata lived 21 years. Sertorius Saturninus her son made this. [This monument] cost 5 [sesterces?].
EDCS-22700031.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Sertoria Fortunata by her son Sertorius Saturninus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700032.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Servilius Ianuarius lived 70 years. Numitoria Data lived 60 years. Their sons made this for their parents. To the Spirits of the Underworld.
EDCS-22700032.summary: A funerary dedication for Servilius Ianuarius and Numitoria Data, made by their sons.

EDCS-22700033.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Sextilius Ingenuus lived 15 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-22700033.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Sextilius Ingenuus, noting his age and burial.

EDCS-22700034.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Sextilia Quieta placed this for her father, Publius Sextilius Liberalis, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-22700034.summary: A funerary dedication for Publius Sextilius Liberalis, made by his daughter Sextilia Quieta.

EDCS-22700035.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Sextilius Rogatus, her son, made this for Sextilia Spaesina, who lived 70 years.
EDCS-22700035.summary: A funerary dedication for Sextilia Spaesina, made by her son Caius Sextilius Rogatus.

EDCS-22700036.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Sextilia Lesbia lived thirty years and two months. Caius Iulius Ruf made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-22700036.summary: A funerary dedication for Sextilia Lesbia, made by her husband Caius Iulius Ruf.

EDCS-22700037.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Se Blandus lived 55 years. Sextius Fortunatus [made this].
EDCS-22700037.summary: A funerary dedication for Se Blandus, made by Sextius Fortunatus.

EDCS-22700038.summary: A funerary dedication for Silicianus, noting his age.

EDCS-22700039.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Silicius Primus lived 3 years.
EDCS-22700039.summary: A funerary dedication for Caius Silicius Primus, noting his young age.

EDCS-22700040.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Sextilius Felix, the second heir, made this for Lucius Silicius ninus, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-22700040.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Silicius ninus, made by his heir Lucius Sextilius Felix.

EDCS-22700041.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Silius Cicero made this for his devoted wife, Silia Veneria, a good keeper, who lived 43 years.
EDCS-22700041.summary: A funerary dedication for Silia Veneria, made by her husband Lucius Silius Cicero.

EDCS-22700042.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Silia Saturnina lived 65 years. Arrunti Longus Saturninus Felix Valens and Marcus, sons, made this for their most cherished mother.
EDCS-22700042.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Silia Saturnina, set up by her sons, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-22700043.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sittius Bassus lived 30 and a half years. His wife made this for her most worthy husband.
EDCS-22700043.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sittius Bassus, erected by his wife, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-22700044.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sittia Respecta lived 110 years.
EDCS-22700044.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sittia Respecta, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-22700045.translation: Seek joys not impervious to our losses, who recently wept, embracing a neighbor's tomb, after unfortunate marriage rites and eager nuptials, widowed from her husband within five months of the marriage bed, here lies Sotira, finding solace for harsh fortune, received again after death by her Augustale, if only, at least after such cruel losses, to renew her senses in eternal seats among the pious. She was of highest fidelity and probity.
EDCS-22700045.summary: This is a complex poetic epitaph for Sotira, lamenting her early death after marriage and suggesting a continuation of consciousness in the afterlife, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-22700046.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Spendon the pious lived 35 years. He rests here.
EDCS-22700046.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Spendon, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-22700047.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Stabilia Ianuaria lived 60 years. Maevii Herennianus and Saturninus arranged for this to be made for their well-deserving mother.
EDCS-22700047.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Stabilia Ianuaria, set up by her sons, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-22700048.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Successa, a pious woman, lived 31 years. Marcus Verrius Secundus made this for his wife. She rests here.
EDCS-22700048.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Successa, erected by her husband, Marcus Verrius Secundus, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-22700049.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Successa Fortunata lived [---] years. Her mother Iulia placed this.
EDCS-22700049.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Successa Fortunata, set up by her mother, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-22700050.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Successa Saturnina lived seven years. Her mother Iulia placed this from her own funds.
EDCS-22700050.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Successa Saturnina, set up by her mother, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-22700051.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sulpiciae Iustae lived 40 years. Aemilius Ianuarius [made this] for his deserving wife.
EDCS-22700051.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sulpicia Iusta, erected by her husband, Aemilius Ianuarius, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-22700052.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sulpicia Paulina lived 37 years, 5 months, 10 days. Domitius Sardonyx made this for his most rare wife, because of her merits. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-22700052.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sulpicia Paulina, erected by her husband Domitius Sardonyx.

EDCS-22700054.translation: To the Divine Manes. Tadia Prima lived 3 years. Iulius Baricio made this.
EDCS-22700054.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tadia Prima, erected by Iulius Baricio, likely her father.

EDCS-22700055.translation: To the Divine Manes. Tadia lived 8 years. Iulius Baricio made this.
EDCS-22700055.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tadia, erected by Iulius Baricio, likely her father.

EDCS-22700056.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Tadia Veneria, who lived 55 years. Iulia Veneria, her dutiful daughter, made this.
EDCS-22700056.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tadia Veneria, erected by her daughter Iulia Veneria.

EDCS-22700057.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Tannonius Iucundus, dutiful, lived 19 years. C. Silvanus made this for him as he deserved.
EDCS-22700057.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Tannonius Iucundus, erected by C. Silvanus.

EDCS-22700058.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Publius Tannonius Salutaris, Tannonius Speratus, his only son, made this for his father.
EDCS-22700058.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Tannonius Salutaris, erected by his son Tannonius Speratus.

EDCS-22700059.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Tannonius Secundus lived 20 years. Valerianus made this for his parents.
EDCS-22700059.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Tannonius Secundus, erected by Valerianus for his parents.

EDCS-22700060.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Tanonia Procula, daughter of Marcus, who lived 70 years. Gaius Caecilius Aductus, her heir, saw to this.
EDCS-22700060.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tanonia Procula, whose heir Gaius Caecilius Aductus arranged for the inscription.

EDCS-22700061.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Tannonian Romana, who lived 38 years. Iulius Sedatus made this for his most rare wife.
EDCS-22700061.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tannonian Romana, erected by her husband Iulius Sedatus.

EDCS-22700062.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Terentius Donatus lived 80 years. Iulia Nina made this for her husband.
EDCS-22700062.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Terentius Donatus, erected by his wife Iulia Nina.

EDCS-22700063.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Terentius Saturninus lived 150 years. Flora made this for her husband and son.
EDCS-22700063.summary: A funerary epitaph for Terentius Saturninus, set up by his wife Flora, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700064.translation: For Tertulla, who lived 45 years. Her husband made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-22700064.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tertulla, erected by her husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700065.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Titedius Fuscus.
EDCS-22700065.summary: A funerary dedication to the spirits of the departed for Caius Titedius Fuscus, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700066.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Titienus Optatus lived 19 years. Lucius Titienus Optatus, his father, made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-22700066.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Titienus Optatus, erected by his father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700067.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titinio Fortunato lived 75 years. Titini Donati made this for his deserving father.
EDCS-22700067.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titinio Fortunato, erected by his son Titini Donati, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700068.translation: To Titus Titius, son of Titus, of the Velina tribe, pious, from Ricina in Picenum.
EDCS-22700068.summary: A dedication or epitaph for Titus Titius, a Roman citizen with tribal and local affiliations, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700069.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Tonneia Avitula lived 3 years.
EDCS-22700069.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tonneia Avitula, who died as a child, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700070.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Tuccius Privatus lived [unknown years]. Salvia made this for her son.
EDCS-22700070.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tuccius Privatus, erected by his mother Salvia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700071.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Tuccius Fabia Silvanio of Berytus lived 60 years. Tuccia Tertulla made this for her well-deserving father.
EDCS-22700071.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Tuccius Fabia Silvanio, erected by his daughter Tuccia Tertulla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700072.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Tuccia Tertullina lived 30 years. Tuccius Bucco made this for his most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-22700072.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tuccia Tertullina, erected by her father Tuccius Bucco, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22700073.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Datus lived 11 years. His dear mother made this.
EDCS-22700073.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Julius Datus by his mother, likely dating from Roman Imperial times.

EDCS-22700075.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Valerius Candidus lived 80 years. He made this for one who deserved well, with his own money.
EDCS-22700075.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Candidus, who erected his own tomb and lived to 80.

EDCS-22700076.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Valerius Firmus lived 70 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-22700076.summary: A funerary inscription stating that Gaius Valerius Firmus, who lived to 70, is buried here.

EDCS-22700077.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Valerius Firmus lived 18 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-22700077.summary: A funerary inscription stating that Gaius Valerius Firmus, who died at 18, is buried here.

EDCS-22700078.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Valerius Ivatices lived 71 years. His wife Gebena Silvana made this.
EDCS-22700078.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Ivatices, erected by his wife Gebena Silvana.

EDCS-22700079.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valerius Martialis lived 35 years. His brothers Valerius Saturus and Valerius Felix made this.
EDCS-22700079.summary: A funerary inscription for Valerius Martialis, erected by his brothers Valerius Saturus and Valerius Felix.

EDCS-22700080.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. His devoted wife Iulia Satura made this for Aulus Valerius Saturninus. He lived 70 years.
EDCS-22700080.summary: A funerary inscription for Aulus Valerius Saturninus, erected by his wife Iulia Satura.

EDCS-22700081.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valerius Saturninus, also called Sinsicus, made this for himself while alive and for his family.
EDCS-22700081.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Valerius Saturninus (also known as Sinsicus) for himself and his family during his lifetime.

EDCS-22700082.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Valerius Torquatus lived 50 years. Antonia Donata established this.
EDCS-22700082.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Valerius Torquatus, established by Antonia Donata.

EDCS-22700083.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valerius Ingenuus made this for Valeria Ampliata, who lived 90 years.
EDCS-22700083.summary: A funerary inscription for Valeria Ampliata, erected by her son Valerius Ingenuus.

EDCS-22700084.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Valeria Tuca, her most beloved daughter.
EDCS-22700084.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Valeria Tuca, daughter of the deceased.

EDCS-22700085.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Valeria Celsina lived 70 years. Her sons made this for their mother.
EDCS-22700085.summary: This tombstone records the death of Valeria Celsina, who lived 70 years, and was erected by her sons.

EDCS-22700086.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Valeria Dionysia lived 20 years.
EDCS-22700086.summary: This tombstone records the death of Valeria Dionysia, who lived 20 years.

EDCS-22700087.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Valeria Donata, her most beloved daughter, who lived 18 years.
EDCS-22700087.summary: This tombstone records the death of Valeria Donata, daughter of the deceased, who lived 18 years.

EDCS-22700088.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Valeria Faustina lived 26 years. Aemilius made this.
EDCS-22700088.summary: This tombstone records the death of Valeria Faustina, who lived 26 years, and was erected by Aemilius.

EDCS-22700089.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Valeria Festa, most holy, lived 32 years; she rests here.
EDCS-22700089.summary: This tombstone records the death of Valeria Festa, who lived 32 years and is buried here.

EDCS-22700090.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Valeria Fortunula lived 26 years and 3 months. Vitellius Felix made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-22700090.summary: This tombstone records the death of Valeria Fortunula, who lived 26 years and 3 months, and was erected by her husband Vitellius Felix.

EDCS-22700091.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Valeria Gemellina, daughter of Lucius, his most rare wife, who lived 18 years. Marcus Coceius Marinus [made this].
EDCS-22700091.summary: This tombstone records the death of Valeria Gemellina, daughter of Lucius and wife of Marcus Coceius Marinus, who lived 18 years.

EDCS-22700092.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Valeria Istercula lived 72 years. Flavius Secundus made this for his most beloved wife.
EDCS-22700092.summary: This tombstone records the death of Valeria Istercula, who lived 72 years, and was erected by her husband Flavius Secundus.

EDCS-22700093.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Whoever you are who passes by and reads this inscription, I beg you, moved by piety, to say: 'May the earth be light for Donata.' She lived 65 years. Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Valeria Maxima, who lived 75 years and 6 months. Valerius Fuscus made this for his most dear grandmother and father, eternal abodes.
EDCS-22700093.summary: This complex tombstone records the death of Valeria Maxima, who lived 75 years and 6 months, and includes a plea to passersby to wish a peaceful afterlife for Donata (who lived 65 years), with Valerius Fuscus dedicating the monument to his grandmother and father.

EDCS-22700094.translation: To the Divine Shades. Valeria Potosa lived 50 years. Felicianus, Potosa, and Felicia, her grandchildren, made this for their beloved grandmother.
EDCS-22700094.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Potosa, erected by her grandchildren.

EDCS-22700095.translation: To the Divine Shades. Vetidius Saturninus made this for his pious wife, Valeria Rogatina. And whoever reads this inscription, may you say, 'Rogatina, may the earth be light upon you, for your piety.' She lived 23 years.
EDCS-22700095.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Rogatina, erected by her husband, who also includes a plea for piety and a wish for her to rest lightly.

EDCS-22700096.translation: To the Divine Shades. Valeria Rogatina lived 50 years. Her sons and heirs made this.
EDCS-22700096.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Rogatina, erected by her sons and heirs.

EDCS-22700097.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sinsicus made this for his daughter, Valeria Sufa, who lived 9 years.
EDCS-22700097.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Sufa, erected by her father.

EDCS-22700098.translation: To the Divine Shades. Valeria Venustula lived 70 years; Valerius Victor lived 80 years. His brother Vic made this for his mother.
EDCS-22700098.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Venustula and Valerius Victor, erected by their brother.

EDCS-22700100.translation: To the Divine Shades. This was made for Caio Vario Crescentino, a freedman, who deserved well, by Varia Clementia. He lived 40 years.
EDCS-22700100.summary: A funerary epitaph for the freedman Caio Vario Crescentino, erected by Varia Clementia.

EDCS-22700101.translation: To the Divine Shades. Her husband made this for Varia Rogata, a most rare woman, his excellent wife. She lived 26 years.
EDCS-22700101.summary: A funerary epitaph for Varia Rogata, erected by her husband.

EDCS-22700102.translation: To the Divine Shades. Vatinia Victoria lived 30 years.
EDCS-22700102.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vatinia Victoria.

EDCS-22700103.translation: To the Divine Shades. This was made for Vatronia Lucia, his most sacred wife. She lived 30 years.
EDCS-22700103.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vatronia Lucia, erected by her husband.

EDCS-22700104.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caius Caecilius Nigrinus made this for his well-deserving mother, Venuleia Castula. She lived 60 years.
EDCS-22700104.summary: A funerary epitaph for Venuleia Castula, erected by her son.

EDCS-22700105.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Venustius Saturninus lived for 50 years.
EDCS-22700105.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Quintus Venustius Saturninus, who lived to be 50 years old.

EDCS-22700106.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Vessius Caelsulus lived for 17 years.
EDCS-22700106.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Caius Vessius Caelsulus, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-22700107.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vettius Saturninus made this for his brother, Caius Vettius Antipatro, who lived for 28 years.
EDCS-22700107.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Caius Vettius Antipatro at age 28, with the monument erected by his brother, Vettius Saturninus.

EDCS-22700108.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vettia Venusta lived for 10 years.
EDCS-22700108.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Vettia Venusta, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-22700109.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vettia Victorina, the dutiful one, lived for 15 years. Vettius Saturninus made this for his most dutiful daughter; she rests here.
EDCS-22700109.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Vettius Saturninus erected a monument for his dutiful daughter, Vettia Victorina, who died at age 15.

EDCS-22700110.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Vibius Anicetus.
EDCS-22700110.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Vibius Anicetus, likely a tomb marker.

EDCS-22700111.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vibia Tyche lived for 37 years; she rests here. Vibius Anicetus made this for his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-22700111.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Vibius Anicetus erected a monument for his wife, Vibia Tyche, who died at age 37.

EDCS-22700112.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Vibius Doryphorus.
EDCS-22700112.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Vibius Doryphorus, likely a tomb marker.

EDCS-22700113.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Vibius Pudens lived for 22 years, 8 months, and 13 days.
EDCS-22700113.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Caius Vibius Pudens, who died at the age of 22 years, 8 months, and 13 days.

EDCS-22700114.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Vibius Catullinus made this for his deserving mother, Iunia Hilarisia, who lived 80 years; she lies buried here.
EDCS-22700114.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Caius Vibius Catullinus erected a monument for his deserving mother, Iunia Hilarisia, who died at age 80.

EDCS-22700115.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Vibius Catullinus made this for his most worthy wife, Trebia Tertia. She lived X years. She is buried here.
EDCS-22700115.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Trebia Tertia by her husband Gaius Vibius Catullinus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-22700116.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Vibius Ingenuus lived 12 years. Lucius Vibius Laetus, his father, made this.
EDCS-22700116.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Vibius Ingenuus, who died at age 12, dedicated by his father Lucius Vibius Laetus.

EDCS-22700117.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vibia Exorata lived 17 years. Lucius Vibius Laetus, her father, made this.
EDCS-22700117.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibia Exorata, who died at age 17, dedicated by her father Lucius Vibius Laetus.

EDCS-22700118.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Vibius Valens lived 13 years. His mother made this for her son.
EDCS-22700118.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Vibius Valens, who died at age 13, dedicated by his mother.

EDCS-22700119.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Vibia Donata, who lived 35 years. Her devoted sons dutifully made this for their mother.
EDCS-22700119.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibia Donata, who died at age 35, dedicated by her devoted sons.

EDCS-22700120.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vibia Felicula lived 57 years. Her most devoted son made this for his mother.
EDCS-22700120.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibia Felicula, who died at age 57, dedicated by her devoted son.

EDCS-22700121.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Munius Victor made this for his most loving wife, Vibia Ingenua, who lived 35 years, out of devotion.
EDCS-22700121.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibia Ingenua, who died at age 35, dedicated by her loving husband Munius Victor.

EDCS-22700123.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Victor, who was also called Utica, lived 20 years. Grillus lived 25 years. Fructus made this for himself and his brothers.
EDCS-22700123.summary: This is a funerary epitaph possibly recording multiple burials, with lives for Victor (Utica) and Grillus, and dedicated by Fructus for himself and his brothers.

EDCS-22700124.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Victoria, who lived 35 years, a dutiful wife.
EDCS-22700124.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Victoria, who died at age 35, dedicated by her husband as a dutiful wife.

EDCS-22700125.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Victoria and her husband Augendus. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Augendus and his wife Victoria. Gaius Vibius Celerinus, the master, made this.
EDCS-22700125.summary: This epitaph, likely from a household or estate, records the dedication for a couple, Victoria and Augendus, by their master Gaius Vibius Celerinus.

EDCS-22700126.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Victoricus, foster-child of Petronia Ampliata, lies here.
EDCS-22700126.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Victoricus, foster-child of Petronia Ampliata.

EDCS-22700127.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius set this up for Victoricus Primitivus and Secundus, his own slaves.
EDCS-22700127.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Aurelius for his slaves Victoricus Primitivus and Secundus.

EDCS-22700128.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Vibius Germanus, father, made this for his devoted son, Caius Vitellius Heraclas.
EDCS-22700128.summary: A funerary inscription made by Caius Vibius Germanus for his son, Caius Vitellius Heraclas.

EDCS-22700130.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Ulpius Adauctus lived for 70 years; he made this for his dearest husband.
EDCS-22700130.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Adauctus, who lived for 70 years, set up by his husband.

EDCS-22700131.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ulpius Felix lived for 60 years; Ulpia Aeternitas, Iulia, Donata, and Ulpius Donatus [made this] for their parent.
EDCS-22700131.summary: A funerary inscription for Ulpius Felix, who lived for 60 years, set up by his children.

EDCS-22700132.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ulpia Mustia lived for 45 years; Ulpia Aeternitas, Iulia, Donata, and Ulpius Donatus made this for their mother.
EDCS-22700132.summary: A funerary inscription for Ulpia Mustia, who lived for 45 years, set up by her children.

EDCS-22700133.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Ulpius Saturninus lived for one year.
EDCS-22700133.summary: A funerary inscription recording the short life of Marcus Ulpius Saturninus, who lived for one year.

EDCS-22700134.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Ulpius, father, made this for his dearest son, Lucius Crispinulus Rogatus.
EDCS-22700134.summary: A funerary inscription made by Lucius Ulpius for his son, Lucius Crispinulus Rogatus.

EDCS-22700135.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelius [made this] for his mother, Ulpia Agrippinia.
EDCS-22700135.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Aelius for his mother, Ulpia Agrippinia.

EDCS-22700136.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ulpia Bonifatia lived for 40 years; her husband, Iunius Hospitalis, made this.
EDCS-22700136.summary: A funerary inscription for Ulpia Bonifatia, who lived for 40 years, set up by her husband.

EDCS-22700137.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Ulpia Lucia lived 95 years.
EDCS-22700137.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Ulpia Lucia, who lived to be 95 years old.

EDCS-22700138.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Ulpia Marcia lived 100 years. Ulpius Rogatus made this.
EDCS-22700138.summary: This funerary inscription records that Ulpia Marcia lived to be 100 years old and was commemorated by Ulpius Rogatus.

EDCS-22700139.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Ulpia Urbana, who lived 40 years, Flavius Castus for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-22700139.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Flavius Castus to his wife, Ulpia Urbana, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-22700141.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Umbrius Mar[---]
EDCS-22700141.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Gaius Umbrius Mar[---].

EDCS-22700143.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Lucius Volussius Pudentus, who lived 27 years. Lucius Fabius Castresis, father, for his devoted son.
EDCS-22700143.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Volussius Pudentus at the age of 27, dedicated by his father Lucius Fabius Castresis.

EDCS-22700144.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Volusia Fortunata, most faithful always, made this tomb for herself while alive. She lived [---] years.
EDCS-22700144.summary: This funerary inscription records that Volusia Fortunata, a devoted woman, constructed her own tomb during her lifetime.

EDCS-22700145.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Volusia Victoria lived 36 years. Her husband, Bennius Felix, made this, and it was made while he was alive. He lived 80 years.
EDCS-22700145.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Volusia Victoria at 36 and her husband Bennius Felix, who also prepared the tomb for himself and lived to 80.

EDCS-22700146.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Urbanus lived 15 years. Suavis made this for his most devoted son.
EDCS-22700146.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Urbanus at the age of 15, commemorated by Suavis.

EDCS-22700147.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Urbanus, sweetest son, I lived 3 years. Felicianus made this, grieving. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Felix, sweetest son, I lived 6 years. Valerius, sweetest.
EDCS-22700147.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates two young sons, Urbanus (died aged 3) and Felix (died aged 6), with Felicianus and Valerius as the dedicator(s).

EDCS-22700148.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Geminius made this for his son Ursacius. He lived 8 months.
EDCS-22700148.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Ursacius at 8 months of age, with Geminius as the dedicator.

EDCS-22700149.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Urvinius Victor, son of Gaius, lived 30 years. Urvinius Secundus [made this] for his brother.
EDCS-22700149.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Urvinius Secundus to his deceased brother, Gaius Urvinius Victor, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-22700150.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Utica Valentina lived 35 years.
EDCS-22700150.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Utica Valentina, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-22700151.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Vibius Optatus lived 70 years.
EDCS-22700151.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Vibius Optatus, who lived 70 years.

EDCS-22700152.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Gemellus Apollinaris made [this] for his most unique daughter, Zabulla Quieta, who lived 13 years.
EDCS-22700152.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Gaius Gemellus Apollinaris to his daughter, Zabulla Quieta, who lived 13 years.

EDCS-22700153.translation: With incomparable affection from his wife. He lived 29 years. Her husband made [this] for her, who deserved well of him, for 2000 sesterces. She is buried here.
EDCS-22700153.summary: This funerary epitaph records that a wife lived 29 years and was honored by her husband with a monument costing 2000 sesterces.

EDCS-22700154.translation: She lived 46 years. [Someone] made [this] for her, who deserved well. May your bones rest well. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22700154.summary: This funerary epitaph honors a woman who lived 46 years and expresses wishes for her peaceful repose.

EDCS-22700155.translation: Her husband placed [this]. She lived 15 years and 8 months.
EDCS-22700155.summary: This funerary epitaph records that a woman lived 15 years and 8 months and was honored by her husband.

EDCS-22700156.translation: S.E.X. lived 31 years. He made [this] for his pious wife with his own money.
EDCS-22700156.summary: This funerary epitaph states that S.E.X. lived 31 years and financed a monument for his wife.

EDCS-23100001.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Antonia Sura, who lived 70 years. Julius Silvanus, an equestrian of the legion, made [this] for his most pious, most unique wife, a deserving soul, with his own money.
EDCS-23100001.summary: This funerary epitaph, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE, records that Julius Silvanus, an equestrian, erected a monument for his wife Antonia Sura, who lived 70 years.

EDCS-23100002.translation: Quintus, son of Quintus, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-23100002.summary: This inscription records Quintus, son of Quintus, fulfilling a votive offering.

EDCS-23100003.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Monnica, wife of Metillius BIOAOO, made this in memory of her husband. He lived 47 years.
EDCS-23100003.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife for her deceased husband, Metillius Monnicus.

EDCS-23100004.translation: She lived 15 years. She is buried here. May her bones rest in peace.
EDCS-23100004.summary: A short funerary epitaph for a young woman who died at 15.

EDCS-23100005.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Tertula Flavia lived 9 years.
EDCS-23100005.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child named Tertula Flavia.

EDCS-23100006.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Julius Gemellus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 60 years. He is here. May he rest in peace. And Sextus Julius Saturninus, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, his heirs, placed this here. He is here. May he rest in peace.
EDCS-23100006.summary: A funerary inscription set up by the heirs of two men, Lucius Julius Gemellus and Sextus Julius Saturninus.

EDCS-23100007.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Licinius Marcellus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 52 years. He is buried here. May his bones rest in peace. Lucilia Matrona, daughter of Marcus, is buried here.
EDCS-23100007.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Licinius Marcellus, with a mention of Lucilia Matrona also buried there.

EDCS-23100008.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Clodius Atticus lived 73 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23100008.summary: A funerary epitaph for Clodius Atticus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-23100009.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Falcidius Vindex lived 100 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23100009.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Falcidius Vindex, noting his exceptional age of 100 years.

EDCS-23100025.translation: To the Genius [of the place/of Lucius Licinus?]. Lucius Licinus... and
EDCS-23100025.summary: An incomplete dedication to a Genius, possibly related to Lucius Licinus.

EDCS-23100026.translation: To the Most Holy Genius of the town council, Caius Valerius Secundus, on the occasion of the honor of perpetual priesthood conferred upon him by the entire council, added 2400 sesterces to the legally required amount and 600 sesterces more, making a total of 5000 sesterces to be spent and dedicated.
EDCS-23100026.summary: A dedication by Caius Valerius Secundus to the Genius of his town council upon receiving the office of perpetual priest, detailing his financial contribution.

EDCS-23100027.translation: To the Genius of the Fatherland, Augustus. Caius Iulius V...
EDCS-23100027.summary: An incomplete dedication to the Genius of the Fatherland, associated with Augustus.

EDCS-23100030.translation: To the genius of the homeland and to Augustus, Publius Horatius Restitutianus, son of Publius Horatius Crescentis, of the Quirina tribe, formerly aedile, formerly prefect, formerly designated as one of the three officials for the four colonies of Cirta, erected this altar and also dedicated it, a perpetual priest and Roman knight, [signed] Euasius Mulasius.
EDCS-23100030.summary: An altar dedicated to the genius of the homeland and Augustus by a prominent local official in the four colonies of Cirta, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100032.translation: Sempronia Paulina, his wife, and Quintus Caecilius Castus, perpetual priest, saw to the construction and dedication of this monument, in addition to the legal amount of 2,000 sesterces, in fulfillment of a promise of 4,000 sesterces for the office of perpetual priest.
EDCS-23100032.summary: A dedication made by a wife and son to fulfill a deceased husband's promised endowment for his perpetual priesthood, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100033.translation: Cnaeus Baebius Caerealis Orta, son of Cnaeus, of the Stellatina tribe, perpetual priest, in addition to the legal endowment of 2,000 sesterces, contributed 1,700 sesterces from 3,700 sesterces and an additional 1,000 sesterces to the public treasury, and he himself dedicated it.
EDCS-23100033.summary: A perpetual priest in Caere made a substantial additional contribution to his endowment and the public treasury, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100035.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Protector of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 15th time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 4th time, Proconsul, son of the deified Severus, and to Julia Augusta, mother of the Emperor and of the camp and of the Senate and of the homeland, Lucius Propertius Martialis, son of Lucius, a veteran and perpetual priest, having had the honor of the perpetual priesthood conferred upon him by the town council, and having promised 4,000 sesterces, had the monument constructed and dedicated with the sum increased.
EDCS-23100035.summary: A veteran and perpetual priest dedicated a monument honoring Jupiter and the imperial family, likely during the reign of Caracalla (211-217 CE).

EDCS-23100036.translation: To Juno Concordia Augusta, for the health of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 15th time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 4th time, Proconsul, son of the deified Severus, and to Julia Augusta, mother of the Emperor and of the camp and of the Senate and of the homeland, Lucius Propertius Martialis, son of Lucius Victor, a veteran and perpetual priest, and his brother Propertius Victor, a retired soldier, and his son, saw to the construction and dedication of this monument with the sum increased, having promised 4,000 sesterces for the honor of the priesthood conferred upon him.
EDCS-23100036.summary: Two brothers, one a veteran and perpetual priest, dedicated a monument to Juno Concordia Augusta for the imperial family's health, likely during the reign of Caracalla (211-217 CE).

EDCS-23100037.translation: To Minerva Augusta, Lucius Licinius Optatianus undertook the construction and dedication, having contributed 6,140 sesterces for the office of pontificate.
EDCS-23100037.summary: A monument to Minerva Augusta was dedicated by Lucius Licinius Optatianus upon his assumption of the pontificate, likely in the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100041.translation: To Victoria Germanica Augusta, Caius Julius Secundinus, with the consent of his father Iulius Tertiolus and his paternal uncles Iulius Thevestinus and Iulius Silvanus, for the honor of the perpetual priesthood, having contributed the legal sum of 2,000 sesterces, and double sportulas to the joint senators and 120 sesterces to each curia, undertook the construction and dedication of a statue which he had promised for 8,000 sesterces.
EDCS-23100041.summary: A statue dedicated to Victoria Germanica Augusta by Caius Julius Secundinus, likely in the 3rd century CE, commemorating his perpetual priesthood and including provisions for public distributions.

EDCS-23100043.translation: By order of the Senate, dedicated by Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus, legate of the Emperor with proconsular power, while Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 23rd time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland.
EDCS-23100043.summary: An inscription recording the dedication of a monument by a legatus Augusti pro praetore on behalf of the Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely during his 23rd year of tribunician power (159-160 CE).

EDCS-23100049.translation: The state of Verecundenses, dedicated by Marcus Aemilius Macro Saturninus, legate of the Emperor with proconsular power and patron.
EDCS-23100049.summary: A dedication made by the community of Verecundenses, formally presented by their patron and imperial legate, Marcus Aemilius Macro Saturninus.

EDCS-23100052.translation: To the deified Commodus Augustus, by decree of the town council, with public funds.
EDCS-23100052.summary: A monument erected to the deified Commodus Augustus from public funds by decree of the town council, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100053.translation: To the Emperors, Caesars Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius, the greatest Parthian, the greatest Briton, the greatest German, Augusti.
EDCS-23100053.summary: This inscription names the emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, likely as part of a dedication or honorific monument from the Severan period.

EDCS-23100054.translation: To Julia Domna Augusta, mother of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Briton, father of the fatherland, of the camp, of the senate, and of the fatherland, by decree of the local councilors, with public funds.
EDCS-23100054.summary: This inscription honors Julia Domna, mother of Caracalla, likely on a public monument funded by the city, during the reign of Caracalla.

EDCS-23100055.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix, invincible, beloved, prince of youth, father of the fatherland, son of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, greatest Parthian, father of the fatherland, and [greatest Parthian, greatest Briton, greatest German, chief priest, father of the fatherland], by decree of the local councilors, with public funds.
EDCS-23100055.summary: This inscription honors Caracalla, son of Septimius Severus, detailing his titles and lineage, likely for a public monument funded by the city and decreed by its councilors.

EDCS-23100057.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus, Invictus, Pius, Felix, Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, Imperator for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, grandson of the divine Gordian, and son of the brother of the divine Gordian, the state of the Verecundenses, devoted to his divine majesty and authority.
EDCS-23100057.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument to Emperor Gordian III by the community of Verecundenses, highlighting his titles and divine lineage.

EDCS-23100058.translation: For the health and safety of our lords Valerian and Gallienus Augusti, Gaius Julius Victorinus, aedile, made and dedicated with his own money the statue of Marsyas which he had promised in honor of his aedileship.
EDCS-23100058.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue of Marsyas by the aedile Gaius Julius Victorinus to honor his office and ensure the well-being of Emperors Valerian and Gallienus.

EDCS-23100059.translation: To the Emperor Gaius Marcus Aurelius Carus, Invictus, Pius, Felix, Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, the state of the municipality of Verecundenses.
EDCS-23100059.summary: This inscription records the devotion of the municipality of Verecundenses to Emperor Carus, acknowledging his imperial titles.

EDCS-23100062.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Numerian, Invictus, Pius, Felix, Augustus, the state of the municipality of Verecundenses.
EDCS-23100062.summary: This inscription records the devotion of the municipality of Verecundenses to Emperor Numerian, acknowledging his imperial titles.

EDCS-23100064.translation: To the most strong and most victorious Emperor Pius Felix Augustus, the state of the municipality of Verecundenses.
EDCS-23100064.summary: This inscription expresses the devotion of the municipality of Verecundenses to an unnamed 'most strong and victorious Emperor Pius Felix Augustus', likely dating to the late 3rd century.

EDCS-23100065.translation: To the perpetual and most strong Caesar Flavius Constantine Junior, the order of the municipality of Verecundenses, devoted to their divine majesty and authority.
EDCS-23100065.summary: This inscription records the devotion of the municipal order of Verecundenses to Caesar Flavius Constantine Junior, likely dating to the early 4th century.

EDCS-23100071.translation: To Publilius Justus, Caeciliana Numisiana, most distinguished children, grandsons of Petronius Justus.
EDCS-23100071.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring the deceased children of Publilius Justus and Caeciliana Numisiana, who were grandsons of Petronius Justus.

EDCS-23100072.translation: To Ulpia Aristonica, daughter of Marcus, for Marcus Valerius Maximianus, consul, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-23100072.summary: This is a public dedication honouring Ulpia Aristonica, likely commissioned by the town council.

EDCS-23100073.translation: The legate of the emperor with the power of a praetor, Lucius Iulius Iucundus, made this for the honour of the augurship with 4400 sesterces, as he promised, from his own money, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-23100073.summary: This inscription records a public work funded by Lucius Iulius Iucundus to celebrate his appointment as an augur.

EDCS-23100074.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Iovinus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived for 25 years. Lucius Arranius Saturninus made this.
EDCS-23100074.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Iovinus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by Lucius Arranius Saturninus.

EDCS-23100075.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Quintus Arruntius Ianuarius, a veteran, my father, who lived among the living for 67 years and 7 months.
EDCS-23100075.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Arruntius Ianuarius, a veteran, erected by his son.

EDCS-23100076.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Decia Victoria, who lived for 57 years. Lucius Emeritus Felix, a veteran, willingly made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-23100076.summary: This tombstone was erected by Lucius Emeritus Felix, a veteran, for his wife, Decia Victoria.

EDCS-23100077.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Gaius Florius Honoratus, son of Gaius, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, who served at the praetorium. He lived 22 years. Florius Mauricus made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-23100077.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Florius Honoratus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his father.

EDCS-23100078.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Garrillia Marcellina, who lived 55 years. Lucius Catanus, a veteran, for his deserving wife.
EDCS-23100078.summary: This tombstone was erected by Lucius Catanus, a veteran, for his wife, Garrillia Marcellina.

EDCS-23100079.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Gaius Julius Secundus, a soldier of the legion, a deserving father, for his memory and as testimony.
EDCS-23100079.summary: This inscription is a tombstone dedicated to Gaius Julius Secundus, a soldier and father.

EDCS-23100080.translation: Marius Gemellus, a veteran, added 4000 sesterces to the required amount of 6000 sesterces for the honour of the perpetual priesthood conferred upon him by the order. His brother and heir, Marcus Marius Gemellus, saw to its construction and dedication.
EDCS-23100080.summary: This inscription details a financial contribution made by Marius Gemellus, a veteran, to secure a perpetual priesthood, with his brother and heir completing the dedication.

EDCS-23100081.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Gaius Paconius Bassus, a veteran, who lived 82 years. His sons made this.
EDCS-23100081.summary: This tombstone was erected by the sons for their father, Gaius Paconius Bassus, a veteran.

EDCS-23100082.translation: Gaius Paconius Saturninus, a veteran honorably discharged from the Praetorian Guard, and Paconia Bassilla, and Paconius Iustus, and Paconius Emeritus, a veteran honorably discharged from the 3rd Augustan Legion, and his wife Antonia Saturnina, and their sons Paconius Antonius Fortunatus, Bassus, Emeritus, and the sons of Iustus: Fortunata, Saturninus, Bassila, Paconius, Iustus, and Emeritus, veterans, made and dedicated this [tomb] for themselves and their families.
EDCS-23100082.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by several veterans and their families, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE, to honor their kin.

EDCS-23100083.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ulpia Monica placed this monument for her husband, Cornelius, a veteran of the legion, who lived 12 years, a most loving husband.
EDCS-23100083.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelius, a legionary veteran, erected by his wife Ulpia Monica, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100084.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aulus Valerius Victor, a veteran, lived 75 years. His wife and heir, Lia Valentina.
EDCS-23100084.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aulus Valerius Victor, a veteran, set up by his wife and heir, Lia Valentina, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100085.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gnaeus Baebius Cerealis, son of Gnaeus, of the Stellatina tribe, a resident of the municipia of Orto and Faleri, and Verecundenses, perpetual flamen and princeps, and his daughter Severa, daughter of Ingenuus Severus, [set this up for] their parents; the father lived 80 years, the mother [lived] years.
EDCS-23100085.summary: A funerary dedication for parents, possibly by their children, honoring their roles as perpetual flamen and princeps in the municipia of Orto and Faleri, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100086.translation: Gnaeus Baebius Proculinus, his son, dedicated this in honor of his augurship, having promised 4,000 sesterces.
EDCS-23100086.summary: A dedication by Gnaeus Baebius Proculinus for his augurship, funded by a promised sum of money, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100087.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Caecilius Barbarus, a Roman knight, [set this up for] his brother Quintus Caecilius Rufus, a Roman knight, who lived 51 years.
EDCS-23100087.summary: A funerary dedication by Quintus Caecilius Barbarus for his brother Quintus Caecilius Rufus, both Roman knights, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100089.translation: The perpetual flamen and decurion of the municipium of Lamasba, with his own money, made and dedicated this temple, completed and adorned with paintings, [at a cost] of 4,000 sesterces, for the innocents.
EDCS-23100089.summary: This inscription records the construction and dedication of a temple by a local magistrate, a perpetual flamen and decurion, at his own expense, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100090.summary: A fragment of an inscription, likely funerary, with missing details about the dedicators and the deceased, possibly from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100091.translation: Curius Aeticus, the heir, made this for the deserving. Sacred to the departed spirits. Laelius Ianuarius lived seventy years.
EDCS-23100091.summary: A funerary epitaph for Laelius Ianuarius, erected by his heir Curius Aeticus, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100092.translation: Hail, mother! Aemilia Vitalis, who lived among the living 82 years and 2 months. Purpurius, her son, made this.
EDCS-23100092.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aemilia Vitalis, erected by her son Purpurius, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100093.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Amullia Quintula, mother-in-law, lived 70 years.
EDCS-23100093.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Amullia Quintula, recording her relationship to the dedicator and her age at death.

EDCS-23100094.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Anneia Maxima lived 80 years. Anneia Libosa, her daughter, had this made at her own expense for her dearest mother.
EDCS-23100094.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where the daughter Anneia Libosa honors her mother Anneia Maxima, providing her age at death and noting the expense was borne by the dedicator.

EDCS-23100095.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Antonius Severus lived 55 years. Lucius Arranius Saturninus made this.
EDCS-23100095.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Severus, recording his age at death and the maker of the monument.

EDCS-23100096.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Marcus Antonius Severus the Younger. Lucius Arranius Saturninus, pious heir.
EDCS-23100096.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication to Marcus Antonius Severus the Younger by his heir, Lucius Arranius Saturninus.

EDCS-23100097.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Antonius Valens lived 70 years. Antonius Severus and Iovinus (made this) for (their) father.
EDCS-23100097.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Valens, recording his age at death and made by his sons, Antonius Severus and Iovinus.

EDCS-23100098.translation: Concessa the pious lived 60 years. Lucius Arranius Saturninus made this.
EDCS-23100098.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Concessa, recording her age at death and the maker of the monument.

EDCS-23100099.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Gaius Bruttius Martialis, dearest father, lived 50 years. Gaius Bruttius Faustinus, Gaius Bruttius Ianuarius, Gaius Bruttius (made this).
EDCS-23100099.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Gaius Bruttius Martialis, providing his age at death and listing his sons who erected the monument.

EDCS-23100100.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tiberius Claudius Flavianus lived 70 years. Claudii Flavianus, Silvanus, and Victor made this in memory of their dearest father.
EDCS-23100100.summary: This funerary epitaph records Tiberius Claudius Flavianus' age at death and was erected by his sons in his memory.

EDCS-23100101.translation: For Cornelia Fortunata, lived [XX?] years. Her sons made this.
EDCS-23100101.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Fortunata, mentioning her age (partially lost) and that it was made by her sons.

EDCS-23100102.translation: Lucius Crepereius Hermogenes lived 34 years. Lucius Crepereius Ariston (made this) for his pious brother.
EDCS-23100102.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of Lucius Crepereius Hermogenes and was erected by his brother, Lucius Crepereius Ariston.

EDCS-23100103.translation: Gaius Fabius made this for his wife.
EDCS-23100103.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Gaius Fabius for his wife.

EDCS-23100104.translation: Flavius Verus, a devoted father, made this for his dearest daughters, Flavia Vera, who lived 18 years, and Flavia Sura, who lived 12 years.
EDCS-23100104.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Flavius Verus for his two daughters, Flavia Vera and Flavia Sura, with their ages at death.

EDCS-23100105.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Florius Cesellianus, who lived 30 years. Florius Mauricius made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-23100105.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Florius Mauricius for his son, Gaius Florius Cesellianus, with his age at death.

EDCS-23100106.translation: To the departed spirits. Gemellus Pomponius, who lived 20 years. Gemellus Scribonius made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-23100106.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Gemellus Scribonius for his son, Gemellus Pomponius, with his age at death.

EDCS-23100107.translation: To their mothers, the twin Geminae and Vandiae, and to Acedonia Timinia and Marciana, their sons dedicated this.
EDCS-23100107.summary: This is a dedication by sons to their mothers, Geminae, Vandiae, Acedonia Timinia, and Marciana.

EDCS-23100108.translation: To the departed spirits. To Herennia Fortunata, who lived 20 years. Sutorius Victorinus made this for his most beloved wife.
EDCS-23100108.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Sutorius Victorinus for his wife, Herennia Fortunata, with her age at death.

EDCS-23100109.translation: To the departed spirits. Masamon, son of Alcimus Iapthmes, who lived 55 years.
EDCS-23100109.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Masamon, son of Alcimus Iapthmes, with his age at death.

EDCS-23100110.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Julius VLCIDRRIVS, who lived 65 years. Ael[...]
EDCS-23100110.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Julius VLCIDRRIVS, with his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100111.translation: To the departed spirits. To Julia Maximosa, who lived 17 years. Iulius Tertiolus made this for his dearest daughter.
EDCS-23100111.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Iulius Tertiolus for his daughter, Julia Maximosa, with her age at death.

EDCS-23100112.translation: To the departed spirits. To Iulia Pannonia, who lived 45 years. Nonius Felix [made this] for his most modest wife.
EDCS-23100112.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Nonius Felix for his wife, Iulia Pannonia, with her age at death.

EDCS-23100113.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Sempronia lived for six years. Caius Iulius Sempronianus and Sallustia Quartilla [made this] for their daughter.
EDCS-23100113.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of a young girl, Iulia Sempronia, with her parents named as the dedicants.

EDCS-23100114.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Lucila, dearest mother. Gura and Sutara, daughters, placed this tomb.
EDCS-23100114.summary: A funerary epitaph where daughters Gura and Sutara honor their deceased mother, Lucila, by erecting a tomb.

EDCS-23100115.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Marius Rufus lived for 80 years. Marcus Marius Gemellus made [this] for his most dutiful father.
EDCS-23100115.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Marius Rufus, erected by his son, Marcus Marius Gemellus.

EDCS-23100116.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Maria Lauta lived for 13 years and 4 months. Iulia Tevestina [made this] for her dearest daughter.
EDCS-23100116.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the short life of Maria Lauta, made by her mother Iulia Tevestina.

EDCS-23100117.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Naevius Pudens lived for 70 years. His sons made [this] for their dutiful father. He is buried here.
EDCS-23100117.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Naevius Pudens, erected by his sons.

EDCS-23100118.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sagria Prima Verna Italica lived for 70 years. Her grandchildren made [this] for her.
EDCS-23100118.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sagria Prima Verna Italica, erected by her grandchildren.

EDCS-23100119.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Trebia Iucunda lived for 1 year and 7 months. Trebius Fortunatus [made this] for his sweetest daughter.
EDCS-23100119.summary: A funerary epitaph for Trebia Iucunda, erected by her father Trebius Fortunatus.

EDCS-23100120.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Fortunata lived for 60 years. Caius Terentius Severus made [this] for his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-23100120.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Fortunata, erected by her husband Caius Terentius Severus.

EDCS-23100121.summary: A dedication to the divine Caelestis Augusta, likely a goddess, without further context on the dedicator or purpose.

EDCS-23100126.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Propagator, Protector, and to the Genius of the spring. Lucius Ficilius Felix, veteran father, constructed the basin, Lucius Ficilius Secundus, his son, paid his vow willingly.
EDCS-23100126.summary: A dedication to Jupiter and the Genius of a spring, with one person constructing a basin and his son fulfilling a vow, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23100127.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Quintus Aelius Rufinus Polianus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, prefect of the Mauritanian cohort, military tribune of the III Augusta legion, a beloved friend of fraternal affection, by Titus Atilius Iuvenalis, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, prefect of the VI Commagenian cohort.
