EDCS-23100127.summary: This is a tomb inscription honoring Quintus Aelius Rufinus Polianus, detailing his military and prefectural career and dedicating him with affection by Titus Atilius Iuvenalis.

EDCS-23100128.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Marius Donatus, veteran, lived 75 years. Caius Marius Secundus made this for his dearest father, with care undertaken by Iulia Maiorica, his wife.
EDCS-23100128.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Caius Marius Donatus, a veteran, made by his son Caius Marius Secundus and cared for by his wife Iulia Maiorica.

EDCS-23100129.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sextus Rutilius Melior, soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 45 years. To his deserving friend, Munatius Renuntianus, optio princeps.
EDCS-23100129.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Sextus Rutilius Melior, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, honoring him as a deserving friend by Munatius Renuntianus.

EDCS-23100130.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. His sons made this for Marius Antoninus, priest.
EDCS-23100130.summary: This tomb inscription is a dedication made by the sons for Marius Antoninus, a priest.

EDCS-23100131.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Flavia Aphodina lived 75 years.
EDCS-23100131.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Flavia Aphodina, stating her name and age.

EDCS-23100132.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Flavia Secunda lived 100 years. Her son Calpurnius made this for his most dutiful mother.
EDCS-23100132.summary: This tomb inscription records the death of Flavia Secunda at the age of 100, with the monument erected by her dutiful son Calpurnius.

EDCS-23100134.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Iulius Victor lived 97 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23100134.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Caius Iulius Victor, stating his age and burial location.

EDCS-23100135.translation: Lucius Octavius Scaeva, son of Lucius, of the Arnensian tribe, lived 8 years, 8 months, and 21 days. His father made this.
EDCS-23100135.summary: This tomb inscription records the death of Lucius Octavius Scaeva at a young age, with the monument erected by his father.

EDCS-23100136.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Lucius Risennius Rufinus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe. [---].
EDCS-23100136.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated to Lucius Risennius Rufinus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, with some damaged text.

EDCS-23100137.summary: This is a minimal tomb inscription for Saturninus, simply stating his name and that he lived.

EDCS-23100138.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Victor, most faithful, lived 40 years. Julius Rhodinus made this for a deserving friend. He is buried here.
EDCS-23100138.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Julius Rhodinus to his faithful friend Victor, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-23100139.translation: Publius Fundanius, fulfilling his vow, dedicated this altar as a gift to Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, conqueror of Armenia and Parthia the Great, conqueror of Media, with his 23rd tribunician power, 2nd consulship, father of the country, and to his children and his divine house.
EDCS-23100139.summary: This dedication honors Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and his family by Publius Fundanius, likely from the period of his 23rd tribunician power (c. 213-214 AD).

EDCS-23100140.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Aurelius, son of Lucius, a veteran, lived 70 years. Aurelia Lambaese Tertio made this.
EDCS-23100140.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Aurelius, a veteran who lived 70 years, dedicated by Aurelia Lambaese Tertio.

EDCS-23100141.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Bebius Iulianus, of the legionary arms of the III Augusta legion, lived 45 years. Iunia Portia made this for her husband.
EDCS-23100141.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gaius Bebius Iulianus, associated with the III Augusta legion, who lived 45 years and was commemorated by his wife Iunia Portia.

EDCS-23100142.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Memius Rogatus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, lived 56 years. He made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-23100142.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Memius Rogatus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, who commissioned his tomb at the age of 56.

EDCS-23100143.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Memius, of the III Augusta legion. [---]
EDCS-23100143.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning Memius and the III Augusta legion.

EDCS-23100144.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Vibia Maxima lived 31 years. Gaius Iulius Valens, a veteran of Syrian origin, approved in the III Gallica legion, discharged from the III Augusta legion, made this for his deserving wife, with their common children Basila and Valentinus.
EDCS-23100144.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Gaius Iulius Valens, a Syrian veteran of the III Gallica and formerly III Augusta legions, for his wife Vibia Maxima, who died at 31, and their children.

EDCS-23100145.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Domitius Victor lived 50 years.
EDCS-23100145.summary: A simple funerary inscription recording the death of Domitius Victor at the age of 50.

EDCS-23100146.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Iulius Blandus lived 70 years. Lucius Iulius Blandus made this for his deserving father.
EDCS-23100146.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Iulius Blandus at 70, commemorated by his son of the same name.

EDCS-23100147.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Statilia Iucunda lived 60 years. Lucius Iulius Blandus made this for his deserving mother.
EDCS-23100147.summary: A funerary inscription for Statilia Iucunda, who lived 60 years, dedicated by her son Lucius Iulius Blandus.

EDCS-23100148.translation: To the departed spirits. Donata lived 40 years. Maemius Rogatus made this for his wife.
EDCS-23100148.summary: A funerary epitaph for Donata, erected by her husband Maemius Rogatus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100149.translation: To the departed spirits. Memmia Fortunata lived 40 years. Iulia Syra made this for her most beloved daughter.
EDCS-23100149.summary: A funerary epitaph for Memmia Fortunata, erected by her mother Iulia Syra, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100150.translation: To the departed spirits. Mulea Monnica made this for herself while alive, well-deserving of herself; here she lies.
EDCS-23100150.summary: A funerary epitaph for Mulea Monnica, who prepared her own tomb during her lifetime, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100151.translation: To the departed spirits. Oppius Asarcius, son of Oppius, lived 40 years. Memmia Veneria made this for her deserving husband, along with her parent Publius Trebius Honoratus.
EDCS-23100151.summary: A funerary epitaph for Oppius Asarcius, erected by his wife Memmia Veneria and his parent Publius Trebius Honoratus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100152.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Valerius Maximus lived 3 years. Sextia Antonia, his mother, made this. To the departed spirits. Gaius Valerius Musteolus lived 35 years. Sextia Antonia made this for her deserving betrothed.
EDCS-23100152.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating two individuals named Gaius Valerius, one a child and the other a betrothed, erected by Sextia Antonia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100153.translation: A mausoleum which he ordered to be built by his will within the day specified in the will, for Volussenia Maiorica, his wife, and for their sons.
EDCS-23100153.summary: A testamentary inscription commissioning the construction of a mausoleum for a wife and sons, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100156.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the Nymphs, for the safety of the Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus Augustus, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus 'Pius Felix Parthicus', Julia Augusta, mother of the Emperors and of the camp, and of the entire divine house, by a detachment of the Third Augustan Legion, staying to perform the fodder cutting, have willingly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-23100156.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter and Nymphs for the safety of Emperors Severus, Caracalla, and Julia Domna, by a detachment of the Third Augustan Legion, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-23100158.translation: In the age of our lords Diocletian and Maximian, the most blessed, and Constantius and Maximian [who were Caesars]. Valerius Florus.
EDCS-23100158.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely mentioning the Tetrarchic emperors, possibly a dedication or a marker related to Valerius Florus, dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-23100159.translation: To the Emperor, holding tribunician power, consul, proconsul, greatest Parthicus, Maximian Pius Felix Invictus Augustus. Flavius Flavianus, a most perfect man, governor of the province of Numidia, from the cornicularii of the most eminent prefects of the praetorium, dedicated to their divinity and majesty.
EDCS-23100159.summary: A dedication to Emperor Maximian by Flavius Flavianus, governor of Numidia, likely dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-23100160.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Julianus, Pius Felix, powerful in every kind of virtue, unconquered prince, restorer of liberty and the Roman religion, and conqueror of the world.
EDCS-23100160.summary: An honorific inscription praising Emperor Julian (the Apostate), likely dating to his reign in the mid-4th century AD.

EDCS-23100162.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Felix, a veteran, lived 70 years. His heirs had this made.
EDCS-23100162.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Felix, a veteran, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE, with his heirs commissioning the monument.

EDCS-23100164.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Julius Speratus, a veteran, had this made for his dearest wife, Papiria Venusta, who lived approximately 38 years.
EDCS-23100164.summary: A funerary epitaph for Papiria Venusta, wife of the veteran Caius Julius Speratus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23100165.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Sentius Valerianus, a veteran, formerly a principalis adiutor (second-in-command) of the commander in Legio III Augusta; he lived 53 years. Lucius Sentius Verus and Victor, his sons and heirs, and Julius Victor, their grandfather, a veteran, saw to its construction for 1,000,000 sesterces.
EDCS-23100165.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Sentius Valerianus, a veteran and former officer, likely from the 2nd century CE, commissioned by his sons and grandfather.

EDCS-23100166.translation: He honorably completed his service as a legionary, a principalis signifer (standard-bearer), and was honorably discharged. Elected perpetual flamen in his home city, he always maintained an exceptionally neat appearance. He lived over 60 years and arranged for this to be made for himself during his lifetime.
EDCS-23100166.summary: A self-funded epitaph for a veteran who served as a standard-bearer and later became a perpetual flamen, dating likely to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100167.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. His heirs had this made for the veteran, who lived 7 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23100167.summary: A funerary epitaph for a veteran who died at the age of 7, with his heirs commissioning the monument, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23100169.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Aemilia Major, his most obedient daughter, who lived 20 years, because of her outstanding conduct and devotion towards him. Lucius Aemilius Felix, her father.
EDCS-23100169.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aemilia Major, a devoted daughter, commissioned by her father Lucius Aemilius Felix, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23100170.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Celia Rogata had this made for herself while alive.
EDCS-23100170.summary: A self-funded funerary epitaph for Celia Rogata, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23100171.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Trebius Felix had this made for his wife, Cornelia Peia.
EDCS-23100171.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelia Peia, commissioned by her husband Lucius Trebius Felix, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23100172.translation: For Fabia Fortunata, his most devoted wife, who lived 45 years. Her husband Martialis.
EDCS-23100172.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fabia Fortunata, commissioned by her husband Martialis, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23100173.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Geminius Castulus lived 55 years. Silvana had this made for her most sweet husband.
EDCS-23100173.summary: A funerary epitaph for Geminius Castulus, commissioned by his wife Silvana, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23100174.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Helvia Rogata lived 80 years. Her husband Iulius [set this up].
EDCS-23100174.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of Helvia Rogata and mentioning her husband as the dedicator.

EDCS-23100175.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vatinia Verana lived 30 years. Caius Iulius Severus lived 105 years and 6 months. Caius Iulius Severus [set this up].
EDCS-23100175.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages of Vatinia Verana and Caius Iulius Severus, with the latter presumably setting up the monument for both.

EDCS-23100176.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Emerita lived 100 years. Her son Marcus Iulius Felix made this for his mother.
EDCS-23100176.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of Iulia Emerita and states that her son Marcus Iulius Felix erected the monument.

EDCS-23100177.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Rogatina lived [missing age].
EDCS-23100177.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph recording the name of Iulia Rogatina, with her age and the rest of the inscription missing.

EDCS-23100178.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Of Lucius Modius Quadratus, son of Quintus, lived 60 years. His heirs made this.
EDCS-23100178.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of Lucius Modius Quadratus and states that his heirs erected the monument.

EDCS-23100179.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quinto Nonio Nundinario lived 90 years. His most dutiful sons Nonius Crescens, Nonius Ianuarius, Nonius Quintulus, and Nonius Ho[---] [set this up] for their father from memory.
EDCS-23100179.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of Quinto Nonio Nundinario and names his four sons as the dedicators.

EDCS-23100180.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Of Geminia Prima, most kind. Caius Aemi[---] [set this up].
EDCS-23100180.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph mentioning Geminia Prima and her epithet, with the dedicator's name cut off.

EDCS-23100181.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Sextius Donatus lived 67 years. Octavius Torquatus [set this up].
EDCS-23100181.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of Quintus Sextius Donatus and identifies Octavius Torquatus as the dedicator.

EDCS-23100182.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vibia Dionisia lived 28 years. She is buried here by her parents.
EDCS-23100182.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of Vibia Dionisia and states that her parents buried her here.

EDCS-23100183.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vibia Victoria lived 24 years. Aurelius made this for his dearest daughter.
EDCS-23100183.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of Vibia Victoria and states that Aurelius erected the monument for his daughter.

EDCS-23100184.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Voluntilia Zoa, my wife, lived 31 years and 6 months. [---] Viuius [made this].
EDCS-23100184.summary: A funerary epitaph for a wife, set up by her husband, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100185.translation: To [his] father. He lived 58 years and made [this] for himself.
EDCS-23100185.summary: A funerary epitaph for a father, likely set up by his son, indicating a self-purchased burial plot, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100186.translation: Arcantius, deacon, made [this] for [himself/his family]; may you live.
EDCS-23100186.summary: A funerary epitaph for a deacon, likely set up by himself for his family, dated to the Late Roman or Early Byzantine period.

EDCS-23100187.translation: To our Emperors, our Lords Flavius Constantinus and Anastasia, most pious Augusti. With the help of God, Vitalius, a magnificent and illustrious master of soldiers in Africa, and through Flavius Triguetius, the citizens of this place, consenting among themselves, have built this fort from their own labor, providentially guided by him. Gudulus, Ianuarius, Felix, Iulius Senior, Victor, M. Faustinus, Dominicus, Camp Doctor, Donatus, Marifer, Lucianus, Dontius, Secundus, Saturninus, L. Victoria, Guntarius, Felix, F.R. Crescens, Victor, S.C. Focas, master, built [it].
EDCS-23100187.summary: An official dedication of a fort built by local citizens under the supervision of military officers, dedicated to the emperors Constantine and Anastasia, dating to the 4th century CE.

EDCS-23100188.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Antonius Felix, veteran, lived 67 years and 6 months, and Aurelia Hiberna, lived 57 years. Their son Ballantius is buried here.
EDCS-23100188.summary: A funerary epitaph for a veteran and his wife, set up by their son, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100189.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Iulius Rufinus, most devoted father. [He] lived [X] years. [His] sons made [this].
EDCS-23100189.summary: A funerary epitaph for a father, set up by his sons, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100190.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Iulia Germana. [She] lived [X] years. [She] was [a wife?] and [daughter?].
EDCS-23100190.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Germana, with an incomplete record of her life and familial status, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100191.translation: Iulia Marcellina made this in memory of her innocent children, Iulia Victorina and Iulius Felix.
EDCS-23100191.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating two innocent children, set up by their mother, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100192.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publilius Verecundus lived 45 years. [His] sons made [this] for the deserving one.
EDCS-23100192.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publilius Verecundus, set up by his sons, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100193.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Postumius Macedo lived 95 years. Postumius Victor placed [this] for the deserving one.
EDCS-23100193.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Postumius Macedo, set up by Postumius Victor, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100194.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Veturius Successus lived 30 years. His heirs had this made, under the supervision of Vir Vict and Baebi Saturninus. They made it.
EDCS-23100194.summary: A funerary monument set up by the heirs of Gaius Veturius Successus, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-23100195.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vibius Ianuarius lived 41 years. His mother Antonia made this for her excellent son.
EDCS-23100195.summary: A funerary monument set up by Antonia for her son, Vibius Ianuarius, who lived 41 years.

EDCS-23100196.translation: A paved footway of the Cibbensians.
EDCS-23100196.summary: An inscription recording the paving of a footway by the Cibbensians.

EDCS-23100197.translation: The Gibbensians made this for Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, Imperator for the 5th time, Consul for the 2nd time, Father of the Fatherland, costing one million sesterces.
EDCS-23100197.summary: A public dedication by the Gibbensians to Emperor Septimius Severus, likely dating to his third tribunician power, fifth acclamation as Imperator, and second consulship.

EDCS-23100198.translation: The Gibbensians made this for Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, Imperator for the 5th time, Consul for the 2nd time, Father of the Fatherland, costing one million sesterces, under the supervision of Pomponius Faustus, son of Aurelius, with Socratenes, procurator of our Augusti, dedicating.
EDCS-23100198.summary: A public dedication by the Gibbensians to Emperor Septimius Severus, supervised by Pomponius Faustus and dedicated by the procurator Socratenes, likely dating to his third tribunician power, fifth acclamation as Imperator, and second consulship.

EDCS-23100199.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Quintus Pontenius Baricus and Licinia Caelia, dearest parents. Caelia lived 65 years. Her heirs made and dedicated this.
EDCS-23100199.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by heirs for their parents, Quintus Pontenius Baricus and Licinia Caelia, and also for Caelia, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-23100200.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Crispinus lived 35 years, according to his will. His daughter for her father.
EDCS-23100200.summary: A funerary monument set up by a daughter for her father, Crispinus, who lived 35 years, in accordance with his will.

EDCS-23100201.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Iulia Monnina, most dutiful mother, 70 years old. Her sons made this.
EDCS-23100201.summary: A funerary monument set up by sons for their mother, Iulia Monnina, who lived 70 years.

EDCS-23100202.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sulpicia Messallina lived 30 years. Sulpicius Messalinus made this.
EDCS-23100202.summary: A funerary monument set up by Sulpicius Messalinus for Sulpicia Messallina, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-23100203.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Virius Victor lived 60 years. Octavia Dativa made this for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-23100203.summary: A funerary monument set up by Octavia Dativa for her husband, Marcus Virius Victor, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-23100204.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ocetisimo Clis, who lived 8 years.
EDCS-23100204.summary: A funerary dedication for Ocetisimo Clis, who died at the age of 8.

EDCS-23100205.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For my most dutiful father, Calendio, who lived 30 years. So that it may be believed he produced a son well and deservedly, after a long interval, I, Primus, son of Augusti, assistant of the archives, erected this tomb for you, having collected your remains, for an eternal home.
EDCS-23100205.summary: A funerary inscription by Primus for his father Calendio, detailing the collection of remains and the erection of a tomb.

EDCS-23100206.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For my most dutiful mother, Veneria, who lived 50 years. After your death, most dutiful mother, and after your funeral rites were performed, I made sure that your remains were collected and buried comfortably in the tomb; I, Primus, son of Augusti, assistant of the archives, accomplished this.
EDCS-23100206.summary: A funerary inscription by Primus for his mother Veneria, expressing care in the burial and collection of her remains.

EDCS-23100207.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Made by Lucius Magnius Fortunatus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion.
EDCS-23100207.summary: A funerary dedication made by Lucius Magnius Fortunatus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion.

EDCS-23100208.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cassia Prima lived 15 years. Octavius Rogatus, her husband, made and dedicated this.
EDCS-23100208.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Octavius Rogatus for his wife Cassia Prima, who died at age 15.

EDCS-23100209.translation: To Iulius Geminius. His wife and heir Iulia, daughter of Caius, [erected this?].
EDCS-23100209.summary: A dedication to Iulius Geminius by his wife and heir, Iulia.

EDCS-23100210.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Hilarius, who lived 35 years, because of his exceptional integrity. His actions [are remembered by] Antoni.
EDCS-23100210.summary: A funerary inscription for Hilarius, who died at 35, honoring his integrity, possibly erected by Antoni.

EDCS-23100211.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Virrullius Hilarus, known as Concordius, lived [X years and] 12 days. His father erected this memorial for his dearest son.
EDCS-23100211.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a father for his son, Virrullius Hilarus (Concordius), highlighting the father's grief.

EDCS-23100212.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Publius Annius Optatus, immunis of the III Augusta legion, who lived 53 years. Annia Victoria made and dedicated this for her husband.
EDCS-23100212.summary: A funerary dedication by Annia Victoria for her husband, Publius Annius Optatus, an immunis of the III Augusta legion who died at 53.

EDCS-23100214.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Bruttius Firmus, veteran, lived 66 years. Caius Bruttius Rogatus [erected this] for his most dutiful father.
EDCS-23100214.summary: A funerary monument erected by Caius Bruttius Rogatus for his father, Caius Bruttius Firmus, a veteran who died at 66.

EDCS-23100215.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titus Flavius Aper, formerly a centurion of the III Augusta Pia Vindex legion, honorably discharged, lived 82 years. Claudia Rufina, his wife, lived 60 years. Flavius Paetinus and Aper, likewise Flavius Urbanus.
EDCS-23100215.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Titus Flavius Aper, a retired centurion, and his wife Claudia Rufina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100216.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titus Flavius Rogatus, veteran, lived 115 years. Rogatus made and dedicated this for his dearest and most remarkable father, at his own expense.
EDCS-23100216.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Rogatus, a veteran who lived to 115, erected by his son Rogatus.

EDCS-23100217.translation: To Iae, his most dutiful wife, who lived 131 years. Titus Flavius Rogatus, veteran, [erected this] for her on account of her merits.
EDCS-23100217.summary: This funerary inscription honors Iae, the wife of Titus Flavius Rogatus, a veteran, and highlights her exceptional longevity.

EDCS-23100218.translation: Caius Iulius Antoninus Iunior served as a speculator in the III Augusta legion, lived 31 years, 10 months, and 3 days. His most unfortunate parents wrote over the inscription of the fortification's monument for their most remarkable son; he is buried here.
EDCS-23100218.summary: This inscription records the death of Caius Iulius Antoninus Iunior, a soldier who served in the III Augusta legion, and was erected by his grieving parents.

EDCS-23100219.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Lucretius Bassus, an interrogator (quaestionarius) of the III Augusta legion, lived 45 years, served 25 years. Lucius Lucretius Bassus, his son.
EDCS-23100219.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Lucretius Bassus, who served as a 'quaestionarius' in the III Augusta legion, erected by his son of the same name.

EDCS-23100220.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus, a Roman equestrian, lived 80 years.
EDCS-23100220.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus, an equestrian, indicating his name and age.

EDCS-23100221.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Antistia Urbana, also called Tanonia, the dutiful, who lived 63 years. Quintus G G, her heir and husband, made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-23100221.summary: This funerary inscription honors Antistia Urbana, wife and heir of Quintus G G, noting her age and his devotion.

EDCS-23100222.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caius Antoni Caesianus, who lived 42 years and 5 months. C IIVS Nigrinus, his son-in-law, saw to its construction.
EDCS-23100222.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Antoni Caesianus, who died at a young age, erected by his son-in-law, C IIVS Nigrinus.

EDCS-23100223.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Ballio Ianuarius, who lived 48 years. Lucius Ballius Saturninus and his heirs made this for their brother.
EDCS-23100223.summary: This funerary inscription is for Lucius Ballio Ianuarius, erected by his brother Lucius Ballius Saturninus and heirs.

EDCS-23100224.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titus Canininius NSA AMEAVA, who lived [seventy] years, arranged for the construction of this monument for himself while alive, and for his well-deserving wife Saturnina, daughter of Ninius, as well as the form and dimensions of the monument for Obstoria Belirea and Petronius Verecundus, at a cost of 200,000 sesterces, which he dedicated. Saturnina NIII, To the Divine Shades. Titus Canininius N VM IMIFV ATO I ISA EQ, arranged for the construction of the monument for his well-deserving wife Saturnina, as well as the form and dimensions of the monument for Obstoria Belirea and Petronius Verecundus, at a cost of 1,700 sesterces, which he dedicated. Saturninus.
EDCS-23100224.summary: This complex funerary inscription records Titus Canininius's arrangements for his own monument and for his wife Saturnina, as well as for Obstoria Belirea and Petronius Verecundus, detailing costs and dedications.

EDCS-23100225.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cassia Rogata lived 70 years. Her husband made this.
EDCS-23100225.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Cassia Rogata by her husband, dating to an indeterminate period within Roman antiquity.

EDCS-23100226.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Clodia Rogata, my most rare wife. Her husband made and dedicated this.
EDCS-23100226.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a monument by a husband to his wife, Clodia Rogata.

EDCS-23100227.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gargilia Fausta, aged 56. Her death memorial was made. Her sons made this.
EDCS-23100227.summary: This funerary inscription records that Gargilia Fausta, who died at age 56, was memorialized by her sons.

EDCS-23100228.translation: To the memory of Gaius Julius C. Aeviter. He lived 7 years and 25 days. My most rare brother, buried here, was built from the ground up.
EDCS-23100228.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Aeviter, who died at a young age, erected by a sibling who likely built the monument from its foundation.

EDCS-23100229.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Gaius Julius Antoninus, my most worthy father.
EDCS-23100229.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication to Gaius Julius Antoninus by a descendant.

EDCS-23100230.translation: To Gaius Julius Fabianus, my most devoted brother.
EDCS-23100230.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Gaius Julius Fabianus by his sibling.

EDCS-23100231.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Felix lived 70 years. His most devoted father. His sons are buried here.
EDCS-23100231.summary: This funerary inscription records Gaius Julius Felix, who lived to 70, and mentions his devoted father and his sons who are buried there.

EDCS-23100232.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Julia Donata. Her husband made and dedicated this. Made by them while alive.
EDCS-23100232.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Julia Donata by her husband, made by them while they were still alive.

EDCS-23100233.translation: To Julia Maxima, mother. She lived 75 years. Gaius Cetranius Processus, her son, made and dedicated this.
EDCS-23100233.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a monument by Gaius Cetranius Processus to his mother, Julia Maxima, who lived to age 75.

EDCS-23100234.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Numerius Catullinus lived 60 years.
EDCS-23100234.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Numerius Catullinus, who lived to age 60.

EDCS-23100235.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Obstorius R lived 90 years.
EDCS-23100235.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Obstorius R, who lived 90 years.

EDCS-23100236.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Octavius io made and dedicated this while alive. He lived...
EDCS-23100236.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Quintus Octavius io made and dedicated the monument during his lifetime, and records his lifespan.

EDCS-23100237.translation: To Gaius Obstorius Processus, dearest father. Lived 65 years. Gaius Obstorius V, his son.
EDCS-23100237.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Gaius Obstorius V for his father, Gaius Obstorius Processus, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-23100238.translation: To Obstoria Lucilla, his most excellent daughter. Lucius Obstorius D nimus made and dedicated this.
EDCS-23100238.summary: This funerary epitaph was made and dedicated by Lucius Obstorius D nimus for his daughter, Obstoria Lucilla.

EDCS-23100239.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Obstoria Primosa lived 53 years and 9 months.
EDCS-23100239.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Obstoria Primosa, who lived 53 years and 9 months.

EDCS-23100240.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Stabiria Monnica, who was also called Gusura, lived 25 years, 5 months, and 4 days.
EDCS-23100240.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Stabiria Monnica (also known as Gusura) as 25 years, 5 months, and 4 days.

EDCS-23100241.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Tadia Saturnina lived 32 years. Octavius Maximus M dedicated it.
EDCS-23100241.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Tadia Saturnina lived 32 years and was dedicated by Octavius Maximus M.

EDCS-23100242.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Tittia Amobbali, who lived 80 years. Her heirs and legatees made this, and dedicated it under the care of Kalendio.
EDCS-23100242.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Tittia Amobbali lived 80 years and was made by her heirs and legatees, dedicated under the care of Kalendio.

EDCS-23100243.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Valerius Priscus lived 60 years. Magnia Saturnia made this with her own money.
EDCS-23100243.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Publius Valerius Priscus lived 60 years and was funded by Magnia Saturnia.

EDCS-23100244.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Verria Salla made this for herself while alive and for her husband Hortensius Successus, for the sake of memory, who lived 90 years. Hortensius Successus Victor Rusticus, his son and heir, saw to its inscription.
EDCS-23100244.summary: This funerary epitaph was made by Verria Salla for herself and her deceased husband Hortensius Successus, who lived 90 years, and was inscribed by their son and heir.

EDCS-23100246.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius, Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, the council of the municipality of Lambiridis.
EDCS-23100246.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the council of Lambiridis to Emperor Claudius Gothicus, likely in the 270s AD.

EDCS-23100247.translation: To our Emperor and Lord, Flavius Valerius Constantine, Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, divine, the council of the municipality of Lambiridis, devoted to his divine will and majesty.
EDCS-23100247.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the council of Lambiridis to Emperor Constantine the Great, likely in the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-23100248.translation: To the Pious, Fortunate Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power, Proconsul, the state of the municipality of Lambiridis, devoted to his divine will and majesty.
EDCS-23100248.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the state of Lambiridis to an emperor, possibly Constantine I, in the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-23100249.translation: To Aurelius Marcus, decurion of the I Hispana regiment in the province of Upper Moesia, desired in battle; Aurelius Suruclius, duplicarius, to his brother, well-deserving.
EDCS-23100249.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelius Marcus, a decurion, erected by his brother Aurelius Suruclius, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-23100250.translation: To the departed spirits. Mussius Felix, a veteran, made this for his well-deserving husband. He lived 65 years.
EDCS-23100250.summary: This is a tombstone for a veteran named Mussius Felix, commemorating his husband, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23100251.translation: Lucius Antistius Antoninus, former duumvir of the municipality of Lambiridis, in honor of his fellow citizens, because they had conferred the duumvirate upon him with full consent and affection, he promised and made this with his own money and dedicated it.
EDCS-23100251.summary: This inscription records Lucius Antistius Antoninus funding a public work or building to honor the citizens of Lambiridis who elected him duumvir, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23100253.translation: IA S Strabo C runt qui C
EDCS-23100253.summary: This is a heavily damaged inscription with incomplete text, making its meaning and context unclear.

EDCS-23100256.translation: Annius Saturninus, pious, XLX Rogatinus MT ORVVE for the honor of EVTID OPV, from his own money DMO. His heir FN VS SAVVASX, his most pious wife, made this.
EDCS-23100256.summary: This inscription records Annius Saturninus and his wife commissioning a monument or honor for an unspecified purpose, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23100258.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fabricius Phai lived 80 years. His heirs made this at his expense.
EDCS-23100258.summary: This is a tombstone for Fabricius Phai, erected by his heirs, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23100259.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Grania Victoria lived 24 years. Cornelius Victor made this for his obedient wife.
EDCS-23100259.summary: This is a tombstone for Grania Victoria, erected by her husband Cornelius Victor, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23100260.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius Herennius Saturninus lived 70 years. His most dutiful wife made this.
EDCS-23100260.summary: A tombstone erected by a wife for her husband, Lucius Herennius Saturninus, who lived 70 years.

EDCS-23100261.translation: To the Divine Manes. Herenia Apollonia lived 23 years.
EDCS-23100261.summary: A tombstone for Herenia Apollonia, who died at age 23.

EDCS-23100262.translation: To the Divine Manes. Arrania Marina made this for her deserving husband, Publius Horatius Felix, who lived 56 years.
EDCS-23100262.summary: A tombstone erected by Arrania Marina for her husband, Publius Horatius Felix, who lived 56 years.

EDCS-23100263.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Caius Rubrius Felix made this for his wife, Iulia Gemelina, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-23100263.summary: A tombstone erected by Caius Rubrius Felix for his wife, Iulia Gemelina, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-23100264.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Octavius Iulianus made this for Quintus Octavius Felix, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-23100264.summary: A tombstone erected by Octavius Iulianus for Quintus Octavius Felix, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-23100265.translation: To the Divine Manes. Pinaria Matrona lived 80 years. Her daughters, the heirs, made this for their deserving mother.
EDCS-23100265.summary: A tombstone erected by the daughters for their mother, Pinaria Matrona, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-23100266.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Iulius Felix made and dedicated this for Pulonia Fortunata, who lived 30 years, because of her merits.
EDCS-23100266.summary: A tombstone erected and dedicated by Iulius Felix for Pulonia Fortunata, who lived 30 years, honoring her merits.

EDCS-23100267.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius V. Saturninus lived 65 years.
EDCS-23100267.summary: A tombstone for Lucius V. Saturninus, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-23100268.summary: A fragmented inscription possibly dedicated to Jupiter, mentioning a name Orisia and a concept of pleasure.

EDCS-23100269.translation: To Fortuna Redux, the august goddess. Hostilius, son of Arnensius Felix of Carthage, a veteran, formerly a beneficiary of the legion, and priest of the municipality of Lambaesis, father of Hostilius Saturninus, honored with public horse, [did this].
EDCS-23100269.summary: A dedication to Fortuna Redux by Hostilius, a veteran and local official from Lambaesis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100270.translation: To the genius of Lambaesis, Vettia Saturnina, mother of the two Roman knights Hostilius Saturninus and Felix, wife of Lucius Hostilius Felix, pontiff of the municipality of Lambaesis, dedicated this in honor of his perpetual priesthood.
EDCS-23100270.summary: This is a dedication by Vettia Saturnina to the genius of Lambaesis in honor of her husband's perpetual priesthood, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23100271.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Gaius Julius Castus the younger, who lived 1 year and 5 months. Aelia Saturnina. Who lived 20 years. Gaius Julius Castus the younger, perpetual flamen of the municipality of Lambaesis, made this for his wife and son.
EDCS-23100271.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Julius Castus the younger, a perpetual flamen, for his deceased wife and son, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23100272.summary: This is a dedication to Adelphius, son of Stephen, likely a funerary or honorific inscription from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23100274.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Appius Ianuarius, who lived 60 years. Lucius Appius Rogatus made this.
EDCS-23100274.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Lucius Appius Rogatus for his father Lucius Appius Ianuarius, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23100275.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Arrania Spesina, who lived 45 years.
EDCS-23100275.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Arrania Spesina at the age of 45, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23100276.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius Felix, who lived 2 years. A PE made this.
EDCS-23100276.summary: This is a funerary monument for Aurelius Felix, who died at the age of 2, with the maker identified only by initials, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23100277.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Cordia Sarrana, snatched away in her youth, his beloved wife. Gaius Timinius Florus made this. She lived 47 years.
EDCS-23100277.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Timinius Florus for his wife Cordia Sarrana, who died at the age of 47, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23100278.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. [Name illegible] lived 5 years. Monument made by his sons.
EDCS-23100278.summary: This is a funerary monument for an unnamed individual who died at the age of 5, made by his sons, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23100279.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavius Felix, who lived 30 years. Flavius Primus for his dearest son.
EDCS-23100279.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Flavius Primus for his son Flavius Felix, who died at the age of 30, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23100280.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. This is an eternal home, this is a rest from labor, this is a memorial. Quintus Fonteius Saturninus made and dedicated this in his lifetime.
EDCS-23100280.summary: This is a funerary monument ('dis Manibus') self-funded by Quintus Fonteius Saturninus for himself during his lifetime, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23100281.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Fonteia Prisca lived 18 years, to her most beloved and delightful wife.
EDCS-23100281.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fonteia Prisca, who died at 18 years old, set up by her husband.

EDCS-23100282.translation: Elena made this while alive. She lived 100 years.
EDCS-23100282.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Elena, who lived to 100 years old and set it up herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-23100283.translation: Caius Julius Anicetus lived 80 years. Iulia Extricata, his wife, lived 70 years. For their most dutiful parents.
EDCS-23100283.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Julius Anicetus and his wife Iulia Extricata, set up by their son, Iulius Africanus (also known as Tillus).

EDCS-23100284.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Caius Julius Candidus. Flavia [---] made [---] her living.
EDCS-23100284.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Julius Candidus, with a partial inscription by Flavia, likely his wife or daughter, indicating she erected it during his lifetime or after his death.

EDCS-23100285.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Quintus Julius Primus, who lived 60 years. Sulpicia Dativa made this for her dearest husband. She gave [this].
EDCS-23100285.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Julius Primus, who died at 60 years old, set up by his wife Sulpicia Dativa.

EDCS-23100286.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulius Repositus made this while he was alive.
EDCS-23100286.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Repositus, who set it up himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-23100287.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Tsala lived 80 years. For her dutiful mother.
EDCS-23100287.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Tsala, who died at 80 years old, set up by her son, Iulius Restutus.

EDCS-23100288.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Victoria lived [---] years. She made this while alive for herself and her dearest husband.
EDCS-23100288.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Victoria, who set it up herself during her lifetime for herself and her husband.

EDCS-23100289.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iuventius lived 100 years. His son Saturus made this.
EDCS-23100289.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iuventius, who lived to 100 years old, set up by his son Saturus.

EDCS-23100292.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Silicius Felix lived 88 years. Iulia Rogatina [made this] for her husband.
EDCS-23100292.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Silicius Felix, who died at 88 years old, set up by his wife Iulia Rogatina.

EDCS-23100293.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sulpicia Prima lived 38 years.
EDCS-23100293.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Sulpicia Prima.

EDCS-23100294.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Egnatius Silvanus made this for his deserving mother, Aelia Tertulla, who lived about 60 years.
EDCS-23100294.summary: A funerary inscription made by Egnatius Silvanus for his mother Aelia Tertulla.

EDCS-23100295.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Titia Ianuaria lived 95 years.
EDCS-23100295.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Titia Ianuaria.

EDCS-23100296.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulius Gallus lived 40 years. His father made this.
EDCS-23100296.summary: A funerary inscription made by a father for his son Iulius Gallus.

EDCS-23100297.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For my son Fortunatus Honoratus Festus, son of Cirtaesia Festa. Caneus Superatus made this for his most wretched wife, with whom he lived 25 years and some days.
EDCS-23100297.summary: A funerary inscription made by Caneus Superatus for his wife and son.

EDCS-23100301.translation: for the safety and eternity of the empire of Flavius Valerius Constantius, with Aurelius Almacius, the most excellent governor of the province of Numidia, always devoted to their divinity, dedicating this, under the supervision of Lucius Tonneius.
EDCS-23100301.summary: An inscription dedicating a monument for the empire and its rulers, made by the governor Aurelius Almacius.

EDCS-23100302.translation: Orinus, a priest, made this for himself and his beloved Titus Flavius. He lived 82 years.
EDCS-23100302.summary: A funerary inscription made by the priest Orinus for himself and Titus Flavius.

EDCS-23100303.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Fortunata made this for her deserving husband, S. Victor. He lived 55 years.
EDCS-23100303.summary: A funerary inscription made by Fortunata for her husband S. Victor.

EDCS-23100306.translation: To the Divine Shades. Cesi, son of Iamirus, lived 20 years. His family and freedmen made this for him as a son and husband.
EDCS-23100306.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cesi, son of Iamirus, indicating his age at death and who erected the monument.

EDCS-23100314.translation: To the deified Commodus, brother of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus, Quintus Calpurnius Quintus son of Papiriacus erected this statue in honor of his second magistracy, which he promised from his own generosity, and dedicated it himself.
EDCS-23100314.summary: A statue base inscription dedicated by Quintus Calpurnius to the deified Commodus in honor of his own second term as magistrate.

EDCS-23100315.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Caesar, grandson of the deified Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, great-grandson of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus Maximus Adiabenicus Maximus, and of the entire divine house, the worshippers of the divine will of Victoria, through Iulius Florus, have made and dedicated this as tax-exempt in perpetuity.
EDCS-23100315.summary: An inscription by worshippers of the goddess Victoria, through Iulius Florus, dedicated for the safety of the imperial family and the divine house, likely from the Severan period.

EDCS-23100317.translation: Marcus Valerius Scribonianus, son of Marcus Valerius Papiria, erected and dedicated this from his own funds beyond the legal requirement in honor of his second magistracy. Lucius Valerius Rufinus Scribonianus, son of Marcus Valerius Papiria, who held the aedileship, dedicated it with his own money.
EDCS-23100317.summary: An inscription recording the erection and dedication of a monument by two brothers, Marcus Valerius Scribonianus and Lucius Valerius Rufinus Scribonianus, related to their public offices.

EDCS-23100319.translation: The metalworker made and dedicated this for himself and labeled it: 'We made it because our sons do not.'
EDCS-23100319.summary: A self-dedicated epitaph by a metalworker, expressing a sentiment about his sons' lack of filial duty.

EDCS-23100322.translation: To the splendor of the age's felicity.
EDCS-23100322.summary: A brief dedicatory or honorific inscription celebrating the happiness and splendor of the era.

EDCS-23100323.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Caecilia Faustina, my wife, who lived 45 years. Pompeius Ianuarius made this.
EDCS-23100323.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by Pompeius Ianuarius for his wife, Caecilia Faustina, stating her age at death.

EDCS-23100324.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caelius Caecum [erected this].
EDCS-23100324.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Caelius Caecum, dedicated to the Divine Shades.

EDCS-23100325.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Cosgonius Maximus, who lived 35 years [erected this].
EDCS-23100325.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Cosgonius Maximus, stating his age at death and dedication to the Divine Shades.

EDCS-23100327.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marc [erected this], who lived [X] years.
EDCS-23100327.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for an individual named Marc, intended for the Divine Shades, indicating their lifespan.

EDCS-23100328.summary: This is a funerary dedication to a wife, likely from a husband.

EDCS-23100330.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Pomponius Caianus lived 87 years. His sons made and dedicated this to their deserving father, with the son, a soldier, overseeing.
EDCS-23100330.summary: This funerary monument was erected by sons for their father, Marcus Pomponius Caianus, who lived 87 years, with one son, a soldier, supervising.

EDCS-23100331.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Domitius Florus lived 90 years.
EDCS-23100331.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Marcus Domitius Florus, who lived 90 years.

EDCS-23100332.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Decimus Herennius Victor lived 75 years. His sons erected this for their deserving father.
EDCS-23100332.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Decimus Herennius Victor, who lived 75 years, and was dedicated by his sons.

EDCS-23100333.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Iulius Victor lived 95 years, for the memory of Barica.
EDCS-23100333.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Caius Iulius Victor, who lived 95 years, for the memory of Barica.

EDCS-23100334.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Valeria Saturnina lived 95 years, for her own memory. She made this herself while alive. You, traveler, say 'May the earth be light upon you.'
EDCS-23100334.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument for Valeria Saturnina, who lived 95 years, with a wish for the traveler to invoke a blessing.

EDCS-23100335.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Volussius Fortis lived 30 years. Pamit and dedicated.
EDCS-23100335.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Lucius Volussius Fortis, who lived 30 years, and was dedicated by Pamit.

EDCS-23100336.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caecilius Creticus, priest of Saturn, and Aulus Ianuarius, soldier of the III Augusta legion, and Gnaeus Methun, erected this memorial for their well-deserving father. He lived 85 years. And you passing by, say, I pray, 'May the earth be light upon you.' Here he lies buried.
EDCS-23100336.summary: This funerary monument was erected by his sons, including a priest and a soldier, for their father, Caecilius Creticus, who lived 85 years, and includes a prayer for passersby.

EDCS-23100337.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caecilia Rogata lived 80 years.
EDCS-23100337.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Caecilia Rogata, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-23100338.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Fortunatus Tertinus lived FL years, a cattle dealer.
EDCS-23100338.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Fortunatus Tertinus, who was a cattle dealer.

EDCS-23100340.translation: Sacred to the Good Goddess. Julius Martis paid his vow willingly with a contented spirit and expended his own money on the altar he promised.
EDCS-23100340.summary: An altar dedicated to Bona Dea by Julius Martis, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-23100341.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the safety of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Britannicus Maximus. Gaius Asinius Felix, curator of the cavalry of the Flavian ala, paid his vow willingly with a contented spirit.
EDCS-23100341.summary: An altar dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Asinius Felix, curator of a cavalry unit, for the emperor's safety.

EDCS-23100345.translation: Sacred to Victoria Augusta. Caius Masinius Donatus paid his vow willingly with a contented spirit.
EDCS-23100345.summary: An inscription dedicating something to Victoria Augusta by Caius Masinius Donatus.

EDCS-23100346.translation: For the most flourishing age of our lords Emperors [[Caesar Maximinius]] Pius Felix and [[Maximus the most noble]] Caesar Augusti. The covered colonnade, which Caius Iulius Maximi son Saturninus Messapanus and Quintus Canneius, son of Quintus Canneius Gemellinus, a retired soldier from Apollonia, had vowed of their own accord to their fellow citizens, they gave and dedicated from their own resources on the sixth day before the Kalends of April, during the consulship of Perpetuus and Cornelianus.
EDCS-23100346.summary: A dedication of a covered colonnade by two citizens, Caius Iulius Saturninus Messapanus and Quintus Canneius Emenus Apollonianus, to their fellow citizens, dated to the consulship of Perpetuus and Cornelianus.

EDCS-23100348.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Caesar, Pius Felix Augustus, consul for the fifth time, father of the country. He built and dedicated the praetorium for Caius Iulius Romanus.
EDCS-23100348.summary: A record of the construction and dedication of a praetorium for Caius Iulius Romanus, likely for the emperor's safety.

EDCS-23100350.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Aelius Octavianus, a veteran, lived for 70 years. He made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-23100350.summary: A tombstone set up by Caius Aelius Octavianus, a veteran, for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-23100352.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Tito Flavio Crescentioni, who lived 85 years, his sons Martialis and Laetus, soldiers of the III Augusta legion, and Rusticus, made this for their most dear father, who deserved well.
EDCS-23100352.summary: A tombstone erected by the sons of Tito Flavio Crescentioni, including two soldiers from the III Augusta legion, for their father.

EDCS-23100353.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For the memory and piety of Caius Iulius Crescentianus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, who lived 87 years and 15 days. Iulia Vernacula, his wife, dedicated this.
EDCS-23100353.summary: A tombstone set up by Iulia Vernacula for her husband, Caius Iulius Crescentianus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion.

EDCS-23100354.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Iulius Liberalis, a veteran, lived for 102 years. His sons made this for their deserving father.
EDCS-23100354.summary: A tombstone erected by the sons of Caius Iulius Liberalis, a veteran who lived to be 102 years old.

EDCS-23100355.translation: Caius Iulius Martialis, a veteran, made these seats for his father while he was still alive, and also for his most holy wife, Iulia Victorina, who lived 52 years and five days, and for his children, the Iulii Rogatus and Quinteia, who died before him. He established this with his children, Martialis and Caninia, having spent 26,000 sesterces. He died at the age of eighty.
EDCS-23100355.summary: A tombstone and burial plot created by Caius Iulius Martialis, a veteran, for himself, his wife Iulia Victorina, and their children, with details of the cost and his age at death.

EDCS-23100356.translation: Sacred to the divine Manes. Julius Maximus, a veteran, lived 75 years. Iulia Rufina erected this for her deserving wife and dedicated it.
EDCS-23100356.summary: A funerary epitaph for Julius Maximus, a veteran, erected by his wife Iulia Rufina.

EDCS-23100357.translation: Sacred to the divine Manes. For Julius Mus, my dearest son, a soldier of the VIth Cohort of Commageni, who lived 21 years. Geminia Lacaena, his most dutiful mother, erected this for him, her deserving son, and dedicated it.
EDCS-23100357.summary: A funerary epitaph for Julius Mus, a soldier, erected by his mother Geminia Lacaena.

EDCS-23100358.translation: Sacred to the divine Manes. Lucretius Maximus, standard-bearer of the Ist Cohort of Flavians, cavalry-armed, served 17 years, lived 37 years. His heir erected this dutifully for him.
EDCS-23100358.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucretius Maximus, a standard-bearer, erected by his heir.

EDCS-23100359.translation: Sacred to the divine Manes. Caius Marrucinus Martialis, a veteran, lived 90 years. His heirs erected this for their deserving father.
EDCS-23100359.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Marrucinus Martialis, a veteran, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-23100360.translation: Sacred to the divine Manes. S. Optata lived 45 years. Her most dutiful sons erected this. Marcus Numisius Gemellus, a veteran, lived 50 years.
EDCS-23100360.summary: A funerary epitaph for S. Optata and Marcus Numisius Gemellus, a veteran, erected by Optata's sons.

EDCS-23100361.translation: Sacred to the divine Manes. Publius Aelius Felix, priest, lived 85 years. Publius Aelius Panus erected this for his deserving father.
EDCS-23100361.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Aelius Felix, a priest, erected by his son Publius Aelius Panus.

EDCS-23100363.translation: Sacred to the divine Manes. Quintus Aemilius Felix lived 45 years. His dutiful wife erected this.
EDCS-23100363.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Aemilius Felix, erected by his wife.

EDCS-23100364.translation: Sacred to the divine Manes. Aemilius Rogatianus lived 5 years. Aemilius Rogatus for his son.
EDCS-23100364.summary: A funerary epitaph for the young Aemilius Rogatianus, erected by his father Aemilius Rogatus.

EDCS-23100365.translation: Sacred to the divine Manes. Aemilius Servil lived 90 years. Sacred to the divine Manes. Valerius Victor lived 65 years. Valerius Rogatus erected this for his deserving [son] at his own expense and dedicated it.
EDCS-23100365.summary: A funerary epitaph recording two individuals, Aemilius Servil and Valerius Victor, with the latter erected by Valerius Rogatus.

EDCS-23100366.translation: Sacred to the departed gods, the Manes. Aufidius Felix lived 60 years. Iulia Secunda erected this for her husband.
EDCS-23100366.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aufidius Felix, erected by his wife Iulia Secunda.

EDCS-23100367.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Arrania Zabullina lived 40 years. Ulpius Iovinus made [this] for his wife.
EDCS-23100367.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Arrania Zabullina and was erected by her husband, Ulpius Iovinus.

EDCS-23100368.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aurelius Saturninus lived 30 years. Marcus Aurelius Rogatus made [this] for his deserving mother.
EDCS-23100368.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Marcus Aurelius Saturninus and was erected by his son, Marcus Aurelius Rogatus, for his mother.

EDCS-23100369.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Badesia Primosa lived 53 years.
EDCS-23100369.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Badesia Primosa.

EDCS-23100370.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sextus Cornelius Fortunatus lived 80 years. Numissia Calliala made [this] for her most loving husband.
EDCS-23100370.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Sextus Cornelius Fortunatus and was erected by his wife, Numissia Calliala.

EDCS-23100371.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Domitius Pasenius lived 73 years. His sons made [this] for their most dutiful and deserving father.
EDCS-23100371.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Gaius Domitius Pasenius and was erected by his sons.

EDCS-23100372.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fabia Fortunata lived 75 years. She lies here, placed by her sons.
EDCS-23100372.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Fabia Fortunata and notes that she was placed here by her sons.

EDCS-23100373.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia Donatula lived 33 years. Caesius Alivianus made [this] for his wife.
EDCS-23100373.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Flavia Donatula and was erected by her husband, Caesius Alivianus.

EDCS-23100374.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Geminia Lacaena lived 75 years. Caveius Firmus made [this] for his most sweet and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-23100374.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Geminia Lacaena and was erected by her husband, Caveius Firmus.

EDCS-23100375.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Granius Castus lived 80 years. Grania Maxima and Caesita made [this] for their most dutiful father.
EDCS-23100375.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Granius Castus and was erected by his daughters, Grania Maxima and Caesita.

EDCS-23100376.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Grania Caesita made [this] for herself while alive. She lived [number] years.
EDCS-23100376.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Grania Caesita made it for herself during her lifetime, with a missing lifespan.

EDCS-23100377.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, Julius Crescens made this for the memory of his sons, Cicero Florus and Secundus.
EDCS-23100377.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Julius Crescens to his deceased sons, Florus and Secundus.

EDCS-23100378.translation: Sacred to the gods below, the departed spirits. Caius Julius Felix, pious, lived 60 years. Rufus, his son, erected this for his deserving father.
EDCS-23100378.summary: A funerary dedication by Rufus for his deserving father, Caius Julius Felix, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-23100379.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Julius Florus lived 80 years.
EDCS-23100379.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Caius Julius Florus at the age of 80.

EDCS-23100380.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Julius Ingenuus lived 34 years.
EDCS-23100380.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Caius Julius Ingenuus at the age of 34.

EDCS-23100381.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Julius Primulus lived 75 years. The heirs made this.
EDCS-23100381.summary: A funerary inscription by the heirs of Caius Julius Primulus, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-23100382.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Caius, a worthy and innocent man, who passed away after completing his time, his father Julius, nicknamed Rufulus, who lived 80 years, was his natural father Caius Julius Rufulus.
EDCS-23100382.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Julius Rufulus, honored by his father, also named Caius Julius Rufulus, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-23100383.translation: Julius Victor made this for his exceedingly rare wife.
EDCS-23100383.summary: A dedication by Julius Victor for his wife.

EDCS-23100384.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia lived 45 years. Longinus is buried here for his most pious wife.
EDCS-23100384.summary: A funerary inscription by Longinus for his wife Iulia, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-23100385.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Aestrikata lived 25 years. Longinus, her husband, made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-23100385.summary: A funerary dedication by Longinus for his wife, Iulia Aestrikata, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-23100386.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Fortunata lived 35 years. Caius Aemilius Certianus made this sepulchre.
EDCS-23100386.summary: A funerary dedication by Caius Aemilius Certianus for Iulia Fortunata, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-23100387.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Fortunata lived 73 years. Lucius Cestius Saturninus lived 75 years. Cestia Cornelia dedicated this.
EDCS-23100387.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lives of Iulia Fortunata and Lucius Cestius Saturninus, dedicated by Cestia Cornelia.

EDCS-23100388.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Gaetula lived 50 years.
EDCS-23100388.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Iulia Gaetula, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100389.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Ianuaria lived 31 years. Quintus Iulius Ianuarius made this for his deserving daughter.
EDCS-23100389.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Iulia Ianuaria, made by her father Quintus Iulius Ianuarius.

EDCS-23100391.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iunia Bonica lived 40 years. Caius Anneus Gemellus made this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-23100391.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Iunia Bonica, made by her husband Caius Anneus Gemellus.

EDCS-23100392.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Caius Licinius Speratus, his father. Licini Speratus and Victor made this for their most kind father, who lived 90 years.
EDCS-23100392.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Caius Licinius Speratus, made by his sons Licini Speratus and Victor.

EDCS-23100393.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Matrona lived 63 years. Matrona the daughter made this for her deserving mother.
EDCS-23100393.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Matrona, made by her daughter, also named Matrona.

EDCS-23100394.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Minucia Extricata lived 65 years.
EDCS-23100394.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Minucia Extricata.

EDCS-23100395.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Numisia Rogatina lived 48 years. Caius Iulius Primulus made this and dedicated it for his deserving wife.
EDCS-23100395.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Numisia Rogatina, made by her husband Caius Iulius Primulus.

EDCS-23100396.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Octavius Felix lived 5 years. Made for the sweetest [son].
EDCS-23100396.summary: This funerary epitaph records the short life of Caius Octavius Felix, dedicated by a parent.

EDCS-23100397.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Petronia Fortunata lived 45 years. Veteran.
EDCS-23100397.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Petronia Fortunata, noting her status as a veteran.

EDCS-23100398.translation: To the Divine Shades. Pomponia Felicitas lived 30 years. Her mother made this memorial for her, deserving.
EDCS-23100398.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pomponia Felicitas, set up by her mother, dating likely to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100399.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Qmudina Saturnina lived 33 years. Perpetuus Aemilius made this for his mother.
EDCS-23100399.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Qmudina Saturnina, set up by her son Perpetuus Aemilius, dating likely to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100400.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caius Sergius Festus lived 31 years. He made this.
EDCS-23100400.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Sergius Festus, likely set up by himself or a relative, dating likely to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100401.translation: Hail, Tertia. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sittia Tertia lived 54 years. Caesennia Paulina and Caesennia Festa made this for their most dutiful daughter. Farewell.
EDCS-23100401.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sittia Tertia, set up by her daughters Caesennia Paulina and Caesennia Festa, dating likely to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100402.translation: To the Divine Shades. Valerius Victor lived 14 years. Valerius Luc made this. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Valeria Feliccia lived 16 years. She made and dedicated this with her own money.
EDCS-23100402.summary: This inscription contains two funerary epitaphs: one for Valerius Victor set up by Valerius Luc, and another for Valeria Feliccia, set up by herself, dating likely to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100403.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Valeria Iuliosa lived 23 years. Valerius Rogatianus made this for his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-23100403.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Iuliosa, set up by her husband Valerius Rogatianus, dating likely to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100404.translation: To the Divine Shades. Ulpius Severus lived 33 years for his children.
EDCS-23100404.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ulpius Severus, who lived 33 years and had children, dating likely to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100405.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Volussenia Sabina lived 87 years. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius Barbarus lived 85 years. Happily, well to good people.
EDCS-23100405.summary: This inscription contains two funerary epitaphs: one for Volussenia Sabina and another for Iulius Barbarus, dating likely to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100406.translation: To the Genius of the People. Lucius Volussius Saturninus, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, aedile.
EDCS-23100406.summary: This is a dedication to the Genius of the People by Lucius Volussius Saturninus, who held the office of aedile, likely dating to the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-23100407.translation: Sacred to Janus, Father, August. Lucius Atrilius Felix, son of Lucius, of the Felix tribe, fulfilled his vow freely with his own money. He made and dedicated this.
EDCS-23100407.summary: This is a dedication to the god Janus, funded and dedicated by Lucius Atrilius Felix, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23100408.translation: Sacred to Victory-bringing Jupiter. Caius Crepereius Romanus, son of Caius, of the Papirian tribe, who had held the offices of pontifex, aedile, quaestor, public prefect, and prefect with judicial power, and was twice chief magistrate, gave and dedicated from his own funds 6,000 sesterces beyond the legal requirements for the sake of his second tenure as chief magistrate.
EDCS-23100408.summary: This inscription records a monetary donation by Caius Crepereius Romanus, likely an official from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE, for public games or a building project during his second term as chief magistrate.

EDCS-23100410.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Marcus Aurelius Aemilianus, son of Quitus, of the Papirian tribe, who served as quinquennial magistrate and aedile, and twice as chief magistrate, erected the statue which he had promised for the honor of his magistracy, at a cost of 5,000 sesterces, in addition to the legal contributions for his offices, and paved ten cubits of the forum, and dedicated it all.
EDCS-23100410.summary: This inscription details the erection and dedication of a statue and paving work by Marcus Aurelius Aemilianus, a local magistrate, around the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-23100411.translation: To Saturn Augustus. Lucius Papius Apollus, son of Lucius, of the Papirian tribe, who served as aedile, quaestor, chief magistrate, prefect with judicial power, and quinquennial magistrate, erected a statue for himself at the end of his year of office, on account of his priestly honor, and dedicated it.
EDCS-23100411.summary: This inscription records the erection and dedication of a statue by Lucius Papius Apollus, a local magistrate and priest, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100412.translation: To the Father God of Fruits, the Fruit-bearer Augustus. Lucius Aufustius Perpetuus gladly fulfilled with his children the vow which he had promised.
EDCS-23100412.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Aufustius Perpetuus, likely from the 2nd century CE, dedicating something to the god of fruits.

EDCS-23100416.translation: Saturius, who served as quaestor, aedile, chief magistrate, perpetual pontifex, and priest, oversaw and adorned the addition of a pronaos and portico with architraves to the temple, along with the completion of sacrificial rites and bread distribution, and other matters pertaining to the dedication, and all these things together with Mu and Festus, equestrians.
EDCS-23100416.summary: This inscription details building work and associated religious ceremonies undertaken by Saturius and two equestrians, likely in a provincial city during the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23100417.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Caesar, Imperator, son of Antoninus Augustus Pius, Father of the Fatherland, by decree of the local council, with public funds.
EDCS-23100417.summary: This is an official dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, funded by the city, likely from the period of his co-rule with his father Antoninus Pius (c. 161 CE).

EDCS-23100418.translation: To Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan the Parthian, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the fourth time, consul for the third time, Father of the Fatherland, by decree of the local council of the people of Dianium, with public funds.
EDCS-23100418.summary: This is an elaborate dedication to Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely commissioned by the city of Dianium, dating to his later reign (c. 158-161 CE).

EDCS-23100419.translation: To the deified Pius, father of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Armeniacus and Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus Armeniacus. Caius Iulius Caesianus, son of Caius, of the Papirian tribe, who served as aedile, twice chief magistrate, quinquennial magistrate, and perpetual flamen, erected the statue which he had promised for the honor of his perpetual priesthood, at a cost of 10,000 sesterces beyond the legal contributions, and dedicated it, by decree of the local council.
EDCS-23100419.summary: This inscription records the erection and dedication of a statue by Caius Iulius Caesianus, a local magistrate and priest, to the deified Emperor Pius, likely in the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-23100424.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Armeniacus, Medicus, Parthicus Maximus, son of the deified Antoninus Pius, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the twenty-first time, Imperator for the fifth time, consul for the third time, Father of the Fatherland, by decree of the local council, with public funds.
EDCS-23100424.summary: This is an extensive dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, issued by a local council using public funds, dating to the period of his co-rule with his father (c. 177-180 CE).

EDCS-23100426.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of our Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus.
EDCS-23100426.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, identifying him as the son of Emperor Septimius Severus, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23100427.translation: To the deified Commodus, brother of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, who holds the tribunician power for the 8th time, is Emperor for the 11th time, Consul for the 2nd time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul. Marcus Iulius Montanianus, son of Caius, of the Papiria tribe, having been Aedile twice, Duumvir, Duumvir for five years, and Perpetual Flamen, having contributed 4,000 sesterces to the state, and having increased the funds, also contributed 10,000 sesterces as Perpetual Flamen. He himself dedicated it.
EDCS-23100427.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deified Commodus by Marcus Iulius Montanianus, who held various civic and religious offices, likely dating to the reign of Septimius Severus (late 2nd/early 3rd century CE).

EDCS-23100428.translation: To the deified Commodus, brother of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Parthicus Germanicus Maximus Arabicus, who holds the tribunician power for the 9th time, is Emperor for the 11th time, Consul for the 3rd time, Father of the Fatherland.
