Lucius Mucius Mucianus, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, having been Quaestor, Aedile, Duumvir, Prefect for administering justice in place of the Duumvirs for five years, and Perpetual Flamen, restored this statue, which he had pledged beyond the legal requirement for the honor of perpetual flaminship, and he himself dedicated it.
EDCS-23100428.summary: This inscription records the restoration and dedication of a statue to the deified Commodus by Lucius Mucius Mucianus, who held various civic and religious offices, likely dating to the reign of Septimius Severus (late 2nd/early 3rd century CE).

EDCS-23100429.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power, Consul designate, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, the most Provident and Holy Prince, and to Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus, the most noble Caesar, Prince of Youth, by the state of the people of Dianium, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-23100429.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperors Macrinus and Diadumenianus by the state of Dianium, a municipality, likely dating to 217-218 CE.

EDCS-23100432.translation: Papiria Dexter, Duumvir, made this statue, which she had promised for 2,000 sesterces, with an additional 1,000 sesterces of her own money, and she herself dedicated it, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-23100432.summary: This inscription records the funding and dedication of a statue by Papiria Dexter, a female official, for the municipality, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100433.translation: To Gaius Julius Capito, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, most faithful advocate, by the state of the people of Dianium, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-23100433.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Gaius Julius Capito, an advocate, by the state of Dianium, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100434.translation: Caius Julius, son of Caius, of the Papiria tribe, of the municipality of Dianium, adorned Diana.
EDCS-23100434.summary: This inscription records an action by Caius Julius of Dianium, likely related to the local cult of Diana, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100435.translation: To Corchivius Paulinus, perpetual flamen, most faithful advocate, by the state of the most distinguished order of Dianium, for his services.
EDCS-23100435.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Corchivius Paulinus, a perpetual flamen and advocate, by the distinguished council of Dianium for his merits, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100436.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Astius Flavianus, a veteran, lived for 60 years. Castricia Quintula made the funeral rites and tomb for her husband.
EDCS-23100436.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of a veteran, Caius Astius Flavianus, and the erection of his tomb by his wife, Castricia Quintula, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100437.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Fortunata, a most rare wife. Quintus Marius Rogatus, a veteran, made this for himself while alive and dedicated it, having lived 34 years, for her merits.
EDCS-23100437.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the self-funded dedication of a tomb by a veteran, Quintus Marius Rogatus, to his wife Fortunata, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100438.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Papinia Victoria lived for 60 years with her husband Caius Mirus Nampamon, a veteran of the II Parthian legion. Their sons made this.
EDCS-23100438.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Papinia Victoria and her husband Caius Mirus Nampamon, a veteran, with the tomb erected by their sons, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100439.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelia Saturnina lived 80 years; she made and dedicated this for herself and her husband, Iulius Rogatus.
EDCS-23100439.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Saturnina, who made it for herself and her husband Iulius Rogatus.

EDCS-23100440.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilius Adventus lived 42 years; Caecilia Fruenda made this for her husband.
EDCS-23100440.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilius Adventus, made by his wife Caecilia Fruenda.

EDCS-23100441.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilia Fruenda lived [some number of] years; she made this for herself while alive.
EDCS-23100441.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilia Fruenda, made by herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-23100442.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits for Marcus Cossinius, son of Quintus.
EDCS-23100442.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Marcus Cossinius, son of Quintus.

EDCS-23100443.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Dupidius Pasinicus, freedman, lived 45 years; Aquilia Clara, his wife, made this for him while alive.
EDCS-23100443.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the freedman Dupidius Pasinicus, made by his wife Aquilia Clara.

EDCS-23100444.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Marcus Iulius Proculus.

EDCS-23100445.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Cypris lived 65 years.
EDCS-23100445.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Iulia Cypris.

EDCS-23100446.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits and for perpetual security. Iulia Datiana lived 43 years.
EDCS-23100446.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Datiana, likely emphasizing eternal peace.

EDCS-23100447.translation: Iulia Restutina lived 50 years; Nei Bebi lived 55 years.
EDCS-23100447.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespans of two individuals, Iulia Restutina and Nei Bebi.

EDCS-23100448.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Tevestina made this for herself while alive.
EDCS-23100448.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Iulia Tevestina for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-23100449.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Octavia Dativa, wife, lived 80 years. Julius Victor, son, erected and dedicated this tombstone.
EDCS-23100449.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Octavia Dativa, erected by her son Julius Victor, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100450.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Publius Papinius Ianuarius made this for himself and Regilia Quartula while alive; the heir will record the years. Lived [number] years.
EDCS-23100450.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Papinius Ianuarius and Regilia Quartula, made by the former for himself and his wife, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100451.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Papius Fronto lived 50 years. His sons, the heirs, [made this] for their dearest father.
EDCS-23100451.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Papius Fronto, erected by his sons, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100452.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Rubria Maiorica lived 60 years. Deceived by Clodia Pastore, her husband. Her sons made this for their dearest mother.
EDCS-23100452.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rubria Maiorica, erected by her sons, who mention her husband Clodia Pastore, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100453.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Sallustia Octavia lived 42 years and 2 months and 16 days. Publius Cornelius Augurinus, her husband, made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-23100453.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sallustia Octavia, erected by her husband Publius Cornelius Augurinus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100454.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus Statius Vettonianus lived 17 years. His mother, Iulia Donata, dedicated this.
EDCS-23100454.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Statius Vettonianus, erected by his mother Iulia Donata, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100455.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. C. Q. C. Z. made this for his son. Lived 18 years.
EDCS-23100455.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed son, erected by his father (identified by initials), likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100457.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Sextus Aelius Cai filius Ladibelus, Papiria tribe, quaestor, aedile twice, duovir, lived 53 years. Sextus Aelius Valentinus made this for his dearest, deserving father. He is buried here.
EDCS-23100457.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Aelius Cai filius Ladibelus, a former magistrate, erected by his son Sextus Aelius Valentinus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100458.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Arruntia Prisca lived 45 years. Sextus Aelius Valentinus made this for his very rare, deserving stepmother. She is buried here.
EDCS-23100458.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Arruntia Prisca, erected by her stepson Sextus Aelius Valentinus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100459.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aelia Donata lived 64 years. Her grandchildren made this for her.
EDCS-23100459.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Donata, erected by her grandchildren, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23100460.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Fortunata lived 75 years; she is buried here. Iulius Egnatius made this for his mother.
EDCS-23100460.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Iulia Fortunata and stating that her son Iulius Egnatius erected it.

EDCS-23100461.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Secunda lived 40 years. Marcus Caecilius Saturninus, and her brothers Iuli Siahin and Lucius, made this for [their] wife.
EDCS-23100461.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Secunda, erected by her husband Marcus Caecilius Saturninus and her brothers.

EDCS-23100462.translation: Caius Herennius Maximus, from his own funds, [dedicated this] for approximately 20 years.
EDCS-23100462.summary: An inscription recording a dedication made by Caius Herennius Maximus, possibly related to a funerary monument or a public work, with an indication of its duration.

EDCS-23100463.translation: Quintus Aurelius Victor lived 30 years. Obstoria Fuscilla [made this] for her most beloved husband.
EDCS-23100463.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Aurelius Victor, erected by his wife Obstoria Fuscilla.

EDCS-23100464.translation: M Crescentis IV passed away on the 7th day before the Kalends of August.
EDCS-23100464.summary: A brief funerary notice recording the date of death for M Crescentis IV.

EDCS-23100465.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Annius Muttyn lived 80 years.
EDCS-23100465.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Annius Muttyn.

EDCS-23100466.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed, a holy house. F Afrodisia lived 71 years.
EDCS-23100466.summary: A funerary epitaph for F Afrodisia, describing her tomb as a 'holy house'.

EDCS-23100467.translation: Caius Calventius Crispus died at the age of 40.
EDCS-23100467.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age at death of Caius Calventius Crispus.

EDCS-23100468.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Marcus Aiphrenis Fugundus.

EDCS-23100469.translation: Iulia Crispa lived 70 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23100469.summary: A funerary epitaph stating the lifespan of Iulia Crispa and indicating her burial place.

EDCS-23100470.translation: To the gods of the underworld, Elphina lived for 2 years.
EDCS-23100470.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Elphina, who died at the age of two.

EDCS-23100472.translation: Marcellus departed in peace and harmony; may he rest well here.
EDCS-23100472.summary: A funerary inscription wishing peace and rest for Marcellus.

EDCS-23100473.translation: To the gods of the underworld, Publius Iulius Castus lived for 11 years; he is placed here. May your bones rest well, father of my son.
EDCS-23100473.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Iulius Castus, who died at 11, with a dedication from his father.

EDCS-23100474.translation: To the gods of the underworld, Quintus Iulius Ermanus lived for 25 years and 8 months; to his husband.
EDCS-23100474.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Iulius Ermanus, who lived for 25 years and 8 months, dedicated by his husband.

EDCS-23100475.translation: Sacred to the gods of the underworld, Caius Pomponius Primus lived for 75 years and 20 hours.
EDCS-23100475.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Pomponius Primus, who lived for 75 years and 20 hours.

EDCS-23100476.translation: Sacred to the gods of the underworld, Quintus Pomponius Namulius lived for 65 years; he made it.
EDCS-23100476.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Pomponius Namulius, who lived for 65 years, with the maker indicated.

EDCS-23100477.translation: By God's favor, in the times of Justinian and Theodora, our most pious Lords and Emperors, by the providence of our Lords, this was constructed by Solomon, most excellent master of soldiers, twice consul, prefect of the praetorium of Africa, and patrician.
EDCS-23100477.summary: This inscription records the construction of a building under the reigns of Emperors Justinian and Theodora, commissioned by Solomon, a high-ranking military and administrative official.

EDCS-23100478.translation: Sacred to the gods of the underworld, Publius Licinius Agathopus, a veteran prefect who served in Britain, an equestrian soldier of the ala, serving at Brauniacum, returning to his homeland Gadiaufala, lived for 81 years. His sons, Publius Licinius Ianuarius, and...
EDCS-23100478.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Licinius Agathopus, a veteran officer, detailing his service and age, with a partial dedication from his son.

EDCS-23100480.translation: Sacred to the gods of the underworld, Titus Aelius Dativus the pious lived for 20 years; he is placed here.
EDCS-23100480.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Aelius Dativus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-23100481.translation: Aemilia Donata established this tomb for herself and for her [daughter] Donata, who lived for 21 years. Sacred to the gods of the underworld. [Done in the] third year.
EDCS-23100481.summary: A funerary monument established by Aemilia Donata for herself and her daughter Donata, who died at 21.

EDCS-23100482.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Antonius Datus, dutiful, lived 31 years; he rests here.
EDCS-23100482.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Marcus Antonius Datus, recording his age at death and burial.

EDCS-23100483.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Publius Aufidius Primosus, dutiful, lived 31 years.
EDCS-23100483.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Publius Aufidius Primosus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-23100484.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caecilia Rustika, dutiful, lived 35 years; she rests here.
EDCS-23100484.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Caecilia Rustika, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-23100485.translation: To the memory of Corinthiadis Theodorae, the sweetest children of Chinithus, Fidelis and Thalusa, their parents.
EDCS-23100485.summary: This is a funerary dedication by parents to their children Corinthiadis Theodorae, Fidelis, and Thalusa.

EDCS-23100486.translation: Marcus Geminius Maximinus, son of Caius, lived 17 years; he rests here.
EDCS-23100486.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Marcus Geminius Maximinus, recording his age at death and burial.

EDCS-23100487.translation: Lucius Nonius Olympus made this, while alive, for his dutiful mother Nonia Olympias and his father Thalamus.
EDCS-23100487.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Lucius Nonius Olympus for his parents, Nonia Olympias and Thalamus.

EDCS-23100488.translation: Alexai lived 85 years; he rests here.
EDCS-23100488.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Alexai, recording his age at death and burial.

EDCS-23100489.translation: ONdia lived 10 years; she rests here.
EDCS-23100489.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to ONdia, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-23100490.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flaminius Lucillianus lived 17 years and 5 months.
EDCS-23100490.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Flaminius Lucillianus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-23100491.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To the memory of Hadrianus, son of L. [---].
EDCS-23100491.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Hadrianus, son of L., likely incomplete due to damage.

EDCS-23200375.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Agrius Gen[---] lived for 75 years.
EDCS-23200375.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Agrius Gen[---], who lived to be 75.

EDCS-23200376.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Nundinarius lived for 30 years.
EDCS-23200376.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Nundinarius, who lived to be 30.

EDCS-23200378.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. C. Tertulla lived for 35 years.
EDCS-23200378.summary: This is a funerary inscription for C. Tertulla, who lived to be 35.

EDCS-23200379.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Arrania Monnica lived for 37 years. To the Spirits of the Departed. Carvilius Fortunatus. To the Spirits of the Departed. Iuniar. To the Spirits of the Departed. Carvilius Iunior lived for 28 years.
EDCS-23200379.summary: This inscription appears to contain multiple funerary notices, including for Arrania Monnica and Carvilius Iunior.

EDCS-23200380.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sextus Arranius Felicianus lived for 120 years.
EDCS-23200380.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Arranius Felicianus, who lived to be 120.

EDCS-23200381.translation: Lucius Fannius Ianuarius lived for 61 years; he lies here.
EDCS-23200381.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Fannius Ianuarius, who lived to be 61 and is buried here.

EDCS-23200382.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Faustinius lived for 27 years; he lies here.
EDCS-23200382.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Faustinius, who lived to be 27 and is buried here.

EDCS-23200383.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sedata Namphame lived for 57 years; her heir made this. To the Spirits of the Departed. Fulcinius Secundus lived for 95 years.
EDCS-23200383.summary: This inscription contains funerary notices for Sedata Namphame, whose heir erected the monument, and Fulcinius Secundus.

EDCS-23200385.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Iulius Coscovius lived for 75 years.
EDCS-23200385.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Iulius Coscovius, who lived to be 75.

EDCS-23200387.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius, son of Lucius, lived for 75 years; he lies here.
EDCS-23200387.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius, son of Lucius, who lived to be 75 and is buried here.

EDCS-23200388.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of L. Postumianus.

EDCS-23200389.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Marcius Donatus lived for 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23200389.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Marcius Donatus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-23200393.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Postumius Faustus lived for 30 years.
EDCS-23200393.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Postumius Faustus.

EDCS-23200394.translation: Pr. Anicenia, freedwoman of Sextus, lived for 75 years.
EDCS-23200394.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Pr. Anicenia, a freedwoman.

EDCS-23200395.translation: Rogata, a pious daughter, lived for 99 years; her heir made this.
EDCS-23200395.summary: A funerary epitaph for Rogata, stating her age and that her heir erected the monument.

EDCS-23200397.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Sulpicius Caruleus lived for 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23200397.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Sulpicius Caruleus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-23200398.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Terbaricinus lived for 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23200398.summary: A funerary epitaph for Terbaricinus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-23200400.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the short lifespan of V.L.

EDCS-23200401.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the short lifespan of Iia.

EDCS-23200403.translation: This memorial was placed here by Faustinianus the bishop.
EDCS-23200403.summary: A dedication by Faustinianus, identified as a bishop, for a memorial.

EDCS-23200404.translation: She lived 30 years, 10 months, and 13 days, and departed in peace.
EDCS-23200404.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of a woman and her peaceful death, likely from the later Roman period.

EDCS-23600004.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus.
EDCS-23600004.summary: This is a dedication made for the well-being of Emperor Septimius Severus, likely erected by an official or a community during his reign.

EDCS-23600007.translation: During the reign of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, most brave, most fortunate, chief priest, in his 7th year of tribunician power, 11th acclamation as emperor, 2nd consul, proconsul, son of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius, brother of the deified Commodus, nephew of the deified Pius Antoninus, great-nephew of the deified Hadrian, great-great-nephew of the deified Trajan Parthicus, and great-great-great-nephew of the deified Nerva; and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, in his 2nd year of tribunician power, proconsul; and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, brother of our Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, and Julia Domna Augusta, mother of the camp. The republic of Cuiculitans built granaries from the ground up, dedicated by Quintus Anicius Faustus, consular legate of the Augusti, a most excellent man, patron of the colony.
EDCS-23600007.summary: This inscription records the construction and dedication of granaries by the community of Cuiculitans, funded publicly and dedicated by the legate Quintus Anicius Faustus, during the reign of Septimius Severus and his son Caracalla, likely around 200 AD.

EDCS-23600011.translation: To Julia Domna Augusta, wife of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, father of the fatherland, mother of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus Maximus, chief priest, father of the fatherland, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-23600011.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument to Julia Domna, wife of Septimius Severus and mother of Caracalla, funded by the local council and public funds, indicating her importance during their reigns.

EDCS-23600012.translation: To the deified Commodus, son of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, promoter of the empire, chief priest, in his 10th year of tribunician power, 11th acclamation as emperor, 3rd consul, father of the fatherland; and to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, in his 5th year of tribunician power, proconsul, and to Publius Septimius Geta, most noble Caesar, prince of youth. Quintus Caecilius... SICI placed the statue which he had promised, above the legal limit, and having increased the funds, dedicated it to the divine power.
EDCS-23600012.summary: This inscription records Quintus Caecilius placing and dedicating a statue to the deified Commodus and also to the reigning emperors Caracalla and Geta, exceeding the promised amount, likely around 202-205 AD.

EDCS-23600014.translation: To the deified Commodus, son of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, promoter of the empire, chief priest, in his 11th year of tribunician power, 11th acclamation as emperor, 3rd consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul; to the father of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, in his 6th year of tribunician power, 1st consul, and proconsul; and to Publius Septimius Geta, most noble Caesar, prince of youth. Marcus Tullius, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, who is also Papiria Pudens, gave the statue which he had promised above the legal limit for the honor of his aedileship, with Scribonius Scribonianus, son of his sister, supervising.
EDCS-23600014.summary: This inscription records Marcus Tullius (Papiria Pudens) dedicating a statue to the deified Commodus and the reigning emperors Caracalla and Geta, honoring his aedileship, likely around 205-208 AD.

EDCS-23600015.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, in his 7th year of tribunician power, 2nd consul, son of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, chief priest, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, father of the fatherland, son of Severus Augustus, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, and of the deified Nerva. For Cuiculitan.
EDCS-23600015.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Caracalla, listing his extensive titulature and lineage, likely erected by the community of Cuiculitani during his reign around 204 AD.

EDCS-23600017.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, chief priest, in his 17th year of tribunician power, 3rd acclamation as emperor, 4th consul, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-23600017.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Caracalla with his extensive titles, likely erected by a public body during his reign around 214 AD.

EDCS-23600019.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, chief priest, in his 19th year of tribunician power, 4th consul, 3rd acclamation as emperor, proconsul; and to Julia Domna, pious, fortunate, Augusta, mother of the Senate, and of the fatherland, and of the camp; and to the deified Severus Augustus Pius, father of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus. The republic erected a triumphal arch from the ground up by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-23600019.summary: This inscription records the construction of a triumphal arch by the republic, dedicated to Emperor Caracalla, his mother Julia Domna, and his deified father Septimius Severus, likely around 216 AD.

EDCS-23600022.translation: To Antoninus Augustus by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-23600022.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument to Emperor Antoninus (likely Caracalla) funded by the local council and public money, indicating a public dedication during his reign.

EDCS-23600030.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 4th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, father of the fatherland, grandson of the divine Gordians, the state of Cuiculitanum, devoted to his divine will and majesty by decree of the decurions, at public expense.
EDCS-23600030.summary: A public dedication by the state of Cuiculitanum to Emperor Gordian III, likely erected in his fourth consulship.

EDCS-23600034.translation: To the divine Gordian, by decree of the decurions, at public expense.
EDCS-23600034.summary: A public dedication by the state of Cuiculitanum to the deified Emperor Gordian III.

EDCS-23600035.translation: To Marcia Otacilia Severa Augusta, wife of our lord the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, mother of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Augustus, his son, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, prince of youth, and of the camp, and of the senate, and of the fatherland, the state of Cuiculitanum, devoted to their divine will and majesty by decree of the decurions, at public expense.
EDCS-23600035.summary: A public dedication by the state of Cuiculitanum to Empress Marcia Otacilia Severa and her son, Emperor Philip II, likely erected around 247 AD.

EDCS-23600038.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, chief priest, Germanicus Maximus, with tribunician power for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, consul for the 4th time, proconsul, father of our lord Gallienus Augustus, the state of Cuiculitanum, devoted to his divine will and majesty.
EDCS-23600038.summary: A public dedication by the state of Cuiculitanum to Emperor Valerian I, likely erected around 260 AD.

EDCS-23600040.translation: For the safety of our lords, the Augusti, Quintus Lucilius Crescens and Quintus Lucilius Pelusius, priests, having been warned by a vision, made and dedicated an altar at their own expense.
EDCS-23600040.summary: A private dedication of an altar by two priests for the well-being of the Augusti, possibly during the reign of the Gordians.

EDCS-23600046.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus, Lucius Appius Amenus.
EDCS-23600046.summary: A dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius by Lucius Appius Amenus, possibly a private individual.

EDCS-23600048.summary: A dedication to an unnamed proconsul, possibly on a public building.

EDCS-23600049.summary: A fragmented dedication to an emperor, possibly Augustus himself or a later emperor with that title.

EDCS-23600051.summary: A very fragmented inscription mentioning a son of a deified individual, likely a funerary or honorific text.

EDCS-23600055.summary: An inscription identifying an individual named Maximus who held the office of pontifex.

EDCS-23600056.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Diocletian, Invictus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 4th time, consul for the 3rd time, imperator for the 3rd time, proconsul, by decree of the town councillors, at public expense.
EDCS-23600056.summary: A public monument dedicated to Emperor Diocletian, likely erected by the town council of Cuiculitanorum.

EDCS-23600060.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantius, most noble Caesar, Germanicus Maximus II, Sarmaticus Maximus, Persicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Carpicus Maximus, Armeniacus Maximus, Medicus Maximus, Adiabenicus Maximus, with tribunician power for the 9th time, consul for the 4th time, by the state of the people of Cuiculitanum, devoted to their divine majesty.
EDCS-23600060.summary: An inscription from the state of Cuiculitanum dedicated to Caesar Constantius, likely from the period of his co-rule around 337-340 AD.

EDCS-23600062.translation: To Flavius Valerius Constantius, most noble Caesar, the best and bravest, by the state of the colony of Cuiculitanum.
EDCS-23600062.summary: A dedication by the state of the colony of Cuiculitanum to Caesar Constantius, likely from the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-23600066.translation: To our lords Galerius Valerius Maximianus and Flavius Valerius Severus, unconquered Augusti, and Galerius Valerius Maximinus and Flavius Valerius Constantinus, most noble Caesars, by the state of the colony of Cuiculitanum, devoted to their divine majesty.
EDCS-23600066.summary: An inscription from the state of Cuiculitanum dedicated to Emperors Maximian, Severus, Maximinus, and Constantine, dating to between 307 and 311 AD.

EDCS-23600069.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantine, Pious, Fortunate, Invictus, Augustus, chief priest, Sarmaticus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, with tribunician power for the 10th time, consul for the 3rd time, imperator for the 9th time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, by Iulius Iuvenalis, vir perfectissimus, rationalis, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-23600069.summary: A dedication by Iulius Iuvenalis to Emperor Constantine the Great, likely from around 315-324 AD.

EDCS-23600074.translation: To the most blessed and most noble Caesar Flavius, our lord, by the most distinguished council of the colony of Cuiculitanum.
EDCS-23600074.summary: A dedication by the council of the colony of Cuiculitanum to Caesar Constantius, likely from the mid-4th century AD.

EDCS-23600075.translation: To Constantius, Invictus, Ever Augustus, by the council of the colony of Cuiculitanum, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-23600075.summary: A dedication by the council of Cuiculitanum to Emperor Constantius, likely from the mid-4th century AD.

EDCS-23600076.translation: To the eternal prince, and to the majesty ruling everywhere with unending power, our lord Flavius Claudius Iulian, ever victorious Augustus, by the most distinguished council of the colony of Cuiculitanum, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-23600076.summary: A dedication by the council of Cuiculitanum to Emperor Julian the Apostate, likely from 361-363 AD.

EDCS-23600077.translation: For the blessedness and happiness of the times of our lords Valentinian and Valens, ever Augusti, he constructed and adorned and dedicated a basilica worthy of the colony of Cuiculitanum, from the excavated ruins, which would have already been higher than the very highest points of the city, from its foundations, by Publilius Ceionius Caecina Albinus, vir clarissimus, consularis, sexfascalis of the province of Numidia, with the supervision and completion by Caecilius Patricius, flamen perpetuus, Tullius Praestantius, flamen perpetuus, Pomponius Pudentianus, flamen perpetuus, and Domitius Rusticus Gaius S Faustinianus.
EDCS-23600077.summary: An inscription detailing the construction and dedication of a basilica in Cuiculitanum, funded by the province of Numidia and overseen by various officials, dedicated to Emperors Valentinian I and Valens (364-375 AD).

EDCS-23600079.translation: For the blessedness of the times of our lords Valentinian and Valens, ever Augusti, he arranged and constructed and dedicated a vestiary, by Publilius Ceionius Caecina Albinus, vir clarissimus, consularis, sexfascalis of the province of Numidia, with the supervision and personal expense of Rutilius Saturninus, vir clarissimus.
EDCS-23600079.summary: An inscription recording the construction and dedication of a vestiary in Cuiculitanum by Publilius Ceionius Caecina Albinus, at the expense of Rutilius Saturninus, for the emperors Valentinian I and Valens (364-375 AD).

EDCS-23600081.translation: Flavius Simplicius, a most distinguished man, consular governor of the province of Numidia Constantina, has dedicated the basilica, always devoted to the divinity and majesty of our most great lords, the emperors Valeninian, Valens, and Gratian, perpetual always Augusti; Rutilius Saturninus, however, a most distinguished man, saw to its construction from the ground up and its building as his duty of public games.
EDCS-23600081.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a basilica by the governor of Numidia Constantina, Flavius Simplicius, with Rutilius Saturninus overseeing its construction.

EDCS-23600083.translation: To our lords, the emperors Valeninian, Valens, and Gratian.
EDCS-23600083.summary: This is a dedication to the emperors Valeninian, Valens, and Gratian.

EDCS-23600091.summary: This is a very brief inscription, possibly identifying a consul named Maximus.

EDCS-23600095.translation: Marcus Aemilius Macer, imperial legate with praetorian power, a most distinguished man and patron of the colony, dedicated this.
EDCS-23600095.summary: This records the dedication of a monument by Marcus Aemilius Macer, a patron of the colony and imperial legate.

EDCS-23600096.translation: To Lucius Alfennus Senecio, son of Marcius, of the tribe Quirina, priest of Neptune, procurator of the emperor in the province of Belgica, [honoring him] for the colony of Cuicul because with his most ready kindness he aided the interests of his most splendid colony, recently when he presided over the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, by decree of the decurions, with public funds.
EDCS-23600096.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Alfennus Senecio, a priest and imperial procurator, for his benefits to the colony of Cuicul, funded by public money.

EDCS-23600099.translation: The republic of Cuicul, by decree of the decurions, with public funds, [erected this] to the most distinguished girls, Tiberia Claudia Aquilina and Tiberia Claudia Digna Saturnina, daughters of Tiberius Claudius Proculus, a most distinguished man, imperial legate with praetorian power of the most splendid province of Numidia, designated consul, legate of the VI Legion Ferrata, Fidelis Constantis, curator of the Athenians and Patrensians, urban praetor, candidate, tribune candidate, urban quaestor, subprefect of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, prefect of the Constantian cavalry ala, tribune of the VI cohort of Roman citizens, prefect of the III cohort of the Alpinis, [honoring him] for his outstanding excellence in his homeland.
EDCS-23600099.summary: This inscription by the colony of Cuicul honors the daughters of Tiberius Claudius Proculus and celebrates his distinguished career and service to his homeland.

EDCS-23600100.translation: To Claudia Satura, wife of Lucius Titinius Clodianus, a most excellent man, procurator of our emperors, the most splendid order of the colony of Cuicul, with a public collection of sportulae, erected this. To Lucius Titinius Maximus Clodianus, perpetual flamen, son of Lucius Titinius Clodianus, a most excellent man, the most splendid order of the colony of Cuicul, with a collection of sportulae, erected this as patron. To Titinia Clodia Satura, daughter of Lucius Titinius Clodianus, a most excellent man, and Clodia O[---], the most splendid order of the colony of Cuicul, with a collection of sportulae, erected this.
EDCS-23600100.summary: This inscription records memorials erected by the colony of Cuicul for members of the family of Lucius Titinius Clodianus, a procurator and patron.

EDCS-23600103.translation: The Cuiculitani, by decree of the decurions, with public funds, dedicating [this] by Gaius Julius Lepidus Tertullus, imperial legate with praetorian power.
EDCS-23600103.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the people of Cuicul, funded publicly and overseen by Gaius Julius Lepidus Tertullus, an imperial legate.

EDCS-23600109.translation: To Lucius Titinius Clodianus, a most excellent man, who held four military commands, from the records of the prefects of the praetorium, prefect and procurator of the province of Epirus, also procurator of the 40% tax on ports in Asia and Bithynia, procurator of the province of Lower Moesia, procurator of the province of Numidia, acting as governor, Caius Pomponius Saturninus, scribe of the ala of the Pannonians.
EDCS-23600109.summary: This inscription lists the extensive administrative and military career of Lucius Titinius Clodianus, honored by Caius Pomponius Saturninus.

EDCS-23600110.translation: To the imperial legate with praetorian power of the III Augusta Legion.
EDCS-23600110.summary: This is a dedication to an unnamed imperial legate with praetorian power serving with the III Augusta Legion.

EDCS-23600114.translation: Marcellus, overseer of the tax farmers of Africa, made this cistern for his deserving wife.
EDCS-23600114.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a wife, set up by her husband who held a financial position in Africa.

EDCS-23600115.translation: To the memory of Plotius Pidanius, who lived [for] ... years during his procuratorship.
EDCS-23600115.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and lifespan of an individual who served as a procurator.

EDCS-23600118.translation: Sextus Caecilius Iulianus, father of the colony, perpetual priest of five colonies, gave this.
EDCS-23600118.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Sextus Caecilius Iulianus, a prominent local official and priest.

EDCS-23600119.translation: To Quintus Gresius Tertullus, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, twice-serving magistrate, aedile, quaestor, from the legacies of friends, [and] to his very dear father, Sextus Caecilius Maximus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-23600119.summary: This is a dedication honoring two individuals, one likely a magistrate and the other a father, possibly on a funerary monument.

EDCS-23600121.translation: under the supervision of Gaius Cosinius.
EDCS-23600121.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Gaius Cosinius as someone involved in overseeing a project.

EDCS-23600123.translation: Lucius Cosinius Primus and [---] with columns.
EDCS-23600123.summary: This fragmented inscription mentions Lucius Cosinius Primus and architectural elements, possibly related to a building project.

EDCS-23600124.translation: To Lucius Cosinius Primus, son of Lucius, of the Arnensian tribe, aedile, quaestor, quinquennial duovir, perpetual priest, prefect, imperial duovir of the Emperor Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country, decurion of the Julian Colony of Concordia Karthago, aedile, augur, admitted into five decades, for whom, when the people and the most holy order of Cuiculitanorum decreed a statue for his generosity, Gaius Cosinius Maximus, his brother, content with the honor of the inscription, erected [it] with his own money and dedicated it by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-23600124.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Lucius Cosinius Primus, detailing his many civic and religious offices in Carthage and Cuiculitanum, with a statue erected by his brother.

EDCS-23600125.translation: To Lucius Cosinius Primus, son of Lucius, of the Arnensian tribe, aedile, quaestor, quinquennial duovir, perpetual priest, prefect, imperial duovir of the Emperor Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country, decurion of the Julian Colony of Concordia Karthago, aedile, augur, admitted into five decades, for whom, when the people and the most holy order of Cuiculitanorum decreed a chariot drawn by two horses for his generosity, Gaius Cosinius Maximus, his brother, content with the honor of the inscription, erected [it] with his own money and dedicated it by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-23600125.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Lucius Cosinius Primus, detailing his many civic and religious offices in Carthage and Cuiculitanum, with a two-horse chariot dedicated by his brother.

EDCS-23600126.translation: Lucius Cosinius Primus, son of Lucius, perpetual priest, made [this] with his own money.
EDCS-23600126.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Lucius Cosinius Primus, who was a perpetual priest and funded the project himself.

EDCS-23600127.translation: To Gargilianus, to his mother Curtia Auctilla, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-23600127.summary: This is a dedication from Gargilianus to his mother, Curtia Auctilla.

EDCS-23600131.translation: To Flavius Sempronianus, son of Flavius, of the Papiria tribe, annual priest of Augustus, aedile, twice *duumvir*, quinquennial official, prefect of youth, perpetual priest, for his distinguished munificence in reducing the price of grain when the market was rising, by returning the contribution to the people, Titus Flavius Marcianus, his son, made this statue with his own money for his most devoted father.
EDCS-23600131.summary: This is a dedication of a statue to Flavius Sempronianus for his public service in reducing grain prices, made by his son.

EDCS-23600132.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Praetorian Guard.
EDCS-23600132.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits, possibly for members of the Praetorian Guard.

EDCS-23600133.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Caius Gargilius Victor, quaestor, aedile, *duumvir*, for himself and for his Aurelia Victoria Cyrilla, who lived 27 years, and for his most devoted mother Sempronia Rogata, who lived 55 years, and for his most rare father Lucius Gargilius Victor, who lived 55 years, and for his brother Lucius Gargilius Praetorianus, skilled in law, then advocate for the state, who fulfilled all public offices, who lived 40 years, he made [this monument]. Dedicated by his sons Gargilius Victor and Extricata, also Praetorians, and by Marcus Iulius Victor, son of Gargilia Rogata, sister of the above-named Gargilii, who lived 21 years.
EDCS-23600133.summary: This is a funerary monument for Caius Gargilius Victor and his family members, including his parents, brother, and wife, likely erected by his sons.

EDCS-23600134.translation: Caius Aemilius Martialis, son of Caius, of the Papiria tribe, pontifex, quaestor, and Aemilius Martialis Iunior and Martialis Iunior of our order, Roman knights, with their brother Honoratus, placed [this monument] for their most worthy mother Gargilia Marciana, daughter of Caius, wife of Caius, most rare, with divided distributions to both decurions and citizens, and with theatrical games performed at their own expense. Dedicated on the Kalends of September in the year of the consulship of Fuscus and Dexter. By decree of the order, on the 16th day before the Kalends of September, in the year of the above-named consuls, on her birthday, the 15th day before the Kalends of May.
EDCS-23600134.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by the sons of Gargilia Marciana for their mother, including details of distributions and games held in her honor, with dating by consulship and her birthday.

EDCS-23600137.translation: To Caius Iulius Crescentianus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, Didius Crescentianus, honored with the public horse by the Emperor, tribune of the cohort of the Sardinians, prefect of youth of Cirta, perpetual priest of the four colonies of Cirta and Cuicul, pontiff, and one who fulfilled all offices in the five colonies, Didia Cornelia Ingenua, daughter of Caius, perpetual priestess, [made this] for her most rare father.
EDCS-23600137.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Caius Iulius Crescentianus, a prominent local official and priest, made by his daughter.

EDCS-23600138.translation: For Caius Iulius Crescentianus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, Didius Crescentianus, honored with the public horse by the Emperor, perpetual priest of the 5 colonies, pontiff, and one who fulfilled all offices in the 5 colonies, [this was arranged and ordered to be made] from the will of Didia Cornelia, woman of most illustrious memory, his daughter.
EDCS-23600138.summary: This funerary inscription specifies that the monument for Caius Iulius Crescentianus was commissioned by his daughter, Didia Cornelia, according to her will.

EDCS-23600139.translation: He arranged and ordered it to be made, under the supervision of Iulius Montanianus, quinquennial priest, perpetual priest, who, out of love and for the growth of his own community, instituted, completed, and dedicated [this] according to the vows of the province with his own strength and own money.
EDCS-23600139.summary: This inscription records the completion and dedication of a public work by Iulius Montanianus, a quinquennial and perpetual priest, funded and executed by himself.

EDCS-23600140.summary: This is a simple dedication, likely funerary, to Iulia Honorina.

EDCS-23600141.translation: Quintus Gresius Tertullus made [this] for his most rare wife Iulia Impetrata, daughter of Caius, and for his most devoted mother Iulia Maximina, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-23600141.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by Quintus Gresius Tertullus for both his wife and his mother.

EDCS-23600144.translation: For Gn. Nervia Iunior and Leontius V.S. [made this].
EDCS-23600144.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gn. Nervia Iunior and Leontius, possibly by a relative or friend.

EDCS-23600145.translation: Claudia Marciana, his mother, set this up for him because of his outstanding service and devotion: Marco Papio Marciano, son of Marco Papio, who was honored with the public equestrian rank and made Lupercus for the administration of public rites; the site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-23600145.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument erected by a mother for her son, a distinguished individual who held public office and religious positions, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23600146.translation: D(is) A(manibus). [He/She] set [this up] with the money from Ampliata, together with his wife Rufina and his sons Primianus and Rusticus.
EDCS-23600146.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a funerary monument set up by Ampliata and her family, possibly in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600147.translation: The Sallustii brothers and the Congruii Constantius, having been honored by their sons, [set this up] in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-23600147.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by the Sallustii and Congruii brothers, honoring their sons and fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600148.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly mentioning a person named Horta Marcus Terentius, but its purpose and date are unclear.

EDCS-23600184.translation: Of Titus Livius Crescentis, servant and assistant of the Greek Ceres.
EDCS-23600184.summary: This dedication honors Titus Livius Crescentis, who served as a minister or assistant related to the worship of Ceres, probably in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600185.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Modestus Nepos, ordinary augur and master of augurs, lived 33 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23600185.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Modestus Nepos, an augur, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600187.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudia Orfita, daughter of Lucius, priestess, lived 70 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23600187.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Claudia Orfita, a priestess, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600188.summary: This inscription simply records the name and title of Marcus Coelius Felix, a priest, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600189.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Flavius Castus, priest, lived 45 years; he is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Flavius Felix lived 21 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23600189.summary: This double funerary inscription records the deaths and burials of two individuals, Titus Flavius Castus (a priest) and Titus Flavius Felix, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600190.translation: Iulia Herbasa, priestess, lived 65 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23600190.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Iulia Herbasa, a priestess, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600191.summary: This inscription likely names Secundus, a priest, possibly on a monument related to his priestly duties.

EDCS-23600194.summary: This inscription expresses the devotion of Fel to his wife, possibly on a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-23600202.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titus Iulius Sabinus, a soldier of the 21st legion, is buried here.
EDCS-23600202.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Iulius Sabinus, a soldier of the legion XXI, who died after service.

EDCS-23600203.translation: To the Divine Shades and eternal security. Valerius Valentinus, a citizen of Anaunus, veteran, aged twenty, served for ten years, lived for thirty years.
EDCS-23600203.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius Valentinus, a citizen from Anaunus, detailing his age, service, and life span.

EDCS-23600206.translation: A dutiful veteran lived seventy-two years, is buried here.
EDCS-23600206.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a veteran who lived to be seventy-two years old.

EDCS-23600207.translation: Herodes, a most distinguished man, former consul, established a bronze measure for a sextarius of wine. Herodes, a most distinguished man, former consul, established a bronze measure for heads of barley. Herodes, a most distinguished man, former consul, established a bronze measure for a modius of wheat.
EDCS-23600207.summary: This inscription records the establishment of bronze measures for wine, barley, and wheat by Herodes, a former consul, likely for public use or a market.

EDCS-23600208.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titus Flavius Fortunatus, an 'offector', lived sixty-seven years. Also for his wife, Iulia Victorina, an 'offectrix', who lived fifty-three years. Iovinus, their son, erected this.
EDCS-23600208.summary: This funerary inscription was erected by Iovinus for his parents, Titus Flavius Fortunatus and Iulia Victorina, both 'offectores', detailing their ages and his filial piety.

EDCS-23600209.translation: To the Divine Shades. Primus, a tailor, lived thirty-six years.
EDCS-23600209.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Primus, a tailor, who died at the age of thirty-six.

EDCS-23600212.translation: Whatever we possess has been made from our own resources, so that it may be called a home. Castorius made this.
EDCS-23600212.summary: This inscription records that Castorius built a home using his own resources.

EDCS-23600216.summary: This inscription indicates that an object or monument was made in the workshop of Ascanius.

EDCS-23600221.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Gaius.
EDCS-23600221.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Divine Spirits by Gaius, likely a funerary marker.

EDCS-23600222.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Gaius.
EDCS-23600222.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Divine Spirits by Gaius, likely a funerary marker.

EDCS-23600223.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Aelius Antioxus lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23600223.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Aelius Antioxus, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-23600224.translation: Marcus Aelius Saturninus. Iulia Fortunata and her children set this up for her husband, who passed away. He lived 70 years.
EDCS-23600224.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Iulia Fortunata and her children for her husband, Marcus Aelius Saturninus, who lived 70 years.

EDCS-23600225.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Agatangelus lived 28 years.
EDCS-23600225.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the life of Agatangelus, who died at the age of 28.

EDCS-23600226.translation: To Agrius M., who lived 110 years, his son made this.
EDCS-23600226.summary: This funerary monument was made by a son for his father, Agrius M., who lived 110 years.

EDCS-23600228.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Ampliata lived 4 years.
EDCS-23600228.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Ampliata, who died at the age of 4.

EDCS-23600229.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Annia Grata lived 101 years, and Iulia Gratiana lived 45 years.
EDCS-23600229.summary: This funerary inscription honors Annia Grata, who lived 101 years, and Iulia Gratiana, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-23600230.translation: For the memory of Antonius Quietus the Younger. Quietus and Mariosa made this for their deserving son. He lived 30 years.
EDCS-23600230.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Quietus and Mariosa for their son, Antonius Quietus the Younger, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-23600231.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Antonius Rogatus lived 75 years.
EDCS-23600231.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Antonius Rogatus, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-23600232.translation: To Antonia Hesychia, daughter, who lived 10 years, 4 months, and 9 days; her father and mother made this.
EDCS-23600232.summary: A funerary monument set up by parents for their deceased daughter, Antonia Hesychia.

EDCS-23600233.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Apollonius lived about 67 years.
EDCS-23600233.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Apollonius, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-23600234.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aponia Satura, pious, while alive, established this monument for herself.
EDCS-23600234.summary: A funerary monument established by Aponia Satura for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-23600236.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Aquilia Magna, daughter of Gududion, who lived 45 years.
EDCS-23600236.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Aquilia Magna, recording her age.

EDCS-23600237.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Arruntius Marcianus lived 60 years. Rutilia Probatula, his most loving wife, provided this.
EDCS-23600237.summary: A funerary monument provided by a wife for her deceased husband, Arruntius Marcianus.

EDCS-23600238.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits of the Aufidii and Antonii.
EDCS-23600238.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits of the Aufidii and Antonii families.

EDCS-23600239.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Avidius Sa[---]
EDCS-23600239.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirit of Avidius Sa[---].

EDCS-23600240.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Luciosa lived 36 years. Iulius Iulianus made this for his most loving wife while alive.
EDCS-23600240.summary: A funerary monument made by a husband for his wife, Aurelia Luciosa, during his lifetime.

EDCS-23600242.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Baia lived 3 years and 7 months.
EDCS-23600242.summary: A funerary inscription recording the short lifespan of Baia.

EDCS-23600243.translation: Maxima for herself and her spouse. Cornelius Novelus made this, sacred to the departed spirits. C. Nampulus lived 80 years.
EDCS-23600243.summary: A funerary monument established by Cornelius Novelus for himself and his spouse, also commemorating C. Nampulus.

EDCS-23600244.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Bassilia lived 60 years.
EDCS-23600244.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Bassilia, likely a freedwoman given the common use of 'Dis Manibus' for such individuals.

EDCS-23600247.translation: To the spirits of the departed. This was erected by Macrina for her husband Sextus Caecilius Maximus, for himself and his descendants, and for his brother-in-law Satrio, son of C. T. Mustiolus, and his father-in-law Julius Vo Maximus. The cost was 300 sesterces.
EDCS-23600247.summary: A funerary monument erected by Macrina for her family, including her husband Sextus Caecilius Maximus, indicating a familial burial plot and a significant expenditure.

EDCS-23600248.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Caecilius Victorinus lived 73 years. Iunia Ianuaria lived 51 years. This was overseen by Junius Martialis and Caecilia Gududia, their daughter.
EDCS-23600248.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lives of Quintus Caecilius Victorinus and Iunia Ianuaria, erected by their daughter Caecilia Gududia, indicating a family burial.

EDCS-23600249.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextus Caecilius Urbanus lived 18 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23600249.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextus Caecilius Urbanus, who died at a young age.

EDCS-23600250.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Baraect lived 50 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600250.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Baraect, with a wish for peaceful repose of the deceased.

EDCS-23600251.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Cattula lived 50 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23600251.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Caecilia Cattula.

EDCS-23600252.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sacred to Caecilia Paterna.
EDCS-23600252.summary: A funerary dedication to Caecilia Paterna.

EDCS-23600253.translation: Caecilia Rogata lived 67 years. She is buried here. Karito made this for her husband.
EDCS-23600253.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Rogata, erected by her husband Karito.

EDCS-23600254.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sacred to Caius Caecilius Datinus. Quintus Ater and Felix lived 15 years. To the spirits of the departed. Sacred to Aurelia Faustina. She lived 70 years. She is buried here. Caecilius Honoratus lived 32 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23600254.summary: A complex funerary inscription documenting the lives and burials of multiple individuals: Caius Caecilius Datinus, Quintus Ater, Felix, Aurelia Faustina, and Caecilius Honoratus.

EDCS-23600255.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sacred to Caecilia Primosa, dearest wife. She lived 23 years. Flavius Nonnus made this.
EDCS-23600255.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Primosa, erected by her husband Flavius Nonnus, emphasizing their marital affection.

EDCS-23600256.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Bas lived 50 years and Cald Matr lived 70 years.
EDCS-23600256.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two individuals, Bas and Cald Matr, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-23600258.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cassia Ingenua fulfilled a vow and lived 44 years.
EDCS-23600258.summary: This funerary inscription records Cassia Ingenua fulfilling a vow and her age at death.

EDCS-23600259.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Catellius Severus made this for his dearest son Catelius Soricus, who lived 20 years, while she herself was alive.
EDCS-23600259.summary: This funerary inscription records that Catellius Severus, while alive, made a monument for her son Catelius Soricus, stating his age at death.

EDCS-23600260.translation: Romanus made this for his most excellent and incomparable father.
EDCS-23600260.summary: This inscription records Romanus dedicating a monument to his father.

EDCS-23600261.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Claudia Thyche lived 70 years and is buried here.
EDCS-23600261.summary: This funerary inscription records Claudia Thyche's age at death and burial location.

EDCS-23600262.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Coelius Modestus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years and is buried here.
EDCS-23600262.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Coelius Modestus's age at death and burial location.

EDCS-23600263.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Coelius Rogatus's age at death.

EDCS-23600265.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Cornelius Pequarius, son of Lucius, of the Arnensis tribe, lived 77 years.
EDCS-23600265.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Cornelius Pequarius's age at death.

EDCS-23600267.translation: For Cosinia Iachene, the foster-child of the most distinguished man Cosinius Maximus, who lived 36 years. FFCOREVS to his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-23600267.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Cosinia Iachene at age 36 and is dedicated by her husband, FFCOREVS, to his well-deserving wife.

EDCS-23600268.translation: Marcus Crepereius Honoratus, the father, lived 85 years. Domitia Mustia, the mother, lived 52 years. Crepereius Nundinarius made and dedicated (this monument) at Nundinae.
EDCS-23600268.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages at death of Marcus Crepereius Honoratus and Domitia Mustia, and states that Crepereius Nundinarius dedicated the monument.

EDCS-23600271.translation: Titus Dom lived 5 years. Bibu Secun [restored epitaph].
EDCS-23600271.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Dom, who died at age 5, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23600272.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Domitius Proculus lived 48 years. Domitia Secundula made this for her master.
EDCS-23600272.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Domitius Proculus at age 48 and was set up by Domitia Secundula for her master.

EDCS-23600274.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Fabius Crispinus, son of Caius, lived 13 years, 9 months, and 8 days.
EDCS-23600274.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Fabius Crispinus, who died at the age of 13.

EDCS-23600275.translation: Felicis' daughter lived 23 years. She lies here. May your bones rest well. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-23600275.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of an unnamed daughter of Felicis at age 23 and includes a common epitaph wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-23600277.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. A VR. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavius C. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. H. Primosus, a freedman, lived 40 years. Flavius SA R.
EDCS-23600277.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for H. Primosus, a freedman, who died at age 40, with other names or fragments also present.

EDCS-23600279.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Crispus lived 100 years.
EDCS-23600279.summary: This funerary inscription records the exceptional longevity of Titus Flavius Crispus, who lived to be 100 years old.

EDCS-23600280.translation: Flavia Matrona lived 53 years. Titus Flavius Felix lived [unknown years].
EDCS-23600280.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Flavia Matrona (53 years) and partially records that of Titus Flavius Felix.

EDCS-23600281.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavia Rogata lived [unknown years]. Caius Maius Felix lived 60 years. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavia Bonosa lived 100 years. Titus Flavius Gudullus lived 60 years. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Flavianus lived 40 years. Alegnius Flaccus lived 60 years.
EDCS-23600281.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths and ages of multiple individuals: Flavia Rogata, Caius Maius Felix (60), Flavia Bonosa (100), Titus Flavius Gudullus (60), Titus Flavius Flavianus (40), and Alegnius Flaccus (60).

EDCS-23600282.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Flavius Saturninus lived 43 years.
EDCS-23600282.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Flavius Saturninus at the age of 43.

EDCS-23600283.translation: Flavius, son of Marci, of the Quirina tribe, Veus, lived [unknown years].
EDCS-23600283.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for Flavius, son of Marci, of the Quirina tribe, also named Veus, with his lifespan incomplete.

EDCS-23600284.translation: Titus Flavius Victor, his grandson, son of Flavi Extricatus, lived 75 years.
EDCS-23600284.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of Titus Flavius Victor and mentioning his grandson.

EDCS-23600285.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titus Flavius Victor lived more than 10 years.
EDCS-23600285.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of Titus Flavius Victor.

EDCS-23600286.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of Flavia Coronaria.

EDCS-23600287.summary: A funerary epitaph, likely for Flavius Felicissimus.

EDCS-23600288.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Flavia Felicitas, a most rare wife, wife of Flavi Priscus, she lived 70 years; cared for by Flavius Felix and Flavius Flavianus, her sons.
EDCS-23600288.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Felicitas, wife of Flavi Priscus, who lived 70 years and was cared for by her sons.

EDCS-23600289.summary: A funerary epitaph, likely for Flavia Germanilla, identified as a daughter.

EDCS-23600290.translation: To the Divine Shades. Flavia Longina, daughter of Primus, lived [age missing] years.
EDCS-23600290.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and parentage of Flavia Longina, with missing age.

EDCS-23600291.translation: To the Divine Shades. Flavia Pollitta lived 40 years.
EDCS-23600291.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of Flavia Pollitta.

EDCS-23600292.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sacred to Flavia Pusinica, daughter of Titus, lived 40 years.
EDCS-23600292.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Pusinica, daughter of Titus, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-23600293.translation: Flavia Saturnina lived 50 years. May your bones rest well here.
EDCS-23600293.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Saturnina, who lived 50 years, with a wish for peace.

EDCS-23600298.translation: To the memory of Quintus Gargilius Felix, a distinguished man, who lived 72 years, and of Livia Siliqua, his betrothed, who lived 16 years.
EDCS-23600298.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating a married couple.

EDCS-23600299.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gargilius Peculiaris lived 50 years.
EDCS-23600299.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gargilius Peculiaris.

EDCS-23600300.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gargilia.
EDCS-23600300.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Gargilia.

EDCS-23600301.translation: To the memory of Gargilia Procula, a most exceptional woman; to his incomparable wife, Gaius Aelius Successus.
EDCS-23600301.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Gaius Aelius Successus to his wife, Gargilia Procula.

EDCS-23600303.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Gessius Nampulus lived 51 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23600303.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Marcus Gessius Nampulus.

EDCS-23600304.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Helvius Martialis dedicated this to his son Helvius Maximus, who lived 2 years and 54 days.
EDCS-23600304.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Helvius Martialis for his young son, Helvius Maximus.

EDCS-23600305.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Helvia Casta lived 10 years.
EDCS-23600305.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Helvia Casta.

EDCS-23600306.translation: Helvia Saturnina lived 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23600306.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Helvia Saturnina.

EDCS-23600307.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Omullia Honorata lived 80 years.
EDCS-23600307.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Omullia Honorata.

EDCS-23600308.translation: To Crispus, for his mother and for the sons of Julius Felcis and [---].
EDCS-23600308.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph indicating a dedication to a mother and sons.

EDCS-23600309.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ostilia Optata lived 41 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23600309.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Ostilia Optata after 41 years of life.

EDCS-23600311.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed, to Gaius Julius and Flavia Rustica.
EDCS-23600311.summary: A funerary dedication to Gaius Julius and Flavia Rustica.

EDCS-23600312.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Julius Adiutor lived 83 years.
EDCS-23600312.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Lucius Julius Adiutor after 83 years of life.

EDCS-23600313.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Adventus lived 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23600313.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Gaius Julius Adventus after 60 years of life.

EDCS-23600314.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Concessus lived 66 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23600314.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Gaius Julius Concessus after 66 years of life.

EDCS-23600315.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Corbulo made this in his lifetime; he lived 80 years.
EDCS-23600315.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Gaius Julius Corbulo after 80 years of life, dedicated by himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-23600316.translation: Secundus, son of Quintus, lived 70 years.
EDCS-23600316.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Secundus, son of Quintus, after 70 years of life.

EDCS-23600317.translation: Julius Urbanus, dutiful, lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23600317.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Julius Urbanus after 75 years of life.

EDCS-23600318.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed, in memory of Julia Matrona. Quintus Julius Quietus made this for his most exceptional wife during his lifetime, because of her outstanding devotion. She lived 80 years. It was cared for by their sons Quintus Julius Candidus and Crementius, and Quintus Julius Quietus Candidianus, along with their sister Julia Vitalica.
EDCS-23600318.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Quintus Julius Quietus for his wife Julia Matrona, commemorating her devotion and her 80 years of life, with care also provided by their children.

EDCS-23600319.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Nampamina made this for her husband Augustalis.
EDCS-23600319.summary: A funerary dedication made by Iulia Nampamina for her husband Augustalis.

EDCS-23600320.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Novella lived 90 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23600320.summary: This is a tombstone epitaph for Iulia Novella, who lived 90 years.

EDCS-23600321.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, in memory of Iulia Restuta, her incomparable sister.
EDCS-23600321.summary: This inscription is a memorial dedicated to Iulia Restuta by her sister.

EDCS-23600322.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Rosa lived 85 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-23600322.summary: This tombstone epitaph for Iulia Rosa records her age at death and includes wishes for her repose.

EDCS-23600323.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Saturnina lived 80 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23600323.summary: This is a tombstone epitaph for Iulia Saturnina, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-23600325.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Secuta made this for herself while alive.
EDCS-23600325.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Iulia Secuta for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-23600326.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iunius Iunianus lived 80 years; Timonia Victorina, his wife, lived 56 years. Iunius Martialis dedicated this to his dearest parents.
EDCS-23600326.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of Iunius Iunianus and his wife Timonia Victorina, and was dedicated by their son Iunius Martialis.

EDCS-23600327.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Decima L. Barsa, daughter of Clare, lived 72 years.
EDCS-23600327.summary: This tombstone epitaph records the name, parentage, and age of Decima L. Barsa.

EDCS-23600330.translation: Ma[ma] of Quinti, daughter Dubitata, lived 24 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23600330.summary: This tombstone epitaph records the name and age of Dubitata, daughter of Quinti.

EDCS-23600331.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ma[ma] Minas lived 45 years.
EDCS-23600331.summary: This tombstone epitaph records the name and age of Ma[ma] Minas.

EDCS-23600333.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Magia Fortunata lived 40 years.
EDCS-23600333.summary: This tombstone epitaph records the name and age of Magia Fortunata.

EDCS-23600334.translation: Olus Malius Martialis lived 60 years in Arna; he is buried here.
EDCS-23600334.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Olus Malius Martialis, recording his age and burial place.

EDCS-23600335.translation: To the spirits of the underworld,lius Marcellus [lived] V.
EDCS-23600335.summary: A dedication to the Manes forlius Marcellus, with an incomplete lifespan.

EDCS-23600338.translation: To the memory of Maximianus, a good man, 100 [years old]; he is buried here.
EDCS-23600338.summary: This inscription commemorates Maximianus, noting his virtue and age, and his burial.

EDCS-23600339.summary: This funerary text records the long life of a slave named Maximus.

EDCS-23600340.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Memmia Asclepias lived 2 years and 3 months; she is buried here.
EDCS-23600340.summary: A dedication to the Manes for Memmia Asclepias, who died at a young age and is buried here.

EDCS-23600341.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Memmia Venusta lived 43 years.
EDCS-23600341.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Manes for Memmia Venusta, recording her age.

EDCS-23600343.translation: Mustacus, also called Mus, lived 8 years; he is buried here. Mustacus the father, most rare, lived 71 years; he is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Annius Maximus the dutiful son, he is buried here; he lived 30 years, 9 months, and 8 days. NA the mother lived 63 years; she is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Annia Romana, the most rare mother, who has guided you well. Annia Fortunata the sister and Iulia SO the daughter lived 75 years. Valeria the dutiful daughter lived 20 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23600343.summary: This complex funerary inscription records the deaths and ages of multiple family members, including Mustacus, his father, his mother NA, his brother Annius Maximus, his sister Annia Fortunata, and his daughters Iulia SO and Valeria, all dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-23600344.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Novia Iulia lived 30 years.
EDCS-23600344.summary: A dedication to the Manes for Novia Iulia, recording her age.

EDCS-23600346.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Orcivius Primus lived 10 [years].
EDCS-23600346.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Manes for Orcivius Primus, with an incomplete record of his age.

EDCS-23600347.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Publius Petronius Crescenti, an incomparable husband, Iulia Fortunata, deserving, made this; he lived 64 years.
EDCS-23600347.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Iulia Fortunata to her husband Publius Petronius Crescenti, recording his age and her devotion.

EDCS-23600348.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Pollius Rogatus lived 80 years.
EDCS-23600348.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gaius Pollius Rogatus.

EDCS-23600349.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Manlius Rogatus placed this for his most devoted wife, Pompeia Restituta, daughter of Marcus. She lived 28 years. [---] For Marcus Pompeius Veter, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe. [---] For Flavia Novella, daughter of Titus. She lived 45 years.
EDCS-23600349.summary: This is a funerary epitaph with multiple individuals, likely a family tomb, recording the lifespans of Pompeia Restituta and Flavia Novella, with Quintus Manlius Rogatus dedicating a part to his wife.

EDCS-23600350.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Postimia Optiva, dutiful, lived 5 years.
EDCS-23600350.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the short lifespan of a child, Postimia Optiva.

EDCS-23600354.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. M. [---] lived 20 years. O. [---] son lived 11 years and Saturnina daughter lived 31 years.
