EDCS-23600354.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespans of multiple individuals, likely family members, including two children.

EDCS-23600355.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Navigius placed this for his father, Saturo. He lived 64 years.
EDCS-23600355.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Navigius dedicated a monument to his father, Saturo.

EDCS-23600356.translation: Here lies Sedia Hilara, mother. Iulia Paula lived 10 years.
EDCS-23600356.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Iulia Paula, daughter of Sedia Hilara.

EDCS-23600358.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Serveia Saturnina.
EDCS-23600358.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph for Serveia Saturnina.

EDCS-23600360.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Spurius Silicius Successus lived 60 years.
EDCS-23600360.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Spurius Silicius Successus.

EDCS-23600361.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Tadius Saturninus lived 35 years. Aufidia Maiorica made this for her most devoted husband while she was alive, with care from Tadius Saturninus, son, and Victoria, daughter. [---]
EDCS-23600361.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Aufidia Maiorica dedicated a monument to her deceased husband, Lucius Tadius Saturninus, with contributions or inscriptions from their children.

EDCS-23600362.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Tammonius Saturus lived 65 years and 10 days. Minthonia Satura lived 78 years and 1 month and 10 days.
EDCS-23600362.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespans of Publius Tammonius Saturus and Minthonia Satura, likely a married couple.

EDCS-23600364.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Terentius Alexander for himself and for Flavia Rogata, his exceedingly rare wife.
EDCS-23600364.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Gaius Terentius Alexander for himself and his wife, Flavia Rogata.

EDCS-23600365.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aulus Terentius Donatus lived 20 years. For Caecilia Fortunata and Aulus Terentius Rufus lived 70 years.
EDCS-23600365.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages at death of Aulus Terentius Donatus and Aulus Terentius Rufus, likely his father, and mentions Caecilia Fortunata.

EDCS-23600366.translation: To the memory of Tilia, daughter of F., our [daughter?] Marcus Ant. Qu. [made this].
EDCS-23600366.summary: This fragmentary inscription is likely a dedication to Tilia, made by Marcus Ant. Qu.

EDCS-23600367.translation: Sacred to the Infernal Divine Shades. Titus V. Urbicus lived 13 years.
EDCS-23600367.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death of Titus V. Urbicus.

EDCS-23600370.translation: M. Us Valens lived 68 years. Marcianus made this for his grandfather.
EDCS-23600370.summary: This funerary inscription records the age at death of M. Us Valens and states it was made by his grandson, Marcianus.

EDCS-23600371.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that begins with the name Marcus Valerius.

EDCS-23600372.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Vettius Antonianus made this for himself while alive. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Donata lived 55 years. She rests here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600372.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Vettius Antonianus made a dedication for himself during his lifetime, and also commemorates Iulia Donata, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-23600373.summary: This fragmentary inscription records the age at death of a child named Marcus Vib[---].

EDCS-23600374.translation: Vibia Va made this for her benevolent husband, with the care of Titus Flavius Ianvarius, [her] son.
EDCS-23600374.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Vibia Va for her husband, managed by their son Titus Flavius Ianvarius.

EDCS-23600375.translation: V. A. Victor R. Caius, son of C. Quinti[---].
EDCS-23600375.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial, possibly involving V. A. Victor and Caius, son of C. Quinti.

EDCS-23600377.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Vindex.

EDCS-23600378.translation: To the Divine Shades of the Ulpii.
EDCS-23600378.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Ulpii family.

EDCS-23600379.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ulpia Victoria lived 8 months, 3 days, and 3 hours.
EDCS-23600379.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Ulpia Victoria.

EDCS-23600381.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ulpia Data lived 50 years; she rests here.
EDCS-23600381.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial place of Ulpia Data.

EDCS-23600393.translation: He lived 30 years; he rests here. For the memory of Lais, who lived 10 months and a half. In peace. The 8th day before the Ides of April.
EDCS-23600393.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Lais and the date of her death.

EDCS-23600406.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus.

EDCS-23600466.translation: To F. Addvetus in the light.
EDCS-23600466.summary: This inscription, possibly funerary or votive, mentions F. Addvetus.

EDCS-23600468.translation: Names of the martyrs Donatus, Aemilianus, Aurelius, Dosiare, Victoriae. On the 12th day before the Kalends of August, they suffered in Arcibini.
EDCS-23600468.summary: This inscription commemorates martyrs, listing their names and the date of their martyrdom.

EDCS-23600471.translation: In the name of the Lord, amen. Pallasius the lector, may he live in God.
EDCS-23600471.summary: This is a Christian inscription identifying Pallasius as a lector and expressing a wish for his salvation.

EDCS-23600475.translation: Flavius Felix, most illustrious man, formerly a princeps, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-23600475.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Flavius Felix, a prominent man, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-23600476.translation: Flavius Paulus, a man of highest rank, formerly a tribune, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-23600476.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Flavius Paulus, who was formerly a tribune.

EDCS-23600477.translation: Flavius Veclus, an honest man, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-23600477.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Flavius Veclus, an honest man.

EDCS-23600478.translation: Ponponius Rusticus, an honest man, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-23600478.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Ponponius Rusticus, an honest man.

EDCS-23600479.translation: Here rests Ponponia Rusticula, a most distinguished woman, lived for 15 years, 10 months, and 17 days, buried in peace on the 6th day before the Kalends of March, after the consulship of our lord Marcianus Augustus and Adelphius, men of highest rank.
EDCS-23600479.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ponponia Rusticula, recording her lifespan and burial date, which falls after the consulship of Marcianus Augustus and Adelphius.

EDCS-23600480.translation: Tulius Adeodatus, a priest, completed his vow.
EDCS-23600480.summary: This inscription records a vow completed by Tulius Adeodatus, who held a priestly office.

EDCS-23600481.translation: Turasius the presbyter, in peace, lived for 43 years, 3 months, and 17 days, buried on the 10th day before the Ides of October in the consulship of Aetius and Studius. Receive worthy tears, pious offerings from your brother, and farewell forever. That night of the tomb is short; may your face never offend me, your image is always with me.
EDCS-23600481.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Turasius the presbyter, detailing his lifespan, burial date during the consulship of Aetius and Studius, and includes a personal epitaph.

EDCS-23600483.translation: Here, where the walls are praised with such glorious roofs, and you see the sacred altars shine, it is not by the powerful but by the glory of such a deed that the name of Cresconius the rector resounds through the ages, by whom, as fame points out his honorable labors, the ancients joyfully placed them in a beautiful seat, whom lasting rest deceived, now they shine with light, supported by a decorated altar, and they rejoice that their collected crown flourishes, which the clever and honest custodian has fulfilled, and the Christian age runs with the desire to see, it flows into one collection to speak praises to God and to touch the sacred thresholds with happy feet, every hand singing the sacrament of God rejoices to receive medicine and chrism, Cresconius, born by the laws themselves and the altars and having served with honors in the Catholic Church, a guardian of chastity, dedicated to charity and peace, whose doctrine makes the innumerable populace of Cuiculitan flourish, a lover of the poor, devoted to alms, to whom there was never a lack of means by which he might do heavenly work, his soul finds refreshment, his body rests in peace, awaiting the future resurrection in Christ, so that he may be a companion in the seat of the heavenly kingdom.
EDCS-23600483.summary: This is a lengthy epitaph for Cresconius, a devoted church official and lover of the poor, praising his virtues, service to the church in Cuiculitan, and his hope for resurrection in the heavenly kingdom.

EDCS-23600487.translation: C... is placed here on the 2nd day before the Kalends of March in the 24th year of Carthage.
EDCS-23600487.summary: This inscription records the placement of an object or person on a specific date, calculated in the 24th year of Carthage.

EDCS-23600492.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus. Quintus Gargilius, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, Quietus, former aedile and prefect, for the position of duumvir, placed this statue, which he had promised for the honor of his aedileship beyond the legal requirement, with his own money.
EDCS-23600492.summary: This dedication records Quintus Gargilius Quietus, a former aedile and prefect, dedicating a statue to Mars Augustus using his own funds, fulfilling a promise made for his aedileship.

EDCS-23600496.translation: Sacred to Neptune Augustus. Marcus Iulius D...
EDCS-23600496.summary: This is a dedication to Neptune Augustus by Marcus Iulius D...

EDCS-23600497.translation: Sacred to Augustus Saturn. Iulia Vernula.
EDCS-23600497.summary: This is a dedication to the god Saturn, by Iulia Vernula.

EDCS-23600499.summary: This is a dedication to the god Sarapis, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-23600500.translation: Marcus Anius Datus, priest of Saturn, and his son Felix gladly paid their vow, sacred to God Saturn.
EDCS-23600500.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Saturn made by a priest and his son, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23600501.translation: Sacred to Augustus Saturn. Publius Anius Namfamo, priest, and Publius Anius Fructus, priest, and likewise Publius Anius Victor, priest.
EDCS-23600501.summary: This inscription lists three priests dedicating to Augustus Saturn, possibly indicating multiple priesthoods or a family of priests.

EDCS-23600502.translation: Sacred to Augustus Saturn. Quintus Caecilius Honoratus and his wife Ortensia Germanilla.
EDCS-23600502.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Augustus Saturn by Quintus Caecilius Honoratus and his wife, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23600503.translation: Calpurnius Musteolus and Flavia Venusta gladly paid their vow for their son Calpurnius Maximus, sacred to Saturn.
EDCS-23600503.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Saturn made by Calpurnius Musteolus and Flavia Venusta on behalf of their son, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23600504.translation: Calpurnius OSTA for Donatus, sacred to Saturn.
EDCS-23600504.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Saturn made by Calpurnius OSTA on behalf of someone named Donatus.

EDCS-23600505.translation: Caius Calpurnius Pastor, priest of Lord Saturn, his wife Pompea Saturnina, and their son Caius Calpurnius Pulicio gladly paid their vow, sacred to Lord Saturn.
EDCS-23600505.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Saturn by a priest, his wife, and son, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23600508.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Tiberius Claudius on behalf of his children.

EDCS-23600509.summary: This inscription appears to be a simple record of two individuals identified as sons, possibly from a funerary context.

EDCS-23600510.translation: Sacred to the Lord God Saturn. Publius Cornelius Privatus, priest, fulfilled his vow willingly. Ianuarius, priest, fulfilled his vow willingly.
EDCS-23600510.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Saturn by two priests, Publius Cornelius Privatus and Ianuarius, who fulfilled their vows willingly.

EDCS-23600511.translation: Sacred to Saturn Augustus. Cosinius Armatius and Cosinia Apistiane fulfilled their vow willingly for their son, Cosinius Gorgonius.
EDCS-23600511.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled for Cosinius Gorgonius by Cosinius Armatius and Cosinia Apistiane, dedicated to Saturn Augustus.

EDCS-23600512.translation: Sacred to Saturn Augustus. CV Victorinus.
EDCS-23600512.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication to Saturn Augustus by CV Victorinus.

EDCS-23600513.translation: Sacred to Saturn Augustus. Lucius Domitius Suriacus, priest, and his wife Rustica fulfilled their vow willingly.
EDCS-23600513.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by the priest Lucius Domitius Suriacus and his wife Rustica, dedicated to Saturn Augustus.

EDCS-23600515.translation: Sacred to Saturn Augustus. Felix and Gressia Saturnina.
EDCS-23600515.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Saturn Augustus by Felix and Gressia Saturnina.

EDCS-23600517.translation: Sacred to the Lord God Saturn. Titus Flavius Crescens and Marcus Flavius Saturninus, his son, a priest, fulfilled their vow willingly.
EDCS-23600517.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled for Titus Flavius Crescens and his son Marcus Flavius Saturninus, a priest, dedicated to Saturn.

EDCS-23600518.translation: Sacred to Saturn Augustus. Titus Flavius Honoratus, priest, consecrated this with Flavius Securus, acting as supervisor, and fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-23600518.summary: This inscription records the consecration and vow fulfillment for Saturn Augustus by the priest Titus Flavius Honoratus and Flavius Securus, acting supervisor.

EDCS-23600519.translation: Sacred to Saturn Augustus. Titus Flavius Honoratus, priest, fulfilled his vow willingly.
EDCS-23600519.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Titus Flavius Honoratus, priest, to Saturn Augustus.

EDCS-23600520.translation: Sacred to Saturn Augustus. Geminius Rogatianus and Victoria fulfilled their vow willingly for their son, Victorinus.
EDCS-23600520.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled for Victorinus by Geminius Rogatianus and Victoria, dedicated to Saturn Augustus.

EDCS-23600521.translation: Quintus Gresius Pacatus fulfilled his vow willingly. Quintus Gresius Pacatus fulfilled his vow willingly.
EDCS-23600521.summary: This inscription records Quintus Gresius Pacatus fulfilling his vow willingly, possibly a repetition for emphasis or a record of two separate vows.

EDCS-23600522.translation: Sacred to Augustus Saturn. Hermadio for himself, for his wife, for his children.
EDCS-23600522.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Saturn, likely made by Hermadio on behalf of his family, possibly on a votive altar or shrine.

EDCS-23600523.translation: Sacred to Augustus Saturn. Ianuarius with his wife Fortunata for Gu(stus?) Ne(pos?).
EDCS-23600523.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Saturn, made by Ianuarius and his wife Fortunata, possibly on behalf of a person named Gu[stus?] Ne[pos?].

EDCS-23600524.translation: Sacred to the holy Lord Augustus Saturn. Caius Iulius Adiutor, priest, with his wife Virtia Pusinna, and his wife Caius Iulius Iulianus, gladly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-23600524.summary: This inscription records a fulfillment of a vow to Saturn, made by Caius Iulius Adiutor, a priest, along with his wife Virtia Pusinna and possibly another wife or associate named Caius Iulius Iulianus.

EDCS-23600525.translation: Sacred to Augustus Saturn. Sextus Iulius Saturninus, priest, with his wife Fortunata and his daughter Saturnina and son Subitanus, gladly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-23600525.summary: This inscription records a family vow fulfillment to Saturn, made by Sextus Iulius Saturninus, a priest, along with his wife, daughter, and son.

EDCS-23600526.translation: Sacred to Augustus Saturn. Iulius Sodalis gladly fulfilled his vow for his daughter Victoria.
EDCS-23600526.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to Saturn, made by Iulius Sodalis on behalf of his daughter Victoria.

EDCS-23600527.translation: Sacred to Augustus Saturn. Iulia Vernula gladly fulfilled her vow for her son, for Felix, with merit.
EDCS-23600527.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to Saturn, made by Iulia Vernula on behalf of her son Felix.

EDCS-23600528.translation: Manilius Crestus gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-23600528.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to an unspecified deity, made by Manilius Crestus.

EDCS-23600529.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Manilius Nepos, priest, and Servatus.
EDCS-23600529.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the 'Departed Spirits' (Dis Manibus), likely a tombstone or funerary monument, mentioning Caius Manilius Nepos, a priest, and Servatus.

EDCS-23600530.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to an unspecified deity, made by Maximinus.

EDCS-23600531.translation: Maximus, priest, and his wife Spes.
EDCS-23600531.summary: This inscription likely records the names and possibly the marriage of Maximus, a priest, and his wife Spes, possibly on a private monument or dedication.

EDCS-23600532.translation: Sacred to Augustus Saturn. Lucius Nonius Felix, son of Lucius, of the Arnensian tribe, priest, gladly paid his vow for himself, his wife, and his children.
EDCS-23600532.summary: This is a votive inscription by a priest, Lucius Nonius Felix, to Saturn Augustus, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23600533.translation: Sacred to the Lord God Saturn. Onesimus, the steward of Cuicul, gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-23600533.summary: This is a votive inscription by Onesimus, a steward, to Saturn, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23600534.translation: Quintus Otacilius Felix, priest, and his wife Celsina, gladly paid their vow to the Holy Lord God Saturn.
EDCS-23600534.summary: This is a votive inscription by a priest, Quintus Otacilius Felix, and his wife Celsina, to Saturn, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23600535.translation: Otacilius Quintianus and Otacilius Felix, sons.
EDCS-23600535.summary: This is a dedication by Otacilius Quintianus and Otacilius Felix, possibly sons of the individuals mentioned in preceding inscriptions, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23600536.translation: Sacred to Augustus Saturn. Petronius Felix gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-23600536.summary: This is a votive inscription by Petronius Felix to Saturn Augustus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23600538.translation: Slaccius Messor gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-23600538.summary: This is a votive inscription by Slaccius Messor, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23600539.translation: Sacred to Augustus Saturn. Rogatus Caelestis, priest, gladly paid his vow with Iulia Victorina.
EDCS-23600539.summary: This is a votive inscription by a priest, Rogatus Caelestis, and Iulia Victorina to Saturn Augustus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23600540.translation: Sacred to Augustus Saturn. Sittius Se...
EDCS-23600540.summary: This is a partial votive inscription to Saturn Augustus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23600541.translation: Sacred to the Lord God Saturn. Vereius Potitus, priest.
EDCS-23600541.summary: This is an inscription by Vereius Potitus, a priest, to the Lord God Saturn, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23600542.translation: Caius Terebellius Paternus, priest, and his wife Titurnia Maximina, and his sons Nestor and Grania, gladly paid their vow.
EDCS-23600542.summary: This is a votive inscription by a priest, Caius Terebellius Paternus, his wife Titurnia Maximina, and sons Nestor and Grania, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23600543.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Saturn by an individual named Nestor.

EDCS-23600546.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Saturn, addressing him as 'Holy Lord'.

EDCS-23600547.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Saturn, referring to him as 'God Lord'.

EDCS-23600549.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Saturn, referring to him as 'August'.

EDCS-23600551.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Saturn, referring to him as 'August'.

EDCS-23600552.translation: Sacred to August Saturn; he paid his vow willingly.
EDCS-23600552.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Saturn and mentions the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-23600557.translation: Sacred to August Victory by Lucius C B.
EDCS-23600557.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deified Victory by Lucius C B.

EDCS-23600558.translation: Sacred to August Victory. Lucius Cassius Barbarus, son of Caius, quaestor, aedile, augur, promised a sum of money above the legal requirement for the honor of his augurship, and having amplified the funds, he placed this with a base and dedicated it.
EDCS-23600558.summary: This inscription records a dedication to August Victory by Lucius Cassius Barbarus, detailing his financial contributions for his augurship and dedicating the monument.

EDCS-23600559.translation: Sacred to August Victory. Lucius Claudius Brutto, augur, placed this at his own expense and dedicated it.
EDCS-23600559.summary: This inscription records a dedication to August Victory by Lucius Claudius Brutto, who paid for and dedicated the monument himself.

EDCS-23600562.translation: Sacred to the Parthian Victory, the Greatest, by decree of the decurions, at public expense.
EDCS-23600562.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the 'Parthian Victory, the Greatest,' funded by public money through a decree of the local council.

EDCS-23600564.translation: To the Victories of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, conqueror of Armenia, and Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, conqueror of Armenia and Parthia, by decree of the decurions, at public expense.
EDCS-23600564.summary: This is a public monument dedicated to the military victories of Emperors Marcus Aurelius and Lucius Verus, likely dating to their joint reign (161-169 CE).

EDCS-23600565.translation: Cornelius Iustinus, a man of honored memory, twice duovir, had promised one thousand denarii from his own funds for the divine virtue of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, father of the divine Commodus, conqueror of Sarmatia and Germania, father of the fatherland, prince of youth, for the honor of his augurship, above the legal sum. His son, Cornelius Pudentianus, erected this with the promised sum doubled, paid for with public funds.
EDCS-23600565.summary: This inscription records a man, Cornelius Iustinus, who doubled his promised donation for the augurship of Emperor Marcus Aurelius, with the monument erected by his son, likely dating to the reign of Marcus Aurelius (161-180 CE).

EDCS-23600566.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus, by decree of the decurions, at public expense.
EDCS-23600566.summary: This is a dedication to Mars Augustus, funded by the community, likely dating from the imperial period.

EDCS-23600567.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus, Claudia, daughter of Tiberius, erected at her own expense in her capacity as priestess.
EDCS-23600567.summary: This is a private dedication to Mars Augustus by Claudia, a priestess, funded by herself, likely dating from the imperial period.

EDCS-23600571.translation: Marcus Ulpius Firmus Olympianus, the haruspex, fulfilled and dedicated this vow for Egnatuleia Naevilla, with Ulpia Olympiana and Naevila, her daughters, overseeing.
EDCS-23600571.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by a haruspex, Marcus Ulpius Firmus Olympianus, on behalf of Egnatuleia Naevilla, with her daughters supervising, likely dating from the imperial period.

EDCS-23600573.translation: He fulfilled his vow to his son Domitius Calpurnianus willingly.
EDCS-23600573.summary: This is a personal votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to a son named Domitius Calpurnianus, likely dating from the imperial period.

EDCS-23600574.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Nerva, Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 11th time, consul for the 5th time, father of the fatherland, by decree of the decurions, at public expense.
EDCS-23600574.summary: This is a public monument dedicated to Emperor Trajan, commemorating his titles and powers, likely dating to 112 CE.

EDCS-23600578.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Trajan, conqueror of Parthia, grandson of the divine Nerva, Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, by decree of the decurions, at public expense.
EDCS-23600578.summary: This is a public monument dedicated to Emperor Hadrian, commemorating his titles and powers, likely dating to 119 CE.

EDCS-23600579.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Trajan, conqueror of Parthia, grandson of the divine Nerva, Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 6th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, by decree of the decurions, at public expense.
EDCS-23600579.summary: This is a public monument dedicated to Emperor Hadrian, commemorating his titles and powers, likely dating to 123 CE.

EDCS-23600583.translation: To Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, Caius Iulius Rogatus, the father, erected the statue that Marcus Iulius Rogatus had promised for the honor of the priesthood, above the legal contribution to the state, with the funds increased.
EDCS-23600583.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue of Emperor Antoninus Pius, promised by Marcus Iulius Rogatus for his priesthood and funded by his father Caius Iulius Rogatus, likely dating to the reign of Antoninus Pius (138-161 CE).

EDCS-23600584.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Caesar, son of Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland, with tribunician power, consul for the second time, from the will of Marcus Pompeius Veteris Flaviani, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, augur; Lucius Pompeius Novellus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, aedile twice, augur, master of augurs twice, brother, placed this with an added base and dedicated it after staging games.
EDCS-23600584.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Marcus Aurelius by Lucius Pompeius Novellus, likely erected around 161-166 CE, as a testament to Marcus Aurelius's consulship and his imperial status.

EDCS-23600587.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Caesar, son of Emperor Caesar Antoninus Hadrianus Augustus, father of the fatherland, with tribunician power for the second time, consul for the second time, proconsul, by decree of the town councilors, with public funds.
EDCS-23600587.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to Marcus Aurelius, likely erected by the town council of an unspecified location during his second consulship and tribunician power, around 161-166 CE.

EDCS-23600592.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, conqueror of Armenia, Medes, and Parthia, chief priest, with tribunician power for the twenty-third time, emperor for the fifth time, consul for the third time, father of the fatherland; Gaius Julius Crescens Didius Crescentianus, honored by the emperor with a public horse, perpetual priest of the four colonies of Cirta and Cuiculitan, pontiff, having fulfilled all honors in five colonies, placed this statue, which he promised from his generosity for 3,000 sesterces, with increased funds in the Basilica Julia, which he himself built from the ground up with his own money, and he himself dedicated it.
EDCS-23600592.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue and the Basilica Julia to Emperor Marcus Aurelius by Gaius Julius Crescens Didius Crescentianus, a prominent local official, around 191 CE.

EDCS-23600593.translation: To the deified Verus, brother of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, conqueror of Armenia, Medes, and Parthia, chief priest, with tribunician power for the twenty-third time, emperor for the fifth time, consul for the third time, father of the fatherland; Gaius Julius Crescens Didius Crescentianus, honored by the emperor with a public horse, perpetual priest of the four colonies of Cirta and Cuiculitan, pontiff, having fulfilled all honors in five colonies, placed this statue, which he promised from his generosity for 3,000 sesterces, with increased funds in the Basilica Julia, which he himself built from the ground up with his own money, and he himself dedicated it.
EDCS-23600593.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue and the Basilica Julia to the deified Lucius Verus, brother of Marcus Aurelius, by Gaius Julius Crescens Didius Crescentianus, around 169 CE (after Verus's death).

EDCS-23600595.translation: To Aesculapius Augustus, from the will of Caius Flavius Crescentis Novellianus; Flavius Novella, son and heir, placed this with increased funds, with Sextus Caecilius Julianus, perpetual priest, in charge.
EDCS-23600595.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Aesculapius Augustus made by Flavius Novella, fulfilling the will of Caius Flavius Crescentis Novellianus, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23600596.translation: Sacred to Aesculapius Augustus; Marcus Sosius Secundianus, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, aedile, prefect, acting as duumvir, placed this statue, which he had promised for the honor of his aedileship, beyond the legal requirement, for 4,000 sesterces, with increased funds, and dedicated it, with his son Marcus Sosius Cornelius in charge.
EDCS-23600596.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue to Aesculapius Augustus by Marcus Sosius Secundianus, who served as aedile and later as a duumvir, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23600597.translation: To Concord of the Augusti, Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Armenicus Medicus Parthicus Maximus, father of the fatherland, and Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Armenicus Medicus Parthicus Maximus, father of the fatherland; Lucius Gargilius Augustalis, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, aedile, placed this statue, which he had promised for the honor of his aedileship, beyond the legal requirement, for 4,000 sesterces, with increased funds, in his year and dedicated it, with Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus, clarissimos vir, legate of the Augusti pro praetore, patron of the colony, in charge.
EDCS-23600597.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue to the Concord of Emperors Marcus Aurelius and Lucius Verus by Lucius Gargilius Augustalis, an aedile, likely around 166 CE.

EDCS-23600600.translation: Sacred to Diana Providentia Augusta; Marcus Caelius Felix, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe.
EDCS-23600600.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Diana Providentia Augusta by Marcus Caelius Felix, likely from the Roman colony of Cuicul, in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23600601.translation: To Fortuna Augusta, to Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland, to Mars, to the Genius of the August colony; Gaius Julius Crescens, perpetual priest of the five colonies of Cirta and also Cuiculitana, who was the first and only one from his colony of Cuicul to hold this honor, ordered an arch, which from his will for 15,000 sesterces and additionally two statues of Fortuna and Mars, to be made; his grandson, Gaius Julius Crescens Didius Crescentianus, added a statue of Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland, and doubled the funds, made it and dedicated it, with Decimus Fonteius Frontinianus, clarissimos vir, legate of the Augusti pro praetore, patron of the colony, dedicating it.
EDCS-23600601.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an arch and statues by Gaius Julius Crescens and his grandson Gaius Julius Crescens Didius Crescentianus to various deities and the emperor Antoninus Pius, likely around 138-161 CE.

EDCS-23600607.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus; Titus Memmius Bellicus, son of Titus, of the Papiria tribe, and Alfia Quieta, his wife, placed this statue, which he had promised for the victory of our lord the Emperor for 220 sesterces, with increased funds, from the foundations, along with its base, and dedicated it, with Decimus Alfius Catullinus and Alfia Magna, daughter of Alfia, in charge.
EDCS-23600607.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue to Hercules Augustus by Titus Memmius Bellicus and his wife Alfia Quieta, celebrating an imperial victory, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23600608.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Gaius Julius Saturninus, priest of Liber Pater, made and dedicated this altar of quarried stone from the foundations at his own expense for 1000 sesterces, with his wife Iulia Getula and his sons Iulius Getuleius Apa and Saturninus the Younger supervising; he willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-23600608.summary: This is a private dedication of an altar to Hercules Augustus by a priest of Liber Pater, funded by the dedicator, in the second century CE.

EDCS-23600616.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Sergia Matrona lived 32 years. Cel Frumentius, an equestrian, made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-23600616.summary: This is a funerary dedication by an equestrian to his wife, dated to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23600617.translation: Gaius Julius Felix, formerly a decurion of a troop, and Gaius Julius Pulcher, a veteran, brothers most beloved, fulfilled their vow after their military service, with their wives Nonnia Iuliosa and Valeria Felicissima supervising.
EDCS-23600617.summary: This is a funerary dedication by two brothers and former soldiers who fulfilled a vow, likely to the Di Manes, with their wives supervising.

EDCS-23600618.translation: Calventius Felix, Octavia, Clau, Iulius Felix, Iulius Secundus, cacus, Perus, Gargilius Felix, Sattarus, Saturninus, Mros, Metilius Lupus, Felix.
EDCS-23600618.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly related to a funerary monument or a group of individuals, with unclear context or dedicatory formula.

EDCS-23600619.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Staberia Quarta, a weaver, lived 95 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Calpurnius Victor lived 85 years.
EDCS-23600619.summary: This inscription records the ages at death of two individuals, Staberia Quarta and Calpurnius Victor, under a funerary dedication to the Di Manes.

EDCS-23600620.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aedinius Valens lived 100 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Attia Lucia lived 95 years.
EDCS-23600620.summary: This inscription records the ages at death of two individuals, Publius Aedinius Valens and Attia Lucia, under a funerary dedication to the Di Manes.

EDCS-23600621.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Flavius Felix lies here; he lived 21 years.
EDCS-23600621.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death and age of Titus Flavius Felix, with a dedication to the Di Manes.

EDCS-23600622.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Flavius V NUS NACCID lived 60 years; he lies here.
EDCS-23600622.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death and age of Titus Flavius V NUS NACCID, with a dedication to the Di Manes.

EDCS-23600623.translation: The place of Caius Gargilius Victor.
EDCS-23600623.summary: This inscription likely marks a burial place or a property belonging to Caius Gargilius Victor.

EDCS-23600624.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Valeria Septima lived 40 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Hortensius Ianuarius lived 50 years.
EDCS-23600624.summary: This inscription records the ages at death of two individuals, Valeria Septima and Marcus Hortensius Ianuarius, under a funerary dedication to the Di Manes.

EDCS-23600625.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Iulius Lupus lived 70 years.
EDCS-23600625.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Publius Iulius Lupus.

EDCS-23600626.translation: Of Caius Iulius Saturninus Masulis, and Iulia Victorina his wife, and their sons Iulius [---].
EDCS-23600626.summary: A funerary dedication to Caius Iulius Saturninus Masulis, his wife, and their sons.

EDCS-23600627.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Malius Valens lived 60 years.
EDCS-23600627.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Lucius Malius Valens.

EDCS-23600628.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia Ianuaria lived 81 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Memmius Felix lived 103 years.
EDCS-23600628.summary: Two funerary epitaphs, each recording the name and age at death of a person.

EDCS-23600629.translation: Lived 40 years. Lucius Pineus Quadratus lived 21 years. Valeria Urbana lived [---]. He made this for his son, Lucius Pineus Quadratus, and Caecilia Gemina, and for himself and his descendants. Lived [---] years.
EDCS-23600629.summary: A funerary monument set up by Lucius Pineus Quadratus and Caecilia Gemina for their son, themselves, and their descendants, recording the ages at death of the son and Lucius Pineus Quadratus.

EDCS-23600631.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Sittius.
EDCS-23600631.summary: A funerary dedication to Publius Sittius.

EDCS-23600633.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Aburrius Secundus lived 60 years.
EDCS-23600633.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Gaius Aburrius Secundus.

EDCS-23600635.translation: Sacred to the Good Gods. Gaius C. Primus, priest of Saturn, [sacrificed] a lamb to the Lord, a bull to the Lord, a small sheep to Tellus, a ram to Iuppiter, a small sheep to the Nurse, a capon to Hercules, a young pig to Mercury, a young female pig to Venus, a ram to Testimony; a total of 9 animals.
EDCS-23600635.summary: A dedication of sacrifices by Gaius C. Primus, priest of Saturn, to various deities, including the Lord (possibly Sol or a deified emperor) and Testimony (possibly the gods in general or witnesses).

EDCS-23600636.translation: Sacred to the Good Gods. Gaius Aponius Secundus, priest, [sacrificed] a lamb to the Lord, a bull to the Lord, a small sheep to the Nurse, a ram to Iuppiter, a small sheep to Tellus, a lamb to Hercules, a young female pig to Venus, a young pig to Mercury, a ram to Testimony. Total: 1165 animals.
EDCS-23600636.summary: A dedication of sacrifices by Gaius Aponius Secundus, priest, to various deities, including the Lord (possibly Sol or a deified emperor) and Testimony (possibly the gods in general or witnesses), likely recording the year 1165 AUC.

EDCS-23600637.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Gaius O. F. paid his vow willingly.
EDCS-23600637.summary: A votive inscription recording Gaius O. F. fulfilling a vow to Mercury Augustus.

EDCS-23600638.translation: Sacred to Neptune Augustus. Caecilius Fortunatus, former quaestor and praetor, made this at his own expense.
EDCS-23600638.summary: This is a dedication to Neptune Augustus by Caecilius Fortunatus, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23600639.translation: Caius Hostilius Felix, priest of Saturn, made and dedicated this temple to the Augustan Nurse at his own expense.
EDCS-23600639.summary: This records the construction and dedication of a temple to the Augustan Nurse by Caius Hostilius Felix, priest of Saturn, probably in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23600640.translation: To Silvanus Silvestris Augustus, we fulfill our vow to you for the health of our lord the Emperor, as you commanded. Marcus Rubrius Felix and Caius Iulius Ianuarrius gladly offer this.
EDCS-23600640.summary: This is a votive offering to Silvanus Silvestris Augustus by Marcus Rubrius Felix and Caius Iulius Ianuarrius for the emperor's health, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23600641.translation: Marcus P. Silvanus paid the vow he promised.
EDCS-23600641.summary: This is a record of a vow being paid by Marcus P. Silvanus, possibly in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23600642.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 7th time, Imperator for the 11th time, Consul for the 2nd time, Proconsul, propagator of the empire, father of the fatherland, the bravest and most fortunate prince, father of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, Proconsul, father of the fatherland, the bravest prince, the state of the people of the Suburbium Colonorum, through Quintus Anicius Faustus, clarissimus vir, legate of the Augusti with proconsular power, consul.
EDCS-23600642.summary: This is a lengthy honorific inscription for Emperor Septimius Severus, likely from the early 3rd century CE, addressed by the province of the Suburbium Colonorum.

EDCS-23600643.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, the most indulgent prince.
EDCS-23600643.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Caracalla, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23600644.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Domitius Felix, priest of Saturn, lived.
EDCS-23600644.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the 'Dis Manibus' by Caius Domitius Felix, priest of Saturn, possibly in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23600645.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caius P. Felix, priest, lived 110 years, and Cornelia Victoria, his wife, lived 65 years. They are buried here.
EDCS-23600645.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the long lives of Caius P. Felix, a priest, and his wife Cornelia Victoria, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23600646.translation: Lucius Papirius Natalis Hibernalis, honored with military distinctions, restored the spring head of the Amsaga River, which had fallen into ruin from age and been obstructed from the bottom by constant torrents, from base to top with squared stone and new masonry, at a cost of 600,000 sesterces, from his own funds, out of love for his community, and repaired its cult; he also dedicated it.
EDCS-23600646.summary: This records the restoration and dedication of a spring head by Lucius Papirius Natalis Hibernalis, a local dignitary, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23600647.translation: Cio rinu H C Dio ni
EDCS-23600647.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription with illegible text, possibly names or titles, its purpose and date are uncertain.

EDCS-23600648.translation: Publius Aelius Fedosus made this in his lifetime; he lived 97 years.
EDCS-23600648.summary: A funerary inscription recording the self-funded monument of Publius Aelius Fedosus, who lived to be 97.

EDCS-23600649.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Aurelius Germanus, also called Victorinus, lived 14 years.
EDCS-23600649.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Aurelius Germanus (Victorinus), who died at the age of 14.

EDCS-23600650.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Aurelia Satura, his dearest daughter, who lived 1 year and 3 months.
EDCS-23600650.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelia Satura, who died at the age of 1 year and 3 months.

EDCS-23600651.translation: Titus C[...] Felix, a trained [---] accepting [---] was awarded [---], lived 21 years and 5 days.
EDCS-23600651.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus C. Felix, likely a soldier or public servant, who died at 21 years and 5 days.

EDCS-23600652.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Caelius Turanus lived 60 years and 5 months and 7 hours. His son, Numerius Caelius, made this.
EDCS-23600652.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Caelius Turanus, who lived 60 years, 5 months, and 7 hours, with the monument erected by his son.

EDCS-23600653.summary: A funerary inscription for Fortunata, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-23600654.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gargilius Firmus, son of [---]
EDCS-23600654.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription for Gargilius Firmus.

EDCS-23600655.translation: Ospitalis, son of [---], lived 55 years and is buried here.
EDCS-23600655.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Ospitalis, who lived to be 55.

EDCS-23600656.translation: For Caius Julius Saturus, who lived 90 years. His wife Iulia Modesta, loving and devoted, made this, [ensuring] she would not die before him.
EDCS-23600656.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Julius Saturus, who lived 90 years, erected by his wife Iulia Modesta with a poignant sentiment about their shared fate.

EDCS-23600657.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Germana lived 52 years.
EDCS-23600657.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Germana, who died at the age of 52.

EDCS-23600658.translation: Iulia Tecusa lived 35 good, cultivated years; Iulia Luciosa lived 53 years.
EDCS-23600658.summary: This inscription records the ages at death of two individuals, Iulia Tecusa and Iulia Luciosa.

EDCS-23600659.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iunia Galla lived 5 years and 9 days.
EDCS-23600659.summary: This funerary inscription records the age at death of Iunia Galla.

EDCS-23600660.translation: Memmius Aprilis lived 75 years. For the departed spirits. Valeria Stercula lived 65 years. AN AV (likely for 'annos vixit' or similar).
EDCS-23600660.summary: This inscription records the ages at death of Memmius Aprilis and Valeria Stercula, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-23600661.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudius Apa the innocent lived one year. Quintus Numisius lived 30 years, because son made this for father, father for son.
EDCS-23600661.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages at death of Claudius Apa and Quintus Numisius and highlights a reciprocal familial dedication.

EDCS-23600662.translation: Aurelia Honorata lived 10 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Orchivius Faustinus lived 50 years.
EDCS-23600662.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages at death of Aurelia Honorata and Caius Orchivius Faustinus.

EDCS-23600663.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Orchivius Namphamo lived 66 years and Varenia Marina his wife lived 60 years.
EDCS-23600663.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages at death of Caius Orchivius Namphamo and his wife Varenia Marina.

EDCS-23600664.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Orchivia Monnica lived 56 years. Caius Marinus, son to his most worthy mother.
EDCS-23600664.summary: This funerary inscription records the age at death of Orchivia Monnica and is a dedication from her son, Caius Marinus.

EDCS-23600665.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Orchivia Mustia lived 35 years.
EDCS-23600665.summary: This funerary inscription records the age at death of Orchivia Mustia.

EDCS-23600666.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Pomponius Baric lived 65 years.
EDCS-23600666.summary: This funerary inscription records the age at death of Caius Pomponius Baric.

EDCS-23600668.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription recording the name of Ius Victor, with the age missing.

EDCS-23600670.translation: Domitius Donatus lived for 120 years and died. Donatus Fatius made this table, which was more substantial, by placing it underneath; he did not come to those years (of age).
EDCS-23600670.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Domitius Donatus and noting the construction of a table by Donatus Fatius, likely a tomb marker.

EDCS-23600671.translation: The table of Maflasius, a citizen of Sacilium.
EDCS-23600671.summary: This inscription identifies a table belonging to Maflasius, a citizen from Sacilium, possibly a funerary monument or a public marker.

EDCS-23600672.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sextus Iulius Marius lived for 95 years.
EDCS-23600672.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicating the monument to the Divine Spirits and recording the lifespan of Sextus Iulius Marius.

EDCS-23600673.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulius Messius lived for 35 years.
EDCS-23600673.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicating the monument to the Divine Spirits and recording the lifespan of Iulius Messius.

EDCS-23600674.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Iulius Rusticus lived for 75 years.
EDCS-23600674.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicating the monument to the Divine Spirits and recording the lifespan of Lucius Iulius Rusticus.

EDCS-23600675.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caius Iulius VVV lived for 105 years.
EDCS-23600675.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicating the monument to the Divine Spirits and recording the lifespan of Caius Iulius VVV.

EDCS-23600676.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Festa lived for 75 years.
EDCS-23600676.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, sacred to the Divine Spirits, recording the lifespan of Iulia Festa.

EDCS-23600677.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulia Rogata lived for 75 years. She lies here.
EDCS-23600677.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicating the monument to the Divine Spirits, recording the lifespan of Iulia Rogata, and stating she is buried there.

EDCS-23600678.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Lesbius Quirina Quintus lived for 35 years.
EDCS-23600678.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicating the monument to the Divine Spirits and recording the lifespan of Lucius Lesbius Quirina Quintus.

EDCS-23600679.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of P. Ianuarius, likely a funerary epitaph.

EDCS-23600680.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Sextus.
EDCS-23600680.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sextus.

EDCS-23600682.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Nomia Lucciola lived 18 years.
EDCS-23600682.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nomia Lucciola, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-23600683.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Iulia Lilosa lived 80 years.
EDCS-23600683.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Lilosa, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-23600684.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Marcus Caecilius.
EDCS-23600684.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Caecilius.

EDCS-23600685.translation: In memory of Felicia, who lived 23 years. Iolus, her husband.
EDCS-23600685.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Felicia, commemorated by her husband Iolus, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-23600686.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Iulius Secundus.
EDCS-23600686.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulius Secundus.

EDCS-23600687.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fortunatus, who died at the age of 5.

EDCS-23600688.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nigrinus, who died at the age of 105.

EDCS-23600689.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Satria Saturnina lived 81 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600689.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Satria Saturnina, who died at the age of 81.

EDCS-23600692.translation: Trophimus fulfilled his vow willingly with his spirit.
EDCS-23600692.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Trophimus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-23600693.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, conqueror of Armenia and Parthia, greatest conqueror of Media, Pacuvius Crispinianus and Restitutus dedicated this.
EDCS-23600693.summary: This is a dedication by Pacuvius Crispinianus and Restitutus to the Roman Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, likely dating to his reign (161-180 CE).

EDCS-23600694.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aemilius Felix Iunior lived 33 years. Nisivius Macedo made this for his deserving brother.
EDCS-23600694.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Aemilius Felix Iunior, made by his brother Nisivius Macedo, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23600696.translation: Return of Arator on the 4th day before the Kalends of October. Return of Fortune on the 9th day before the Ides of October.
EDCS-23600696.summary: This inscription records specific dates for the 'return' or reappearance of two individuals or entities, Arator and Fortune.

EDCS-23600697.translation: Return of Badinis on the 8th day before the Ides of December.
EDCS-23600697.summary: This inscription records the date of the 'return' or reappearance of someone named Badinis.

EDCS-23600698.translation: Return of Belica on the Ides of July.
EDCS-23600698.summary: This inscription records the date of the 'return' or reappearance of someone named Belica.

EDCS-23600699.translation: Return of Cresconius on the Nones of October.
EDCS-23600699.summary: This inscription records the date of the 'return' or reappearance of someone named Cresconius.

EDCS-23600700.translation: Return of Dasonius on the 5th day before the Nones.
EDCS-23600700.summary: This inscription records the date of the 'return' or reappearance of someone named Dasonius.

EDCS-23600701.translation: Return of Dativa on the 3rd day before the Kalends of March.
EDCS-23600701.summary: This inscription records the date of the 'return' or reappearance of someone named Dativa.

EDCS-23600702.translation: Return of Diaucis on the day before the Ides of January.
EDCS-23600702.summary: This inscription records the date of the 'return' or reappearance of someone named Diaucis.

EDCS-23600704.translation: Return of Donatus on the Kalends of April.
EDCS-23600704.summary: This inscription records the date of the 'return' or reappearance of someone named Donatus.

EDCS-23600705.translation: The return of Fa on the fourth day before the Kalends of June.
EDCS-23600705.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Fa on a specific date.

EDCS-23600706.translation: The return of Faustina on the third day before the Kalends of June.
EDCS-23600706.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Faustina on a specific date.

EDCS-23600707.translation: The return of Felix on the sixth day before the Ides of August.
EDCS-23600707.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Felix on a specific date.

EDCS-23600708.translation: The return of Fidens on the Kalends of March.
EDCS-23600708.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Fidens on a specific date.

EDCS-23600709.translation: The return of R and the return of Donatus on the seventh day before the Kalends of December.
EDCS-23600709.summary: This inscription records the return of two individuals, R and Donatus, on a specific date.

EDCS-23600710.translation: The return of Leaurentei and the return of the gods on the fifth day before the Kalends of arias.
EDCS-23600710.summary: This inscription records the return of Leaurentei and the gods on a specific date, likely August.

EDCS-23600711.translation: The return of M on the seventh day before the Kalends.
EDCS-23600711.summary: This inscription records the return of an individual identified as M on a specific date, with the month missing.

EDCS-23600712.translation: The return of MARN in December.
EDCS-23600712.summary: This inscription records the return of an individual identified as MARN during the month of December.

EDCS-23600713.translation: The return of Migginis on the eleventh day before the Ides of May.
EDCS-23600713.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Migginis on a specific date.

EDCS-23600716.translation: The return of SaIIaris on the third day before the Kalends of March.
EDCS-23600716.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named SaIIaris on a specific date.

EDCS-23600717.translation: Return of Satur on the Ides of December.
EDCS-23600717.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Satur on a specific date.

EDCS-23600720.translation: Return of Urbanius on the Ides.
EDCS-23600720.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Urbanius on an unspecified Ides.

EDCS-23600723.translation: Return of Victors on the 6th day before the Ides of April.
EDCS-23600723.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Victors on April 7th.

EDCS-23600724.translation: Return of Victors on the 9th day before the Kalends of March.
EDCS-23600724.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Victors on February 21st.

EDCS-23600725.translation: Return of Victors on the 13th day before the Kalends of November.
EDCS-23600725.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Victors on October 20th.

EDCS-23600738.translation: Return of Praestans on the 1st day before the Ides of April.
EDCS-23600738.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Praestans on April 12th.

EDCS-23600740.translation: Return of Saturina on the 9th day before the Kalends of March.
EDCS-23600740.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Saturina on February 21st.

EDCS-23600741.translation: Return of Victors on the 2nd day before the Kalends of December.
EDCS-23600741.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Victors on November 30th.

EDCS-23600742.translation: Return of Victorias on the 3rd day before the Kalends of November.
EDCS-23600742.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Victorias on October 30th.

EDCS-23600747.translation: Return of Donatus on the 6th day before the Nones of July.
EDCS-23600747.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Donatus on July 2nd.

EDCS-23600754.translation: Return of Donatus, Nones of September.
EDCS-23600754.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Donatus on the Nones of September, likely related to a calendar or record-keeping practice.

EDCS-23600755.translation: Return of F., VI days before the Kalends.
EDCS-23600755.summary: This inscription records the return of a person identified by the initial 'F.' on the sixth day before the Kalends of an unspecified month, likely related to a calendar or record-keeping practice.

EDCS-23600756.translation: Return of Felicia, III days before the Ides of May.
EDCS-23600756.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Felicia on the third day before the Ides of May, likely related to a calendar or record-keeping practice.

EDCS-23600757.translation: Return of Iulianus, VI days before the Ides of Ju[nii?].
EDCS-23600757.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Iulianus on the sixth day before the Ides of June (or another month beginning with 'Ju'), likely related to a calendar or record-keeping practice.

EDCS-23600758.translation: Return of Misac, XVIII days before the Kalends of January.
EDCS-23600758.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Misac on the eighteenth day before the Kalends of January, likely related to a calendar or record-keeping practice.

EDCS-23600759.translation: Return of Prestans, IIII days before the Ides of August.
EDCS-23600759.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Prestans on the fourth day before the Ides of August, likely related to a calendar or record-keeping practice.

EDCS-23600760.translation: Return of Victor, VI days before the Kalends of November.
EDCS-23600760.summary: This inscription records the return of a person named Victor on the sixth day before the Kalends of November, likely related to a calendar or record-keeping practice.

EDCS-23600764.translation: In the name of the Lord God Jesus Christ, God and our Savior, and of our greatest benefactors, the most pious Emperor Heraclius, by divine command, in the twenty-sixth year and after his twenty-fifth consulship, and of Constantine, benefactor Emperor, and Heraclius, benefactor Caesar, in the twenty-fourth year of Constantine indeed, benefactor Emperor, and after his fifth consulship, and in the fifth year of Heraclius, benefactor Caesar, in the fifth year of the perpetual Emperors Augusti, on the sixth day before the Ides of March, the tenth indiction, in this venerable holy place have been deposited the relics of the holy martyrs, that is, of Saint Stephen, Saint Phocas, Saint Theodorus, Saint Victor, and Corona, by the hands of the most blessed bishops, that is, Leoncius, bishop of the holy church of Sestinum, Felix, bishop of the holy church of Centurihon, Benenatus, bishop of the holy church of C S F Mediana, and Januarius, of the holy church of Laifensis, while our lord and God-preserving Peter, patrician, rules from the province of Africa. For whose life let us all give thanks to God. I, Martius, a most devoted man, have written and subscribed.
EDCS-23600764.summary: This inscription, dating to the reign of Emperors Heraclius and Constantine (likely 7th century CE), records the deposition of relics of several saints in a holy place, listing the involved bishops and a high official, and is signed by a devoted man named Martius.

EDCS-23600765.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Fortunatus lived 5 years.
EDCS-23600765.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the underworld deities, commemorating Fortunatus who died at the age of five.

EDCS-23600766.translation: To the memory of Iulia Vitula.
EDCS-23600766.summary: This is a memorial inscription dedicated to the memory of a woman named Iulia Vitula.

EDCS-23600767.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Victoria lived 27 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600767.summary: A funerary epitaph for Victoria, recording her age at death.

EDCS-23600768.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Pacuvius Aprilis, his freedman, lived 70 years.
EDCS-23600768.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Pacuvius Aprilis, likely a freedman, recording his age at death.

EDCS-23600771.translation: Lucius Caecilius Saturninus lived 50 years, is buried here, a veteran, at his own expense.
EDCS-23600771.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Caecilius Saturninus, a veteran, recording his age and that he paid for his own burial.

EDCS-23600772.translation: To the memory of Iulius Maximus.
EDCS-23600772.summary: A dedication or memorial to Iulius Maximus.

EDCS-23600774.translation: Antonia, daughter of Lucius, established a village and market days on the 5th day before the Kalends and the 5th of the Ides of the month, and he [or she]...
EDCS-23600774.summary: A dedication by Antonia, daughter of Lucius, concerning the establishment of a village and market days, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-23600775.translation: Marcus Afinius Carpus, dutiful, lived 80 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23600775.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Afinius Carpus, recording his age at death and that he is buried in this location.

EDCS-23600776.translation: Lucius C. Felix Maiosi lived XX years; his mother made [this].
EDCS-23600776.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius C. Felix Maiosi, stating his age and that his mother erected the monument.

EDCS-23600777.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Eutyche, freedwoman of Tertinus Crassus, lived 14 years and 3 months. Crematus made this monument for himself while alive; he will not be followed by an heir.
EDCS-23600777.summary: A funerary epitaph for Eutyche, a freedwoman, stating her age and that Crematus made this monument for himself during his lifetime, indicating it is not meant for inheritance.

EDCS-23600778.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Datus lived 35 years.
EDCS-23600778.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Datus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-23600779.translation: To the memory of Gaius Iulius Donatus.
EDCS-23600779.summary: A dedication or memorial to Gaius Iulius Donatus.

EDCS-23600782.translation: The memorial of Gaia Iulia Kasta.
EDCS-23600782.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Gaia Iulia Kasta.

EDCS-23600783.translation: Secundus made this table over the new ditch.
EDCS-23600783.summary: This inscription records Secundus constructing a table over a new ditch.

EDCS-23600784.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Calgesia VA, pious, lived 65 years. Messius Metun lived 77 years.
EDCS-23600784.summary: This funerary inscription records the lives of Calgesia VA and Messius Metun, likely a dedication to the Manes.

EDCS-23600785.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating the tomb of Valerius Marinus.

EDCS-23600786.translation: The memorial of Cirillus, which his heirs made.
EDCS-23600786.summary: This inscription records that the heirs of Cirillus erected a memorial for him.

EDCS-23600788.translation: The memorial which M. Marius made for his most beloved son, son of Iader.
EDCS-23600788.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording M. Marius dedicating a memorial to his son.

EDCS-23600789.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Precilius Felix lived.
EDCS-23600789.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the Manes, records the life of Lucius Precilius Felix.

EDCS-23600792.summary: This inscription records a command given by Speratianus, identified as faithful.

EDCS-23600798.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Licinius Crispinus, son of Cai, lived 5 years. Maria lived 3 years.
EDCS-23600798.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the Manes, records the short lives of Marcus Licinius Crispinus and Maria.

EDCS-23600799.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Felicus lived 9 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23600799.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the Manes, records that Felicus lived 9 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-23600800.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius Martialis lived for 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23600800.summary: A funerary epitaph for Julius Martialis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600801.translation: Caius I Victor lived for 85 years.
EDCS-23600801.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius I Victor, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600802.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Magonius Saturninus lived for 85 years; he is buried here. His son made this for his father.
EDCS-23600802.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Magonius Saturninus, made by his son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600803.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maura lived for 55 years.
EDCS-23600803.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maura, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600804.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Mustius Certus lived 65 years.
EDCS-23600804.summary: A funerary epitaph for Mustius Certus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600805.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Trebius Iustinanus lived for 14 years. Lucius Trebius Florus made this for his deserving son; he is buried here.
EDCS-23600805.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Trebius Iustinanus, made by his father, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600806.translation: Table for Florentus, 16 days before the Kalends of February.
EDCS-23600806.summary: An inscription recording a payment or dedication for someone named Florentus, possibly related to a meal or a table, with a specific date.

EDCS-23600812.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Acilia Amanta made this for her most rare husband; she is buried here.
EDCS-23600812.summary: A funerary epitaph for Acilia Amanta's husband, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600814.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ceius Optatus lived 90 years.
EDCS-23600814.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ceius Optatus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600815.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia Monula lived for 30 years.
EDCS-23600815.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Monula, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600816.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Gaius Bombius gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-23600816.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mercury Augustus by Gaius Bombius.

EDCS-23600817.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Iulius Martiallis lived for 60 years.
EDCS-23600817.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Gaius Iulius Martiallis.

EDCS-23600818.translation: Aprilis lived for 75 years. Valeria Secundia lived for 65 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed.
EDCS-23600818.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespans of Aprilis and Valeria Secundia.

EDCS-23600819.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Alia Munatia lived for 35 years.
EDCS-23600819.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Alia Munatia.

EDCS-23600820.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Arius Felix lived for 18 years.
EDCS-23600820.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Quintus Arius Felix.

EDCS-23600821.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. The remains of Ari Rusticus, who lived for 18 years, may rest well here.
EDCS-23600821.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ari Rusticus, who lived 18 years, with a wish for his remains.

EDCS-23600822.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Caecilius Pastor lived for 48 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23600822.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial place of Gaius Caecilius Pastor.

EDCS-23600823.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caelius Faustus lived for 80 years. Solemnis and Saturninus made this for their pious father. He is buried here. May your remains rest well.
EDCS-23600823.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Solemnis and Saturninus for their father, Caelius Faustus, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-23600824.translation: Quintus Iulius Crescens lived for 65 years. Aufidia Fructa, his wife, lived for 55 years. Their son, Quintus Iulius Crispinianus, made this for his parents. Their remains are buried here.
EDCS-23600824.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Quintus Iulius Crispinianus for his parents, Quintus Iulius Crescens and Aufidia Fructa, recording their lifespans and burial.

EDCS-23600825.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Primus lived for about 65 years.
EDCS-23600825.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the approximate lifespan of Iulius Primus.

EDCS-23600829.translation: By the authority of the Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Nerva, Nerva Traianus Optimus Augustus, Germanicus, Dacicus, Parthicus, lands were assigned to the people of the Suburburi by Titus Sabinius Barbarus, imperial legate with praetorian authority.
EDCS-23600829.summary: This inscription records the official assignment of land by Emperor Trajan to the Suburburi people, as proclaimed by his legate.

EDCS-23600832.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Luciosa lived 85 years. May it be well with you.
EDCS-23600832.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Iulia Luciosa.

EDCS-23600833.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. MAEA Masauna, chieftain of the A tribe, lived 85 years. Herem milum.
EDCS-23600833.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of MAEA Masauna, a tribal chieftain.

EDCS-23600837.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ferias Ialfinis lived 27 years.
EDCS-23600837.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Ferias Ialfinis.

EDCS-23600838.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Honoratus Nimmire lived 21 years.
EDCS-23600838.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Honoratus Nimmire.

EDCS-23600839.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. A Pudentianus Gennunis lived 100 years, Tiftene, his wife, lived 60 years, and Germanus, his son, lived [---] years.
EDCS-23600839.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespans of A Pudentianus, his wife Tiftene, and their son Germanus.

EDCS-23600840.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Iugarta.

EDCS-23600841.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iusmarta made this for herself and for her dear wife, who lived 37 years.
EDCS-23600841.summary: This funerary inscription records that Iusmarta dedicated it for herself and her wife, whose age is given.

EDCS-23600842.translation: Artinio lived eighty years. Octavia placed this.
EDCS-23600842.summary: This funerary inscription records that Octavia dedicated a monument for Artinio, who lived eighty years.

EDCS-23600843.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Silbentus lived 105 years and is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Cata lived 75 years and is buried here.
EDCS-23600843.summary: This inscription is a double funerary monument recording the lifespans and burial of Silbentus and Aurelia Cata.

EDCS-23600844.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Here lie innocent Cretia Ania and Honoratus with his sister.
EDCS-23600844.summary: A funerary inscription for Cretia Ania and Honoratus, dated by the mention of their familial relationship.

EDCS-23600845.summary: A funerary inscription for Donatus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-23600846.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Saturninus, who lived 62 years.
EDCS-23600846.summary: A funerary inscription for Saturninus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-23600847.translation: Monument of Titus Flavius [Quirina?]. Lived 80 years.
EDCS-23600847.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Flavius, recording his age at death and identifying it as a monument.

EDCS-23600848.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Safidia Rogatiana, who lived 18 months. Safidius made this for his most beloved daughter.
EDCS-23600848.summary: A funerary inscription for Safidia Rogatiana, set up by her father Safidius, recording her age at death.

EDCS-23600849.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Titus Aurelius lies here.
EDCS-23600849.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Aurelius.

EDCS-23600850.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Feda, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-23600850.summary: A funerary inscription for Aurelia Feda, recording her age at death.

EDCS-23600851.translation: [Name illegible]. Lived 66 years. His sons made this for him, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-23600851.summary: A funerary inscription for a man whose name is lost, set up by his sons, recording his age at death.

EDCS-23600852.translation: Taltius Masulis made and dedicated this monument for himself and his family, especially for his most rare wife Mantisua, on the 11th day before the Kalends of October, during the consulship of the two Aspri.
EDCS-23600852.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Taltius Masulis for himself, his family, and his wife Mantisua, dated by the consuls of 38 BC.

EDCS-23600853.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavia Seve[ra?].
EDCS-23600853.summary: A funerary inscription for Flavia Seve[ra?].

EDCS-23600855.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aurelius Maximus to his incomparable brother, Marcus Aurelius Honoratianus Concessus, son of Subur, a town councilor of the Tutcenses, defender of the tribe, a brave and most faithful man, who lived 53 years.
EDCS-23600855.summary: This funerary monument, dedicated by Aurelius Maximus to his brother, honors Marcus Aurelius Honoratianus, a local official, and records his age at death.

EDCS-23600856.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Antonia Sabina lived 50 years.
EDCS-23600856.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Antonia Sabina, noting she lived for 50 years.

EDCS-23600857.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Fabia Gudulla lived 85 years, buried here.
EDCS-23600857.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Fabia Gudulla, who lived to be 85 years old.

EDCS-23600858.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Julia Quintosa, a most rare woman, lived 10 years, taken from her peers in such bloom, buried here.
EDCS-23600858.summary: This funerary monument honors Julia Quintosa, a young girl who died at the age of 10, emphasizing her rarity and promising afterlife.

EDCS-23600859.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Julia Urbana lived 105 years, buried here; may her bones rest well.
EDCS-23600859.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Julia Urbana, who lived an exceptionally long life of 105 years, and wishes her peaceful rest.

EDCS-23600861.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lollia Amiperina lived 60 years and 7 hours.
EDCS-23600861.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Lollia Amiperina, specifying her lifespan as 60 years and 7 hours.

EDCS-23600863.translation: Hail! Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Omuno, son of Ogni, lived 10 years.
EDCS-23600863.summary: This funerary inscription honors Omuno, son of Ogni, noting that he died at the age of 10.

EDCS-23600864.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Clarus, son of Celsus, lived 11 years.
EDCS-23600864.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Clarus, son of Celsus, who died at the age of 11.

EDCS-23600865.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Aurelius M. II lived 35 years, buried here.
EDCS-23600865.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Aurelius M. II, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-23600866.translation: Sacred to the Genius of the Auburutenses, Augustus, for the well-being of our prefect. Marcus Cornelius Martis CAS D EM.
EDCS-23600866.summary: This votive inscription is dedicated to the Genius of the local people and the Emperor Augustus for the health of their prefect, Marcus Cornelius Martis.

EDCS-23600867.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest of Parthia, greatest of Britain, greatest of Germany, chief priest, in his 18th year of tribunician power, acclaimed imperator 3 times, consul 4 times, father of the country, proconsul, and of Julia Augusta Pia Felix, mother of the emperor, of the camp, of the senate, and of the country, the public community of the fortress of Subzua.
EDCS-23600867.summary: This is a dedication by the community of the fortress of Subzua for the safety of Emperor Caracalla and Julia Domna.

EDCS-23600869.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Gaius Antius Victor.

EDCS-23600870.translation: Antonia Salsa lived 9 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600870.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Antonia Salsa.

EDCS-23600871.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Arrius Aprilis lived 91 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23600871.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial place of Gaius Arrius Aprilis.
