EDCS-23600872.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Caelius Ianuarius, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 66 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600872.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial place of Quintus Caelius Ianuarius.

EDCS-23600873.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription recording the name Cornelia Luctosa.

EDCS-23600874.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Marcus F. Fortunatus.

EDCS-23600875.translation: Felicitas, daughter of Lucius, who lived 25 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600875.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Felicitas.

EDCS-23600877.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavius, son of Flavius, of the Quirina tribe, Flavianus.
EDCS-23600877.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name Flavius Flavianus, son of Flavius, and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-23600878.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Modestus lived 51 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600878.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Titus Flavius Modestus.

EDCS-23600879.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Flavius Rufianus lived 100 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600879.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Flavius Rufianus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600880.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Galerius Ianuarius, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 45 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600880.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Galerius Ianuarius, identifying his father and tribal affiliation, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600881.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Galerius Nampulus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 15 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600881.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Galerius Nampulus, identifying his father and tribal affiliation, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600882.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Gellius lived 105 years. Sittia Seneciosa made this.
EDCS-23600882.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Gellius, with Sittia Seneciosa identified as the maker, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600883.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Coricius, possibly a child or indicating his age at death, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600884.translation: Iulia Marcela lived. Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Iulius Rufinus lived 85 years.
EDCS-23600884.summary: A funerary epitaph mentioning both Iulia Marcela and Quintus Iulius Rufinus, likely a couple or family members, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600885.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Iulius Saturus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600886.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Mustia lived 103 years; she rests here.
EDCS-23600886.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Mustia, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600887.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Postuma, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600888.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Urbanila lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23600888.summary: A funerary epitaph for Urbanila, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600890.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Urbanaa lived 80 years, daughter, wife, [---].
EDCS-23600890.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Urbanaa, stating her age and familial relationships.

EDCS-23600891.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Macasatus, 35 [years old].
EDCS-23600891.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Macasatus, stating his age.

EDCS-23600892.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Marcius Severus lived 61 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600892.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Marcius Severus, stating his age and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23600893.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Musosus MATVS, stating his age.

EDCS-23600894.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Quintus Mustius, stating his age.

EDCS-23600895.translation: Lived 51 years. Nevia Iulia made this tomb for her sister because her brother Ambibolus was negligent and did not want to make it.
EDCS-23600895.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of an unnamed woman, stating her age and that her sister erected the tomb due to their brother's neglect.

EDCS-23600896.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcia Ovia Severa, most virtuous wife. Caius Flavius Rufus erected this tomb for his wife; she lived 30 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600896.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcia Ovia Severa, erected by her husband Caius Flavius Rufus, stating her age and praising her virtue.

EDCS-23600897.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Pomponius Sittius Messor lived 90 years; he rests here. Caius Pompulus Rufus lived 11 years.
EDCS-23600897.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of two individuals, Pomponius Sittius Messor and Caius Pompulus Rufus, stating their ages.

EDCS-23600899.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Publius Sittius Consortius lived 80 years.
EDCS-23600899.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Publius Sittius Consortius, stating his age.

EDCS-23600900.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sitia Paulina lived 35 years.
EDCS-23600900.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Sitia Paulina, stating her age.

EDCS-23600901.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Sosius Maritimianus lived 93 years. Sosius Caecilianus placed this for his most precious father. He lies here.
EDCS-23600901.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Sosius Maritimianus at the age of 93 and was erected by his son, Sosius Caecilianus.

EDCS-23600903.translation: Quintus Volusius Marsus lived 70 years. He lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600903.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Volusius Marsus, who lived to 70 years of age.

EDCS-23600909.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Clodius.
EDCS-23600909.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription for Marcus Clodius.

EDCS-23600910.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Asinia lived 75 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600910.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulia Asinia at the age of 75.

EDCS-23600912.translation: Aulus Gaius Quartus, son of Lucius, a cavalryman of the Veterana ala serving at sesquiplicarius rank, lived 40 years and served 20 years. He lies here.
EDCS-23600912.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Gaius Quartus, a cavalryman in the Veterana ala who died at 40 after 20 years of service.

EDCS-23600914.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Arrius Restutus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 30 years. He lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600914.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Caius Arrius Restutus at the age of 30.

EDCS-23600915.translation: Sextus Arrius Restutus lived 115 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld.
EDCS-23600915.summary: This funerary inscription honors Sextus Arrius Restutus, who lived to the remarkable age of 115.

EDCS-23600916.translation: Marcus Arrius Urbanus lived 55 years. He lies here.
EDCS-23600916.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Arrius Urbanus, who lived to 55 years of age.

EDCS-23600917.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Cominia Honorata lived 75 years. She lived well.
EDCS-23600917.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Cominia Honorata at the age of 75.

EDCS-23600918.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Helvidia Quarta lived 81 years.
EDCS-23600918.summary: This funerary inscription notes that Helvidia Quarta lived for 81 years.

EDCS-23600919.translation: Marcela, daughter of Lucius Lucius, lived 132 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23600919.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Marcela.

EDCS-23600920.translation: Gaius Mundicus Urbanus lived 30 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600920.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Gaius Mundicus Urbanus.

EDCS-23600921.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Publilius Fabullus lived 51 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600921.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Marcus Publilius Fabullus and offers a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23600922.translation: Lucius Sittius Heremon, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, a veteran, lived 85 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600922.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Lucius Sittius Heremon, identifying him as a veteran.

EDCS-23600928.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Rufus lived [X] years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23600928.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Iulius Rufus, with his age lost.

EDCS-23600929.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Aberrinus Conductor lived 31 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23600929.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Lucius Aberrinus Conductor.

EDCS-23600930.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Arruntia Optata lived 61 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600930.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Arruntia Optata and offers a wish for her repose.

EDCS-23600931.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Avillia SextullIa.
EDCS-23600931.summary: This very brief funerary inscription is dedicated to Avillia SextullIa.

EDCS-23600932.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Avillia Vitalica lived 70 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23600932.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Avillia Vitalica.

EDCS-23600933.translation: C. Contentus lived 85 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600933.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of C. Contentus and offers a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23600934.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Caecilia Quinta lived 105 years. May your bones rest well, Amessocitana F.
EDCS-23600934.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caecilia Quinta, recording her lifespan and a wish for her repose.

EDCS-23600935.translation: Caninia Nuptialis, daughter of Caius, lived 65 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23600935.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caninia Nuptialis, recording her lifespan and burial location.

EDCS-23600936.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Clodius Vitalis, son of Clodius, lived 86 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600936.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clodius Vitalis, recording his lifespan and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23600938.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Curiatius Mus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-23600940.translation: Daughter Extricata lived 7 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23600940.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Extricata, recording her lifespan and burial location.

EDCS-23600941.translation: Lucius Fabius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, Fus[---].
EDCS-23600941.summary: This is a funerary epitaph fragment for Lucius Fabius, likely recording his name, filiation, and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-23600942.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Fabia Lucia lived 110 years. She is buried here. May you rest well.
EDCS-23600942.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabia Lucia, recording her lifespan, burial location, and a wish for her repose.

EDCS-23600945.translation: Irea Pusinica, daughter of Marcus, lived 20 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600945.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Irea Pusinica, recording her lifespan, burial location, and a wish for her repose.

EDCS-23600946.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulius Honoratus lived 20 years.
EDCS-23600946.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Honoratus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-23600948.translation: Iulius Ianuarius lived 71 years. He is buried here. May he rest well.
EDCS-23600948.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Ianuarius, recording his lifespan, burial location, and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23600949.translation: Gaius Julius Urbanus lived 55 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600949.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gaius Julius Urbanus.

EDCS-23600965.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Restuta lived 25 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600965.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Iulia Restuta, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-23600966.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Laeta.

EDCS-23600967.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gargilius, a mother, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-23600968.translation: Publius Messor lived 45 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23600968.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Publius Messor.

EDCS-23600969.translation: Musonius lived 41 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600969.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Musonius.

EDCS-23600970.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Fonteius Vitalus lived 55 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600970.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Fonteius Vitalus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-23600972.translation: Lucius Satrius Severus lived 70 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23600972.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Lucius Satrius Severus.

EDCS-23600973.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Sittius Massuetus lived 35 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600973.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Marcus Sittius Massuetus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-23600974.translation: Marcus Sittius Messor, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 45 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23600974.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Marcus Sittius Messor, son of Caius.

EDCS-23600975.translation: Turrania Exorata, daughter of Caius, lived 45 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23600975.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Turrania Exorata, who lived 45 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600976.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Vigellia Mania lived 19 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600976.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vigellia Mania, who lived 19 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600982.translation: Italis lived 26 years; he is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23600982.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Italis, who lived 26 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23600985.translation: Marcus, son of Aelius, of the Quirina tribe, Hos AV, is buried here.
EDCS-23600985.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Aelius, of the Quirina tribe, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23601002.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius Cornelius Hospes lived 45 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23601002.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Cornelius Hospes, who lived 45 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23601003.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius Flavius Rufus placed this tomb for his most rare wife, Novia Severila, most innocent; she lived 40 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23601003.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Novia Severila, placed by her husband Gaius Flavius Rufus, who lived 40 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23601004.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Antonia Pusinica lived 70 years.
EDCS-23601004.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Pusinica, who lived 70 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23601005.translation: From MVS, of bishop Adeodatus, AD, from the workshop of Urbanus and Calendarius, of the presbyter Probus.
EDCS-23601005.summary: This inscription records the work of artisans from the workshop of Urbanus and Calendarius, associated with ecclesiastical figures, likely dating to the Late Roman or Early Byzantine period.

EDCS-23601007.translation: The state of the Saddaritans, by decree of the council and public expense, made this for the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Chief Priest, Tribune of the Potestas for the 16th time, Consul for the 2nd time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, son of the deified Severus Pius, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva.
EDCS-23601007.summary: This is a public dedication by the people of Saddar to Emperor Caracalla, listing his extensive titles and ancestry, dating to his reign (211-217 AD).

EDCS-23601009.translation: Sextus Fabius Valens, veteran, lived 55 years; he is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23601009.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Fabius Valens, a veteran, who lived 55 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23601010.summary: This inscription is a tombstone naming Lucius Gondadius, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-23601011.translation: Firmidia Impetrata, daughter of Lucius, the high priestess, lived 100 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23601011.summary: This tombstone records the death and burial of Firmidia Impetrata, identified as a high priestess, and specifies her lifespan.

EDCS-23601012.summary: This inscription is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Poia, a priestess.

EDCS-23601013.translation: Africanus, [son of ...], lived 101 years. May your bones rest well. He is buried here.
EDCS-23601013.summary: This tombstone records the death and burial of Africanus, specifying his age and wishing him a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-23601015.translation: Avianila Quirina, daughter of Lucius, [inscription text incomplete].
EDCS-23601015.summary: This inscription names Avianila Quirina, daughter of Lucius, but the text is incomplete and its purpose is unclear.

EDCS-23601016.translation: Elpis lived 90 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23601016.summary: This tombstone records the lifespan of Elpis and includes a common funerary wish for rest.

EDCS-23601017.translation: Lucius Caecilius Pacatus lived 30 years. He is buried here. May you rest well here.
EDCS-23601017.summary: This tombstone records the death and burial of Lucius Caecilius Pacatus, stating his age and including a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23601018.translation: Caecilia Marcia, daughter of Lucius, lived 45 years. She is buried here. May you rest well here.
EDCS-23601018.summary: This tombstone records the death and burial of Caecilia Marcia, daughter of Lucius, specifying her age and a wish for her eternal rest.

EDCS-23601019.translation: Lucius Caelius of Quirina, from Naples, lived [incomplete duration]. May you rest well here.
EDCS-23601019.summary: This tombstone names Lucius Caelius of Quirina, from Naples, but the inscription is incomplete regarding his age.

EDCS-23601020.summary: This inscription names Claudius but is incomplete, lacking details about his filiation, lifespan, or purpose.

EDCS-23601021.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Clasias Secundus lived 120 years.
EDCS-23601021.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Clasias Secundus.

EDCS-23601022.translation: Gaius Clodius Severus lived 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23601022.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Gaius Clodius Severus.

EDCS-23601023.translation: Quintus Cominius Quintillus lived 125 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23601023.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Quintus Cominius Quintillus and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23601024.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Cornelius Mustius lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23601024.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Cornelius Mustius.

EDCS-23601025.translation: Lucius Cornelius Mustiolus lived 58 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23601025.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Cornelius Mustiolus and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23601026.translation: Cornelia Clara, daughter of Quintus, from Anucula, lived 32 years; she is buried here. Cambia Cambilla, daughter of Caius, placed this for her mother.
EDCS-23601026.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Cornelia Clara, her parentage, origin, and the fact that her daughter Cambilla erected it.

EDCS-23601027.translation: Cornelia Galla, daughter of Rusticus, daughter of Cornelius, lived 37 years; may you rest well here.
EDCS-23601027.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and parentage of Cornelia Galla.

EDCS-23601028.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Didius Apronianus lived 50 years.
EDCS-23601028.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Didius Apronianus.

EDCS-23601029.translation: Fabius Saturnus lived 51 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23601029.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Fabius Saturnus and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23601030.translation: Fabia Flavila lived 6 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23601030.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Fabia Flavila and a wish for her repose.

EDCS-23601031.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Farsuleia, daughter of Lucius, a dutiful father, who lived 29 years; she herself lived 75 years.
EDCS-23601031.summary: A funerary epitaph for Farsuleia, likely recording her lifespan and perhaps that of her father.

EDCS-23601032.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Galerius Namphamus made this for his most exceptional father.
EDCS-23601032.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Caius Galerius Namphamus for his father.

EDCS-23601033.translation: Germanus, son of B. Sca[r.], is buried here. He lived for [---] years.
EDCS-23601033.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial and lifespan of Germanus.

EDCS-23601034.translation: Honoratus, son of Quintus, of the tribe Quirina, from Nampulus. He lived 50 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-23601034.summary: A funerary epitaph for Honoratus, son of Quintus, noting his age and wishing him rest.

EDCS-23601035.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the short lifespan of Caius Julius Andianus.

EDCS-23601036.translation: Lucius Julius Crescens lived 29 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23601036.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial and lifespan of Lucius Julius Crescens.

EDCS-23601037.translation: Caius Julius F... lived 50 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23601037.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Caius Julius F... and wishing him rest.

EDCS-23601038.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the short lifespan of Caius Julius Martialis.

EDCS-23601039.translation: Caius Julius Mellarius lived 85 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23601039.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the long lifespan of Caius Julius Mellarius and wishing him rest.

EDCS-23601040.translation: Lucius Julius Musicus lived 55 years. He is buried here. May you rest well here.
EDCS-23601040.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial and lifespan of Lucius Julius Musicus, with a wish for his rest.

EDCS-23601041.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Iulius Quintosus, son of Marci(us), of the voting tribe Quirina, lived 70 years.
EDCS-23601041.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Quintus Iulius Quintosus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23601042.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Iulius Siripo lived 31 years.
EDCS-23601042.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Quintus Iulius Siripo, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23601043.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Namfamilla lived 110 years.
EDCS-23601043.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Iulia Namfamilla, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23601044.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Paulina, daughter of Quinti(us), lived 75 years.
EDCS-23601044.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Iulia Paulina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23601045.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Rogata lived 50 years; may her bones rest well here.
EDCS-23601045.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Iulia Rogata and a wish for her peaceful rest, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23601046.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Sorora lived 55 years.
EDCS-23601046.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Iulia Sorora, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23601048.translation: Caius Iunius Datus lived 100 years; he lies here.
EDCS-23601048.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Caius Iunius Datus and his burial place, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23601049.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Latinius Hospitalis lived 70 years; he lies here.
EDCS-23601049.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Publius Latinius Hospitalis and his burial place, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23601050.translation: Lucretia Fidelis, freedwoman of Marci(us), lived 70 years; she lies here.
EDCS-23601050.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucretia Fidelis, a freedwoman, and her burial place, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23601051.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Manilius Victor lived 75 years.
EDCS-23601051.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Quintus Manilius Victor, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23601052.translation: Marcia Dapali lived 8 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23601052.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcia Dapali, recording her age at death.

EDCS-23601053.translation: Maximus lived 25 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23601053.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maximus, recording his age at death and a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-23601054.translation: NampamilIla, daughter of Ianuarius, lived 100 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23601054.summary: A funerary epitaph for NampamilIla, daughter of Ianuarius, recording her age at death.

EDCS-23601055.translation: Sextus Porcius Restitus, son of Sextus, lived [---] years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23601055.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextus Porcius Restitus, son of Sextus, recording his death and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23601056.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Marcus Publi us Fab lived 30 years.
EDCS-23601056.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the underworld gods for Marcus Publi us Fab, stating his age at death.

EDCS-23601057.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Publilia Potta, daughter of Rogatus, lived 45 years.
EDCS-23601057.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the underworld gods for Publilia Potta, daughter of Rogatus, stating her age at death.

EDCS-23601058.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Marcus Septimius Etruscus lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23601058.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the underworld gods for Marcus Septimius Etruscus, stating his age at death and burial location.

EDCS-23601059.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Caius Septimius Quadratus lived 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23601059.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the underworld gods for Caius Septimius Quadratus, stating his age at death and burial location.

EDCS-23601060.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Caius Septimius Successus lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23601060.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the underworld gods for Caius Septimius Successus, stating his age at death and burial location.

EDCS-23601061.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed: Caius Sittius Aemilianus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 8 years; he is buried here. May he rest well.
EDCS-23601061.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the underworld gods for Caius Sittius Aemilianus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, stating his age at death, burial location, and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23601062.translation: Chest of Caius Sittius, son of Sittianus.
EDCS-23601062.summary: This is a funerary chest inscription for Caius Sittius, son of Sittianus.

EDCS-23601063.translation: Sittia Lucilia, daughter of Lucius, lived for some years; she is buried here. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-23601063.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Lucilia, daughter of Lucius, stating her place of burial and offering a wish for her rest.

EDCS-23601064.translation: Sittia Lucula, daughter of Lucius, lived for 20 years; she is buried here. May she rest well.
EDCS-23601064.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Lucula, daughter of Lucius, stating her age at death and her burial place.

EDCS-23601065.translation: Sitia Rogata lived for 101 years. To the divine Manes.
EDCS-23601065.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sitia Rogata, recording her long life and dedicating it to the divine Manes.

EDCS-23601066.translation: Lucius Volusius Quietus lived for 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23601066.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Volusius Quietus, stating his age at death and his place of burial.

EDCS-23601067.translation: To the divine Manes, Sitius Honoratus, of the CAMV family.
EDCS-23601067.summary: This funerary inscription dedicates the burial to the divine Manes for Sitius Honoratus.

EDCS-23601068.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the age of Lucia.

EDCS-23700001.translation: Sacred to the Genius of the Elephant Castle. Claudia Donata, wife of Propertius Crescentis, established a statue of the Genius of the fatherland Elephant Castle, with its base, which she had promised from her own liberality for the adornment of the castle, with her own money, for 8,000 sesterces; for its dedication, she gave and dedicated single denarii for everyone and wine through the colleges for feasting.
EDCS-23700001.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue and associated expenses by Claudia Donata to the Genius of the Elephant Castle, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23700002.translation: Aulus Vibius Nivalis, son of Quirinus, of the ACIA tribe, paid his vow willingly with a good heart to the Genius of the Qvinas Vost Lucretiani district.
EDCS-23700002.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aulus Vibius Nivalis to the Genius of a district named Qvinas Vost Lucretiani.

EDCS-23700003.translation: Caius Nonius Hospes built the temple of Mercury and paid his vow willingly with a good heart.
EDCS-23700003.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple to Mercury by Caius Nonius Hospes and the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-23700004.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Augustus, the Preserver of the Emperors, by order of the lord Saturn. Marcus Sittius Fortunatus, son of Rogatus, grandson of Novellius, made this with his own money, fulfilling his vow gladly.
EDCS-23700004.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Marcus Sittius Fortunatus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-23700006.translation: Publius Sittius Rogatus, son of Novellius, gladly fulfilled his vow and built this from the ground up with the furnishings, and dedicated it himself.
EDCS-23700006.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the construction and dedication of a building by Publius Sittius Rogatus.

EDCS-23700008.translation: Quintus Iulicus, son of Sextus, gladly fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-23700144.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sittia Calditana lived for 85 years; she is buried here.

EDCS-23700147.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sittia Mustia lived for 85 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23700147.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Sittia Mustia.

EDCS-23700174.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vibius Lucretianus lived 25 years.
EDCS-23700174.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Vibius Lucretianus.

EDCS-23700175.translation: Quirina Victor lived [---] years; he is buried here.

EDCS-23700176.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Ummidius Aptichus Nepos lived 15 years; he is buried here.

EDCS-23700177.translation: [---] lived 85 years; he is buried here.

EDCS-23700178.translation: [---] lived 25 years; she is buried here.

EDCS-23700180.translation: [---] lived 10 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23700180.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial and lifespan of an unnamed child.

EDCS-23700181.translation: On the third day before the Ides of June, the deposition of the blood of holy martyrs who suffered under the governor Florus in the city of Milevum, on the day of the incense offering, among whom Innocentius is buried here, he himself being in peace.
EDCS-23700181.summary: This inscription records the martyrdom and burial of Innocentius in Milevum on a specific date during the persecution under governor Florus.

EDCS-23700182.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caninia Iucunda lived 62 years. To the Divine Shades. Pissiii Asutus lived 60 years.
EDCS-23700182.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lives of two individuals, Caninia Iucunda and Pissiii Asutus, likely a married couple or family members, with their ages at death.

EDCS-23700184.translation: To the Divine Shades. Tiberius Iulius Falco Acutus lived 35 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700184.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Tiberius Iulius Falco Acutus, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-23700185.translation: To the Divine Shades. Tiberius Iulius Messor lived 25 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23700185.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Tiberius Iulius Messor, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-23700186.translation: To the Divine Shades. Tiberius Iulius Sedatus lived 60 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23700186.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Tiberius Iulius Sedatus, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-23700187.translation: To the Divine Shades. Tiberius Iulius Stephanus lived 105 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700187.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Tiberius Iulius Stephanus, who died at the remarkable age of 105.

EDCS-23700188.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia Impetrata, daughter of Iulius Sedatus, lived 20 years. She is buried here. Your bones.
EDCS-23700188.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Iulia Impetrata, daughter of Iulius Sedatus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-23700189.translation: To the Divine Shades. IVA I Sorora lived 24 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23700189.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of IVA I Sorora, who died at the age of 24.

EDCS-23700190.translation: [...]nius Fruc[...], Iuia Mustia, and [...] from Pomponiae, made this temple [...] 105.
EDCS-23700190.summary: This inscription seems to record the construction of a temple by named individuals and possibly a group from Pomponiae, with a possible date or age of 105.

EDCS-23700191.translation: To the Divine Shades. Alfia Maximina lived 30 years.
EDCS-23700191.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Alfia Maximina, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-23700192.translation: To the Divine Shades. Antonia Sabina lived 46 years. To the Divine Shades. Lucius Domitius Martialis lived 53 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700192.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lives of two individuals, Antonia Sabina and Lucius Domitius Martialis, likely a married couple or family members, with their ages at death.

EDCS-23700193.translation: Giminia Pannicis lived 35 years. May you rest well here.

EDCS-23700194.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Matrona, daughter of Iulius Saturninus, lived 80 years.
EDCS-23700194.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Matrona, daughter of Iulius Saturninus, who died at age 80.

EDCS-23700195.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Ovinia lived 30 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld.
EDCS-23700195.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Ovinia, who died at age 30.

EDCS-23700196.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Optata, who died at age 20.

EDCS-23700198.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Aemilius Matlio lived 107 years.
EDCS-23700198.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aemilius Matlio, who died at age 107.

EDCS-23700199.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iunia Agathe lived 21 years.
EDCS-23700199.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iunia Agathe, who died at age 21.

EDCS-23700201.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Gargilia Monnula.
EDCS-23700201.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gargilia Monnula.

EDCS-23700202.translation: Quintus Mellius Catulonus willingly fulfilled his vow to the August Saturn.
EDCS-23700202.summary: This is a votive inscription by Quintus Mellius Catulonus to the god Saturn.

EDCS-23700203.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius Seius Pacatus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 67 years.
EDCS-23700203.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Seius Pacatus, son of Lucius, who died at age 67.

EDCS-23700204.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Seius, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, from Puteoli.
EDCS-23700204.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Seius, identified by his name, filiation, tribe, and origin.

EDCS-23700205.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Seius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, Quintianus, lived 62 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700205.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Seius, who lived 62 years, and includes a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23700206.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iulia Severina, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-23700206.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Iulia Severina.

EDCS-23700208.translation: Here, the unhappy Georgius, son of the reader, rested in peace with Tiberius and Capria, of the church. He lived 24 years. Died on the 9th day before the Kalends of August, in the 10th indiction.
EDCS-23700208.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Georgius, son of a reader, who died at age 24, with a specific date of death and reference to a church community.

EDCS-23700209.summary: This is a funerary epitaph consisting of a woman's name, Clodia Ettricata, followed by the number five, possibly indicating years or a rank.

EDCS-23700210.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gargilia Satulla lived 53 years. She is buried here. May you rest well here.
EDCS-23700210.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gargilia Satulla, who lived 53 years, including a wish for her repose.

EDCS-23700211.translation: Iulius Paris lived 101 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23700211.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Paris, who lived 101 years.

EDCS-23700212.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Mania, wife of Marcius Rusticus, lived 75 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23700212.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Mania, wife of Marcius Rusticus, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-23700213.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Tintirius Primosus lived 80 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23700213.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Tintirius Primosus, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-23700214.translation: For the health of the Geminii. Saenius Successus made this for Saturninus Augustus, the nurse.
EDCS-23700214.summary: This is a dedication made by Saenius Successus for the health of the Geminii, and mentions a nurse named Saturninus Augustus.

EDCS-23700216.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Julius Caecilianus, son of Caecilius, a veteran of the Legio III Augusta. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-23700216.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Caecilianus, a veteran of Legio III Augusta, indicating he served in the Roman army. The phrase 'Dis Manibus' and 'sit tibi terra levis' are common in Roman burial inscriptions.

EDCS-23700218.translation: Lucius Aedinius Citinus lived for 30 years.
EDCS-23700218.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Lucius Aedinius Citinus, a Roman citizen.

EDCS-23700219.translation: To the Divine Shades. Edinius Messor lived for 61 years.
EDCS-23700219.summary: This funerary inscription honors Edinius Messor, noting his age at death and dedicating the monument to the 'Divine Shades' (Dis Manibus).

EDCS-23700220.translation: Marcus Aedinius Qetus lived for 19 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23700220.summary: This inscription marks the burial place of Marcus Aedinius Qetus, a young man who died at the age of 19.

EDCS-23700221.summary: This is a simple inscription identifying Quintus Aemilius and his father, Marcius, likely part of a larger monument or tomb.

EDCS-23700222.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Aemilius Ianuarius.

EDCS-23700223.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Aemilius Ianuarius lived for 65 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-23700224.summary: This inscription records the death and burial of Aemilius Martialis, who lived to be 51 years old.

EDCS-23700225.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aemilius Martis lived for 31 years. To the Divine Shades. Anicia Ianuaria lived for 50 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23700225.summary: This inscription likely commemorates two individuals, Aemilius Martis and Anicia Ianuaria, who died at ages 31 and 50 respectively, and were buried together.

EDCS-23700229.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Afidius Namphamo lived 85 years. F S [possibly 'fecit sibi' - made for himself or 'fuit suae' - was his own].
EDCS-23700229.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Afidius Namphamo, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-23700234.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antistia Victorina lived 70 years.

EDCS-23700236.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Antonius lived 51 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23700236.summary: This funerary inscription states that Quintus Antonius lived 51 years and is buried at this site.

EDCS-23700237.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Antonius Arator lived [XX?] years. To the spirits of the departed. Numisia Nampamilla lived 71 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23700237.summary: This inscription records two burials: Quintus Antonius Arator, with his lifespan partially preserved, and Numisia Nampamilla, who lived 71 years.

EDCS-23700238.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Antonius Crescens lived for 21 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23700238.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Lucius Antonius Crescens.

EDCS-23700241.translation: Quintus Antonius Hospes lived for 19 years.

EDCS-23700246.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life of Tiberius Antonius Pacatus.

EDCS-23700247.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Antonius Proclus lived for 70 years. To the spirits of the departed.

EDCS-23700249.translation: Caius Antonius Quetus lived for 25 years; may your bones rest well here.

EDCS-23700251.translation: Lucius Antonius Quetus lived 20 years. He lies here.

EDCS-23700267.translation: Arator lived 65 years; he is buried here.

EDCS-23700713.summary: This is a funerary dedication recording the life of C.M. who lived for 26 years.

EDCS-23700714.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits: VA I VI. Honoratus, another, is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits: Sallustius Lucianus lived for 60 years. Salustia to her most precious father.
EDCS-23700714.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication for Sallustius Lucianus, with a mention of another person named Honoratus and a dedication from his daughter Salustia.

EDCS-23700715.translation: To the departed spirits: Quintus Iulius, son of Caius Quirinus Martialis, Senator of the Roman People, lived for 31 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23700715.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Quintus Iulius, son of Caius Quirinus Martialis, who was a Senator and lived for 31 years.

EDCS-23700717.translation: To Fortuna Redux, Augusta, from the state of Phuensium, dedicated and consecrated; for the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 16th time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, son of the deified Severus Pius Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus; grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus; great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius; great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian; great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva.
EDCS-23700717.summary: This inscription records the dedication by the state of Phuensium to Fortuna Redux and provides a lengthy titulature for Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-23700720.translation: Sacred to Saturn, Augustus. Lucius Iulius Urbanus gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-23700720.summary: An altar dedicated to Saturn by Lucius Iulius Urbanus, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-23700722.translation: To Iulia Augusta, mother of the camp, wife of Emperor Caesar Divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici son, brother of Divi Commodi, grandson of Divi Antonini Pii, great-grandson of Divi Hadriani, great-great-grandson of Divi Traiani Parthici, great-great-great-grandson of Divi Nervae, Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 13th time, imperator for the 11th time, consul for the 3rd time, proconsul, propagator of the empire, the most brave and fortunate prince, father of the fatherland; to the mother of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, son of Divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici, grandson of Divi Antonini Pii, great-grandson of Divi Hadriani, great-great-grandson of Divi Traiani Parthici and Divi Nervae, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 9th time, consul for the 3rd time, proconsul, the most brave and fortunate prince, father of the fatherland; and to all the most unconquered princes before him: the state of Phuensium.
EDCS-23700722.summary: A monumental inscription honoring Empress Iulia Domna, wife of Septimius Severus and mother of Caracalla, dedicating it to her and the imperial family by the community of Phuensium, likely dating to the reign of Caracalla (c. 202-211 AD).

EDCS-23700723.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, son of Divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici, grandson of Divi Antonini Pii, great-grandson of Divi Hadriani, great-great-grandson of Divi Traiani Parthici and Divi Nervae, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, proconsul, the most brave and fortunate prince, father of the fatherland; and to all the most indulgent princes before him: the state of Phuensium.
EDCS-23700723.summary: A dedication to Emperor Caracalla by the community of Phuensium, likely dating to his early reign (c. 200-202 AD).

EDCS-23700725.translation: Having achieved many things for the glorious life and virtue of his son, Caius Iulius Montanus of the Quirina tribe, having come into the world, rejoices in the return of the Roman Mars, in a dated arch.
EDCS-23700725.summary: An incomplete inscription possibly related to a son's achievements and a triumphal arch, with the dedicant Caius Iulius Montanus, dating uncertain but likely Imperial.

EDCS-23700726.translation: Boutius, son of Celius, an equestrian of the I Ala of Pannonians.
EDCS-23700726.summary: A tombstone for Boutius, an equestrian soldier of the I Ala of Pannonians, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700727.translation: Tiberius Claudius Mantai filius Cilius Lusitanus, an equestrian of the I Ala of Pannonians, lived for 45 years, served for 27 years. He is buried here. His freedwomen Claudia Primigenia and Fortunata placed this.
EDCS-23700727.summary: A tombstone for Tiberius Claudius Lusitanus, an equestrian soldier of the I Ala of Pannonians, erected by his freedwomen, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700728.translation: To the departed spirits. In memory of Numerius Datus, a veteran of the Legio III Augusta Pia Vindex. He lived for 70 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700728.summary: A tombstone for Numerius Datus, a veteran of Legio III Augusta Pia Vindex, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700729.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Tannonius Felix, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, also called Aquensis, a soldier of the Legio III, served for 2 years, lived for 21 years.
EDCS-23700729.summary: A tombstone for Caius Tannonius Felix, a soldier of Legio III, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700731.translation: To the departed spirits. Monula lived for 120 years. Priestess.
EDCS-23700731.summary: A tombstone for Monula, noted for her exceptional age and role as a priestess, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700732.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Abidius Servatus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-23700732.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Abidius Servatus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700734.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. I lived 51 years. Here I am. May you rest in peace. To the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius Aedinius Crescens lived 60 years. Here I am buried. To the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius Aedinius Gloriosus lived 25 years. Here I am buried.
EDCS-23700734.summary: A funerary inscription recording the ages at death of three individuals: ILA, Gaius Aedinius Crescens, and Gaius Aedinius Gloriosus.

EDCS-23700735.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aedinius Messor lived 35 years. Here I am buried.
EDCS-23700735.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age at death of Aedinius Messor.

EDCS-23700736.translation: Aelius Impetratus Iosa Augustalis, a freedman of the emperor, lived.
EDCS-23700736.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription recording the name of Aelius Impetratus Iosa Augustalis and his status as a freedman of the emperor.

EDCS-23700737.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aemilia Cide lived.
EDCS-23700737.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription recording the name of Aemilia Cide and the beginning of her age at death.

EDCS-23700738.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aemilia Maximina lived 59 years. Here lie my bones. May you rest in peace.
EDCS-23700738.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age at death of Aemilia Maximina and a wish for her repose.

EDCS-23700739.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. In memory of Antistia, daughter of Lucius, Honora. Lived 70 years. Here lie my bones. May you rest in peace.
EDCS-23700739.summary: A funerary inscription recording the name, parentage, and age at death of Antistia Honora.

EDCS-23700740.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Publius Antonius Macer lived 23 years. Here I am buried.
EDCS-23700740.summary: A funerary inscription recording the name and age at death of Publius Antonius Macer.

EDCS-23700741.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Antonius Optius lived 30 years. Here I am buried.
EDCS-23700741.summary: A funerary inscription recording the name and age at death of Marcus Antonius Optius.

EDCS-23700742.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Antonia Fiscu lived 103 years. Here lie my bones. May you rest in peace.
EDCS-23700742.summary: A funerary inscription recording the name and age at death of Antonia Fiscu, who lived to be 103.

EDCS-23700743.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Antullus Martialis lived 96 years. Here I am buried.
EDCS-23700743.summary: A funerary inscription recording the name and age at death of Lucius Antullus Martialis.

EDCS-23700744.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age of Gaius Antullus Novellus.

EDCS-23700745.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Gaius Arnesius.
EDCS-23700745.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicating the tomb to Gaius Arnesius.

EDCS-23700747.translation: Primula, daughter of Marcus Arnesius, lived 35 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700747.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name, parentage, age at death, and a hopeful wish for the deceased Primula.

EDCS-23700749.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Aruntia Inuaria.
EDCS-23700749.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicating the tomb to Aruntia Inuaria.

EDCS-23700750.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Marcus Baconius.
EDCS-23700750.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicating the tomb to Marcus Baconius.

EDCS-23700751.translation: Marcus Baconius Quintosus lived 18 years; buried here, may you rest well here.
EDCS-23700751.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name, age at death, and location of burial for Marcus Baconius Quintosus.

EDCS-23700752.translation: Baebia Romana lived 45 years; Cor Plu of the Romans and Anni, her granddaughter, [erected this] with her own money.
EDCS-23700752.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age at death of Baebia Romana and notes that it was funded by her granddaughter Anni, possibly with the involvement of 'Cor Plu of the Romans'.

EDCS-23700753.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Baricio lived 90 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23700753.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name, age at death, and burial location of Baricio.

EDCS-23700754.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Basilius.
EDCS-23700754.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicating the tomb to Basilius.

EDCS-23700755.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Lucius Caecilius Felix lived 60 years.
EDCS-23700755.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name and age at death of Lucius Caecilius Felix.

EDCS-23700756.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Caecilius Pudens the Younger lived 26 years and 25 days; here he lies; may your bones rest well. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caecilia Fortunata lies here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700756.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Gaius Caecilius Pudens Iunior and Caecilia Fortunata.

EDCS-23700758.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ceilia Venusta lived 103 years; may you rest well here; she lies here.
EDCS-23700758.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Ceilia Venusta, who lived to be 103.

EDCS-23700760.translation: Gaius Claudius Claudianus, son of Quirina.
EDCS-23700760.summary: A fragmentary inscription that may record the name and filiation of Gaius Claudius Claudianus.

EDCS-23700761.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Claudius Victor lived for...
EDCS-23700761.summary: A funerary epitaph that begins by recording the death of Claudius Victor and his age, but is incomplete.

EDCS-23700762.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Tiberius Claudius Victor.
EDCS-23700762.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name of Tiberius Claudius Victor.

EDCS-23700763.translation: Claudius Ato lived 30 years; here he lies.
EDCS-23700763.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death and age of Claudius Ato.

EDCS-23700764.translation: Here lies E. Claudius enus O placed [this].
EDCS-23700764.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph indicating that Claudius enus O placed the monument for E.

EDCS-23700765.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Clius Ianuarius lived 27 years.
EDCS-23700765.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death and age of Gaius Clius Ianuarius.

EDCS-23700766.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Cluius Crescens lived 101 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Mutia Cinidia.
EDCS-23700766.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Gaius Cluius Crescens and potentially Mutia Cinidia, noting Crescens' age.

EDCS-23700767.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Cornelius.
EDCS-23700767.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name of Lucius Cornelius.

EDCS-23700768.translation: To the spirits of the departed of Quintus Cornelius.
EDCS-23700768.summary: A funerary dedication to Quintus Cornelius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700769.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cornelius Victor lived 45 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700769.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelius Victor, recording his age at death, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700770.translation: Cornelia Maxima lived 73 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23700770.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelia Maxima, recording her age at death and burial location, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700771.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Crescens lived 53 years. He is buried here. May you rest well here.
EDCS-23700771.summary: A funerary epitaph for Crescens, recording his age at death and burial location, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700772.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Datius lived 31 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23700772.summary: A funerary epitaph for Datius, recording his age at death and burial location, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700773.summary: A funerary epitaph for S. Estricatus, recording his age at death, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700774.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Flavius, a man of consular rank, lived...
EDCS-23700774.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication for Lucius Flavius, mentioning his high status, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700775.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavia Honorata lived 15 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23700775.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Honorata, recording her age at death and burial location, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700776.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavia Rogata.
EDCS-23700776.summary: A funerary dedication to Flavia Rogata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700778.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Fullonius Libonianus lived 29 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23700778.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Fullonius Libonianus, recording his age at death and burial location, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700779.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Fullonius Secundus, son of Marcus, lived 61 years.
EDCS-23700779.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Fullonius Secundus, who lived 61 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700780.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fullonia Germulia lived 75 years.
EDCS-23700780.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fullonia Germulia, who lived 75 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700781.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gabinius Meridianus lived 91 years, is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700781.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gabinius Meridianus, who lived 91 years and is buried here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700782.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Gabinius Servatus lived 65 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23700782.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Gabinius Servatus, who lived 65 years and is buried here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700783.translation: To the spirits of the departed. B. Gabinia A. ESA.
EDCS-23700783.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph mentioning B. Gabinia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700784.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gabius Victor lived 85 years.
EDCS-23700784.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gabius Victor, who lived 85 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700785.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextus Gargirius Respectus lived 45 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23700785.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Gargirius Respectus, who lived 45 years and is buried here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700786.translation: Lucius Gargilius Sattas lived 40 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23700786.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Gargilius Sattas, who lived 40 years and is buried here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700787.translation: To the spirits of the departed. L. NI Martialis Quirina Nepos lived 66 years, is buried here; may your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed. Geminia Fortunula lived 65 years, is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700787.summary: This inscription contains two funerary epitaphs, one for L. NI Martialis Quirina Nepos (66 years) and one for Geminia Fortunula (65 years), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700788.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Helene lived 17 years, is buried here.

EDCS-23700789.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Hist.
EDCS-23700789.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hist, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700791.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Lucius Hordionius Crescens lived 85 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well. Hordionia Festosa lived 65 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23700791.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Hordionius Crescens and Hordionia Festosa, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700792.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Hordionia Mariana lived 61 years; she is buried here. May you rest well. Lucius Hordionius Messor lived 80 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700792.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hordionia Mariana and Lucius Hordionius Messor, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700793.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Hordionius Silieha lived 16 years.
EDCS-23700793.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hordionius Silieha, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700794.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Hordonius Urbanus lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23700794.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hordonius Urbanus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700796.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Iulia Fortunata lived 36 years.
EDCS-23700796.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Fortunata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700797.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Sextus Ianuarius Peculiaris lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23700797.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Ianuarius Peculiaris, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700798.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Iulius Felix lived 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23700798.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Felix, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700799.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Sextus Iulius Felix lived 50 years.
EDCS-23700799.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Iulius Felix, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700800.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Lucius Iulius Fronto lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23700800.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Iulius Fronto, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700801.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Gaius Julius Ianuarius.

EDCS-23700802.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Julius Ianuarius lived for 31 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-23700803.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Julius Ianuarius lived for 51 years. May you rest well here.

EDCS-23700804.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Julius Marcianus lived for 67 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-23700805.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Maximus lived for 14 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-23700807.translation: To the departed spirits. Sitetia Colosida lived for 80 years. To the departed spirits. Julius Musteolus lived for 60 years.
EDCS-23700807.summary: This inscription records the lifespans of two individuals, Sitetia Colosida and Julius Musteolus.

EDCS-23700808.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Mustiolus lived for 7 years.
EDCS-23700808.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Gaius Julius Mustiolus.

EDCS-23700809.translation: To the departed spirits. Julius Nigrinus lived for 51 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-23700810.translation: To the departed spirits. Julius Pultarius lived for 16 years.
EDCS-23700810.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Julius Pultarius.

EDCS-23700811.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Iulius Urbanus lived 63 years. Buried here, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700811.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Iulius Urbanus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23700812.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Iulius Urbanus lived 81 years. He is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700812.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Iulius Urbanus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23700813.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulius Castosus, son of Titus, lived 30 years.
EDCS-23700813.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulius Castosus, son of Titus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23700814.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia.
EDCS-23700814.summary: A very brief funerary inscription for Iulia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23700815.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Feliciosa lived 55 years. She is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700815.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Feliciosa, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23700816.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Quirina Flavila lived 61 years.
EDCS-23700816.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Quirina Flavila, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23700817.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Maximina lived 75 years. She is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700817.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Maximina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23700818.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Modesta, daughter of Lucius, wife of Caius Maevius Crescentis, lived 26 years; may your bones rest well.

EDCS-23700819.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Paula lived 50 years. She is buried here; may your bones rest well.

EDCS-23700821.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Satura lived.
EDCS-23700821.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Satura, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23700822.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Soliaca lived 37 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23700822.summary: A tombstone epitaph for Iulia Soliaca, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700823.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Urbanica lived 35 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700823.summary: A tombstone epitaph for Iulia Urbanica, dating to the Roman Imperial period, with a wish for her peaceful repose.

EDCS-23700824.summary: An incomplete tombstone epitaph recording the lifespan of an individual identified only by initials and a praenomen, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700825.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Labia Honorata lived 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23700825.summary: A tombstone epitaph for Labia Honorata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700826.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucilius Felix lived 60 years; he is buried here. To the spirits of the underworld. Seia Lucilia lived 75 years; she is buried here. May you rest well.
EDCS-23700826.summary: A tombstone epitaph commemorating two individuals, Lucilius Felix and Seia Lucilia, likely a couple, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700827.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucilia Maliana was well for 61 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.

EDCS-23700833.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Magnia Lucia, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-23700834.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Manius Martialis lived 20 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700834.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Manius Martialis, recording his name, age at death, and a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-23700835.translation: Martialis lived 55 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700835.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Martialis, recording his name and age at death, with a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-23700836.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Martis Pisonis lived 36 years.
EDCS-23700836.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Martis Pisonis, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-23700837.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Maximilla lived 20 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23700837.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maximilla, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-23700838.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Meclasia Cu[l]a lived 51 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23700838.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Meclasia Cula, recording her name and age at death. The "u" in Cula is restored.

EDCS-23700839.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Memmi Felici lived 60 years.
EDCS-23700839.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Memmi Felici, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-23700840.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Memmius Nampamo, farewell. Lived 85 years.
EDCS-23700840.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Memmius Nampamo, recording his name and age at death, with a farewell.

EDCS-23700841.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antonia Ianuaria lived 51 years. May your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed. Memmius Sodalis lived 60 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23700841.summary: This inscription contains two funerary epitaphs, one for Antonia Ianuaria and another for Memmius Sodalis, recording their names, ages at death, and burial details.

EDCS-23700844.translation: Montanus lived 71 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-23700844.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Montanus.

EDCS-23700845.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Quintus Mucidius Victor lived 80 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-23700845.summary: This tombstone inscription records the lifespan and burial of Quintus Mucidius Victor, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-23700846.translation: Publius Munatius lived 7 years, he is buried here.

EDCS-23700847.translation: Iolus lived 31 years, he is buried here.

EDCS-23700848.translation: Namphamus lived 41 years, he is buried here.

EDCS-23700850.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Nonia Albula lived 61 years, she is buried here, may her bones rest well with you.

EDCS-23700852.translation: Numisius Florus lived 50 years, he is buried here.

EDCS-23700853.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Numisius Sodalis lived 75 years, may his bones rest well here.

EDCS-23700854.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Lucius Octavius Crescens lived 36 years, he is buried here, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700854.summary: This tombstone inscription records the lifespan and burial of Lucius Octavius Crescens, dedicated to the departed spirits with a wish for his rest.

EDCS-23700855.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Octavius Rogatus lived 70 years.

EDCS-23700856.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Octavia Sorora, dutiful.

EDCS-23700857.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Octavia lived 20 years.
EDCS-23700857.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Octavia.

EDCS-23700858.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for the memory of Pettedius Felix, who lived 30 years; may you rest well here; here he lies. To the spirits of the departed, for the memory of Pettediae Fortunatae, who lived 15 years; here she lies; may you rest well here. To the spirits of the departed, for the memory of Vibiae Extricatae, who lived 65 years; here she lies; may your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed, Quintus Claudius [---] LVN STV ICV O V VI to his husband, lived 50 years.
EDCS-23700858.summary: This complex funerary inscription records the lifespans and dedications for multiple individuals, including Pettedius Felix, Pettedia Fortunata, Vibia Extricata, and Quintus Claudius, possibly related to family members and a husband.

EDCS-23700859.translation: Caius Pettedius Fortunatus lived 50 years; here he lies.

EDCS-23700860.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Pontius Victor lived 30 years; may you rest well here.

EDCS-23700861.translation: Porcius Crescens lived 56 years; here he is buried.

EDCS-23700867.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period, for a person named Sedi[a/us].

EDCS-23700870.translation: To the spirits of the departed; Lucius Seius Nepos lived 75 years and is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700870.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Seius Nepos, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-23700871.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Seius Saturninus, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording his exceptional lifespan.

EDCS-23700875.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Sextilius Namphamo, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-23700876.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextia Mammia, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-23700878.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Sittius Contentus, son of Lucius, lived 24 years, placed here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700878.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Sittius Contentus, a young man, with a wish for his repose, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700879.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Sittia Magna Acuta, an elderly woman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700881.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Publius Scanius Ianuarianus lived 71 years, placed here.
EDCS-23700881.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Publius Scanius Ianuarianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700883.summary: This is a funerary inscription naming Spica, daughter of Urbanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700884.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Statius Victor, with a wish for his repose, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700885.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Titurnia Monula lived 20 years.
EDCS-23700885.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Titurnia Monula, a young woman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700886.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Quintus Tullius Montanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700887.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Quintus Tullius Pontianus, a young man, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700888.summary: A funerary inscription for Valeria Silvana and Valeria Nampanulosa, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-23700889.summary: A funerary inscription for MACV Victor, recording his age at death.

EDCS-23700895.translation: ELI lived 65 years; he is buried here.
