EDCS-23700902.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Pardinius.
EDCS-23700902.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Pardinius.

EDCS-23700907.summary: An inscription with names that may be a dedication or a record of individuals.

EDCS-23700912.translation: Sacred to Imperial Ceres. Iulia Mussiosa Kasariana, by consent of the council of the settlement of Arsacalitana, made this with her own money. The site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-23700912.summary: This dedication to Imperial Ceres was funded by Iulia Mussiosa Kasariana and approved by local officials in Arsacalitana.

EDCS-23700914.translation: To Jupiter the Repeller, in accordance with the will of Marcus Annius Martialis, a veteran.
EDCS-23700914.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter, established by the will of the veteran Marcus Annius Martialis.

EDCS-23700915.translation: Sacred to Imperial Mercury. Lucius Iulius Peregrinus Sardilianus, son of Lucius, of the voting tribe Quirina, master of the settlement, made and dedicated this with his own money.
EDCS-23700915.summary: Lucius Iulius Peregrinus Sardilianus, the master of a settlement, dedicated this monument to Imperial Mercury at his own expense.

EDCS-23700917.translation: Sacred to Imperial Victory. Gaius Julius Victor, aedile, prefect acting as a board of three, made this statue of Victory with his own money, which he had promised in the name of his sons Iulius Tertullus Martialis, Iulius Quadratus, Iulius Julianus, and Iulius Honoratus, and he himself dedicated it and celebrated the day of its dedication with games.
EDCS-23700917.summary: Gaius Julius Victor, holding multiple civic offices, funded and dedicated a statue of Victory, also sponsoring games to celebrate its dedication.

EDCS-23700924.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Clodius Iulianus, son of Quintus, of the voting tribe Quirina, honored with public equestrian rank. Clodia Marcella [made this] for her most beloved brother. He lived 25 years. He lies here.
EDCS-23700924.summary: This tombstone for Quintus Clodius Iulianus, a man honored with equestrian rank, was erected by his sister Clodia Marcella and dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23700925.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Marcus Iulius Quadratus, son of Caius, of the voting tribe Quirina, admitted to the public equestrian order in the fifth decury, centurion of the 13th Gemina Legion in Dacia, centurion of the 3rd Augusta Legion in Africa, centurion of the 2nd Augusta Legion Britanniae, died while on active duty in Britannia. He lived 38 years. He lies here.
EDCS-23700925.summary: This epitaph records the extensive military career of Marcus Iulius Quadratus, a centurion in multiple legions, who died on active service in Britannia at the age of 38.

EDCS-23700926.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Atilus Aptus, a veteran of the 3rd Cyrenaica Legion. He lived 90 years.
EDCS-23700926.summary: This epitaph commemorates Quintus Atilus Aptus, a veteran of the 3rd Cyrenaica Legion, who lived to be 90.

EDCS-23700927.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. [Epitaph for] Publius Sinicius Munatus, a veteran, who lived 22 years. Livia, his wife, made this for him as one who deserved well.
EDCS-23700927.summary: Livia erected this tombstone for her husband, Publius Sinicius Munatus, a veteran who died at the young age of 22.

EDCS-23700928.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Aelius Brutianus, a priest of the goddess Caelestis, full of all sacred rites, lived 44 years. He lies here. To the Spirits of the Dead. Valeria Victorina lived 75 years. She lies here.
EDCS-23700928.summary: This tombstone records the deaths of Quintus Aelius Brutianus, a priest of Caelestis, and Valeria Victorina, providing their ages at death.

EDCS-23700929.summary: This fragmentary inscription records the age of a priest who lived to 77.

EDCS-23700932.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Quintus Aemilius Basilus.

EDCS-23700933.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Aemilius Cenator lived 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23700933.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Lucius Aemilius Cenator.

EDCS-23700934.translation: Lucius Aemilius Quirina Quetus lived 31 years.
EDCS-23700934.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Aemilius Quirina Quetus.

EDCS-23700935.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Aemilius Sato.

EDCS-23700936.translation: Marcus Aemilius Victor, son of Marcus, lived 85 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23700936.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Marcus Aemilius Victor.

EDCS-23700937.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Aemilia Dativa, though the duration is incomplete.

EDCS-23700938.translation: In memory of Aemilia Novella, most innocent wife.
EDCS-23700938.summary: This inscription is a memorial dedicating Aemilia Novella, described as a "most innocent wife."

EDCS-23700939.translation: Lucius Albius Crescens lived 11 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23700939.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Albius Crescens and expressing a wish for his peaceful repose.

EDCS-23700940.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Albius Messorinus.

EDCS-23700941.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Alfia Ammia lived 105 years; may your bones rest well; she is buried here.
EDCS-23700941.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the exceptional lifespan and burial of Alfia Ammia.

EDCS-23700942.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph naming Quintus Antius Rusticus.

EDCS-23700943.translation: To the departed spirits: Caius Antius Urbanus lived 70 years.
EDCS-23700943.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Antius Urbanus, who lived to be 70 years old.

EDCS-23700944.translation: Marcus Antonius Martialis lived 75 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-23700944.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Martialis, who lived 75 years and is buried here.

EDCS-23700945.translation: Atilia, daughter of Ianuaria, lived 20 years, she is buried here, may her bones rest well.
EDCS-23700945.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Atilia, daughter of Ianuaria, who died at 20 years of age, with a wish for her peaceful repose.

EDCS-23700947.translation: Benniuss Martialis, son of Quinti, of the Quirina tribe, lived 70 years.
EDCS-23700947.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Benniuss Martialis, son of Quinti, who lived 70 years and belonged to the Quirina tribe.

EDCS-23700948.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits: Caius Boqurius Fronto lived 41 years. Vocurius Mustiolus, his heir, had this made.
EDCS-23700948.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Caius Boqurius Fronto, who lived 41 years, and was erected by his heir, Vocurius Mustiolus.

EDCS-23700949.translation: Publius Caecilius Flaccus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 90 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-23700949.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Publius Caecilius Flaccus, son of Publius, who lived 90 years and is buried here.

EDCS-23700950.translation: To the departed spirits: Marcus Caecilius Honoratus lived 15 years, may his bones rest well here.
EDCS-23700950.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Marcus Caecilius Honoratus, who died at 15 years of age, with a wish for his peaceful repose.

EDCS-23700951.translation: Marcus Caecilius Namphamo, son, lived 25 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-23700951.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Marcus Caecilius Namphamo, who lived 25 years and is buried here.

EDCS-23700952.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph for Lucius Caecilius Novelus, recording his name and the beginning of his age at death.

EDCS-23700953.translation: To the divine spirits. Cecilia Satura lived for [number] years.
EDCS-23700953.summary: A funerary inscription for Cecilia Satura, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-23700954.translation: To the divine spirits. Lucius Caelius Saturus lived for 51 years.
EDCS-23700954.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Caelius Saturus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-23700955.translation: To the divine spirits. Caelia Publilia lived for 54 years. She lies here.

EDCS-23700959.translation: Lucius Caqeius Ragatus lived for 60 years. He lies here.

EDCS-23700960.summary: A complex funerary inscription for multiple individuals, including Casius Aemilianus, Iulia Cornelia Casia, and Iulia Septimina, with their lifespans and burial information.

EDCS-23700962.translation: Marcus Cassius Optatus lived for 70 years. Cassius [---]

EDCS-23700963.translation: To the divine spirits. Marcus Cassius Victor lived for 30 years. Lucius Cassius Quirina Placidus lived for 70 years. He lies here. Lucius Cassius Severus lived for 70 years. He lies here. MVR SANICA MO SA. To the divine spirits. Lucius Cassius Messor lived for 70 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-23700963.summary: A multi-part funerary inscription for several members of the Cassius family, including Marcus Cassius Victor, Lucius Cassius Quirina Placidus, Lucius Cassius Severus, and Lucius Cassius Messor, noting their lifespans, burial places, and a possible garbled or abbreviated phrase.

EDCS-23700964.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Cassia Honorata lived [XX] years. Lucius Cassius Manius lived 75 years.

EDCS-23700975.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Cornelius Clarus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-23700976.translation: Lucius Messor Cornelius lived 41 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.

EDCS-23700977.translation: Caius Cornelius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, Quintillius, lived [X] years.

EDCS-23700978.translation: Marcus Cornelius Quintilius lived 35 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-23700978.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Cornelius Quintilius, recording his lifespan and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23700979.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Cornelius Verecundus lived 31 years.
EDCS-23700979.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelius Verecundus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-23700980.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Cornelia, daughter of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 45 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23700980.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia, daughter of Lucius, recording her lifespan, tribal affiliation, and burial.

EDCS-23700981.translation: Cornelia Aprata, freedwoman of Quintus, lived 56 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23700981.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Aprata, a freedwoman, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-23700983.translation: Corsilia Potiana, daughter of Potitus, lived 65 years. She is buried here.

EDCS-23700984.translation: Crispus lived [X] years. May your bones rest well here.

EDCS-23700985.translation: Caius Decrius Catlus lived 33 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23700985.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Decrius Catlus, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-23700986.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Decria Optata lived 75 years and is buried here.
EDCS-23700986.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death and burial of Decria Optata at age 75.

EDCS-23701273.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus, companion and protector of our lord Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus, son of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan and the deified Nerva, great-great-great-grandson of Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 16th time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 4th time, Proconsul, Father of the Fatherland. The Republic of the Uzelitani made this with its own funds.
EDCS-23701273.summary: A dedication to Emperor Septimius Severus by the Republic of the Uzelitani, honoring his titles and lineage, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23701275.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Genius of the grain supply. Publius Marcius Crescens, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, magistrate, made this bronze statue, which he promised for the honor of his magistracy on the 3rd day before the Nones of January in the Capitol, with the highest honors conferred upon the state, and the office of decurion and magistrate, with his own money, and dedicated it in the same year on the 16th day before the Kalends of October; the location was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-23701275.summary: A dedication of a bronze statue to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Marcius Crescens, a magistrate, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23701276.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the camps, wife of Emperor Caesar Divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici filii divi Commodi fratris divi Antonini Pii nepotis divi Hadriani pronepotis divi Traiani Parthici abnepotis divi Nervae adnepotis Lucii Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Parthici Arabici Parthici Adiabenici propagatoris imperii pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate IX imperatoris XI consulis II proconsulis patris patriae fortissimi felicissimi ac sanctissimi principis, mother of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus S...
EDCS-23701276.summary: An inscription honoring Julia Augusta, wife of Septimius Severus and mother of Caracalla, listing her extensive imperial titles and familial connections, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23701277.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, descendant of the deified Hadrian, descendant of the deified Trajan Parthicus, Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 15th time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, by the Republic of the Uzelitani.
EDCS-23701277.summary: This is a dedication by the Republic of the Uzelitani to Emperor Caracalla, likely from the period of his co-reign with his father Septimius Severus, given the titulature.

EDCS-23701278.translation: To the deified Pius, Lucius Septimius Severus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, father of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 15th time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 3rd time, by the Republic of the Uzelitani.
EDCS-23701278.summary: This is a dedication by the Republic of the Uzelitani to the deified Emperor Septimius Severus, likely from the period of his co-reign with his son Caracalla.

EDCS-23701279.translation: To Julia Augusta Pia Felix, mother of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Britannicus Maximus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 15th time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, son of the deified Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, descendant of the deified Hadrian, descendant of the deified Trajan Parthicus, by the Senate, the camps, and the fatherland, and by the Republic of the Uzelitani.
EDCS-23701279.summary: This is a dedication by the Republic of the Uzelitani, the Senate, the camps, and the fatherland to Julia Domna, mother of Caracalla and wife of Septimius Severus.

EDCS-23701282.translation: Sittius Honoratus placed and dedicated this, by her will, to Datulla, daughter of Lucius, wife of Sittius Quintillus the quaestor, mother of Sittius Honoratus, honored with the public horse.
EDCS-23701282.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument by Sittius Honoratus for his mother Datulla, who had designated him as her heir in her will.

EDCS-23701285.translation: A Roman knight, decurion of four colonies, having held all offices, lived 81 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701285.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates a Roman knight who achieved high office in four colonies and lived to the age of 81.

EDCS-23701286.translation: To the departed spirits. Munatius, son of Sextus, a veteran of the British legion, lived 61 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701286.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Munatius, a veteran of the British legion, who died at the age of 61.

EDCS-23701287.translation: Aemilia Stercula lived 57 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23701287.summary: This is a simple tombstone inscription recording the name and age at death of Aemilia Stercula.

EDCS-23701288.translation: To the departed spirits. Agria Victorina lived 55 years; she is buried here. May you rest well here.
EDCS-23701288.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Agria Victorina at the age of 55.

EDCS-23701289.translation: To the departed spirits. Sextus Annius Novellus, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 68 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701289.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Sextus Annius Novellus, who died at the age of 68.

EDCS-23701290.translation: To the departed spirits. Caecilia Urbana lived 71 years; may your bones rest well. To the departed spirits. Antonia Mustia lived 21 years.
EDCS-23701290.summary: This inscription commemorates two women, Caecilia Urbana (died at 71) and Antonia Mustia (died at 21), with votive formulae to the departed spirits.

EDCS-23701292.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Basilia Felicia lived 45 years.
EDCS-23701292.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Basilia Felicia, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-23701293.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Caius Caecilius Ingenuus lived 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701293.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Caecilius Ingenuus, recording his name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-23701294.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Iulia Maxima, daughter of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 92 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed: Lucius Caecilius Novellus Leo, lived 55 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed: Sittia Faustina, daughter of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 41 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701294.summary: This funerary inscription contains multiple epitaphs for members of the Caecilius and related families, recording their names, ages, and burial wishes.

EDCS-23701295.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Cecilius Saturnus lived 27 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701295.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cecilius Saturnus, recording his name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-23701296.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Cecilia Ganosa lived 25 years.
EDCS-23701296.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cecilia Ganosa, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-23701297.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Sextus Geminius Faustus lived 101 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed: Nautia, daughter of SITAQEACA, lived 80 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed: Caius Geminius Securus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 60 years; he is buried here. To the spirits of the departed: Marcus Caminius Castinius, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years; he is buried here. To the spirits of the departed: Marcus Caminus Egonianus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 14 years; he is buried here. To the spirits of the departed: Marcus Pompeius, son of Marcus, of the T...
EDCS-23701297.summary: This lengthy funerary inscription records the lives and burials of several individuals from the Geminius, Caminius, and Pompeius families.

EDCS-23701298.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Quintus Cassius Rogatianus lived 15 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701298.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Cassius Rogatianus, recording his name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-23701299.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Pompea Lucilla lived 106 years; she is buried here. To the spirits of the departed: Lucius Clodius Musteolus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 85 years; may you rest well here. To the spirits of the departed: Clodia Mamilla, daughter of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 51 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed: Caius Iulius Fortis, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 70 years; may you rest well here.
EDCS-23701299.summary: This funerary inscription contains multiple epitaphs for members of the Clodius and Iulius families, recording their names, ages, and wishes for peaceful rest.

EDCS-23701301.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Clodia Rogata lived 26 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23701301.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clodia Rogata, recording her name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-23701302.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Marcus Cornelius Pequarius, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 47 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701302.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Cornelius Pequarius, recording his name, age at death, burial, and a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-23701303.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Cosmius Felicio, aged 65, is placed here; may your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed, Domitia Urbana lived 85 years, is placed here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701303.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the deaths and ages of Cosmius Felicio and Domitia Urbana.

EDCS-23701304.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Cossinius Fortunnais lived 51 years; may you rest well. To the spirits of the departed, Quintus Iulius Felix lived 31 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701304.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the deaths and ages of Cossinius Fortunnais and Quintus Iulius Felix.

EDCS-23701305.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Aulus Ianuarius, aged 14, [lived...]. To the spirits of the departed, Caecilia Luciosa lived 55 years, is placed here; may your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed, Caius Crepereius Rogatus lived 78 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701305.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the deaths and ages of Aulus Ianuarius, Caecilia Luciosa, and Caius Crepereius Rogatus.

EDCS-23701306.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Mamiae Iuliae Furiae, aged 15, is placed here; may your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed, Damius Impetratus lived 42 years, is placed here. To the spirits of the departed, Iulia Castula lived 95 years, is placed here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701306.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the deaths and ages of Mamiae Iuliae Furiae, Damius Impetratus, and Iulia Castula.

EDCS-23701307.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Curlonia Fortunata lived 32 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701307.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death and age of Curlonia Fortunata.

EDCS-23701308.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Lucius Egnatius Honoratianus lived 55 years, is placed here; may your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed, [---] lived 31 years, is placed here. To the spirits of the departed, Gargilia Honorata lived 105 years, is placed here; may you rest well. To the spirits of the departed, Lucius Egnatius, son of Marcus, Quirina Rufinus, lived 80 years, is placed here; may you rest well. To the spirits of the departed, Lucius Egnatius, son of Lucius, Quirina Commodus, lived 70 years, is placed here.
EDCS-23701308.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the deaths and ages of Lucius Egnatius Honoratianus, an unnamed individual, Gargilia Honorata, Lucius Egnatius Rufinus, and Lucius Egnatius Commodus.

EDCS-23701310.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Fabia Fabulla lived 96 years, is placed here; may your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed.
EDCS-23701310.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death and age of Fabia Fabulla.

EDCS-23701311.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Curia Quintilla lived 35 years, is placed here. To the spirits of the departed, Quintus Fannius, son of Mardi, Quirina Felix, lived 11 years, is placed here. To the spirits of the departed, Quintus Fannius, son of Severus, lived 61 years, is placed here. To the spirits of the departed, Tanginia Quintilla lived 51 years. To the spirits of the departed, Tanginia Quintilla lived 15 years, is placed here. To the spirits of the departed, Quintus Tanginius Martialis lived 63 years; may you rest well. To the spirits of the departed, Claudia Rogata lived 32 years, is placed here.
EDCS-23701311.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the deaths and ages of Curia Quintilla, Quintus Fannius Felix, Quintus Fannius Severus, Tanginia Quintilla (twice), Quintus Tanginius Martialis, and Claudia Rogata.

EDCS-23701312.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Fania Ianuaria lived 45 years, is placed here.
EDCS-23701312.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death and age of Fania Ianuaria.

EDCS-23701313.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Geminus GRENS is placed here.
EDCS-23701313.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of Geminus GRENS.

EDCS-23701314.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Honorata, daughter of Fuscinus, lived 35 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701314.summary: A funerary epitaph for Honorata, recording her name, parentage, age at death, and a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-23701315.translation: Publius Ortesius Saturus lived 55 years; he is buried in this place. May your bones rest well. Quintus Ortesius Martialis lived 15 years; he is buried in this place. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701315.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burials and ages of two individuals, Publius Ortesius Saturus and Quintus Ortesius Martialis.

EDCS-23701316.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Marius, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 15 years; he is buried here. To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Iulius Namphamo lived 55 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Iulius Ianuarius lived 61 years; he is buried here. To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Honorata lived 53 years; may your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Catervarius lived 42 years; may you rest well here.
EDCS-23701316.summary: A funerary inscription for at least five individuals, including Iulius Marius, Lucius Iulius Namphamo, Quintus Iulius Ianuarius, Iulia Honorata, and Iulius Catervarius, recording their ages and burial locations.

EDCS-23701317.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sittia Sodala lived 105 years; she is buried here. To the spirits of the departed. Crescens, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 85 years; he is buried here. Bocca lived 60 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23701317.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burials and ages of Sittia Sodala, Crescens, and Bocca.

EDCS-23701318.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Manilia Extricata lived 85 years. To the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Maximilla lived 65 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Iulius Faustus lived 75 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701318.summary: A funerary inscription for Manilia Extricata, Caecilia Maximilla, and Quintus Iulius Faustus, noting their ages and burial locations.

EDCS-23701319.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Felix lived 90 years; he is buried here. To the spirits of the departed. Urbana Ania lived 65 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23701319.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Felix and Urbana Ania, recording their ages and burial locations.

EDCS-23701320.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Felix, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 81 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed. Antonia Fortunata lived 61 years; may your bones rest well. Lucius Iulius, son of Quirina Felix, lived 85 years; he is buried here. Caius Iulius Sittius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 21 years; he is buried here. May you rest well here.
EDCS-23701320.summary: A funerary inscription detailing the burials and ages of Caius Iulius Felix, Antonia Fortunata, Lucius Iulius, and Caius Iulius Sittius.

EDCS-23701321.translation: Caius Iulius Honoratus lived 21 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well here. To the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Faustus lived 30 years; may he rest here. Sittia Gala lived 75 years; may she rest.
EDCS-23701321.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Honoratus, Caius Iulius Faustus, and Sittia Gala, specifying their ages and expressing wishes for their repose.

EDCS-23701322.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Iulius Felix lived 60 years; may you rest well here. To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Vindicta lived 61 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23701322.summary: A funerary inscription recording the ages and burial of Marcus Iulius Felix and Iulia Vindicta.

EDCS-23701323.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Fronto lived 27 years. To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Iulius Slvanus lived 35 years; he is placed here. To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Mustia lived 102 years; she is buried here. May you rest well here. To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Iulius Victor lived 61 years; he is buried here. May you rest well here. To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Iulius Messor lived 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701323.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulius Fronto, Lucius Iulius Slvanus, Iulia Mustia, Lucius Iulius Victor, and Marcus Iulius Messor, noting their ages and burial locations.

EDCS-23701324.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Quintus Iulius Ianuarius lived for 61 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701324.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Iulius Ianuarius, who lived to 61 and is buried here.

EDCS-23701325.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Getula Satria, may you rest in this place. To the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Iulius Mustiolus lived for 110 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aercia Mapo, 41 [years].
EDCS-23701325.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Getula Satria and Caius Iulius Mustiolus, who lived to 110, and Aercia Mapo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23701326.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulius Nampame lived for 60 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701326.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Iulius Nampame, who lived to 60.

EDCS-23701327.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Relus lived [here]. To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia [placed this]; she lived for 76 years. To the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Iulius Nampulus lived for 85 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701327.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages and burials of Iulia (76) and Marcus Iulius Nampulus (85), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23701328.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Iulius VBNVS lived for 5 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the Spirits of the Underworld. Honorata lived for 60 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Iulius Quetus lived for 85 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the Spirits of the Underworld.
EDCS-23701328.summary: This inscription records the burial and ages of Lucius Iulius VBNVS (5), Honorata (60), and Caius Iulius Quetus (85), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23701329.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Quintus Iulius Rogatianus lived for 67 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the Spirits of the Underworld. Gargilia Extricata lived for 29 years; [she is] buried. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701329.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial and ages of Quintus Iulius Rogatianus (67) and Gargilia Extricata (29), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23701330.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aurelia Honorata lived for 81 years; may you rest well here. To the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius AF VM Victor lived for 50 years. May your bones rest well. To the Spirits of the Underworld. Quintus Iulius, son of Quinti, Quirina Septimius lived for 54 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701330.summary: This inscription records the burial and ages of Aurelia Honorata (81), Gaius AF VM Victor (50), and Quintus Iulius Quirina Septimius (54), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23701331.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Honorata lived for 53 years. To the Spirits of the Underworld. Quintus Iulius Silvanus.
EDCS-23701331.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Iulia Honorata (53) and mentioning Quintus Iulius Silvanus.

EDCS-23701332.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Ius SE lived for 45 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulius Vincentius lived for 55 years.
EDCS-23701332.summary: This inscription records the burial and ages of Ius SE (45) and Iulius Vincentius (55), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23701333.translation: Caius Iulius Urbanus lived for 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701333.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Iulius Urbanus, who lived to 50 and is buried here.

EDCS-23701334.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Felicia lived 75 years. May your bones rest well here.
EDCS-23701334.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Felicia, recording her lifespan and a wish for her repose.

EDCS-23701335.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulius Fortunatus lived 61 years.
EDCS-23701335.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Fortunatus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-23701337.translation: L. Pesurius' daughter Galla lived 75 years. She rests here.
EDCS-23701337.summary: A funerary epitaph for Galla, daughter of L. Pesurius, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-23701338.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Lollius Felix lived 15 years. He rests here. To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Lollius Faustus, son, lived 25 years. He rests here. To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Lollius Iunianus lived 25 years. He rests here. To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Quieta lived 71 years. She rests here. To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Lollius Faustus lived 85 years. May your bones rest well here. To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Lollius Rogatus lived 21 years. He rests here.
EDCS-23701338.summary: A complex funerary epitaph for multiple members of the Lollius family, recording their lifespans and burials.

EDCS-23701339.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcius Suriacus lived 65 years. To the spirits of the underworld. Lollia Urbana lived 30 years.
EDCS-23701339.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespans of Marcius Suriacus and Lollia Urbana.

EDCS-23701340.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Caius Manilius Crescentianus lived 25 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-23701340.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Manilius Crescentianus, recording his lifespan and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23701341.translation: Marcellina lived 75 years. She rests here. May you rest well here.
EDCS-23701341.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcellina, recording her lifespan and burial, with a wish for her repose.

EDCS-23701342.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Marius Saturus, son of Caius, of the Menenia tribe, lived 28 years. May your bones rest well here. To the spirits of the underworld. Statia Pontia lived 37 years. May your bones rest well here.
EDCS-23701342.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespans and burials of Lucius Marius Saturus and Statia Pontia.

EDCS-23701343.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Ius Maximina rests here. She lived 47 years.
EDCS-23701343.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ius Maximina, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-23701344.translation: Donaminia Daderis, daughter of SA II, rests here. To the spirits of the underworld. Ina, daughter of Iis, lived 55 years. She rests here. May her bones rest.
EDCS-23701344.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ina, daughter of Iis, recording her lifespan and burial, and also mentioning Donaminia Daderis.

EDCS-23701345.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Nonia Dativa lived 103 years; may your bones rest well here. Cilia lived 30 years; may your bones rest well here.
EDCS-23701345.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages at death of two individuals, Nonia Dativa and Cilia.

EDCS-23701346.translation: To the memory of Octavia Spesias, freedwoman of Octavia Marianilla; she lived 50 years.
EDCS-23701346.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name, former status, and age at death of Octavia Spesias, a freedwoman.

EDCS-23701347.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Manilia Basila lived 111 years; may your bones rest well here. To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Papirius Nampulus lived 61 years; may you rest well here. To the Spirits of the Departed. [---] Vor lived 75 years, buried here; may you rest well here. To the Spirits of the Departed. Caecilia Urbana lived 27 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23701347.summary: This funerary epitaph records the names and ages at death of four individuals: Manilia Basila, Marcus Papirius Nampulus, an unnamed man, and Caecilia Urbana.

EDCS-23701348.translation: [---]lius lived 25 years; may your bones rest well here. To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Papius Victor lived 17 years; may your bones rest well here. To the Spirits of the Departed. Papia Iulia lived 85 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23701348.summary: This funerary epitaph records the names and ages at death of three individuals, including Lucius Papius Victor and Papia Iulia.

EDCS-23701349.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Papia Pocilla Mura lived 1 year. To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Papius Decimi Primus lived 85 years; may your bones rest well here.
EDCS-23701349.summary: This funerary epitaph records the names and ages at death of two individuals: Papia Pocilla Mura and Lucius Papius Decimi Primus.

EDCS-23701350.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sitia Sorara lived 65 years; she is buried here; may your bones rest well here. To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Pompeus Felix lived 60 years; he is buried in this place.
EDCS-23701350.summary: This funerary epitaph records the names and ages at death of two individuals, Sitia Sorara and Caius Pompeus Felix.

EDCS-23701351.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Pompeius Ianuarius lived 40 years; he is buried here; may your bones rest well here. To the Spirits of the Departed. Altroatia Lucilla lived 71 years; may your bones rest well here. To the Spirits of the Departed. Felix Ospes lived 65 years; he is buried here; may you rest well here.
EDCS-23701351.summary: This funerary epitaph records the names and ages at death of three individuals: Lucius Pompeius Ianuarius, Altroatia Lucilla, and Felix Ospes.

EDCS-23701352.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Claudia Laeta lived 87 years; she is buried here. To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Pompeius Verus lived 85 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701352.summary: This funerary epitaph records the names and ages at death of two individuals, Claudia Laeta and Caius Pompeius Verus.

EDCS-23701353.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Pomponius lived 12 years. To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius [---] GENTIV lived 20 years; he is here.
EDCS-23701353.summary: This funerary epitaph records the names and ages at death of two individuals, Caius Pomponius and an incompletely preserved Caius.

EDCS-23701354.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Pontenia Felicia lived 80 years; she is buried here; may your bones rest well here.
EDCS-23701354.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name and age at death of Pontenia Felicia.

EDCS-23701355.translation: To the memory of Praetextatus, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-23701355.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Praetextatus, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-23701356.translation: Primus, who lived 21 years. Here he lies. May your bones rest well. Who lived 75 years. Here he lies. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701356.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Primus, with contradictory ages of death (21 and 75), and includes a wish for his bones to rest well.

EDCS-23701357.translation: To the departed spirits. Rogata, who lived 45 years. Here she lies.
EDCS-23701357.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the departed spirits for Rogata, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-23701358.summary: This inscription is dedicated to the departed spirits for Rogata, but the text is mostly illegible.

EDCS-23701359.translation: Seponia Secunda, daughter of Decimus, who lived 61 years. Here she lies.
EDCS-23701359.summary: This funerary inscription records that Seponia Secunda, daughter of Decimus, who lived 61 years, lies here.

EDCS-23701360.translation: To the departed spirits. Iosa. Who lived 35 years. Here she lies. May your bones rest well. Septimius Fi made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-23701360.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, records that Septimius Fi made it for his wife Iosa, who lived 35 years, and includes a wish for her bones to rest well.

EDCS-23701361.translation: Lucius Sittius Felix. 10. Here he lies.
EDCS-23701361.summary: This funerary inscription records that Lucius Sittius Felix, possibly aged 10, lies here.

EDCS-23701362.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Sittius Faustinianus, who lived 70 years. Here he lies. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701362.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the departed spirits for Marcus Sittius Faustinianus, who lived 70 years, and includes a wish for his bones to rest well.

EDCS-23701363.translation: To the departed spirits. Sittia Ianuaria, also known as Codimna, who lived 98 years. May you rest well. To the departed spirits.
EDCS-23701363.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the departed spirits for Sittia Ianuaria (also called Codimna), who lived 98 years, and includes a wish for her to rest well.

EDCS-23701364.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Titenius Crescentianus, who lived 25 years. Here he lies. To the departed spirits. Caius Titenius VVS, who lived [---] years. May your bones rest well. Sextus Titenius Messor, who lived 55 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701364.summary: This funerary inscription honors multiple individuals from the Titenius family, including Lucius Titenius Crescentianus (25 years), Caius Titenius VVS (age unknown), and Sextus Titenius Messor (55 years), with wishes for their bones to rest well.

EDCS-23701365.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Titenius Mustolus lived 71 years, is buried here. To the Spirits of the Dead. Titus Titenius Ianuarius lived 23 years, is buried here. To the Spirits of the Dead. Titenia Venusta lived 44 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23701365.summary: This tomb inscription records the burials and lifespans of three individuals: Quintus Titenius Mustolus, Titus Titenius Ianuarius, and Titenia Venusta.

EDCS-23701366.translation: Maxima Galitilla lived 52 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23701366.summary: This tomb inscription records the burial and lifespan of Maxima Galitilla.

EDCS-23701367.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Valeria Primula lived 60 years, may her bones rest well. IV NI O II I A lived. To the Spirits of the Dead. Titus Valerius E lived 21 years, is buried here. May his bones rest well. To the Spirits of the Dead. Valerius Getulus lived 75 years, is buried here. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-23701367.summary: This tomb inscription records the burials and lifespans of Valeria Primula, an individual whose name is partially illegible, Titus Valerius E, and Valerius Getulus, with a wish for their bones to rest well.

EDCS-23701368.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Valeria Ilua lived 35 years, here may her bones rest well. To the Spirits of the Dead. Numerius Valerius Iustus, son, lived 85 years, here may his bones rest well. To the Spirits of the Dead. Valeria Secunda lived 73 years, here may her bones rest well.
EDCS-23701368.summary: This tomb inscription records the burials and lifespans of Valeria Ilua, Numerius Valerius Iustus, and Valeria Secunda, with a wish for their bones to rest well.

EDCS-23701369.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Valerius Pudens lived 22 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23701369.summary: This tomb inscription records the burial and lifespan of Lucius Valerius Pudens.

EDCS-23701370.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Vergilius Satulus lived 21 years, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701370.summary: This tomb inscription records the lifespan of Vergilius Satulus and wishes for his bones to rest well.

EDCS-23701371.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. VergiGlia Urbana, daughter, lived 20 years, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701371.summary: This tomb inscription records the lifespan of VergiGlia Urbana, daughter, and wishes for her bones to rest well.

EDCS-23701372.translation: Daughter of Luci, Quirina Victoria lived 24 years, is buried here, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701372.summary: This tomb inscription records the burial and lifespan of Quirina Victoria, daughter of Luci, with a wish for her bones to rest well.

EDCS-23701373.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. VS IIVS lived 81 years.
EDCS-23701373.summary: This tomb inscription records the lifespan of an individual identified by the abbreviation VS IIVS.

EDCS-23701374.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aercia NV NGED
EDCS-23701374.summary: This inscription, dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead, appears to record the name Aercia but is significantly abbreviated or incomplete.

EDCS-23701375.translation: Titus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 50 years. May he rest well.
EDCS-23701375.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, tribal affiliation, and age at death of Titus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23701377.translation: To the memory of Lucius Gargilius Rogatus the Younger.
EDCS-23701377.summary: This is a funerary dedication commemorating Lucius Gargilius Rogatus the Younger, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23701379.translation: To the memory of Cassia Quieta.
EDCS-23701379.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Cassia Quieta, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23701380.translation: To the memory of Quintus Gargilius Felix Spiculus.
EDCS-23701380.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Quintus Gargilius Felix Spiculus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23701381.translation: To the memory of Lucius Gargilius Rogatus.
EDCS-23701381.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Lucius Gargilius Rogatus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23701382.translation: To the departed spirits, Celia Matrona.
EDCS-23701382.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Celia Matrona, invoking the Di Manes, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23701383.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaitanus lived 23 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701383.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, age at death, and burial location of Gaitanus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23701384.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Gaius Pontius Auctus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-23701384.summary: This is a religious dedication to Mercury Augustus by Gaius Pontius Auctus, recording the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23701385.translation: Licinia, daughter of Aulus, MITVA, lived 65 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23701385.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, filiation, and age at death of Licinia, likely from the Imperial period. The meaning of 'MITVA' is unclear.

EDCS-23701386.translation: Cecilia Procula, daughter of Publius, lived 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23701386.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, filiation, and age at death of Cecilia Procula, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23701387.translation: To the god ci ar, Marcus Aemilius Felix, master of the fort of Phuenses, on behalf of the public community of Phuenses.
EDCS-23701387.summary: This inscription records a dedication to a deity by a fort commander for the community of Phuenses, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23701388.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus, by the public community of Phuenses, Publius Alfius Clemens, master of the fort of Phuenses.
EDCS-23701388.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by a fort commander on behalf of the community of Phuenses, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23701389.translation: The people of Phuenses gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow to the god, under the magistracy of PQ.
EDCS-23701389.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to a deity by the people of Phuenses under a magistrate, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23701390.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus, by the public community of Phuenses, under the magistrate of the quarter, Quintus Iugurtha.
EDCS-23701390.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by the community of Phuenses, associated with a quarter magistrate, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23701391.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus, by Ca[---]us M., master of the quarter M[---].
EDCS-23701391.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by an official identified as a master of a quarter, with partially preserved names, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23701392.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus, by the public community of Phuenses, Caius Coelius Felix, master of the quarter.
EDCS-23701392.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by a quarter master on behalf of the community of Phuenses, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23701394.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus, by the public community of Phuenses, Lucius Claudius Avitus, master of the quarter.
EDCS-23701394.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by a quarter master on behalf of the community of Phuenses, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23701395.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus, by the public community of Phuenses, under the magistracy of Tiberius Claudius Honoratus.
EDCS-23701395.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by the community of Phuenses, associated with the magistracy of Tiberius Claudius Honoratus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23701396.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus, by the public community of Phuenses, under the magistracy of the quarter, Claudius Mansuetus.
EDCS-23701396.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by the community of Phuenses, associated with a quarter magistrate named Claudius Mansuetus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23701397.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus, by the public community of Phuenses, Lucius Clodius Florus, master of the quarter.
EDCS-23701397.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by a quarter master on behalf of the community of Phuenses, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23701398.translation: Sacred to the Divine Augustus, the local supervisor of the pagus, on behalf of his region, for Luciosia, paid his vow willingly.
EDCS-23701398.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by a local official to the Divine Augustus for his region.

EDCS-23701399.translation: Sacred to the Divine Augustus. Lucius Gaius, an Augustalis.
EDCS-23701399.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Divine Augustus by Lucius Gaius, identified as an Augustalis.

EDCS-23701400.translation: Sacred to the Divine Augustus. The state of the Phuenses, Lucius Curius Da[...], magister.
EDCS-23701400.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Divine Augustus by the state of Phuenses, with Lucius Curius Da[...] serving as magister.

EDCS-23701401.translation: Sacred to the Divine Augustus. Lucius Gabinius Cassianus, magister pagi.
EDCS-23701401.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Divine Augustus by Lucius Gabinius Cassianus, who held the office of magister pagi.

EDCS-23701402.translation: Sacred to the Divine Augustus. Lucius Iulius Antoninus and the people of Phuenses paid their vow willingly.
EDCS-23701402.summary: This inscription records a shared vow paid to the Divine Augustus by Lucius Iulius Antoninus and the people of Phuenses.

EDCS-23701403.translation: Sacred to the Divine Augustus. The state of Phuenses, under the magister Iulius Crescens.
EDCS-23701403.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Divine Augustus by the state of Phuenses, mentioning Iulius Crescens as magister.

EDCS-23701404.translation: Sacred to the Divine Augustus. The state of Phuenses, Caius Iulius Donatus, magister.
EDCS-23701404.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Divine Augustus by the state of Phuenses, with Caius Iulius Donatus serving as magister.

EDCS-23701405.translation: Sacred to the Divine Augustus. The state of Phuenses, Caius Iulius Extricatus, magister.
EDCS-23701405.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Divine Augustus by the state of Phuenses, with Caius Iulius Extricatus serving as magister.

EDCS-23701406.translation: Sacred to the Divine Augustus. The state of Phuenses paid its vow, Quintus Iulius Martialis Acinarius, magister.
EDCS-23701406.summary: This inscription records a vow paid to the Divine Augustus by the state of Phuenses, through their magister Quintus Iulius Martialis Acinarius.

EDCS-23701407.translation: Sacred to the Divine Augustus. The state of Phuenses, Quintus Iulius Martialis Iugurta, magister.
EDCS-23701407.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Divine Augustus by the state of Phuenses, with Quintus Iulius Martialis Iugurta serving as magister.

EDCS-23701408.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus; Magnius Phuenses, the local magistrate of the pagus.
EDCS-23701408.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by a local magistrate named Magnius Phuenses, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23701409.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus; the community of the Phuensians, Memmius Fortunatus, the local magistrate of the pagus, made a vow.
EDCS-23701409.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to the divine Augustus by Memmius Fortunatus, who served as the local magistrate for the community of Phuensium.

EDCS-23701410.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus; the community of the Phuensians, Lucius Memius Pacatianus, the local magistrate of the pagus.
EDCS-23701410.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by Lucius Memius Pacatianus, the local magistrate of the pagus of Phuensium.

EDCS-23701411.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus; the community of the Phuensians, Sextus Metilius Crescens, the local magistrate of the pagus.
EDCS-23701411.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by Sextus Metilius Crescens, who held the office of local magistrate for the community of Phuensium.

EDCS-23701412.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus; with Lucius Nonius Felix as magistrate, the people of Puensium.
EDCS-23701412.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus made by the people of Puensium, mentioning Lucius Nonius Felix as the local magistrate.

EDCS-23701413.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus; Lucius Nonius Felix Rotasius, the local magistrate of the pagus.
EDCS-23701413.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by Lucius Nonius Felix Rotasius, who was the local magistrate of the pagus.

EDCS-23701414.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus; with Lucius Nonius Urbanus as magistrate, the people of Phuenses.
EDCS-23701414.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by the people of Phuenses, indicating Lucius Nonius Urbanus as the local magistrate.

EDCS-23701415.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus; the community of the Phuensians, with Lucius S. Nepos as magistrate.
EDCS-23701415.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by the community of Phuensium, with Lucius S. Nepos serving as magistrate.

EDCS-23701416.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus; the community of the Phuensians, with Quintus Seius Fortunatus Novellianus as local magistrate of the pagus.
EDCS-23701416.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by the community of Phuensium, identifying Quintus Seius Fortunatus Novellianus as the local magistrate.

EDCS-23701417.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus; the community of the Phuensians, with Seius Ianuarius as local magistrate of the pagus.
EDCS-23701417.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by the community of Phuensium, mentioning Seius Ianuarius as the local magistrate.

EDCS-23701418.translation: Sacred to the deified Augustus. The community of the Phuesians, with Lucius Seius Nampulus as magistrate of the pagus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-23701418.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the deified Augustus by the community of the Phuesians, with Lucius Seius Nampulus as pagus magistrate, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-23701419.translation: Sacred to the deified Augustus. The Phuesians, with Lucius Sex. Crescens as magistrate of the pagus, fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-23701419.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the deified Augustus by the Phuesians, with Lucius Sex. Crescens as pagus magistrate, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-23701420.translation: Sacred to the deified Augustus. The community of the Phuesians, with Lucius Sittius Felix Eucrates as magistrate of the pagus.
EDCS-23701420.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deified Augustus by the community of the Phuesians, with Lucius Sittius Felix Eucrates as pagus magistrate, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-23701421.translation: Sacred to the deified Augustus. The community of the Phuesians, with Quintus Sittius Victor Lentinus as magistrate of the pagus.
EDCS-23701421.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deified Augustus by the community of the Phuesians, with Quintus Sittius Victor Lentinus as pagus magistrate, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-23701422.translation: Sacred to the deified Augustus. The community of the Phuesians, with Quintus V. Fortunatus as magistrate of the Phuesian pagus.
EDCS-23701422.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deified Augustus by the community of the Phuesians, with Quintus V. Fortunatus as pagus magistrate, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-23701423.translation: Sacred to the deified Augustus. The community of the Phuesians, with Publius Vellinius Crescens as magistrate of the pagus.
EDCS-23701423.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deified Augustus by the community of the Phuesians, with Publius Vellinius Crescens as pagus magistrate, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-23701427.translation: Sacred to the deified Augustus. The community of the Phuesians, with [---] F. as magistrate of the pagus.
EDCS-23701427.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deified Augustus by the community of the Phuesians, with an incomplete name for the pagus magistrate, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-23701431.summary: This inscription records the name of Cecilius Urbanus, identified as a 'magister', likely a local official, possibly from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23701432.summary: This inscription records the name of Claudius Honoratus, identified as a 'magister', likely a local official, possibly from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23701433.translation: To the deified Augustus. The Phuesians, with Magnio Victor as magistrate of the pagus, dedicated [---].
EDCS-23701433.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deified Augustus by the Phuesians, with Magnio Victor as pagus magistrate, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-23701434.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Magister Nonius Honoratus.

EDCS-23701435.translation: Sextus Seius Snocvs, Magister of the castle of Phuensium
EDCS-23701435.summary: This inscription records Sextus Seius Snocvs as the Magister of the castle of Phuensium.

EDCS-23701436.translation: Marcus Sittius Martialis, Magister of the Castle of Phuensium
EDCS-23701436.summary: This inscription records Marcus Sittius Martialis as the Magister of the Castle of Phuensium.

EDCS-23701438.translation: To VI HONO SIAIV DARNES, magister of Phuensium E
EDCS-23701438.summary: This inscription is a dedication to VI HONO SIAIV DARNES, who served as magister of Phuensium E.

EDCS-23701439.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Caius Cantinius.

EDCS-23701440.translation: Quinti Iuli Urbani, magister of the pagi
EDCS-23701440.summary: This inscription records Quinti Iuli Urbani as the magister of the pagi.

EDCS-23701441.translation: Quintus Euticus, son of Quintus, fulfilled his vow willingly and happily with his family.
EDCS-23701441.summary: This is a votive inscription by Quintus Euticus, son of Quintus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-23701449.summary: This is likely a funerary epitaph for Quintus Titurnius Fortunatus.

EDCS-23701451.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Cassius Gracilis, veteran, lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701451.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Cassius Gracilis, a veteran who lived 70 years.

EDCS-23701452.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Ummidius Nerullinus Chullitanus, son of Lucius, lived 63 years; he is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-23701452.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Ummidius Nerullinus Chullitanus, son of Lucius, who lived 63 years.

EDCS-23701453.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antia Regilla lived 95 years.
EDCS-23701453.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Antia Regilla.

EDCS-23701454.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius C. Severinus, 85 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-23701454.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age and burial of Lucius C. Severinus.

EDCS-23701455.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for the memory of Julia, lived 21 years.
EDCS-23701455.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Julia.

EDCS-23701456.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cecilia Katula lived 65 years.
EDCS-23701456.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Cecilia Katula.

EDCS-23701457.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Honorata lived 23 years and is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701457.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Caecilia Honorata, with a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-23701458.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Luciosa lived 75 years.
EDCS-23701458.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Caecilia Luciosa.

EDCS-23701459.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Cassius Polic lived [X] years.
EDCS-23701459.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the partial lifespan of Marcus Cassius Polic.

EDCS-23701460.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Cassius Severianus lived 30 years; may he rest well here.
EDCS-23701460.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gaius Cassius Severianus, with a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-23701461.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Casia Acuta lived 27 years.
EDCS-23701461.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Casia Acuta.

EDCS-23701462.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Casia Rogata lived 19 years.
EDCS-23701462.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Casia Rogata.

EDCS-23701463.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudia Liceala lived 91 years. May she rest well here.
EDCS-23701463.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Claudia Liceala.

EDCS-23701464.translation: To the memory of Codenis. He lived 6 years.
EDCS-23701464.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Codenis, likely a child.

EDCS-23701465.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Cornelius Crescens lived 107 years.
EDCS-23701465.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Quintus Cornelius Crescens.

EDCS-23701466.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Deciria Rogata lived 45 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23701466.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Deciria Rogata.

EDCS-23701468.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Fabius Calus lived 115 years. May he rest well here.
EDCS-23701468.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Quintus Fabius Calus.

EDCS-23701469.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Fabius Maximus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 61 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-23701469.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Quintus Fabius Maximus, including his father and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-23701470.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Fabius Messor lived 26 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-23701470.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Fabius Messor.

EDCS-23701471.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fabius Modestus lived 61 years.
EDCS-23701471.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Fabius Modestus.

EDCS-23701472.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fabia Paula lived 18 years.
EDCS-23701472.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Fabia Paula.

EDCS-23701473.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [---] lived 65 years. To the spirits of the departed. Fabia Rogate lived 55 years.
EDCS-23701473.summary: A fragmented funerary epitaph that records the lifespans of two individuals, one unidentified and Fabia Rogate.

EDCS-23701474.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fabia Urbana lived 85 years. May she rest well here.
EDCS-23701474.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabia Urbana, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-23701475.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fulvia Gaetula, daughter of Quintus, lived 81 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23701475.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fulvia Gaetula, daughter of Quintus, who lived 81 years.

EDCS-23701476.translation: Gabinia Rogata lived 41 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23701476.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gabinia Rogata, who lived 41 years.

EDCS-23701477.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gargilia Luciosa lived 33 years. May her bones lie well here.
EDCS-23701477.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gargilia Luciosa, who lived 33 years.

EDCS-23701478.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Erenia Estricata lived 40 years.
EDCS-23701478.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Erenia Estricata, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-23701479.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus I Proclus lived 61 years.

EDCS-23701761.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pactumeia Hedone, who died at age 25.

EDCS-23701762.translation: Propertia Rogata lived 70 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23701762.summary: A funerary epitaph for Propertia Rogata, who died at age 70.

EDCS-23701763.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Sempronius lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701763.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Sempronius, who died at age 40.

EDCS-23701767.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Profuturus lived 67 years; he spent 35 years on these estates with his father Butra, and after his father's death, 22 years. Imber is buried here with his most worthy brother.
EDCS-23701767.summary: A funerary epitaph for Profuturus, detailing his lifespan and time spent on an estate with his father, and also noting the burial of Imber with his brother.

EDCS-23701768.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fuficia Secura lived 15 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23701768.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Fuficia Secura at age 15.

EDCS-23701769.translation: Fusca Saiuris lived 110 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23701769.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Fusca Saiuris at the advanced age of 110.

EDCS-23701772.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Statius Caecilius Butra lived 70 years and spent 25 years on these estates; his wife Antiochis, with her sisters, placed this here for their most deserving father, who is buried here.
EDCS-23701772.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Statius Caecilius Butra, who lived 70 years and managed estates for 25 years, dedicated by his wife and sisters.

EDCS-23701773.translation: The public community of the fortress of Zugal, devoted to the divine power and majesty of our lord the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, son of the deified Magnus Antoninus Pius, grandson of the deified Pius Severus, dedicated this with public funds.
EDCS-23701773.summary: A dedication by the public community of the fortress of Zugal to Emperor Severus Alexander, likely dating to his reign (222-235 CE).

EDCS-23701774.translation: For the health of the Emperor Caesar, Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, son of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power V, consul, the most fortunate and courageous princeps, father of the fatherland, proconsul, most indulgent over all princes.
EDCS-23701774.summary: A lengthy titulature for Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus (Elagabalus), likely part of a larger inscription related to imperial well-being.

EDCS-23701775.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tiberius Claudius os, having served in all honors, lived 75 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701775.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius os, who lived 75 years and held various offices, with a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-23701776.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Aemilius Munianus lived 109 years; may you rest well here; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701776.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Aemilius Munianus, who lived to the exceptional age of 109.

EDCS-23701777.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Catullina lived 79 years; she is placed in this spot; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701777.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aemilia Catullina, who lived 79 years, with a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-23701778.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Dative lived 55 years.

EDCS-23701779.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Honorata lived 62 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23701779.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Aemilia Honorata at age 62.

EDCS-23701780.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Alfius Martialis lived 27 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701780.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Alfius Martialis, recording his age at death and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23701781.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Alfia Citosa lived 20 years. She is buried here.

EDCS-23701782.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Annius Claudianus the Younger lived 62 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-23701783.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Arvivius Valens lived 71 years. He made this in his lifetime.

EDCS-23701784.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Auleus Processianus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 17 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701784.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Auleus Processianus, detailing his age, tribal affiliation, burial location, and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23701785.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sextus Claudius Claudianus lived 54 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.

EDCS-23701789.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Flavius Vitalis lived 110 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-23701790.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Fortunata, daughter of Nampamonis, lived 25 years.

EDCS-23701797.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavius Iucundus lived 20 years; may your bones rest well.

EDCS-23701798.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Iulius Clarus lived 28 years.

EDCS-23701815.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Titus Pontius Isidorus lived 51 years; he is buried in this place.

EDCS-23701817.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Sertorius lived 85 years.

EDCS-23701821.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sittia Helena, a most splendid memory, lived for 103 years; laid in this place, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701821.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sittia Helena, who lived to be 103 years old.

EDCS-23701823.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Staberia Matrona lived for 80 years.
EDCS-23701823.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Staberia Matrona, who lived for 80 years.

EDCS-23701824.translation: Sufidia Honorata, Sufidius Optatus for their most rare and dutiful daughter, lived for 5 years; she is laid here.

EDCS-23701825.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sulpicia Matronica lived for 55 years.
EDCS-23701825.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sulpicia Matronica, who lived for 55 years.

EDCS-23701826.translation: Titurnius Vitalis lived for 80 years; he is laid here.
