EDCS-23701827.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vettius Goden lived for 8 years.
EDCS-23701827.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vettius Goden, who lived for 8 years.

EDCS-23701828.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Volussius Castus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 95 years.
EDCS-23701828.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Volussius Castus, son of Caius, who lived for 95 years.

EDCS-23701829.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sextus Volussius Rogatus, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 24 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-23701829.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Volussius Rogatus, son of Sextus, who lived for 24 years.

EDCS-23701832.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an individual identified by the initials S. Ti., who lived for 65 years.

EDCS-23701834.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death of an unnamed woman.

EDCS-23701835.translation: He lived 41 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23701835.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death and burial of an unnamed man.

EDCS-23701839.translation: Quintus Iulius Minucianus lived for 59 years, 6 months, and 31 days. In peace.
EDCS-23701839.summary: This funerary inscription records the full lifespan of Quintus Iulius Minucianus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23701840.translation: Sacred to Caelestis Augusta, for the health of our Caius Arrius Antoninus, Antonius Philetus gladly fulfilled his vow and made temples at his own expense and dedicated them.
EDCS-23701840.summary: This is a votive inscription by Antonius Philetus to the goddess Caelestis, likely from the Roman imperial period, dedicating temples for the health of Caius Arrius Antoninus.

EDCS-23701843.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aemilia Petita lived 28 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23701843.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death and burial of Aemilia Petita.

EDCS-23701844.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Aurellius lived 69 years.
EDCS-23701844.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death of Caius Aurellius.

EDCS-23701845.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus GaNlerius Marcellinus lived 25 years.
EDCS-23701845.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death of Quintus GaNlerius Marcellinus.

EDCS-23701846.translation: Aurelius BV VBRI lived 8 years, most rare.
EDCS-23701846.summary: This funerary inscription records the age at death of Aurelius BV VBRI, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23701847.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Fittau Feriusis lived 115 years. Aurelia Vedera made this for her most worthy father.
EDCS-23701847.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the extreme age at death of Fittau Feriusis and made by his daughter Aurelia Vedera.

EDCS-23701849.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Decrius Fronto, son of Quirina, lived 30 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701849.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death and burial of Decrius Fronto, son of Quirina.

EDCS-23701850.translation: Gaius Decrius, son of Sextus, lived 17 years.
EDCS-23701850.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gaius Decrius.

EDCS-23701851.translation: Fatrica, daughter of Ductus, lived 25 years.
EDCS-23701851.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Fatrica.

EDCS-23701854.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Pompeia Martilla lived 80 years; she rests here.
EDCS-23701854.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Pompeia Martilla.

EDCS-23701856.translation: Salutarii, son of Danus Cresconius, freedman of Arcentium, Fidelis, victor of 7 battles. Cessonius Diaz Castus, freedman Pompeianus, N.
EDCS-23701856.summary: This inscription seems to record military honors and achievements for several individuals, possibly a monument to soldiers or a dedication.

EDCS-23701857.translation: Altus Pompeianus, you are unique, you soar like a mountain. Pullentianus Delicatus, may you conquer or not conquer, we love you. Polydoxe Titas Scholasticus, Incredula, you came with good things.
EDCS-23701857.summary: This inscription appears to be a collection of dedicatory phrases or personal remarks, possibly for a monument or a group of friends, with personal names and affectionate sentiments.

EDCS-23701858.translation: Of the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, father of the fatherland, unt...
EDCS-23701858.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription likely dedicating a monument or structure in honor of Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-23701859.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Iulius Licinianus.

EDCS-23701860.translation: Sacred to the Good Gods. Quintus Amalasius Ipaliarius, entered as priest of chastity.
EDCS-23701860.summary: This is a dedication to the 'Good Gods' by Quintus Amalasius Ipaliarius, who served as a priest of chastity.

EDCS-23701861.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iulia Extricata lived 75 years.
EDCS-23701861.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Iulia Extricata.

EDCS-23701862.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Lucia lived 60 years; she rests here.
EDCS-23701862.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Lucia.

EDCS-23701863.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius M. Crescens lived 95 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23701863.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Lucius M. Crescens.

EDCS-23701864.translation: Quintus Mucatrali, son of [---], an equestrian of the I Ala of Pannonians, under the turma of Liccaius, aged 35, a Thracian by nationality, served for 17 years. He is buried here. Placed by Spinus Mucacenti, son of [---], an equestrian of the same ala, his heir.
EDCS-23701864.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Mucatrali, a Thracian cavalryman who served for 17 years, detailing his rank, unit, age at death, and nationality, and identifying his heir and the monument's dedicator.

EDCS-23701865.translation: Talanus, son of Surnis, equestrian, standard-bearer.
EDCS-23701865.summary: An epitaph or inscription identifying Talanus as an equestrian and standard-bearer, likely a soldier.

EDCS-23701866.translation: Etinia Mausa, his wife, lived [---]. Etinia Mausa.
EDCS-23701866.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription mentioning Etinia Mausa, possibly as the wife of the deceased.

EDCS-23701867.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Urbana lived 100 years.
EDCS-23701867.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the exceptional lifespan of Iulia Urbana.

EDCS-23701868.summary: A very brief inscription possibly identifying an individual named Marnita Suburbur and a designation 'Colonus TVC', the meaning of which is unclear.

EDCS-23701872.translation: Burial place of Marcus Allius Victor. He lived 71 years. He is buried here.

EDCS-23701873.summary: This is a tombstone commemorating Annia Sapida, who lived to be 70 years old.

EDCS-23701874.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the Divine Manes in memory of Cornelia Monica, who lived to 76 and is buried here.

EDCS-23701875.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Honoratus, the son, lived for 60 years.

EDCS-23701878.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. In memory of Quinta Iulia, who lived for 70 years. She is buried here.

EDCS-23701893.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Sittia Spes, who lived 18 years.

EDCS-23701902.translation: [...atton] lived 100 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701902.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan of an individual identified only by the end of their name, who lived for 100 years.

EDCS-23701903.summary: This funerary epitaph states that Iulia Gida lived for 75 years.

EDCS-23701906.summary: This epitaph records that Claudius Agathopus lived for 40 years.

EDCS-23701907.translation: SELV lived 65 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701907.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of an individual identified by the abbreviation SELV, who lived for 65 years.

EDCS-23701909.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavia Melita, daughter of Lucius, lived 25 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701909.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Flavia Melita, daughter of Lucius, who lived for 25 years, and includes a wish for her peaceful repose.

EDCS-23701911.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caius Papirius Messor lived 115 years.
EDCS-23701911.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Caius Papirius Messor, who lived for 115 years.

EDCS-23701912.translation: Aulus Morasius Oclatianus, for the Dacia [province], [dedicated this] in memory. Aelius lived 53 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701912.summary: This inscription, possibly commemorative or funerary, mentions Aulus Morasius Oclatianus and Aelius, who lived for 53 years and is buried here, with a reference to the province of Dacia.

EDCS-23701913.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Edinius Rusticus lived 71 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701913.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Edinius Rusticus, dated by the phrasing and typical funerary formula.

EDCS-23701916.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Clodius Sacxo lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701916.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clodius Sacxo, indicating his age and burial place.

EDCS-23701917.translation: Lucius Iulius Sarnus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, descendant of Calidus, lived 75 years; he is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701917.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Iulius Sarnus, providing his name, tribal affiliation, patronymic, age, and a wish for his remains.

EDCS-23701918.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph for a woman named Iulia, recording her name and age.

EDCS-23701919.translation: Anitia Novella, reminded in a dream, willingly paid her vow to Augusta Diana.
EDCS-23701919.summary: This inscription records Anitia Novella fulfilling a vow to the goddess Diana Augusta, likely after a divine prompting.

EDCS-23701920.translation: To the genius of the colony of Milevum, from the will of Publius Sittius Adiutor, augur, 2000 sesterces, administered by Sittia Vitalis, his daughter.
EDCS-23701920.summary: This inscription records a bequest of 2000 sesterces to the genius of Milevum, made from the will of Publius Sittius Adiutor and administered by his daughter.

EDCS-23701922.translation: Caius Clodius Surus, son of Caius, of the Surus tribe, [dedicated this] on a good day.
EDCS-23701922.summary: This inscription records Caius Clodius Surus dedicating something, possibly on a specific auspicious day.

EDCS-23701923.translation: Publius Amp C willingly paid his vow to Saturn.
EDCS-23701923.summary: This inscription records Publius Amp C fulfilling a vow to the god Saturn.

EDCS-23701925.translation: Caius Flavius Modestus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-23701925.summary: This inscription records Caius Flavius Modestus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-23701926.translation: Caius Iulius Contentus paid his vow.
EDCS-23701926.summary: This inscription records Caius Iulius Contentus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-23701927.translation: Gaius Julius Pudens willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-23701927.summary: This inscription records Gaius Julius Pudens fulfilling a vow, likely on a religious monument.

EDCS-23701928.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying Iulia as the daughter of Publius.

EDCS-23701929.translation: Sextius Donus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-23701929.summary: This inscription records Sextius Donus fulfilling a vow, likely on a religious monument.

EDCS-23701930.translation: Publius Sittius Senex, son of Catuari, willingly and with a ready spirit fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-23701930.summary: This inscription records Publius Sittius Senex fulfilling a vow, likely on a religious monument.

EDCS-23701931.summary: This inscription records Sittius Travclaus fulfilling a vow, likely on a religious monument.

EDCS-23701932.summary: This inscription records Lucius Terentius fulfilling a vow, likely on a religious monument.

EDCS-23701933.translation: Sacred to Augustus. Gaius Ferrius Viator willingly and with a ready spirit fulfilled his vow, with the curators, sons.
EDCS-23701933.summary: This inscription, dedicated to Augustus, records Gaius Ferrius Viator fulfilling a vow, with mention of curators and sons.

EDCS-23701938.translation: For the safety of Augustus. CV FORMA ANIAS O. With Sextilius Quetus and Julius Sat as curator.
EDCS-23701938.summary: This inscription records a dedication for the safety of Augustus, mentioning officials including a curator.

EDCS-23701941.translation: To Quintus Caecilius Laetus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, a most distinguished man, proconsul of the province of Baetica, a sodalis Augustalis, legate of the XIII Gemina legion, curator of the colony of Pisaurensium, curator of the colony of Formianum, praetor.
EDCS-23701941.summary: This lengthy inscription lists the impressive career of Quintus Caecilius Laetus, highlighting his many prestigious civic and military roles.

EDCS-23701957.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. For Turanius Commodus, who served as aedile, augur three times, as a prefect with judicial power in the colony of Rusicada and in the colony of Chullu, and twice in the colony of Milev, a quinquennial official, having also paid the contribution by the people of Cirta, again in his home colony of Milev as chief magistrate for life, for his merits in office and in public life, which were offered to him by suffrage, he lived for 60 years. Here he is buried. May your bones rest well. Turania Cassia Commodilla to her most devoted father.
EDCS-23701957.summary: This tomb inscription honors Turanius Commodus, detailing his extensive public service in various colonies and his lifetime appointment as chief magistrate, and is dedicated by his daughter.

EDCS-23701958.translation: Valeria Pupa, daughter of Lucius, priestess of Ceres, lived 82 years.
EDCS-23701958.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Pupa, a priestess of Ceres, noting her name, parentage, priesthood, and age at death.

EDCS-23701960.translation: Quintus Granius Martialis Latiarius, with 25 years of service, cared for by his son.
EDCS-23701960.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Granius Martialis Latiarius, mentioning his military service and the care of his son.

EDCS-23701961.translation: Lucius Sicinius Gallus, with 25 years of service, cared for by his sons Sicinius and L. (and others?).
EDCS-23701961.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Sicinius Gallus, noting his military service and mentioning his sons.

EDCS-23701962.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Fabius Solutor, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, a certain and faithful friend among friends, originally from Rusicade, lived 100 years.
EDCS-23701962.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Fabius Solutor of Rusicade, noting his tribal affiliation, character, and age at death.

EDCS-23701963.summary: This is a brief inscription identifying Valerius Sodalis as an assistant, likely on a monument or as a dedication.

EDCS-23701965.translation: C. Silon, may your life be everlasting. If you ask who I am, I am Iulia Vitalis, once your dearest wife. May your virtue remain with me forever. In vain the light which is quickly completed; and Phoebe, if she had brought true things to your mind, and CN. Q. CO... we could have snatched (you) from fate, only having added ten (years?) and the number n... which at that time light at opt Iulia Vitalis.
EDCS-23701965.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from a wife, Iulia Vitalis, to her deceased husband, C. Silon, lamenting his early death and praising his virtue, with considerable damage and difficulty in interpretation.

EDCS-23701966.translation: To Caecilia Maia, daughter of Quintus, his deserving wife, who lived 39 years. She rests here. May her bones rest well. Quintus Aemilius Iuciundus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 16 years. He rests here. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-23701966.summary: This is a paired funerary epitaph for Caecilia Maia and her son Quintus Aemilius Iuciundus, providing their names, parentage, ages at death, and wishes for their rest.

EDCS-23701967.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Aemilius Saturninus lived 21 years. He rests here. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-23701967.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Aemilius Saturninus, noting his name, age at death, and a wish for his rest.

EDCS-23701968.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Optata, noting her name and age at death.

EDCS-23701969.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aemilia Placida lived 25 years. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-23701969.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Placida, noting her name, age at death, and a wish for her rest.

EDCS-23701970.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Annius Cassius lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701970.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Annius Cassius, who lived 50 years and is buried here.

EDCS-23701971.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Antistius Saturninus lived 5 years.
EDCS-23701971.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Antistius Saturninus, who lived 5 years.

EDCS-23701972.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antonia Urbana.
EDCS-23701972.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Urbana.

EDCS-23701973.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Apertius Sodalis lived [X] years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701973.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Apertius Sodalis, recording his lifespan and burial place.

EDCS-23701975.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Apronius Felix, who lived 22 years.

EDCS-23701976.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sacred to Arisia Clara, daughter of Sextus. She lived 20 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23701976.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Arisia Clara, daughter of Sextus, who lived 20 years and is buried here.

EDCS-23701977.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sacred to Quintus Arrius Felicianus. He lived 91 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701977.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Arrius Felicianus, who lived 91 years and is buried here.

EDCS-23701978.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Arria Victoria.
EDCS-23701978.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Arria Victoria.

EDCS-23701979.translation: Lucius Atilius Catulus lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23701979.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Atilius Catulus, who lived 35 years and is buried here.

EDCS-23701980.translation: To the spirits of the departed. B. Sura [lived/is buried].
EDCS-23701980.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for B. Sura, whose full details are lost.

EDCS-23701981.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Caecilius Felicio lived 41 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-23701981.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Caecilius Felicio, who lived 41 years.

EDCS-23701982.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Caecilius Florentinus lived 15 years.
EDCS-23701982.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Caecilius Florentinus, who lived 15 years.

EDCS-23701983.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilius Rogatus, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-23701984.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caecilia Quirina, daughter of Quitus, lived 80 years. She is laid here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701984.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilia Quirina, daughter of Quitus, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-23701985.translation: To the Divine Shades. Calpurnia Vitalis lived 61 years.
EDCS-23701985.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Calpurnia Vitalis, who lived 61 years.

EDCS-23701986.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Cassius Tueusius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived [unspecified] years. Lucius Tannonius Crescens dedicated this altar to his most dutiful son, who lived [unspecified] years.
EDCS-23701986.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Lucius Tannonius Crescens to his son, Lucius Cassius Tueusius.

EDCS-23701987.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Casicanus lived 60 days. Valeria Tertia, his mother, placed this for her son.
EDCS-23701987.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Casicanus, who lived 60 days, set up by his mother Valeria Tertia.

EDCS-23701988.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Publius Claudius.

EDCS-23701989.translation: Claudius Amphionis, son of Amphion, by nationality [---]
EDCS-23701989.summary: This fragmentary inscription identifies Claudius Amphionis by his father and nationality.

EDCS-23701990.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Claudius Muriclus lived 80 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-23701990.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Claudius Muriclus, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-23701991.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Claudius Urbanus lived 51 years.
EDCS-23701991.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Claudius Urbanus.

EDCS-23701992.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Clodius Pugel lived 30 years.
EDCS-23701992.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Quintus Clodius Pugel.

EDCS-23701993.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Clodius Silvanus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 60 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701993.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Clodius Silvanus, including a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-23701995.translation: Crescens, son of Caius, lived 13 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701995.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the short life of Crescens, son of Caius, with a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-23701996.translation: Crescentianus Martialis Felicianus for his most dutiful father.
EDCS-23701996.summary: A dedication by Crescentianus Martialis Felicianus to his father.

EDCS-23701997.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Daphnus.

EDCS-23701998.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Domitius Maximus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 50 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23701998.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Domitius Maximus, stating his age and burial place, with a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-23701999.translation: Epidia Urbanilla, daughter of Quintus, was placed here.
EDCS-23701999.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Epidia Urbanilla.

EDCS-23702000.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Extricatus lived 105 years.
EDCS-23702000.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the exceptionally long lifespan of Extricatus.

EDCS-23702001.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Victor lived 25 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23702001.summary: A funerary epitaph for Victor, stating his age and burial place.

EDCS-23702002.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Saturnina lived for 36 years.
EDCS-23702002.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Saturnina.

EDCS-23702003.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Fabius Crispinus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 70 years; he is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23702003.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Quintus Fabius Crispinus.

EDCS-23702004.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Fabius Tannonianus lived for 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23702004.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Quintus Fabius Tannonianus.

EDCS-23702005.translation: Fabia Fausta, daughter of Caius, lived for 80 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23702005.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Fabia Fausta.

EDCS-23702006.translation: Fabia Matrona lived for 60 years; she is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23702006.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Fabia Matrona.

EDCS-23702007.translation: Fabiana Sperata, daughter of Lucius, lived for 30 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23702007.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Fabiana Sperata.

EDCS-23702008.translation: Falcidia Secunda lived for 51 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23702008.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Falcidia Secunda.

EDCS-23702009.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Favonius Nampamo lived for 115 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23702009.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the exceptionally long lifespan of Quintus Favonius Nampamo.

EDCS-23702011.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Ferrius Avitus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 80 years.
EDCS-23702011.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Ferrius Avitus.

EDCS-23702012.translation: Flavius Harmanes lived for 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23702012.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Flavius Harmanes.

EDCS-23702013.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Flavius Municeps.
EDCS-23702013.summary: A funerary dedication to Flavius Municeps.

EDCS-23702014.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Flavia Eutychia.

EDCS-23702015.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Aulus Florius Belicus lived 61 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23702015.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Aulus Florius Belicus.

EDCS-23702016.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Fulvia Euplia.

EDCS-23702017.translation: Gargilia Clementis, daughter of Mustia, lived 27 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23702017.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Gargilia Clementis.

EDCS-23702018.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Iulius Alexander lived 90 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23702018.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Iulius Alexander.

EDCS-23702019.translation: Iulius Arator lived 10 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23702019.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Iulius Arator.

EDCS-23702020.translation: Quintus Iulius Arnus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 70 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23702020.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Quintus Iulius Arnus, noting his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-23702021.translation: Iulius Crescens lived 52 years, is buried here. [S L V I L I?]
EDCS-23702021.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Iulius Crescens, with a partially illegible addition.

EDCS-23702022.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Marcus Iulius Faustus lived 40 years.
EDCS-23702022.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Marcus Iulius Faustus.

EDCS-23801665.translation: Sacred to the Genius of the Head of Amsaga. Gaius Arruntius Faustus, son of Arruntius Proculus, with the permission of the town council, made this at his own expense and dedicated it with a willing heart.
EDCS-23801665.summary: This is a dedication by a magistrate to the Genius of the Head of Amsaga, likely a local deity or natural feature, funded by himself.

EDCS-23801668.translation: To the memory of the land of Barbarinus.
EDCS-23801668.summary: This is a dedication or memorial related to a piece of land belonging to someone named Barbarinus.

EDCS-23801669.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Antonius Victoricus lived 26 years. To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Antonius Faustus lived 23 years.
EDCS-23801669.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the ages of two individuals, Quintus Antonius Victoricus and Quintus Antonius Faustus.

EDCS-23801670.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Arruntius Faustus lived 19 years.
EDCS-23801670.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the age of Gaius Arruntius Faustus.

EDCS-23801672.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus for the health of Lucilius Princinianus and Martialis and Tertullus. Iulius Herennius Primosus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-23801672.summary: This is a votive inscription by Iulius Herennius Primosus to Silvanus Augustus for the well-being of several individuals.

EDCS-23801673.translation: Lumis lived 65 years. Gaius Fabius Vasentinus lived 60 years.
EDCS-23801673.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the ages of two individuals, Lumis and Gaius Fabius Vasentinus.

EDCS-23801674.translation: Titus Flavius Iulianus is buried here. May your bones rest well. He lived 80 years.
EDCS-23801674.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Titus Flavius Iulianus, expressing a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-23801675.translation: Galla Publiana lived 105 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23801675.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the long life and burial of Galla Publiana.

EDCS-23801676.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Herennius Florus lived 100 years, for himself and his family.
EDCS-23801676.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Herennius Florus, who lived to 100 and is buried with his family, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801677.translation: Guddus Monistellus lived 105 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23801677.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the long life and burial of Guddus Monistellus.

EDCS-23801678.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Maximus lived 101 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801678.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Gaius Julius Maximus, who lived 101 years.

EDCS-23801679.translation: Gaius Julius Rufinus lived 30 years; he rests well here.
EDCS-23801679.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Gaius Julius Rufinus, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-23801680.translation: Julius Victoricus made [this] for his parents.
EDCS-23801680.summary: An inscription recording that Julius Victoricus dedicated a monument to his parents.

EDCS-23801681.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Saturiscus lived 37 years; buried here.
EDCS-23801681.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Gaius Julius Saturiscus, who lived 37 years.

EDCS-23801682.translation: Lucius Julius Severus lived 85 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801682.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Lucius Julius Severus, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-23801683.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Manlius Iaravius lived 40 years. A[...].
EDCS-23801683.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Manlius Iaravius, who lived 40 years, with an incomplete addition.

EDCS-23801684.translation: To the memory of Julia Crescentia, the most modest mother, always to be named by women, a matron, and by men.
EDCS-23801684.summary: A dedication honoring Julia Crescentia as a model mother and matron.

EDCS-23801685.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Julia Novella, who lived 27 years.

EDCS-23801686.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Novela lived 25 years.
EDCS-23801686.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Iulia Novela, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-23801687.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Hospita lived 50 years. Titus Antonius Faustus lived 100 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801687.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the deaths of Iulia Hospita (50 years) and Titus Antonius Faustus (100 years).

EDCS-23801688.translation: Iulia Procula lived for 30 years, together with her four children; here she lies.
EDCS-23801688.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Procula, recording her lifespan and her children, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801689.translation: To Marcellina, my most rare wife, who lived for 55 years.
EDCS-23801689.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Marcellina by her husband, recording her lifespan, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801690.translation: To the memory of Publius Sextius Clementianus, Roman knight. Marcus P. Bo made this.
EDCS-23801690.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Publius Sextius Clementianus, a Roman knight, by his heir Marcus P. Bo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801692.translation: [Name] son of Titus lived for 40 years.
EDCS-23801692.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of an individual identified by his father's name, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801694.translation: [Name] lived for 70 years; here he lies.
EDCS-23801694.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of an individual, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801696.translation: From the children of [Name], loyal and faithful; pray for us.
EDCS-23801696.summary: A funerary dedication made by the children of a person or persons, likely from the Roman Imperial period, with a plea for prayers.

EDCS-23801697.translation: Iulius Victor lived for 25 years; here lie his bones. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801697.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Victor, recording his lifespan and wishing peace for his remains, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801698.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Extricata lived for 61 years.
EDCS-23801698.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits for Iulia Extricata, recording her lifespan, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801699.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Licinius Crescens lived for 40 years. Rufina made this during her lifetime.
EDCS-23801699.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits for Licinius Crescens, recording his lifespan and noting that it was made by Rufina during her lifetime, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801700.translation: Marcella lived for 80 years; here she lies. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801700.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcella, recording her lifespan and wishing peace for her remains, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801701.translation: To the memory of Quintus S Saturninus. Aemiliae Fida made this for her most loving husband. He lived 45 years.
EDCS-23801701.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commemorating Quintus S Saturninus, dedicated by his wife Aemiliae Fida.

EDCS-23801702.translation: To Philoccalus, her deserving husband. Aurelia made this, through her freedman Marius. He lived 76 years.
EDCS-23801702.summary: This funerary epitaph records Philoccalus, with his wife Aurelia as the dedicator and Marius as the agent.

EDCS-23801706.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavius Fuliqus lived 35 years and some months.
EDCS-23801706.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Flavius Fuliqus, noting his age at death.

EDCS-23801707.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Novius Catulinus lived 95 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801707.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Novius Catulinus, stating his age and burial location.

EDCS-23801709.translation: A soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, he served for 6 years and lived 29 years. May your bones rest well. Vitalis lived 35 years.
EDCS-23801709.summary: This epitaph records a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion and also Vitalis, likely two separate burials or a complex funerary monument.

EDCS-23801710.translation: Publius Iulius Quintulus lived 17 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well. In memory of Caius Iulius Rufinus, who lived 19 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well. Sacred to the departed spirits. Kalpurni Rufina, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801710.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription commemorating at least three individuals: Publius Iulius Quintulus, Caius Iulius Rufinus, and Kalpurni Rufina.

EDCS-23801711.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Volussius Primus lived 30 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801711.summary: A funerary dedication for Quintus Volussius Primus, noting his age and invoking peace for his remains.

EDCS-23801712.translation: Manilia Martis lived 55 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801712.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph recording the life span and burial place of Manilia Martis.

EDCS-23801713.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia, daughter of Caius, Germana, lived 51 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801713.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aemilia Germana, daughter of Caius, detailing her age and burial.

EDCS-23801715.translation: To the memory of Naevia, daughter of Asipaenis, our sweetest wife. She lived 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801715.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Naevia, described as a wife and daughter, stating her age and burial.

EDCS-23801716.translation: To the spirits of the departed; Fabia Spica lived 45 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801716.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fabia Spica, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-23801717.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marciana, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-23801718.summary: A funerary epitaph for Volumnius Quintillis, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-23801719.translation: Q. I. C. N. A. I. F. willingly with spirit.
EDCS-23801719.summary: An inscription possibly recording a votive offering or dedication, with an abbreviated name or formula, stating it was done willingly.

EDCS-23801720.translation: The birthday of the martyrs Nivalis, Matrona, and Salvius; the 9th day before the Ides of November.
EDCS-23801720.summary: A memorial inscription for the martyrs Nivalis, Matrona, and Salvius, noting their feast day.

EDCS-23801721.translation: Nivalis, Matrona, Salvius; Fortunatus fulfilled what he promised.
EDCS-23801721.summary: A votive inscription where Fortunatus fulfilled a promise related to the martyrs Nivalis, Matrona, and Salvius.

EDCS-23801722.translation: To the most blessed martyrs O. F. M. Petrus, Florus, and Susanna; for honor.
EDCS-23801722.summary: A dedication to the martyrs Petrus, Florus, and Susanna, possibly with an abbreviation of a religious order or status.

EDCS-23801723.translation: Publius Sitius Rogatus willingly made and dedicated this vow to Hercules.
EDCS-23801723.summary: A votive inscription by Publius Sitius Rogatus, dedicating a vow to Hercules.

EDCS-23801724.translation: To the spirits of the departed; Tonneia Restuta the pious lived 51 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801724.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tonneia Restuta, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-23801725.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed; in memory of the Accian family; Sextus A. I. C. C.
EDCS-23801725.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the Accian family, with an abbreviated name, likely a memorial.

EDCS-23801728.translation: He lived 50 years; here he rests. She lived 100 years.
EDCS-23801728.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of two individuals.

EDCS-23801729.translation: He lived 53 years; here he rests.
EDCS-23801729.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of an individual.

EDCS-23801730.translation: He lived 60 years; here he rests.
EDCS-23801730.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of an individual.

EDCS-23801731.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of a child, Caecilius Ianuarius.

EDCS-23801732.translation: To the divinity and majesty of the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius, unconquered, pious, fortunate, Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 14th time, consul for the 6th time, father of the country, proconsul, Valerius Antoninus, most perfect man, president of the province of Numidia Cirtensis, devoted, erected at public expense.
EDCS-23801732.summary: A dedication to Emperor Constantius by Valerius Antoninus, governor of Numidia Cirtensis, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-23801734.translation: Quintus Geminius Marcianus, clarissimus vir, quaestor candidate, designated by our most invincible Lords the Emperors as a most faithful and rare master, erected this for Cornelius Rusticus, son of Marcius, of the Quirina tribe, honored with the public equestrian order.
EDCS-23801734.summary: A dedication by Quintus Geminius Marcianus to Cornelius Rusticus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-23801736.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Iulius Fortunatus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, a veteran of the 5th Praetorian Cohort, lived 41 years; here he rests.
EDCS-23801736.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Iulius Fortunatus, a veteran of the Praetorian Guard.

EDCS-23801737.translation: Marcus Sittius Constans, honored with the public equestrian order, prefect of the First Loyal Cohort of Varduli, lived 50 years for his dearest and sweetest brother.
EDCS-23801737.summary: An inscription commemorating Marcus Sittius Constans, a prefect in the Roman army, and possibly mentioning a deceased brother.

EDCS-23801738.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius G. Domitius, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, perpetual priest, lived 75 years; here he rests.
EDCS-23801738.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius G. Domitius, a perpetual priest.

EDCS-23801739.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Vipsanius Martialis, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, priest, lived... Sacred to the Divine Shades. Titia Valentina, daughter of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years; here she rests.
EDCS-23801739.summary: A double funerary inscription for Marcus Vipsanius Martialis, a priest, and Titia Valentina.

EDCS-23801740.translation: Quirina Catullinus made this and dedicated it himself.
EDCS-23801740.summary: This inscription records the maker and dedicator of a monument, Quirina Catullinus.

EDCS-23801741.translation: Aedinia Lucilla, daughter of Publius, lived for 20 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-23801741.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aedinia Lucilla, daughter of Publius, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-23801742.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Aemilius Fortis, son of Marcus, of the voting tribe Quirina, lived for 63 years; he is laid here, with hope.
EDCS-23801742.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Aemilius Fortis, son of Marcus, who died at the age of 63, possibly from the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-23801743.translation: Marcus Aemilius Pudens, son of Marcus, of the voting tribe Quirina, lived for 16 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-23801743.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Aemilius Pudens, son of Marcus, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-23801744.translation: Agria Vitalis, daughter of Publius, of the voting tribe Quirina, lived for 17 years. M Valeria Prima, daughter of Marcus, of the voting tribe Quirina, lived for 50 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-23801744.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of two women, Agria Vitalis (age 17) and M Valeria Prima (age 50).

EDCS-23801745.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Antistius Ianuarius, son of Quintus, of the voting tribe Quirina, lived for 85 years. Antistii Firmus and Maximus made this for their father; he is laid here.
EDCS-23801745.summary: This funerary epitaph records Quintus Antistius Ianuarius, who died at age 85, and was commemorated by his sons Firmus and Maximus.

EDCS-23801746.translation: Antistia Epicaris lived for 91 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-23801746.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antistia Epicaris, who died at the age of 91.

EDCS-23801747.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Antistia Sotira lived for 16 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-23801747.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antistia Sotira, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-23801748.translation: Lucius Aurelius Augustalis. Aemilia Itala lived for [---]. Lucius Aurelius Augustalis.
EDCS-23801748.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a funerary epitaph mentioning Lucius Aurelius Augustalis and Aemilia Itala, possibly a couple.

EDCS-23801749.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Aurelius Pu[---]. Iulia Prima Qu[---].
EDCS-23801749.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning Marcus Aurelius and Iulia Prima.

EDCS-23801750.translation: Basilia Rustica, daughter of Publius, lived 60 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-23801750.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Basilia Rustica.

EDCS-23801751.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Blastus lived 58 years. His son Blastus set this up for his dearest father; he is laid here.
EDCS-23801751.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by a son for his father, recording their lifespans and burial.

EDCS-23801752.translation: Lived 75 years; they are laid here. The son Blastus made this.
EDCS-23801752.summary: This tombstone inscription records a lifespan of 75 years and indicates it was made by a son named Blastus.

EDCS-23801753.translation: Marcus Bruttius Urbicus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, is laid here.
EDCS-23801753.summary: This is a tombstone inscription identifying Marcus Bruttius Urbicus and stating he is buried here.

EDCS-23801754.translation: Caecilius Namphamo, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 35 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-23801754.summary: This tombstone inscription records the lifespan and burial of Caecilius Namphamo.

EDCS-23801755.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Caecilius Rogatus lived 85 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. C. Marciana lived [---] years.
EDCS-23801755.summary: This inscription, likely part of a larger funerary monument, records the lifespans of Lucius Caecilius Rogatus and C. Marciana.

EDCS-23801756.translation: Caecilia Matrona lived 15 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-23801756.summary: This tombstone inscription records the lifespan and burial of Caecilia Matrona.

EDCS-23801757.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilia Suitiis, daughter of Caecilius Victor, of the Quirina tribe, lived 31 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-23801757.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Caecilia Suitiis.

EDCS-23801758.translation: Veneria, daughter of Quintus, lived 10 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-23801758.summary: This tombstone inscription records the lifespan and burial of Veneria.

EDCS-23801759.translation: Urbana, daughter of Quintus, lived 60 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-23801759.summary: This tombstone inscription records the lifespan and burial of Urbana.

EDCS-23801760.translation: Gaius Geminius Faustus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801760.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Geminius Faustus, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-23801761.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Cornelius, son of [---], of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-23801761.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, mentioning a person named Cornelius from the Quirina tribe.

EDCS-23801762.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Cornelius, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-23801762.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, mentioning Marcus Cornelius, son of Marcus, from the Quirina tribe.

EDCS-23801763.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Decimus Cornelius Hesperus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 85 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801763.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Decimus Cornelius Hesperus, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-23801764.translation: Marcus Cornelius Martialis, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 15 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801764.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Cornelius Martialis, who lived 15 years.

EDCS-23801766.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Cornelia, daughter of Marcus, who lived 16 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801766.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia, daughter of Marcus, who lived 16 years.

EDCS-23801767.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Cornelia Honorina, freedwoman of Decimus, lived 29 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801767.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Honorina, a freedwoman, who lived 29 years.

EDCS-23801768.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Cornelia Iucunda lived 101 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801768.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Iucunda, who lived 101 years.

EDCS-23801769.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Cornelia Rustica, daughter of Quintus.

EDCS-23801770.translation: Decius Naso lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801770.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Decius Naso, who lived 70 years.

EDCS-23801771.translation: Dia Boroc lived for 16 years; she rests here.
EDCS-23801771.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Dia Boroc, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-23801772.translation: Marcus Dirius Marinus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 39 years.
EDCS-23801772.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Dirius Marinus, who died at the age of 39.

EDCS-23801773.translation: Quintus Egnatius Victor, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 80 years; he rests here.
EDCS-23801773.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Egnatius Victor, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-23801774.translation: To the departed spirits, Egnatia Tertulla, daughter of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-23801774.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirits for Egnatia Tertulla.

EDCS-23801775.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Egri Auficialius, of the Quirina tribe, of the Maximian family, lived for 85 years; he rests here.
EDCS-23801775.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Egri Auficialius, who died at the age of 85.

EDCS-23801776.translation: Egrilius Postumus, son of Caius, lived for 12 years; he rests here.
EDCS-23801776.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Egrilius Postumus, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-23801777.translation: Egrilia Silvana, daughter of Lucius, lived for 80 years; she rests here.
EDCS-23801777.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Egrilia Silvana, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-23801778.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Euporus lived for 55 years.
EDCS-23801778.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirits for Euporus, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-23801779.translation: Publius Cornelius Felix, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 71 years; he rests here. Maxima lived for 61 years; she rests here.
EDCS-23801779.summary: This is a joint funerary epitaph for Publius Cornelius Felix and Maxima.

EDCS-23801780.translation: Titus Flavius Annianus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 10 years; he rests here. He had this placed for himself in his lifetime.
EDCS-23801780.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Annianus, who died at the age of 10, and records that he prepared his own tomb while alive.

EDCS-23801781.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Fuficius Victor, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe. Sacred to the departed spirits. Fidenia Victoria.
EDCS-23801781.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Fuficius Victor and Fidenia Victoria, likely a married couple, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-23801782.translation: Fuficia Secunda, daughter of Marcus, lived 17 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23801782.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fuficia Secunda, recording her name, parentage, age at death, and burial location.

EDCS-23801783.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fuficia Septimina, daughter of Fuficius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 45 years. She is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Caecilius Ianuarius, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived [---] years.
EDCS-23801783.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Fuficia Septimina and Lucius Caecilius Ianuarius, including their names, parentage, tribal affiliation, and age at death.

EDCS-23801784.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gargilia Urbana, daughter of Quintus, lived [---] years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Mattius Baric, son of Quintus, of the Voltinia tribe, made this for himself while alive and for his wife. He lived [---] years. He is buried here, numbering 103.
EDCS-23801784.summary: This funerary inscription records Gargilia Urbana and Quintus Mattius Baric, with the latter dedicating the monument for himself and his wife, and mentions a total of 103 burials.

EDCS-23801785.translation: Geminia Urbana, daughter of Macr, lived 75 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23801785.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Geminia Urbana, recording her name, parentage, age at death, and burial location.

EDCS-23801786.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Herennius Libosus lived 60 years. He is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Coppronia Matrona lived 60 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23801786.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Herennius Libosus and Coppronia Matrona, each having lived 60 years.

EDCS-23801787.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Herennius Celsus, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-23801788.translation: Lucius Herennius Genialis, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 70 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23801788.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Herennius Genialis, including his name, parentage, tribal affiliation, age at death, and burial location.

EDCS-23801789.translation: Horatia Marisa lived 105 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23801789.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Horatia Marisa, recording her name, age at death, and burial location.

EDCS-23801790.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Iulius, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe.
EDCS-23801790.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-23801791.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Iulius Celer, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 30 years.
EDCS-23801791.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Gaius Iulius Celer.

EDCS-23801792.translation: Gaius Iulius Celer, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801792.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death and burial of Gaius Iulius Celer.

EDCS-23801793.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Iulius Felix, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 80 years.
EDCS-23801793.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Gaius Iulius Felix.

EDCS-23801794.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Iulius Iulianus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 36 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801794.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death and burial of Quintus Iulius Iulianus.

EDCS-23801795.translation: Gaius Iulius Macer, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 27 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801795.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death and burial of Gaius Iulius Macer.

EDCS-23801796.translation: Lucius Iulius Rogatus, son of Lucius, lived 19 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801796.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death and burial of Lucius Iulius Rogatus.

EDCS-23801797.translation: Iulius Rogatus of the Quirina tribe lived ... years
EDCS-23801797.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription recording the name and tribe of Iulius Rogatus and his lifespan.

EDCS-23801798.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius Solutor lived ... years. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Valeria Cicilia.
EDCS-23801798.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription mentioning Iulius Solutor and Valeria Cicilia.

EDCS-23801799.summary: A dedication to Iulia, daughter of Gaius, possibly a tomb inscription.

EDCS-23801800.translation: Iulia Auge, freedwoman of Erotis, lived 30 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801800.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death and burial of Iulia Auge, a freedwoman.

EDCS-23801801.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Mellita, wife...
EDCS-23801801.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirits by Iulia Mellita, likely a wife.

EDCS-23801802.translation: Iulia Rocim, daughter of Caius, lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801802.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the life and burial of Iulia Rocim, daughter of Caius.

EDCS-23801803.translation: Iulia Rufilla, daughter of Lucius, lived 70 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801803.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the life and burial of Iulia Rufilla, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-23801804.translation: To Quirina Secunda, daughter of Titus, a most rare daughter.
EDCS-23801804.summary: This is a dedication to Quirina Secunda, daughter of Titus, likely set up by her parents.

EDCS-23801805.translation: Annianus lived 30 years; he is buried here. [Female name] lived 61 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801805.summary: This inscription records the lives and burials of two individuals, Annianus and an unnamed female.

EDCS-23801806.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Laelius Nepos, son of Quintus; Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcia Pupa, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-23801806.summary: This inscription appears to be a combined funerary dedication for two individuals, Quintus Laelius Nepos and Marcia Pupa.

EDCS-23801807.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Livia Libosa, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-23801807.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirits by Livia Libosa, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-23801808.translation: Lucius Optatus, son of Lucius Lucceius, lived 41 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801808.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the life and burial of Lucius Optatus, son of Lucius Lucceius.

EDCS-23801809.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Mallia Dativa, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-23801809.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirits by Mallia Dativa, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-23801810.translation: Manilia Tertia, daughter of Lucius, lived [X] years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801810.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the life and burial of Manilia Tertia, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-23801812.translation: Quirina Martialis, while alive, set this up for herself and for herself. She lived 55 years and is buried here. For Sil [her wife], most beloved wife, who lived 55 years and is buried here.
EDCS-23801812.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Quirina Martialis for herself and her wife Sil, providing their ages at death.

EDCS-23801813.translation: Maxima lived 68 years and is buried here.
EDCS-23801813.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age at death of Maxima.

EDCS-23801814.translation: Milonia Secunda, daughter of Marcus, lived 35 years.
EDCS-23801814.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age at death of Milonia Secunda.

EDCS-23801815.translation: Publius Modius Felix, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 100 years and is buried here.
EDCS-23801815.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age at death and burial place of Publius Modius Felix.

EDCS-23801816.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ulus Nondinarius Ocania Fus A[---]uli lived [---] years.
EDCS-23801816.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits and recording the name and partial lifespan of Ulus Nondinarius Ocania Fus A[---]uli.

EDCS-23801817.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Nunnius Honoratus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 80 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Silicia Lucilla, daughter of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 76 years.
EDCS-23801817.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the lifespans of Lucius Nunnius Honoratus and Silicia Lucilla.

EDCS-23801818.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Nunnius Honoratus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 80 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. For Maria Faustina, wife, lived 75 years.
EDCS-23801818.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the lifespans of Marcus Nunnius Honoratus and his wife Maria Faustina.

EDCS-23801819.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Nius, of the Quirina tribe, lived [---] years and is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Nunnia Honorata, daughter of Lucius, lived 25 years and is buried here.
EDCS-23801819.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the burial of Nius and the lifespan and burial of Nunnia Honorata.

EDCS-23801820.translation: Octavia Maxima, daughter of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 60 years.
EDCS-23801820.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age at death of Octavia Maxima.

EDCS-23801821.translation: Petronia Lollia, daughter of Sextus, lived 27 years and is buried here.
EDCS-23801821.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age at death and burial place of Petronia Lollia.

EDCS-23801822.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pompeius Honoratus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived [unspecified number of] years. Lucius Pompeius Septimianus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, and Vettia, daughter of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, [rest of text missing].
EDCS-23801822.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Pompeius Honoratus and a partial record of Lucius Pompeius Septimianus and Vettia.

EDCS-23801823.translation: Pompeia Quarta, daughter of Marcus, lived 55 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801823.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Pompeia Quarta.

EDCS-23801824.translation: Popilius Florus, son of Caius, lived 53 years.
EDCS-23801824.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Popilius Florus.

EDCS-23801825.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Publicius Fidus lived 62 years.
EDCS-23801825.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Publius Publicius Fidus.

EDCS-23801826.translation: [---], daughter of Servius, lived 25 years; she is buried here. Quirina [name missing], lived 15 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801826.summary: This funerary inscription records the lives and burials of two individuals, one identified as the daughter of Servius and another with an incomplete name.

EDCS-23801827.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sempronia Gallita, wife of Gaius Herennius.
EDCS-23801827.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Sempronia Gallita, identifying her as the wife of Gaius Herennius.

EDCS-23801828.translation: Sempronia Gemella, daughter of Lucius, lived 58 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801828.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Sempronia Gemella.

EDCS-23801829.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Servenius Felix, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 89 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801829.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Marcus Servenius Felix.

EDCS-23801830.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sextia Saturnina, daughter of Lucius, lived 15 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801830.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Sextia Saturnina.

EDCS-23801831.translation: Aulus Sextilius Nampamo, son of Aulus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801831.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Aulus Sextilius Nampamo.

EDCS-23801832.translation: Sextilia Matrona lived for 52 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801832.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextilia Matrona, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-23801833.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Silius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for [---] years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Matrona, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 75 years.
EDCS-23801833.summary: This inscription, sacred to the departed spirits, records the lives of Gaius Silius and Aemilia Matrona, likely a married couple.

EDCS-23801834.translation: Publius Sittius Honoratus, son of Publius, lived for 90 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801834.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Sittius Honoratus, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-23801835.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Sittius Sittianus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 72 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801835.summary: This funerary epitaph, sacred to the departed spirits, records the lifespan and burial of Lucius Sittius Sittianus.

EDCS-23801836.translation: Publius Sittius Sittienus Curtus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 80 years.
EDCS-23801836.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Sittius Sittienus Curtus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-23801837.translation: Caius Sittius Urbanus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 31 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801837.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan and burial of Caius Sittius Urbanus.

EDCS-23801838.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Sittius Urbanus. Lucius Sittius erected this for his brother, lived for 20 years.
EDCS-23801838.summary: This funerary inscription, sacred to the departed spirits, records that Lucius Sittius erected a monument for his brother Gaius Sittius Urbanus, who lived for 20 years.

EDCS-23801839.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sittia Herennia lived for 21 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801839.summary: This funerary epitaph, sacred to the departed spirits, records the lifespan and burial of Sittia Herennia.

EDCS-23801840.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sittia Romana, daughter of Titus Sitius, lived for 85 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801840.summary: This funerary epitaph, sacred to the departed spirits, records the lifespan of Sittia Romana and includes a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-23801841.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. This monument is for Sittia Secunda, wife of Sitius Quirina, her most obedient wife, who lived for 26 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801841.summary: This funerary epitaph, sacred to the departed spirits, records the lifespan and burial of Sittia Secunda, described as the devoted wife of Sitius Quirina.

EDCS-23801842.translation: Speronia, daughter of Marcus, lived 60 years.
EDCS-23801842.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Speronia.

EDCS-23801843.translation: To Julius Stephano, a most rare husband, his wife Maxima lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801843.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband, Julius Stephano.

EDCS-23801844.translation: Marcus Tanonius Saturninus lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801844.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Marcus Tanonius Saturninus.

EDCS-23801845.translation: Publius Valerius Fundanus, son of Titus, of the Galeria tribe, lived 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801845.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Publius Valerius Fundanus.

EDCS-23801846.translation: Marcus Valerius Secundio lived 61 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801846.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Marcus Valerius Secundio.

EDCS-23801847.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Valeria Fii.
EDCS-23801847.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Dis Manes by Valeria Fii.

EDCS-23801848.translation: Valeria Sperata, daughter of Marcus, lived 90 years. Caius Julius Optatus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived [---] years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801848.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespans and burial of Valeria Sperata and Caius Julius Optatus.

EDCS-23801849.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Culcius Marcianus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801849.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Culcius Marcianus.

EDCS-23801850.translation: Urbana lived 51 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801850.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Urbana.

EDCS-23801851.translation: A Roman knight, a soldier of the 3rd Cyrenaic Legion, lived 41 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801851.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a Roman knight and soldier of the 3rd Cyrenaic Legion.

EDCS-23801852.translation: Rest in peace, Crescens the Younger, aged 15. Rest in peace.
EDCS-23801852.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Crescens Iunior, a 15-year-old, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801853.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Munatius Quintillus lived for 65 years. He lies here; may his bones rest well. Sacred to the spirits of the departed.
EDCS-23801853.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Munatius Quintillus, who lived 65 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801854.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Munatius Victor lived for 30 years. He lies here; may his bones rest well. To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Munatius Monnosus lived for 25 years. He lies here; may his bones rest well.
EDCS-23801854.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two individuals, Marcus Munatius Victor and Quintus Munatius Monnosus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801855.translation: Herenia Rogata lived for 45 years. She lies here.
EDCS-23801855.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Herenia Rogata, who lived 45 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801857.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Melius lived for 85 years. He lies here.
EDCS-23801857.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Melius, who lived 85 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801863.translation: To Sabina Augusta, mother of Hadrian Augustus, father of the fatherland, by decree of the local councilors, at public expense.
EDCS-23801863.summary: This is a public dedication to Sabina Augusta, likely a wife or mother of an emperor, funded by the local community, possibly dating to the Hadrianic period.

EDCS-23801865.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the camps, wife of Emperor Caesar divi Marci Antonini Germanici Sarmatici son of divi Commodi, brother of divi Antonini Pii, grandson of divi Hadriani, great-grandson of divi Traiani Parthici, great-great-grandson of divi Nervae, Lucius Septimius Pius Pertinax Augustus Parthicus Arabicus Parthicus Adiabenicus, propagator of the empire, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 5th time, imperator for the 8th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, most brave and most holy prince, mother of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar, the designated emperor; [by order of] the state of the Siguitani.
EDCS-23801865.summary: This is a lengthy public dedication by the state of the Siguitani to Julia Augusta, wife of Emperor Septimius Severus and mother of the future emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), dating to the Severan period.

EDCS-23801866.translation: To Sabina Tranquillina Augusta, wife of our lord Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 5th time, imperator for the 6th time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul; [by order of] the state of the Siguitani.
EDCS-23801866.summary: This is a public dedication by the state of the Siguitani to Sabina Tranquillina Augusta, wife of Emperor Gordian III, dating to the Gordianic period.

EDCS-23801869.translation: Publius Sittius Felix, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, designated master of the village, lived for 40 years. He lies here; may your bones rest well. Publius Sittius Felix lived for 51 years. He lies here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801869.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two individuals named Publius Sittius Felix, one of whom held the office of village master, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801870.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Attius Maximus, priest of Saturn, lived for 75 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801870.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Attius Maximus, a priest of Saturn, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801871.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Clodius Honoratus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, priest of the Great Mother, lived 55 years. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-23801871.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Clodius Honoratus, a priest of the Great Mother, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-23801872.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Munatius Gemellus, priest of Bellona, lived 55 years. Iulia Saturnina, his wife, is buried here. Munatia Marcellina, his daughter, lived 23 years.
EDCS-23801872.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Lucius Munatius Gemellus, a priest of Bellona, his wife Iulia Saturnina, and their daughter Munatia Marcellina.

EDCS-23801873.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For the memory of Geminia Bona, dearest wife, who always loved me. She lived 32 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Volussius Felix, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, priest of Neptune, lived 75 years. May your bones rest well here.
EDCS-23801873.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Marcus Volussius Felix, a priest of Neptune, to his wife Geminia Bona, who lived 32 years.

EDCS-23801874.summary: This brief funerary inscription records the death of Sentia Tertia, a priestess, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-23801875.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Crescens the public servant lived 70 years and Nina his wife lived 65 years.
EDCS-23801875.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Crescens, a public servant, and his wife Nina.

EDCS-23801876.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tiberius Claudius Alexander. Sacred to the departed spirits. Publicia Nina.
EDCS-23801876.summary: This funerary inscription is a double dedication to the departed spirits of Tiberius Claudius Alexander and Publicia Nina.

EDCS-23801877.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Publicius Crescens lived 16 years and Neptunalis his brother lived 13 years and Urbana his sister lived 25 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23801877.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of three siblings: Caius Publicius Crescens (16), his brother Neptunalis (13), and his sister Urbana (25).

EDCS-23801878.translation: Titus Aelius lived [some number of] years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23801878.summary: This incomplete funerary inscription records that Titus Aelius lived and is buried here.

EDCS-23801879.translation: Alfia, daughter of the Sodales, Secunda, lived 60 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23801879.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Alfia Secunda, daughter of the Sodales, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-23801880.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelvius Clarus lived 50 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23801880.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Aelvius Clarus, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-23801882.translation: Quintus Aemilius Martial, son of Marcus, of the voting tribe Quirina, lived 60 years. He lies here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801882.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Aemilius Martial, recording his name, filiation, tribe, age at death, and a wish for his afterlife.

EDCS-23801883.translation: Aemilius Victor Satu, while alive, and Valeria Aemilia novice, lived 20 years.
EDCS-23801883.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely for Valeria Aemilia novice, recording her name and age at death, with Aemilius Victor Satu potentially being a relative or the dedicator.

EDCS-23801884.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Marciana lived 25 years. Lucius Iulius Rufinus for his dearest wife.
EDCS-23801884.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the memory of Aemilia Marciana by her husband, Lucius Iulius Rufinus, and records her age at death.

EDCS-23801885.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Quinta, who is also called Akzucia, lived 12 years and 6 months. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801885.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Quinta (also known as Akzucia), recording her name, her age at death, and a wish for her afterlife.

EDCS-23801886.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Satura lived 65 years. She lies here.
EDCS-23801886.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Satura, recording her name, age at death, and that she is buried at this location.

EDCS-23801887.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Tertullina lived 30 years. Aminia Estricatula lived 5 years. She lies here.
EDCS-23801887.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for both Aemilia Tertullina and Aminia Estricatula, recording their names and ages at death, and that they are buried here.

EDCS-23801888.translation: Quintus Alfius Catus, son of Publius, lived 60 years. He lies here.
EDCS-23801888.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Alfius Catus, recording his name, filiation, age at death, and burial location.

EDCS-23801890.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Alfius Pullarius lived 50 years. He lies here. Cameria Rogata lived 40 years. She lies here.
EDCS-23801890.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for both Quintus Alfius Pullarius and Cameria Rogata, recording their names, ages at death, and burial locations.

EDCS-23801891.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Aminia Matrona, my departed wife, who lived 30 years. Caius Mussius Florinus for his most rare wife. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-23801891.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Aminia Matrona by her husband, Caius Mussius Florinus, recording her age at death and expressing his devotion.

EDCS-23801892.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Panteius Romulus, son of Publius, a dutiful man, lived 25 years. He lies here.
EDCS-23801892.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Panteius Romulus, recording his name, filiation, a character descriptor, his age at death, and burial location.

EDCS-23801893.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Apronia Rustica lived 75 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801893.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Apronia Rustica, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-23801894.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aulus Bibius Clarus lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801894.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Aulus Bibius Clarus, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-23801895.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus C. Rufus lived 90 years; he is buried here. S. Iulia lived 51 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801895.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the deaths of Marcus C. Rufus (90 years) and S. Iulia (51 years).

EDCS-23801896.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Catullina lived 26 years. May your bones rest well. Ianuarius made this for his most loving wife.
