EDCS-23801896.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Caecilia Catullina at age 26 and was made by her husband, Ianuarius.

EDCS-23801897.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Caecilius Concessus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 35 years; may you rest well here. Andana Frontilla lived 90 years; may your bones rest well here.
EDCS-23801897.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Lucius Caecilius Concessus (35 years) and Andana Frontilla (90 years).

EDCS-23801898.translation: Quintus Caecilius Dubitatus lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801898.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Quintus Caecilius Dubitatus, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-23801899.translation: Publius Caecilius Marcellus lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801899.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Publius Caecilius Marcellus, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-23801900.translation: Publius Caecilius Martialis lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801900.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Publius Caecilius Martialis, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-23801901.translation: To the distinguished memory of Caecilius Urbanus, also called Zurumius, who is buried here. He lived 23 years.
EDCS-23801901.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Caecilius Urbanus (also known as Zurumius), who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-23801902.translation: Caecilia Marcella, daughter of Marcus, lived 20 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801902.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Caecilia Marcella, daughter of Marcus, who lived 20 years.

EDCS-23801903.translation: Lucius Caelius Felix lived 110 years, is buried here. Lucius Caelius Marcellus lived 25 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23801903.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the ages and burial of two individuals, Lucius Caelius Felix and Lucius Caelius Marcellus.

EDCS-23801905.translation: Cafrania Vitalis lived 75 years, is buried here. Publius Sittius Coccoitchanus lived 90 years.
EDCS-23801905.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages of Cafrania Vitalis and Publius Sittius Coccoitchanus, noting the burial of the former.

EDCS-23801906.translation: Callonia Rana lived 64 years, is buried here. May your bones rest well. Marcus Ufelius Rufinus lived 30 years, is buried here. Iulius Rufus lived 80 years, is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801906.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages and burial of Callonia Rana, Marcus Ufelius Rufinus, and Iulius Rufus, with blessings for the latter two.

EDCS-23801907.translation: Carisia Tertulla lived 80 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23801907.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph stating the age and burial of Carisia Tertulla.

EDCS-23801908.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cassia Rogatina, mother, made this for herself while alive; she lived 90 years, is buried here. L. Luria Rogatula, mother, made this for herself while alive, lived 22 years, is buried here. Lucius Aemilius Rogatulus, son of Lucius Aemilius, lived 30 years, is buried here. Lucius Aemilius Cassianus, son of Lucius Aemilius, lived 36 years, is buried here. May his bones rest well. Aemilia Sabina lived 6 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23801908.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the Divine Shades, records the ages and burials of Cassia Rogatina, L. Luria Rogatula, Lucius Aemilius Rogatulus, Lucius Aemilius Cassianus, and Aemilia Sabina, with some of the epitaphs being self-made by the deceased while alive.

EDCS-23801909.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Chreste, slave of the Clodii, lived 60 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23801909.summary: This funerary epitaph, dedicated to the Divine Shades, records the age and burial of Chreste, a slave of the Clodii.

EDCS-23801910.translation: Tiberius Claudius Proculus lived 55 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801910.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Tiberius Claudius Proculus and offering a blessing for his rest.

EDCS-23801911.translation: Quintus Clodius Gallicanus lived 65 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23801911.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age and burial of Quintus Clodius Gallicanus.

EDCS-23801912.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. In memory of Clodius Processus, I sought this resting place through many years of sailing and travel; I placed this eternal home for myself while alive, and for my wife. And this is what you see, friends, farewell. Lived 85 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801912.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the Divine Shades, records the age of Clodius Processus and states that he constructed his own tomb for himself and his wife during his lifetime after extensive travel.

EDCS-23801913.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius Clodius Victor lived 10 years. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-23801913.summary: This funerary epitaph, dedicated to the Divine Shades, records the age of the child Iulius Clodius Victor and offers a blessing for his rest.

EDCS-23801914.translation: Clodia Apvoa lived 80 years, here she lies. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-23801914.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the life and death of Clodia Apvoa.

EDCS-23801915.translation: Clodia Urbana, daughter of Marcus, lived 80 years, here she lies. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-23801915.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the life and death of Clodia Urbana.

EDCS-23801916.translation: L. Lucia lived 65 years, here she lies. L. Brocus lived 87 years, here he lies.
EDCS-23801916.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the lives and deaths of two individuals, L. Lucia and L. Brocus.

EDCS-23801917.translation: Cornelius Caecilianus lived 50 years, here he lies. May his bones rest well. Agria Successa lived 49 years, here she lies. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-23801917.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the lives and deaths of Cornelius Caecilianus and Agria Successa.

EDCS-23801918.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Cornelius Clarus lived 80 years, here he lies. May his bones rest well. Cassia Quintilla lived 100 years. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-23801918.summary: A tombstone inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits, recording the lives of Marcus Cornelius Clarus and Cassia Quintilla.

EDCS-23801919.translation: Lucius Cornelius Hospes lived 30 years, here he lies.
EDCS-23801919.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the life and death of Lucius Cornelius Hospes.

EDCS-23801920.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Cornelius Matutinus lived 32 years, here he lies. May his bones rest well. Honoratus lived 20 years.
EDCS-23801920.summary: A tombstone inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits, recording the lives of Lucius Cornelius Matutinus and Honoratus.

EDCS-23801921.translation: Cornelia Victoria lived 13 years. Cornelia Ianuaria lived 25 years, here she lies. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-23801921.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the lives and deaths of Cornelia Victoria and Cornelia Ianuaria.

EDCS-23801922.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Domitia Urbana lived 25 years. May her bones rest well. Lucius Domitius Felicio lived 40 years.
EDCS-23801922.summary: A tombstone inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits, recording the lives of Domitia Urbana and Lucius Domitius Felicio.

EDCS-23801923.translation: Iulia Donata lived 30 years, here she lies.
EDCS-23801923.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the life and death of Iulia Donata.

EDCS-23801924.translation: Aulus Fabius Aprilis, son of Aulus, lived for 20 years.
EDCS-23801924.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Aulus Fabius Aprilis.

EDCS-23801925.translation: Fabia Mevia lived for 22 years; her bones are here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801925.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Fabia Mevia and a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-23801926.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Flavius Proculus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 81 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well. Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Flavius Paulinus lived for 35 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801926.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of two individuals, Marcus Flavius Proculus and Titus Flavius Paulinus, with standard phrases for the departed.

EDCS-23801927.translation: Gargilius Silvanus lived for 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801927.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Gargilius Silvanus.

EDCS-23801928.translation: Gargilia Crispilla lived for 70 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-23801928.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Gargilia Crispilla.

EDCS-23801929.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gargilia Restuta, daughter of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 44 years; may her bones rest well. Caius Vibius Vellanus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, to his wife.
EDCS-23801929.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Gargilia Restuta and is dedicated by her husband, Caius Vibius Vellanus.

EDCS-23801930.translation: Marcus Gemellus lived for 36 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801930.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Marcus Gemellus.

EDCS-23801931.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Geminius Aprilis lived for 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-23801931.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Caius Geminius Aprilis with a dedication to the departed spirits.

EDCS-23801932.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Geminia Potita, daughter of Geminius Potitus, lived for 16 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801932.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Geminia Potita and includes a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-23801933.translation: Valeria Maximina lived for 45 years. Valerius Novellus lived for 55 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Valerius P. lived for 75 years. Saturnina lived for 55 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Valerius Placidianus lived for 46 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801933.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of multiple individuals: Valeria Maximina, Valerius Novellus, Caius Valerius P., Saturnina, and Caius Valerius Placidianus.

EDCS-23801934.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Helvius Ianuarius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, made this for himself while alive. He lived 22 years. May his bones rest well. May his bones rest well. Helvius Iucundus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 43 years. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-23801934.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Helvius Ianuarius and Helvius Iucundus, likely dating to the Imperial period, with the former dedicating the monument to himself.

EDCS-23801935.translation: Helvia Saturnina lived 90 years. May her bones rest well. Publius Sittius Optatus lived 60 years. He is buried here. May his bones rest well. Publius Sittius Gudullus lived 40 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801935.summary: A funerary epitaph for Helvia Saturnina and Publius Sittius Optatus and Publius Sittius Gudullus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23801937.translation: Caius Ingenius Barbarus, son of Caius, lived 17 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801937.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Ingenius Barbarus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23801938.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Iulius Acutus lived 80 years. Iulia Urbana, his wife, lived...
EDCS-23801938.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Iulius Acutus and his wife Iulia Urbana, likely dating to the Imperial period, with the wife's lifespan incomplete.

EDCS-23801939.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Iulius Carisius lived 38 years. Rufina placed this for her husband.
EDCS-23801939.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Carisius, dedicated by his wife Rufina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23801940.translation: Caius Iulius Crispus lived 60 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23801940.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Crispus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23801941.translation: Lucius Iulius Felix lived 85 years. He is buried here. Mussia Satura, daughter of Caius, lived 80 years. She is buried here. May their bones rest well.
EDCS-23801941.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Iulius Felix and Mussia Satura, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23801942.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Iulius Gemellus, also called Constantius, lived 55 years and 5 months.
EDCS-23801942.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Iulius Gemellus (Constantius), likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23801943.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Iulius Ianuarius, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 86 years. Antistia Rufina, daughter of Lucius, lived 75 years.
EDCS-23801943.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Ianuarius and Antistia Rufina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23801944.translation: Caius Iulius Optatus lived 75 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23801944.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Optatus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23801945.translation: To the divine spirits of the underworld. Gaius Julius Pacatus lived 120 years.
EDCS-23801945.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gaius Julius Pacatus.

EDCS-23801946.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits of the underworld. Good traveler, I wish you well. Gaius Julius Ponticus, son of Gaius, of the voting tribe Quirina, who professed his skills abroad rather than at home, as he deserved, was deprived of life by his household gods. He lived 62 years. May his bones rest well. Iulia Optata, daughter of Gaius, lived 13 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801946.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Ponticus and Iulia Optata, likely a father and daughter, recording their lifespans and wishing them well in the afterlife.

EDCS-23801947.translation: To the divine spirits of the underworld. Lucius Julius Proculus lived 60 years. Marcus Julius Rufinus lived 22 years. Gaius Julius Honoratus lived 20 years. May you rest well.
EDCS-23801947.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commemorating the lives of Lucius Julius Proculus, Marcus Julius Rufinus, and Gaius Julius Honoratus.

EDCS-23801948.translation: To the divine spirits of the underworld. Iulius Rufinus lived 40 years. Buried here is Gaius Julius Potitus, son of Marcus, of the voting tribe Quirina, who lived 25 years. Buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801948.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lives and burials of Iulius Rufinus and Gaius Julius Potitus.

EDCS-23801949.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits of the underworld. Lucius Julius Saturninus lived 65 years.
EDCS-23801949.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Julius Saturninus.

EDCS-23801950.translation: Julius Togatus to his son IA RIMAN I, lived for...
EDCS-23801950.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, possibly a dedication from Julius Togatus to his son.

EDCS-23801951.translation: Julius Urbanus, son of Marcus, lived 11 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the divine spirits of the underworld. Marcus Julius Urbanus, son of Marcus, of the voting tribe Quirina, lived 15 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well. Pompeia Gaetula lived 37 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well. To the divine spirits of the underworld. I Max...
EDCS-23801951.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespans and burials of Julius Urbanus, Marcus Julius Urbanus, and Pompeia Gaetula, possibly family members.

EDCS-23801952.translation: To the divine spirits of the underworld. Publius Julius Urbanus lived 60 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801952.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Publius Julius Urbanus.

EDCS-23801953.translation: Iulia Bellica, daughter of Quitus, lived 13 years. Marcus Julius Candidus lived 40 years. They are buried here.
EDCS-23801953.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Bellica and Marcus Julius Candidus, likely indicating their shared burial.

EDCS-23801955.translation: Iulia Laeta, daughter of Caius, lived 20 years. She is buried here. Publius Porrega Africanus, son of Publius, lived 35 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23801955.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespans and burials of Iulia Laeta and Publius Porrega Africanus.

EDCS-23801956.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Luciosa lived 35 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801956.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Luciosa, who lived for 35 years.

EDCS-23801957.translation: Iulia Luculla lived 40 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23801957.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Luculla, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-23801958.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Manilla, who lived for 45 years.

EDCS-23801959.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Optata lived 40 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801959.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Optata, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-23801960.translation: Iulia Optatula, daughter of Maximu, lived 65 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801960.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Optatula, daughter of Maximu, who lived for 65 years.

EDCS-23801961.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, for the memory of Iulia Rogata, my wife, who shared with me one spirit, one counsel, for 40 years. She lived 55 years. May her bones rest well. Lucius Gargilius Venator, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, erected this tomb for himself while alive, and departed to the gods, so that he might enjoy good times hereafter.
EDCS-23801961.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Rogata, wife of Lucius Gargilius Venator, and also indicates that Lucius erected the tomb for himself while alive.

EDCS-23801962.translation: Iulia Rustica lived 60 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23801962.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Rustica, who lived for 60 years.

EDCS-23801963.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Saturnina, who lived for 80 years.

EDCS-23801964.translation: Iulia Saturnina lived 40 years. She is buried.
EDCS-23801964.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Saturnina, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-23801965.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Secunda lived 83 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23801965.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Secunda, who lived for 83 years.

EDCS-23801966.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Secura lived 22 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23801966.summary: A funerary dedication for Iulia Secura, who died at age 22.

EDCS-23801967.translation: My dear wife Iulia Spesina, beloved for many years, you surpassed your husband in dutiful devotion; all that we have was acquired by your labor. Marinus [dedicated this].
EDCS-23801967.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Spesina, dedicated by her husband Marinus, praising her devotion and labor.

EDCS-23801968.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Iunia Frontilla, daughter of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, my most rare wife, Caius Iulius Surus, son of Caius, who lived 47 years. She is buried here. For Calpurnia Iuliosa, daughter of Lucius, my most dutiful mother, who lived 97 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-23801968.summary: A funerary inscription honoring two women, Iunia Frontilla and her mother Calpurnia Iuliosa, with their ages at death.

EDCS-23801969.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Licinius Kalendius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-23801969.summary: A funerary monument set up by Lucius Licinius Kalendius for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-23801970.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Licinius Eventus lived 45 years. May your bones rest well. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Licinia Gudulla, daughter of Lucius, lived 40 years. She is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Pullius Festus lived 75 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801970.summary: A funerary inscription recording the deaths and ages of three individuals: Lucius Licinius Eventus, Licinia Gudulla, and Lucius Pullius Festus.

EDCS-23801971.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Luculina lived 70 years. Dubidius made this for a well-deserving person with his own money. Dubidius Victor lived 50 years.
EDCS-23801971.summary: A funerary monument erected by Dubidius for Luculina, and also recording the life span of Dubidius Victor.

EDCS-23801972.translation: Lucius Lurius Proculus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 55 years.
EDCS-23801972.summary: A funerary inscription recording the name, parentage, tribal affiliation, and age at death of Lucius Lurius Proculus.

EDCS-23801973.translation: Publius Lurius Successus lived 100 years. Mesia, slave of Publius Lurius, lived 25 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well. Luria Epistesis lived 90 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801973.summary: A funerary inscription noting the long lives of Publius Lurius Successus and Luria Epistesis, and the death of Mesia, a slave of Publius Lurius.

EDCS-23801974.translation: Magnia Tertulla lived 60 years. May you rest well here. Lucius Cornelius Florentinus lived 70 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801974.summary: A funerary inscription recording the deaths and ages of Magnia Tertulla and Lucius Cornelius Florentinus.

EDCS-23801975.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Publius Marcellus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, Quietus Mellitus, lived 19 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801975.summary: A funerary dedication for Publius Marcellus Quietus Mellitus, who died at age 19.

EDCS-23801976.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gnaeus Marcius Cerialis lived 65 years, he is buried here. May your bones rest well. Quintus Marcius Monnosus lived 8 years, he is buried here. May [your] bones rest well. Octavia Extricata lived 80 years, she is buried here. May [your] bones rest well.
EDCS-23801976.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lives and burials of Gnaeus Marcius Cerialis, Quintus Marcius Monnosus, and Octavia Extricata.

EDCS-23801977.translation: Lucius Marcius Crispinus lived 60 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-23801977.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Lucius Marcius Crispinus.

EDCS-23801978.translation: Marcus Marcius Magnus lived 23 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-23801978.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Marcus Marcius Magnus.

EDCS-23801979.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Marcius Marcianus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 85 years, he is buried here. May your bones rest well. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Attia Rufina lived 81 years, she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801979.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lives and burials of Quintus Marcius Marcianus and Attia Rufina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23801980.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcia Matrona, daughter of Sextus, lived 40 years. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-23801980.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life of Marcia Matrona, daughter of Sextus.

EDCS-23801981.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Mattia Crispa lived 50 years, she is interred here. May her ashes rest well.
EDCS-23801981.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial and repose of Mattia Crispa.

EDCS-23801982.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Metilia Quartilla, daughter of Lucius Metilius, lived 70 years. May your bones rest well here.
EDCS-23801982.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life of Metilia Quartilla, daughter of Lucius Metilius.

EDCS-23801983.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Mevia Rufina lived 35 years. May your bones rest well. She is buried here.
EDCS-23801983.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Mevia Rufina.

EDCS-23801984.translation: Decimus Minucius Urbanus lived 40 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801984.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life of Decimus Minucius Urbanus.

EDCS-23801985.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Munatia Saturnina, daughter of Lucius, lived 23 years. May your bones rest well. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Munatia Procula, daughter of Lucius, lived 25 years. May your bones rest well. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Munatius Urbanus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 40 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23801985.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lives and burials of three individuals: Munatia Saturnina, Munatia Procula, and Munatius Urbanus, all children of Lucius.

EDCS-23801986.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Mussius Nampamo lived 81 years.
EDCS-23801986.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of Lucius Mussius Nampamo, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-23801987.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Mussius Florentius lived; Mussia Castula lived 34 years; Mussius Florinus lived 30 years.
EDCS-23801987.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the ages of Mussius Florentius, Mussia Castula, and Mussius Florinus.

EDCS-23801988.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Numisia Secundilla lived 30 years; her bones lie here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801988.summary: A funerary epitaph for Numisia Secundilla, stating her age and expressing a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-23801989.translation: Quintus Octavius Felix, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 80 years; his bones lie here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801989.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Octavius Felix, stating his age, tribal affiliation, and expressing a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-23801990.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Octavius Saturninus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years; his bones lie here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801990.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Octavius Saturninus, stating his age, tribal affiliation, and expressing a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-23801991.translation: Marcus Oculatius Faustinus lived 18 years; his bones lie here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801991.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Oculatius Faustinus, stating his age and expressing a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-23801992.translation: Marcus Pescennius Catullinus lived 45 years; his bones lie here; Quintus Pescennius Urbanus lived 39 years; his bones lie here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801992.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Pescennius Catullinus and Quintus Pescennius Urbanus, stating their ages and expressing a wish for their peaceful rest.

EDCS-23801993.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Pompeius Ingenuus lived 41 years.
EDCS-23801993.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of Quintus Pompeius Ingenuus.

EDCS-23801994.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Pompeiano lived 80 years; may your bones rest well. To Gaius Julius Rufinus lived 10 months.
EDCS-23801994.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pompeiano (age 80) and Gaius Julius Rufinus (10 months), indicating a double burial or adjacent plots.

EDCS-23801995.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of Porcia Ampliata.

EDCS-23801996.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Postumius Saturninus lived 56 years and 6 months. Postumia Urbana lived 11 years. Here lie the remains in memory of the Postumius family and of his wife Lucilia Urbana, who lived 60 years. Here lie the remains.
EDCS-23801996.summary: This funerary inscription records the lives and burials of Marcus Postumius Saturninus, his wife Lucilia Urbana, and Postumia Urbana, likely their daughter, and commemorates the Postumius family.

EDCS-23801997.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publicia Vitellia lived 100 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23801997.summary: This funerary inscription records the long life of Publicia Vitellia and is a dedication to the spirits of the departed.

EDCS-23801998.translation: Sextus Pullius Avitus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 41 years. He lies here. Pullia Matrona, daughter of Sextus, lived 12 years. She lies here.
EDCS-23801998.summary: This funerary inscription records the lives and burials of Sextus Pullius Avitus and Pullia Matrona.

EDCS-23801999.translation: In memory of the Tatii. Lucius Tatius Neptunalis, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-23801999.summary: This funerary inscription is a self-funded tomb (memoria) for Lucius Tatius Neptunalis, dedicating it to the memory of the Tatius family.

EDCS-23802000.translation: Quintus Pullius Bassus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 25 years. He lies here.
EDCS-23802000.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Quintus Pullius Bassus.

EDCS-23802001.translation: Lucius Pullius Comatus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 10 years. He lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23802001.summary: This funerary inscription records the short life and burial of Lucius Pullius Comatus.

EDCS-23802002.translation: Quintus Sallustius Rusticus lived 55 years. He lies here. Caius Sallustius Saturninus lived 35 years. He lies here.
EDCS-23802002.summary: This funerary inscription records the lives and burials of Quintus Sallustius Rusticus and Caius Sallustius Saturninus.

EDCS-23802003.translation: In memory of Salustia Honorata, who lived 44 years. Iulia Rufina placed this for her mother.
EDCS-23802003.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Salustia Honorata and was placed by her daughter, Iulia Rufina.

EDCS-23802004.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cnaeus Seius Iucundus lived 80 years. He lies here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Laelia Proc...
EDCS-23802004.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Cnaeus Seius Iucundus and begins a dedication for Laelia Proc.

EDCS-23802005.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sempronius Caecilianus lived 70 years while alive. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Sempronius Crescentianus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 35 years, and his wife Quintilla, are consecrated under this tomb. Marcus Sempronius Caecilianus made this for his most holy parents.
EDCS-23802005.summary: This funerary inscription records the self-dedication of Sempronius Caecilianus and the burial of his son Marcus Sempronius Crescentianus and his wife Quintilla, built by Caecilianus for his parents.

EDCS-23802006.translation: Saturnina lived 30 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-23802006.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Saturnina, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-23802007.translation: [---]tius, son of Quintus, Dona, lived 40 years; may your bones rest well here.
EDCS-23802007.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, possibly for Dona, recording her name (partially restored), father's name, and age at death.

EDCS-23802008.translation: Marcus Sittius Frontillus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 23 years; he is laid here; may your bones rest well here.
EDCS-23802008.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Sittius Frontillus, recording his name, father's name, tribal affiliation, age at death, and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23802009.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Honoratus, son of Marcus Sitius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 55 years; he is here; may he rest well. His wife Artoria Faustina lived 75 years; may you rest well here.
EDCS-23802009.summary: This is a joint funerary epitaph for Honoratus and his wife Artoria Faustina, recording their names, parentage (for Honoratus), tribal affiliation (for Honoratus), ages at death, and wishes for their repose.

EDCS-23802010.translation: Publius Sittius Maximus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 31 years; may your bones rest well here.
EDCS-23802010.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Sittius Maximus, recording his name, father's name, tribal affiliation, and age at death.

EDCS-23802011.translation: Publius Sittius Sedatus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 19 years; he is laid here; may your bones rest well here.
EDCS-23802011.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Sittius Sedatus, recording his name, father's name, tribal affiliation, age at death, and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23802012.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Publius Sittius Velox, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 65 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-23802012.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Sittius Velox, recording his name, father's name, tribal affiliation, and age at death.

EDCS-23802013.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sittius lived 7 years; may your bones rest well here. Geminius Avellius lived 85 years; he is laid here; may your bones rest well here. Quintus Caecilius Rufinus lived 51 years; may your bones rest well here.
EDCS-23802013.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely for multiple individuals including Sittius, Geminius Avellius, and Quintus Caecilius Rufinus, recording their names and ages at death.

EDCS-23802014.translation: Sittia Eleutera, daughter of Publius, lived 37 years; she is laid here; may your bones rest well here.
EDCS-23802014.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sittia Eleutera, recording her name, father's name, age at death, and a wish for her repose.

EDCS-23802015.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sittia Extricata lived 60 years.
EDCS-23802015.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sittia Extricata, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-23802016.translation: Sittia Spicula lived 55 years, she is buried here.
EDCS-23802016.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sittia Spicula, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-23802017.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sertinia Maia lived 40 years, she is buried here.
EDCS-23802017.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sertinia Maia, a dedication to the Manes, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-23802018.translation: Turia Optata lived 40 years, she is buried here.
EDCS-23802018.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Turia Optata, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-23802019.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valia Matrona lived 37 years. Surdinia Rogatula lived 12 years.
EDCS-23802019.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valia Matrona and Surdinia Rogatula, a dedication to the Manes, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-23802020.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Valerius Clar us, son of Quintus, of the voting tribe Quirina, lived 68 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-23802020.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Valerius Clar us, a dedication to the Manes, recording his age at death and burial.

EDCS-23802021.translation: Quintus Valerius Sodalis lived 65 years, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23802021.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Valerius Sodalis, recording his age at death and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-23802022.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valgia Saturnina, recording her age at death.

EDCS-23802023.translation: Lucius Vetlo lived 18 years, he is buried here. Lucius Vet Rufinus lived 45 years. Alfia Procula, daughter of Caius, lived 38 years, she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23802023.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Vetlo, Lucius Vet Rufinus, and Alfia Procula, recording their ages at death and burials, with a wish for repose.

EDCS-23802024.translation: Caius Vibius Felix lived 70 years, may your bones rest well. Caecilia Maniosa lived 60 years, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23802024.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Vibius Felix and Caecilia Maniosa, recording their ages at death and wishes for their repose.

EDCS-23802025.translation: Victor, slave of the Vibii, lived 60 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-23802025.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Victor, a slave of the Vibii family, recording his age at death and burial.

EDCS-23802026.translation: To the memory of Vitruvius, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina. He lived 24 years.
EDCS-23802026.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Vitruvius.

EDCS-23802027.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Volusius Felix, son of Marcus, of the tribe Quirina, lived 61 years, and for his wife Iulia Iulosa, who lived 55 years. He is buried here, and he made this for himself by his own merits.
EDCS-23802027.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Volusius Felix and his wife, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE, noting their ages at death and his self-funded burial.

EDCS-23802028.translation: Urbana lived 30 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23802028.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Urbana, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-23802029.translation: To the departed spirits. Urania Donia lived 54 years and 8 months.
EDCS-23802029.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Urania Donia.

EDCS-23802030.translation: Urbanus lived 35 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-23802030.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Urbanus.

EDCS-23802032.translation: To the departed spirits. Proclia Narcissa, daughter of Marcus, lived 10 years. Proclia, daughter of Ido, son of Marcus.
EDCS-23802032.summary: This funerary inscription records the short life of Proclia Narcissa and mentions another Proclia, likely her parent or relative.

EDCS-23802033.translation: Considia Donata lived 15 years. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-23802033.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Considia Donata.

EDCS-23802035.translation: That it may be good and fortunate and successful. Sacred to the Holy Lord Saturn, soul for soul, blood for blood, life for life, for salvation and vow for vow. Molchomor Rufus and C. (?) paid their vow.
EDCS-23802035.summary: This is a dedication to the god Saturn, likely from a private individual fulfilling a vow, possibly from late antiquity.

EDCS-23802036.translation: That it may be good and fortunate. Sacred to the Holy Lord Saturn, great nocturnal god. Soul for soul, blood for blood, life for life, for the salvation of Concessa, from what was seen and by vow. Molchomor Felix and Diodora gladly rendered the sacrifice for the substitute.
EDCS-23802036.summary: This is a dedication to the god Saturn, likely from late antiquity, recording a vow made by Molchomor Felix and Diodora for the salvation of Concessa.

EDCS-24000004.translation: For your thirtieth anniversary, our lords Diocletian and Maximian, eternal Augusti, and Constantius and Maximian, most noble Caesars, for your most fortunate twentieth anniversary. The council of our municipality, while Aurelius Quintianus, most perfect man, governed your province of Numidia, made an arch. Pompeius Donatus, perpetual priest, and Sittius Frontonianus, pontiff, made it at their own expense.
EDCS-24000004.summary: This inscription records the erection of an arch by local officials in Numidia to celebrate the twentieth anniversary of the Augusti Diocletian and Maximian and the Caesars Constantius and Maximian, likely around 303 CE.

EDCS-24000007.translation: For the eternal well-being of our lords Valentinian and Valens, perpetual Augusti, Publius Ceionius Caecina Albinus, most distinguished man, consular governor of the province of Numidia, arranging and dedicating the arch, and Popilius Concessus, perpetual priest, made this in honor of the priesthood.
EDCS-24000007.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an arch in Numidia by the governor Publius Ceionius Caecina Albinus and the perpetual priest Popilius Concessus, likely during the reign of Valentinian and Valens (4th century CE).

EDCS-24000008.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Antonius Fronto lived 31 years.
EDCS-24000008.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Antonius Fronto, who died at the age of 31.

EDCS-24000009.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Prosa Primosa lived 21 years.
EDCS-24000009.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Prosa Primosa, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-24000011.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Julianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Ulpius Mariscianus, most distinguished man, consular governor of the province of Numidia, dedicated to his divine will, offered by the order.
EDCS-24000011.summary: This inscription records the dedication to Emperor Julian by Ulpius Mariscianus, the consular governor of Numidia, likely in the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-24000012.translation: To Innocentia, daughter of Quintus Peticius, of Numidia. She lived 1 year and 2 days.
EDCS-24000012.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Innocentia, the daughter of Quintus Peticius, who died at a very young age.

EDCS-24000014.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Egnatius Napulus lived 19 years. NDVAIARIAN Cai Valeri Esiicis.
EDCS-24000014.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Egnatius Napulus, who died at the age of 19, with additional names that may refer to his family or patrons.

EDCS-24000015.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Egrilia Maxima lived 75 years.
EDCS-24000015.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Egrilia Maxima, who died at the age of 75.

EDCS-24000016.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius M S lived 57 years.
EDCS-24000016.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius M S, who died at the age of 57.

EDCS-24000017.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Peticius Severus lived 41 years.
EDCS-24000017.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quintus Peticius Severus, who died at the age of 41.

EDCS-24000018.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus S Primus lived 20 years.
EDCS-24000018.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quintus S Primus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-24000019.translation: From the workshop of Anteius, MM [perhaps initials of the craftsman or a measure of quantity].
EDCS-24000019.summary: This is an inscription likely from a workshop or indicating a product, possibly dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24000020.translation: Peticius, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, Victor, a memorable equestrian, a man, lived 50 years.
EDCS-24000020.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Peticius Victor, an equestrian, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24000022.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Salustius Gaius lived 70 years. Turania Fortunata lived 70 years.
EDCS-24000022.summary: This is a double funerary inscription for Salustius Gaius and Turania Fortunata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24000023.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Marcus Ulpius Saturninus lived 67 years.
EDCS-24000023.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Saturninus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24000029.translation: For the good of the state, born to our lords Constantinus Augustus, colonists.
EDCS-24000029.summary: This inscription is a dedication by colonists to Emperor Constantine, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-24100246.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar, designated emperor, son of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of Caesar Commodus, grandson of Caesar Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of Caesar Hadrianus, great-great-grandson of Caesar Traianus Parthicus, descendant of Caesar Nerva, descendant of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Parthicus Arabicus Parthicus Adiabenicus, propagator of the empire, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 5th time, imperator for the 8th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, the most courageous and most holy prince, the state of the Siguitani.
EDCS-24100246.summary: This is a dedication from the state of the Siguitani to Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar (likely Caracalla), dating to the period of his co-rule with Septimius Severus (around 198-202 CE).

EDCS-24100248.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the camps, wife of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, daughter of Caesar Commodus, granddaughter of Caesar Antoninus Pius, great-granddaughter of Caesar Hadrianus, great-great-granddaughter of Caesar Traianus Parthicus, descendant of Caesar Nerva, descendant of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Parthicus Arabicus Parthicus Adiabenicus, propagator of the empire, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 5th time, emperor for the 9th time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the country, proconsul, by decree of the decurions of the state of the Tidditani.
EDCS-24100248.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the state of the Tidditani to Julia Domna, wife of Septimius Severus, dating to approximately 202-211 CE.

EDCS-24100250.translation: Julius Anianus the Jew placed this for his dearest father, lived 80 years.
EDCS-24100250.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Julius Anianus for his father, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24100251.translation: Pompeia Cara made this for her most beloved Pompeius Restutus the Jew.
EDCS-24100251.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Pompeia Cara for her father, Pompeius Restutus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24100252.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Julia Victoria the Jewess, who...
EDCS-24100252.summary: This is a partial funerary inscription for Julia Victoria, a Jewish woman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24100253.translation: Satia Iudaea, daughter of Rufinus, for herself and her family.
EDCS-24100253.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Satia Iudaea, possibly on a tomb or as a votive offering.

EDCS-24100254.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aria Anulla, high priestess, lived 101 years. She was a good priestess.
EDCS-24100254.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aria Anulla, who lived to be 101 and was a high priestess.

EDCS-24100255.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. A monument for Gaius Sittius Castrensis, father. Lived 90 years.
EDCS-24100255.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument for Gaius Sittius Castrensis, who lived to be 90.

EDCS-24100256.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Pl Genita lived 21 years.
EDCS-24100256.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pl Genita, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-24100257.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Sittius Felix lived 51 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-24100257.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Sittius Felix, who lived to be 51.

EDCS-24100259.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus I Felix lived 55 years. He is buried here. To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Vin Dieta lived 61 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-24100259.summary: This inscription is a double funerary text for Marcus I Felix (died aged 55) and Iulia Vin Dieta (died aged 61).

EDCS-24100264.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Naevia Lucilla lived 35 years. She is buried here. To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Flavius Nampulus lived 55 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-24100264.summary: This inscription is a double funerary text for Naevia Lucilla (died aged 35) and Publius Flavius Nampulus (died aged 55).

EDCS-24100265.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Numisia Honorata lived 22 years.
EDCS-24100265.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Numisia Honorata at the age of 22.

EDCS-24100266.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Petreia Extricata lived 80 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-24100266.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Petreia Extricata, who lived to be 80.

EDCS-24100267.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Petreia Ianuaria lived 90 years.
EDCS-24100267.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Petreia Ianuaria at the age of 90.

EDCS-24100268.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Propertius Victor lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-24100268.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Propertius Victor, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-24100269.translation: Tadia Sisipa, daughter of Tadius, lived 13 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-24100269.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tadia Sisipa, who lived 13 years.

EDCS-24100271.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Valerius Victor lived 71 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-24100271.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Victor, who lived 71 years.

EDCS-24100272.translation: Marcus Laelius Urbanus gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-24100272.summary: This is an inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Marcus Laelius Urbanus.

EDCS-24100273.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. His father Agilis set this up for his son Primosus, most innocent, who lived 5 years.
EDCS-24100273.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a father for his young son, Primosus, who died at the age of 5.

EDCS-24100275.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Bonia Rogata lived 75 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-24100275.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Bonia Rogata, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-24100277.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Calidius Fortunatus lived [---] years; he is buried here. To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Calidius Extricatus lived 20 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-24100277.summary: This inscription records the burials of two individuals, Caius Calidius Fortunatus and Marcus Calidius Extricatus, with the latter's age given as 20.

EDCS-24100278.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a son who died at the age of 3.

EDCS-24100279.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed and sacred. Fuficia Rogata lived 55 years.
EDCS-24100279.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fuficia Rogata, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-24100280.translation: To Saturnus Augustus, sacred. Iulius Martialis gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-24100280.summary: This is an inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to Saturnus Augustus by Iulius Martialis.

EDCS-24100282.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Quintus Cofidius Victorinus lived 22 years.
EDCS-24100282.summary: A funerary dedication for Quintus Cofidius Victorinus, who died at the age of 22.

EDCS-24100283.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Matrona Griana, lying here, lived 80 years.
EDCS-24100283.summary: A funerary dedication for Matrona Griana, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-24100285.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulia Saturnina lived 18 years. Sextus Iulius Saturninus made this for his most rare daughter.
EDCS-24100285.summary: A funerary dedication made by Sextus Iulius Saturninus for his daughter, Iulia Saturnina, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-24100288.translation: Iulius Iucundus, the husband, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-24100288.summary: A dedication recording a vow fulfilled by Iulius Iucundus.

EDCS-24100289.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sixtius Sai, lived 50 years and 16 months. His sons made this for their dearest father. He lies here.
EDCS-24100289.summary: A funerary dedication made by the sons for their father, Sixtius Sai, who died at the age of 50 years and 16 months.

EDCS-24100292.translation: The Legate of the Emperor, a man of highest distinction, dedicated this.
EDCS-24100292.summary: A dedication made by a Legatus Augusti pro praetore, a high-ranking imperial official.

EDCS-24100294.summary: An inscription identifying Marcus Coelius Felix as a priest.

EDCS-24100295.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Felicissimus lived 62 years.
EDCS-24100295.summary: A funerary dedication for Felicissimus, who died at the age of 62.

EDCS-24100296.summary: An inscription indicating a table belonging to Adeodatus.

EDCS-24100297.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Domitius Maximianus lived 85 years. He lies here.
EDCS-24100297.summary: A funerary dedication for Domitius Maximianus, who died at the age of 85.

EDCS-24100298.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Victor lived 110 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-24100298.summary: A funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Gaius Julius Victor, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24200380.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Caecilius Butel lived 51 years. He is buried here. His wife Flavia Salvilla made this.
EDCS-24200380.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Flavia Salvilla for her husband Quintus Caecilius Butel, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24200383.translation: The daughter of Lucius Flaccus, Urbana, lived 115 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-24200383.summary: A funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Urbana, daughter of Lucius Flaccus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24300182.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Anno, the priest, lived 65 years. He is buried here. His devoted wife made this.
EDCS-24300182.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband, Anno the priest, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24300272.translation: For Faustina Augusta, wife of Emperor Caesar Antoninus Augustus, conqueror of the Armenians, greatest conqueror of the Parthians, the Medes, at Thibilis, with public money, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-24300272.summary: An inscription dedicating a public monument or honorific statue to Empress Faustina, wife of Marcus Aurelius, funded by the state in Thibilis, likely dating to the Antonine period.

EDCS-24300274.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Quinta, daughter of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 95 years.
EDCS-24300274.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Caecilia Quinta, daughter of Marcus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24300275.translation: Publius Clodius Quadratus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 53 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-24300275.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Publius Clodius Quadratus, son of Marcus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24300276.translation: Helvia Victoria, daughter of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 95 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-24300276.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Helvia Victoria, daughter of Lucius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24300277.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Lucia lived 65 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-24300277.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Iulia Lucia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24300278.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Pinneus, son of Caius Pinneus, of the Quirina tribe. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Aurelia, daughter of Iulius Aurelius Gargilius.
EDCS-24300278.summary: This inscription appears to be a palimpsest or a poorly executed funerary monument, containing fragments relating to both Gaius Pinneus and Iulia Aurelia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24300281.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Gaius Munatius Victor and his wife Antonia Maxima, who always lived well together.
EDCS-24300281.summary: A funerary dedication to the deified dead for a couple, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24300282.translation: He lived 65 years; may your bones rest well. Iulia Marcellina, daughter of Publius, lived 45 years; may your bones rest well. Gaius Julius Kalpurnianus lived 18 years; he is buried here; may your bones rest well. Publius Julius Timon, freedman of Faustus, lived 20 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-24300282.summary: A funerary inscription recording the ages at death and providing blessings for the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24300283.summary: A dedication to Emperor Constantius, likely from the Late Roman Empire.

EDCS-24300286.translation: To the memory of Victor Dudda, erected by his deserving husband.
EDCS-24300286.summary: A funerary monument set up by a husband for his deceased wife, Victor Dudda, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24300289.summary: An inscription indicating a table belonging to two individuals, Dacunis and Restuta, possibly a tombstone or a marker for a public space, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24300290.summary: An inscription indicating a table belonging to two individuals, Miginis and Sabina, possibly a tombstone or a marker for a public space, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400366.translation: Arruntia Honorata, daughter of Lucius, lived 41 years.
EDCS-24400366.summary: A funerary inscription recording the name and age at death of a woman, Arruntia Honorata, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400367.translation: Marcus Didius Romulus, freedman of Marcus, lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-24400367.summary: A funerary inscription recording the name, status, age at death, and burial location of Marcus Didius Romulus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400368.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Herennius, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, [lived ...].
EDCS-24400368.summary: A funerary dedication to the deified dead for Quintus Herennius, including his filiation and tribal affiliation, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400369.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publius Ovinius Martialis lived 100 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-24400369.summary: A funerary inscription recording the name, extreme age at death, and burial location of Publius Ovinius Martialis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400373.translation: To the memory of Caelius Felix, Caelius Felix [erected this] for a deserving father.
EDCS-24400373.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Caelius Felix for his father, also named Caelius Felix.

EDCS-24400374.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of Iulia Maxima.

EDCS-24400375.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of Lucius Callius Iulianus.

EDCS-24400376.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Tiberius Claudius Maximus lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-24400376.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death and burial place of Tiberius Claudius Maximus.

EDCS-24400379.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Furnius Felix lived 35 years.
EDCS-24400379.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of Lucius Furnius Felix.

EDCS-24400380.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Octavia Nicrosa lived 50 years; she is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Tiberius Claudius Pacatus lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-24400380.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death and burial place of Octavia Nicrosa and Tiberius Claudius Pacatus.

EDCS-24400381.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Proculus Catalus lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-24400381.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death and burial place of Proculus Catalus.

EDCS-24400382.translation: His wife Regilia Castula, with her children, made [this] for the most innocent [man].
EDCS-24400382.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Regilia Castula and her children for her husband.

EDCS-24400383.translation: Rogatus Faustiniani lived 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-24400383.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death and burial place of Rogatus Faustiniani.

EDCS-24400384.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Crescens lived 105 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-24400384.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death and burial place of Crescens.

EDCS-24400386.translation: Ardus lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-24400386.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and age of the deceased, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400388.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul.
EDCS-24400388.summary: A fragment of an inscription likely honouring an emperor, possibly for a public monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400855.translation: Quintus Baebius Bato willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-24400855.summary: A votive inscription recording the fulfilment of a vow by Quintus Baebius Bato, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400859.summary: A very brief funerary epitaph, possibly for a child, recording the name and age (likely 6 years old), dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400860.translation: Sacred to the Infernal Gods. Mascara Daobtio lived 55 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-24400860.summary: A funerary epitaph for Mascara Daobtio, recording her age and a wish for the deceased's repose, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24500174.translation: To the Holy God Frugifer Augustus, for the health of the Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Pius. Numerius Calatius Felix, procurator of the Mu cass estate.
EDCS-24500174.summary: A dedication to the god Frugifer Augustus by a procurator for the emperor's health, dating to the reign of Septimius Severus (AD 193-211).

EDCS-24500176.translation: To the divine spirit of the Nymphs and of Dracon. Abidius Bassus, tribune and curator of the municipium of Masc(a)ulita, gave a vow.
EDCS-24500176.summary: A votive inscription by Abidius Bassus, a tribune and curator, dedicated to Nymphs and a spirit named Dracon, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24500187.translation: Sacred to the Infernal Gods. Oseus Donatus lived for 63 years. His heirs made this for him out of piety.
EDCS-24500187.summary: A funerary epitaph for Oseus Donatus, recording his age and stating that his heirs erected it due to piety, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24500188.translation: Sacred to the Infernal Gods. Aphrodisia the pious lived 16 years and 2 months and 11 days. Saturninus, father, for his most pious daughter.
EDCS-24500188.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aphrodisia, recording her age at death and erected by her father, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24500189.translation: Sacred to the Infernal Gods. Gaius Donatus lived 90 years. His heirs made this for their father; they made it during their lifetime.
EDCS-24500189.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Donatus, recording his age and stating that his heirs erected it during their own lifetime, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24500190.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Kampatia Dativa, dutiful, lived 9 years; she is placed here.
EDCS-24500190.summary: A funerary inscription for Kampatia Dativa, a young girl, dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500191.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Saturninus made (this) for his wife.
EDCS-24500191.summary: A funerary inscription by Saturninus for his deceased wife, dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500192.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Terentius Datus, dutiful, lived 85 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-24500192.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Terentius Datus, an elderly man, dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500194.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Triaccius, dutiful, lived 11 years.
EDCS-24500194.summary: A funerary inscription for Triaccius, a child, dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500195.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caelia Vera lived.
EDCS-24500195.summary: A funerary inscription for Caelia Vera, dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500198.translation: Iulius Pullenus P T Spicaunis lived 63 years and 7 months. Iulia Mazica, wife of Verus, lived 67 years and 7 months.
EDCS-24500198.summary: A funerary inscription for two individuals, Iulius Pullenus and Iulia Mazica, dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500199.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Caius.
EDCS-24500199.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius, dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500200.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Enia Secunda, 80 years (old).
EDCS-24500200.summary: A funerary inscription for Enia Secunda, an elderly woman, dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500204.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Isrix lived, dutiful, 30 years; she is deposited here.
EDCS-24500204.summary: A funerary inscription for Isrix, a woman, dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500207.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Aemilius Potitus lived 75 years. Aemilia Caliste made (this) in her own lifetime for herself, her husband, and her son Aemilius, (who) lived 25 years and deserved (it).
EDCS-24500207.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Aemilius Potitus and his family, commissioned by Aemilia Caliste, dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500208.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Augustus, for the safety of the Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius.
EDCS-24500208.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter for the emperor's well-being, likely from the Antonine period.

EDCS-24500210.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Claudia Sedatia lived 70 years. Quintus Claudius Victor, her son, Lucius's son, her husband, erected this monument. He lived 15 years.
EDCS-24500210.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Sedatia and her husband Quintus Claudius Victor, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-24500211.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Titus Flavius Seneca lived 30 years. Flavia P, his mother, made this for her son.
EDCS-24500211.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Seneca, made by his mother, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-24500212.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Rogatus Severi lived 75 years. Cornelia Letoria lived 82 years. The heirs made this for the deceased.
EDCS-24500212.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rogatus Severi and Cornelia Letoria, with the monument erected by their heirs, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-24500213.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Seius Clebonianus, also known as Lactantius, lived 35 years.
EDCS-24500213.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Seius Clebonianus, who also went by the name Lactantius, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-24500215.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Volusenius Donatus lived 70 years. Volusennus Donatus and Felix, his grandsons, made this.
EDCS-24500215.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Volusenius Donatus, with the monument erected by his grandsons, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-24500216.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Julianus, Pious, Fortunate, Ever Victorious Augustus.
EDCS-24500216.summary: This is a dedication to the emperor Flavius Claudius Julianus (Julian the Apostate), likely from the 4th century AD.

EDCS-24500218.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Cecilius Anci us lived 65 years. OM DA MI INA lived 60 years. He made this for himself and made this for himself.
EDCS-24500218.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cecilius Anci us and another individual (OM DA MI INA), likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-24500219.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Cecilius Namphamo lived 61 years.
EDCS-24500219.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cecilius Namphamo, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-24500220.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Didius Sinus lived 40 years, and Aurelia rona, his wife. Helvia made this for her family.
EDCS-24500220.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Didius Sinus and his wife Aurelia rona, with the monument erected by Helvia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-24500222.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Arrius Clodius lived 75 years. His parents placed this monument. He is buried here.
EDCS-24500222.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Gaius Arrius Clodius, who lived to be 75 years old, and states that his parents erected the monument.

EDCS-24500223.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Prima Secundina lived...
EDCS-24500223.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph recording the name Prima Secundina and the start of her lifespan.

EDCS-24500224.translation: Emperor Caesar Septimius [---] II [---] IVG FE NVG
EDCS-24500224.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragmented imperial dedication, possibly mentioning Emperor Septimius Severus and consuls or other officials.

EDCS-24500225.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vettia TA, a respectable matron, lived...
EDCS-24500225.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph identifying Vettia TA as a matron and beginning to state her lifespan.

EDCS-24500226.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ulpius Tinus, Tamat NAONNI, pious, lived... RVXTI made this.
EDCS-24500226.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name Ulpius Tinus and mentions RVXTI as the maker of the monument.

EDCS-24500228.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelius Crescens lived 65 years. Aelianus made this monument for his dearest father and for Diogenia Piperi, while she was alive.
EDCS-24500228.summary: This funerary epitaph records Aelius Crescens' death at age 65 and that Aelianus erected the monument for his father and Diogenia Piperi.

EDCS-24500229.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cocceia Salvia lived 18 years. Her parent made this for her deserving daughter.
EDCS-24500229.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Cocceia Salvia at age 18 and states her parent erected the monument.

EDCS-24500230.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Felix CV C FA lived 60 years.
EDCS-24500230.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph recording the name Felix and his lifespan of 60 years.

EDCS-24500231.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fortunatus lived 60 years. Ingenuus, his son, made this at his own expense.
EDCS-24500231.summary: This funerary epitaph records Fortunatus' death at age 60 and that his son Ingenuus erected the monument.

EDCS-24500234.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelia Fortunata lived 75 years. Cornelius Severus made this.
EDCS-24500234.summary: This funerary epitaph records Cornelia Fortunata's death at age 75 and that Cornelius Severus erected the monument.

EDCS-24500235.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Florius Saturninus, pious, lived 85 years, and Valeria Kara, his wife, pious, lived 80 years. Their sons made this for their pious parents. He is buried here.
EDCS-24500235.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Florius Saturninus and his wife Valeria Kara, set up by their sons.

EDCS-24500236.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Floria lived 75 years. Her heirs made this at their own expense.
EDCS-24500236.summary: A tombstone for Floria, erected by her heirs.

EDCS-24500237.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Monna, daughter of Nampamonis, lived 71 years. They dedicated this to her while she was alive.
EDCS-24500237.summary: A tombstone for Monna, daughter of Nampamonis, dedicated by her family during her lifetime.

EDCS-24500238.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Valerius Sambucus lived [X] years. He is buried here.

EDCS-24500241.translation: Aliamia Mirona lived 26 years. ICius Saibunus made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-24500241.summary: A tombstone for Aliamia Mirona, dedicated by her husband.

EDCS-24500242.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of an individual, whose name is lost.

EDCS-24500243.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aponia Maxima lived 28 years and Buca, her daughter, lived 7 years.
EDCS-24500243.summary: A tombstone for Aponia Maxima and her daughter Buca.

EDCS-24500244.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus C A [name] lived 65 years. [Text illegible]
EDCS-24500244.summary: A tombstone for Marcus C A, likely recording his lifespan.

EDCS-24500245.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius K Ianuarius lived 95 years. Dedicated by his sons.
EDCS-24500245.summary: A tombstone for Caius K Ianuarius, dedicated by his sons.

EDCS-24500250.translation: To Concordia, of our August Emperors, the Imperators Lucius Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Augustus, and Julia Augusta. Lucius Licinius Optatianus, because of the office of perpetual flamen, promised statues with bases, which for 20,000 sesterces beyond the legal amount, he dedicated with expanded funds for 35,000 sesterces, and he dedicated them with sportulae given to decurions and with banquets and stage performances held for the curiae and games.
EDCS-24500250.summary: A dedication by Lucius Licinius Optatianus to Concordia, honoring the emperors Severus and Caracalla and Empress Julia Domna, for the office of perpetual flamen, involving the dedication of statues and public distributions.

EDCS-24500251.translation: To Diana Augusta, Publius Iulius [dedicated this].
EDCS-24500251.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Diana Augusta by Publius Iulius, likely on a small shrine or altar, with an approximate date in the early Imperial period.

EDCS-24500252.translation: Cara, priestess and Tranquilla, [having purchased] the statue with money from Protus and his father Annius Hilarius, which Annius Protus had bequeathed in his will for 22,000 sesterces, erected it, and with an added shrine from their own funds at a cost of 4,400 sesterces, dedicated it by decree of the town council.
EDCS-24500252.summary: This is a dedication of a statue and a shrine to Fortuna Augusta by Cara and Tranquilla, funded by the legacy of Annius Protus, with the construction costs specified, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-24500255.translation: Quintus Mamius Cassianus, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, [erected] the statue to the Genius of the Roman People and to Augustus, which he had promised in honor of his aedileship, also bringing into public funds a sum of money, and he himself dedicated it by decree of the town council.
EDCS-24500255.summary: This is a dedication of a statue to the Genius of the Roman People and Augustus by Quintus Mamius Cassianus in honor of his aedileship, funded partly by his own money and partly by public funds, likely from the late Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-24500256.translation: To Mars Augustus the Preserver of our Lords Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Germanicus Maximus, Augusti, and Julia Domna.
EDCS-24500256.summary: This is an inscription honoring Mars Augustus the Preserver, dedicated to the emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, Empress Julia Domna, and their titles, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-24500258.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus for the health of our Lords Emperors Severus and Antoninus and Geta Caesar, the most valiant Augusti, and Julia Augusta, mother of the Augusti and of the camps. Lucius Germeus Silvanus, augur, [having paid] 21,200 sesterces into public funds for the honor of his augurship, placed Mercury [here] from his own generosity, and for the dedication he presented stage plays.
EDCS-24500258.summary: This is a dedication of a statue to Mercury Augustus by Lucius Germeus Silvanus, an augur, for the health of the imperial family, including Caracalla and Geta, with specified costs and public entertainment, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-24500259.translation: Sacred to Victoria Augusta. Lucius Julius Bradua, son of Caius, of the Papiria tribe, aedile and prefect for dispensing justice, [having paid] into public funds the statutory amount for his aedileship, erected the statue which he had promised for 3,000 sesterces, for 3,900 sesterces, and he himself dedicated it by decree of the town council.
EDCS-24500259.summary: This is a dedication of a statue to Victoria Augusta by Lucius Julius Bradua, an aedile and prefect, specifying the costs of the statue and its dedication, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-24500260.translation: Marcus Varius Impetratianus gave the consecrated altar as a gift.
EDCS-24500260.summary: This is a gift of a consecrated altar by Marcus Varius Impetratianus, likely a private donation, with an indeterminate date.

EDCS-24500262.translation: For the health and victory of the Emperor Nerva Trajan Caesar Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, founder of the colony, Titus Flavius Felix, contractor of the quintae Marsianae, made [this] at his own expense. The Ides.
EDCS-24500262.summary: This inscription records the construction of a monument or religious object by Titus Flavius Felix, a contractor, for Emperor Trajan, the founder of the colony, likely dating to Trajan's reign (98-117 CE).

EDCS-24500266.translation: To Lucius Aelius Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian Augustus, consul for the second time, by decree of the town council, at public expense.
EDCS-24500266.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Aelius Caesar, son of Hadrian and consul, erected by public expense via a decree of the town council, likely dating to his lifetime (c. 136-138 CE).

EDCS-24500274.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland, by decree of the town council, at public expense.
EDCS-24500274.summary: This is a public dedication to Emperor Antoninus Pius, honoring him as father of the fatherland, by decree of the town council, likely dating to his reign (138-161 CE).

EDCS-24500276.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Caesar, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-24500276.summary: This inscription records a public dedication to Marcus Aurelius Caesar, funded by the local council.

EDCS-24500277.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Caesar, son of Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-24500277.summary: This inscription records a public dedication to Marcus Aurelius Caesar, son of Antoninus Pius, funded by the local council.

EDCS-24500278.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Caesar, son of Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, Marcus Caelius Saturninus, son of Marcus, of the Horatian tribe, for the honor of his quinquennial office, having presented to the state five thousand sesterces, set up this [monument] and dedicated it, with a decree of the decurions.
EDCS-24500278.summary: This inscription records a private dedication by Marcus Caelius Saturninus to Marcus Aurelius Caesar, commemorating his quinquennial office with a donation of 5000 sesterces, funded by public decree.
