EDCS-25600050.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Fausta, who lived 50 years, with a wish for her repose.

EDCS-25600052.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus C. Martialis.
EDCS-25600052.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Dis Manes by Quintus C. Martialis.

EDCS-25600053.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Fra res. He lived 60 years.
EDCS-25600053.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Fra res, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-25600054.translation: Quintus I. Iunior lived 72 years. Victoria lived 70 years. Quintus I. Victor, during his own lifetime.
EDCS-25600054.summary: This epitaph records the lifespans of Quintus I. Iunior (72 years) and Victoria (70 years), and notes it was set up by Quintus I. Victor during his lifetime.

EDCS-25600055.translation: To the memory of Quintus Julius Saturninus Tegonius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe. To the memory of Saturninus's most loving wife, Sittia Crescentia, daughter of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, who lived 18 years. Quintus Julius Saturninus Tegonius made the monument for his deserving son Tegnio, who lived 22 years. To the memory of his most holy wife, Favilia Julia Prisca Scholia, who lived 27 years. Quintus Julius Saturninus Tegonius.
EDCS-25600055.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Quintus Julius Saturninus Tegonius for his son Tegnio, his wife Favilia Julia Prisca Scholia, and his first wife Sittia Crescentia, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-25600056.translation: Malianus Rogatianus, a most reverend man, has been laid to rest here. This place [is a] table.
EDCS-25600056.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Malianus Rogatianus and indicates the burial plot contained a funerary table.

EDCS-25600057.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Digausus Aelius lived 5 years.
EDCS-25600057.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Digausus Aelius, who died at the age of 5.

EDCS-25600058.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Salus lived 75 years. For Primulia.
EDCS-25600058.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the spirits of the departed and records the death of Salus at 75 years, with a dedication to Primulia.

EDCS-25600059.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sittia Rogata lived 55 years and 2 months.
EDCS-25600059.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sittia Rogata, who lived 55 years and 2 months.

EDCS-25600060.translation: By decree of the decurions, at public expense: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul for the 2nd time, Father of the Fatherland.
EDCS-25600060.summary: This inscription is a public dedication honoring Emperor Septimius Severus, likely for a building or monument, funded by the local decurions.

EDCS-25600061.translation: The council of Tigisis, devoted to his divine majesty, set up this statue at its own expense and dedicated it to Flavius Valerius Constantinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-25600061.summary: This is a dedication by the council of Tigisis to Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-25600063.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Arria Gaetula lived 70 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600063.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Arria Gaetula, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600064.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Didustius Musteolus lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600064.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caius Didustius Musteolus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600082.translation: Aulus Vinnicius, son of Publius, of the Voting Tribe Quirina.
EDCS-25600082.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording the name and tribal affiliation of Aulus Vinnicius, likely part of a larger dedication or epitaph from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600083.translation: Lucretianus the father made this; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600083.summary: This is a tombstone inscription placed by Lucretianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600085.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Antistius Philetus, son of Quintus, of the Voting Tribe Quirina, lived 20 years; he is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Antistius, son of Quintus, of the Voting Tribe Quirina, and Antistia Valentina, daughter of Daphnis, lived 95 years; may you live and flourish, hope and fortune, farewell; she is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Antistia Victoria, daughter of Daphnis, lived 60 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600085.summary: This is a complex tombstone inscription for multiple family members, including Quintus Antistius Philetus, Antistia Valentina, and Antistia Victoria, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600086.translation: Omnus, son of S., lived 65 years. Iulia Boroc lived 55 years.
EDCS-25600086.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the lifespans of two individuals, likely a couple, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600087.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Ponponius Felix, during his own lifetime, and Sitia Urbana, who lived 51 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600087.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Sitia Urbana, set up by Quintus Ponponius Felix, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600088.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Rogata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600089.translation: Aelia Dativa lived 70 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600089.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Aelia Dativa, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600090.translation: Sacred to Juno Augusta. Cassia Rogata, having seen the head (of the statue), erected and dedicated this altar at her own expense; the site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-25600090.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to Juno Augusta by Cassia Rogata, with the site provided by local officials, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-25600091.translation: Sacred to Neptune Augustus. The worshippers from Cararis fulfilled their vow willingly.
EDCS-25600091.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by a group called the 'cultores Cararienses' to Neptune Augustus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-25600092.translation: Sacred to Venus Augusta. Lucius Iulius Cerialis, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-25600092.summary: This is a votive inscription to Venus Augusta by Lucius Iulius Cerialis, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-25600093.translation: Sacred to the divine Augusta Bellona. Marcus Satur, priest, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, made the portico with columns at his own expense.
EDCS-25600093.summary: This inscription records the construction of a portico with columns dedicated to Bellona Augusta by the priest Marcus Satur, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-25600094.translation: By decree of the decurions, in place of the III viri. Publius Sittius Veloxs, magistrate of the pagi, and Marcus Porrega Pacatus, Sextus Geminius Iulianus, Caius Iulius Saturninus, Marcus Arrius Priscus, Caius Aemilius Rufinus, Marcus Lucilius Lucianus, Titus Flavius Sabinus, Quintus Pompeius Anenna, Avennius Frontonianus, Mecilius Narcissus, Furius Cirinus, Quintus Marcius Baeticus, Sextus Apronius Lurianus, Quintus Caelius Saturninus, Publius Cornelius Caecilia, Caius Sittius Severus, Marcus Pompeius Proclianus, Marcus Lucilius Dexter, Publius Iulius Faustus, Lucius Licinius Marcellus, Quintus Clodius Quintillus, Marcus Aemilius Optatus, Caius Iulius Musteolus, Quintus Aufidius Saturninus.
EDCS-25600094.summary: This is a list of names, likely officials or prominent citizens, associated with a magistracy (praefecto pro III viris) and a pagi, decreed by the local decurions.

EDCS-25600095.translation: Iulia Monnica lived 3 years, more or less.
EDCS-25600095.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph recording the name and age of a child, Iulia Monnica.

EDCS-25600121.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Iulius Victor lived 95 years.
EDCS-25600121.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Iulius Victor, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-25600123.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Marcus Aemilius Septimianus lived 13 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-25600123.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name and age of Marcus Aemilius Septimianus, with a wish for his remains to rest well.

EDCS-25600124.translation: Titus Flavius Secundus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 82 years; may you rest well here, sacred to the spirits of the dead. Titus Flavius Secundianus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 35 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-25600124.summary: This epitaph records the lives and deaths of two individuals, Titus Flavius Secundus and his son Titus Flavius Secundianus, including wishes for their eternal rest.

EDCS-25600125.translation: Clodius Meridianus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 35 years. Lucilia Optata, daughter of Quintus, lived 70 years; she is buried here, may your bones rest well. Clodia Honorata, daughter of Marcus, lived 30 years; she is buried here, may your bones rest well. Clodius Possessor, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 42 years; may your bones rest well. Grasidia Saturnina, daughter of Caius, lived 23 years.
EDCS-25600125.summary: This extensive funerary inscription lists the names, ages at death, and burial locations of five individuals from the Clodius and Grasidia families, likely related, with wishes for their peace.

EDCS-25600127.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Ianuaria lived for 80 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600127.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Ianuaria, who lived to be 80 years old.

EDCS-25600128.translation: To the genius of the colony of Cirta, to Gulugae, king of the Numidians, son of King Masinissa, Drusus, prefect, a junior officer, dedicated this gift with his own money, having received the lightning strike.
EDCS-25600128.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Drusus, a prefect and junior officer, to the genius of Cirta and to Gulugae, king of the Numidians, son of Masinissa.

EDCS-25600129.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Arruntius Catulus lived for 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600129.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Arruntius Catulus, who lived for 35 years.

EDCS-25600130.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aulius Cudeus, dutiful, lived for about 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600130.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulius Cudeus, who lived for approximately 70 years.

EDCS-25600131.translation: To the memory of Domitia Festa, an infant.
EDCS-25600131.summary: This is a memorial inscription for an infant named Domitia Festa.

EDCS-25600132.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Felicia, dutiful, lived for 30 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600132.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Felicia, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-25600133.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Ianuaria, dutiful, lived for 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600133.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Ianuaria, who lived for 35 years.

EDCS-25600134.translation: Iulia Maxima, daughter of Caius, lived for 97 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600134.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Maxima, daughter of Caius, who lived to be 97 years old.

EDCS-25600135.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Manius Sabidius Martialis, dutiful, lived for 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600135.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Manius Sabidius Martialis, who lived for 50 years.

EDCS-25600138.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius AM dionius lived for 80 years.
EDCS-25600138.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius AM dionius, who lived for 80 years.

EDCS-25600141.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Caecilius Adiectus lived 5 years.
EDCS-25600141.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Caecilius Adiectus, who died at the age of 5.

EDCS-25600142.translation: Quintus Iulius Cerdo lived 5 years. Quintus Iulius Optatus, son of Quintus, lived 60 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600142.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two individuals, Quintus Iulius Cerdo and Quintus Iulius Optatus.

EDCS-25600143.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Gemellus lived 45 years. To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Matrona lived 35 years.
EDCS-25600143.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Gemellus and Iulia Matrona.

EDCS-25600144.translation: Quintus Iulius Novellus lived 21 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600144.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Iulius Novellus.

EDCS-25600145.translation: Q. Li[---] lived 100 years. Q. lius I[---] Pius lived.
EDCS-25600145.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph mentioning the lifespans of two individuals, possibly related.

EDCS-25600146.translation: In memory of na[---] filia ta lived.
EDCS-25600146.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription commemorating someone, likely a daughter.

EDCS-25600149.translation: Caninia Secundilla the pious lived 21 years.
EDCS-25600149.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caninia Secundilla.

EDCS-25600150.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Veratia Stratonice lived 26 years.
EDCS-25600150.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Veratia Stratonice.

EDCS-25600152.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Baebia Laurina lived 117 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25600152.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Baebia Laurina, who lived to a remarkable age.

EDCS-25600153.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Aemilius Ianuarius the younger, nicknamed 'Agentis'. In memory of his skill in letters, to Amandus, due to an unlucky fate and harsh fortune, who lived his life for sixteen years, but did not receive what he deserved, having met death by pestilence, innocent, while completing three ministries. May he rest well here, blessed and conscious of the heavens.
EDCS-25600153.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Aemilius Ianuarius Iunior, who died at 16 years old due to illness, with praise for his character and service.

EDCS-25600160.translation: For the memory of Gaius Annaeus Fortunatus, Gaius Flavius Satullus, on land given by himself, made [this tomb] for burial because of his dearest affection, and Ofelius Tiro, to whom he bequeathed his own frugality in his will, made [this tomb]. He lived 103 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600160.summary: This funerary monument commemorates Gaius Annaeus Fortunatus, who lived 103 years and is buried here, with the burial plot provided by Gaius Flavius Satullus and a legacy from Ofelius Tiro.

EDCS-25600161.summary: This is a tombstone for Adruentinus, who lived 51 years.

EDCS-25600162.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Euplia lived 70 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25600162.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits for Euplia, who lived 70 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25600163.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Lollius Noviciolus, grandson, lived five years and one day.
EDCS-25600163.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits for Quintus Lollius Noviciolus, a grandson, who lived five years and one day.

EDCS-25600164.translation: Iulia Victoria lived 25 years. Quintus Mevius Donatus lived 35 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600164.summary: This tombstone records the lives of Iulia Victoria (25 years) and Quintus Mevius Donatus (35 years), who is buried here.

EDCS-25600165.translation: Lucius Mevius Solutor lived 21 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600165.summary: This tombstone records the life of Lucius Mevius Solutor, who lived 21 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25600166.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, for the memory of Postumus Sertus, son of Lucius Victor. Lived [number of] years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600166.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits for Postumus Sertus, son of Lucius Victor, who is buried here, though his age is lost.

EDCS-25600168.translation: The worshippers of Neptune made the temple themselves with their own money, under the curators NIO Atr[---] and Eio Norbanus.
EDCS-25600168.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple to Neptune by its worshippers, funded by themselves, under the curators NIO Atr[---] and Eio Norbanus.

EDCS-25600169.translation: Sacred to Pluto Augustus. Tiberius Claudius Sinnulus willingly paid his vow for the health and safety of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-25600169.summary: This inscription records Tiberius Claudius Sinnulus fulfilling a vow made to Pluto Augustus for the well-being of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander.

EDCS-25600170.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fortunatus the treasurer made this memorial for himself while alive, and for his most rare and incomparable wife.
EDCS-25600170.summary: This tombstone was created by Fortunatus the treasurer for himself and his wife, indicating he set up his own memorial.

EDCS-25600171.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Fruendus, assistant in the office of our Caesar Verus, lived 33 years, 5 months, and 1 day. He is buried here. Vitulus made this.
EDCS-25600171.summary: This is a tombstone for Fruendus, an assistant in an imperial office, who died at age 33, erected by Vitulus.

EDCS-25600172.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius Aurelius Felix, son of Lucius, lived 51 years.
EDCS-25600172.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Aurelius Felix, who died at age 51.

EDCS-25600173.summary: This is a tombstone for Baebia Quinta, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-25600174.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Caius Caelius Donatus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 95 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600174.summary: This is a tombstone for Caius Caelius Donatus, who died at age 95.

EDCS-25600175.translation: Caelia Fortunata lived 25 years. She is buried here. Caelius Salvicus dedicated this for his sweetest daughter and for himself and his wife Pintunia.
EDCS-25600175.summary: This is a tombstone for Caelia Fortunata, who died at age 25, dedicated by her father Caelius Salvicus for her, himself, and his wife.

EDCS-25600176.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Cornelia Optata, 45 years.
EDCS-25600176.summary: This is a tombstone for Cornelia Optata, who died at age 45.

EDCS-25600177.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Felix Victorina lived 26 years.
EDCS-25600177.summary: This is a tombstone for Felix Victorina, who died at age 26.

EDCS-25600178.translation: Coratius Maurus lived 85 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600178.summary: This is a tombstone for Coratius Maurus, who died at age 85.

EDCS-25600179.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Pactumeia Graecula lived 35 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25600179.summary: This is a tombstone for Pactumeia Graecula, who died at age 35.

EDCS-25600180.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Sittia Florina lived 25 years.
EDCS-25600180.summary: This is a tombstone for Sittia Florina, who died at age 25.

EDCS-25600181.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Flavius Pudens lived 25 years.
EDCS-25600181.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Pudens, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-25600182.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Fundana lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600182.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fundana, who died at the age of 25 and is buried here.

EDCS-25600183.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Urban.
EDCS-25600183.summary: This inscription appears to be two separate funerary notices for Iulia and Urban.

EDCS-25600184.translation: Iulia Primula, wife of Iapasaces, lived 32 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600184.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Primula, wife of Iapasaces, who died at the age of 32 and is buried here.

EDCS-25600186.translation: Under our emperor and lord Gordianus Augustus and Pompeianus, Lucius C. Mat. Victor was chief magistrate.
EDCS-25600186.summary: This inscription records Lucius C. Mat. Victor as chief magistrate under Emperor Gordian III and Pompeianus, likely in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-25600187.translation: Sacred to Bacchus Augustus. For the health of B. Aurelia Br... Crispus Ianuarius, procurator, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-25600187.summary: This is a dedication to Bacchus Augustus made by Crispus Ianuarius, a procurator, for the health of Aurelia Br..., likely in the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25600188.translation: With Saecularis and Donatus as consuls, Quintus Cae. Maximus, chief magistrate of the Thibilitani, dedicated this sacred to Bacchus Augustus on the day before the Kalends of April.
EDCS-25600188.summary: This inscription records Quintus Cae. Maximus as chief magistrate of the Thibilitani and a dedication to Bacchus Augustus, dated to the day before April 1st, during the consulship of Saecularis and Donatus (likely 235 CE).

EDCS-25600189.translation: Sacred to Bacchus Augustus. With Caro and Carinus as consuls, on the Kalends of April, Nonius Felix and Caecilius Ianuarius were chief magistrates of the Thibilitani.
EDCS-25600189.summary: This inscription records Nonius Felix and Caecilius Ianuarius as chief magistrates of the Thibilitani and a dedication to Bacchus Augustus, dated to April 1st, during the consulship of Caro and Carinus (likely 285 CE).

EDCS-25600190.translation: Sacred to Bacchus Augustus. With man IS AV ta as consuls, Bessus A R MA V [---].
EDCS-25600190.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Bacchus Augustus with incomplete consular names and the name Bessus, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25600191.translation: Sacred to Bacchus Augustus. Probus [was consul for the] fourth [time] and Vigellius Goddeus was chief magistrate, and Aufidius Iasau [---].
EDCS-25600191.summary: This inscription records Vigellius Goddeus as chief magistrate and Aufidius Iasau in association with Probus (likely Probus Augustus, who had multiple consulships in the late 3rd century CE) and a dedication to Bacchus Augustus.

EDCS-25600192.translation: Sacred to Bacacius Augustus. Gaius Iulius Paulinus gladly fulfilled his vow as was deserved.
EDCS-25600192.summary: A dedication to the local deity Bacacius Augustus, fulfilling a vow by Gaius Iulius Paulinus.

EDCS-25600193.translation: Calpurnia wrote: Sacred to Bacacius Augustus. In the consulship of Volusianus and Maximus, Publius Mo Maxsimus, the magistrate of the Thibilitani, [dedicated this].
EDCS-25600193.summary: A dedication to Bacacius Augustus, commissioned by Publius Mo Maxsimus, magistrate of the Thibilitani, during the consulship of Volusianus and Maximus.

EDCS-25600194.translation: Sacred to Bacacius Augustus. With Ulpius as magistrate, [he] gladly fulfilled [his] vow with [his] soul and [dedicated] the place of sacred rites...
EDCS-25600194.summary: A dedication to Bacacius Augustus, with a magistrate named Ulpius, and the donor fulfilling a vow and dedicating a sacred site.

EDCS-25600195.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. His devotees made this at their own expense for the safety of the Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius.
EDCS-25600195.summary: A dedication to Hercules Augustus, made by his devotees for the safety of Emperor Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-25600197.translation: Antisiia Saturnina lived 25 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25600197.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Antisiia Saturnina.

EDCS-25600198.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Gaius Iulius Augustalis lived 100 years. Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Ticia lived [X] years.
EDCS-25600198.summary: Two epitaphs for Gaius Iulius Augustalis and Iulia Ticia, dedicated to the Divine Spirits, recording their lifespans.

EDCS-25600199.translation: Sacred to Concordia Augusta. Iulius Probatus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, master of the district, saw to its construction.
EDCS-25600199.summary: A dedication to Concordia Augusta, commissioned by Iulius Probatus, a local district official.

EDCS-25600200.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta. Marcus Herenius Victor, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, an outstanding man, by her inspiration and with his own money, gave this offering.
EDCS-25600200.summary: A votive offering to Fortuna Augusta, given by Marcus Herenius Victor, inspired by the goddess.

EDCS-25600201.translation: Sacred to the Genius of the house. For the safety of Quintus Antistius Adventus Postumus Aquilinus, legate of Augustus of the II Adiutrix legion, and his wife Novia Crispa, and Lucius Antistius Mundicus Burrus and his mother Antonia Prisca, and their children and their household. Antistius Agathopus, his freedman, gave the offering as seen in a vision. Quintus Antistius Agathopus gave the offering as seen in a vision and dedicated it himself on the Kalends of March in the consulship of Macrinus and Celsus. Sacred to the Genius of the house. For the safety of Quintus Antistius Adventus Postumus Aquilinus, legate of Augustus of the II Adiutrix legion, and his wife Novia Crispa, and Lucius Antistius Mundicus Burrus and his mother Antonia Prisca, and their children and their household. Agathopus, his freedman, gave the offering as seen in a vision. Quintus Antistius Agathopus gave the offering as seen in a vision and dedicated it himself on the Kalends of March in the consulship of Macrinus and Celsus.
EDCS-25600201.summary: A lengthy dedication to the Genius of a household, for the well-being of the Antistii family and their dependents, with the offering made and dedicated by the freedman Antistius Agathopus during the consulship of Macrinus and Celsus.

EDCS-25600202.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. For the safety and victory of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus. Quintus Furnius Crescentianus made this at his own expense and dedicated it.
EDCS-25600202.summary: A dedication to Hercules Augustus, made by Quintus Furnius Crescentianus at his own expense for the safety and victory of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus.

EDCS-25600203.summary: A votive dedication to Jupiter, likely a very common type of inscription.

EDCS-25600205.translation: Marcus Vipsanius Martialis, a priest, consecrated to Holy Saturn concerning the flocks.
EDCS-25600205.summary: A dedication to the god Saturn by a priest regarding livestock.

EDCS-25600206.translation: Sacred to Augusta Victoria. Agathopus, a freedman, dedicated [this] for the safety of Antistius Adventus and Burrus and their children.
EDCS-25600206.summary: A dedication to Victoria Augusta by a freedman for the health of members of the Antistii family.

EDCS-25600208.translation: Sacred, [in honor of the] office of magistery by C. Ti[---]anus, Quadratus, IIIVS.
EDCS-25600208.summary: A dedication, possibly to a deity, made by Quadratus in connection with his magisterial office.

EDCS-25600212.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantius, most noble Caesar Augustus, Niu[---]sus.
EDCS-25600212.summary: An inscription honoring the Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-25600213.translation: Valerius Paulus, a most perfect man, governor of the province of Numidia, devoted to their divine majesty, dedicated this and erected it with public money to the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus Victorious, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 9th time, consul for the 3rd time, imperator for the 7th time, father of the fatherland, proconsul.
EDCS-25600213.summary: A public dedication of a monument by Valerius Paulus, governor of Numidia, to Emperor Constantine, dating to his 9th tribunician power and 3rd consulship (likely 315 CE).

EDCS-25600215.translation: To Publius Iulius Castus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, his most excellent uncle, Caius Iulius Pudens, urban quaestor, tribune of the plebs, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-25600215.summary: An inscription honoring Publius Iulius Castus, erected by his nephew Caius Iulius Pudens, who held significant civic offices, as decreed by the local council.

EDCS-25600217.translation: To Caius Iulius Pudentus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, a most distinguished man, patron of Thibilis, [dedicated] with public money.
EDCS-25600217.summary: A public monument dedicated to Caius Iulius Pudentus, honored as a distinguished citizen and patron of Thibilis.

EDCS-25600219.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Sittius Severus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, centurion.
EDCS-25600219.summary: A funerary dedication to the Manes by Lucius Sittius Severus, a centurion.

EDCS-25600220.translation: To Clodia Vitosa Tertullina, flaminica of the four colonies. Publius Clodius Quadratus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, who is also Scipio, his cousin, honored with the public horse, a decurion of the same colonies, gave and dedicated [this] at his own expense, [for] his outstanding generosity; the place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-25600220.summary: A dedication by Publius Clodius Quadratus, a local official and relative, to Clodia Vitosa Tertullina, a religious official for four colonies, in recognition of her generosity.

EDCS-25600221.translation: Marcus Vitruvius Mamurra made the arch with his own money.
EDCS-25600221.summary: This is an arch dedication by Marcus Vitruvius Mamurra, likely from the 1st century BCE.

EDCS-25600222.translation: With pious praise, I, Timis, leave behind these small things, my father, so that this might be a covering for them; the earth, victor over the crowds, holds this returned thing.
EDCS-25600222.summary: This is a funerary epigram for Timis, possibly from the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-25600223.translation: Abullia Maxima, daughter of Quintus, lived [X] years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25600223.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Abullia Maxima, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-25600224.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Accia Calliope lived 25 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25600224.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Accia Calliope, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-25600225.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Aemilius Adzetus, grandson of Marcus Aemilius.
EDCS-25600225.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Aemilius Adzetus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-25600226.translation: Lucius Aemilius Celer, son of Lucius, of the Fabia tribe, lived 18 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600226.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aemilius Celer, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-25600227.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Aemilius Felix, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, set this up for himself while alive. He lived [X] years. He is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Augusta, daughter of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 45 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25600227.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aemilius Felix and Aurelia Augusta, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25600228.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Aemilius Martialis, son of Aemilius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 85 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600228.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aemilius Martialis, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-25600229.translation: Lucius Aemilius Martialis, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 35 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600229.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aemilius Martialis, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-25600230.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Festiva lived 65 years. She is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Sextius, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, lived [X] years.
EDCS-25600230.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilia Festiva and Titus Sextius, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-25600231.translation: Aemila Urbana, daughter of Marius, lived 5 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600231.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Aemila Urbana.

EDCS-25600233.translation: Agria Maximina, daughter of Maximus, lived 91 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600233.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Agria Maximina.

EDCS-25600234.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph for a person named Alexo.

EDCS-25600235.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Sofonniba lived 65 years; she is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Annaeus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, Gentius, lived 45 years; he is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Annaeus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, Victor, lived 15 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600235.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of three individuals: Iulia Sofonniba, Marcus Annaeus Gentius, and Marcus Annaeus Victor, likely family members.

EDCS-25600236.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Annaeus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, Rufus, lived 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600236.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Annaeus Rufus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe.

EDCS-25600237.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Annaea Victoria lived 65 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600237.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Annaea Victoria.

EDCS-25600238.translation: Civilis, son of Quintus Antistius Asiaticus, lived 9 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600238.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy named Civilis, son of Quintus Antistius Asiaticus.

EDCS-25600239.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Antistius Faustus lived 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600239.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a man named Antistius Faustus.

EDCS-25600240.translation: Quintus Antistius Maximus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 90 years.
EDCS-25600240.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Antistius Maximus.

EDCS-25600242.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Antistia Iulla lived 45 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600242.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Antistia Iulla.

EDCS-25600243.translation: Antistia Procula, daughter of Numerius, lived 5 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600243.summary: This is a tombstone for Antistia Procula, who died at the age of 5, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600245.translation: Antistia Rusticina lived 90 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600245.summary: This is a tombstone for Antistia Rusticina, who died at the age of 90, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600246.translation: Antistia Severa, daughter of Lucius, lived 23 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600246.summary: This is a tombstone for Antistia Severa, who died at the age of 23, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600247.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Antonia Maxima, daughter of Antonius, lived 20 years.
EDCS-25600247.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Antonia Maxima, who died at the age of 20, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600248.translation: Marcus Antonius Pietas, son of Marcus Antonius, lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600248.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Antonius Pietas, who died at the age of 35, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600249.translation: Caius Aufidius Maximus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 65 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600249.summary: This is a tombstone for Caius Aufidius Maximus, who died at the age of 65, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600250.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Gellia Boroc, daughter of Rogatus. Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Avianius S.
EDCS-25600250.summary: This is a fragmented funerary dedication for Gellia Boroc and possibly Avianius S., likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600252.translation: Sextus Aemilius Saturninus lived 95 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600252.summary: This is a tombstone for Sextus Aemilius Saturninus, who died at the age of 95, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600253.translation: Aurelia Lucilla lived 10 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600253.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelia Lucilla, who died at the age of 10, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600254.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Aurelia Lucilla lived 30 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600254.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelia Lucilla, who died at the age of 30, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600255.translation: Babbia Peregrina, daughter of Publius, lived 55 years; she is placed here.
EDCS-25600255.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Babbia Peregrina.

EDCS-25600256.translation: Bigetia, daughter of Caius, aged 70 years, is placed here.
EDCS-25600256.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Bigetia.

EDCS-25600257.translation: Gaius Acilius Bromius, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 60 years; he is placed here.
EDCS-25600257.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Gaius Acilius Bromius.

EDCS-25600258.translation: For the spirits of the departed. Memoria, daughter of Nigellio, the freedwoman Mellita, lived 17 years; she is placed here.
EDCS-25600258.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Memoria, identified as a freedwoman.

EDCS-25600259.translation: Quintus Caecilius Mundus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 70 years; he is placed here.
EDCS-25600259.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Quintus Caecilius Mundus.

EDCS-25600260.translation: Gaius Caecilius Quetus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 23 years.
EDCS-25600260.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gaius Caecilius Quetus, though it does not explicitly state burial.

EDCS-25600261.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilius Saturninus lived 65 years. Victoria Ingenua placed this.
EDCS-25600261.summary: This funerary dedication, sacred to the departed spirits, records the life of Caecilius Saturninus and was set up by Victoria Ingenua.

EDCS-25600262.translation: Caecilia Favosa, daughter of Lucius, lived 41 years; she is placed here.
EDCS-25600262.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Caecilia Favosa.

EDCS-25600263.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Caelius.
EDCS-25600263.summary: This is a funerary dedication, sacred to the departed spirits, for Publius Caelius.

EDCS-25600264.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Caesellius Faustinus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years; he is placed here. Geminia Spes lived 55 years; she is placed here.
EDCS-25600264.summary: This funerary inscription records the lives and burials of two individuals, Gaius Caesellius Faustinus and Geminia Spes.

EDCS-25600265.translation: Caesonia Secunda lived 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600265.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caesonia Secunda, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-25600266.translation: Quintus Calpurnius, daughter of Lucius, Lucia.
EDCS-25600266.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely identifying a person, possibly Quintus Calpurnius and his daughter Lucia, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-25600267.translation: Carvilia Mustela, freedwoman of Publius, lived 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600267.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Carvilia Mustela, a freedwoman, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-25600268.translation: Who was also called Clarus, lived 17 years and 5 months, a virgin created by a chaste father. She obeyed her parents and they placed her in a common grave with them in unequal seating; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600268.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Clarus, a virgin who died at 17 years and 5 months, mentioning her chaste father and her placement in a family grave.

EDCS-25600269.translation: Lucius Clodius Amerianus lived 18 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600269.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Clodius Amerianus, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-25600270.translation: Coelia Quinta, daughter of Caius, lived 21 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600270.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Coelia Quinta, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-25600271.translation: Marcus Cornelius Dexter, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 60 years.
EDCS-25600271.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Cornelius Dexter, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-25600272.translation: Lucius Cornelius Pudens, son of Marcus, lived 23 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600272.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Cornelius Pudens, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-25600273.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Reducta, recording her exceptional lifespan.

EDCS-25600274.translation: Cornelia Urbanilla, daughter of Marcus, lived 31 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600274.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Urbanilla, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-25600275.translation: Lucius Cupronius Potitus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 27 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600275.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Cupronius Potitus, recording his name, filiation, tribe, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-25600276.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Dexia Iulia, daughter of Lucius, lived 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600276.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Dexia Iulia, mentioning her dedication to the Manes, name, filiation, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-25600277.translation: Diria Apta, daughter of Marcus, lived 7 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600277.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the child Diria Apta, recording her name, filiation, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-25600278.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Domitia Butura, of the Quirina tribe, lived 82 years; she is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits.
EDCS-25600278.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Domitia Butura, mentioning her dedication to the Manes, name, tribe, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-25600279.translation: Egnatia Rogata, daughter of Quintus, lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600279.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Egnatia Rogata, recording her name, filiation, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-25600280.summary: This is a very brief funerary epitaph, likely just naming the deceased Egrilius Threptus.

EDCS-25600281.translation: Marcus Egrilius Urbanus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 90 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600281.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Egrilius Urbanus, recording his name, filiation, tribe, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-25600282.translation: Lucius Fabius Saturninus Novatus lived 15 years.
EDCS-25600282.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Fabius Saturninus Novatus, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-25600283.translation: Faustiolus lived 81 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600283.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Faustiolus, recording his name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-25600286.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Fulvius Victor, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 17 years; Felicianus, to his dearest son, buried here.
EDCS-25600286.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Fulvius Victor, erected by his father Felicianus, recording his name, filiation, tribe, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-25600288.translation: Lucius Gellius Urbanus Iunior, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 40 years, is buried here.
EDCS-25600288.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Gellius Urbanus Iunior, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600289.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Herennius Iulius, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, from the Ic Iulius district.
EDCS-25600289.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Marcus Herennius Iulius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600290.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Manilius Caiolus lived 85 years, is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Herennia Rogata lived 80 years, is buried here.
EDCS-25600290.summary: This inscription records the burial of two individuals, Quintus Manilius Caiolus and Herennia Rogata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600291.translation: Honoratus, son of Marci, of the Quirina tribe, lived 15 years, is buried here. Gratianus lived 35 years, is buried here.
EDCS-25600291.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates two individuals, Honoratus and Gratianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600292.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Hordionius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived [X] years, is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Hordionius Solutor, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 13 years, is buried here.
EDCS-25600292.summary: This inscription records the burial of two individuals named Lucius Hordionius, likely father and son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600293.translation: Caius Hortensius Solutor, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years.
EDCS-25600293.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Hortensius Solutor, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600294.translation: Quintus Humanius Getulicus, son of Quinti, of the Quirina tribe, lived 15 years, is buried here.
EDCS-25600294.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Quintus Humanius Getulicus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600295.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Sextus Iavolenius Victor Iunior, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 17 years, is buried here. Marcus Iavolenius Victor Asinus Maior, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 63 years, is buried here. Iavolenia Sextulla, daughter of Iavolenus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 21 years, is buried here. Sextus, her brother, placed [this]. Aurelia Salasula, daughter of Aureli, of the Quirina tribe, lived [X] years.
EDCS-25600295.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription commemorating multiple members of the Iavolenii and Aurelii families, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600296.translation: Caius Iulius Colonus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years, is buried here.
EDCS-25600296.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Caius Iulius Colonus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600297.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulius Honoratus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iunia Hospitta, wife of Iulius Honoratus, daughter of Caius, in this tomb.
EDCS-25600297.summary: This inscription records the burial of two individuals, Iulius Honoratus and his wife Iunia Hospitta, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600298.translation: Gaius Julius Ianuarius, son of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600298.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Ianuarius, who lived 45 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25600299.translation: Sextus Julius Macri, son of Gaius, lived 70 years.
EDCS-25600299.summary: This is a tombstone for Sextus Julius Macri, who lived 70 years.

EDCS-25600300.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Julius Maximus, aged 55.
EDCS-25600300.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Julius Maximus, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-25600301.translation: Gaius Julius Muntanus lived 90 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600301.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Muntanus, who lived 90 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25600302.translation: Gaius Julius Nigrinus, son of Gaius, who lived 30 years, is buried here.
EDCS-25600302.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Nigrinus, who lived 30 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25600303.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius Julius Sarpes, son of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 75 years; he is buried here. Erected by Eleusius Julius for his dearest father.
EDCS-25600303.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Sarpes, dedicated by his son Eleusius Julius, who lived 75 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25600306.translation: Publius Julius Soricio, son of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 71 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600306.summary: This is a tombstone for Publius Julius Soricio, who lived 71 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25600307.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius Julius Vericulus, son of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 21 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600307.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Vericulus, who lived 21 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25600308.translation: Iulia Africana, daughter of Publius, lived 27 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600308.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulia Africana, who lived 27 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25600309.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Augurina lived 32 years. Quintus Fuficius Quetus erected this for his obedient wife; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600309.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulia Augurina, erected by her husband Quintus Fuficius Quetus, who lived 32 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25600310.translation: Iulia Chubudit, daughter of Publius, lived 91 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600310.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Chubudit, recording her name, parentage, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-25600311.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Mellita lived; Sacred to the departed spirits. Sittia Quirina Solutrix, daughter of Marcus, lived 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600311.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sittia Quirina Solutrix, recording her name, parentage, tribe, age at death, and burial, with a repeated invocation to the departed spirits.

EDCS-25600312.translation: Iulia Optata, daughter of Caius, lived 15 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600312.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Optata, recording her name, parentage, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-25600313.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Romana lived 75 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600313.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Romana, recording her name, age at death, and burial, with an invocation to the departed spirits.

EDCS-25600314.translation: Iulia Vitalis lived 101 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600314.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Vitalis, recording her name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-25600315.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sextus Iuventius Catervarius, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600315.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextus Iuventius Catervarius, recording his name, parentage, tribe, age at death, and burial, with an invocation to the departed spirits.

EDCS-25600316.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Libosus lived 41 years.
EDCS-25600316.summary: A funerary epitaph for Libosus, recording his name and age at death, with an invocation to the departed spirits.

EDCS-25600317.translation: Licinia Fuscilla, daughter of Marcus, lived 45 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600317.summary: A funerary epitaph for Licinia Fuscilla, recording her name, parentage, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-25600318.translation: Licinia Hymnis lived 15 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600318.summary: A funerary epitaph for Licinia Hymnis, recording her name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-25600319.translation: Publius Livius Martialis, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 71 years.
EDCS-25600319.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Livius Martialis, recording his name, parentage, tribe, and age at death.

EDCS-25600320.translation: Gnaeus Lucilius Ammicar, freedman of Gnaeus, lived 84 years. He lies here.
EDCS-25600320.summary: This is a tombstone for Gnaeus Lucilius Ammicar, recording his name, his freedman status, his age at death, and that he is buried here.

EDCS-25600321.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucceius Egrilius lived 80 years.
EDCS-25600321.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucceius Egrilius, recording his name and age at death, and is dedicated to the Divine Spirits.

EDCS-25600322.translation: Marcus Lucretius Nundinarius lived 80 years. He lies here.
EDCS-25600322.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Lucretius Nundinarius, recording his name, his age at death, and that he is buried here.

EDCS-25600323.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Borocia, daughter of Lucius, who lived 61 years, lies here. Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Messius Nepos, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, lived 61 years. He lies here.
EDCS-25600323.summary: This is a double tombstone recording the deaths of Iulia Borocia and Lucius Messius Nepos, noting their parentage, tribe, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-25600324.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Manilius, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina.
EDCS-25600324.summary: This is a tombstone fragment for Lucius Manilius, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, indicating it is dedicated to the Divine Spirits.

EDCS-25600325.translation: Manlius, son of [---], of the tribe Quirina.
EDCS-25600325.summary: This is a tombstone fragment for Manlius, son of an unnamed father, of the tribe Quirina.

EDCS-25600326.translation: [...ria] Marcia lived 90 years. Marcella lived 10 years. She lies here.
EDCS-25600326.summary: This tombstone records the deaths of two individuals, Marcia and Marcella, noting their ages and that Marcella is buried here.

EDCS-25600327.translation: [...]ana, daughter of Caius, of the tribe Quirina, Marcia lived 33 years. She lies here.
EDCS-25600327.summary: This tombstone records the death of Marcia, noting her parentage, tribe, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-25600328.translation: Maria Fausta, daughter of Publius, lived 25 years. She lies here.
EDCS-25600328.summary: This is a tombstone for Maria Fausta, daughter of Publius, recording her age at death and that she is buried here.

EDCS-25600329.translation: For the daughters of Marcus, Matrona lived 46 years. She lies here.
EDCS-25600329.summary: This tombstone records the death of Matrona, daughter of Marcus, noting her age at death and that she is buried here.

EDCS-25600330.translation: Maxima, daughter of Lucius, lived 14 years; she lies here.
EDCS-25600330.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maxima, dating to when the person was 14 years old.

EDCS-25600331.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Memmius Maximus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 45 years; he lies here.
EDCS-25600331.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Memmius Maximus, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-25600334.translation: [Name], son of Lucius, lived 78 years; he lies here.
EDCS-25600334.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an unnamed individual who lived 78 years.

EDCS-25600335.translation: Lucius Nunius Pudens, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 35 years.
EDCS-25600335.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Nunius Pudens, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-25600336.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Oppius Rusticus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 50 years; he lies here.
EDCS-25600336.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Oppius Rusticus, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-25600337.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Ianuarius placed this for his dearest wife Peregrina, who lived 27 years and 20 days; she lies here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Ianuarius.
EDCS-25600337.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Ianuarius for his wife Peregrina, who died at the age of 27 years and 20 days.

EDCS-25600338.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Pomponius Datuleus lived 16 years.
EDCS-25600338.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Pomponius Datuleus, who lived 16 years.

EDCS-25600339.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Saturnina lived 20 years.
EDCS-25600339.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Saturnina, who lived 20 years.

EDCS-25600340.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Sempronius Capito, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-25600340.summary: This funerary inscription records Quintus Sempronius Capito, son of Sextus.

EDCS-25600341.translation: Publius Servilius Gallus lived 10 years; he lies here.
EDCS-25600341.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Servilius Gallus, who lived 10 years.

EDCS-25600342.translation: Didius Severus, son of Caius, of the tribe Quirina, lived 37 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600342.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Didius Severus.

EDCS-25600343.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits: Sextius Felix, son of Sextius, of the tribe Quirina, lived...
EDCS-25600343.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life of Sextius Felix, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-25600344.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits: Silicia Honorata lived 55 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600344.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Silicia Honorata, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-25600345.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits: Lucius Sittius Italus lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600345.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Lucius Sittius Italus, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-25600347.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits: Ianuaria, daughter of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, lived 51 years; she is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits: Sittius Nabor lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600347.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lives and burials of Ianuaria and Sittius Nabor, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-25600348.translation: Sittius Quetus, son of Sittius, of the tribe Quirina, lived 22 years.
EDCS-25600348.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life of Sittius Quetus.

EDCS-25600349.translation: Publius Sittius Felix, son of Urbanius, of the tribe Quirina, pious, lived 31 years; he is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits: Manilia Pollitta, pious, lived 85 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600349.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lives and burials of Publius Sittius Felix and Manilia Pollitta, with the latter dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-25600350.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits: Gaius Valerius Carvilianus, son of Gaius, of the tribe Quirina, lived 10 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600350.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Gaius Valerius Carvilianus, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-25600351.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits: for Valeria Afrodite, daughter of Marcius, of the tribe Quirina, his most obedient wife, lived 75 years; she is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits: Valerius Alcibiades, son of Marcius, of the tribe Quirina, wanted this for himself while alive, for his chaste wife.
EDCS-25600351.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Valeria Afrodite and the self-provided burial arrangements of her husband Valerius Alcibiades, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-25600352.translation: Lucius Valerius Faustinus, son of Lucius, lived 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600352.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Lucius Valerius Faustinus.

EDCS-25600354.translation: Publius Valerius Urbanus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 61 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600354.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Valerius Urbanus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600355.translation: Vipsania Iulia, daughter of Marcus, lived 21 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600355.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vipsania Iulia, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600356.translation: Vitruvia Pottula lived 20 years; Marcus Vitruvius.
EDCS-25600356.summary: This inscription likely relates to Vitruvia Pottula, possibly indicating her age and mentioning Marcus Vitruvius, perhaps a relative, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600357.translation: Aulus Umbricius Gallus lived 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600357.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Umbricius Gallus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600363.translation: Catervarius lived 41 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600363.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Catervarius, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600364.translation: Marcus [son of?] Quirina and for his descendants.
EDCS-25600364.summary: This inscription possibly records a dedication or familial claim made by Marcus, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600368.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Anius Florus, noting his exceptional longevity, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600369.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Gemilius Balbinius lived 80 years.
EDCS-25600369.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes (departed spirits), for Lucius Gemilius Balbinius, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600373.translation: Anicia Urbana lived 53 years; she is buried here. Aemilius Datus to his deserving wife.
EDCS-25600373.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aemilius Datus for his wife Anicia Urbana, who lived 53 years, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600374.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Emilia Casta made this for her mother, who lived 101 years.
EDCS-25600374.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Emilia Casta for her mother, who lived 101 years, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600375.translation: To the departed spirits. Bupaemillus Rogaius lived 87 years.
EDCS-25600375.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Bupaemillus Rogaius, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-25600376.translation: To the memory of Senpronius Ianurius. To the memory of Senpronius Ianurius.
EDCS-25600376.summary: This inscription is a memorial dedication for Senpronius Ianurius, possibly a tombstone or a marker.

EDCS-25600385.translation: Regilla lived 10 years and Renatula, their incomparable daughter, made this; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600385.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Regilla, set up by her daughter Renatula, recording her age and burial place.

EDCS-25600390.translation: Caia Silvana lived 23 years, may her bones rest well. Gargilia Silvana lived 50 years, she is buried here.
EDCS-25600390.summary: This is a double funerary epitaph for Caia Silvana and Gargilia Silvana, recording their ages and burial places.

EDCS-25600391.translation: Sacred to the native god Baliddirus Augustus. Quintus Tadius Victor, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, having promised to make a bronze statue, which he would make for the honor of the flamen of the deified Severus of the Siguitanian fortress, for 500 denarii, increased by his generosity to 1000 denarii, with a base, Tadius Victor the Younger, Saturninus, Honorata, and Felix, his children and heirs, gave and dedicated. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-25600391.summary: This is a dedication of a bronze statue to the god Baliddirus Augustus, commissioned by Quintus Tadius Victor and completed by his heirs, likely related to his priestly office and funded by a significant sum of money.

EDCS-25600392.translation: He placed the statue of Baliddiris Augustus, the holy native god, which Marcus Julius Proculus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, having promised for the honor of the perpetual flamen of the deified great Antoninus, for 2200 sesterces, the total honorarium for that office, and added to that amount from his own generosity 1000 sesterces for the statue and 400 sesterces for the base, for a total of 3600 sesterces, and dedicated it himself. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-25600392.summary: This is a dedication of a statue to the god Baliddiris Augustus, funded by Marcus Julius Proculus, who was fulfilling a promise related to his priestly office as perpetual flamen.

EDCS-25600393.translation: Sacred to the holy god Baliddirus.
EDCS-25600393.summary: This is a brief dedication to the god Baliddirus.

EDCS-25600394.translation: To Mars Augustus, protector of our lord the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus. Publius Sittius, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, contractor, for the honor of the perpetual flaminship, which he promised to hold, made this for 5200 sesterces, and dedicated it himself. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-25600394.summary: This is a dedication to Mars Augustus, made by Publius Sittius, a contractor, in honor of his expected perpetual flaminship, and funded by a specific sum of money.

EDCS-25600395.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods Augusta. Licinia, daughter of Titus Ertricatus, with her own money.
EDCS-25600395.summary: This is a religious dedication to the Mother of the Gods Augusta, funded by Licinia Ertricata.

EDCS-25600396.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus. Marcus [---] fulfilled his vow willingly.
EDCS-25600396.summary: This is a dedication to Mars Augustus, recording that Marcus fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-25600397.translation: Gaius Caecilius made a vow and gladly paid it.
EDCS-25600397.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Gaius Caecilius fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-25600398.translation: To Saturn, Quintus Caecilius Seneca paid a vow.
EDCS-25600398.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Quintus Caecilius Seneca paid a vow to Saturn.

EDCS-25600399.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Caesar, son of Emperor Hadrian Antoninus Augustus Pius, grandson of the deified Hadrian, by decree of the council of decurions, with public money.
EDCS-25600399.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Marcus Aurelius, likely on a public monument, funded by the local council.

EDCS-25600404.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Urbana, high priestess, lived 101 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600404.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Urbana, a high priestess, who lived 101 years.

EDCS-25600405.translation: Aemilia Donata lived 50 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600405.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilia Donata, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-25600406.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Alfius Tertullus lived 35 years.
EDCS-25600406.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Alfius Tertullus, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-25600407.translation: Iulia Anicia Babuis, daughter of Urbana, is buried here.
EDCS-25600407.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Anicia Urbana, daughter of Babuis.

EDCS-25600408.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Asilia Sasa, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-25600409.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Avellia Urbana lived 70 years. Cocilius Honoratus Barhic, grandson of Avellia, lived 23 years and is buried here. Cavellius Hospes lived 25 years and is buried here. Iulia Matrona lived 25 years and is buried here. Quintus Aufidius Saturninus lived 75 years and is buried here.
EDCS-25600409.summary: This is a large funerary inscription recording the lives and burials of Avellia Urbana and several other individuals, including her grandson.

EDCS-25600410.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Avellius Donatus lived 20 years. Pompeius Rufus lived 60 years and is buried here. Quintus Avellius Urbanus lived 25 years and is buried here. May your bones rest well. Iulia Secunda lived 60 years and is buried here.
EDCS-25600410.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lives and burials of Titus Avellius Donatus, Pompeius Rufus, Quintus Avellius Urbanus, and Iulia Secunda.

EDCS-25600411.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Baebia Aristilla lived X years. Lucius Tatius Capito made this for his most rare wife.
EDCS-25600411.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Lucius Tatius Capito to his wife Baebia Aristilla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600412.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Caecilius Saturninus lived 36 years for his brother. The brothers [made this?]. CVBVLIO lived 104 years.
EDCS-25600412.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages of Lucius Caecilius Saturninus and another individual named CVBVLIO, likely erected by brothers.

EDCS-25600413.translation: Lived 28 years. His ashes are buried here. May his ashes rest well. Clodia Fortunula [made this] for her dearest husband.
EDCS-25600413.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating a husband who lived 28 years, made by his wife Clodia Fortunula, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600414.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits for Donata Fidecis.
EDCS-25600414.summary: A brief funerary epitaph dedicating the inscription to Donata Fidecis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600415.translation: Dupidia Sabina, daughter of Publius, lived 16 years, 7 months, 3 days. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-25600415.summary: A funerary epitaph for Dupidia Sabina, recording her age at death and expressing a wish for her peaceful rest, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600416.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Cargilius Messor lived 35 years. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-25600416.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Cargilius Messor, stating his age and wishing for his peaceful rest, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600417.translation: VET ama AV liu I Caius Iulius Baustinus lived 105 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-25600417.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Baustinus, recording his age at death and location of burial, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600418.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Faustus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 1 year. Iulia Saturnina lived [X] years.
EDCS-25600418.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Faustus and Iulia Saturnina, likely parents and child, indicating their ages and from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600419.translation: Marcus Iulius Martialis lived 30 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600419.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Iulius Martialis, stating his age at death and burial location, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600420.translation: Caius Iulius Primus lived 27 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600420.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Primus, stating his age at death and burial location, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600421.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Iulius Tertullus lived 20 years. To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Iulius Iulianus lived 70 years.
EDCS-25600421.summary: This epitaph records the deaths of two individuals, Lucius Iulius Tertullus and Lucius Iulius Iulianus, with their lifespans.

EDCS-25600423.translation: Iulia Nicopolis lived 105 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25600423.summary: This epitaph records the death and burial of Iulia Nicopolis, noting her long lifespan.

EDCS-25600424.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Laberius Dexter lived 61 years. May your bones rest well. Lucius Laberius Dexter, son, lived 20 years.
EDCS-25600424.summary: This epitaph records the deaths of Lucius Laberius Dexter and his son, also named Lucius Laberius Dexter, with their ages and a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-25600425.translation: Quintus Lerisanitus lived 75 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600425.summary: This epitaph records the death and burial of Quintus Lerisanitus, noting his lifespan.

EDCS-25600426.translation: Lucius Licinius Capo, grandson, lived 22 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600426.summary: This epitaph records the death and burial of Lucius Licinius Capo, specifying he was a grandson and noting his age.

EDCS-25600427.translation: Lurius Hermeros lived 9 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-25600427.summary: This epitaph records the death of Lurius Hermeros, noting his young age and including a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-25600428.translation: Publius Mantius Rogatus is buried here. He lived 77 years.
EDCS-25600428.summary: This epitaph records the death and burial of Publius Mantius Rogatus, noting his lifespan.

EDCS-25600429.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Maria Rufina lived 95 years. From Maribus, Quintus Marius, son of Quintus, Quirina Crescens lived 65 years.
EDCS-25600429.summary: This epitaph records the deaths of Maria Rufina and Crescens (likely identified with Quintus Marius), noting their lifespans and origin from Maribus.

EDCS-25600431.translation: For the memory of Saturnina, she lived 13 years and four months.
EDCS-25600431.summary: This epitaph records the death of Saturnina, noting her young age in years and months.

EDCS-25600432.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Sabina, my well-deserving wife, Publius Iulius Victor dedicated this eternal home. She lived 33 years.
EDCS-25600432.summary: This epitaph records the dedication of a tomb by Publius Iulius Victor to his deserving wife, Sabina, noting her age at death.

EDCS-25600433.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mutthun, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-25600434.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Numisius Marcianus, son of Quintus Numisius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 40 years. Quintus Numisius Maximus, son of Quintus Numisius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 35 years. May your bones rest well. Aemilia Nivosa made this for herself while alive.
EDCS-25600434.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aemilia Nivosa for herself and her deceased husband and son, listing their ages at death.

EDCS-25600435.translation: Marcus Octavius Cinnamus lived 5 years and 3 months.
EDCS-25600435.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short life of Marcus Octavius Cinnamus.

EDCS-25600436.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Ovinius Nicomedes lived 60 years; may you rest well here. Ovinia Spes lived 15 years; may you rest well here.
EDCS-25600436.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Ovinius Nicomedes and Ovinia Spes, indicating their ages at death and wishing them well in the afterlife.

EDCS-25600437.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Porreca Buturaria lived 20 years; may she rest well here. Marcus Porreca Pacatus Catervarius lived 29 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600437.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of death for Porreca Buturaria and Marcus Porreca Pacatus Catervarius.

EDCS-25600438.translation: To the memory and innocence of (son?) who lived 5 years, 4 months, and 2 days, and of a daughter who lived 7 months and 10 days, and of Primitiva their wife and mother. Gaius, freedman of Augustus. If anyone attempts to damage the monument, which is called the Pyramid, he will incur a penalty to the treasury of our lord. [---]
EDCS-25600438.summary: This funerary inscription, potentially for a pyramid-shaped monument, records the ages of death of two children and their mother, and includes a legal warning against damaging the tomb.

EDCS-25600439.translation: Quintus Propertius Martialis lived 65 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600439.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death of Quintus Propertius Martialis.

EDCS-25600440.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sextus Pullius Avitus, son of Sextus Pullius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 20 years.
EDCS-25600440.summary: This funerary inscription records the age at death of Sextus Pullius Avitus.

EDCS-25600441.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilia Recepta lived 55 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600441.summary: This funerary inscription records the age at death of Caecilia Recepta.

EDCS-25600443.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sallustia Honorata, daughter of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived [---] years.
EDCS-25600443.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Sallustia Honorata, with her age at death incomplete.

EDCS-25600444.translation: To the memory of Sallustia Quieta, daughter of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, who lived 24 years.
EDCS-25600444.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Sallustia Quieta.

EDCS-25600445.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To the memory of the Sittii. Lucius Sittius Servilius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 75 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-25600445.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Sittius Servilius.

EDCS-25600446.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sittia Fedosa lived 45 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Paula Quartila lived 85 years. She is buried here. May her bones rest well.
EDCS-25600446.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Sittia Fedosa and Paula Quartila.

EDCS-25600447.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sittia Honorata, daughter of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 12 years. May you rest well here.
EDCS-25600447.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Honorata.

EDCS-25600448.translation: Sittia Rufina lived 25 years. She is buried here. Ovinia Rogata lived 85 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25600448.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Sittia Rufina and Ovinia Rogata.

EDCS-25600449.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittia Urbana.

EDCS-25600450.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Zitius Felix, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 70 years. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-25600450.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Zitius Felix.

EDCS-25600451.translation: To the memory of Turranius Avaritus, who lived 58 years. Ponponia Iulia, dearest.
EDCS-25600451.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Turranius Avaritus by Ponponia Iulia.

EDCS-25600452.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Valerius Maximus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, made this for himself while alive. He lived... years. Sacred to the departed spirits. To his dearest wife, Pomponia Fortunula, who lived... years. Lucius Valerius Maximus... and to Pomponia Fortunula, his dearest wife... Pomponia Fortunula, dear to me, wife, beloved, you outlived your husband by many years. All that we have was acquired by your honest labor. To the memory of a woman.
EDCS-25600452.summary: This is a complex funerary monument inscription for Lucius Valerius Maximus and his wife Pomponia Fortunula, likely erected by Maximus himself.

EDCS-25600453.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vibia Iullitta lived 60 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-25600453.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vibia Iullitta.

EDCS-25600454.summary: This inscription records the name of a woman, Via Extricata, likely a funerary or ownership marker.
