EDCS-25600456.translation: Son of Quirina, Capito, and in memory of the Tatii.
EDCS-25600456.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication related to a family or group named Tatii, possibly mentioning a man named Capito.

EDCS-25600458.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Silvanus Augustus. Lucius Herennius Urbanus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, master of arms, made and dedicated this at his own expense; the place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-25600458.summary: This is a dedication of a sacred place to Jupiter Silvanus Augustus by Lucius Herennius Urbanus, who served as a magister armorum, funded by himself, with official approval from the local council.

EDCS-25600459.translation: Solutor, son of Sumucis, willingly fulfilled his vow to Saturn.
EDCS-25600459.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Solutor, son of Sumucis, fulfilling a vow to the god Saturn.

EDCS-25600460.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Canicius Geminus, priest of Saturn, lived 80 years. Aemilia Optata lived 70 years. Here lies [her/him]. May [she/he] rest well.
EDCS-25600460.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Canicius Geminus and Aemilia Optata, noting their ages and priestly role, with a wish for their peaceful rest.

EDCS-25600461.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Aelius Ceparius lived 70 years. Buried here.
EDCS-25600461.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription stating the name, age, and burial of Caius Aelius Ceparius.

EDCS-25600462.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Decimus Gargilius Rogatus lived 75 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-25600462.summary: This funerary inscription records the name, age, and burial place of Decimus Gargilius Rogatus, with a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-25600463.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Barbarus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 85 years. He is buried here. May his bones rest well. Ennius Cellinianus and Sittia Fortunula, daughter of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 111 years. She is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-25600463.summary: This is a double funerary inscription for Caius Iulius Barbarus and Sittia Fortunula, noting their ages, tribal affiliations, and burial places, with wishes for their peaceful rest.

EDCS-25600464.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Rogatus lived 63 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-25600464.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name, age, and burial of Caius Iulius Rogatus, with a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-25600465.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Victor lived 70 years.
EDCS-25600465.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Caius Iulius Victor.

EDCS-25600466.translation: Marcus Sittius, son of Quirina, from the Valerian tribe, lived 80 years; he rests here. May his bones rest well. His descendant Bileaca lived [X] years; may his bones rest well. Marcus Sittius Paulus, son of Quirina, lived 10 years; he rests here. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-25600466.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lives and burials of Marcus Sittius, his descendant Bileaca, and Marcus Sittius Paulus, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-25600560.translation: Iulia Marcella lived 70 years. Her heir made this monument, and her sister Sixtilia Nemesis made this monument.
EDCS-25600560.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Iulia Marcella and was erected by her heir and her sister, Sixtilia Nemesis, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-25600561.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Iulia Maria lived 25 years. Tonneius Ianuarius, her husband, made this. May your bones rest well. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-25600561.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Iulia Maria and was erected by her husband, Tonneius Ianuarius, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-25600562.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Iulia Prima lived 26 years, 3 months, and 9 days. Her husband made this tomb for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-25600562.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Iulia Prima and was erected by her husband, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-25600563.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. For Iulia Secunda, pious daughter of Caius, who lived 25 years. Caius Iulius Probus [erected this] for his most rare wife.
EDCS-25600563.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Iulia Secunda and was erected by her husband, Caius Iulius Probus, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-25600564.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Her husband, Lucius, erected this monument for Iuventia Ianuaria while she was alive, and also for their son Luciolus.
EDCS-25600564.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Lucius erected a monument for his living wife, Iuventia Ianuaria, and their son Luciolus, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-25600565.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Quintus [son of] L. lived 60 years. L. Min [erected this].
EDCS-25600565.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Quintus and was erected by L. Min, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-25600566.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Saturninus, son of H. L., lived 59 years and 1 month. His wife and son made this monument.
EDCS-25600566.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Saturninus and was erected by his wife and son, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-25600567.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. For Lucia Valeria, who lived 5 years. Fulvia Casta, her mother, made this monument.
EDCS-25600567.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Lucia Valeria and was erected by her mother, Fulvia Casta, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-25600568.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. For Lucius Manilius Victorinus, who lived 13 years. Valeria [made this] for her son.
EDCS-25600568.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Lucius Manilius Victorinus and was erected by his mother, Valeria, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-25600569.translation: Marcius Fortunatus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-25600569.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a husband to his wife.

EDCS-25600570.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of Mercurius.

EDCS-25600571.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Her husband made this for his deserving wife, Mindia Tertulla, who lived for 45 years.
EDCS-25600571.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a husband to his wife, Mindia Tertulla, including her age at death.

EDCS-25600572.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Octavius Agathopus lived for 70 years. His son for his deserving father.
EDCS-25600572.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a son to his father, Lucius Octavius Agathopus, including his age at death.

EDCS-25600574.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Pannonius Iucundus Pius lived for 19 years. He made this.
EDCS-25600574.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Pannonius Iucundus Pius, stating his age at death and that he made it himself.

EDCS-25600575.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pompeia Saturnina lived for 40 years. Genialis made this for his spouse. Papiria Saturnia lived for 7 years. Made for her dearest son.
EDCS-25600575.summary: This is a funerary epitaph with multiple dedications, one by Genialis for his spouse Pompeia Saturnina (including her age) and another for his son Papiria Saturnia.

EDCS-25600576.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucio Percennio Optato lived for 53 years. His son Pudens made this.
EDCS-25600576.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Pudens to his father, Lucio Percennio Optato, including his age at death.

EDCS-25600577.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Percennia Secunda lived for 5 years. Her heir, Percennius Optatus, made this for his daughter.
EDCS-25600577.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Percennius Optatus to his daughter, Percennia Secunda, mentioning her age and his role as heir.

EDCS-25600578.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Petronius Maximus lived for 23 years. His mother, Valeria Zosime, arranged for this to be made for her most beloved son.
EDCS-25600578.summary: This is a funerary epitaph arranged by Valeria Zosime for her son, Titus Petronius Maximus, stating his age at death.

EDCS-25600579.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Pomponius Proculus lived for 80 years. His sons made this for their devoted father.
EDCS-25600579.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by sons to their father, Publius Pomponius Proculus, including his age at death.

EDCS-25600580.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Quintus Petronius Romulus lived 27 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25600580.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Quintus Petronius Romulus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600581.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Popea Faustina lived 40 years. Iulius Victor made this for his sister.
EDCS-25600581.summary: A tombstone erected by Iulius Victor for his sister, Popea Faustina, who lived 40 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600582.translation: Rogatianus made this for his wife, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-25600582.summary: An epitaph for a deserving wife, made by her husband Rogatianus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600583.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Salvius Victor made this for his wife Iulia Urbana, who lived 45 years and deserved well of him.
EDCS-25600583.summary: A tombstone erected by Salvius Victor for his wife Iulia Urbana, who lived 45 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600584.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Flavia Saturnina made this for her husband Lucius Scantius Probatus, who lived 60 years and deserved well of her.
EDCS-25600584.summary: A tombstone erected by Flavia Saturnina for her deserving husband Lucius Scantius Probatus, who lived 60 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600585.translation: To our... Sicia, daughter of Numidius, Ofelia.
EDCS-25600585.summary: A fragmented inscription possibly commemorating a woman named Ofelia, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600586.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Hermes made this for his dearest son Sellarius Macar.
EDCS-25600586.summary: A tombstone erected by Hermes for his beloved son Sellarius Macar, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600588.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Caia Silicia Matutina lived 65 years.
EDCS-25600588.summary: A tombstone recording the life of Caia Silicia Matutina, who lived 65 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600589.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Publius Sittius Donatus lived one year and four months. Publius Sittius [erected this].
EDCS-25600589.summary: A tombstone for Publius Sittius Donatus, who died in infancy, erected by Publius Sittius Fatalis, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600590.translation: Sittia Rufina, daughter of Publius, granddaughter and grandsons.
EDCS-25600590.summary: A dedication mentioning Sittia Rufina and her grandchildren, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600591.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Terentius Bassus lived for 2 years. Publius Terentius Donatus made this for his son.
EDCS-25600591.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Terentius Bassus, set up by his father, Publius Terentius Donatus.

EDCS-25600592.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Terentius Venustus, son of Caius, of the Arnense tribe, from Carthage, lived for 14 years. Aegrinia Fortunata made this for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-25600592.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Terentius Venustus, set up by his wife, Aegrinia Fortunata.

EDCS-25600593.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Tiburtius Saturninus lived for 96 years. Tiburtius Rusticus placed this at his own expense for his deserving father.
EDCS-25600593.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Tiburtius Saturninus, set up by his son, Tiburtius Rusticus.

EDCS-25600594.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titurnia, daughter of Titurnus, lived for 60 years. Caius Sextius Bassus made this for his most rare wife, who deserved it.
EDCS-25600594.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titurnia, set up by her husband, Caius Sextius Bassus.

EDCS-25600596.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Tuccius Fortunatus lived for 3 years. Salvia made this for her husband.
EDCS-25600596.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tuccius Fortunatus, set up by his wife, Salvia.

EDCS-25600597.summary: A funerary inscription fragment for a freedman named Valerius.

EDCS-25600598.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Valerius Donatus, son of Caius, from Spoletium, lived for 21 years. Cornelia Tuta made this for her dearest son.
EDCS-25600598.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Valerius Donatus, set up by his mother, Cornelia Tuta.

EDCS-25600599.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Valerius Tertius lived for 18 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600599.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Valerius Tertius, stating his burial place.

EDCS-25600600.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valeria Donata, who lived for 25 years. From her dutiful sons to their kind mother.
EDCS-25600600.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Donata, set up by her sons.

EDCS-25600601.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vaterius Publicius lived for 25 years.
EDCS-25600601.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vaterius Publicius.

EDCS-25600602.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vibius Ianuarius, son of Lucius, lived here for 16 years.
EDCS-25600602.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Vibius Ianuarius at the age of 16.

EDCS-25600603.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vibia Secunda lived for 3 years.
EDCS-25600603.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Vibia Secunda at the age of 3.

EDCS-25600604.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Vicus, most faithful, who lived 11 years. Honnus, his friend, placed this here as he deserved.
EDCS-25600604.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a friend to Vicus, who died at age 11.

EDCS-25600605.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To the well-deserving father, who lived 70 years. His wife and son made this.
EDCS-25600605.summary: A funerary epitaph for a father who lived 70 years, set up by his wife and son.

EDCS-25600606.translation: To the incomparable Parthinii, a pious daughter. His R. H. B. V. A. XI made this; she is buried here.
EDCS-25600606.summary: A funerary epitaph for Parthinii, set up by a relative (R.H.B.V.A. XI) after her death.

EDCS-25600609.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. The father ordered this to be placed for his son.
EDCS-25600609.summary: A funerary epitaph indicating a father commissioned a monument for his son.

EDCS-25600617.translation: From the workshop of Caius Ianuarius Africanus.
EDCS-25600617.summary: An inscription identifying the workshop of Caius Ianuarius Africanus, likely a craftsman.

EDCS-25600619.translation: Ianuarius, the trumpeter, fulfilled his vow willingly.
EDCS-25600619.summary: A votive inscription by Ianuarius, a trumpeter, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-25600623.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cornelius Victor, trumpeter of the III Augusta Legion, arranged for this to be made for Claudia Victorina, daughter of Marcus Claudius Marcellus, a veteran, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-25600623.summary: A funerary epitaph for Claudia Victorina, daughter of a veteran, arranged by a soldier from the III Augusta Legion.

EDCS-25600624.summary: An incomplete inscription likely recording the name and possibly age or status of Aurelius Hermia.

EDCS-25600626.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Hortensius Primus, veteran, lived 65 years.
EDCS-25600626.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Hortensius Primus, a veteran, and includes his age at death.

EDCS-25600627.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription to Valentino Maximo.

EDCS-25600629.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. [---] to [---] LVANVS, son of Ti. [---] opericus, lived.
EDCS-25600629.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely commemorating a person named LVANVS.

EDCS-25600630.translation: Antonius Paulus dedicated this to the Great Eternal God while alive.
EDCS-25600630.summary: This is a dedication to the god 'Magno Aeterno' made by Antonius Paulus during his lifetime.

EDCS-25600631.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Titia Iulia, my most unique mother.
EDCS-25600631.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Titia Iulia by her child.

EDCS-25600633.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Arruntius Victorinus lived 18 years.
EDCS-25600633.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Arruntius Victorinus and his age at death.

EDCS-25600636.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Titus Flavius Rogatus and his son lived 40 years.
EDCS-25600636.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Titus Flavius Rogatus and his son, noting the son's age at death.

EDCS-25600637.translation: The Manes and Genius of Proclinus, attendant of Lucius Julius Crispinus, made this under the supervision of Publius Antonius Valentis, the freedman, a good and holy man.
EDCS-25600637.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to Proclinus, an attendant of Lucius Julius Crispinus, by his freedman Publius Antonius Valentis.

EDCS-25600639.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. A centurion of the Legio III Augusta lived 77 years; the heir erected this for the deserving man.
EDCS-25600639.summary: This funerary inscription honors a centurion of Legio III Augusta who lived to be 77 and was commemorated by his heir.

EDCS-25600640.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Clodia Paulina, daughter of Caius, lived 28 years. Her father made this.
EDCS-25600640.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Clodia Paulina, daughter of Caius, and states that her father erected the monument.

EDCS-25600641.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Domitia Maxima lived 80 years. Caius Annius Saturninus made this for both while they were alive.
EDCS-25600641.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Domitia Maxima, erected by her husband Caius Annius Saturninus.

EDCS-25600642.translation: To the departed spirits. Fadina Saturnina lived 9 years.
EDCS-25600642.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Fadina Saturnina.

EDCS-25600643.translation: To Julia Antonina, my dearest sister.
EDCS-25600643.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Julia Antonina, likely from a sibling.

EDCS-25600645.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aurelius Victor, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 37 years. Titus Antonius Lucianus, his father-in-law, made this for him deserving.
EDCS-25600645.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Victor, a soldier, erected by his father-in-law.

EDCS-25600646.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Herennucia lived 32 years in her own (home/possession). Anicius Iulius, a soldier of the XIV legion, father and father-in-law, made this.
EDCS-25600646.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Herennucia, erected by her husband and father-in-law, who was also a soldier.

EDCS-25600647.translation: To the departed spirits. Bassilla lived 20 years. Hilarosa made this for her mother.
EDCS-25600647.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Bassilla, erected by her mother Hilarosa.

EDCS-25600648.translation: To the departed spirits. Caninius Saturninus lived 37 years. His wife Valeria made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-25600648.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caninius Saturninus, erected by his wife Valeria.

EDCS-25600649.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Caecilia, who lived 30 years. Octavius Iulianus, son, for his deserving mother.
EDCS-25600649.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caecilia, erected by her son Octavius Iulianus.

EDCS-25600650.translation: To the departed spirits. Flavia Fortunata lived 25 years. Her deserving wife made this, here she lies.
EDCS-25600650.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavia Fortunata, erected by his wife.

EDCS-25600651.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaea Secunda, memory for her deserving mother. She lived 55 years.
EDCS-25600651.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaea Secunda, erected by her mother.

EDCS-25600653.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caius Iulius Fl [made this for] his son Caius Iulius Tens, [who lived] years.
EDCS-25600653.summary: A funerary dedication for Caius Iulius Tens, made by his father.

EDCS-25600654.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulia Victoria lived 25 years. Caius Caecilius Mar made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-25600654.summary: A funerary dedication by Caius Caecilius Mar for his wife Iulia Victoria, who died at 25.

EDCS-25600655.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Ividnia Idrinni lived 25 years. [possibly a name] Obano.
EDCS-25600655.summary: A fragmented funerary dedication, likely for Ividnia Idrinni.

EDCS-25600656.translation: Tomb of Sallustia, girl, who lived 16 years.
EDCS-25600656.summary: A funerary monument for a girl named Sallustia.

EDCS-25600657.summary: An inscription recording an exceptionally long lifespan for Caius Valerius Justus.

EDCS-25600658.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Quintus Valerius Pudens, father, who lived 71 years, his sons made this.
EDCS-25600658.summary: A funerary dedication by sons for their father, Quintus Valerius Pudens.

EDCS-25600659.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Quintus Vat Pudatus the pious, Ianuarius made this for his brother, son of N.F., who lived 33 years.
EDCS-25600659.summary: A funerary dedication by Ianuarius for his brother, Quintus Vat Pudatus.

EDCS-25600660.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aianius lived 67 years. His son Flavius C.II made this.
EDCS-25600660.summary: A funerary dedication for Aianius by his son Flavius.

EDCS-25600661.translation: [---] N. Pa made this for [his] deserving [---].
EDCS-25600661.summary: A fragmented dedication, possibly funerary, by N. Pa.

EDCS-25600672.translation: To Imperator, our lord Constantine Maximus Victor, always Augustus, born for the good of the state, 22 [years/date].
EDCS-25600672.summary: A dedication to Emperor Constantine, likely from the 22nd year of his reign or life.

EDCS-25600674.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, in his fourth tribunician power, consul for the third time, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-25600674.summary: This is a titulary inscription for Emperor Trajan, listing his official titles and dignities.

EDCS-25601330.translation: Ammaedara, standard-bearer of the I Augusta African cavalry squadron from Carthage. I C from Carthage, I F I C III I from Carthage, I O from Carthage. From Carthage, Cuicul, I R O A A II. From Carthage, I L O I T O Oea. From Carthage, Castra immunis, Leptis, Leptis A I, equestrian soldier. From Ammaedara, Carthage, Carthage, Carthage, Hadrumetum, Carthage, Carthage, F I. From Carthage, Castra, Ammaedara, Carthage, Carthage, Hadrumetum, C I I. From Vaga Oea, immune. From Carthage, L A, Carthage, S I O N I A S I. From Augusta, Carthage. Marcus Antonius Hebenus from Carthage, immune. Caius AC, Caius Iunius Felix from Carthage. Marcus I inius Felix from Carthage. Caius A i us Victor from Theveste. Titus Iulius Victor, Caius Cornelius Saturninus from Hadrumetum. Lucius Loreius Donatus from Theveste. Caius Lucretius Maximus from Hadrumetum. Caius Iulius Saturninus from Theveste. Caius Iulius Fortunatus from Carthage. Iulius Licinius Valens from Theveste. Iulius Mii cius Festus from Carthage. Publius Ia us Britto from Carthage. Caius I aius Secundus from Carthage. Sextus acius Felix from Carthage. M Quintus A ius Fortunatus from Hadrumetum. Quintus Varius Vicanus from Carthage, immune. Caius Sempronius Rufinus from Carthage. Caius I S Fructus from Thamugadi. Lucius Annius Pudens from Carthage. Caius Aelius Faustinus from Carthage. Iulius Aurelius Martialis from Ammaedara. Quintus Aufidius Felix from Carthage, equestrian soldier. Marcus Annonius Saturninus from Hadrumetum. Lucius Pompeius Amandus from Ammaedara, immune. Caius Vibius Saturninus from Thyzdro. Quintus Caecilius Faustus, Sextus Iulius Liberalis from Carthage. Aulus Marcius Crescens from Cirta. Marcus Antonius Faustus from Carthage, equestrian soldier. C I. Lucius Aemilius Bassus from Hadrumetum, equestrian soldier. Caius Terentius Graesianus from Maxula. Caius Iulius Hilarus from Carthage. C. Marcus Aelius Saturninus from Vaga. R. Publius Aurelius Felix from Uri. Quintus Maximius Extricatus from Madaura. Caius S us Idiu i from Carthage. Quintus Aemilius FelIix from Manliana. Lucius Propertius Felix from Vina. Publius Caiveius Fuscus IRI IVB. Caius Muannus Secundus from Carthage. Publius I aus Fa ipa s from Carthage, standard-bearer.
EDCS-25601330.summary: This extensive inscription lists numerous individuals, their places of origin, and sometimes their military or civilian status, likely serving as a roster or record of personnel associated with a military unit or administrative division, possibly related to the province of Africa Proconsularis.

EDCS-25601709.translation: To Caesennius Alexander, who is also called Nat-
EDCS-25601709.summary: This is a dedication to Caesennius Alexander, indicating a funerary context or a dedication of some kind.

EDCS-25601710.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Camerius Felix lived for-
EDCS-25601710.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Camerius Felix, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-25601711.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Cattius lived for-
EDCS-25601711.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Cattius, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-25601712.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Cessius Rogatianus lived for 6 years and 9 months.
EDCS-25601712.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Cessius Rogatianus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-25601713.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Claudius Adiectus-
EDCS-25601713.summary: This is a dedication to Claudius Adiectus, likely in a funerary context.

EDCS-25601714.translation: He lived for 45 years. Caius Coelius Adiutor made this for his most devoted patron.
EDCS-25601714.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Caius Coelius Adiutor and indicates he commissioned the monument for his patron.

EDCS-25601715.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Cornelius Pius, who lived 36 years.
EDCS-25601715.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelius Pius, recording his age at death.

EDCS-25601717.translation: muasi to his son F Dionisius Fro-
EDCS-25601717.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely part of a funerary monument or dedication.

EDCS-25601718.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Donata lived for 13 years.
EDCS-25601718.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Donata.

EDCS-25601719.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Eutychetes, most dutiful son, lived for 11 years, 8 months, and 3 days. Philadelphus and Mazica, parents, made this. He is buried here.
EDCS-25601719.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan and burial of Eutychetes, commissioned by his parents.

EDCS-25601720.translation: Saturninus, son of Titus, [made this] for his son Fortunatus, who lived for 21 years, 1 month, and 11 days.
EDCS-25601720.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Fortunatus, commissioned by his father Saturninus.

EDCS-25601721.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Felix, the son.
EDCS-25601721.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to a son named Felix.

EDCS-25601722.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Flavius Hermes lived for 40 years. Iulia Dextriana made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-25601722.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Gaius Flavius Hermes, commissioned by his wife Iulia Dextriana.

EDCS-25601723.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavius Sabinus lived for 77 years, 6 months, and 5 days. Flavius Iustus [made this] for his deserving father.
EDCS-25601723.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Flavius Sabinus, commissioned by his son Flavius Iustus.

EDCS-25601724.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavius Victor lived for 35 years. His heirs made this.
EDCS-25601724.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Flavius Victor, commissioned by his heirs.

EDCS-25601725.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Tiberius Furnius Claudianus lived for 5 years. Claudia Gabinia, his mother, made this.
EDCS-25601725.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Tiberius Furnius Claudianus, commissioned by his mother Claudia Gabinia.

EDCS-25601726.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. G. Claudia lived for 27 years. A. Fortunatus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-25601726.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of G. Claudia, commissioned by her husband A. Fortunatus.

EDCS-25601727.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Helena is buried here.
EDCS-25601727.summary: This is a funerary epitaph indicating the burial place of Helena.

EDCS-25601730.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Julius Titus [---]
EDCS-25601730.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, mentioning Julius Titus.

EDCS-25601731.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed, Julius Annianus Valerius.
EDCS-25601731.summary: This is a funerary inscription sacred to the spirits of the departed, mentioning Julius Annianus Valerius.

EDCS-25601732.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Julius Claudianus lived 35 years.
EDCS-25601732.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julius Claudianus, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-25601734.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Iavarius lived 8 years. Gellia Victorina, his mother, made this.
EDCS-25601734.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of 8-year-old Gaius Julius Iavarius, made by his mother Gellia Victorina.

EDCS-25601735.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed, Julius Ianuarius, also called Coddeus, lived. His mother made and dedicated this.
EDCS-25601735.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Julius Ianuarius, also known as Coddeus, and was made and dedicated by his mother.

EDCS-25601736.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed, Julius Martialis lived 85 years. Aurelius Communis, Antonius Felix, and Afranius Victor, his heirs, made this.
EDCS-25601736.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Julius Martialis at 85 years old, made by his heirs Aurelius Communis, Antonius Felix, and Afranius Victor.

EDCS-25601737.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Julius Processus lived 10 years.
EDCS-25601737.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julius Processus, who lived 10 years.

EDCS-25601738.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. The pious boy, Iulius, lived 16 years. Mami made this for his pious son who deserved well of him.
EDCS-25601738.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of the 16-year-old Iulius, made by Mami for his deserving son.

EDCS-25601739.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Gaius Julius Rufinus lived 2 years. Rufinus made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-25601739.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of 2-year-old Gaius Julius Rufinus, made by Rufinus for his son.

EDCS-25601740.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed, Gaius Julius Sabinus lived 75 years. Domitia, his wife [made this].
EDCS-25601740.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gaius Julius Sabinus at 75 years old, made by his wife Domitia.

EDCS-25601743.summary: This is a dedication to an individual named Julius Victor.

EDCS-25601744.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Dextriana lived 50 years. Dexter, her husband, made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-25601744.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Dextriana, erected by her husband Dexter, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25601745.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Iulia Felicia, who lived 95 years.
EDCS-25601745.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Felicia, indicating her lifespan.

EDCS-25601746.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Honorata, aged 40, made this for herself while alive.
EDCS-25601746.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Honorata, who commissioned her own tomb in advance.

EDCS-25601747.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Manlius Saturninus, who lived 60 years. Iulia Honorata, his wife, made this for Manlius.
EDCS-25601747.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Manlius Saturninus, erected by his wife Iulia Honorata.

EDCS-25700380.translation: To the divine Augustus, our lord Flavius Claudius Julianus.
EDCS-25700380.summary: This is a dedication to the deified emperor Flavius Claudius Julianus, possibly dating to the late Roman period.

EDCS-25700381.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, with tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, restored [this] 176.
EDCS-25700381.summary: This inscription records a restoration by Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), detailing his titles and powers, and indicating a restoration project completed in the 176th year of some era, likely AD 213.

EDCS-25700382.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, with tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, restored [this] 176.
EDCS-25700382.summary: This inscription records a restoration by Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), detailing his titles and powers, and indicating a restoration project completed in the 176th year of some era, likely AD 213.

EDCS-25700393.translation: Emperor Caesar, grandson of the divine Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, son of the divine Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus Maximus Adiabenicus Maximus, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus (called 'Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix'), pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country; from Carthage [distance] miles, from Hippona Regia [distance] miles, from Cirta [distance] miles, from Lambaesis [distance] miles, from Theveste [distance] miles.
EDCS-25700393.summary: This inscription is a lengthy titulature of Emperor Elagabalus (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus), issued during his third consulship and tribunician power, indicating distances to various cities from a specific point.

EDCS-25700395.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-25700395.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Philip the Arab, likely on a public monument.

EDCS-25700396.translation: To our Lord Flavius Valerius Claudius Caesar
EDCS-25700396.summary: This is a dedication to the emperor Claudius Gothicus, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25700397.translation: To Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, supreme pontiff, father of the country, proconsul, and to Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus
EDCS-25700397.summary: This inscription honors the emperors Valerian I and Gallienus, probably from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-25700399.translation: Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, descendant of Emperor Caesar the son of the divine Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, grandson of the divine Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus Maximus Adiabenicus Maximus, supreme pontiff, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country
EDCS-25700399.summary: This titulature lists the full imperial titles of Caracalla, dating to around 205 CE.

EDCS-25700401.translation: To Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Augustus and Publius Licinius Gallienus Augustus
EDCS-25700401.summary: This inscription honors the emperors Valerian I and Gallienus, likely from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-25700403.translation: To the Perpetual Emperor Maximian Augustus
EDCS-25700403.summary: This is a dedication to the emperor Maximian, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-25700426.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Gaius Maecenius Aphrodisius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25700447.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Domitia Oto lived 12 years, she is buried here
EDCS-25700447.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Domitia Oto, who died at the age of 12, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25700449.translation: Caecilia Marcella lived 30 years, she is buried here
EDCS-25700449.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilia Marcella, who died at the age of 30, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25700450.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Antonia Anucla lived 25 years
EDCS-25700450.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonia Anucla, who died at the age of 25, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25700458.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Lollius Faustus lived 85 years; may his bones rest in peace
EDCS-25700458.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Lollius Faustus, who died at the age of 85, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25700459.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Lollius Faustus, son, lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25700459.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Lollius Faustus, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-25700460.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Lollius Felix lived 15 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25700460.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Lollius Felix, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-25700461.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Lollius Iunius lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25700461.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Lollius Iunius, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-25700546.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilia Helias lived 71 years; she is buried here. To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Iulius Secundus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 70 years.
EDCS-25700546.summary: This inscription contains two funerary epitaphs, one for Aemilia Helias (71) and another for Quintus Iulius Secundus (70).

EDCS-25800004.translation: For the happy Claudius, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, consul, father of the fatherland, by the state of the colony of Thumugadis.
EDCS-25800004.summary: This is a dedication by the colony of Thumugadis to Claudius, honoring his titles as pontifex maximus, consul, and father of the fatherland.

EDCS-25800007.translation: To Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, son of the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul, by the state of the colony of Tamugadis I.
EDCS-25800007.summary: This is a dedication by the colony of Tamugadis I to Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, son of Emperor Maximinus, honoring his imperial titles.

EDCS-25800008.translation: Under Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, consul, proconsul.
EDCS-25800008.summary: This inscription commemorates the consulship and other honors of Emperor Gordian III.

EDCS-25800010.translation: To our lord Emperor Caesar Licinius Invictus Augustus, from the colony, [the] milestones [dedicated by] the state of the colony of Thamugadis I.
EDCS-25800010.summary: This inscription records the dedication of milestones by the colony of Thamugadis I to Emperor Licinius.

EDCS-25800012.translation: To our lord Emperor Flavius Claudius Julianus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-25800012.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Emperor Julian.

EDCS-25800013.translation: To our most invincible lords, the Emperors Valentinian and Valens, always Augusti.
EDCS-25800013.summary: This inscription honors the co-emperors Valentinian and Valens.

EDCS-25800018.translation: Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex, with tribunician power, consul for the second time, father of the fatherland, [in his] 29th year.
EDCS-25800018.summary: This is an inscription of Emperor Antoninus Pius, dating to his 29th year.

EDCS-25800019.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius, the Unconquered, Pious, Happy Augustus.
EDCS-25800019.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Claudius Gothicus.

EDCS-25800020.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Aurelius Maximianus Augustus.
EDCS-25800020.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Maximian.

EDCS-25800021.translation: To Publius Licinius Gallienus, most noble Caesar, Augustus.
EDCS-25800021.summary: This is a dedication to the Emperor Gallienus.

EDCS-25800022.translation: To the Perpetual Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus, Pious, Happy Augustus.
EDCS-25800022.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Aurelian.

EDCS-25800023.translation: To Aurelianus, Unconquered, Pious, Happy Augustus.
EDCS-25800023.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Aurelian.

EDCS-25800024.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Diocletianus, Pious, Happy Augustus.
EDCS-25800024.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Diocletian.

EDCS-25800025.translation: To our Lord Marcus Aurelius Maximianus ino Augustus.
EDCS-25800025.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Maximian.

EDCS-25800026.translation: Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, grandson of the deified Septimius Severus, son of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Magnus.
EDCS-25800026.summary: This names Emperor Severus Alexander, referencing his imperial ancestry.

EDCS-25800027.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus, the Unconquered, Pious, Happy Augustus.
EDCS-25800027.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Gordian III.

EDCS-25800028.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-25800028.summary: This is a titulary inscription for Emperor Philip the Arab, likely dating to his reign (244-249 AD).

EDCS-25800029.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus.
EDCS-25800029.summary: This is a fragmentary titulary inscription for Emperor Philip the Arab.

EDCS-25800030.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Licinius Valerianus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, and to Licinius Gallienus, most noble Augustus.
EDCS-25800030.summary: This inscription records the joint acclamation of Emperors Valerian and Gallienus, dating to their co-rule (253-260 AD).

EDCS-25800031.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Valerius Maximianus, the Unconquered, Augustus.
EDCS-25800031.summary: This is a titulary inscription for Emperor Maximian, likely dating to his reign (286-305 AD).

EDCS-25800032.summary: This inscription honors Flavius Constantius, likely Constantius Chlorus, dating to the Tetrarchy (late 3rd/early 4th century AD).

EDCS-25800033.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, and to Marcus Aurelius Carinus, most noble Caesar, and to [him] Numerianus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-25800033.summary: This titulary inscription records the acclamation of Emperors Carus and his sons Carinus and Numerian, dating to their co-rule (283-284 AD).

EDCS-25800034.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Numerian, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-25800034.summary: This is a titulary inscription for Emperor Numerian, dating to his reign (283-284 AD).

EDCS-25800035.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-25800035.summary: This is a titulary inscription for Emperor Claudius Gothicus, dating to his reign (268-270 AD).

EDCS-25800036.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-25800036.summary: This is a titulary inscription for Emperor Aurelian, dating to his reign (270-275 AD).

EDCS-25800037.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, Father of the Fatherland, High Priest, with Tribunician Power, Proconsul, and to the Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Afinius Gallus Veldumnianus Volusianus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, on behalf of the public affairs of the Cirta community from the public funds of the Siguitani.
EDCS-25800037.summary: This inscription records honors granted by the people of Cirta to Emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Volusianus, dating to their co-rule (251-253 AD).

EDCS-25800038.translation: To the Emperor Diocletian, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-25800038.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Diocletian, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-25800041.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, the Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, consul, proconsul. (VIDICA?)
EDCS-25800041.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Maximinus Thrax, dating to his reign between 235 and 238 CE.

EDCS-25800042.summary: This is a possessive or genitive attribution to Emperor Maximinus Thrax, likely part of a larger inscription.

EDCS-25800043.translation: To Perpetuus, Emperor Caesar Domitius Aurelianus, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-25800043.summary: This inscription dedicates to Emperor Aurelian, who reigned from 270 to 275 CE.

EDCS-25800048.translation: greatest of the Adiabeni, son of the greatest. IMX the republic Cron
EDCS-25800048.summary: This fragmentary inscription seems to refer to a significant individual, possibly a military leader, and mentions a republic, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-25800049.translation: Marcus Aurelius, son of the Emperor Caesar, grandson of the deified Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, and of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus Maximus Adiabenicus Maximus.
EDCS-25800049.summary: This lengthy titulature identifies Marcus Aurelius (likely Caracalla) and lists his extensive imperial honors and ancestry, dating to his co-rule with Septimius Severus and/or Caracalla's sole reign.

EDCS-25800050.translation: The Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, and of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus Maximus Adiabenicus Maximus, XLII
EDCS-25800050.summary: This inscription lists the extensive imperial titles of an emperor (likely Caracalla) and includes the number XLII, possibly referring to a year of reign or a quantity, dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25800051.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus, the Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, father of the country, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 25th time, consul.
EDCS-25800051.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Gordian III, likely dating to the later part of his reign (238-244 CE) given the mention of his 25th consulship.

EDCS-25800053.summary: This is a dedication to Flavius Constantine (Constantine the Great) in his capacity as Caesar, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-25800055.translation: To our Lords the Augusti Flavius Valentinianus and Valens (consuls IIII), to our Lord Flavius Gratianus, the eternal Augustus, to our Lord Flavius Claudius, to our Lord Magnus Decentius, the most noble Caesar (consuls IIII).
EDCS-25800055.summary: This inscription honors multiple Western Roman emperors and a Caesar, likely dating to the period of shared rule in the mid-to-late 4th century CE.

EDCS-25800059.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Constantinus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, VII.
EDCS-25800059.summary: This is a dedication to the emperor Constantine I, likely on a monument, with the emperor holding the seventh consulship.

EDCS-25800060.translation: To our lords, the invincible Diocletian and Maximian, senior Augusti and Emperors, and to our lords, the invincible Constantinus and Maximianus, Augusti, and to the most noble Caesars Severus and Maximinus.
EDCS-25800060.summary: This inscription honors multiple emperors of the Tetrarchy, including Diocletian, Maximian, Constantius, Severus, and Maximinus.

EDCS-25800061.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Constantinus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, VIIII.
EDCS-25800061.summary: This is a dedication to the emperor Constantine I, likely on a monument, with the emperor holding the ninth consulship.

EDCS-25800062.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, son of the deified Magnus Antoninus, grandson of the deified Severus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul, father of the country, restored [this] XVI.
EDCS-25800062.summary: This inscription records the restoration of something by Emperor Severus Alexander, referencing his imperial titles and parentage, with a numeral possibly indicating the year or a quantity.

EDCS-25800063.translation: To the invincible, pious, fortunate, always Augustus XVI.
EDCS-25800063.summary: This is a dedication to an emperor, likely on a monument, referencing his perpetual status and a numeral XVI which could indicate a consulship or anniversary.

EDCS-25800064.translation: To our lord Valerius Constantinus, invincible, pious, fortunate, most noble Augustus.
EDCS-25800064.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Constantine I (Constantinus), likely on a monument, emphasizing his virtues and noble status.

EDCS-25800065.translation: To our lords Flavius Constantius, most flourishing Caesar.
EDCS-25800065.summary: This inscription honors Flavius Constantius as Caesar, likely on a monument, emphasizing his flourishing status.

EDCS-25800066.translation: To the perpetual, most victorious, most indulgent Emperor Lucius Domitius Aurelianus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power V, consul II, father of the country VIII.
EDCS-25800066.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Aurelian, detailing his imperial titles and his fifth tribunician power, second consulship, and eighth designation as father of the country.

EDCS-25800067.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, father of the deified Constantius, Emperor, to the protecting gods.
EDCS-25800067.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Emperor Constantius II to the gods, honoring his father Constantius I and referencing his own imperial titles.

EDCS-25800068.translation: To Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, most noble Caesar, Augustus, prince of the youth, VII.
EDCS-25800068.summary: This inscription honors the Caesar and later Emperor Maximinus Thrax, likely on a monument, with a numeral VII possibly indicating a legion or year.

EDCS-25800069.translation: To our emperor, our lord, Lucius Domitius Aurelianus, the Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-25800069.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Aurelian (ruled 270-275 AD), likely on a public monument.

EDCS-25800070.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, grandson of the deified Septimius Severus, son of the deified Antoninus Pius, chief priest, invested with tribunician power, consul, father of the country, for the sixth time.
EDCS-25800070.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Emperor Severus Alexander (ruled 222-235 AD), detailing his titles and powers.

EDCS-25800071.translation: To our lord, the Unconquered Aurelius Claudius, the Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, for the sixth time.
EDCS-25800071.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Claudius Gothicus (ruled 268-270 AD), likely on a public monument.

EDCS-25800073.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, Aurelius Diocletianus, the Unconquered, the Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-25800073.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Emperor Diocletian (ruled 284-305 AD), likely on a public monument.

EDCS-25800076.translation: To Flavius Valerius Constantius Herculius Junior, most noble Caesar, by the state of Mascula.
EDCS-25800076.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Constantius I (ruled as Caesar 293-305 AD), made by the province of Mascula.

EDCS-25800078.translation: To Publius Flavius Claudius Constantius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-25800078.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Caesar Constantius I (ruled as Caesar 293-305 AD).

EDCS-25800079.translation: To our lord, Flavius Claudius Julianus, the Great, victor and triumphant, always Augustus.
EDCS-25800079.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Julian (ruled 361-363 AD), likely on a public monument.

EDCS-25800080.translation: To our lord, Flavius Valerius Constantius, most noble Caesar, for the twenty-second time.
EDCS-25800080.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Constantius I (ruled as Caesar 293-305 AD), likely on a public monument.

EDCS-25800082.translation: To our lord, the Pious Valerius Constantius, Augustus, for the twenty-second time.
EDCS-25800082.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Constantine I (ruled 306-337 AD), likely on a public monument.

EDCS-25900001.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, invested with tribunician power, consul, father of the country, proconsul, and to Marcus Julius Philippus, [who repaired] the road, damaged by rains and age.
EDCS-25900001.summary: This inscription records the repair of a road by Emperor Philip the Arab (ruled 244-249 AD) and his son, also named Marcus Julius Philippus.

EDCS-25900002.translation: Under Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, father of the fatherland, proconsul, and Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-25900002.summary: This inscription records the names and titles of emperors Valerian and Gallienus, likely as part of a larger dedication or honorary monument.

EDCS-25900006.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, son of the deified Antoninus Pius, grandson of the deified Severus Pius, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul for the 2nd time, designated for the 3rd time, proconsul, most fortunate and most unconquered, and most indulgent of all past princes, restored the road, which had collapsed due to rains and age, along with its bridges.
EDCS-25900006.summary: This inscription records Emperor Marcus Aurelius restoring a road and bridges, likely dating to his earlier reign based on the consular and tribunician dignities.

EDCS-25900007.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 19th time, imperator for the 4th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul maximus, most unconquered, most holy, bravest, most fortunate, and most indulgent of all princes, son of the deified Septimius Pius Augustus, born for the good of the republic.
EDCS-25900007.summary: This inscription details the extensive titles and honors of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus (Caracalla), emphasizing his military victories and divine ancestry.

EDCS-25900009.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Sarmaticus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 11th time, imperator for the 8th time, consul for the 5th time, father of the fatherland, son of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius, grandson of the deified Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, the noblest and most fortunate of all princes, restored [the road].
EDCS-25900009.summary: This inscription records Emperor Commodus restoring a road and highlighting his extensive imperial ancestry, likely from the period when he held tribunician power for the 11th time.

EDCS-25900010.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, son of the deified Antoninus Pius, grandson of the deified Severus Pius, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, most fortunate and most unconquered, and most indulgent of all past princes, restored the road, which had collapsed due to rains and age, along with its bridges.
EDCS-25900010.summary: This inscription records Emperor Marcus Aurelius restoring a road and bridges, dating to the early part of his reign based on the consular and tribunician dignities.

EDCS-25900011.translation: The Emperor Caesar Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, grandson of the deified Severus Pius, son of the deified Antoninus Pius, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, restored the road, which had collapsed due to rains and age, along with its bridges.
EDCS-25900011.summary: This inscription records Emperor Severus Alexander restoring a road and bridges, likely from a period early in his sole reign.

EDCS-25900012.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-25900012.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to Flavius Valerius Constantinus (Constantine the Great) as Caesar, likely dating from the early part of his career.

EDCS-25900015.translation: The Emperor Caesar Caius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, consul for the 2nd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul.
EDCS-25900015.summary: This inscription lists the titles of Emperor Trajan Decius, likely serving as a preface to a larger dedication or public work.

EDCS-25900017.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carinus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, father of the fatherland, proconsul, [he] restored the road, which had collapsed due to rains and age, along with its bridges.
EDCS-25900017.summary: This inscription records Emperor Carinus restoring a road and bridges, dating to his period of rule.

EDCS-25900019.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul, and Marcus Iulius Philippus, most noble Caesar Augustus.
EDCS-25900019.summary: This inscription lists the titles of Emperor Philip the Arab and his son, also named Philip, likely part of an honorary monument or dedication.

EDCS-25900020.translation: The emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, consul for the second time, father of the country, proconsul, restored the road, which had collapsed with its bridges due to rains and age.
EDCS-25900020.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a road and its bridges by Emperor Trajan Decius, likely in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-25900021.translation: To our lord Flavius Constantius, the most brave and noble Caesar, for the 30th time.
EDCS-25900021.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Flavius Constantius, indicating his high status and possibly a regnal year or significant achievement, likely from the Constantinian period.

EDCS-25900022.translation: The emperors Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, father of the country, proconsul, and Caesar Gaius Vibius Afinius Voldumianus Volusianus Pius Felix Augustus restored the road, which had collapsed with its bridges due to rains and age.
EDCS-25900022.summary: This inscription records the joint restoration of a road and its bridges by Emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Volusianus, dating to their shared reign in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-25900023.translation: To Marcus Aurelius, most pious and happy Augustus.
EDCS-25900023.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, likely from the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-25900025.translation: The emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, consul for the second time, father of the country, proconsul, and the emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Afinius Gallus Valdumianus Volusianus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, consul, father of the country, proconsul, restored the road, which had collapsed with its bridges due to rains and age, for the ninth time.
EDCS-25900025.summary: This inscription records the joint restoration of a road and its bridges by Emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Volusianus, possibly indicating a repeated restoration or a specific phase of work, dating to the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-25900027.translation: The emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-25900027.summary: This is a standard titulature inscription for Emperor Trebonianus Gallus, dating to his reign in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-25900028.translation: To the pious and happy Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, father of the country, proconsul; the state of the Phuensians, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-25900028.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the community of Phuensium to an unnamed emperor, likely during the period of the Tetrarchy or later, showing their devotion.

EDCS-25900029.translation: By the authority of the emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country, the road was fortified by the people of Milevitae, by his indulgence, from the wheel tax, twice.
EDCS-25900029.summary: This inscription records the fortification of a road by the people of Milevitae, authorized by Emperor Antoninus Pius and funded by a wheel tax, likely in the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-25900030.translation: By the authority of the emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country, the road was fortified by the people of Milevitae, by his indulgence, from the wheel tax, four times.
EDCS-25900030.summary: This inscription records the fortification of a road by the people of Milevitae, authorized by Emperor Antoninus Pius and funded by a wheel tax, likely in the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-25900031.translation: To the emperor, our lord Valerius Galerius Maximianus Pius Invictus Augustus.
EDCS-25900031.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Galerius Maximianus, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE during the Tetrarchy.

EDCS-25900032.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, grandson of the deified Gordians, milestone 1.
EDCS-25900032.summary: This inscription is a milestone dating to the reign of Emperor Gordian III.

EDCS-25900034.translation: To our Emperor and Lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus Maximus, Unconquered, Always Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Father of the Fatherland, with tribunician power, consul, Protector of the Republic.
EDCS-25900034.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Constantine the Great with numerous titles, likely on a public monument, and dates to his reign.

EDCS-25900036.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, Unconquered, Pious, Happy Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland, consul, proconsul, 11.
EDCS-25900036.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Caracalla with several titles and appears to be a milestone (indicated by 'XI'), dating to his reign.

EDCS-25900037.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 19th time, Imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 3rd time, Father of the Fatherland, proconsul, restored the exhausted roads of the Republic of the Cent Suburburitanus and enlarged them with new fortifications, 21.
EDCS-25900037.summary: This inscription records Emperor Caracalla's restoration and expansion of roads near Centum Umbria, dating to his reign (circa 211-217 AD).

EDCS-25900403.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fulvia Marcella lived X years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25900403.summary: This is a tombstone for Fulvia Marcella, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-25900405.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Minucius Cornelianus lived 12 years. Leontius erected this monument for himself while alive.
EDCS-25900405.summary: This is a tombstone for Minucius Cornelianus, erected by Leontius for himself during his lifetime, also commemorating the boy.

EDCS-25900406.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Lucius Voluntilius Fronton, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, a strong man, who lived 75 years, 2 months, 1 day. Voluntilius Pinetus and Voluntilius Tertullus made this for their deserving patron.
EDCS-25900406.summary: This tombstone commemorates Lucius Voluntilius Fronton, who lived 75 years, 2 months, and 1 day, and was erected by his freedmen.

EDCS-25900409.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Appellenia Paulina, also known as Minna, a slave from Sitifis, lived 45 years. Repostus to his sweetest wife.
EDCS-25900409.summary: This tombstone records the death of Appellenia Paulina (Minna), a slave from Sitifis, and was erected by her husband.

EDCS-25900410.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. C. Iulianus made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-25900410.summary: This is a tombstone erected by C. Iulianus for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-25900411.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Bultianus [lived] XXXX [years]. Fortu[nate?].
EDCS-25900411.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone for Caius Iulius Bultianus, likely recording his lifespan.

EDCS-25900413.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Egnatius Sabinus lived for 75 years; his heirs [dedicated this].
EDCS-25900413.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commemorating Quintus Egnatius Sabinus, who lived to 75 years old.

EDCS-25900414.translation: Ianuarius lived for 3 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25900414.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph recording the short life of Ianuarius, who died at the age of 3.

EDCS-25900417.translation: Lucius Aurelius C. Vetustus of the III Augusta Legion [dedicated this].
EDCS-25900417.summary: This inscription records Lucius Aurelius C. Vetustus, a member of the III Augusta Legion.

EDCS-25900418.translation: Marcus Cornelius Marcianus, priest, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-25900418.summary: This is a dedication recording Marcus Cornelius Marcianus, a priest, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-25900419.translation: In memory of the saints Paul, Peter, Donatus, Migginis, and Baricus.
EDCS-25900419.summary: This inscription is a memorial to several saints.

EDCS-25900420.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Ctesia [dedicated this].
EDCS-25900420.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Shades for Iulia Ctesia.

EDCS-25900421.translation: Here rests in the peace of the Lord Publia Festia of honored memory, who lived more or less 32 years. Felix makes this at Thamugadi.
EDCS-25900421.summary: This is a Christian funerary epitaph for Publia Festia, who died at approximately 32 years of age, dedicated at Thamugadi.

EDCS-25900423.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Augustus Caesar.

EDCS-25900424.translation: [This was made] for Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Septimius Severus.
EDCS-25900424.summary: This is a dedication to the Emperor Caesar (Caracalla), son of Septimius Severus.

EDCS-25900425.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Q. SfrimusaX [dedicated this].
EDCS-25900425.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Shades, possibly for an individual named SfrimusaX.

EDCS-25900426.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius V. Felix lived 32 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25900426.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Gaius V. Felix, who lived 32 years.

EDCS-25900427.translation: The priest fulfilled his vow to Castor Augustus.
EDCS-25900427.summary: A votive inscription dedicating a vow to the god Castor by a priest.

EDCS-25900428.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Iulius Lucius lived 71 years, full of the rites of Pluto.
EDCS-25900428.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Gaius Iulius Lucius, who lived 71 years and was 'full of the rites of Pluto'.

EDCS-25900429.translation: To SM Secunda lived 70 years. Saturninus lived 95 years. Their sons made this; may their bones rest well with you.
EDCS-25900429.summary: A funerary inscription set up by sons for their mother Secunda and father Saturninus, who lived to 70 and 95 respectively.

EDCS-25900431.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Septimius Severus Arabicus Adiabenicus Maximus, and of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Maximus.
EDCS-25900431.summary: A dedication to Emperor Caracalla, naming his father Septimius Severus and his paternal grandfather Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-25900432.translation: To our lord the Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, the Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul, proconsul, father of the fatherland, under whom we live most devotedly.
EDCS-25900432.summary: A dedication to Emperor Maximinus Thrax, listing his titles and acknowledging his rule.

EDCS-25900433.translation: To our lord Flavius Constantinus, the Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-25900433.summary: A dedication to Emperor Constantine I, bestowing upon him honorific titles.

EDCS-25900439.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Imius Af Euhodus. Sacred to the departed spirits. Antistia Iulia lived 105 years.
EDCS-25900439.summary: A double funerary inscription for Imius Af Euhodus and Antistia Iulia, who lived to 105.

EDCS-25900441.translation: Valerius Namphamo lived 13 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25900441.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Valerius Namphamo, who lived 13 years.

EDCS-25900442.translation: Valeria Maxima, daughter of Vegetus, lived 11 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25900442.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Valeria Maxima, daughter of Vegetus, who lived 11 years.

EDCS-25900443.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Valeria Rogata [dedicated this].
EDCS-25900443.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Shades by Valeria Rogata.

EDCS-25900445.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Vilia, daughter of Caius.

EDCS-25900447.translation: For the well-being of our emperor, the Invictus Augustus, Kalendio the slave made this.
EDCS-25900447.summary: This is a dedication made by a slave named Kalendio for the well-being of the emperor.

EDCS-25900448.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Manilia Vera lived 5 years, [some number of] months.
EDCS-25900448.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Manilia Vera, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-25900449.translation: Quintus, son of Quintus, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-25900449.summary: This records Quintus, son of Quintus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-25900450.translation: Iulius Icustus of Atrima lived 4 years.
EDCS-25900450.summary: This tombstone inscription records the lifespan of Iulius Icustus.

EDCS-25900467.translation: To Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, most noble Caesar, son of the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul.
EDCS-25900467.summary: This is a dedication to Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, son of the emperor, possibly from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-25900468.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, the state of the colony of Thamugadi [dedicates this] for the ninth time.
EDCS-25900468.summary: This is a dedication by the state of the colony of Thamugadi to Emperor Philip the Arab.

EDCS-25900469.translation: To Perpetuus Victoriosissimus indulgentissimus Emperor, restorer of the world, Lucius Domitius Aurelianus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power V, consul II, father of the fatherland, proconsul, the state of the colony of Thamugadi [dedicates this] for the ninth time.
EDCS-25900469.summary: This is a dedication by the state of the colony of Thamugadi to Emperor Aurelian.

EDCS-25900470.translation: To our lord Constantius, most noble Caesar, the state of the colony of Thamugadi [dedicates this].
EDCS-25900470.summary: This is a dedication by the state of the colony of Thamugadi to Caesar Constantius.

EDCS-25900471.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Carinus, most noble Caesar, prince of the youth, the state of the colony of Thamugadi [erected this].
EDCS-25900471.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the municipality of Thamugadi to the Caesar Carinus.

EDCS-25900472.translation: While our lord Maximianus Augustus reigns, the state of the colony of Thamugadi [erected this].
EDCS-25900472.summary: This inscription records a statement by the municipality of Thamugadi during the reign of Emperor Maximianus.

EDCS-25900474.translation: To our eternal lord Caesar Constantius Augustus, restorer of the world, the state of the colony of Thamugadi [erected this].
EDCS-25900474.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the municipality of Thamugadi to the Caesar Constantius.

EDCS-25900475.translation: To our lord Licinius Licinianus Augustus, the state of the colony of Thamugadi [erected this].
EDCS-25900475.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the municipality of Thamugadi to the Emperor Licinius.

EDCS-25900476.translation: To our eternal lord Licinius Augustus, the state of the colony of Thamugadi [erected this].
EDCS-25900476.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the municipality of Thamugadi to the Emperor Licinius.

EDCS-25900477.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, father of the fatherland, consul, son of the deified Magnus Antoninus.
EDCS-25900477.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Severus Alexander.

EDCS-25900478.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, son of the deified Magnus Antoninus.
EDCS-25900478.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Severus Alexander.

EDCS-25900479.translation: To the perpetual, most noble Caesar Maximian.
EDCS-25900479.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Maximian.

EDCS-25900480.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, most noble Caesar, the state of the colony of Lambaesis [erected this].
EDCS-25900480.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the municipality of Lambaesis to the Caesar Constantinus.

EDCS-25900481.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Severus Pius Felix Augustus, the state of the colony of Lambaesis [erected this].
EDCS-25900481.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the municipality of Lambaesis to the Emperor Severus.

EDCS-25900483.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, greatest Parthian, greatest Briton, greatest German, chief priest, with the tribunician power for the 18th time, Imperator for the 3rd time, Consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, and to Julia Augusta, mother of the Emperor and of the camp and of the Senate and of the fatherland, from the camp, 4 miles.
EDCS-25900483.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Caracalla and Julia Domna, likely indicating a military road marker at a distance of 4 miles from a camp.

EDCS-25900485.translation: To our Lords Emperors Gaius Valerius Diocletian and Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximian Augusti Nostri, Numerian, Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-25900485.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the co-emperors Diocletian and Maximian, and possibly to their Caesar Numerian, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century.

EDCS-25900486.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Parthian, greatest Briton, greatest Armenian, chief priest, with the tribunician power for the 18th time, Imperator for the 3rd time, Consul for the 4th time, proconsul, father of the fatherland, 12 miles from Lambaesis.
EDCS-25900486.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Caracalla, likely a milestone indicating a distance of 14 miles from Lambaesis.

EDCS-25900489.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Briton, greatest German, with the tribunician power for the 18th time, Imperator for the 3rd time, Consul for the 4th time, proconsul, father of the fatherland, 59.
EDCS-25900489.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Caracalla, likely a milestone indicating a distance of 59 units, possibly miles.

EDCS-25900490.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, Augusti, 59.
EDCS-25900490.summary: This inscription honors Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, likely a milestone indicating a distance of 59 units.

EDCS-25900491.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with the tribunician power, father of the fatherland, proconsul, 59 miles.
EDCS-25900491.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Philip the Arab, likely a milestone indicating a distance of 59 miles.

EDCS-25900493.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with the tribunician power, father of the fatherland, proconsul.
EDCS-25900493.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Gordian III, likely part of a dedication or honorary monument during his reign.

EDCS-25900494.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Valerius Diocletian Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest.
EDCS-25900494.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Diocletian, likely part of a dedication or honorary monument during his reign in the late 3rd century.

EDCS-25900497.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Aurelius Valerius Maximian.
