EDCS-25900497.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Maximian, likely part of a dedication or honorary monument during his reign in the late 3rd century.

EDCS-25900498.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Constantine the Great, likely part of a dedication or honorary monument during his reign in the early 4th century.

EDCS-25900499.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, father of the fatherland, with tribunician power, twice consul, proconsul, and to Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Veldumius Volusianus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, father of the fatherland, with tribunician power, consul, proconsul.
EDCS-25900499.summary: This is a dedication to Emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Volusianus, likely set up by a public body or official.

EDCS-25900502.translation: To Emperors Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus and Gaius Vibius Afinius Gallus Veldumnianus Volusianus Augusts, most invincible, for the third time.
EDCS-25900502.summary: This inscription honors Emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Volusianus, indicating a joint rule and possibly a military context given the epithets.

EDCS-25900503.translation: Under Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, conqueror of Parthia, conqueror of Britain, conqueror of Germany, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the 3rd time, emperor for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, and to Julia Augusta, mother of the emperor, mother of the camp, mother of the senate and fatherland.
EDCS-25900503.summary: This is a dedication for Emperor Caracalla (Severus Antoninus Pius Felix) and his mother Julia Augusta, likely dating to his 18th year of tribunician power.

EDCS-25900504.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, prince of the youth, Augustus.
EDCS-25900504.summary: This inscription dedicates honors to Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximinus Caesar, likely a public dedication.

EDCS-25900505.translation: For all time Emperor Constantius Augustus, one mile.
EDCS-25900505.summary: This milestone records a distance of one mile attributed to Emperor Constantius, likely indicating a road maintained or constructed during his reign.

EDCS-25900506.translation: For all time Emperor Maximian Augustus, the state of the colony of Lambaesis.
EDCS-25900506.summary: This dedication is from the colony of Lambaesis to Emperor Maximian, likely commemorating a public building or event.

EDCS-25900507.translation: For all time Emperor Maximian Augustus, the state of the colony of Lambaesis.
EDCS-25900507.summary: This dedication is from the colony of Lambaesis to Emperor Maximian, likely commemorating a public building or event.

EDCS-25900508.translation: For all time most noble Caesar Constantius, the state of the colony of Lambaesis.
EDCS-25900508.summary: This dedication is from the colony of Lambaesis to Caesar Constantius, likely commemorating a public building or event during his co-rule.

EDCS-25900509.translation: For all time most noble Caesar Maximian, one mile, the state of the colony of Lambaesis.
EDCS-25900509.summary: This inscription from the colony of Lambaesis marks one mile and honors Caesar Maximian, likely related to infrastructure or a public work.

EDCS-25900510.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix, conqueror of Parthia, conqueror of Britain, conqueror of Germany, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 18th time, emperor for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, and to Julia Augusta, mother of the emperor, mother of the camp, mother of the senate and fatherland.
EDCS-25900510.summary: This inscription dedicates honors for Emperor Caracalla (Severus Antoninus Pius Felix) and his mother Julia Augusta, likely dating to his 18th year of tribunician power.

EDCS-25900512.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, greatest conqueror of Parthia, greatest conqueror of Britain, greatest conqueror of Germany, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 18th time, emperor for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, proconsul, and to Julia Augusta, mother of the emperor, mother of the camp, mother of the senate and the country, 3 miles.
EDCS-25900512.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus (Caracalla) and Julia Domna, likely commemorating a road, dating to his 18th tribunician power year.

EDCS-25900516.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, grandson of the deified Septimius Severus Pius, greatest conqueror of Arabia, of Adiabene, greatest conqueror of Britain, son of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius, greatest conqueror of Parthia, greatest conqueror of Britain, of Germany, of Adiabene, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, 4 miles.
EDCS-25900516.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Elagabalus), likely commemorating a road, dating to his 3rd tribunician power year.

EDCS-25900517.translation: To our lords the Augusti Constantius Invictus and Caesar Constantius, 4 miles.
EDCS-25900517.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the emperors Constantius I and his Caesar, likely commemorating a road, during the reign of Constantius I.

EDCS-25900518.translation: To the perpetual Emperor Maximian Augustus, 4 miles.
EDCS-25900518.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Maximian Augustus, likely commemorating a road, during his reign.

EDCS-25900519.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Caesar Maximian, likely commemorating a road, during his co-rule.

EDCS-25900520.translation: To our lords Valentius, Caesar Constantius, 6.
EDCS-25900520.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the emperors Valens and Constantius Caesar, likely commemorating a road, during their co-rule.

EDCS-25900521.translation: To our lord the eternal Flavius Constantinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus.
EDCS-25900521.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Constantine I, likely commemorating a road, during his reign.

EDCS-25900522.translation: To our lord Iovianus, the triumphant, always Augustus.
EDCS-25900522.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Jovian, likely commemorating a road, during his reign.

EDCS-25900523.translation: To our lord Claudius Julianus, the victor and triumphant, always Augustus, 9.
EDCS-25900523.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Julian, likely commemorating a road, during his reign.

EDCS-25900527.translation: To our lord Valentinianus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-25900527.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Valentinian I, likely commemorating a road, during his reign.

EDCS-25900528.translation: To the unconquered, pious, fortunate Augustus, chief priest, father of the country, consul for the 17th time.
EDCS-25900528.summary: This inscription is a dedication to an emperor, likely honored as chief priest and consul.

EDCS-25900529.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Aurelius Maximianus, the unconquered, pious, fortunate Augustus, from the community of Dianium.
EDCS-25900529.summary: This inscription is a dedication from the community of Dianium to Emperor Maximian, honoring him as Augustus.

EDCS-25900531.translation: To our lord the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Numerius Numerianus Augustus, for the 29th time.
EDCS-25900531.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Numerian, likely commemorating his reign or consulship.

EDCS-25900532.translation: To our lord the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Numerius Numerianus, most noble Caesar, designated consul.
EDCS-25900532.summary: This inscription honors Caesar Numerian, designating him as consul.

EDCS-25900533.translation: To our lord the Emperor Gaius Valerius Diocletian Augustus, for the 31st time.
EDCS-25900533.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Diocletian, likely commemorating his reign or consulship.

EDCS-25900534.translation: To Aurelius Valerius Maximianus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-25900534.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Maximian.

EDCS-25900536.translation: To the Emperor Marcus Valerius Constantius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-25900536.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Constantius.

EDCS-25900538.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus, for the 43rd time.
EDCS-25900538.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Maximian, likely commemorating his reign or consulship.

EDCS-25900539.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus, unconquered, pious, fortunate Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul, from the community.
EDCS-25900539.summary: This inscription is a dedication from a community to Emperor Gordian, honoring him with various titles including chief priest and consul.

EDCS-25900540.translation: To our lord, the most noble Caesar Galerius Valerius Constantius, for the 8th time.
EDCS-25900540.summary: This inscription honors Caesar Constantius, likely commemorating his reign or consulship.

EDCS-25900541.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, consul-designate for the second time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, and to the Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Afinius Gallus.
EDCS-25900541.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Afinius Gallus, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25900547.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the second time, consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul, concerning the road damaged by rains.
EDCS-25900547.summary: This inscription records a road repair initiated by Emperor Gordian III, likely in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-26000946.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, holder of tribunician power for the second time, consul for the third time, proconsul, father of the fatherland. (Value) 5.
EDCS-26000946.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26000965.summary: This is a dedication to Constantine the Great when he held the title of Caesar, likely in the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-26000967.translation: To our eternal lord Maximian, most noble Caesar. Our Lords (plural). (Value) 5.
EDCS-26000967.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Maximian when he held the title of Caesar, likely in the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-26000968.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Constantine the Younger, most noble Caesar. (Value) 20.
EDCS-26000968.summary: This is a dedication to Constantine the Younger, likely in the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-26000969.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius, Divine Augustus.
EDCS-26000969.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Claudius Gothicus, who was deified after his death, likely in the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26000970.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus Pius Felix Augustus restored the milestones of his world.
EDCS-26000970.summary: This inscription records Emperor Aurelian's restoration of milestones, likely in the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26000971.translation: To our Lords Valerius Maximian, Divine Augustus.
EDCS-26000971.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Maximian, possibly after his deification or by someone honoring him as divine, likely in the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-26000972.translation: To our Lords Flavius Valerius Constantius, Divine Augustus.
EDCS-26000972.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Constantius Chlorus, possibly after his deification or by someone honoring him as divine, likely in the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-26000975.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, conqueror of Parthia, conqueror of Britain, conqueror of Germany, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 19th time, emperor for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, proconsul, father of the country. August NA DOL
EDCS-26000975.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Augustus), likely an honorific inscription dating to his later reign given his titles.

EDCS-26000977.translation: To our lord Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, the Invincible, Pious, Happy Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, consul, father of the country, proconsul, from the public assembly of the town of Dianium.
EDCS-26000977.summary: This is an inscription by the public assembly of Dianium dedicated to Emperor Caracalla, likely an honorific text.

EDCS-26000978.translation: To our lord Maxentius Augustus, most noble Caesar, consul, chief priest, promoter of the state, consul for the second time. AI RICIAI P K F
EDCS-26000978.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Maxentius, likely erected by the AI RICIAI, recording his consulship and his role in promoting the state.

EDCS-26000981.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius, the Invincible, Pious, Happy Augustus, from the public assembly of Augusta Diana.
EDCS-26000981.summary: This is an honorific inscription from the public assembly of Augusta Diana dedicated to Emperor Claudius Gothicus.

EDCS-26000984.translation: Emperor Caesar Antoninus Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus restored milestone I.
EDCS-26000984.summary: This inscription records Emperor Gordian III restoring a milestone, likely a practical infrastructure inscription.

EDCS-26000988.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Maximian Pius Felix Augustus, the senior, for the 4th time.
EDCS-26000988.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Maximian, indicating his status as senior Augustus and possibly marking a tenure of office or celebration.

EDCS-26000990.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, father of the country, conqueror of Parthia, conqueror of Britain, conqueror of Germany, chief priest, restored milestones worn out by age.
EDCS-26000990.summary: This inscription records Emperor Caracalla restoring ancient milestones, likely a public works inscription.

EDCS-26000991.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, father of the country, consul, son of the divine Antoninus Magnus, renewed milestone V.
EDCS-26000991.summary: This inscription records Emperor Severus Alexander renewing a milestone, identifying him as the son of Emperor Antoninus Pius, and dating it to his consulship and tribunician power.

EDCS-26000992.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, father of the country, consul, son of the divine Antoninus Magnus, renewed milestones for travelers.
EDCS-26000992.summary: This inscription records Emperor Severus Alexander renewing milestones, identifying him as the son of Emperor Antoninus Pius and likely related to public infrastructure.

EDCS-26000993.translation: To our lord Aurelius Valerius Maximian, the Invincible, Pious, Happy Augustus, most noble Caesar, 7 miles.
EDCS-26000993.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Maximian and likely indicates a distance of 7 miles, possibly related to a road or a military context.

EDCS-26000994.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest conqueror of Parthia, greatest conqueror of Britain, greatest conqueror of Germany, chief priest, restored the milestones worn out by age, nine.
EDCS-26000994.summary: This inscription records the restoration of nine milestones by Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, likely during his reign (211-217 AD).

EDCS-26000995.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest conqueror of Parthia, greatest conqueror of Britain, greatest conqueror of Germany, chief priest, restored the milestones dilapidated by age.
EDCS-26000995.summary: This inscription records the restoration of dilapidated milestones by Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, likely during his reign (211-217 AD).

EDCS-26000997.translation: Son of the divine Antoninus, renewed the milestones for travellers.
EDCS-26000997.summary: This inscription records the renewal of milestones for travelers by the son of the deified Antoninus (likely Caracalla), probably in the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26000998.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, father of the country, consul, son of the divine Antoninus, renewed the milestones for travellers; the state of Lamasba devoted.
EDCS-26000998.summary: This inscription records the renewal of milestones for travelers by Emperor Alexander Severus in Lamasba, attributing the action to the devotion of the state, likely during his reign (222-235 AD).

EDCS-26000999.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aemilius Aemilianus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-26000999.summary: This is a titulature inscription for Emperor Aemilianus, likely commemorating his accession or a significant event during his brief reign in 253 AD.

EDCS-26001000.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest conqueror of Parthia, greatest conqueror of Britain, greatest conqueror of Germany, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 17th time, emperor for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, proconsul; the state of Lamasba Antoniniana, nine miles.
EDCS-26001000.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Caracalla with his full titles and records nine miles of the Lamasba Antoniniana, likely erected during his reign (198-217 AD).

EDCS-26001001.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest conqueror of Parthia, greatest conqueror of Britain, greatest conqueror of Germany, restored the milestones dilapidated by age, eleven.
EDCS-26001001.summary: This inscription records the restoration of eleven milestones by Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, likely during his reign (211-217 AD).

EDCS-26001002.translation: And with Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Afinius Gallus Volusenus Volusianus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus as consul, eleven miles.
EDCS-26001002.summary: This inscription mentions eleven miles being recorded during the consulship of Emperor Gallus, likely related to road maintenance or distance markers, circa 251-253 AD.

EDCS-26001003.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Diocletianus Augustus, eleven miles.
EDCS-26001003.summary: This inscription records eleven miles under Emperor Diocletian, likely relating to milestones or distances, possibly in the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-26001004.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-26001004.summary: This is a titulature inscription for Emperor Maximian, likely commemorating his accession or a significant event during his co-reign with Diocletian (286-305 AD).

EDCS-26001005.translation: To our lord Augustus, Galerius Valerius Maximianus Caesar.
EDCS-26001005.summary: This is a dedication to the emperor Galerius Valerius Maximianus, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century.

EDCS-26001006.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius, the unconquered, pious, and fortunate Augustus.
EDCS-26001006.summary: This inscription honors the emperor Constantius I, also known as Constantius Chlorus, dated to his reign as Augustus (306-306 AD).

EDCS-26001007.translation: To our lord Constantine Caesar Augustus.
EDCS-26001007.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Constantine I, likely from the early 4th century.

EDCS-26001008.translation: To our lords Flavius Constantius Augustus and Constantius Caesar.
EDCS-26001008.summary: This inscription honors two rulers named Constantius, likely Constantius I and his son Constantine I, from the early 4th century.

EDCS-26001009.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Julianus Augustus.
EDCS-26001009.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Julian, also known as Julian the Apostate, likely from the mid-4th century.

EDCS-26100125.translation: To Flavius Valerius Maximianus, ever Augustus.
EDCS-26100125.summary: This inscription honors the emperor Maximian (Galerius Valerius Maximianus), likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century.

EDCS-26100126.translation: To Flavius Valerius Constantinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-26100126.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Constantine I when he held the title of Caesar, likely from the early 4th century.

EDCS-26100127.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, pious, fortunate, the younger Augustus.
EDCS-26100127.summary: This inscription honors Constantine I as Augustus, likely from the early 4th century.

EDCS-26100128.translation: To our lords Valentinian and Valens, ever Augustuses.
EDCS-26100128.summary: This is a dedication to the emperors Valentinian I and Valens, who ruled jointly in the mid-4th century.

EDCS-26100129.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, supreme conqueror of the Britons, supreme conqueror of the Germans, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul, proconsul, father of the country.
EDCS-26100129.summary: This is a detailed honorific inscription for Emperor Caracalla, dated to around 213 AD based on his titles.

EDCS-26100130.translation: To the Emperor, Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, the Invincible, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, son of the divine Antoninus the Great, grandson of the divine [Antoninus Pius], the colony of Cast [commissioned this to be made].
EDCS-26100130.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by the colony of Cast to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely in the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26100132.translation: To our lord and most powerful Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, the Invincible, Pious, Fortunate, our Augustus, the colony of Cast [commissioned this].
EDCS-26100132.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the colony of Cast to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander (also known as Severus Alexander), likely in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26100134.translation: To our Emperors and Lords Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus and Gaius Vibius Afinius Gallus Vopiscus (or Volusianus), the most invincible princes, chief pontiffs, Augusti, consuls for the second time, fathers of the fatherland.
EDCS-26100134.summary: This inscription is a formal dedication to the co-emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Volusianus, likely dating to their joint consulship in 252 CE.

EDCS-26100135.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantius, the Invincible, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-26100135.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Constantius I (Constantius Chlorus), likely in the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-26100136.translation: To our lord Gratian Augustus, consul for the second time.
EDCS-26100136.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Gratian, likely dating to his second consulship in 367 CE.

EDCS-26100137.translation: To our lords Valentinian and Valens and Gratian, always Augusti.
EDCS-26100137.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the co-emperors Valentinian I, Valens, and Gratian, likely dating to the period 367-375 CE.

EDCS-26100315.translation: To the divine shades. Grania Saturnina lived 4 years. She is placed here.
EDCS-26100315.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Grania Saturnina, who died at a very young age, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100317.summary: This inscription is a dedication or possibly a tombstone fragment mentioning Augustus and Lucius Iulius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100319.translation: Quintus Afronius Felix lived 55 years. He is placed here.
EDCS-26100319.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Afronius Felix, who died at the age of 55, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100320.translation: To the divine shades. Atilia Saturnina.
EDCS-26100320.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Atilia Saturnina, dedicated to the divine shades, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100321.translation: Hordonia Vera lived 40 years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-26100321.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan of Hordonia Vera and includes a wish for her peaceful repose.

EDCS-26100322.translation: To the departed spirits. Pactumeia Donata lived 81 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26100322.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan of Pactumeia Donata and her burial place.

EDCS-26100323.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Papinius Focarius lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26100323.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan of Lucius Papinius Focarius and his burial place.

EDCS-26100324.translation: [---]nesis lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26100324.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan and burial place of an individual whose name is partially lost.

EDCS-26100325.translation: Lived 20 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-26100325.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan and burial place of an individual, likely male, and includes a wish for peaceful repose.

EDCS-26100326.translation: Lived 20 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-26100326.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan and burial place of an individual, likely male, and includes a wish for peaceful repose.

EDCS-26100327.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan and burial place of an individual, likely male.

EDCS-26100347.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Herannia Titi filia Quirina Mustia lived 80 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26100347.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan and burial place of Herannia Titi filia Quirina Mustia.

EDCS-26100356.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Bebia Ispes lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26100356.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan and burial place of Bebia Ispes.

EDCS-26100359.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Iulius Secundus lived 50 years.
EDCS-26100359.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan of Publius Iulius Secundus and is dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-26100360.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Spanius Modestus lived 92 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-26100360.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Spanius Modestus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-26100361.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Spania Quadratilla lived [XX] years.
EDCS-26100361.summary: A funerary inscription for Spania Quadratilla, recording her age at death.

EDCS-26100362.translation: To Saturn. The property of Gnaeus Calpurnius.
EDCS-26100362.summary: An inscription indicating ownership by Gnaeus Calpurnius, likely on a boundary stone or dedication to the god Saturn.

EDCS-26100364.translation: Fabia Fausta, daughter of Caius, lived 82 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-26100364.summary: A funerary inscription for Fabia Fausta, recording her age and burial.

EDCS-26100368.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Lurius Felix lived 90 years.
EDCS-26100368.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Lurius Felix, recording his age at death.

EDCS-26100369.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Luria Salvia lived 22 years.
EDCS-26100369.summary: A funerary inscription for Luria Salvia, recording her age at death.

EDCS-26200011.translation: Son of Publius, Son of Publius.
EDCS-26200011.summary: A very brief inscription, possibly indicating a lineage or relationship, likely on a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-26200036.summary: An inscription with a personal name, likely a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-26200039.summary: An inscription recording a patronymic, likely on a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-26200040.summary: An inscription with a man's name and origin, likely a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-26200049.translation: Celer, son of Rasinius, to Faustus.
EDCS-26200049.summary: This is a dedication by Celer to Faustus, likely a personal or familial dedication.

EDCS-26200050.summary: This is a dedication to Clodius Proculus, likely from a tomb or a monument.

EDCS-26200051.summary: This is a dedication to Clodius Proculus, likely from a tomb or a monument.

EDCS-26200055.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Fastidienus, likely from a tomb or a monument.

EDCS-26200717.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Antonius Amehsius lived XX years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Saenia Iulia lived XX years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Anius Victor lived LV years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Victorina lived XX years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Ant Donatiano lived XX years.
EDCS-26200717.summary: This funerary inscription records the lives of several individuals: Antonius Amehsius, Saenia Iulia, Anius Victor, Iulia Victorina, and Ant Donatiano, noting their ages at death.

EDCS-26200725.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Baebius Salus lived LXXXV years. Victor lived LXXV years.
EDCS-26200725.summary: This funerary inscription records the lives of Baebius Salus and Victor, noting their ages at death.

EDCS-26200726.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Ianuar.
EDCS-26200726.summary: This is a funerary dedication to someone named Ianuar.

EDCS-26200727.translation: In memory of Sempronius Sperantius. Pelagius and Donatus, from the province, [lived] 125 years.
EDCS-26200727.summary: This inscription commemorates Sempronius Sperantius and records the lives of Pelagius and Donatus, who lived 125 years and were from the province.

EDCS-26200787.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maria Flora, wife of Aridus, lived C years.
EDCS-26200787.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Maria Flora, wife of Aridus, noting her age at death as 100 years.

EDCS-26801063.translation: In memory of our lords Peter and Paul
EDCS-26801063.summary: This inscription is a memorial dedicated to Saints Peter and Paul.

EDCS-26801065.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus and to Tranquillina Sabina Augusta his wife
EDCS-26801065.summary: This is a dedication to the Roman Emperor Gordian III and his wife Tranquillina, likely from the period of their reign (238-244 AD).

EDCS-26801066.translation: Publius Domitius Donatus lived for 72 years and Iulia Donata his wife lived for
EDCS-26801066.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the ages of Publius Domitius Donatus and his wife Iulia Donata.

EDCS-26801069.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Maxim lived 95 years, and Thiehor Satunie his wife, to her husband, from the hand...
EDCS-26801069.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespans of Maxim and his wife, likely from the 3rd or 4th century AD.

EDCS-26801082.translation: Yield, old age, yield to novelty; it is pleasing to dedicate royal vows. This seat of Peter and Paul rises again by God's command. I ask that you become one, accept one gift; let one honor celebrate those whom one faith holds. However, a priest is needed here, and the care of Probantius. The church from the workshop of Donatus and Sic TIST IVISV.
EDCS-26801082.summary: This is a dedication inscription likely for a church, mentioning the workshop of Donatus and referencing Peter and Paul, with an inferred date of the early Christian period.

EDCS-26801084.translation: From the workshop of Donatus and Sic IVISV.
EDCS-26801084.summary: This is a short inscription indicating the workshop of Donatus and Sic IVISV, possibly related to the preceding inscription.

EDCS-26801088.translation: To the god Mars and the Holy Genius, the college of beneficiarii. Paconius Castus, a beneficiarius of the consular legion III Augusta, with those under him, having completed his duty, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-26801088.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mars and the Genius of the college of beneficiarii by Paconius Castus, a soldier of the legion III Augusta, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801089.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Mars Victor, the helping gods, and the Genius of the Vazanitana station. Saturninus, a beneficiarius of the legion III Augusta, having completed his duty at the station and promoted to the centurionate of the legion II Italica, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-26801089.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter, Mars, various gods, and the Genius of the Vazanitana station by Saturninus, a soldier promoted from legion III Augusta to legion II Italica, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801090.translation: To Mars Augustus, and all the gods and goddesses, Gaius Julius Gaetulicus.
EDCS-26801090.summary: This is a dedication by Gaius Julius Gaetulicus to Mars Augustus and all the gods and goddesses, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801091.translation: The legatus Augusti pro praetore, consul-designate, restored the cohort VII Lusitanorum.
EDCS-26801091.summary: This inscription records the restoration of the cohort VII Lusitanorum by a legatus Augusti pro praetore and consul-designate, indicating a military or public works project during the Roman Empire.

EDCS-26801092.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Felix the steward lived [for] years.
EDCS-26801092.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Felix, identified as an 'actor' (steward or manager), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801093.summary: This is a dedication to the deified Emperor Constantius, likely from the Constantinian or post-Constantinian period.

EDCS-26801101.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Ebutius Intacomius lived 50 years.
EDCS-26801101.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ebutius Intacomius, recording his age at death as 50, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801102.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Pronius lived 80 years.
EDCS-26801102.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Pronius, recording his age at death as 80, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801103.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sextus Maior lived 95 years.
EDCS-26801103.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Maior, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-26801104.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Pompeius Donatus lived 110 years.
EDCS-26801104.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Pompeius Donatus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-26801105.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Staberius Urbanus, for himself while alive and for his wife Anna.
EDCS-26801105.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Staberius Urbanus setting up a monument for himself and his wife, Anna.

EDCS-26801106.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Valeria lived 70 years. Her husband Iulius Felix made this.
EDCS-26801106.summary: This funerary epitaph records Iulia Valeria's lifespan and was erected by her husband, Iulius Felix.

EDCS-26801107.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. [His/Her] daughter lived 35 years. Victor ZMIZ.
EDCS-26801107.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed daughter who lived 35 years.

EDCS-26801108.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius Mustolus, an excellent man, lived 45 years. His wife made this.
EDCS-26801108.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Iulius Mustolus and was erected by his wife.

EDCS-26801109.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Lucius Vitellius Augurinus, innocent, who lived 19 years. Rcia Luciosa, mother, for her son.
EDCS-26801109.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by Rcia Luciosa, a mother, to her son Lucius Vitellius Augurinus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-26801110.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Vitellius Donatus lived 80 years. Flavius Ianuarius placed this.
EDCS-26801110.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Lucius Vitellius Donatus and was erected by Flavius Ianuarius.

EDCS-26801111.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Vitellius Romanus lived 30 years and 5 days. Vitellius, for his son.
EDCS-26801111.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Lucius Vitellius Romanus and was erected by his father, Vitellius.

EDCS-26801116.summary: This is a dedication to the Campestral Gods, likely from a military context.

EDCS-26801118.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Victoria lived 30 years.
EDCS-26801118.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Victoria who lived 30 years.

EDCS-26801119.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Saturnina who lived 70 years.

EDCS-26801120.translation: Sacred to Mercury. Advenus [dedicated this] from his own [property] to Venerandus.
EDCS-26801120.summary: This is a dedication of an object or place to the god Mercury by Advenus, likely for or concerning Venerandus.

EDCS-26801121.translation: Caecilius Vincentius and Valeria Matrona made and dedicated this altar to the Good August Goddess from their own funds.
EDCS-26801121.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to the Bona Dea Augusta by Caecilius Vincentius and Valeria Matrona.

EDCS-26801125.translation: Aviana Ianuaria lived 38 years for her most rare husband, Bacchus.
EDCS-26801125.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aviana Ianuaria, who lived 38 years and was the wife of Bacchus.

EDCS-26801126.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Egnatius Pulcher.
EDCS-26801126.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely indicating a tomb or dedication by Lucius Egnatius Pulcher.

EDCS-26801127.translation: To Panicia Victoria. [She] lived 85 years and grew old.
EDCS-26801127.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Panicia Victoria, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-26801128.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Prosia Ianuaria lived 85 years. Lucius Seppius Nepos lived 30 years.
EDCS-26801128.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the ages of two individuals, Prosia Ianuaria (85) and Lucius Seppius Nepos (30).

EDCS-26801129.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Septimia Beata lived 55 years. Caius Valerius Saturus made [this] for his most pious wife.
EDCS-26801129.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Septimia Beata, who lived 55 years, dedicated by her husband Caius Valerius Saturus.

EDCS-26801130.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Volusia Victorina lived 65 years. Urbanus [made this] for his wife.
EDCS-26801130.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Volusia Victorina, who lived 65 years, dedicated by her husband Urbanus.

EDCS-26801131.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Co Primus lived 75 years, died a natural death, having earned this through his own merits.
EDCS-26801131.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Co Primus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period, noting his age and peaceful death.

EDCS-26801132.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius D.M. lived 69 years.
EDCS-26801132.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius D.M., noting his age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801133.summary: This is a simple epitaph recording the lifespan of Mantesia Saturnina, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801134.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcella lived 25 years. Her father made the tomb for his dearest daughter; she rests here.
EDCS-26801134.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Marcella at age 25, with her father as the dedicator, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801135.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Her father made this for Marcellina.
EDCS-26801135.summary: A brief funerary inscription where a father dedicated a tomb for his daughter Marcellina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801136.translation: Monument of Sedi Victoris. Lived 50 years. Expenses: Rogatus XX.
EDCS-26801136.summary: This inscription records the death of Sedi Victoris at age 50 and mentions Rogatus XX concerning expenses, likely a funerary monument from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801137.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Sosia Pulchra lived 2 years. May she rest well.
EDCS-26801137.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sosia Pulchra, who died at the age of 2, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801138.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caia lived 60 years. She rests here.
EDCS-26801138.summary: This funerary inscription notes the death of Caia at age 60, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801139.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. To Caelia Mangeddeni, my dearest wife. Lived 75 years. Gaius Memmius Baliahon for his wife who deserved well of him. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Memmius Baliahon lived 85 years.
EDCS-26801139.summary: A complex funerary epitaph for Caelia Mangeddeni and her husband Gaius Memmius Baliahon, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording their ages at death and their marital relationship.

EDCS-26801141.translation: In the name of God Almighty and Christ our saviour, with the father safe. Felice Gemelianus and Donatiana made this.
EDCS-26801141.summary: This inscription, likely from the early Christian period, is a dedication made by Felice Gemelianus and Donatiana, invoking God and Christ.

EDCS-26801143.translation: To my wife Elicatis, who lived 60 years and [some] months.
EDCS-26801143.summary: A funerary dedication to a wife named Elicatis.

EDCS-26801146.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead, Mina [---].
EDCS-26801146.summary: A funerary dedication to the Manes, mentioning Mina.

EDCS-26801147.summary: A short inscription identifying a person named Geilimer as 'Lord'.

EDCS-26801148.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Pia [---] lived 65 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Fabia Rogate lived 55 years [---].
EDCS-26801148.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lives of two women, Pia and Fabia Rogate.

EDCS-26801149.translation: To Julia Victoria, mother of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus and of the whole Severan household, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-26801149.summary: A public dedication honoring Julia Victoria, mother of Emperor Septimius Severus, funded by the decurions.

EDCS-26900157.translation: Vicciana, from the workshops of Crispina.
EDCS-26900157.summary: An inscription indicating ownership or origin from the workshops of Crispina, possibly related to pottery or brickmaking.

EDCS-27100078.translation: Gaius Matius Optatus, son of Gaius, of the tribus Quirina, lived 57 years; he lies here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Iulius Dexter, son of Gaius, of the tribus Quirina, lived 12 years; he lies here.
EDCS-27100078.summary: A double funerary inscription recording the lives and burials of Gaius Matius Optatus and Gaius Iulius Dexter.

EDCS-27100591.summary: An inscription indicating the origin from the workshop of Kapiton.

EDCS-27100611.summary: An ownership or attribution inscription for Gaius Clodius Successus.

EDCS-27100612.summary: An ownership or attribution inscription for Gaius Clodius Successus.

EDCS-27100613.summary: This is a funerary dedication mentioning Caius Clodius Successus.

EDCS-27100616.summary: This is a funerary dedication mentioning Caius Cornelius Ursus.

EDCS-27100765.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aelius Felicianus lived for 45 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-27100765.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Aelius Felicianus, who lived for 45 years.

EDCS-27100767.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aemilia Cide lived for...
EDCS-27100767.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Cide, but the duration of her life is incomplete.

EDCS-27100768.translation: Sutronia Urbana, daughter of Lucius, lived for 11 years.
EDCS-27100768.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sutronia Urbana, who died at the age of 11.

EDCS-27100769.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Tadius Lucinus lived for 26 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-27100769.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Tadius Lucinus, who died at the age of 26.

EDCS-27300293.translation: Caius Manilius Victorinus, a duplicarius of the Flavian cavalry wing, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27300293.summary: This is a dedication by Caius Manilius Victorinus, a soldier in the Flavian cavalry, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-27300297.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Anestius lived for 66 years.
EDCS-27300297.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Anestius, who lived for 66 years.

EDCS-27300298.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caninius Ianvarius, who lived for 75 years.

EDCS-27600217.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, in his 19th year of tribunician power, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, restored the 180 [units/measures].
EDCS-27600217.summary: This is a dedication by Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, likely from around 178-179 CE, indicating the restoration of 180 units.

EDCS-27600249.translation: To our lord Emperor Flavius Claudius, the seventh.
EDCS-27600249.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Flavius Claudius, likely erected during his reign in the 4th century CE.

EDCS-27600252.translation: To the Perpetual Emperor Lucius Domitius Aurelianus, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus, from the people of KalamRenses.
EDCS-27600252.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Aurelian, a military emperor of the 3rd century CE, likely set up by the community of KalamRenses.

EDCS-27600255.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Severus, Pious, Fortunate, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-27600255.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Flavius Valerius Severus, likely erected in the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-27600256.translation: To our lord Gaius Valerius Diocletian, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus.
EDCS-27600256.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Diocletian, likely erected during his reign in the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-27600257.translation: Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, Emperor Caesar, grandson of the Divine Septimius Severus Pius, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, son of the Divine Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, Adiabenicus Maximus, [did this for] the state of the colony of A...
EDCS-27600257.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla) for the state of a colony, highlighting his lineage and titles, dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27600258.translation: To Gaius Valerius Diocletian, Unconquered Augustus.
EDCS-27600258.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Diocletian, likely erected during his reign in the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-27600259.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Constantinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-27600259.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Flavius Claudius Constantinus, likely erected in the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-27600260.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Diocletian, likely erected during his reign in the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-27600262.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus.
EDCS-27600262.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Philip the Arab, likely erected during his reign in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-27600263.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, most pious and most noble Caesar.
EDCS-27600263.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus, likely erected in the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-27600264.translation: To Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus, the Invincible, Pious, Fortunate, Father of the Fatherland, and to Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus, the Invincible, Pious, Fortunate, Father of the Fatherland. 37 [years/miles?].
EDCS-27600264.summary: This inscription records imperial titles, likely for Emperor Valerian and Gallienus, possibly related to a military or administrative context given the numeral XXXVII.

EDCS-27600265.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, grandson of the deified Severus, son of the deified Antoninus.
EDCS-27600265.summary: This inscription is a formal titulature for Emperor Caracalla, dating from his reign.

EDCS-27600266.translation: To Emperor Caesar Augustus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus.
EDCS-27600266.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument or honours Emperor Maximian, dating from his reign.

EDCS-27600267.translation: To Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius, the Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus.
EDCS-27600267.summary: This inscription is a dedicatory or honorific text for Emperor Constantius I Chlorus, dating from his reign.

EDCS-27600269.summary: This is a simple honorific inscription for Emperor Constantine I, dating from his reign.

EDCS-27600270.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantine, the Pious, Fortunate, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-27600270.summary: This inscription honours Constantine I when he held the title of Caesar, likely before he became Augustus.

EDCS-27600271.translation: Emperor Caesar, grandson of the deified Septimius Severus Pius, the great conqueror of Adiabene, Arabia, and Britain, the deified M...
EDCS-27600271.summary: This inscription is a partial titulature for Emperor Caracalla, listing his extensive honours and victories.

EDCS-27600272.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Se[verus Alexander], grandson of the deified Septimius Severus, son of Antoninus, greatest, with tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, twice.
EDCS-27600272.summary: This inscription provides the formal titulature of Emperor Severus Alexander, including his powers and honours.

EDCS-27600273.translation: To our lord Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-27600273.summary: This inscription honours Maximinus I when he held the title of Caesar, prior to his elevation to Augustus.

EDCS-27600276.translation: To our perpetual Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus, the Pious, Invincible, Fortunate Augustus. 54 [years/miles?].
EDCS-27600276.summary: This inscription honours Emperor Aurelian and may refer to his age or a military distance, dating from his reign.

EDCS-27600280.translation: To Claudius Constantinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-27600280.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Claudius Constantinus, addressed as "most noble Caesar", likely dating to his time as Caesar.

EDCS-27600284.translation: To Diocletian, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus.
EDCS-27600284.summary: This is an acclamation of Diocletian as "Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus", common during his reign.

EDCS-27600285.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Constantinus, most noble Augustus.
EDCS-27600285.summary: This inscription honors Flavius Claudius Constantinus as "our lord and most noble Augustus", likely from the period of his rule as Augustus.

EDCS-27600286.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Septimius Severus, the Pious, the Adiabenic, the Arabic, the greatest Britannic.
EDCS-27600286.summary: This is an imperial acclamation for an emperor, son of Septimius Severus, listing his numerous honorific titles and victories, including those in Britain, Adiabene, and Arabia.

EDCS-27600288.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus, greatest, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus.
EDCS-27600288.summary: This inscription proclaims Flavius Valerius Constantinus as "greatest, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus", likely from the period of his reign as sole emperor.

EDCS-27600290.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus, Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, and to Marcus Aurelius Carinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-27600290.summary: This inscription honors both Emperor Carus and his son Carinus, designating Carus with imperial titles and Carinus as "most noble Caesar", dating to their joint rule.

EDCS-27600291.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius.
EDCS-27600291.summary: This is a brief acclamation for Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius, likely referring to Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus.

EDCS-27600292.translation: To Caesar Augustus, Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar Augustus; the republic of the colony of R C M.
EDCS-27600292.summary: This inscription is dedicated by the republic of the colony of R C M to Caesar Augustus, Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, who held the title "most noble Caesar Augustus".

EDCS-27600293.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, Publius Licinius Gallienus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, proconsul, father of the country; the republic of the colony of R C M.
EDCS-27600293.summary: This inscription is dedicated by the republic of the colony of R C M to Emperor Gallienus, listing his various official titles, including pontifex maximus and pater patriae.

EDCS-27600295.translation: To our Lords Emperors Gaius Valerius Diocletian and Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximian, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate Augusti; the republic of the municipality of Thibilis.
EDCS-27600295.summary: This inscription is dedicated by the republic of the municipality of Thibilis to the co-emperors Diocletian and Maximian, honoring them as "Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate Augusti", dating to their joint reign.

EDCS-27600296.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, invested with the tribunician power.
EDCS-27600296.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Gordian III, likely part of a larger monument, mentioning his titles and status in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-27600298.translation: To our lord Maximinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-27600298.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a dedication to the Caesar Maximinus, likely dating to his period as junior emperor in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27600300.translation: To our lord Flavius Valentinianus, Augustus, holder of the consulship for the 17th time.
EDCS-27600300.summary: This inscription records honors or titles for Emperor Valentinian II, indicating his long reign and status in the late 4th century CE.

EDCS-27600305.translation: To our lords Valentinianus, Valens, and Gratianus, most invincible princes, with reverence to their divine powers.
EDCS-27600305.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperors Valentinian I, Valens, and Gratian, emphasizing their joint rule and divine authority in the mid-to-late 4th century CE.

EDCS-27600306.translation: To our lord, the Emperor, our Augustus, the victor and triumphant, always Augustus, the most courageous.
EDCS-27600306.summary: This inscription is a dedication to an unnamed emperor, praising his military victories and perpetual Augustan status, likely from the later Roman Empire.

EDCS-27600307.summary: This is a fragmentary honorific inscription for an emperor, possibly part of a longer text, celebrating his perpetual Augustan title.

EDCS-27600311.translation: To Emperor our lord Licinius Augustus, the state of the colony of Thamugadi.
EDCS-27600311.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the city of Thamugadi to Emperor Licinius, likely from the early 4th century CE when Licinius ruled the Eastern Roman Empire.

EDCS-27600313.translation: Chief priest, invested with the tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, the state of the colony of Thamugadi.
EDCS-27600313.summary: This inscription lists the imperial titles of a ruler, possibly the emperor whose name is missing, honored by the city of Thamugadi, likely dating to the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27600314.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius, the invincible, pious, and fortunate Augustus, chief priest, invested with the tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, the state of the colony of Thamugadi.
EDCS-27600314.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the city of Thamugadi to Emperor Claudius Gothicus, highlighting his imperial titles and divine favor, dating to his reign in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-27600315.translation: To Aurelius, the invincible, pious, and fortunate Augustus, chief priest, invested with the tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, the state of the colony of Thamugadi.
EDCS-27600315.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the city of Thamugadi to Emperor Aurelian, emphasizing his imperial titles and perceived divine favor, dating to his reign in the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27600317.translation: To our lord and emperor, the most noble Caesar Flavius Julius Crispinus, from the state of Thamugadis.
EDCS-27600317.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Flavius Julius Crispinus by the state of Thamugadis, likely dating to his period as Caesar.

EDCS-27600318.translation: To our lord and emperor Licinianus Licinius.
EDCS-27600318.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Licinius (likely Licinius I or II), the sender is unspecified but likely a state or community.

EDCS-27600319.translation: To our lords and invincible emperors, Valentinian, Valens, and Gratian, ever Augustuses.
EDCS-27600319.summary: This is a dedication to the co-emperors Valentinian I, Valens, and Gratian, likely from a community or official.

EDCS-27600320.translation: To our lords and invincible emperors, Valentinian, Theodosius, and Arcadius, ever Augustuses.
EDCS-27600320.summary: This inscription honors the co-emperors Valentinian II, Theodosius I, and Arcadius, likely dating to their joint rule.

EDCS-27600321.translation: To Flavius Valerius Constantius, the most noble, unconquered, pious, fortunate Augustus, from the state of the colony of Thamugadis.
EDCS-27600321.summary: This is a dedication to the emperor Constantius II by the state of the colony of Thamugadis.

EDCS-27600322.translation: To our lord, the most noble Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus, from the colony of M M.
EDCS-27600322.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Caesar Constantine (likely Constantine I or his son) by the colony of M M.

EDCS-27600325.translation: To our lord, the unconquered, pious, fortunate emperor Caesar Constantine Augustus, from the colony for milestones and the state.
EDCS-27600325.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Constantine I by a colony, possibly related to road maintenance or milestones.

EDCS-27600326.translation: To our lord, the unconquered, pious, fortunate emperor Caesar Maximinus Augustus, from the colony for milestones and the state of the colony of Thamugadis.
EDCS-27600326.summary: This dedication is to Emperor Maximinus Thrax by the state of the colony of Thamugadis, possibly related to road infrastructure.

EDCS-27600329.translation: To the emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, with tribunician power for the 17th time, imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 3rd time, proconsul, father of the fatherland for the 2nd time.
EDCS-27600329.summary: This is a full titulature inscription for Emperor Caracalla, listing his various honors and titles.

EDCS-27600330.translation: To the emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, pious and fortunate.
EDCS-27600330.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Maximinus Thrax, likely from a community or official.

EDCS-27600331.translation: To our lord Flavius Constantius Victor Augustus.
EDCS-27600331.summary: This is an imperial dedication to the emperor Constantius II, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-27600332.translation: To the invincible emperor, our lord, who brings victory and is pious and fortunate.
EDCS-27600332.summary: This is an imperial dedication to an unnamed emperor, likely from the late Roman period, praising his invincibility and virtues.

EDCS-27600333.translation: To the perpetual and most noble Caesar Valerius Constantius, invincible, pious, and fortunate.
EDCS-27600333.summary: This is an imperial dedication to the Caesar Constantius Gallus, likely from the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-27600334.translation: To Gaius Aurelius Valerius Maxentius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-27600334.summary: This is an imperial dedication to the Caesar Maxentius, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-27600335.translation: To Valerius Constantius, most fortunate and most noble Caesar.
EDCS-27600335.summary: This is an imperial dedication to the Caesar Constantius Gallus, likely from the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-27600552.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Munatius Adiectus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27600553.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Munatius Adiectus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27600554.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Munatius Adiectus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27600555.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Munatius Res, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27600615.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Munatius Adiectus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27600629.summary: This inscription appears to be the name of an individual, likely Gaius Iunius Draco, possibly a tombstone or a dedication.

EDCS-27600632.summary: This inscription likely refers to Marcus Novius Iustus, possibly indicating ownership or a dedication by him.

EDCS-27600638.translation: From the workshop of Agnus and Lucullus
EDCS-27600638.summary: This inscription records the origin of an object, stating it comes from the workshop of Agnus and Lucullus.

EDCS-27600668.summary: This inscription is the name of an individual, Lucius Munatius Adiectus, likely a tombstone or a dedication.

EDCS-27700315.summary: This inscription records that Aufidia Silena lived for a certain number of years, likely part of a funerary monument.

EDCS-27800150.translation: Marcus Aurelius Surulio, veteran of the III Legion, lived for 40 years
EDCS-27800150.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aurelius Surulio, a veteran of the III Legion, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-27800182.translation: Sacred to Saturn Augustus. Lucius Sextilius Felix, priest.
EDCS-27800182.summary: This is a dedication to Saturn Augustus made by the priest Lucius Sextilius Felix.

EDCS-27800183.translation: To the same [temple/deity]. Ianuaria dedicated with a willing spirit.
EDCS-27800183.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Ianuaria to a previously mentioned deity or temple.

EDCS-27800185.summary: This inscription identifies Marcus Caelius Felix as a priest, likely part of a religious monument.

EDCS-27800187.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland, conqueror of Germany and Dacia, high priest, holding tribunician power for the 20th time, consul for the 4th time, Quintus Geminius Silvanus, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, a statue which Quintus Geminius... [rest illegible]
EDCS-27800187.summary: This is a dedication for a statue erected to Emperor Antoninus Pius by Quintus Geminius Silvanus, likely in the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-27800188.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aelius Martialis Abinneianus lived 75 years, is buried here. Marcus Aelius Verna, freedman and heir, made this. Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelius Ianuarius, son, lived 28 years. Aelius Macer, son, lived 20 years, is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Martialis, son, lived 30 years. Aelius Verna, freedman, to his patrons. Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelia Optata lived 65 years. Aelia Silene, daughter, lived 26 years, is buried here.
EDCS-27800188.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aelius Martialis and members of his family, including his freedman and heir, detailing their ages at death and burial.

EDCS-27800189.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelius Felix lived 70 years.
EDCS-27800189.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death and age of Aelius Felix.

EDCS-27800190.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Donatus lived 82 years. TIAVRITIA Oilmaia.
EDCS-27800190.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and age of Marcus Donatus, with an additional, possibly related, name.

EDCS-27800191.translation: Sacred to Augustus. The priest, Caelia Maior his wife, and the pronaos (front part of a temple) from its foundations with its ornaments, they made and dedicated for 4,000 sesterces.
EDCS-27800191.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a pronaos and its ornaments to Augustus by a priest and his wife, specifying the cost.

EDCS-27800382.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely indicating the origin or ownership of an object or workshop.

EDCS-27801001.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gherizal, honored man of the colony, freedman, lived 95 years.
EDCS-27801001.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and age of Gherizal, identified by his status within the colony and as a freedman.

EDCS-27801002.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Valerianus Pictor, son of Rutimacc, pious, lived 39 years.
EDCS-27801002.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and age of Marcus Valerianus Pictor, identified by his father and piety.

EDCS-27801005.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that begins to record the life of Amullia.

EDCS-27900239.translation: To our Lord Gratian, Pious, Fortunate, Victorious and Triumphant, Ever Augustus.
EDCS-27900239.summary: This is a dedication or acclamation to the Roman Emperor Gratian, likely from a public monument.

EDCS-28000967.translation: [...]us lived 80 years. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-28000967.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, mentioning an age at death and a common wish for the deceased's repose.

EDCS-28000972.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Saturia Nampamilla lived 100 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-28000972.summary: A funerary epitaph for Saturia Nampamilla, recording her name, lifespan, and burial place.

EDCS-28000998.translation: Marcus Lollius Sodalis lived 9 years; his bones are laid here. May you rest well.
EDCS-28000998.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Lollius Sodalis, recording his name, age, and a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-28100300.translation: To our lord Galerius Valerius Maximianus, the most noble Caesar, prince of youth, Pious, Happy, Augustus, from the Lamasbenses.
EDCS-28100300.summary: An inscription dedicating something to the Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximianus, likely from the community of Lamasbenses.

EDCS-28100301.translation: To Flavius Constantius, the most noble Caesar, prince of youth, Pious, Happy, Augustus, from the state of Augusta Lamasbensis.
EDCS-28100301.summary: An inscription dedicating something to the Caesar Flavius Constantius, from the state of Augusta Lamasbensis.

EDCS-28100302.translation: To Aurelius Augustus, the most Pious, Happiest Caesar.
EDCS-28100302.summary: A dedication to Caesar Aurelius Augustus, likely a public inscription.

EDCS-28100305.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Egnatius Gallienus, Unconquered, Pious, Happy, Augustus, chief priest, greatest German conqueror, holder of tribunician power.
EDCS-28100305.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Gallienus, listing his titles and achievements.

EDCS-28100307.translation: To our lord, the most noble Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius.
EDCS-28100307.summary: A dedication to Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius.

EDCS-28100308.translation: To Emperor Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximianus, Unconquered, Pious, Happy, Augustus, and to Galerius Valerius Maximinus, the most noble Caesar.
EDCS-28100308.summary: An inscription honoring two emperors, Galerius Valerius Maximianus and Galerius Valerius Maximinus.

EDCS-28100310.translation: Emperor Caesar Aemilius Aemilianus, Pious, Happy, Augustus, chief priest, father of the country, restored the milestones of his world.
EDCS-28100310.summary: An inscription stating that Emperor Aemilianus restored milestones across his empire.

EDCS-28100311.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Claudius Tacitus, Pious, Happy, Augustus, restored the milestones of his world.
EDCS-28100311.summary: An inscription stating that Emperor Tacitus restored milestones across his empire.

EDCS-28100312.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Diocletian, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, restored the milestones of his dominion.
EDCS-28100312.summary: This inscription records the restoration of milestones by the Emperor Diocletian, likely in the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-28100313.translation: To Gaius Galerius Valerius Maximian, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-28100313.summary: This is a dedication to the Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximian, likely from the period of the Tetrarchy (late 3rd or early 4th century CE).

EDCS-28100314.translation: SCIO to Valerius Maximian, most noble Caesar, restored the milestones of his dominion.
EDCS-28100314.summary: This inscription records the restoration of milestones by Valerius Maximian, referred to as Caesar, likely during the Tetrarchy.

EDCS-28100315.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantine, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered, ever Augustus.
EDCS-28100315.summary: This is a dedication to the Emperor Constantine the Great, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-28100316.translation: To the Emperor, our lord Flavius Julius Crispus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-28100316.summary: This is a dedication to Crispus, who held the title of Caesar, likely during the reign of his father Constantine the Great in the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-28100317.translation: To the Emperor, our lord Flavius Claudius Constantine, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-28100317.summary: This is a dedication to Constantine the Caesar, likely Constantine II, in the first half of the 4th century CE.

EDCS-28100318.translation: To our perpetual lord Constantius Caesar, after many vicennalia.
EDCS-28100318.summary: This inscription honors Constantius Caesar, likely Constantius Chlorus or Constantius II, noting multiple celebrations of his twentieth anniversary of rule.

EDCS-28100319.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus restored the milestones of his dominion.
EDCS-28100319.summary: This inscription records the restoration of milestones by the Emperor Caracalla, who adopted the name Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28100320.translation: The Emperor Caesar [---] Pius Felix Augustus, father of the fatherland, restored the milestones of his dominion.
EDCS-28100320.summary: This inscription records the restoration of milestones by an Emperor, with titles including Pius Felix Augustus and father of the fatherland, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28100321.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Trajanus Decius S[---]
EDCS-28100321.summary: This is an incomplete inscription honoring Emperor Trajan Decius, who reigned in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28100322.translation: To our eternal lord Maximian, most noble Caesar, for many twenty-year periods.
EDCS-28100322.summary: This inscription honors the Caesar Maximian, likely dedicating a monument or marking a period of service.

EDCS-28100323.translation: To our eternal lord Maximian Augustus, for many twenty-year periods.
EDCS-28100323.summary: This inscription honors the emperor Maximian Augustus, likely dedicating a monument or marking a period of service.

EDCS-28100324.translation: To Emperor Caesar Galerius Maximianus and Caesar Gaius Valerius Maximinus, by the community of Diana.
EDCS-28100324.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the community of Diana to the emperors Galerius Maximianus and Maximinus.

EDCS-28100328.translation: To Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantine, pious, fortunate, unconquered, forever Augustus.
EDCS-28100328.summary: This is a titulature inscription for Emperor Constantine the Great, likely from a public monument.

EDCS-28100329.translation: To our lord Constantine the Younger, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-28100329.summary: This inscription honors Constantine the Younger as Caesar, likely part of an official dedication.

EDCS-28100331.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest conqueror of Parthia, of Britain, of Germany, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 17th time, acclaimed emperor 4 times, consul 3 times, proconsul, by the community of Novensis Lamasba Antoniniana.
EDCS-28100331.summary: This is a comprehensive titulature inscription for Emperor Caracalla, dedicated by the community of Novensis Lamasba Antoniniana.

EDCS-28100332.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurellius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, son of the deified Antoninus, for travelers.
EDCS-28100332.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Emperor Caracalla, likely for the benefit of travelers on a road or public building.

EDCS-28100333.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Antoninus Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-28100333.summary: This is a titulature inscription for Emperor Gordian III, likely from a public monument.

EDCS-28100334.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Claudius Tacitus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-28100334.summary: This is a titulature inscription for Emperor Tacitus, likely from a public monument.

EDCS-28100335.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Probus, unconquered, pious, fortunate Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, proconsul.
EDCS-28100335.summary: This is a full titulature inscription for Emperor Probus, likely from a public monument.

EDCS-28100336.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Probus, Invictus, Pius, Felix, Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the country, proconsul, 10.
EDCS-28100336.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Probus, likely part of a milestone or honorary monument given the mention of '10' which could refer to a distance.

EDCS-28100337.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Valerius Maximianus, Invictus, Pius, Felix, Augustus, 10.
EDCS-28100337.summary: This dedication honors Emperor Maximian, possibly marking a milestone given the numeral '10'.

EDCS-28100338.translation: Under Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest conqueror of Parthia, greatest of Britain, greatest of Germany, with seventeen tribunician powers, three times emperor, twice consul, father of the country, proconsul. From Lamasba, 65 miles.
EDCS-28100338.summary: This inscription commemorates the extensive titles of Emperor Caracalla and records a distance of 65 miles from Lamasba, likely marking a road.

EDCS-28100339.translation: To our Lords Emperors Publius Licinius Valerianus Augustus and Publius Licinius Gallienus Caesar Augustus, milestones.
EDCS-28100339.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperors Valerian and Gallienus, indicating the measurement of milestones for a road.

EDCS-28100340.translation: To our Lord Flavius Valerius Severus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-28100340.summary: This is a dedication to Caesar Severus, likely part of an honorary monument or milestone.

EDCS-28100341.translation: To our Lords Valentinianus and Valens and Gratianus, perpetual Augusti.
EDCS-28100341.summary: This inscription honors the co-emperors Valentinian, Valens, and Gratian, likely for a public work or monument.

EDCS-28100342.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Valerius Maximianus, Invictus, Pius, Felix, Augustus, for the second time father of the country, proconsul, 5.
EDCS-28100342.summary: This dedication to Emperor Maximian, noting his repeated titles, likely marks a milestone with a distance of 5 units.

EDCS-28100343.translation: To the perpetual and most flourishing Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximianus Iovius the younger, 1.
EDCS-28100343.summary: This inscription honors Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximianus Iovius, possibly as part of a milestone or dedication with the numeral '1'.

EDCS-28100344.translation: To our Lord Flavius Valerius Crispinus, most noble Caesar, 2.
EDCS-28100344.summary: This dedication to Caesar Flavius Valerius Crispinus likely marks a milestone or public work, indicated by the numeral '2'.

EDCS-28100345.translation: To our Lord Valentinianus, perpetual Caesar always.
EDCS-28100345.summary: This inscription honors Valentinian as a perpetual Caesar, possibly part of a monument or milestone.

EDCS-28100347.translation: To our lord Emperor Flavius Julius Constantius, pious, fortunate, victorious, and triumphant Augustus.
EDCS-28100347.summary: This inscription is an acclamation to Emperor Constantius, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-28100348.translation: To our lord Emperor Flavius Claudius Julianus, ever Augustus.
EDCS-28100348.summary: This inscription is an acclamation to Emperor Julian, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-28100349.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus, the unconquered, pious, fortunate.
EDCS-28100349.summary: This inscription is an acclamation to Emperor Philip the Arab, likely from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28100350.translation: During the reign of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, holder of tribunician power for the 17th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul for the 14th time, conqueror of Parthia, conqueror of Britain, conqueror of Germany.
EDCS-28100350.summary: This is a titulature inscription for Emperor Caracalla, likely from the 3rd century CE, listing his various honors and titles.

EDCS-28100351.summary: This is a very brief inscription, possibly a measurement or a count, dating from Roman Britain.

EDCS-28100352.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, son of the deified Magnus Antoninus, grandson of the deified Severus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power.
EDCS-28100352.summary: This is a titulature inscription for Emperor Severus Alexander, likely from the early 3rd century CE, listing his imperial titles.

EDCS-28100353.summary: This inscription is a dedication or acclamation to an emperor referred to as 'Senior Augustus', possibly referring to Diocletian or another senior ruler of the Tetrarchy.

EDCS-28100354.translation: Our lord Emperor Caesar Constantius Maximus.
EDCS-28100354.summary: This inscription is an acclamation to Emperor Constantius, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-28100357.translation: During the reign of Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Augustus, for the 8th time.
EDCS-28100357.summary: This is a titulature inscription for Emperor Decius, likely from the mid-3rd century CE, indicating his eighth year in power.
