EDCS-44900089.summary: This epitaph is dedicated to Bryseis, the wife of Exuperans, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-44900090.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Herennia Callas, her mother Laberia Quentula made this. To Laberius Baricio, her father Quentula made this.
EDCS-44900090.summary: This funerary inscription records two parents, Herennia Callas and Laberius Baricio, for whom dedications were made by Laberia Quentula.

EDCS-44900105.translation: Gaius Stertinius Gallus, son of Gaius, lived 95 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-44900105.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Stertinius Gallus, recording his name, parentage, and lifespan.

EDCS-44900108.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Nonius Secundus lived 58 years. Donata set this up.
EDCS-44900108.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Donata for Nonius Secundus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-44900109.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Sextius Secundus lived 36 years.
EDCS-44900109.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Sextius Secundus, recording his name and lifespan.

EDCS-44900110.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Liberalis lived 52 years. Simplicius and Felicitas set this up for their dearest daughter.
EDCS-44900110.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Simplicius and Felicitas for their daughter Liberalis, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-44900111.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirit Caelestis Augusta. Hervius made this for his fifteen-year-old daughter. (Possibly with T. V--- initials).
EDCS-44900111.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Hervius for his daughter, likely a dedication to a divine spirit, possibly including initials.

EDCS-44900112.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Petronia Spica lived 87 years; she is laid here.
EDCS-44900112.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Petronia Spica, recording her name, lifespan, and burial.

EDCS-44900113.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Gellius Silvanus lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-44900113.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gellius Silvanus, recording his name, lifespan, and burial.

EDCS-44900114.translation: On these estates of Quintus Antonius Maximus Acutianius.
EDCS-44900114.summary: This inscription marks the boundaries or ownership of estates belonging to Quintus Antonius Maximus Acutianius.

EDCS-44900115.translation: Sacred to Saturn. Gaius Domitius Gerulanus, perpetual priest, made this at his own expense and also dedicated it.
EDCS-44900115.summary: This is a religious dedication to the god Saturn, funded and inaugurated by Gaius Domitius Gerulanus in his capacity as perpetual priest.

EDCS-44900116.translation: To Caelestis Augusta, Publius Pacuvius, a gift of silver weighing [---] pounds.
EDCS-44900116.summary: This is a votive offering to the goddess Caelestis Augusta, made by Publius Pacuvius with silver of a specified weight.

EDCS-44900165.translation: Sacred to the Unconquered Hercules for the health and victory of the Emperors and Caesars Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Pius and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augusti and Lucius Septimius Geta Caesar, «son of Julia Augusta mother» «of Augustus», Publius Aelius Menecrates dedicated this gift to his homeland in the name of himself and his sister.
EDCS-44900165.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Hercules for the health and victory of the Severan emperors, made by Publius Aelius Menecrates.

EDCS-44900205.summary: This is a brief dedication, likely to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus.

EDCS-44900207.translation: To Gaius Julius Cobas, from Ulpia Zamiza.
EDCS-44900207.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Gaius Julius Cobas by Ulpia Zamiza.

EDCS-44900209.translation: Sacred to the gods. Gumma placed this in the Abaris district, son of Quintus Servilius Victor.
EDCS-44900209.summary: This is a dedication to the gods by Gumma, son of Quintus Servilius Victor, in the Abaris district.

EDCS-45300116.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Apuleius Felix, a veteran, lived for 70 years. Antonia Martis, his wife and co-heir of his sons, made this for them, using 3,000 sesterces.
EDCS-45300116.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Apuleius Felix, a veteran, and was set up by his wife and heir, Antonia Martis, with a specified sum of money.

EDCS-45300117.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 20th time, imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-45300117.summary: This inscription is a formal titulature of Emperor Hadrian, listing his titles and honors.

EDCS-45300118.translation: To our lord Flavius Constantinus Caesar [---].
EDCS-45300118.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Constantine Caesar.

EDCS-45300133.translation: To our lord, the most noble Caesar Galerius Maximianus.
EDCS-45300133.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Galerius Maximianus.

EDCS-45300174.translation: Sedinia Musti, daughter, pious, lived 17 years. She lies here.
EDCS-45300174.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sedinia Musti, recording her age and piety.

EDCS-45300206.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. His most pious wife and son made this for Lucius Arruntius Suriacus.
EDCS-45300206.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Arruntius Suriacus and was dedicated by his devoted wife and son.

EDCS-45300209.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iunia Saturnina lived 31 years. Her partner made this. She rests here.
EDCS-45300209.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Iunia Saturnina's partner, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45300215.translation: To Julia Domna, Augusta, mother of the camp and of Augustus and of the Senate.
EDCS-45300215.summary: This is an honorary inscription likely dedicated to Julia Domna, the wife of Emperor Septimius Severus, during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45300216.translation: Iulius Respectus, 61 years old, with his wife D ia firma and his son Iuli I est.
EDCS-45300216.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Respectus, including his wife and son, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45500009.translation: Hermogenes. I lie here. Born, he lived 25 years. In order that his body be covered and his name be read after death, his devoted colleagues completed this at their own expense.
EDCS-45500009.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hermogenes, erected by his colleagues, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45500010.translation: To the memory of Flavius Matronianus, Sabiniana, Victoria, and their other shared children, who were violently snatched away by their own affections in a pitiable misfortune while fortune raged in the very flower of life. After their most bitter grief, lest he be subjected to tears of bloody fortune by the same thing repeatedly, he cursed the conception of offspring. For the sole solace of his misfortunes and eternal memory, Flavius Victor for himself, his wife, and his children and Speni N.
EDCS-45500010.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Flavius Victor for his deceased family members, reflecting a profound personal tragedy, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45600155.translation: Marcus Aurelius PN gladly fulfilled his vow with the money of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-45600155.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Aurelius, indicating the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45600157.translation: Of the second cohort: Caius Vibius Optat, Caius Minucius Rogatianus, Caius Aurelius Saturninus, Caius Cornelius Rufus, Caius Iulius Crispinus, Caius Geminius Felix, Quintus Iulius Maximus, Lucius Papirius Felicianus, Quintus Iulius Rogatus. Of the third cohort: Publius Popilius Felix, Caius Iulius Apertianus.
EDCS-45600157.summary: This inscription lists individuals, possibly soldiers from the second and third cohorts, with their names, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45600158.translation: Quintus Egrilius Tannonianus, Caius Iulius Felix, Titus Helvius Felix, Marcus Oppius Martialis, Quintus Aemilius Optatus, Lucius Aemilius Vindex, Lucius Publius Victor, Caius Aurelius Annibal, Caius Veturius Augurinus, Caius Caecilius Senecio, Marcus Fabius Sabinus, Marcus Fabius Secundus, Lucius Pomponius Proculus, Quintus Caecilius Victor, Iulius Ianuarius, Caius Iulius Fortunatus, Lucius Antonius Lironinus, Caius Iulius Asellus, Titus Flavius Felix.
EDCS-45600158.summary: This inscription is a list of names, likely belonging to members of a military unit or a collegium, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45900014.translation: Veteran, who was also a subdeacon.
EDCS-45900014.summary: This is a brief epitaph or title identifying an individual as a Veteran and a subdeacon, dating to the Late Roman period.

EDCS-46100001.summary: This is a fragmented inscription mentioning Valentiniani and an age, likely a funerary dedication from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46100002.translation: Felix the veteran and his wife Iulia made this for Valerius Regius, who lived well deservedly, while they were alive.
EDCS-46100002.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valerius Regius, set up by his veteran, Felix, and his wife, Iulia.

EDCS-46100486.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Iulius Pastor lived 55 years.
EDCS-46100486.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Pastor, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-46100487.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aemilius Felix lived 6 years.
EDCS-46100487.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aemilius Felix, who lived 6 years.

EDCS-46300010.translation: Under the rule of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, holding tribunician power, the colony Iulia Augusta Rusazu, exempt from service in the legion VII, nine miles from the river Oron.
EDCS-46300010.summary: A boundary inscription or dedication from the colony of Rusazu, dating to the reign of Emperor Claudius Gothicus.

EDCS-46300014.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus Licinius Secundus lived 55 years. Flavia Concessa made this for her dearest husband. He is buried here.
EDCS-46300014.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Licinius Secundus, set up by his wife Flavia Concessa.

EDCS-46300016.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits, in memory of Marina, [his/her] daughter.
EDCS-46300016.summary: A funerary dedication in memory of a daughter named Marina.

EDCS-46300044.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantius Caesar.
EDCS-46300044.summary: A dedication to the Caesar Constantius, likely Constantius II.

EDCS-46300045.summary: A very brief inscription, possibly a ownership mark or a token.

EDCS-46300131.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, and to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Carinus, the most noble Caesar Augustus.
EDCS-46300131.summary: A joint dedication to the emperors Carus and Carinus.

EDCS-46300132.translation: To the deified Augustus Valerius Maximianus Augustus.
EDCS-46300132.summary: A dedication to the deified emperor Maximian.

EDCS-46300133.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Lucius Atilius, centurion of the XX legion, [---] centurion of the XX legion.
EDCS-46300133.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Atilius, a centurion of the XX legion, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-46300134.translation: To the spirits of the departed. He lived 14 years. Antonius Maximus, a pious father, made this for his son, a veteran.
EDCS-46300134.summary: A funerary inscription by Antonius Maximus for his son, who died at 14 years of age and was a veteran.

EDCS-46300216.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, conqueror of Parthia, conqueror of Britain, conqueror of Germany, holding tribunician power for the 20th time, consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, restored [this] in the 9th year.
EDCS-46300216.summary: An official inscription detailing the titles and achievements of Emperor Marcus Aurelius, possibly related to a building restoration around AD 176.

EDCS-46300217.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, conqueror of Parthia, conqueror of Britain, conqueror of Germany.
EDCS-46300217.summary: A formal inscription listing the titles of Emperor Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-46300223.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Julius F. Maximinus lived 48 years.
EDCS-46300223.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Quintus Julius F. Maximinus.

EDCS-46300224.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius P. Felix Sace lived, and Cornelia Victoria, his wife. They lived 65 years here.
EDCS-46300224.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius P. Felix Sace and his wife Cornelia Victoria, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-46400017.translation: To the life-giving Genius, Cornelius Sacerdos gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-46400017.summary: A dedication to the Genius of the place by Cornelius Sacerdos, likely fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-46400047.translation: Flavius Avianius Caecilius, most illustrious man, former governor of the province of Numidia, erected structures to succeed perpetually.
EDCS-46400047.summary: An inscription commemorating Flavius Avianius Caecilius, a former governor of Numidia, who erected permanent structures.

EDCS-46400228.translation: Frontilla lived 45 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-46400228.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of Frontilla and her burial.

EDCS-46400235.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Caecilius Camdanus lived for 65 years.
EDCS-46400235.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Caecilius Camdanus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-46400236.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Quintus Caecilius Candidianus.
EDCS-46400236.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Quintus Caecilius Candidianus.

EDCS-46400237.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilia Montana the dutiful lived 40 years. Caecilia Quintilia made this for her good mother.
EDCS-46400237.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Caecilia Montana and was erected by her daughter, Caecilia Quintilia.

EDCS-46400238.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caninia Emerita lived for 50 years. Gaius P. Maximus made this for his deserving mother. She lies here.
EDCS-46400238.summary: This funerary inscription honors Caninia Emerita, erected by her son Gaius P. Maximus, and marks her burial place.

EDCS-46400239.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Casia made a tomb for her husband.
EDCS-46400239.summary: This funerary inscription records that Casia commissioned a tomb for her husband.

EDCS-46400240.translation: Iulius Remex the son lived for 50 years. He lies here.
EDCS-46400240.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan and burial place of Iulius Remex.

EDCS-46400241.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Maiorica lived for 40 years. She lies here.
EDCS-46400241.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and burial place of Iulia Maiorica.

EDCS-46400242.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Occius Saturninus lived for 21 years. He lies here.
EDCS-46400242.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan and burial place of Gaius Occius Saturninus.

EDCS-46400243.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Octavia Faustina lived for 40 years. Titus Flavius Felix made this for his pious wife.
EDCS-46400243.summary: This funerary inscription honors Octavia Faustina, erected by her husband Titus Flavius Felix, and records her lifespan.

EDCS-46400244.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pomponia Optata lived for 10 years.
EDCS-46400244.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pomponia Optata, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-46400245.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Terentius Victor lived 75 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-46400245.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Terentius Victor, recording his age and burial place.

EDCS-46400246.translation: Terentia Donata lived 32 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-46400246.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Terentia Donata, recording her age and burial place.

EDCS-46400248.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Decimus Valerius Felix lived 70 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-46400248.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Decimus Valerius Felix, recording his age and burial place.

EDCS-46400249.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Valeria Nicopolis lived 100 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-46400249.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Nicopolis, recording her advanced age and burial place.

EDCS-46400250.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Valeria Saturnina lived 45 years. Iulius Saturninus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-46400250.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and death of Valeria Saturnina, and was erected by her husband, Iulius Saturninus.

EDCS-46400251.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Grata, recording her age and epithet.

EDCS-46400252.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caecilia Urbana.
EDCS-46400252.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caecilia Urbana, possibly indicating her death.

EDCS-46400254.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Titus Fabius Saturninus lived 51 years. His son made this for his parent. He is buried here.
EDCS-46400254.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Titus Fabius Saturninus lived 51 years and was buried here, with the monument erected by his son.

EDCS-46400255.translation: Flavia Secunda, daughter of Titus, the dutiful, lived 16 years.
EDCS-46400255.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and age of Flavia Secunda.

EDCS-46400256.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Fructosa lived 18 years.
EDCS-46400256.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fructosa, recording her age.

EDCS-46400257.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Ianuarius Colonius lived for 7 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-46400257.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Publius Ianuarius Colonius, who died at the age of seven.

EDCS-46400258.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Aelius Fortunatus the pious lived for 15 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-46400258.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a youth named Gaius Aelius Fortunatus, who died at the age of fifteen.

EDCS-46400260.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilia Novella lived for 20 years; Quintus Servilius Fidus made this for his dearest mother. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Servilius Calo lived for 75 years; Servilius Fidus made this for his father; his bones rest here in his eternal home.
EDCS-46400260.summary: This is a double funerary epitaph for a mother and son, Aemilia Novella and Quintus Servilius Calo, with the latter's son Quintus Servilius Fidus as the dedicator for both.

EDCS-46400261.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vettius Faustus lived for 45 years; his wife Paccia Faustina erected this.
EDCS-46400261.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vettius Faustus, erected by his wife Paccia Faustina.

EDCS-46400262.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia [dedicated this] to her son Lucius.
EDCS-46400262.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a mother, Iulia, for her son Lucius.

EDCS-46400874.translation: Quintus Geminius Auctinus of Carthage, candidate; Titus Flavius Honestus of the camp, candidate; Gaius Iulius Lucianus of the camp; Gaius Iulius Faustus of the camp, learning to be a standard-bearer; Gaius Calpurnius Maximus of the camp, learning to be a standard-bearer; Gaius Tarquitius Fronto of the camp; Lucius Saenius Saturninus of the camp; Gaius Iulius Demetrianus of the camp; Gaius Iulius Saturninus of Theveste; Gaius Iulius Malchus of the camp; Gaius Pompeius Firmianus of the camp, first spear-bearer; Marcus Volcacius Hilarianus of Thysdrus; Gaius Magnius Rusticus of Mascula, second spear-bearer; Lucius Faonius Ianuarius of Sufes; Lucius Antonius Saturninus of Bagai; Gaius Valerius Crescens of the camp, trumpeter; Gaius Aelius Rufinus of the camp, troop leader; Publius Sittius Fatalis of Cirta, groom of the fourth cohort; Gaius Vatinius Victor of the camp; Gaius Iulius Iovinus of Thelepte; Titus Flavius Maximus of Capsa; Quintus Aemilius Victor of Tacape; Lucius Geminius Rogatus of the camp; Quintus Octavius Felix of the camp; Quintus Sallustius Hospitalis, second princeps; Iulius Saturninus of the camp; Graeceius Sucessus of Carthage; Quintus Egnatius Ianuarius of Mascula; Lucius Tullius Felix of the camp, learning to be an eagle-bearer; the ninth cohort; Marcus Lutatius Emeritus of the camp, tesserarius; Quintus Orches Saturninus of Cuiculo, by the baths; Lucius Nonius Martialis of Vaga, auxiliary of the prefect; Quintus Secius Secundus of Carthage; Lucius Sempronius Victor of Ammedara; Gaius Iulius Saturninus of the camp; Gaius Domitius Martialis, groom of the Emperor; Gaius Valerius Ianuarius of the camp; Gaius Iulius Valens of the camp, candidate; Marcus Antonius Longinus of the camp, candidate; Quintus Cornelius Florus of Theveste, tesserarius; Publius Popilius Donatus of the camp, armamentarius; Tiberius Claudius Lucius Piladelus of Emesa; Quintus Domitius Victor of the camp, groom; Publius Baebius Fortunatians of Carthage, auxiliary of the prefect; Marcus Annaeus Saturninus of Thatub; Titus Geminius Fortunatus of the camp; Quintus Sallustius Marcianus of Thamugadi; Gaius Ignius Septiminus of Bagai; Gaius Cesellius Urbanus of the camp; Gaius Iulius Saturninus of Thelepte; Gaius Iulius Rufus of the camp; Gaius Iulius Septiminus Flavio; Cilio; Gaius Iulius Gallus of Cirta; Gaius Velcacius Vitalis of Simithu, horn-blower; the tenth cohort; Titus Minucius Regulus of the camp, candidate; Quintus Pescennius Victor of Thamugadi, candidate; Titus Flavius Saturninus of Hadrumetum; Gaius Marius Barosus of Simithu; Titus Aemilius Saturninus of Cirta; Quintus Numitorius Optatus of the camp; Gaius Iulius Faustus of the camp; Gaius Caelius Fortis of the camp, prætorian duplicarius; Gaius Flavius Saturninus of the camp; Gaius Nevius Faustiolus of Tipasa; Gaius Iulius Gemellus of the camp; Gaius Iulius Faustinus of the camp, scribe; Publius Sittius Rogatus of the camp, duplicarius; Quintus Fabius Sabinus of Carthage; Quintus Octavius Concessus; Caius Arruntius Iuvenalis; Titus Aelius Datus of Thabarca; Caius Cutellius Geminianus of the camp; Quintus Longeius Donatus of the camp; Lucius Aelius Maximus; Tus ncacius Saturninus of Ammaedara; ius Flaccus of Sabratha; turninus of the camp; Publius Longeius Victor of Gisira; Nova, candidate; Marcus O linus of the camp, candidate; Quintus Enn Faustinus of Carthage; ADQS; Gaius Herennius Felix, candidate of Carthage; Lucius Orbius Provincialis of Tigiba; Marcus Ofellius Donatus of the camp; Lucius Purius Octavianus of Theveste; cas; Gaius Aemilius Demetrianus of Thenae, learning to be a standard-bearer; Gaius Memmius Ingenuus of Carthage; Marcus Iulius Ostilianus of the camp; Gaius Iulius Severus of the camp, auxiliary of the prefect; Lucius Mummius Vitalis of the camp; Gaius Iulius Felix of the camp, learning to be a standard-bearer; Marcus Iscantius Venustus of the camp; Gaius Aemilius Iulianus of Carthage, learning to be a standard-bearer; Gaius Tiburtius Saturninus of the camp.
EDCS-46400874.summary: This is a long list of military personnel, including candidates, standard-bearers, officers, and other ranks, from various locations and cohorts, likely associated with a military review or recruitment.

EDCS-46601030.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sentia Rogata the pious lived for 22 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-46601030.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Sentia Rogata, who died at the age of twenty-two.

EDCS-46601031.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sentia Rogata Secundula the pious lived for 75 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-46601031.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Sentia Rogata Secundula, who died at the age of seventy-five.

EDCS-46601042.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aurelius Tiro lived for 30 years; Aurelia Salvia made this for her husband.
EDCS-46601042.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Tiro, erected by his wife Aurelia Salvia.

EDCS-46601060.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed and for perpetual security. Gaius Iulius Ianuarius made and dedicated this for himself while alive, and for Antonia Urbana.
EDCS-46601060.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Gaius Iulius Ianuarius for himself and his wife Antonia Urbana, intended to ensure their perpetual well-being.

EDCS-46601061.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Extricata lived 70 years.
EDCS-46601061.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Iulia Extricata.

EDCS-46601069.translation: Iulius Ingenuus, a veteran, lived 60 years.
EDCS-46601069.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of the veteran Iulius Ingenuus.

EDCS-46601074.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Aelius Saturninus lived 55 years. Pescenia Cutta for her dearest husband.
EDCS-46601074.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Pescenia Cutta to her husband, Lucius Aelius Saturninus, noting his lifespan.

EDCS-46601075.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Aelius Martialis, a veteran, lived 75 years. Iulia Festa for her most cherished husband. He is buried here.
EDCS-46601075.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Iulia Festa to her husband, the veteran Quintus Aelius Martialis, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-46601076.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Avianius Hospitalis made this for his most dutiful mother, Apuleia Faustillica. She is buried here.
EDCS-46601076.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Avianius Hospitalis to his mother, Apuleia Faustillica.

EDCS-46601077.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Caninius Victor lived 50 years. Memmia Nicopolis, his wife, made this for her most cherished husband.
EDCS-46601077.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Memmia Nicopolis to her husband, Lucius Caninius Victor, noting his lifespan.

EDCS-46601078.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Felicia lived 4 years.
EDCS-46601078.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the short lifespan of Felicia.

EDCS-46601079.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Feronius Euhodus lived 74 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-46601079.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Gaius Feronius Euhodus.

EDCS-46601080.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Feronius Iustus lived 25 years.
EDCS-46601080.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gaius Feronius Iustus.

EDCS-46601081.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavius Fortunatus lived 20 years, two months, and thirteen days.
EDCS-46601081.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the precise lifespan of Flavius Fortunatus, including years, months, and days.

EDCS-46601082.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Zenobius, a veteran, lived for 62 years.
EDCS-46601082.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of a veteran named Titus Flavius Zenobius.

EDCS-46601083.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fulvia Mellita lived for 84 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-46601083.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death and burial of Fulvia Mellita.

EDCS-46601084.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gargilius Proculus lived for 35 years. Gargilius Martialis, his brother, made this.
EDCS-46601084.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Gargilius Proculus, erected by his brother.

EDCS-46700178.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Iulius Donatus, an equestrian of the ala Pannoniorum, an evocatus, lived for 46 years.
EDCS-46700178.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Gaius Iulius Donatus, a cavalryman and evocatus.

EDCS-46700179.translation: To our Lords forever, Emperors Valentinian, Theodosius, and Arcadius.
EDCS-46700179.summary: This inscription honours the emperors Valentinian, Theodosius, and Arcadius.

EDCS-46800001.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Octavia Donata lived for 45 years. Octavius Victor made this; she is buried here.
EDCS-46800001.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Octavia Donata, erected by Octavius Victor.

EDCS-46800003.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Maiorica made this for her husband, Iulius Victoricus Lesianus, a good and true man, with a willing heart. He lived for 80 years.
EDCS-46800003.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Iulius Victoricus Lesianus, erected by his wife Iulia Maiorica.

EDCS-46800004.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Cocceius Maximus lived for 40 years. Iulia Urbana made this for her husband; he is buried here.
EDCS-46800004.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Lucius Cocceius Maximus, erected by his wife Iulia Urbana.

EDCS-46800131.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antonius Fon
EDCS-46800131.summary: This is an incomplete tombstone inscription for Antonius Fon.

EDCS-46800133.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valeria Crescentia lived for 6 years. Spiculus, her father, made this for his dearest daughter; she is buried here.
EDCS-46800133.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Valeria Crescentia, erected by her father.

EDCS-46800134.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Prima Aug lived 10 years. Lucius Montanus Aug made this for his wife. She is buried here.
EDCS-46800134.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Prima Aug, set up by her husband Lucius Montanus Aug, recording her age at death.

EDCS-46800135.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. P. Anti lived 2 years, 7 months, and 5 days. His father made this for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-46800135.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for P. Anti, set up by his father, recording his age at death.

EDCS-46800136.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulius Septimianus lived 80 years and 6 days. He set this up for himself during his lifetime and dedicated it.
EDCS-46800136.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Septimianus, who set it up for himself during his lifetime, recording his age at death.

EDCS-46800137.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Flavia [---] PIOFP [---].
EDCS-46800137.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Flavia, likely recording her name and possibly her lifespan or relationships.

EDCS-46800150.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Pudentia Exornata lived 48 years.
EDCS-46800150.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pudentia Exornata, recording her age at death.

EDCS-46800151.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Aufidius.
EDCS-46800151.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Caius Aufidius, likely the beginning of a tomb inscription.

EDCS-46800152.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Titus Flavius Gilo lived 95 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-46800152.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Gilo, recording his age at death and burial location.

EDCS-46800154.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iunio Iucundo lived [---] years.
EDCS-46800154.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Iunio Iucundo, recording his name and partially his lifespan.

EDCS-46800155.translation: Lived 74 years. Prima, his wife, set this up.
EDCS-46800155.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed man, set up by his wife Prima, recording his age at death.

EDCS-46800156.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Caelius Rogatus iunior Saturninus lived 50 years.
EDCS-46800156.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Caelius Rogatus iunior Saturninus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-46800157.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Aelius Priscus made this for his AIAITINO.
EDCS-46800157.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, likely by Lucius Aelius Priscus for someone identified as AIAITINO.

EDCS-46800158.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Sextius Victor lived for 50 years. P. Fortunata and her children placed this for her husband.
EDCS-46800158.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Sextius Victor, who lived to be 50 years old, and was set up by his wife P. Fortunata and their children.

EDCS-46800159.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Spathalus lived for 1 year, 2 months, and 8 days. Primulus made this for his pious son.
EDCS-46800159.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Spathalus, who died at a young age, and was set up by his father Primulus.

EDCS-46800160.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Luciae Stertiniae Fortunatae lived for 55 years. Gaius Clodius Feli's son made this; she is buried here.
EDCS-46800160.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Luciae Stertiniae Fortunatae, who lived to be 55 years old, and was set up by Gaius Clodius Feli's son.

EDCS-46800161.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. N. Victor lived for 70 years. [---]
EDCS-46800161.summary: This is a funerary inscription for N. Victor, who lived to be 70 years old, with some illegible text following.

EDCS-46800162.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Volussenius Rufinus lived for 100 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-46800162.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Volussenius Rufinus, who lived to be 100 years old and is buried in this spot.

EDCS-46800163.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Amandus made this for his most deserving son; a slave.
EDCS-46800163.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Amandus, a slave, for his deserving son.

EDCS-46800164.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Nonnius Secundus lived for 58 years. Donatus placed this.
EDCS-46800164.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Nonnius Secundus, who lived to be 58 years old, and was placed by Donatus.

EDCS-46800165.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Billarus lived for 10 years. Acilia Eutiches for her friend.
EDCS-46800165.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Billarus, who died at age 10, and was set up by his friend Acilia Eutiches.

EDCS-46800166.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cossidia Prima lived for 75 years. Her husband, Iulius Gemellus, a veteran, placed this.
EDCS-46800166.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cossidia Prima, who lived to be 75 years old, and was placed by her husband, the veteran Iulius Gemellus.

EDCS-46800167.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Julia Gemellona, my dearest wife, lived 46 years. Julius Cocceus Saturninus, a veteran, made this.
EDCS-46800167.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julia Gemellona, dedicated by her husband, a veteran, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46800168.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulius Gemelus Tatianus lived 36 years. Iulia Ianiata made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-46800168.summary: This funerary epitaph for Iulius Gemelus Tatianus was erected by his wife, Iulia Ianiata, and likely dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46800169.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Lurius Victor, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, guardian through the counsel of all his friends, lived 67 years. His sons and heirs made this; he is buried in this place.
EDCS-46800169.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Lurius Victor, a man noted for his friendships and counsel, and was erected by his sons and heirs, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46800170.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Mania Fortunata lived 10 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-46800170.summary: This is a tombstone for a young girl, Mania Fortunata, who died at the age of 10, and it likely dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46800171.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Memia Severa, my dearest daughter, lived 20 years.
EDCS-46800171.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Memia Severa, likely dedicated by a parent, and probably dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46800172.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Minucia Valeriana, my most rare wife.
EDCS-46800172.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Minucia Valeriana, set up by her husband, and likely dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46800174.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Marcus Plotius Faustus, the perpetual priest, father.
EDCS-46800174.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marcus Plotius Faustus, a perpetual priest, likely erected by his descendants and dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46800176.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Haterius Thevestinus lived 93 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-46800176.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Haterius Thevestinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46800177.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Attius Urbicus lived 70 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-46800177.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Gaius Attius Urbicus and likely dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46800178.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publius Cerellius Saturninus lived 40 years. He is buried here. Salv made this.
EDCS-46800178.summary: This funerary epitaph for Publius Cerellius Saturninus includes a maker's name, Salv, and likely dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46800179.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Pescenius Donatus lived 70 years. Caninia Dontula [erected this] for her dearest husband.
EDCS-46800179.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Pescenius Donatus, set up by his wife.

EDCS-46800180.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cornelius.
EDCS-46800180.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription for someone named Cornelius.

EDCS-46800181.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Lucceius Felix lived 55 years. He is buried here. Minucia Saturnina, his devoted wife, made this.
EDCS-46800181.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Lucceius Felix, set up by his wife.

EDCS-46800186.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the duoviri (chief magistrates) of the municipium of Apronia.

EDCS-46800187.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Fusca lived 45 years. Aurelia Antiocis, freedwoman [of Fusca?], and Aurelius Euodianus, freedman [of Fusca?], [erected this] for their deserving patroness.
EDCS-46800187.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Fusca, set up by her freedmen.

EDCS-46800200.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, conqueror of Parthia, conqueror of Britain, conqueror of Germany, high priest, with seventeen years of tribunician power, acclaimed imperator three times, father of the country, consul four times, proconsul, [this milestone was placed] three miles from Calceus.
EDCS-46800200.summary: This is a milestone inscription dedicated to Emperor Caracalla, indicating a distance of three Roman miles from the town of Calceus.

EDCS-46800215.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aponia Fortunata lived 30 years. Aponia Adluir [erected this] for her sister.
EDCS-46800215.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aponia Fortunata, set up by her sister.

EDCS-46800222.translation: Sedatus, son of Paetus, lived 60 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-46800222.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sedatus, son of Paetus.

EDCS-46800224.translation: To the Flavian Septimian Augustan Nymphs.
EDCS-46800224.summary: This is a dedication to local deities or spirits associated with the Flavian and Septimian imperial families.

EDCS-46800320.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Haterius Fortunatus lived [age illegible]. Hateria Octavia lived 10 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-46800320.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Hateria Octavia, with a mention of Quintus Haterius Fortunatus.

EDCS-46800321.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Octavia Catullina lived 20 years. Quintus Haterius Fortunatus [erected this].
EDCS-46800321.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Octavia Catullina, who lived 20 years, and was likely erected by Quintus Haterius Fortunatus.

EDCS-46800322.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Haterius Securus lived 19 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-46800322.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Quintus Haterius Securus, who lived 19 years.

EDCS-46800323.translation: Publius Iulius Africanus lived 40 years. His son, the heir, made this with his own money.
EDCS-46800323.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Publius Iulius Africanus, who lived 40 years, and was funded by his son and heir.

EDCS-46800324.translation: Iulia Prima lived 75 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-46800324.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Iulia Prima, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-46800325.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Kalpurnia Victoria lived 45 years. Lutatius Saturninus, her husband, made this. She is buried here.
EDCS-46800325.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Kalpurnia Victoria, who lived 45 years, and was erected by her husband, Lutatius Saturninus.

EDCS-46800326.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Lucceius Felix, son, lived 25 years, 1 month, and 10 days. He is buried here. Minucia Saturnina, his devoted mother, made this.
EDCS-46800326.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Caius Lucceius Felix, who lived 25 years, 1 month, and 10 days, and was erected by his mother, Minucia Saturnina.

EDCS-46800327.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucceia Saturnina, daughter, lived 16 years, 4 months, and 5 days. His devoted mother made this.
EDCS-46800327.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Lucceia Saturnina, who lived 16 years, 4 months, and 5 days, and was erected by her mother.

EDCS-46800328.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Minucia Saturnina lived for herself while living.
EDCS-46800328.summary: This funerary inscription records that Minucia Saturnina made provision for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-46800329.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Magnius Felix, veteran, lived 60 years. Iulia DN [for] her husband.
EDCS-46800329.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Magnius Felix, a veteran who lived 60 years, and was likely set up by his wife, Iulia DN.

EDCS-46800330.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Magnia Prima, devoted one, lived 40 years. The heir dedicated this.
EDCS-46800330.summary: This funerary inscription records that the heir dedicated a monument for Magnia Prima, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-46800331.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Maria lived 80 years. Ulpius Donatus made this for his dearest mother.
EDCS-46800331.summary: A funerary dedication by Ulpius Donatus for his mother, Maria, who lived to be 80 years old.

EDCS-46800332.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Venatilla, daughter of Lucius, lived 26 years. Felix, her husband and father of her children, arranged for her burial.
EDCS-46800332.summary: A funerary dedication for Venatilla, who died at 26, arranged by her husband and father of her children, Felix.

EDCS-46900024.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus. Titus Flavius Clemens, son of Titus, of the Papirian tribe, aedile, duovir, quaestor, made and dedicated this with a decree of the decurions, having promised 2000 sesterces from his public funds and 1000 sesterces from his quaestorship.
EDCS-46900024.summary: A dedication to Mars Augustus by Titus Flavius Clemens, an official, for public works funded by his promises of money.

EDCS-46900027.translation: Licinia Donata lived XX years. Lucius Aelius Rusticus made this for his dearest wife. She is buried here.
EDCS-46900027.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Aelius Rusticus for his wife, Licinia Donata, who died at 20.

EDCS-46900028.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulia Lila lived XX years.
EDCS-46900028.summary: A fragment of a funerary inscription for Iulia Lila, who lived an unspecified number of years.

EDCS-46900029.translation: To Julius Philippus, most noble Caesar, prince of the youth, Augustus.
EDCS-46900029.summary: An inscription honoring Julius Philippus, a high-ranking imperial official.

EDCS-46900030.translation: Sacred to Victoria Augusta. Lucius Claudius Clemens, son of Lucius, of the Fabian tribe, erected and dedicated this in honor of his duovirate.
EDCS-46900030.summary: A dedication to Victoria Augusta by Lucius Claudius Clemens upon completing his term as duovir.

EDCS-46900031.translation: He established this, a citizen and lover of the sacred genius of Thamugadi.
EDCS-46900031.summary: A dedication to the Genius of Thamugadi by a citizen and admirer.

EDCS-46900282.translation: Felicitas lived 75 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-46900282.summary: A funerary epitaph for Felicitas, who died at 75.

EDCS-46900283.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Egrilius Gaiuta, dutiful, lived 85 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-46900283.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Egrilius Gaiuta, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-46900284.translation: dutiful, lived 36 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-46900284.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of an unnamed woman who lived 36 years.

EDCS-46900285.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of Sitia Colonica at her death.

EDCS-46900286.translation: lianus lived 21 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-46900286.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of lianus, who lived 21 years.

EDCS-46900287.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of Aemilia Sadeca at her death.

EDCS-46900288.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Varbas with his son Zabonis, Barosus, and his wife Arbule, lived 80 years.
EDCS-46900288.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Varbas, his son Zabonis, and his wife Arbule, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-46900289.translation: To Marcus Plotius Faustus, son of Marcus, Roman knight, prefect of the III Cohort of Ituraeans, tribune of the I Flavian Cohort of Canathenians, prefect of the Ala Gallorum Tauriana, perpetual priest, priest of the city, for his merits towards the citizens and homeland, and for his generosity, the state of the colony of Thamugad by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-46900289.summary: A dedication by the city of Thamugad to Marcus Plotius Faustus, honoring his military and civic service and generosity, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-46900292.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Julia Secunda, a most rare woman, [---] aged 45.
EDCS-46900292.summary: A funerary inscription for Julia Secunda, a woman described as 'most rare', with her age partially preserved.

EDCS-47200118.translation: Under the consulship of Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, the Invincible, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, chief priest, the state of the colony of Thamugad.
EDCS-47200118.summary: A dedication by the state of Thamugad, mentioning Emperor Maximinus and his titles, likely dating to his reign (235-238 AD).

EDCS-47200119.translation: Under Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, the Invincible.
EDCS-47200119.summary: An inscription mentioning Emperor Maximinus and his titles, likely dating to his reign (235-238 AD).

EDCS-47200120.translation: To the son of Augustus and Otacilia Severa Augusta, his wife, by the republic of the colony of Thamugadi.
EDCS-47200120.summary: This is a dedication by the republic of Thamugadi to the son of Emperors Philip the Arab and his wife Otacilia Severa.

EDCS-47200121.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius, Invictus, and to Herennia Etruscilla, Augusta, wife of our Augustus, by the republic of the colony of Thamugadensium.
EDCS-47200121.summary: This is a dedication by the republic of Thamugadensium to Emperor Decius and his wife Herennia Etruscilla.

EDCS-47200123.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus.
EDCS-47200123.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Aurelian.

EDCS-47200124.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Claudius Tacitus, Invictus, Pius, Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-47200124.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Tacitus, likely from the reign of Tacitus (275-276 AD).

EDCS-47200125.translation: To the Emperors Caesars Marcus Aurelius Carinus and Marcus Aurelius Numerianus, Invicti, Pii, Felices, Augusti.
EDCS-47200125.summary: This is a dedication to the co-emperors Carinus and Numerian, sons of Carus, likely from the period 283-284 AD.

EDCS-47200126.translation: To the Eternal Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Diocletianus, Invictus, Pius, Felix Augustus.
EDCS-47200126.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Diocletian, likely from the early part of his reign (284-305 AD).

EDCS-47200127.translation: To our Lord Emperor Caesar Maximinus, Invictus.
EDCS-47200127.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Maximinus Thrax, likely from the period 235-238 AD.

EDCS-47200128.translation: To the princes Valentinian and Valens.
EDCS-47200128.summary: This is a dedication to the co-emperors Valentinian I and Valens, who ruled from 364 to 375 AD.

EDCS-47200129.translation: To our Lords Emperors Valentinian, Theodosius, and Arcadius, Augusti.
EDCS-47200129.summary: This is a dedication to the co-emperors Valentinian II, Theodosius I, and Arcadius, likely from the period 383-392 AD.

EDCS-47200130.summary: This is a general acclamation to the reigning emperors, likely from the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-47200131.translation: To the proconsul, from the state of the colony of Thamugadi.
EDCS-47200131.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by the state of the colony of Thamugadi to a proconsul.

EDCS-47200140.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, consul for the second time, proconsul, and to Herennia Etruscilla, wife of our Augustus.
EDCS-47200140.summary: This inscription is a titulature for Emperor Trajan Decius and his wife Herennia Etruscilla, likely erected by the state of Thamugadi given the context of the other inscriptions.

EDCS-47200142.translation: From the state of the colony of Thamugadi to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-47200142.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the state of the colony of Thamugadi to Emperor Philip the Arab.

EDCS-47200345.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Victoria lived 25 years. Her sons made this for their mother.
EDCS-47200345.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Victoria, set up by her sons.

EDCS-47200346.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Albinus lived 3 months. (Erected by) Stiala.
EDCS-47200346.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Albinus, who died at three months old, erected by Stiala.

EDCS-47200347.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Annius Sanctus and Apronia Venusta made this for themselves, and for their dearest children and their descendants.
EDCS-47200347.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the self-dedication by Marcus Annius Sanctus and Apronia Venusta for themselves and their descendants.

EDCS-47200348.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Antonius Felix made this for himself and his descendants during his lifetime. He lived 70 years.
EDCS-47200348.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the self-dedication by Antonius Felix for himself and his descendants, who lived to be 70.

EDCS-47200349.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Antonia Ianuaria, daughter, lived 3 years. Her father Octavius Victor made this.
EDCS-47200349.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Ianuaria, who died at age three, set up by her father Octavius Victor.

EDCS-47200351.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Caecilius Felix lived 36 years. His most devoted wife Venusta made this.
EDCS-47200351.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Caecilius Felix, erected by his devoted wife Venusta.

EDCS-47200352.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelia Saturna lived 30 years. Her mother Perogi made this.
EDCS-47200352.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Saturna, erected by her mother Perogi.

EDCS-47200353.translation: Decimia, daughter of Decimus, lived 31 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-47200353.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Decimia, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-47200354.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Donatus commissioned the monument.

EDCS-47200355.translation: Egnatius Hospes made this for himself during his lifetime.
EDCS-47200355.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Egnatius Hospes, commissioned by himself while still alive.

EDCS-47200356.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits of Egnatius Victor.
EDCS-47200356.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirit of Egnatius Victor.

EDCS-47200357.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Egnatia Fortunata lived 60 years, having deserved well of all good people. Vulcacius Saturninus, a veteran of the Third Pious Vindicating Legion, made this for his worthy wife.
EDCS-47200357.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Vulcacius Saturninus for his wife Egnatia Fortunata, recording her age at death and her good character.

EDCS-47200358.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Fabia Fortunata lived 18 years.
EDCS-47200358.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabia Fortunata, recording her age at death.

EDCS-47200359.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus Staberius [made this] for his uncle Publius Fabricius Donatus.
EDCS-47200359.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Quintus Staberius dedicated this monument to his uncle, Publius Fabricius Donatus.

EDCS-47200360.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Felicus lived 11 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-47200360.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Felicus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-47200361.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Titus Flavius Demetrianus lived 28 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-47200361.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Demetrianus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-47200362.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Titus Flavius Proculus lived 60 years; he is buried here. His sons made this for their dutiful father.
EDCS-47200362.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by the sons for their father Titus Flavius Proculus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-47200363.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Fulcinius Mercator lived for 60 years and is buried here. During his lifetime, he made this for himself and for his wife, Sittia Satura.
EDCS-47200363.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifetime construction of a tomb by Lucius Fulcinius Mercator for himself and his wife.

EDCS-47200364.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Geminius Saturninus made this for himself and lived for 110 years.
EDCS-47200364.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifetime construction of a tomb by Lucius Geminius Saturninus.

EDCS-47200365.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Hammonius Privatus lived for 71 years.
EDCS-47200365.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Hammonius Privatus.

EDCS-47200366.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius...
EDCS-47200366.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for a person named Iulius.

EDCS-47200367.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucilla Paulina.

EDCS-47200368.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Luria Satu...
EDCS-47200368.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Luria Satu.

EDCS-47200369.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcia Proc... her son for his mother.
EDCS-47200369.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph likely commemorating Marcia Proc, likely made by her son.

EDCS-47200370.translation: Caius Mi Processus made this for himself and his descendants during his lifetime.
EDCS-47200370.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifetime construction of a tomb by Caius Mi Processus for himself and his descendants.

EDCS-47200371.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Plotius Crestus lived well for 53 years and Pin...
EDCS-47200371.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Lucius Plotius Crestus and possibly another individual.

EDCS-47200372.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For his father, Lucius Plotius Nonianus, who lived 60 years, and for his mother, Antonia Capitolina.
EDCS-47200372.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicating a monument to Lucius Plotius Nonianus and Antonia Capitolina, likely commissioned by their son.

EDCS-47200373.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pomponia Saturnina, dutiful, lived 42 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-47200373.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pomponia Saturnina, recording her name, piety, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-47200374.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Redi lived [some number of] years.
EDCS-47200374.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Redi, recording the dedication to the departed and the deceased's name and age.

EDCS-47200375.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cupula Canbo, lived 3 years. Sabinus made (this).
EDCS-47200375.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cupula Canbo, likely a child, recording the dedication, name, age, and maker, possibly her father Sabinus.

EDCS-47200376.translation: To Saturnina, her dutiful son Thelep[---]
EDCS-47200376.summary: A fragmentary inscription possibly commemorating Saturnina, mentioning her son Thelep. The exact nature of the monument is unclear.

EDCS-47200377.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sentia Satura, who lived 39 years, Lucius Aelius Donatus [erected this] to his most deserving wife, with whom he lived 21 years, 9 months, 11 days, without worry.
EDCS-47200377.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sentia Satura, erected by her husband Lucius Aelius Donatus, recording her age at death and the duration of their marriage.

EDCS-47200378.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextia Gellia, aged 90, is buried here.
EDCS-47200378.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextia Gellia, recording her name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-47200379.translation: Marcus Valerius Crs lived 21 years, 6 months.
EDCS-47200379.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Crs, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-47200380.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Valerius Pulcher, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, a dutiful grandfather, lived 87 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-47200380.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Pulcher, recording his name, tribal affiliation, description as a grandfather, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-47200381.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Fusca lived 27 years. Volcacius Thevestinus made (this) for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-47200381.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Fusca, erected by her husband Volcacius Thevestinus, recording her age at death and his devotion.

EDCS-47200382.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Victorinus Pius, who lived 12 days; he is buried here.
EDCS-47200382.summary: A funerary epitaph for Victorinus Pius, recording the dedication, name, and extremely short lifespan, indicating an infant burial.

EDCS-47200383.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Ulpius Felix, freedman, lived 60 years. Ulpia Tyche made this for her deserving husband. Lived
EDCS-47200383.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and death of Marcus Ulpius Felix, commissioned by his wife Ulpia Tyche.

EDCS-47200384.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Cornelia Fortunata.

EDCS-47200387.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Sextus Pedius Luciscus, son of Tarentinus, of the Arnensian tribe, lived 80 years.
EDCS-47200387.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Sextus Pedius Luciscus, son of Tarentinus.

EDCS-47200388.translation: Rufinus, heir of the Laelian estate.
EDCS-47200388.summary: This inscription identifies Rufinus as the heir of the Laelian estate, likely related to property or inheritance.

EDCS-47200414.translation: May this be done well and fortunately. Sacred to the great, nocturnal, Molchomor god Saturn. Aulus Quintius Victor and Aelia Rufina, his wife, gladly fulfilled their vow for their son Impetratus, as a vicarious ram.
EDCS-47200414.summary: This dedication records a votive offering made by Aulus Quintius Victor and Aelia Rufina to the god Saturn for their son Impetratus.

EDCS-47400294.summary: This inscription likely refers to a property or monument belonging to Publius Antistius Valerius.

EDCS-47400296.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Severus.

EDCS-47400297.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Safinius Rufinus lived 40 years.
EDCS-47400297.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Marcus Safinius Rufinus.

EDCS-47400299.summary: This inscription records a sum of 10,000 sesterces contributed by an individual referred to as 'our man'.

EDCS-47400300.translation: Gaius Julius Victata, with his own money.
EDCS-47400300.summary: This inscription indicates that Gaius Julius Victata funded something with his personal funds.

EDCS-47400301.translation: Lucius Cornelius Candidus, Lucius Nonius Victor, Lucius Barsius Victor, Tuburnica, Publius Aelius Crispinus, Caius Geminius Saturninus, Lucius Naerius Maximus, Caius Umbrius Felix, Quintus Gavius Saturninus (from the camp at Carthage).
EDCS-47400301.summary: This inscription lists the names of several individuals, likely soldiers or officials, associated with military camps or locations in Carthage, possibly dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-47400302.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Iulius Pertinax, son of Quintus, lived 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-47400302.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Iulius Pertinax, recording his name, parentage, age at death, and burial, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-47400303.translation: To Basilius, for his brother Fortunatus, (he) lived 23 years, (dedicated) by his parents.
EDCS-47400303.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a dedication, likely funerary, to Fortunatus by his parents, noting his age at death, probably from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-47400304.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ulpius Crementinus lived 3 years.
EDCS-47400304.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Ulpius Crementinus, recording his age at death, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-47400305.translation: Fortunatus the freedman, formerly an optio, lived 28 years, and Septimia Prima (dedicated this) to their most precious son.
EDCS-47400305.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of a son, dedicated by his parents, one of whom, Fortunatus, held a military rank (optio), likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-47400374.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Aelius Saturninus and Aelius Donatus, sons, (placed this) for their most dutiful father, Marcus Aemilius Donatus, son of Marcus Aemilius Macro, a legate of the emperor with praetorian power and designated consul, from Cuicul, who deserved well of them, with permission they placed and dedicated (this). He is buried here.
EDCS-47400374.summary: This elaborate funerary epitaph honors Marcus Aemilius Donatus, son of a prominent official, erected by his sons, and likely dates to the late Roman imperial period.

EDCS-47500641.translation: Publius Iulius Iunianus, son of Caius, (dedicated this) to Marcus Aemilius Macro, son of Marcus Aemilius Macro, legate of the emperor with praetorian power and designated consul, his son and patron.
EDCS-47500641.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Publius Iulius Iunianus to Marcus Aemilius Macro, who held a high imperial office and was the patron of the dedicator, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-47500669.translation: By order of the blessed Muntanus, Purpurius made (this) from the workshop of Donatus.
EDCS-47500669.summary: This is a maker's inscription, indicating that Purpurius produced a work from the workshop of Donatus, under the order of Muntanus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-47501080.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Valerius Victor, son (of Lucius), lived 6 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-47501080.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child, Lucius Valerius Victor, recording his age at death and burial, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-47501081.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Clodius Maximus the younger lived 33 years.
EDCS-47501081.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Clodius Maximus the younger, recording his age at death, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-47501082.translation: Caelia Prima lived X years. Caelia Gargilia lived X years. Caelia Gli [lived X years].
EDCS-47501082.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription recording the lifespans of at least three individuals: Caelia Prima, Caelia Gargilia, and Caelia Gli.

EDCS-48100004.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 19th time, Imperator for the 3rd time, Consul for the 4th time.
EDCS-48100004.summary: This is the titulature of Emperor Caracalla, dating from his 19th year of tribunician power (211-212 AD), listing his imperial titles and ancestry.

EDCS-48600069.translation: Titus Avidius Apollinaris, a medic of the I Flavia cohort, pious in his own affairs, lived 60 years. Caius Herennius Celsus, a friend, took care of you. I ask you, as you pass by, to say: May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-48600069.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Avidius Apollinaris, a medic, with a dedication from his friend Caius Herennius Celsus, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-49800002.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus, the Invincible, Pious, Happy Augustus.
EDCS-49800002.summary: This is an inscription honoring Emperor Aurelian, likely from the period of his reign (270-275 AD).

EDCS-49900102.translation: To Celsus, who gave his vows for himself to emulate.
EDCS-49900102.summary: This is a dedication to a person named Celsus, likely a deity or a revered individual, with a mention of vows, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-49900103.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Valerius Felix lived 57 years. He is here.
EDCS-49900103.summary: This is a standard funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Felix, providing his name, age at death, and a common invocation to the Manes, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-49900104.translation: Licinius Vitalis, son of Valerius Namfamo. Inventus Fabius Donatus. Aelius Maior. Salus, son of Quitus Rogatus. Maximus Iscitus. Livius Saturnus. Valerius Ius. Fo Secundius. Tius Ma. Utorius. Ius Sat. Ius Get. Herennius Rogatus. Marcus Valerius Felix. Publius Annius Faustus. Herennius Marcellus. Publius Annius Ianuarius, priest of Pluto. Marcus Herennius Rogatus. Caius Valerius Viator. Caius Sallustius Secundus.
EDCS-49900104.summary: This inscription lists the names of numerous individuals, including a priest of Pluto, Marcus Herennius Rogatus, suggesting a collective dedication or a list of members of a collegium or association, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51500031.translation: To the flamen perpetuus who held all honors, M.
EDCS-51500031.summary: This is a dedication to a perpetual flamen who had held all honors, possibly a priest of the imperial cult, the inscription is fragmentary and likely dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51500125.translation: To the divine powers on the right and left, Karminius.
EDCS-51500125.summary: This is a dedication to divine powers, mentioning a donor named Karminius, likely related to a sanctuary or shrine, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51500127.translation: The vow which Manlia Pudentilla, a most distinguished woman and also flaminica, had made while alive with her husband Lucius Valerius Maximus; Publius Flavius Pudens, a most distinguished man, her son, and Lucius Valerius Maximus, centurion of the legion and decurion of the colony of Thamugadenses, and Lucia Valeria Maximilla, daughter and foster-child of the fatherland, in their memory and filial piety placed [this monument] to the genius of their fatherland.
EDCS-51500127.summary: This is a dedication by a family for their deceased parents and for the genius of their fatherland, mentioning prominent individuals and civic roles within the colony of Thamugadensium, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-51500128.translation: Caius' slave and Quintus, son of Caius, Flavius made [this]; the contractor of the city completed the work.
EDCS-51500128.summary: This is a building inscription recording the completion of work by a contractor, commissioned by Caius' slave and Quintus, son of Caius, Flavius.

EDCS-52200583.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Vitalis lived 10 years. Iulia made [this] for the most innocent.
EDCS-52200583.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commemorating Iulius Vitalis, who died at age 10, and was honored by Iulia.

EDCS-52200584.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Papiria Iulia lived 23 years. Caius Sabinius Felix, a sesquiplicarius (centurion of the second rank) and standard-bearer, made [this] for his most innocent wife.
EDCS-52200584.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Papiria Iulia, who died at age 23, dedicated by her husband, Caius Sabinius Felix, who was a military officer.

EDCS-52200591.translation: Son of the deified Antoninus Magnus Pius.
EDCS-52200591.summary: This inscription is a fragment, likely from a monument, identifying someone as the son of the deified Emperor Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-53500010.translation: We can celebrate with solemn art and relate all things, but we cannot overcome the decrees of men, nor those which Fate has established and gods cannot alter. Now, because piety compels me, weeping for a father's inheritance, I establish this tomb for you, my worthy son, whom I bore from a pious mother, and by whose name I declared you as Ianuarius, whom I had wished to be my survivor.
EDCS-53500010.summary: This is a funerary epitaph of a father lamenting the death of his son, Ianuarius, and dedicating a tomb, possibly dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-54800021.translation: Charito dedicated the temple of Saturn.
EDCS-54800021.summary: This is a dedication inscription recording that Charito dedicated a temple to the god Saturn.

EDCS-54800027.translation: May what has been done be good, fortunate, and happy. To the Holy Lord, a great nocturnal offering from a vision, sacred.
EDCS-54800027.summary: This is a religious inscription recording a sacred offering made to a 'Holy Lord' in response to a vision.

EDCS-54800030.translation: Publius Aelius Aprilis, priest, and Publius Aelius Primus, priest, made [this].
EDCS-54800030.summary: This is a dedication inscription by two priests, Publius Aelius Aprilis and Publius Aelius Primus.

EDCS-54800031.summary: This is a dedication inscription by a priest named Quintus Papius Optatus.

EDCS-54800032.summary: This is a dedication or honorific inscription identifying Lucius Ranius as a priest.

EDCS-54800042.summary: This is a dedication to the gods Saturn and Augustus.

EDCS-54800044.translation: To Augustus, Saturn. Lucius Aegrilius Honoratus, priest, and his wife Aufidia Victoria, and their son Lucius Aegrilius Secundus, priest.
EDCS-54800044.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Saturn and Augustus by a priest, his wife, and their son, who is also a priest.

EDCS-54800063.translation: Gaius Nonius, priest of Saturn, gladly paid his vow. With good things, well.
EDCS-54800063.summary: This inscription records a priest of Saturn, Gaius Nonius, fulfilling a vow and expressing his good wishes.

EDCS-54800064.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. The priest of Saturn lived 90 years.
EDCS-54800064.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a priest of Saturn who lived to be 90 years old.

EDCS-55701660.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or donation related to the Plotian Senate House.

EDCS-55701661.translation: cessus, pontifex, dedicated a silver statue of Jupiter Optimus Maximus Augustus, which he promised for the honor of his pontificate, at a cost of 10,000 sesterces, and a golden image of our lord Pius Felix Augustus, also for 10,000 sesterces, from the offerings for the order and the curiales; having again given ten thousand sesterces when the same occurred, from the interest of which on the eleventh day before the Kalends of April, which was celebrated, he gave 2,000 sesterces...
EDCS-55701661.summary: This inscription details significant financial contributions and dedications by a pontifex, likely named Celsus, for religious and civic honors, including silver and gold statues and funds for the curiales, indicating a high status and generosity.

EDCS-55800164.translation: To Titus Hellenius, son of Titus, from Ulpia Concesso, his wife, to her husband, chief of the foreigners, primipilaris of the III Augusta legion.
EDCS-55800164.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by a wife for her husband, who held significant military and administrative positions.

EDCS-58000090.translation: Paulina, the eighth day before the Ides of December.
EDCS-58000090.summary: This inscription records a name and a date, possibly a death or commemoration.

EDCS-58200001.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the departed. Caelia Datulla lived 35 years. Caesius Urbanus made this.
EDCS-58200001.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Caesius Urbanus for his wife Caelia Datulla, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-58200006.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. Lucius Aemilius Celsus lived 25 years. Celsi.
EDCS-58200006.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Lucius Aemilius Celsus, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-58200008.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Parthian, greatest Briton, greatest German, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 17th time, imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, proconsul. The Lamasbenses [distance of] 15 miles.
EDCS-58200008.summary: This is an imperial dedication likely associated with a milestone or boundary marker, honoring Emperor Caracalla and indicating a distance of 15 miles from the Lamasbenses.

EDCS-58200009.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Livia Matidia lived 10 years.
EDCS-58200009.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life span of Livia Matidia.

EDCS-58200041.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Ilius Faustus lived 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-58200041.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life span of Ilius Faustus and stating his burial place.

EDCS-58200042.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caelia Ianuaria lived 25 years. Antonius Victor, her husband, made this.
EDCS-58200042.summary: A funerary inscription set up by a husband for his deceased wife, Caelia Ianuaria.

EDCS-58200043.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life span of Marcus Severus Iader.

EDCS-58200044.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Petronius Catullinus lived 75 years. His wife Canneia Silvana made this and dedicated it willingly with good heart; he is buried here.
EDCS-58200044.summary: A funerary inscription set up by a wife for her deceased husband, Caius Petronius Catullinus.

EDCS-58200045.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered, Ever Augustus.
EDCS-58200045.summary: An imperial dedication honoring Emperor Constantine I.

EDCS-58200046.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Constantinus, the Younger, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-58200046.summary: An inscription dedicated to the Caesar Flavius Claudius Constantinus Iunior (Constantine II).

EDCS-58200048.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Aelia Maxima lived 12 years.
EDCS-58200048.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life span of Aelia Maxima.

EDCS-58200049.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Ap Felix lived 5 years.
EDCS-58200049.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life span of Ap Felix.

EDCS-58200053.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Aterius Ianuarius lived for 64 years. He made statues for his wife, Caelia Victorina, his son, Aterius Clodianus, and his grandson, Urbanus, during his own lifetime for them.
EDCS-58200053.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Lucius Aterius Ianuarius for himself and his family, dated to his lifetime.

EDCS-58200054.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Aelia Rufina lived for 75 years. Her husband made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-58200054.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a husband for his wife, Aelia Rufina.

EDCS-58200055.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Senius Cassianus lived for 22 years.
EDCS-58200055.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Publius Senius Cassianus.

EDCS-58200056.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Octavius Thevestinus lived for 60 years and 7 months. His son, Octavius Maius, made this at his own expense.
EDCS-58200056.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Octavius Maius for his father, Quintus Octavius Thevestinus.

EDCS-58200057.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Donata lived for 70 years. Iulius Montanus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-58200057.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Iulius Montanus for his wife, Iulia Donata.

EDCS-58200058.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Domatorosi made this for his deserving son, Iulio Matrono, who lived for 35 years. Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. [He] lived for 80 years.
EDCS-58200058.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Domatorosi for his son, Iulio Matrono, and also records Domatorosi's own lifespan.

EDCS-58200059.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Getulicus lived for 73 years. His son, Aelius Processus, set this up for his deserving father.
