EDCS-58200059.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Aelius Processus for his father, Aelius Getulicus.

EDCS-58200060.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Volussia Tertulla lived for 45 years. Fabius Fortunatus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-58200060.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Fabius Fortunatus for his wife, Volussia Tertulla.

EDCS-58200061.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Messius Iatus' heirs made this for their deserving kinsman, who lived for 56 years.
EDCS-58200061.summary: This is a funerary dedication from the heirs of Messius Iatus for him.

EDCS-58200062.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Thevestina lived for 75 years.
EDCS-58200062.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Iulia Thevestina.

EDCS-58200063.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Julius Aeginus lived 42 years. He set this up for himself while alive.
EDCS-58200063.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julius Aeginus, who set up his own tomb in his lifetime.

EDCS-58200065.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aelia Ninis lived 52 years.
EDCS-58200065.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Ninis, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-58200066.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Licinia Fortunata lived 80 years. Her heirs made this for their deserving mother.
EDCS-58200066.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Licinia Fortunata, set up by her heirs.

EDCS-58200067.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Furia Felicula lived 85 years. Valerius Mansuetus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-58200067.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Furia Felicula, set up by her husband Valerius Mansuetus.

EDCS-58200069.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Cattina lived 53 years. Flavius Victor made this for his most dutiful mother.
EDCS-58200069.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Cattina, set up by her son Flavius Victor.

EDCS-58200070.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Volussinia MA ini lived [lacuna] years.
EDCS-58200070.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Volussinia MA ini, with a missing lifespan and possibly more information.

EDCS-58200071.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Aelius Tuaes lived 55 years. His heirs made this for him as deserving.
EDCS-58200071.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Aelius Tuaes, set up by his heirs.

EDCS-58200072.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Sentius Servandus made this for his deserving wife, Aelia Antonia, who lived 53 years.
EDCS-58200072.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Antonia, set up by her husband Lucius Sentius Servandus.

EDCS-58200073.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aelius A EPD us lived 6 years. Aelius Felix made and dedicated this for his deserving son.
EDCS-58200073.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the child Aelius A EPD us, set up by his father Aelius Felix.

EDCS-58200074.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Dziogaminus lived 80 years. Titus Flavius Lucianus made this monument for his father at his own expense.
EDCS-58200074.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Dziogaminus, set up by his son Titus Flavius Lucianus.

EDCS-58200075.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed, in memory and devotion to father Caesianus.
EDCS-58200075.summary: This is a funerary dedication honoring the deceased father Caesianus.

EDCS-58200076.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valerius Victor lived 11 years. Valeria Felicitas lived 16 years. Valerius Lucidus made and dedicated this for his children.
EDCS-58200076.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of Valerius Victor and Valeria Felicitas and was made by Valerius Lucidus for his children.

EDCS-58200077.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sentia Fortunata lived 92 years. Castus.
EDCS-58200077.summary: This funerary inscription states that Sentia Fortunata lived 92 years and was made by Castus.

EDCS-58200079.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Annia Iuliana, freedwoman of Publius, lived 50 years. T. Martialus, a veteran, lived 81 years. The son made and dedicated this for his deserving parents.
EDCS-58200079.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of Annia Iuliana and T. Martialus, and was made by their son for his parents.

EDCS-58200080.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. IUB onus lived 80 years. Antonia Romana made this from her own means.
EDCS-58200080.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of IUB onus and was made by Antonia Romana.

EDCS-58200083.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Monima lived 41 years. Her devoted sons made this.
EDCS-58200083.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Iulia Monima and was made by her sons.

EDCS-58200084.translation: For himself and his descendants. Lived 40 years. Made for the incomparable one.
EDCS-58200084.summary: This funerary inscription was made for the deceased, who lived 40 years, and was intended for himself and his descendants.

EDCS-58200085.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Maria Castim, wife of Baricus. Lived for... years.
EDCS-58200085.summary: This funerary inscription is for Maria Castim, wife of Baricus, but the age is missing.

EDCS-58200086.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia C. Lived 70 years. Primus made this for his deserving mother. She is buried here.
EDCS-58200086.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Iulia C. and was made by her son Primus for his deserving mother, who is buried here.

EDCS-58200087.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sextus Humanius Baricus lived 95 years.
EDCS-58200087.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Sextus Humanius Baricus.

EDCS-58200088.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pompeius Ucius, veteran, lived 75 years.
EDCS-58200088.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pompeius Ucius, a veteran, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-58200089.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Vibidius Donatus lived 70 years; Geminia Successa [erected this] for her husband.
EDCS-58200089.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Publius Vibidius Donatus and states that Geminia Successa erected it for her husband.

EDCS-58200090.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For the memory of Iulia Extricata, mother. Clodia Extricata, daughter, and Magnius Abagalianus, son-in-law, made [this].
EDCS-58200090.summary: This funerary inscription records that Clodia Extricata and Magnius Abagalianus dedicated this monument to Iulia Extricata, their mother and mother-in-law.

EDCS-58200092.translation: To Aemilia Paul, pious, faithful. Lucius Iulius Felix, an equestrian, [erected this] for his wife, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-58200092.summary: This funerary dedication was made by Lucius Iulius Felix, an equestrian, to his wife Aemilia Paul, praising her virtues.

EDCS-58200094.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Of Opstorius Felix, who lived 75 years.
EDCS-58200094.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Opstorius Felix, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-58200095.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Aemilius Rufus lived 23 years.
EDCS-58200095.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Aemilius Rufus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-58200096.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Gallus Ammonae lived 80 years; he made [this] in his lifetime.
EDCS-58200096.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Iulius Gallus Ammonae and notes that he erected the monument himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-58200098.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. I, Caecilius Victor, made [this] for my wife Petronia Fortunata, with me myself procuring [the materials].
EDCS-58200098.summary: This funerary inscription records that Caecilius Victor erected a monument for his wife Petronia Fortunata, who was involved in its creation.

EDCS-58200099.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Frontias lived 10 years; he lies here.
EDCS-58200099.summary: This funerary epitaph records the short lifespan of Frontias, who is buried at this location.

EDCS-58200100.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Appius Donatus lived 50 years. Iulia Rogata, his wife, and their sons made and dedicated this monument to their most deserving husband.
EDCS-58200100.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Iulia Rogata and her sons for Caius Appius Donatus, honoring his deserving character.

EDCS-58200101.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Successa lived for 41 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-58200101.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Iulia Successa.

EDCS-58200102.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Octavius Felix lived for 80 years; his deserving wife erected this.
EDCS-58200102.summary: A funerary inscription for Octavius Felix, erected by his wife.

EDCS-58200103.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavi[us] Felix lived for 70 years.
EDCS-58200103.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Flavi[us] Felix.

EDCS-58200114.translation: The emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, restored the milestones, which had fallen into ruin due to age.
EDCS-58200114.summary: An inscription from the reign of Emperor Caracalla, recording the restoration of milestones.

EDCS-58200118.translation: The emperor Caesar Marcus Aurellius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, son of the deified Magnus Antoninus, [restored/erected] milestones.
EDCS-58200118.summary: An inscription from the reign of Emperor Caracalla, likely recording the restoration or erection of milestones.

EDCS-58200119.translation: The emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-58200119.summary: An inscription bearing the titulature of Emperor Gordian III.

EDCS-58200120.translation: The emperor Caesar Marcus Claudius Tacitus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-58200120.summary: An inscription bearing the titulature of Emperor Tacitus.

EDCS-58200121.translation: The emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Probus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul.
EDCS-58200121.summary: An inscription detailing the full imperial titles of Emperor Probus.

EDCS-58200122.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Iulius Fronto lived for 60 years; his brother Iulius Urbanus erected this memorial for his deserving brother.
EDCS-58200122.summary: A funerary inscription where a brother erects a memorial for his deceased sibling, Marcus Iulius Fronto.

EDCS-58200124.translation: During the consulship of the emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Invicto Pius Felix Augustus, the most illustrious high priest of the unconquered Sun god Elagabal, and [consuls], the matter concerning the waters, which had been entrusted to Valentinus and those to whom the matter was delegated, was resolved by decree of the order and the townspeople of Lamasba, that the water should flow as it is accustomed to flow when the spring flows, as shown by the diagram below of the flowing water, which has therefore not been distributed for the time being because inauspicious times have prevented it. Scale I: From the first hour of the day on the Kalends of October, when the Claudiana aqueduct descends to irrigate the reservoir, for Mattius Fortis, 308 'heads' [of water]; from the first hour of the day on the Kalends of October to the fifth and a half hour of the same day, for his share, 4 and a half hours. Flavius Adiutor, veteran, 350 'heads'; from the fifth and a half hour of the day on the Kalends of October to the tenth hour of the same day, for his share, 5 hours. The heirs of Apuleius Faustinus, 117 'heads'; from the tenth hour of the day on the Kalends of October to the first and a half hour of the night of the same day, for their share, 1 and a half hours. Apuleius Rogatianus, 110 'heads'; from the first and a half hour of the night on the Kalends of October to the third hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 1 and a half hours. Apuleius Africanus, 110 'heads'; from the first and a half hour of the night on the Kalends of October to the third hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 1 and a half hours. Apuleius Processus, 220 'heads'; from the third hour of the night on the Kalends of October to the sixth hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 3 hours. The heirs of Aelius Chrysi, 500 'heads'; from the sixth hour of the night on the Kalends of October to the first and a half hour of the day on the day before the Kalends of October, for their share, 7 and a half hours. Aemilius Secundus, 450 'heads'; from the first and a half hour of the day on the day before the Kalends of October to the eighth hour of the same day, for his share, 6 hours. Steminia Emerita, 400 'heads'; from the eighth hour of the day on the day before the Kalends of October to the second hour of the night of the same day, for her share, 6 hours. Aelius Felix, 200 'heads'; from the second hour of the night on the day before the Kalends of October to the fifth hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 3 hours. The heirs of Mari Saturnini, 200 'heads'; from the fifth hour of the night on the day before the Kalends of October to the eighth hour of the night of the same day, for their share, 3 hours. Maria Satura, 150 'heads'; from the eighth hour of the night on the day before the Kalends of October to the tenth hour of the night of the same day, for her share, 2 hours. The heirs of Mari Catullini, 150 'heads'; from the tenth hour of the night on the day before the Kalends of October to the twelfth hour of the night of the same day, for their share, 2 hours. Maria Donatula, 100 'heads'; from the twelfth hour of the night on the day before the Kalends of October to the first and a half hour of the day on the fifth day before the Kalends of October, for her share, 1 and a half hours. Marius Felix, 200 'heads'; from the first and a half hour of the day on the fifth day before the Kalends of October to the fourth and a half hour of the same day, for his share, 3 hours. 40 'heads'; from the fourth and a half hour of the day on the fifth day before the Kalends of October to the sixth hour of the same day, for his share, 1 and a half hours. [Number illegible] 'heads'; from the sixth hour of the day on the fifth day before the Kalends of October to the sixth and a half hour of the same day, for her share, half an hour. [Number illegible] 'heads'; from the sixth and a half hour of the day on the fifth day before the Kalends of October to the eleventh hour of the same day, for her share, 4 and a half hours. [Number illegible] 'heads'; from the eleventh hour of the day on the fifth day before the Kalends of October to the fifth hour of the night of the same day, for her share, 6 hours. [Number illegible] 'heads'; from the fifth hour of the night on the fifth day before the Kalends of October to the second hour of the day on the fourth day before the Kalends of October, for her share. Manilius Aufidianus, 260 'heads'; from the fourth hour of the night on the 14th day before the Kalends of November to the eighth hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 4 hours. The heirs of Manilius Rogatus, 780 'heads'; from the eighth hour of the night on the 14th day before the Kalends of November to the half hour of the night of the same day, with the Claudiana aqueduct ascending on the 13th day before the Kalends of November, for his share, 16 and a half hours. Octavia Donata, 406 'heads'; from the half hour of the night on the 13th day before the Kalends of November to the tenth hour of the night of the same day, for her share, 9 and a half hours. Flavius Fortis, veteran, 600 'heads'; from the tenth hour of the night on the 13th day before the Kalends of November to the half hour of the night on the 12th day before the Kalends of November, for his share, 14 and a half hours. The heirs of Manilius Rogatus, 600 'heads'; from the half hour of the night on the 12th day before the Kalends of November to the third hour of the day on the 11th day before the Kalends of November, for his share, 14 and a half hours. Sextilia Macrina, 600 'heads'; from the fourth hour of the day on the 11th day before the Kalends of November to the fifth and a half hour of the night of the same day, for her share, 14 and a half hours. Caius Publilius Valens, 550 'heads'; from the fifth and a half hour of the night on the 11th day before the Kalends of November to the sixth and a half hour of the day on the 10th day before the Kalends of November, for his share, 13 hours. Fuficius Messianus, 165 'heads'; from the sixth and a half hour of the day on the 10th day before the Kalends of November to the tenth and a half hour of the same day, for his share, 4 hours. Fuficius Felix and Priscianus, 360 'heads'; from the tenth and a half hour of the day on the 10th day before the Kalends of November to the seventh hour of the night of the same day, for their share, 8 and a half hours. Dentilius Senex, 300 'heads'; from the seventh hour of the night on the 10th day before the Kalends of November to the second hour of the day on the 9th day before the Kalends of November, for his share, 7 hours. Dentilius Maximus, 340 'heads'; from the second hour of the day on the 9th day before the Kalends of November to the tenth hour of the same day, for his share, 8 hours. Germania Castula, 803 'heads'; from the tenth hour of the day on the 9th day before the Kalends of November and the 8th day before the Kalends of November, when the Claudiana aqueduct descends, to the third hour of the day of the same day, for her share, 17 hours. Germanius Petronianus, 450 'heads'; from the third hour of the day on the 8th day before the Kalends of November to the ninth and a half hour of the same day, for his share, 6 and a half hours. Germanius Dentilianus, 440 'heads'; from the ninth and a half hour of the day on the 8th day before the Kalends of November to the fourth hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 6 and a half hours. Sextilius Emeritus, 250 'heads'; from the fourth hour of the night on the 8th day before the Kalends of November to the seventh and a half hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 3 and a half hours. Germanius Valentinus, 430 'heads'; from the seventh and a half hour of the night on the 8th day before the Kalends of November to the second hour of the day on the 7th day before the Kalends of November, for his share, 6 hours. Marius Honoratus, 102 'heads'; from the second hour of the day on the 7th day before the Kalends of November to the third and a half hour of the same day, for his share, 1 and a half hours. Scale III: Aemilius Secundus, 1300 'heads'; from the third and a half hour of the day on the 7th day before the Kalends of November to the third hour of the night on the 6th day before the Kalends of November, for the purpose of irrigating the reservoir, for his share, 35 and a half hours. Valerius Crassus, 1500 'heads'; from the third hour of the night on the 6th day before the Kalends of November to the first and a half hour of the night on the 5th day before the Kalends of November, 22 and a half hours. Laelius M., 400 'heads'; from the first and a half hour of the night on the 5th day before the Kalends of November to the seventh and a half hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 6 hours. [Number illegible] 'heads'; from the seventh and a half hour of the night on the 5th day before the Kalends of November of the same day, for his share, [Number illegible] hours. VIN kapita 4000; from the seventh and a half hour of the day on the 15th day before the Kalends of December and the 14th, 13th, 12th, and 12th, when the Claudiana aqueduct ascends, to the seventh hour of the day of the same day, for his share, 71 and a half hours. R., 1000 'heads'; from the seventh hour of the day on the 12th day before the Kalends of December to the seventh hour of the day on the 11th day before the Kalends of December, for his share, 24 hours. N tis., 918 'heads'; from the seventh hour of the day on the 11th day before the Kalends of December to the fifth hour of the day on the 10th day before the Kalends of December, for his share, 22 and a half hours. ANI., 700 'heads'; from the fifth hour of the day on the 10th day before the Kalends of December to the ninth and a half hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 16 and a half hours. x., 540 'heads'; from the ninth and a half hour of the night on the 10th day before the Kalends of December to the tenth and a half hour of the day on the 9th day before the Kalends of December, for his share, 13 hours. Appulei., 480 'heads'; from the tenth and a half hour of the day on the 9th day before the Kalends of December to the tenth hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 11 and a half hours. M., 655 'heads'; from the tenth hour of the night on the 9th day before the Kalends of December to the eighth and a half hour of the day on the 8th day before the Kalends of December, when the Claudiana aqueduct descends, for his share, 10 and a half hours. ius., 200 'heads'; from the eighth and a half hour of the day on the 8th day before the Kalends of December to the eleventh and a half hour of the same day, for his share, 3 hours. s Saturninus., 1300 'heads'; from the eleventh and a half hour of the day on the 8th day before the Kalends of December to the seventh hour of the day on the 7th day before the Kalends of December, for his share, 17 and a half hours. s Gallonius., 1210 'heads'; from the seventh hour of the day on the 7th day before the Kalends of December to the first hour of the day on the 6th day before the Kalends of December, for his share, 18 hours. tur., 848 'heads'; from the first hour of the day on the 6th day before the Kalends of December to the first and a half hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 12 and a half hours. Iuvenalis., 420 'heads'; from the first and a half hour of the night on the 6th day before the Kalends of November to the seventh and a half hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 6 hours. ntis., 400 'heads'; from the seventh and a half hour of the night on the 6th day before the Kalends of November to the first and a half hour of the day on the 5th day before the Kalends of November, for his share, 6 hours. Quadratus., 385 'heads'; from the first and a half hour of the day on the 5th day before the Kalends of November to the seventh hour of the same day, for his share, 5 and a half hours. Ianuarius., 360 'heads'; from the seventh hour of the day on the 5th day before the Kalends of November to the half hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 5 and a half hours. Rogatianus., 2065 'heads'; from the half hour of the night on the 5th day before the Kalends of November to the seventh and a half hour of the night on the 4th day before the Kalends of November, for his share, 31 hours. kastus., 730 'heads'; from the seventh and a half hour of the night on the 4th day before the Kalends of November to the sixth and a half hour of the day on the 3rd day before the Kalends of November, for his share, 11 hours. [Number illegible] 'heads'; from the sixth and a half hour of the day on the 3rd day before the Kalends of November to the second hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 7 and a half hours. [Number illegible] 'heads'; from the second hour of the night on the 3rd day before the Kalends of November to the tenth hour of the day on the day before the Kalends of December, for his share, 20 hours. [Number illegible] 'heads'; from the tenth hour of the day on the day before the Kalends of December to the third hour of the day on the Kalends of December, for his share, 17 hours. [Number illegible] 'heads'; from the third hour of the day on the Kalends of December to the third hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 12 hours. [Number illegible] 'heads'; from the third hour of the night on the Kalends of December to the eighth hour of the night of the same day, for his share, 5 hours. [Number illegible] 'heads'; from the eighth hour of the night on the Kalends of December to [the first hour of the day]. Julius Felix, veteran, who was at Furni, 600 'heads'; the heirs of Rutilius Lupus, 1100 'heads'; Cornelius Expectatus, 70 'heads'; Iunius Saturninus, veteran, who was at Nargududis, 650 'heads'; Germanius Valens, 663 'heads'; the heirs of Germanius Petronianus, 620 'heads'; Germanius Valentinus, 664 'heads'; Licinia Domitia, 900 'heads'; Trebius Barbarus, 206 olive trees; Publius Aemilius Rufinus, 481 olive trees; Scala Lollia Mustia, 155 'heads'; Iulia Victoria, 245 'heads'; Apuleius Rogatus, 150 'heads'; Caecilius Victor maior, 254 'heads'; Fuficius Felix and Priscianus, at the Messi reservoir, 600 'heads'; Quintus Caecilius Saturninus, 600 'heads'; Aelius Victor and Valeria Fortunata, 826 'heads'; the heirs of Septimius Felicionis, 631 'heads'; Iulius Fortunatus, 530 'heads'; the heirs of Iulius Petronianus, 530 'heads'; Claudius Euticianus, 840 'heads'.
EDCS-58200124.summary: This extensive inscription records the allocation and distribution of water from the Claudiana aqueduct at Lamasba, detailing the quantities and times for various individuals and families during a specific period around the Kalends of October and extending into December, likely related to irrigation needs.

EDCS-58200125.summary: This is a tombstone recording the short life of a girl named Victoria.

EDCS-58200126.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Munatius Ianuarius lived for 85 years. I made this while alive; it is my burial place.
EDCS-58200126.summary: This is a tombstone set up by Lucius Munatius Ianuarius for himself during his lifetime, recording his age at death.

EDCS-58300026.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Popilia Saturnia lived for 73 years.
EDCS-58300026.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of Popilia Saturnia.

EDCS-58300027.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Modius Namphamo lived for 75 years. His son made this.
EDCS-58300027.summary: This is a tombstone made by the son for Modius Namphamo, recording his age at death.

EDCS-58300028.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Vettius Zabo lived for 65 years. Vettius Victor, his son, made this for the deserving. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Clodia Victorina lived for 61 years. Vettius Victor, her son, made this for the deserving.
EDCS-58300028.summary: This is a tombstone with two inscriptions, likely for a father and daughter, both made by Vettius Victor, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-58300029.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Furia Fortunata lived for 43 years.
EDCS-58300029.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of Furia Fortunata.

EDCS-58300030.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Nonia Victoria lived for 60 years.
EDCS-58300030.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of Nonia Victoria.

EDCS-58300031.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gellia Spes lived for 80 years.
EDCS-58300031.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of Gellia Spes.

EDCS-58300032.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aelia Emerita lived for 80 years.
EDCS-58300032.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age at death of Aelia Emerita.

EDCS-58300033.translation: Fulvia Musta lived for 60 years. Her heirs made this.
EDCS-58300033.summary: This is a tombstone set up by the heirs for Fulvia Musta, recording her age at death.

EDCS-58300034.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Clodia Amata lived for 55 years. Her children made this.
EDCS-58300034.summary: This is a funerary monument (dis manibus) dedicated to Clodia Amata by her children, who also recorded her age at death.

EDCS-58300035.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Fulvius Monimus, also called Zabinius, lived for 80 years. His children made this.
EDCS-58300035.summary: This is a funerary monument (dis manibus) dedicated to Caius Fulvius Monimus by his children, who recorded his age at death and alternative name.

EDCS-58300036.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Fulvius Zabinneus, son of Caius Fulvius Zabinneus, lived for 69 years.
EDCS-58300036.summary: This is a funerary monument (dis manibus) for Caius Fulvius Zabinneus, recording his parentage and age at death.

EDCS-58300037.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Clodius Baric lived for 50 years. Clodius made this for his well-deserving [relative/friend].
EDCS-58300037.summary: This is a funerary monument (dis manibus) for Lucius Clodius Baric, recording his age at death and dedication by a relative or friend named Clodius.

EDCS-58300038.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Apronia Beata lived for 50 years.
EDCS-58300038.summary: This is a funerary monument (dis manibus) for Apronia Beata, recording her age at death.

EDCS-58300039.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Iulius Faustinus lived for 45 years.
EDCS-58300039.summary: This is a funerary monument (dis manibus) for Caius Iulius Faustinus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-58300040.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. His children made this for Lucio Cittio Ianuario, who lived for 72 years, their father.
EDCS-58300040.summary: This is a funerary monument (dis manibus) dedicated to Lucio Cittio Ianuario by his children, who recorded his age at death.

EDCS-58300041.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. His children made this for Caio Modius Barici, who lived for 15 years.
EDCS-58300041.summary: This is a funerary monument (dis manibus) dedicated to Caio Modius Barici by his children, who recorded his age at death.

EDCS-58300042.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. His brothers made this for Cittio Rogatus, their brother, who lived for 22 years.
EDCS-58300042.summary: This is a funerary monument (dis manibus) dedicated to Cittio Rogatus by his brothers, who recorded his age at death.

EDCS-58300043.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucianus and Flavianus, sons, made and dedicated this for their well-deserving Memmia Optata, who lived for 75 years, from their own means.
EDCS-58300043.summary: This is a funerary monument (dis manibus) dedicated to Memmia Optata by her sons, who recorded her age at death and specified the dedication was from their own resources.

EDCS-58400002.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Fabius Felix made this for Aurelia Ianuaria, his wife. She lived for 35 years.
EDCS-58400002.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a husband to his wife.

EDCS-58400003.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Iunius Iustus made this for his deserving mother, Capurnia Nabora, who lived for 85 years.
EDCS-58400003.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a son to his mother.

EDCS-58400006.translation: To Flavius Constantius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-58400006.summary: An inscription dedicating something to the Caesar Flavius Constantius, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-58400009.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Iulius Victor lived for approximately [---] years.
EDCS-58400009.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Victor, with an incomplete lifespan.

EDCS-58400010.translation: Dignus made this. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lived for 17 years.
EDCS-58400010.summary: A funerary epitaph for someone named Dignus, who lived for 17 years.

EDCS-58400011.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Vibius lived for 80 years.
EDCS-58400011.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Vibius, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-58400014.summary: An honorific inscription addressed to the ruling emperors, likely from the Roman Empire period.

EDCS-58400015.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, and to Iulia Augusta.
EDCS-58400015.summary: An imperial dedication to Emperor Caracalla and Empress Julia Domna.

EDCS-58400016.summary: An inscription by an individual named Granius, referring to himself as a god.

EDCS-58400020.summary: An inscription likely recording the origin or name of an individual named Secundus.

EDCS-58400032.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits of Ianuarius.
EDCS-58400032.summary: A funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits for Ianuarius.

EDCS-58400042.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the lifespan of Silicius Gudus.

EDCS-58400043.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the lifespan of Iulius Baric.

EDCS-58400044.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Gudulla lived 85 years.
EDCS-58400044.summary: A funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits for Gudulla, including her lifespan.

EDCS-58400045.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. I, Lucius, made a statue for my father, Publius Umbrius Felix, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-58400045.summary: A funerary monument where Lucius dedicated a statue for his father, Publius Umbrius Felix, stating his lifespan.

EDCS-58400046.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Saronia lived 75 years. Quintus Ateius Ierila lived 75 years. He made this for his wife.
EDCS-58400046.summary: A funerary inscription for a couple, Saronia and Quintus Ateius Ierila, stating their lifespans and that Quintus dedicated the monument to his wife.

EDCS-58400047.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Hail. The sons made this for [---] who lived 70 years.
EDCS-58400047.summary: A funerary dedication by sons for a person (name missing) who lived 70 years.

EDCS-58400048.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Silen lived 65 years. S. made this.
EDCS-58400048.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Silen, who lived 65 years, with the dedicator's initials 'S'.

EDCS-58400049.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulius Rogatus lived 75 years.
EDCS-58400049.summary: A funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits for Iulius Rogatus, including his lifespan.

EDCS-58400050.translation: To Concord of the Emperors. Quintus Aufidius Faustus, son of Quintus, aedile, twice holder of the office of duovir, quinquennalis, pontifex, because of exemption and perpetual [---], [---] the statue which he promised.
EDCS-58400050.summary: An inscription dedicated to the Emperors' Concord by Quintus Aufidius Faustus, a local magistrate, likely commemorating an honor or duty fulfilled.

EDCS-58400052.translation: In fulfillment of a vow, made with a willing heart, Hostilius Iulianus, formerly a frumentarius and now a beneficiary consularis, for the safety of our lord Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus Magnus Antoninus, son of the deified [emperor's father], his candidate.
EDCS-58400052.summary: This inscription is a votive offering dedicated by Hostilius Iulianus for the safety of Emperor Severus Alexander.

EDCS-58400053.translation: To the Pontifex Maximus, invested with tribunician power, Imperator for the fourth time, Consul for the second time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, the Supreme, Greatest, Holiest, Strongest, and Most Indulgent Prince, the colony [dedicates this].
EDCS-58400053.summary: This is a dedication, likely from a colony, to an emperor, listing his various titles and honors.

EDCS-58400055.translation: Metilius Verus, an ordinary centurion, his husband, erected this monument to the memory of Badia Datosa, an honorable and chaste matron.
EDCS-58400055.summary: This epitaph records the dedication of a tomb by Metilius Verus to his wife, Badia Datosa.

EDCS-58800037.translation: Lucius Considius Paulus of Rusicade, Caius Calventius Ianuarius from the camp, Aufidius Rufus of Lambaesa, Lucius Orbius Felix from the tribunes, Lucius Publicius Florentinus of Lambaesa, Caius Caecilius Felix of Bisica, Caius Iulius Dexter of Theveste, Fadius Dubitatus from Hadrumetum, Quintus Iulius Fructuosus from Carthage, Lucius Agrius Felix of Utica, Quintus Iulius Catulus of Lambaesa, Marcus Caesius Honoratus of Thamugada, Lucius Valerius Iulianus of Thamugada, Caius Aelius Iulianus from Sarmizegetusa, Marcus Valerius of Aquileia, Titus Aelius Victorinus of Siscia, Quintus Fulvius Natulus from Carthage, Caelius Victor from Hadrumetum, Marcus Iulius Proculus of Lambaesa, Marcus Aurelius Nicostratus of Tarsus, Publius Cornelius Victor of Cuicul, Lucius Fonteius Demetrianus from Mascula, Marcus Attius Pacatianus of Cirta, Veturius Vitalis of Lambaesa, Decimus Iunius Felix of Utica, Lucius Atilius Barbarus of Milev, Sextus Marcius Felix from Assura, Firmius Felix from the camp, Quintus Duronius Primus of Vaga, Publius Claudius Valentinus from Hadrumetum, Cornelius Claudianus of Lambaesa, Titus Flavius Fortunatus from Hadrumetum, Publius Egnatius Felix from Carthage, Lucius Valerius Niger of Thamugada, Caius Annius Iulianus from the camp, Marcus Septimius Tutianus from Carthage, Marcus Helvius Conductor from the camp, Caius Iulius Donatus from the camp, Titus Marcius Gemellus, Titus Aemilius Victor from Carthage, Quintus Salonius Repentinus of Thamugada, Publius Aelius Tauriscus of Sufetula, Furfanius Felix, Caius Iulius Felix of Thamugada, Valerius Daphnus, Lucius Clodius Concessus from Carthage, Quintus Iulius Victor of Thelepte, Sextus Iulius Felix of Theveste, who made the sacred golden images, under the supervision of Caius Memmius Victor, centurion of the III Augusta legion.
EDCS-58800037.summary: This lengthy inscription lists numerous individuals, including cornicularii, commentarienses, tribuni, speculatores, beneficiarii consulares, quaestores, and haruspices, who commissioned golden sacred images, with the work overseen by a centurion.

EDCS-59600005.summary: This is a very short inscription, likely a name on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-59800040.translation: For Aurelius Maximianus, a man of perfect standing, governor of the province of Numidia, patron of the fatherland. The work of the public square and the great bridge with their carved reliefs, which Caius Statulenius Vitalis Aquilinus, a Roman equestrian, augur of the Thamugadensian republic, had promised to his fatherland from his own generosity during his duumvirate, was completed and dedicated by him (Aquilinus); it was financed.
EDCS-59800040.summary: This inscription records the completion and dedication of public works, a square and a bridge, by Caius Statulenius Vitalis Aquilinus, a local official, in honor of the governor Aurelius Maximianus.

EDCS-60500084.translation: To our Lords Maximinus and Flavius Victor.
EDCS-60500084.summary: This is a dedication to two emperors, Maximinus and Flavius Victor.

EDCS-60500089.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavius Marcellus lived 29 years. Mucianus made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-60500089.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Flavius Marcellus, made by his father Mucianus.

EDCS-60500090.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Pacilius Mustianus lived 53 years. Quintus Pacilius Vitalis buried here for his most rare father.
EDCS-60500090.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Quintus Pacilius Mustianus, stating he lived 53 years and was buried by his son Quintus Pacilius Vitalis.

EDCS-60500091.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Mascel, a freedman, lived 26 years. Thizbe [made this].
EDCS-60500091.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Mascel, a freedman, made by Thizbe.

EDCS-60500092.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Attius Priscus lived 60 years. His heirs, together with his mother, set this up for their deserving father.
EDCS-60500092.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by the heirs and mother for Gaius Attius Priscus.

EDCS-60500095.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. His wife and sons made this for Gaius Postumius Donatus, dearest father. He lived 60 years.
EDCS-60500095.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by the wife and sons for Gaius Postumius Donatus.

EDCS-60500096.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Julius Donatus Victor and Felix made this for Spaniae Spanillae, mother. She lived 70 years.
EDCS-60500096.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Caius Julius Donatus Victor and Felix for their mother, Spaniae Spanillae.

EDCS-60500098.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cedianus lived 11 years. Martialis lived 30 years.
EDCS-60500098.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespans of Cedianus and Martialis.

EDCS-60500139.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-60500139.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Philip the Arab, likely on a public monument.

EDCS-63202246.translation: In the consulship of Maximinus II and Iovius Urbanus, on the Nones of May. The soldiers of the numerus Palmyrenorum made this. Faustus, a tesserarius of the aforementioned legion, a soldier of the aforementioned legion, wrote and carved it.
EDCS-63202246.summary: This inscription records the construction of something by soldiers of the numerus Palmyrenorum, overseen by Faustus, a tesserarius, during the consulship of Maximinus II and Iovius Urbanus (likely 227 AD).

EDCS-63202266.translation: To the Campestres deities. Marcus Celerinius Augendus, prefect of the cavalry of the Pannonian Severian ala, made this to their most holy numina on the second day before the Kalends of June.
EDCS-63202266.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Campestres deities by Marcus Celerinius Augendus, prefect of an ala, likely in the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-63300079.summary: This is a simple personal inscription, likely part of a larger monument or dedication, identifying Helvia as the daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-63300080.translation: For Mpiaps Tilia, while alive, for himself.
EDCS-63300080.summary: This is a dedication made by Mpiaps Tilia for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-63400139.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the safety of the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Sanctissimus Imperator and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus Nobilissimus Caesar Augusti, and of their entire divine house, an altar to Ceres. The vexillation of the Third Legion Augustan Pious Vindican, Maximinian, holding the camp of Dimmidi, devoted to their numen and majesty, under the supervision of Marcus Antonius Aquila, centurion of the same legion, consecrated this on the Vth day before the Kalends of May. S. Victor, trumpeter, and Marius Felix, Quintus Valerius Pomponius, Titus Aelius Silvanus, and Julius Primus, soldiers, wrote and carved this. [Many other soldiers' names follow, likely members of the vexillation or associated units].
EDCS-63400139.summary: This is a major dedication by a vexillation of the Legio III Augustae Piae Vindicis Maximinianae to Jupiter and Ceres for the safety of Emperors Maximinus I and Maximinus II, likely from the castellum Dimmidi around 236-238 AD.

EDCS-63400143.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Invictus Pius Felix Augustus and Julia Mammaea Augusta, mother of the Emperor and of the camp, and of the whole divine house, a detachment of Palmyrene soldiers stationed at the fort of Dimmidi made this, under the care of...
EDCS-63400143.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a detachment of Palmyrene soldiers at the fort of Dimmidi for the safety of Emperor Severus Alexander and his mother Julia Mammaea, likely dating to their reign (222-235 CE).

EDCS-63400148.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Considia Donata, daughter of Marcus, dutiful, lived for 17 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-63400148.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Considia Donata, recording her name, parentage, piety, age at death, and burial, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63400149.translation: Gaius Cu[---] Marcus Aurelius Lucius Suavius Lucius Junius Aulus Silicius Hilaris Titus Flavius Pacatianus Geminius Fortunatus Lucius Aurelius Incurianus Arruntius Ianuarius Gaius Caius Julius Marcus Aurelius Longinus Marcus Aurelius ci[---] Vibius II Castris Sergius Nasiub Fuscius Victorinus NP Aurelius Fortunatus of Carthage, equestrian, Lucius Aelius Constans, equestrian, Titus Manilius Saturninus, equestrian, Publius Valerius Bassus, equestrian, also equestrians of the ala Flavia, Aurelius Nicadas PEIAD, Aurelius Heraclio, M Aurelius Daris CVIAS, supervising, Vibius Ianuarius Caius, at the expense of the above-written, by vote.
EDCS-63400149.summary: This inscription lists numerous individuals, including equestrians from the ala Flavia, and records a construction or dedication made at their expense, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63400151.translation: [---] of the 7th cohort, [---] of Tubursi, Castris [---] Optatus Cillio Gracilis, Castris [---] Iovianus, Segermes [---] Steius Cillio Constans, Thamugadi Gudulus Mascula Felix, Abthugni[---]
EDCS-63400151.summary: This inscription appears to list names and possibly affiliations, possibly related to a military unit and locations like Thamugadi, with missing context.

EDCS-63400155.translation: Caecilius Victor, Gaius Julius Fortunatus, Gaius Modius Cal[---], Quintus C T[---]
EDCS-63400155.summary: This inscription lists several names, possibly of officials or members of a group, with incomplete information, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63400157.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that includes a personal name and patronymic, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63400159.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that mentions the 7th cohort, likely of military origin.

EDCS-66300012.translation: Ulpia Aristonica, daughter of Marcus, wife of Marcus Valerius Maximianus the consul, attendants.
EDCS-66300012.summary: This inscription identifies Ulpia Aristonica, daughter of Marcus and wife of the consul Marcus Valerius Maximianus, as attendants, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-67500057.translation: The state of the Thamugadensians set this up for Marcus Julius Quintianus, a man of most distinguished memory, on account of his munificence.
EDCS-67500057.summary: This inscription records a public monument erected by the city of Thamugadi in honor of Marcus Julius Quintianus for his generosity, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-67500058.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Antonius Zosimus lived 65 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-67500058.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Antonius Zosimus, recording his name, age at death, and a wish for his peaceful rest, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-67500059.translation: To the departed spirits. Flavia Saturnina lived 35 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-67500059.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Saturnina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-67500060.summary: A dedication to the departed spirits, the dedicant is not specified.

EDCS-67500061.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Iulia Soteris, freedwoman of Lucia, lived 65 years. She is placed here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-67500061.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Soteris, a freedwoman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-67500062.translation: To the departed spirits. Lollia Modesta lived 20 years. She is placed here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-67500062.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lollia Modesta, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-67500063.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Oppius Felix lived 55 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-67500063.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Oppius Felix, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-67500064.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Saterninus Maternus, son of Lucius S, from the voting tribe Quirina, lived 10 years.
EDCS-67500064.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Saterninus Maternus, a child, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-68400001.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titus Flavius Flavianus, aedile, quaestor, and duovir, made this.
EDCS-68400001.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by Titus Flavius Flavianus, who held several local magistracies, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-68400002.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Annius Protus, freedman of Marcus Annius, lived 50 years. He is buried here. Annia Africana lived 12 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-68400002.summary: A double funerary epitaph for Marcus Annius Protus and Annia Africana, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69100239.translation: Ianuarianus the glover gave 360 pairs of gloves. Brumasius gave 100 pairs. Cabcoitinus gave 250 pairs. Delile gave 50 pairs. Inus gave 100 pairs. [---] gave 61 pairs. [---] gave 100 pairs.
EDCS-69100239.summary: A list of glove donations by various individuals, possibly related to a collegium or religious festival, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69500195.translation: For the safety of our lord the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Father of the Fatherland, holding tribunician power, consul, proconsul, son of the deified Antoninus Pius, grandson of the deified Pius Severus, and of Julia Maesa Augusta, grandmother of our Augustus, mother of the camp and senate, and of Julia Soaemias Bassiana Augusta, mother of our Augustus, the duplicarii of the III Augusta Pia Vindex Antoniniana legion, devoted to their divine numen and majesty, having returned from a most successful eastern expedition: tesserarius Fabius, tesserarius Sallustius Faustinus, tesserarius Pompeius Datus, tubicen Calabius Fortunatus, tubicen Iulius Datus, tubicen Flavius Victor, tubicen Sittius Crescentinus, cornicen Sextilius Datus, cornicen Lurius Primus, bucinator Iulius Quintianus, bucinator Decimius Maximus, immunis Staberius Marcianus, immunis Aemilius Fuscianus, immunis Petronius Castus, immunis Iulius Ianuarius, armicustos Iulius Dexter, beneficiarius of the tribune Pettius Rogatus, beneficiarius of the tribune Annius Victor, mensor Iulius Felix, librarius Iulius Hebenus, polio Titinius Laurentinus, immunis Rutilius Catus, marsus Aurelius Leon, duplicarius Pomponius Repentinus, duplicarius Aquillius Hilarus, duplicarius Iulius Lucianus, duplicarius X, primus principalium Sextilius Ingenus, duplicarius Antonius Faustus, duplicarius Caecilius Samunio, duplicarius Valerius Saturninus, duplicarius Pomponius Honoratus, duplicarius Aemilius Masculus, duplicarius Annius Honoratus, duplicarius Lurius Rogatianus, duplicarius Aufidius Felix, duplicarius Iulius Victor, duplicarius Aemilius Martialis, duplicarius Manilius C, duplicarius Avianius Castus, duplicarius Geminius Peregrinus, duplicarius Statius Saturninus, duplicarius Iulius Gaianus, duplicarius Cornelius Saturninus, duplicarius Iulius Aprilis, duplicarius Anicius Rogatus, duplicarius Staberius Silvanus, duplicarius Aurelius Cittinus, duplicarius Caelius Verna, duplicarius Geminius Sucessus, duplicarius Gabinius Rogatianus, duplicarius Iulius Victor, duplicarius Iulius Victor, secutor duplicarius Iulius Felix, duplicarius Caecilius Felix, duplicarius Antistius Felix, duplicarius Marius Felix, duplicarius Cosconius Demetrianus, duplicarius Gargilius Saturninus, duplicarius Baebius Martialis, duplicarius Pomponius Secundus, duplicarius Crispius Silvanus, duplicarius Iulius Castus, duplicarius Aufidius Felix, duplicarius Flavius Optatus, duplicarius Helvius Felix, duplicarius Gargilius Urbanus, duplicarius Aufidius Crescens, duplicarius Pinarius Felix, duplicarius Iulius Maximus, duplicarius Aurelius Romanus, duplicarius Aurelius Felix, duplicarius Memmius Rogatus, duplicarius Lucilius Primus, duplicarius Tannonius Laetus, duplicarius Iunius Congius, duplicarius Petitius Catullinus, duplicarius Sallustius Ianuarius, duplicarius Aurelius Princeps, duplicarius Aemilius Rufus, duplicarius Flavius Verus, duplicarius Anneius Rogatus, duplicarius Hortensius Neptunalis, duplicarius Hortensius Verna, duplicarius Aurelius Victor, duplicarius Iulius Victor, duplicarius Aelius Saturninus, duplicarius Cornelius Victor, duplicarius Marius Saturninus, duplicarius Aemilius Fuscus, duplicarius Timinius Castus, duplicarius Iulius Rufinus, duplicarius Iulius Labracenus, duplicarius Emonius Rogatianus, duplicarius Pomponius Victor, duplicarius Valerius Felix, duplicarius Sittius Rogatus, duplicarius Aemilius Donatus, duplicarius Umbrius Victor, duplicarius Licinius Victor, duplicarius Hilarinius Pudens, duplicarius Claudius Ianuarius, duplicarius Iulius Saturninus, duplicarius Aelius Urbanus, duplicarius Arellius Fortunatus, duplicarius Aemilius Demetrianus, duplicarius Flavius Martialis, duplicarius Suellius Saturninus, duplicarius Arrius Vitalis, duplicarius Naevidius Felix, duplicarius Geminius Felix, duplicarius Glicius Victor, duplicarius Aponius Saturninus, duplicarius Tonneius Rogatianus, duplicarius Faonius Primus, duplicarius Areius Rogatus, duplicarius Aurelius Apollonius, duplicarius Octavius Rogatianus, Iulius Saturninus, Numerius Saturninus, Iulius Honoratus, duplicarius Caecilius Felix, duplicarius Annius Faustus, duplicarius Octavius Caelestinus, duplicarius Aemilius Tyrannus, duplicarius Iulius Emeritus, duplicarius Caelius Iovinus, duplicarius Licinius Maximus, duplicarius Iulius Augustalis, duplicarius Caius Iulius Fronto, duplicarius Gellius Fortunatus, duplicarius Lucius Octavius Felix, duplicarius Manilius Birzilus, duplicarius Murius Novellus, duplicarius X, primus principalium Sallustius Victor, duplicarius Valerius Felix, duplicarius Gargilius Victor, duplicarius Octavius Saturninus, duplicarius Maevius Fuscus, duplicarius Staberius Marcianus, duplicarius Iulius Crescentinus, duplicarius Valerius Felix, duplicarius X, primus principalium Sextilius Datus, duplicarius Antistius Zabinus, duplicarius Nonius Rogatus, duplicarius Aelius Quartio, duplicarius Iulius Assenius, Felix Felix, duplicarius Iulius Saturninus, duplicarius Cerellius Martialis, duplicarius Iulius Donatus, duplicarius Numerius Victor, duplicarius Caecilius Donatus, duplicarius Aurelius Derisor, duplicarius Iulius Datus, duplicarius Cornelius Rogatus, duplicarius Cornelius Colonus, duplicarius Herennius Felix, duplicarius Aelius Saturninus, duplicarius Sempronius Dativus, duplicarius Aemilius Africanus, duplicarius Fulvius Victor, duplicarius Staberius Cinnus, duplicarius Murius Felix, duplicarius Aelius Lucidus, duplicarius Caecilius Saturninus, duplicarius Caius Iulius Felix, duplicarius Lucius Sextilius Victor, duplicarius tensius Saturninus, duplicarius acus, duplicarius ctor, duplicarius ndus, duplicarius atus, duplicarius anus, duplicarius X, primus principalium ctus, duplicarius Aelius Saturninus, duplicarius Esuvius Faustianus, duplicarius Crepereius Tertullus, duplicarius Geminius Viator, duplicarius Marius Saturninus, duplicarius Anicius Honoratus, duplicarius Geminius Flaminalis, duplicarius Gavius Acutus, duplicarius Iulius Ianuarius, duplicarius Cornelius Baricio, duplicarius Sittius Crescens, duplicarius Iulius Bassus, duplicarius Iuventius Gallus, duplicarius Bibuleius Concessianus, duplicarius Vibius Rogatus, duplicarius Aurelius Nimmira, duplicarius Iulius Urbanus, duplicarius Rutilius Hospes, duplicarius Volussius Clarus, duplicarius Sempronius Vinitor, duplicarius Valerius Catulus, duplicarius Arabonius Caecilianus, duplicarius Tonneius Ianuarius.
EDCS-69500195.summary: A long dedication by numerous soldiers of the III Augusta Pia Vindicis Antoniniana legion, including officers and various ranks, expressing gratitude for their safe return from an eastern expedition under Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus.

EDCS-69500197.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Mercurius, a soldier of the province of Moesia Superior, with 5 years of service, lived 30 years. Aurelia Mucia erected this for her well-deserving brother.
EDCS-69500197.summary: A tombstone set up by Aurelia Mucia for her brother Aurelius Mercurius, a soldier in Moesia Superior.

EDCS-70000002.translation: To our lords, the ever-triumphant Emperors Valens, Valentinianus, and Gratianus Augusti.
EDCS-70000002.summary: An inscription honoring the emperors Valens, Valentinianus, and Gratianus, likely from the late 4th century CE.

EDCS-70000003.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Herennius Secundus lived [ ] years. Saia lived 5 years. The heirs erected this.
EDCS-70000003.summary: A tombstone for Herennius Secundus and Saia, erected by their heirs.

EDCS-70000007.translation: Iulia Honorata lived 80 years. A brief vision.
EDCS-70000007.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Honorata, noting her age and a brief life.

EDCS-70100135.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. An heir erected this for Titus Flavius Saturninus, a soldier of the Legio III Augusta.
EDCS-70100135.summary: A tombstone for Titus Flavius Saturninus, a soldier of the Legio III Augusta, erected by his heir.

EDCS-70100136.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed for Frugania Storge.
EDCS-70100136.summary: A tombstone dedicating the space to the spirit of Frugania Storge.

EDCS-70100137.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Amatius Felix, a soldier of the Legio III Augusta, a *beneficiarius* of a tribune, lived 34 years. His devoted mother erected this for her deserving son.
EDCS-70100137.summary: A tombstone set up by a mother for her son, Caius Amatius Felix, a soldier and *beneficiarius* in the Legio III Augusta.

EDCS-70100138.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Julius Felix, a soldier of the Legio III Augusta, lived 35 years.
EDCS-70100138.summary: A tombstone for Julius Felix, a soldier in the Legio III Augusta.

EDCS-70100139.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Marcius Saturninus lived 75 years, a veteran of the Legio III Augusta.
EDCS-70100139.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Marcius Saturninus, a veteran of the Legio III Augusta.

EDCS-70100140.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Adauctus, his most precious son, lived 13 years. His father erected this for Licinius Paci.
EDCS-70100140.summary: A tombstone set up by a father for his son, Licinius Paci, who died at the age of 13.

EDCS-70100141.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Hilariata, most dutiful daughter, who lived 4 years. Her father Adauctus made this.
EDCS-70100141.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young daughter named Hilariata, erected by her grieving father Adauctus.

EDCS-70100142.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. To Eeta, wife most rare, who lived 40 years. Adauctus made this.
EDCS-70100142.summary: A funerary epitaph for Eeta, an exemplary wife, erected by her husband Adauctus.

EDCS-70100143.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. For Ania Helvidia, who lived 30 years. Her husband Julius Saturninus made this out of devotion.
EDCS-70100143.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ania Helvidia, erected by her husband Julius Saturninus out of devotion.

EDCS-70100144.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Caecilia Quieta, who lived 4 years. Hortensius Avunculus made this.
EDCS-70100144.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Quieta, who died at a young age, erected by Hortensius Avunculus.

EDCS-70100145.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. For Metria Catulina, daughter of Marcus, dutiful, who lived 8 years. Metrius Catulinus, father.
EDCS-70100145.summary: A funerary epitaph for Metria Catulina, daughter of Marcus, erected by her father Metrius Catulinus.

EDCS-70100146.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. For Tribonia Secunda, dutiful, who lived 80 years. Marcus Julius Proculus, her good son, made this for his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-70100146.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tribonia Secunda, erected by her son Marcus Julius Proculus.

EDCS-70100147.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For [---]onia Monnata, who lived 50 years. Julius and Ronianus and Agrippa made this for their well-deserving mother.
EDCS-70100147.summary: A funerary epitaph for Monnata, erected by her sons Julius, Ronianus, and Agrippa.

EDCS-70100149.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Lucius Licinius Marcellus, son of Lucius, of the Ulpian tribe, a veteran from Thamugadi, made this for himself while alive and in memory of his most dutiful wife Clodia Faustina, who lived 60 years. He also dedicated it together with his children Licinius Martialis, Victor, Germana, and Amata.
EDCS-70100149.summary: A self-funded funerary monument erected by the veteran Lucius Licinius Marcellus, honoring himself and his deceased wife Clodia Faustina, and also dedicating it to their children.

EDCS-70100150.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. For Iunia Donata, who lived 22 years. Iulia Crescentana.
EDCS-70100150.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iunia Donata, erected by Iulia Crescentana.

EDCS-70100151.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. For Julius Cispinus, who lived 2 years.
EDCS-70100151.summary: A funerary epitaph for Julius Cispinus, who died in infancy.

EDCS-70100152.translation: The standard-bearer of the Legio III Augusta lived 37 years; his heir made this for him.
EDCS-70100152.summary: A tombstone for a standard-bearer of Legio III Augusta, erected by his heir.

EDCS-70100153.translation: To the departed spirits. Apuleia Iucunda lived 70 years. Her daughters made this for their mother; standing, 13 grandchildren.
EDCS-70100153.summary: A tombstone for Apuleia Iucunda, erected by her daughters and noting 13 grandchildren.

EDCS-70100154.translation: To the departed spirits. Servilius Ianuarius, a soldier of Legio III Augusta, lived 30 years, his own [heir] made this. To the departed spirits. Servilius Datus, a soldier of Legio III Augusta, lived 40 years; he made this for himself.
EDCS-70100154.summary: Two tombstone epitaphs for soldiers of Legio III Augusta, one erected by themselves and the other by an heir.

EDCS-70100156.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Aelius Probus lived 23 years; his dear mother made this for her son.
EDCS-70100156.summary: A tombstone for Aelius Probus, erected by his mother.

EDCS-70100157.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Lucius Pompeius Maius lived 15 years. His mother, Pompeia Galla, made this for her deserving son.
EDCS-70100157.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Pompeius Maius, erected by his mother.

EDCS-70100158.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Iulia Fronilla [...].
EDCS-70100158.summary: An incomplete tombstone inscription for Iulia Fronilla.

EDCS-70100159.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Caius Iulius Extricatus lived 91 years. [His son] Sittius [made this] for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-70100159.summary: A tombstone for Caius Iulius Extricatus, erected by his son.

EDCS-70100160.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Quintus Octavius Aureleius, dutiful, lived 90 years.
EDCS-70100160.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Octavius Aureleius.

EDCS-70100161.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Caius Aelius Max made a burial chamber for his most rare daughter, Aemilia Casta, who lived 7 years.
EDCS-70100161.summary: A tombstone for Aemilia Casta, erected by her father Caius Aelius Max.

EDCS-70100162.translation: To the departed spirits. Flavia Saturnina lived 16 years. Her most dutiful father placed this.
EDCS-70100162.summary: A tombstone for Flavia Saturnina, erected by her father.

EDCS-70100163.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Satrius Felix lived for 65 years. His son and heir erected this.
EDCS-70100163.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Satrius Felix, dedicated by his son and heir.

EDCS-70100164.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Emeritus, son of Caius, lived for [unknown number of] years. [Erected by] Caius Iulius Antiochianus.
EDCS-70100164.summary: A funerary inscription, possibly recording the lifespans of Caius Iulius Emeritus and Caius Iulius Antiochianus.

EDCS-70100165.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Donatus lived for 30 years. TR [part of a title or office, incomplete].
EDCS-70100165.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Iulius Donatus, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-70100166.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Statius Fortis lived for 80 years. His heirs dedicated this to them; he is buried here.
EDCS-70100166.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Statius Fortis, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-70100167.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, conqueror of Parthia, conqueror of Britain, conqueror of Germany, Pius, having tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the 3rd time, Emperor, Father of the Fatherland, and to Julia Augusta, mother of the camp, of the Senate, and of the Fatherland, 1419 miles.
EDCS-70100167.summary: A dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix, listing his titles and achievements, and to Julia Augusta, possibly recording a donation or donation distance.

EDCS-70100168.translation: To the eternal Emperor Lucius Domitius Aurelianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus. KALA PR [part of a title or designation, incomplete].
EDCS-70100168.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Aurelian with various epithets.

EDCS-70100169.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. In memory of Gaius Iulius Montanianus, who lived for 46 years. Iulia Meseltinia [erected this] for her husband.
EDCS-70100169.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Montanianus, dedicated by his wife Iulia Meseltinia.

EDCS-70100170.translation: To the holy lord Saturn. Quintus Titinius Satur, the priest, fulfilled his vow and dedicated this.
EDCS-70100170.summary: A votive inscription to the god Saturn by the priest Quintus Titinius Satur.

EDCS-70100172.translation: NA S DO [partially illegible]. Caius Iulius [son of] Caius Helvius N. Lucius Atilius Cocceius Helvius, Caius Masticius Saturninus, Marcus Apuleius Donatus, Sittius Victor, Aebutius Victor, Caius Volussius Meritus, Caius Iulius Saturninus, Plotius Felix, Cornelius Flaminalis, Caius Modius Victor, Caius Iulius Avitus, Sallustius Crispus, Caius Faonius Faustus, Caius Tonneius Saturninus, Caius Iulius Peregrinus, Caius Aemilius Castus, Aurelius Fortis, Quintus Faonius Caecilianus, Marcus Octavius Donatus, Maevius Felix, Caius Iulius Marcellinus, Aelius Donatus, Caius Areius Faustus, Quintus Iulius Felix, pr [partially illegible]. Caius Iulius Felix, seq [partially illegible]. Quintus Valerius Processus, Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius, Lucius Volussius Ianuarius, Lucius Fabius Muntanus, Marcus Antonius Castrensis, Caius Iulius Saturninus, Caius Iulius Severus, Vibius Celer, Maenius Crescens, Caius Aelius Felix, Ulpius Neptunalis, Geminius Felicianus, Maenius Rogatianus, Iulius Verecundus, Petronius Valens, Iunius Fortunatus, Caesius Rufinus, Manilius Felix, Caelius Celsus, Lucius Valerius Lupus, Iulius Victorinus.
EDCS-70100172.summary: A long list of names, possibly a dedication or membership roster for an association or collegium.

EDCS-71300355.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Demetrius lived for 96 years. His heirs made and dedicated this to his father.
EDCS-71300355.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulius Demetrius, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-71300356.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Octavius Victor lived 99 years. He dedicated (this).
EDCS-71300356.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Caius Octavius Victor and indicates he was the dedicator of the monument.

EDCS-71300357.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Octavius Gududio lived 21 years. His father (dedicated this).
EDCS-71300357.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Octavius Gududio and identifies his father as the dedicator.

EDCS-72600388.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deity Silvanus Comunalis.

EDCS-74100027.summary: This is a simple naming inscription, likely a tombstone or part of a monument dedicated to Sextus Murius Festus.

EDCS-74100028.summary: This is a simple naming inscription, likely a tombstone or part of a monument dedicated to Sextus Murius Festus.

EDCS-74100029.summary: This is a simple naming inscription, likely a tombstone or part of a monument dedicated to Sextus Murius Festus.

EDCS-74300425.translation: [He/She] lived, son of Us, lived 68 years.
EDCS-74300425.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription records the lifespan of an individual identified as the son of Us.

EDCS-74600223.translation: To the perpetual Emperor Constantine, 2 miles.
EDCS-74600223.summary: This inscription likely marks a road, indicating a distance of two miles to a destination or reference point associated with Emperor Constantine.

EDCS-74600224.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Furius Suavis and Maecia Saturnina, most illustrious woman.
EDCS-74600224.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Caius Furius Suavis and Maecia Saturnina, the latter being described with a high social status.

EDCS-75600155.translation: Peregrina placed (this) for her dearest husband.
EDCS-75600155.summary: This funerary inscription records that Peregrina placed the monument for her beloved husband.

EDCS-75600156.translation: For the memory of Annius Milimo, who lived 60 years. Macaula Rogatula, his wife, set this up.
EDCS-75600156.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Annius Milimo and erected by his wife.

EDCS-75600159.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Gabidius Caiolus lived 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-75600159.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death and burial of Marcus Gabidius Caiolus.

EDCS-75600160.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sallustia Hospita, a pious woman, lived 23 years.
EDCS-75600160.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Sallustia Hospita.

EDCS-75600163.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Manilia Rustica, his wife, lived 75 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-75600163.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death and burial of Manilia Rustica.

EDCS-75600165.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Annius.
EDCS-75600165.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Caius Annius.

EDCS-75600259.translation: Caius Iulius Rogatus willingly fulfilled his vow to Caelestis for a temple.
EDCS-75600259.summary: This inscription records Caius Iulius Rogatus fulfilling a vow to the deity Caelestis by dedicating a temple.

EDCS-75600260.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Quintus Granius Felix, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, a veteran from the praetorian guard, who lived 62 years, and for his mother Antonia Maxima, deserving of kindness, who lived 58 years. Quintus Granius Paulus, their son and heir, made this willingly.
EDCS-75600260.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Quintus Granius Paulus for his father, a veteran, and his mother, with approximate dates of death inferable from their ages.

EDCS-75900213.translation: In this place are laid the remains of the holy martyrs Laurentius and Hippolytus, on the 5th day before the Ides of May in the 333rd year of the province.
EDCS-75900213.summary: This inscription marks the burial place of two Christian martyrs, Laurentius and Hippolytus, with a date relative to the establishment of the province.

EDCS-75900481.translation: For the son of VII VIL, he made this.
EDCS-75900481.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that records someone making something for a son.

EDCS-75900482.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Magnio Victor, who lived 80 years, and for Marina, his most rare wife.
EDCS-75900482.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Magnio Victor and his wife Marina.

EDCS-76000020.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudius Quintus, soldier of the Legio VII Gemina, [dedicated this] to Agrippa Ser, deserving woman. She lived 30 years.
EDCS-76000020.summary: A funerary dedication by a soldier of Legio VII Gemina to a woman named Ser, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76000021.translation: Gaius Fabius Secundus made this for himself and his dearest wife Gabinia Polla and his other relatives and descendants while he was alive.
EDCS-76000021.summary: A self-funded tomb inscription (a 'dis Manibus' alternative) set up by Gaius Fabius Secundus for himself, his wife, and descendants, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76000022.translation: To the Invincible, Pious, Fortunate Emperor, from the community of the colony of Thamugadi.
EDCS-76000022.summary: A dedication from the colony of Thamugadi to an unnamed emperor, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76000023.summary: A dedication to the syncretic deity Saturn Augustus, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76000026.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Thus it is: Marcia, a matron by name, daughter of Tertulla Vibi, a junior official, lived 23 years; buried [here by] Marius Secundus, her husband, [for her] deserving [self].
EDCS-76000026.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcia, daughter of Tertulla Vibi and wife of Marius Secundus, who died at age 23, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76000027.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Annia Donata, wife of Annius Cecrunus, an equestrian, [this was dedicated by] Iulia Martilla. She lived 26 years, she is buried here.
EDCS-76000027.summary: A funerary dedication by Iulia Martilla for her wife Annia Donata, who died at age 26, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-77700192.translation: You may perhaps ask what this place holds; I will tell you, though grieving, and I will not deny you the truth: the bodies of Aurelius Felix and Aurelius Optatus are here, buried by their father Aurelius Felix. Here Felix, called by the error of his ancient parents -- for it is contrary to truth to lie -- with no other word on his lips than 'me, me' and his faithful daughter the maiden Victoria, his wife Rogatina, and his daughter Vamaccra, are buried in righteous graves. They slighted no one among men, they loved all; they walked the narrow and constricted path, they returned the spirit they had received and left all earthly things.
EDCS-77700192.summary: A lengthy and poetic funerary inscription for several members of the Aurelius family, including Aurelius Felix, Optatus, Victoria, Rogatina, and Vamaccra, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-78800047.translation: To the Augustoi and our Augustan rulers. Verna, an exactor of the Masculae region, rebuilt with his own funds a temple that had collapsed from its foundations, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-78800047.summary: A dedication by Verna, a tax collector, to rebuild a temple in the Masculae region with his own funds, based on a decree of the local council, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-80600029.translation: his honored priest, Vetidia Victoria, to her husband, undus, priest, his son.
EDCS-80600029.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning a priest, Vetidia Victoria, and her son, also a priest, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-81300137.summary: A simple inscription likely referring to the Roman general Germanicus, possibly on a monument or dedication, dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-81700357.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus. Lucius Clodius Felix, decurion of the First Ala of the Pannonians, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-81700357.summary: This is a votive inscription from the Roman Imperial period, likely 2nd-3rd century CE, by a military officer dedicating something to Mars Augustus.

EDCS-82500172.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Fortunata and Ingenua, her freedwoman daughter, and to Hilara, her daughter and granddaughter. Sacred to the departed spirits. To Hilara, mother of Honorata, a dresser of Gargilii, freedwoman, for her merits. Sacred to the departed spirits. To Felicitas, daughter of Hilara, who lived 15 years, the aforementioned daughter of Gargilia Victoria and Extricata, daughter of Cai, a Praetorian dresser and her freedwoman. Hilara and Extricata made and dedicated this. Sacred to the departed spirits. To Felicitas, daughter of Hilara, who lived 30 years, cared for by her brother Exsuperatus.
EDCS-82500172.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription from the Roman Imperial period, likely 2nd-3rd century CE, set up by Gargilia Victoria and Extricata for their freedwoman, Hilara, and other family members, including daughters Fortunata, Ingenua, and Felicitas, and granddaughter Honorata.

EDCS-82500173.translation: To Margaridis, nurse of the Rupilii, an excellent woman, she lived 70 years, she is buried here. Rupilius Zosimus made this for his most pious, most holy mother-in-law, for whom nothing in the world could be more chaste.
EDCS-82500173.summary: This is a funerary inscription from the Roman Imperial period, likely 1st-2nd century CE, by Rupilius Zosimus dedicating a tomb to his highly esteemed mother-in-law, Margaridis.
